
Chapter 401 caused a sensation. Li Guo was shocked. "You've given me another big surprise?"


As he got into the car, Jiang Tian glanced discreetly in that direction.

He had been somewhat uncertain before, but now Jiang Tian was certain he was being followed.

However, he still didn't know what the other party wanted, so Jiang Tian didn't do anything.

He'd dealt with things as they came; having experienced this a few times before, Jiang Tian wasn't really worried about these things.

Soon, Jiang Tian took the two girls home one by one, and by the time he took Yu Hui home, it was already past 3 a.m.

"Sister Yu Hui, we're home." Jiang Tian woke Yu Hui.

"Ugh..." Yu Hui bravely opened her heavy eyelids, her eyes still somewhat dazed.

This made Jiang Tian feel even more heartbroken, and he wondered if he had been too cruel.

Even after they got home, the car following them remained at a distance.

Sometimes it would appear, sometimes it would stay very far away.

An ordinary person wouldn't be able to notice.

Trying to follow Jiang Tian, ​​the king of criminal investigations, is simply laughable.

Even after returning home, the other party didn't do anything, which puzzled Jiang Tian.

In the following days,

Jiang Tian was anything but idle.

First, taking action against an entire village, confirming evidence, and then submitting it to his superiors was an enormous undertaking.

During this time, Jiang Tian even secretly went to Shimiao Village, using his body camera to record several rudimentary police devices, finally confirming the complete chain of evidence in his hands.

Don't underestimate Jiang Tian's seemingly fuss; previously, he would act directly.

This time, it wasn't a small matter.

It involved an entire village; once action was taken, it would have to be swift and decisive, requiring the cooperation of at least several hundred, even a thousand, police and military personnel to completely seal it off.

Jiang Tian wouldn't act recklessly.

Besides, Jiang Tian had considerable experience in accumulating crime points.

Even if Jiang Tian didn't personally arrest these people, he would still earn crime points because of the evidence and issues he provided.

Taking the risk was a last resort.

If it could be easily resolved and the credit would be substantial, Jiang Tian wasn't crazy; why would he call for backup instead of doing it himself?

After thoroughly organizing the data, Jiang Tian found Song Liguo.

As the branch captain, Song Liguo had been very curious about Jiang Tian these past few days.

"What have you been up to these past few days? You've been elusive. They wanted our anti-drug team to assist with the investigation of Elder Mu's case, and they wanted your help with your criminal investigation skills, but I turned you down. The attack on Wen Yulin's backers is also at a standstill. The entire city bureau is swamped, and you're the only one working so hard," Song Liguo complained, exasperated.

Of course, despite the complaints, Song Liguo spoke with a smile, even personally getting up to make Jiang Tian a cup of tea.

"It smells so good." Jiang Tian said, his words carrying a double meaning.

Song Liguo, though pleased, didn't feel it was a compliment: "I feel like you're insulting me."

"Uncle Song, you're overthinking it," Jiang Tian said, his face beaming with enjoyment.

After working so hard on this case, even Jiang Tian, ​​with his usual composure, couldn't help but feel proud and elated.

Later, to confirm, Jiang Tian, ​​along with Yu Hui and two young girls working for her, went to the bank and quietly retrieved a batch of bank statements from Shimiao Village, definitively confirming the existence of a huge amount of unexplained funds in Shimiao Village.

As for the attack on Wen Yulin...

One must take things one step at a time.

“Tell me, this branch is on tenterhooks right now, we haven’t had a break in two days, and you, you bastard, sometimes don’t even come to the city bureau.”

Jiang Tian had a habit when handling cases.

“Uncle Song, what can I say to you? When we’re working, you should address me by my title, preferably as Team Leader Jiang.” Jiang Tian took a sip of tea.

In his previous life, he was just a lowly case file administrator. Reborn, he had unknowingly become a police inspector. Regardless, that was a considerable leadership position.

Furthermore, if he became a second-level police inspector, Jiang Tian could even take over the position of head of the anti-drug squad.

That's a genuine section-level cadre position, maybe even a higher-ranking one, like deputy director. The key is, Jiang Tian is only twenty-two this year!

Others haven't even graduated, yet Jiang Tian has already traversed a path others take ten years to complete.

He just needs an opportunity.

After all, Jiang Tian's promotion has been a bit too fast.

It's almost unprecedented.

"Team Leader Jiang, what are your instructions?" Song Liguo asked, his face dark.

He'd lose his temper, but considering Jiang's previous good performance, Song Liguo would give him a chance.

Jiang Tian gave him a look of "you're teachable," and hurriedly pulled out two recently developed photos and placed them on the table before Song Liguo could explode.

"What are these?"

Song Liguo looked confused as he took them.

Hiss!!!

Song Liguo looked at the first photo.

He saw a pile of flour in the photo—no, it should be said, white powder in a transparent bag.

Each bag was a standard container bag, capable of holding approximately ten kilograms.

The first photo showed about twenty bags of heroin piled up.

That's equivalent to two hundred kilograms.

Two hundred kilograms of heroin?

Buzz!

Song Liguo felt a chill run down his spine.

Don't be fooled by Jiang Tian's previous large-scale heroin seizures; in reality, how much heroin is seized nationwide each year?

Even a dedicated anti-drug police officer might not seize two hundred kilograms in their entire career.

Even twenty kilograms is difficult to obtain.

Because most cases don't involve such large sums; most of the time, even half a kilogram or a kilogram is considered a major case.

Especially in mainland China, the discovery of even one kilogram of heroin could cause an uproar within the entire city police department.

Two hundred kilograms—that's a huge amount of valuable material.

"Wow, where is this place? Powder, at least two hundred kilograms! You found it?" Song Liguo exclaimed excitedly.

"Don't rush, keep looking!" Jiang Tian gave him a look.

Song Liguo's hands trembled as he hurriedly looked at the second sheet.

Actually, Jiang Tian wasn't worried about Song Liguo taking credit. First, Song Liguo's character was absolutely impeccable. Second, Song Liguo and Jiang Tian had a close relationship.

Thirdly...

In this world, at this point, no one could overshadow Jiang Tian's brilliance.

Even now, whenever Jiang Tian appears on the task list and reports for credit are submitted, even if the main credit was discovered by someone else, the higher-ups always assume that Jiang Tian is definitely taking credit for what they can't use themselves.

Therefore, his reputation has spread, albeit not by much.

"You little rascal, you're playing mysterious again." Song Liguo was in high spirits.

He quickly looked at the second photo.

What he saw...

Buzz!!!

"Pfft!!!"

"Holy crap."

Song Liguo's eyes widened in a three-part burst of excitement, his pupils contracting rapidly.

"You've given me another big one?"



Chapter 402 With ability, first-class merits are everywhere, and the potential is limitless.


The photo showed a dark, gloomy environment followed by a makeshift factory. Inside, however, were various kinds of equipment.

And this equipment...

wasn't familiar.

Logically, given the environment and tools, Song Liguo should have immediately recognized it as a drug manufacturing plant. But while he recognized it, the drug-making equipment left him utterly shocked.

"Based on my observations and investigations, a new type of drug has recently appeared on the Yunhai market," Jiang Tian said after a moment of silence.

That's right, Jiang Tian had discovered this during recent interrogations. This new drug wasn't exactly novel; it was an improved version of an older drug, but with added addictive power and greater harm.

This was no small matter.

"I know what you're saying. Actually, it's not just Yunhai recently. You probably don't know, but recently we've also received inquiries from other city branches. According to them, a large amount of new types of drugs have appeared in their areas recently, and much of it seems to have originated from Yunhai. We just didn't have concrete evidence, but now it seems it's true."

Song Liguo said, speechless.

That's right.

Before this, Song Liguo was furious. These guys were throwing all sorts of accusations at Yunhai. They didn't even know about the new types of drugs, yet they claimed they came from Yunhai.

Song Liguo had even scolded them on the spot, but one sentence from them left him speechless.

"You can't even catch anyone. What can you do besides collect your salary and eat for free?"

Yes, that sentence was spoken at a provincial meeting.

The events of that meeting before Jiang Tian joined the municipal bureau show just how absurd Yunhai's status is.

Most importantly, it shows how serious the situation in Yunhai is.

This isn't just a scapegoat; it's their problem.

"So that's how it is."

Song Liguo's heart skipped a beat. Another drug manufacturing plant.

Hiss!!!

Thinking this, Song Liguo couldn't help but swallow hard, looking at Jiang Tian with astonishment, "Little Jiang, how many First-Class Merits do you have now?"

"Five First-Class Merits and one Special-Class Merit. However, because of my bravery in saving someone a few days ago, headquarters decided to add another First-Class Merit to my tally. It's already on its way to be awarded. After the Wenye Group case is over, I'll get another First-Class Merit." Jiang Tian counted on his fingers.

Wow.

Jiang Tian's heart stirred. This achievement was unprecedented and unlikely to be repeated.

Jiang Tian sighed inwardly, realizing he had made history.

Even heroes from wartime might not have this many First-Class Merits.

Oh no, not necessarily.

But Jiang Tian believed this wasn't the end. As time went on, he would accumulate more and more First-Class Merits.

"I hadn't heard from you for the past few days, I thought you were resting, but I didn't expect you to be investigating cases again."

Song Liguo swallowed hard.

With the Wenye Group's first-class merit and the merit earned on the way, Jiang Tian now has seven first-class merits and one special-class merit.

Added together...

"That's the Eight Merits King?"

Buzz!!!

Song Liguo's head was buzzing.

You know, in over twenty years of police service, Song Liguo only had three second-class merits, not a single first-class merit!

Jiang Tian, ​​on the other hand, had already...

Hiss!!!

"Speaking of this matter, it was Wen Yulin who gave me some information."

Jiang Tian stroked his chin and slowly continued, "Then Wen Yulin was attacked on the road. I immediately sensed something was wrong, so I quickly investigated based on the information Wen Yulin provided. And as expected, do you know how many people were involved in this case?"

"How many people?" Song Liguo's heart skipped a beat.

He had initially thought it was a drug manufacturing plant, but judging from Jiang Tian's expression, could it be a large-scale drug manufacturing plant?

That means...

Good heavens, this kid is about to get another big merit?

Uncovering such a terrifying major case will likely earn him another first-class merit award.

Wow!!!

Don't think it's outrageous.

Nationwide, how much drug is seized in a whole year?

But Jiang Tian seized so much in such a short time.

This isn't to say that the national anti-drug efforts are insufficient; quite the opposite, the situation in Yunhai is akin to nurturing a tiger that will eventually take its toll.

Elsewhere, without the fertile ground for major drug lords to thrive, and with increasingly stringent crackdowns, drug lords are struggling, constantly on the run. While profits are high, they dare not expand their operations too significantly.

Especially drug manufacturing facilities—how much of the entire Yunhai area, with its extensive network of roads, actually operates there?

It provides fertile ground for criminal activity.

There are countless big tigers, big tigers everywhere, plus those from overseas, etc. It can be said that Jiang Tian's current achievements are due to his own efforts.

In fact, it is inextricably linked to the right timing, location, and people.

So, what's visible to the naked eye are eight First-Class Merits and one Special-Class Merit.

Song Liguo swallowed hard; even with his composure, he felt a surge of overwhelming jealousy.

"Heh..." Jiang Tian grinned.

"Am I wrong?" Song Liguo's eyelids twitched.

"Yes and no."

"Who said the photos show everything? This is just one random spot I took. There are many other places I haven't photographed yet." Although Jiang Tian was agile, he was still unfamiliar with the area, and some families in the village kept hunting dogs, making him too easy to be discovered.

Even taking these two photos got him spotted by two hunting dogs, who chased after him barking. Luckily, Jiang Tian was fast enough to leap up the stairs and escape.

Otherwise, anyone else would have alerted the enemy.

The difficulty of the arrest would have increased dramatically.

Sure enough, upon hearing this, Song Liguo jumped to his feet: "Is this a large drug trafficking organization?"

Song Liguo's breathing became rapid. Although the credit belonged to Jiang Tian, ​​Song Liguo felt that by sharing in the credit, he could at least get some collective credit—or, with a bit of luck, even individual credit wasn't impossible!

"What are you all sitting for? Let's go, let's get moving!"

The location had been identified, and the situation seemed fairly well understood. Song Liguo was extremely anxious, but Jiang Tian interrupted him a moment later: "We can't handle this ourselves. This requires a joint military-police operation."

"Is it that serious?" Song Liguo's breathing became even more rapid.

Although he had recently accumulated many merits—working alongside Jiang Tian, ​​even without outstanding achievements, meant he had at least put in the effort—he had received some recognition.

Recently, the entire municipal police bureau had been revitalized, radiating a vibrant energy.

Everyone was full of enthusiasm.



Chapter 403 A Cataclysmic Move, Yunhai Can't Hold Back Anymore


The saying goes, who would willingly engage in corruption if they could stand upright in the sunlight and be a righteous police officer?

When merit, honor, and countless bonuses—a path to glory—are laid out before them, who could ignore it? Who would choose to succumb to the bribes from drug lords, even if those bribes were indeed substantial?

But you must know, the Municipal Public Security Bureau has recently made a fortune, a huge fortune.

They seized so much heroin, which naturally needs to be destroyed.

Dirty money, even if confiscated, must be turned over.

But after turning it over, a portion of it will be distributed as bonuses to those below.

Everyone is working enthusiastically, and their trust in Jiang Tian has grown even stronger. Unbeknownst to many, Jiang Tian's prestige within the entire Narcotics Control Unit has become the undisputed number one.

He has gained everyone's approval and is the one everyone hopes for.

Not only the Criminal Investigation Unit, but thanks to the narcotics control efforts, the other units within the Municipal Public Security Bureau have also seen a significant improvement in their treatment and lives. Those who know the inside story all feel a strong liking for Jiang Tian.

“Next, that’s what I’m going to talk about. The number of criminals exceeds seven hundred. That’s a conservative estimate; the actual number is likely far higher. So, without the cooperation of the military and police, do you think you can catch them all?” Jiang Tian chuckled.

“Only seven hundred people, huh, seven hundred people?”

“Seven hundred people?”

Song Liguo didn’t react at first, but when he did, his mind went completely blank.

It involved seven hundred people in a crime.

What kind of earth-shattering case is this?

Is this some kind of crazy thing?

"Do you know what seven hundred people means?" Song Liguo's eyes widened.

Yunhai, after all, is a first-tier city. A crime involving seven hundred people is a terrifyingly large number.

To put it bluntly, this is like the sky has fallen.

Forget whether the city leaders can sit still, even the provincial bigwigs are probably sitting on pins and needles.

This is about to cause a catastrophe!

What is a surprise?

This is a surprise, but the shock far outweighs the joy.

It even horrified Song Liguo; his heart was pounding.

"Based on my detailed investigation and reconnaissance over the past few days, seven hundred is a conservative estimate; the actual number could even be higher."

Jiang Tian sighed inwardly.

In fact, Jiang Tian now understood why Yunhai's development had come to a standstill. In other words, Yunhai had a malignant tumor.

The larger Yunhai grew, the bigger these malignant tumors became.

These malignant tumors are deeply rooted in every aspect of Yunhai, and with Yunhai's development, these tumors almost always grow alongside it.

To say nothing of other things, without Jiang Tian's recent severe crackdown, those drug traffickers, colluding with powerful forces in Myanmar, would have continued to reap the benefits of Yunhai's development. The more Yunhai developed, the more serious the drug crime became nationwide.

In the end, the higher-ups were completely dumbfounded.

Now Jiang Tian can understand this to some extent. Moreover, this is just the tip of the iceberg. He doesn't know what other terrifying monsters lie behind, but even what has been exposed is enough to be horrifying.

Bang bang bang!!!

Silence. In the silent office, only the heavy thumping of Song Liguo's heartbeat could be heard.

"Seven hundred people. This is too serious, too big a deal. Don't report it to Chief Gao yet. I'll discuss it with your father," Song Liguo instructed solemnly.

Just like when Jiang Tian brought down the Wenye Group.

The Wenye Group, supported by the full support of Yunnan Province, suddenly exploded with such a huge bombshell, a bomb that shook the heavens. Fortunately, Jiang Tian discovered it in advance and resolved it single-handedly.

This meant that Yunhai itself discovered and cleared these bombshells.

However, if Gao Xiaolong were to lead the cleanup, the nature of the situation would be different. It would mean that a problem existing within Yunnan Province needed to be discovered and cleaned up by forces in Beijing—a serious oversight.

The entire leadership of Yunnan Province, from top to bottom, would bear the responsibility.

"I understand."

This was also why Jiang Tian didn't initially seek out Chief Gao. Then, Jiang Tian followed Song Liguo to find Jiang Peng.

When they arrived, Jiang Peng was still rubbing his temples, forcing a smile as he spoke into the phone. Only after they arrived did he hang up, his face filled with worry.

“It’s a call from the province. They want you, Xiao Jiang, to get involved in the case of Old Mu’s car accident, but I’ve temporarily postponed it,” Jiang Peng sighed.

Even his outstanding son has his worries.

Old Mu’s accident was serious, but priorities must be given. Old Mu’s accident was a catastrophe.

The crucial point is that Wen Yulin was nearly killed in the bombing, and the armed police suffered heavy casualties in the attack. This is a truly catastrophic situation, even headquarters has personally called to demand accountability.

Although headquarters knows it wasn’t Jiang Peng’s fault, who made him the director? If he doesn’t take responsibility, who will?

Priorities must be given.

“Xiao Jiang, people from headquarters are coming soon. You and Miss Qiu from the criminal investigation department will form a joint investigation team with the provincial department and headquarters. However, the team will primarily be led by experts from headquarters.” Jiang Peng rubbed his temples.

This matter can't be covered up.

The problem is too serious.

“So important?” Jiang Tian frowned slightly. He thought that with the city bureau's resources, at most they could work with the provincial department to solve the case.

They would conduct a joint investigation.

Because this case is clearly related to the Wenye Group; in other words, it's also a drug lord case.

It involves drug lords.

Jiang Tian also has some leads in the investigation.

As for the people in Beijing, Jiang Tian doesn't want to get involved right now. Besides Gao Xiaolong, whether it's Zeng Fan, Zeng Fan's mentor, or Han Qi's group from yesterday, they all left a bad impression on Jiang Tian.

Overall, they all gave Jiang Tian the impression of underestimating their opponents, or rather, being arrogant.

This kind of mentality might work elsewhere, but in Yunhai, the chances of failing are extremely high.

"At that time, headquarters will deploy about a thousand armed police officers, along with criminal investigation experts, to launch a sustained and severe crackdown on crime in Yunhai," Jiang Peng continued.

Okay.

Jiang Tian knew it; a severe crackdown, in other words, a crackdown on crime.

In general, once caught, regardless of the circumstances, they will all be punished to the fullest extent of the law if their crime falls within the stipulated timeframe. Those facing three to seven years will all receive seven years; those facing life imprisonment or the death penalty will all receive the death penalty. There are no suspended death sentences.

In short, it's better to arrest the wrong person than let them go.

Jiang Tian had thought that with such a major incident, there would be no reaction from the capital. Well, it turns out they've been brewing all along.

This time, they've finally established a sustained, and possibly long-term, presence in Yunhai.

How much authority do they have? Will it affect Jiang Tian's normal investigative methods? Jiang Tian frowned, deep in thought.

Meanwhile, on the other side, Song Liguo had already relayed Jiang Tian's words to Jiang Peng.

Jiang Peng was instantly shocked.

He looked at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

Finally, Jiang Peng took a deep breath and said, "I can't make this decision myself; I need to consult with my superiors."



Chapter 404 The brewing terrifying storm, is it time to take action against Gao Yangyang?


Jiang Tian expressed his understanding; this matter was fundamentally beyond Jiang Peng's control.

After all, mobilizing thousands of military and police personnel was no small matter.

Moreover, even a thousand people might not be enough. The criminals involved were conservatively estimated at seven hundred, meaning at least three times the number of criminals would be needed to be safe—at least two thousand people.

Good heavens, two thousand people!

Even Zhou Zhao couldn't handle it, let alone Jiang Peng. He would have to consult with provincial leaders. This was no small problem; mishandling it could lead to a riot and potentially social chaos.

This was not something Yunhai could manage on his own.

Sure enough.

Just as Jiang Peng was reporting the issue to his superiors,

at Gao Xiaolong's residence.

In other words, while Gao Xiaolong was at the city police station, several uninvited guests arrived at Gao Yangyang's residence.

Actually, Gao Yangyang was an employee of the Narcotics Control Bureau.

However, due to the nature of her undercover work, Gao Yangyang was under constant psychological pressure, and ultimately nearly killed by the drug traffickers.

She almost perished in the drug traffickers' hideout, only surviving a near-death experience before being rescued by Jiang Tian.

Considering the arduousness of Gao Yangyang's mission, and Jiang Tian's merits—the rescue, the dangerous situation, the subsequent deterrent strike against criminals in Myanmar, and the elimination of a major drug lord—

Jiang Tian himself received a special-class merit for that incident, and Gao Yangyang also received a first-class merit and a long leave of absence.

During her leave, Gao Yangyang occasionally participated in a joint operation with the General Headquarters, the one where Jiang Tian rescued Qin Shiyu.

After that mission, Gao Yangyang simply settled down in Yunhai to rest.

Considering the situation in Yunhai, Gao Yangyang felt that staying in Yunhai was better than staying in Beijing, as the drug dealers there were more rampant and offered her more opportunities for advancement.

This resulted in Gao Yangyang currently being idle at home, awaiting transfer orders during her leave.

Of course, on the other hand, Gao Yangyang mainly wanted to get closer to Jiang Tian.

Although she knew their relationship was just an accident, Gao Yangyang found herself missing Jiang Tian more and more lately. Occasionally, Jiang Tian would call her to chat about recent cases, but most of the time, he didn't even have time to rest, so there wasn't much time to maintain their relationship.

This had led to Gao Yangyang feeling increasingly anxious lately.

"Grandpa, when am I being transferred to Yunhai?" Gao Yangyang called Gao Xiaolong.

"That's not something I can decide. Of course, with your first-class merit and connections in Yunhai, it's relatively easy," Gao Xiaolong explained the recent situation.

In short, Yunhai, a place that was originally ignored, has recently become highly sought after.

Of course.

Ordinary anti-drug police officers don't have much demand for their positions.

But those in leadership positions are being eyed by many who want to move there.

Because there were countless merits and cases everywhere, and with Jiang Tianzhu's success as a precedent, those with good connections had already taken action.

So what should have been a simple transfer now required competition.

"Are you crazy?" Gao Yangyang utterly exasperated.

"But it'll be soon. You're not a big problem, and you've already made contributions. A lateral transfer shouldn't be a problem," Gao Xiaolong replied after a moment's consideration.

Gao Yangyang could only hang up the phone helplessly, rubbing her temples.

With Gao Yangyang's current rank, if she were transferred, she'd at least be a deputy captain.

Even a captain was possible.

Just as Gao Yangyang hung up,

a knock sounded at the door.

Gao Yangyang didn't think much of it, as this was a high-ranking official's residential area, very secure.

But the moment she opened the door, she saw several men in suits standing there.

"Excuse me, who are you?" Gao Yangyang frowned.

The man in the suit who was in the lead didn't speak, but simply pointed to the badge on his chest.

Gao Yangyang's eyes remained unchanged: "Sorry, I don't understand what that means."

"We're from the Yunhai Procuratorate's Anti-Corruption Bureau. This is my identification. My name is Yuan Guangliang."

Yuan Guangliang took out his identification and placed it in front of Gao Yangyang.

This department.

This identification.

And rather, this identity.

No matter the leader, anyone within the system would probably feel their legs go weak.

But Gao Yangyang was different. She lived under immense pressure every day in an overseas criminal organization, cautiously navigating the situation, which had long since honed her incredibly strong and terrifying mental fortitude.

Furthermore, Gao Yangyang herself was upright and honest, never having done anything against the organization or justice, so she didn't really feel any psychological pressure.

And another thing was...

The Yunhai Procuratorate couldn't control a Beijing official like her.

Gao Yangyang's mind raced, but she remained outwardly calm, asking, "What is it?"

"We need you to come back with us for questioning," Yuan Guangliang said directly.

Gao Yangyang frowned slightly.

Questioning?

Gao Yangyang sneered, "Questioning what? Besides, my file belongs to the capital. You have no right to question me."

Yuan Guangliang's face darkened, because what Gao Yangyang said was indeed true.

He had immediately taken the initiative, trying to intimidate Gao Yangyang.

However, it was clear that Gao Yangyang had a high level of composure.

"Miss Gao, you may have misunderstood us. You yourself are innocent, but unfortunately, Jiang Tian has committed a crime," Yuan Guangliang said.

Normally, Gao Yangyang would have panicked upon hearing this.

But at this moment, Gao Yangyang remained extremely calm.

No one knew Jiang Tian better than Gao Yangyang.

Leaving aside others...

Even if the entire Yunhai region were filled with corrupt officials, Jiang Tian wouldn't be among them.

Because it's not worth it.

That night, Gao Yangyang worriedly asked Jiang Tian if he needed money; she could give him two or three hundred thousand yuan. Jiang Tian simply said that he had received several million yuan in rewards from his superiors for capturing those criminals.

Therefore...

Would Jiang Tian, ​​a wealthy, powerful, young, and highly decorated man, really collude with drug lords to embezzle funds?

"I don't know him. Please leave." Gao Yangyang said expressionlessly, about to close the door.

But Yuan Guangliang stopped her with his foot: "Miss Gao, there is irrefutable evidence that Jiang Tian is a black sheep. I hope you won't remain obstinate."



Chapter 405 The terror of the approaching storm, the calm before the storm


A black sheep?

Gao Yangyang stared wide-eyed at them, as if looking at a bunch of idiots—no, even the idiots were praising them.

Are they insane?

If it were someone else, even if these people came to arrest her grandfather, Gao Xiaolong, accusing him of embezzlement and bribery, Gao Yangyang might believe it a little, at least more plausible than accusing Jiang Tian.

Do these people know what they're saying? Is the world too crazy, or are they too crazy?

Or...

Here in Yunhai, some people have already gone mad, unable to sit still, and are launching a desperate, last-ditch counterattack.

Although she didn't speak, Gao Yangyang's mind raced in an instant.

"Miss Gao, according to our investigation, you don't need to deny it, we know you and Jiang Tian have a special relationship, even that you have hotel records together." Yuan Guangliang was confident, a slight smile playing on his lips.

As if everything was under control.

Gao Yangyang narrowed her eyes. There had actually been three encounters between her and Jiang Tian.

The first was when he rescued her; the second was after he got her drunk; and the third was when Gao Yangyang invited him out recently.

Of course, being young, Jiang Tian was physically strong and could easily resist temptation outside, but with an old friend like Gao Yangyang, he practically went along with it.

After all, sometimes, in a pinch, the younger one controls the older one.

The last time was actually in a hotel.

The key was that they didn't hide anything then; they were completely open about it. Neither of them were married, so there was nothing to hide.

"What are you trying to say?" Gao Yangyang scoffed, seemingly unconcerned.

But deep down, Gao Yangyang felt a slight chill. Sure enough, some people couldn't sit still anymore, resorting to any means necessary. And knowing about this meant they had been watching Jiang Tian.

Did Jiang Tian know?

Gao Yangyang's heart turned cold, a hint of malice flashing within her.

Having survived so long in a drug cartel, don't be fooled by Gao Yangyang's facade in front of Jiang Tian. The fake Gao Yangyang is only so considerate and sweet-talking to Jiang Tian; the real Gao Yangyang is ruthless and decisive.

"Miss Gao, we all know you are the granddaughter of Chief Gao. Chief Gao's character and reputation are well-known. We believe you must also be on the side of justice." Yuan Guangliang seemed to have everything under control.

"Upholding justice?" Gao Yangyang sneered.

Yuan Guangliang nodded solemnly: "There are always some people who, for profit, sell their conscience and ultimately go astray. Even in the face of profit, they can become so heartless that they forget their former passion and sense of justice. We resolutely crack down on such black sheep and will never tolerate them. Even if it's fraught with difficulties, this is our mission."

His words were filled with passion and righteous indignation.

Even Yuan Guangliang himself seemed almost moved.

Gao Yangyang's face grew increasingly expressionless.

Some even wanted to laugh.

"Ms. Gao, you probably don't know what Jiang Tian did, do you? He even had close relationships with other women. Look, this is the evidence we photographed."

As they spoke,

Yuan Guangliang produced a stack of photos. The photos showed Jiang Tian and Mu Ya talking intimately together.

There were also photos of Jiang Tian leaving with three girls from the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

“What are you trying to say? What can these photos possibly convey?” Gao Yangyang looked at this crude tactic without any joy.

Because it was obvious that these people's sudden intervention meant one thing:

It was far more than this. These people were clearly not fools.

“Of course, while a chaotic private life violates discipline, neither of us being married constitutes a crime. We just wanted Miss Gao to see the true face of Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep. In fact, we already have concrete evidence of Jiang Tian's crimes and bribery.” Yuan Guangliang said confidently.

“Now that you've said that, get out of here.” Gao Yangyang wanted to contact Jiang Tian as soon as possible to ask what was going on and to warn him to be careful.

Someone was already targeting him, trying to frame him.

"I'm sorry, but you'll have to come with us." Yuan Guangliang didn't move.

"You're courting death," Gao Yangyang said coldly.

What a joke.

You people from Yunhai, what right do you have to access her files in Beijing? Most importantly, she's from headquarters. Without orders from headquarters, they have no right to act.

Of course.

Strictly speaking, the police system is under dual jurisdiction.

One side is from headquarters, and the other is from local authorities.

Locally, people like Jiang Tian fall under their jurisdiction.

But Gao Yangyang, these people don't have the right to arrest her.

Yuan Guangliang, however, looked confident, as if he was completely prepared.

"According to our investigation, Jiang Tian once gave you gifts worth nearly 100,000 yuan," Yuan Guangliang said confidently.

"What do you mean?" Gao Yangyang's gaze grew even colder.

“I don’t mean anything by it, but the watch Jiang Tian gave you is worth over 100,000 yuan according to our investigation. You are also a suspect. Of course, we know you are innocent, but we need you to cooperate with us in the investigation.”

Yuan Guangliang’s tone gradually softened.

Because, according to their investigation, the biggest problem in taking action against Jiang Tian would be the obstruction from Gao Yangyang and Gao Xiaolong. As for Jiang Peng, he was just a bureau chief and couldn’t do much.

What they feared most was that Gao Yangyang would find Gao Xiaolong, and then Gao Xiaolong would interfere, ultimately ruining their efforts.

However, if they arrested Gao Yangyang first, Gao Xiaolong would be overwhelmed, and they wouldn’t do anything to Gao Yangyang. They would simply put her in their detention room, provide her with good food and drink, and once Jiang Tian’s case was settled, it would happen naturally.

Gao Yangyang would be released then.

“I hope you won’t regret it later.” Gao Yangyang was about to make her move.

But soon, Gao Yangyang seemed to have thought of something, and a cold smile appeared on her face.

That smile made Yuan Guangliang somewhat uneasy, but then, thinking of the arrangements and the evidence they possessed, Yuan Guangliang straightened his back, his face full of confidence.

Besides, they had also enlisted Han Qi in this operation.

It could be said that no matter Jiang Tian's identity, background, or who was protecting him, he was already in control, and no one could change that.

Of course.

They didn't know that.

What is Jiang Tian doing at this very moment?

At the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Jiang Tian is keeping his mouth shut, or rather, neither Song Liguo nor Jiang Peng has inquired about the specifics of the case.

Because what Jiang Tian has revealed so far is enough to send chills down their spines.

A new type of drug.

A crime involving seven hundred people.

If handled poorly, it could lead to massive social chaos.

It could potentially cause a catastrophe.



Chapter 406 What does a drug enforcement officer know about criminal investigation?


At this point, who dared to take on this responsibility?

Therefore, Jiang Peng directly called Zhou Zhao.

That's why.

Although there was dual leadership, the situation at the local level and headquarters was different. Jiang Peng, as the local leader, needed to prioritize local stability.

After Jiang Peng reported to Zhou Zhao, Zhou Zhao fell into a long silence.

Seven hundred people!

"Okay, you wait here, we'll hold a meeting to discuss this immediately," Zhou Zhao replied immediately.

"Yes," Jiang Peng nodded.

To be honest, this was because Jiang Tian always preferred to act decisively and without hesitation.

Because the case was so serious, once higher-ups intervened, unless Gao Xiaolong could ignore them, they would basically have to wait for a meeting.

Sometimes, the situation was urgent, and by the time they held a meeting, it was too late.

Jiang Tian was already mentally prepared, so he wasn't flustered at all. Besides, he hadn't alerted the culprits in Shimiao Village.

Most importantly...

Many questions hadn't been fully clarified by Song Liguo and Jiang Peng. Once they confirmed the situation, they understood it wasn't within their jurisdiction.

Furthermore, it was beyond their capabilities to handle.

Reporting this would naturally attract inquiries from higher-ups.

Most importantly, with such a terrifying scale of involvement and such a horrific case, reporting too much detail might raise suspicions.

Of course, it wasn't that they suspected anything.

It was mainly a matter of caution. If they knew exactly who it was, what if it was a powerful force, and while the higher-ups were still holding endless meetings, the criminals escaped?

After hanging up the phone, Jiang Peng wiped the cold sweat from his forehead.

He then glanced at his son with lingering fear, swallowing hard.

"The city is having a meeting, but I reckon we should have a result soon," Jiang Peng said, glancing at the time.

"Alright, I'll go check on the case's progress," Jiang Tian nodded.

"Don't go too far."

Jiang Peng called out.

Jiang Tian waved his hand, then walked out the door.

He saw Yu Hui carrying a huge stack of documents, moving them around.

"Be careful."

Jiang Tian, ​​always eager to help, went to assist her.

“You pervert!” Yu Hui said angrily.

“Who’s a pervert? Where’s the pervert?” Jiang Tian was surprised; he didn’t believe there were perverts in the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Yu Hui’s face flushed red, and she glared at Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian was completely bewildered. Had his arm just touched her? He thought he had, but he didn’t.

“These documents are for the people you killed yesterday. But we’ve only identified a few. A large portion still needs confirmation and needs to be sent to the Criminal Investigation Department for their assistance,” Yu Hui explained.

“Oh, oh, oh,” Jiang Tian said, holding the documents.

To be honest, Jiang Tian felt guilty. He watched everyone busy comparing various data and documents, searching for clues to identify potential suspects.

But what about Jiang Tian?

He suddenly felt that his special emergency response team had become the most relaxed people in the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

The Narcotics Control Division was actually located on the third floor, while the Criminal Investigation Division was on the second floor, not far apart. Jiang Tian delivered the documents to the Criminal Investigation Division.

The person responsible for liaising with them was an old acquaintance of Jiang Tian's.

"Miss Qiu," Jiang Tian warmly shook hands with Miss Qiu.

Miss Qiu, however, still had those lifeless eyes, expressionlessly watching Jiang Tian shake hands. After a moment, she said, "Is there anything else? If not, I have to go now."

Jiang Tian could tell Miss Qiu was in a bad mood.

Then, a thought struck him, and he instantly understood.

You see, Miss Qiu was initially in charge of the investigation direction for Elder Mu's case; of course, she was only a participant.

For such joint investigations, provincial experts usually act as the main investigators.

But without a doubt, Miss Qiu's reputation as a genius might have played a role here.

However, with experts from the Municipal Bureau, the Provincial Bureau, and even headquarters, and still no leads, it was no wonder Miss Qiu was in a bad mood.

"Wait a minute."

Just as Jiang Tian was about to turn and leave, Miss Qiu suddenly called out to him.

"What is it?" Jiang Tian turned and asked.

"Do you have any leads on Old Master Mu's case?" Miss Qiu inquired.

"I'm just a narcotics officer, what do I know about criminal investigation?" Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

Standing beside Miss Qiu, Zhang Zhi's expression gradually froze.

Then a helpless smile appeared on his face.

To be honest.

Old Master Mu was treated by Jiang Tian in this case.

The key point is that Jiang Tian had previously collaborated with the criminal investigation team to solve a case. At that time, the abilities Jiang Tian displayed were unmatched even by those criminal investigation experts.

His logical thinking ability...

He perfectly grasped the psychology of the criminal.

At the time, Zhang Zhi strongly advocated for Jiang Tian's participation in the joint investigation.

But it was rejected at the time.

The provincial experts' reason at the time was exactly this.

"What does a narcotics officer know about criminal investigation?"

Jiang Tian's words were a fatal blow.

Most importantly, when the case stalled, Zhang Zhi even recounted Jiang Tian's previous investigative reasoning, hoping Jiang Tian would participate.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian was too busy with the narcotics team to participate.

"Alright, I'll be going if there's nothing else." Jiang Tian turned and left.

Priorities varied. The narcotics team was swamped, while the criminal investigation team didn't have any major cases.

After Jiang Tian left, Miss Qiu's deadpan eyes flickered slightly.

"Sigh, the experts from the provincial department are a bit arrogant, they've offended Jiang Tian," Zhang Zhi sighed.

He understood that this was mainly because Jiang Tian was young and had already achieved so much.

More importantly, it was because Jiang Tian single-handedly eradicated the drug dealers.

Many criminal investigation experts had a somewhat biased view of Jiang Tian, ​​who did the manual labor.

“Team Leader Jiang might genuinely be busy. If he has the time, he'll get involved,” Miss Qiu said slowly.

“Hmm?” Zhang Zhi looked surprised.

After Miss Qiu finished speaking, she turned and went back without a flicker of emotion in her deadpan expression.

Work like identifying suspects didn't require Miss Qiu's involvement; her subordinates would naturally handle the investigation.

Now, Miss Qiu needed to think of a breakthrough in the case.

"It seems the key to the breakthrough lies with the murderer, but we can't find him domestically. He's most likely from Myanmar. How do we investigate?"

Miss Qiu scratched her messy hair in frustration.

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of all this. After delivering the documents to the criminal investigation department, his phone rang.

He took it out and saw it was Mu Ya.

Jiang Tian then remembered that he hadn't had a chance to contact Mu Ya in the past few days because she had been recuperating in the hospital after Old Master Mu's accident.

"Where are you?" Mu Ya's sweet voice rang out.



Chapter 407 On the eve of a major operation, you dare to arrest your superior?


Soft.

Sweet and chewy.

Comfortable.

Hearing that long-lost voice, Jiang Tian's face softened into a gentle smile.

Although the previous conflict had created some distance, upon reflection, Mu Ya herself was in a difficult position.

After all, she was her own mother.

Why should she be angry?

"At the Municipal Bureau? What's wrong? Grandpa Mu has been discharged from the hospital?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Grandpa was discharged a long time ago and is back at work now," Mu Ya replied.

"Discharged? That's good." Jiang Tian smiled as well.

"I'm at your Municipal Bureau. Do you have time?" Mu Ya asked directly.

"I'll come find you."

Jiang Tian smiled upon hearing this.

Previously, Jiang Tian hadn't figured out how to handle the situation, but now he had a more open mind.

He'd deal with whatever came his way, taking it one step at a time.

Since the relationships were already quite complicated, Jiang Tian could only take things one step at a time. Besides, he was still relatively young, so there was no rush to do anything.

Soon, when Jiang Tian arrived at the city police station, he saw Mu Ya waving from afar.

Upon approaching, Mu Ya obediently took Jiang Tian's arm with both hands.

This surprised Jiang Tian greatly.

He knew from their previous interactions that Mu Ya was a very conservative woman, and she never knew what it meant to take the initiative. Getting Mu Ya to make the first move was practically a pipe dream.

Even holding hands sometimes made her blush with embarrassment.

Now, Mu Ya was actually being so proactive, which truly caught Jiang Tian off guard, causing him to glance at her sideways.

Mu Ya blushed slightly, a smile playing on her lips, revealing her stunning profile: "You're getting a good deal."

"Can you make it even cheaper?" Jiang Tian pressed his advantage.

"Dream on." Mu Ya rolled her eyes, but despite saying that, she moved even closer to Jiang Tian.

Looking at the compliant Mu Ya, Jiang Tian sighed inwardly.

This Mu Ya was lovable, but also heartbreaking.

"I'm starting work in a few days, as a host at a TV station in Yunnan Province," Mu Ya said.

Jiang Tian knew about this; Mu Ya had been pursuing a master's degree in journalism.

But Jiang Tian hadn't expected such a terrifying start right after starting her career?

Starting at the provincial TV station?

Jiang Tian couldn't help but feel envious.

"We'll have even less time to see each other then," Mu Ya said, a hint of unhappiness in her voice.

She was always like this, outwardly so dignified and poised, but in front of the person she loved most, she was always so adorable and charming.

A perfect contrast.

Especially after what had happened, Jiang Tian clearly sensed that Mu Ya had become even more dependent on him.

And even more compliant.

“Ya’er, what do you want to eat?” Jiang Tian asked.

“There’s a hot pot restaurant that just opened not far from here. I heard the food is pretty good. Let’s go eat hot pot,” Mu Ya said without hesitation.

“No problem.” Jiang Tian led Mu Ya to the hot pot restaurant.

“I heard that this Hai Di Hot Pot restaurant not only has excellent service, but the food is also very good.”

Mu Ya pointed ahead.

Jiang Tian glanced at Hai Di Hot Pot.

Suddenly, he understood a lot.

This hot pot restaurant is just starting to expand, and the brand is still in its early stages.

However, Jiang Tian knows all too well what a behemoth the owner of Haidilao Hot Pot will become in just over a decade. He even believes that this one hot pot restaurant will surpass many real estate tycoons, making him worth hundreds of billions.

Coincidentally, Zhao Qiuni is an investor.

Especially in Yunhai, where she's looking for investment projects.

Jiang Tian feels that if he has time, he might as well offer some advice. As for investing himself, Jiang Tian is certain that if he can't make it in the government, he can dominate the business world.

However, it's clear that his current profession is a perfect fit, and Jiang Tian has no intention of changing it.

Besides, money can never compare to power.

That's the most important thing.

"Then let's try it," Jiang Tian said, intrigued.

It must be said, this hot pot restaurant truly lived up to its reputation for courteous service. Upon entering, they were respectfully led along by waiters, who greeted them with smiles.

Jiang Tian and Mu Ya were inseparable.

It could only be said that after their ordeal, their relationship seemed to have progressed by leaps and bounds.

They were even more intimate than before.

Jiang Tian never forced or deliberately pushed things forward; he always let things happen naturally.

“The most triumphant moments in a man's life are his wedding night and his passing the imperial examination. I think I've experienced both.”

Jiang Tian's gaze softened considerably. After all, once the Shimiao Village matter was resolved, he would receive a special merit award.

With so many merits, he might even have a chance to be promoted in his police rank.

Wow.

A second-level police inspector at twenty-two years old.

Jiang Tian himself swallowed hard.

I dare not think about it, I absolutely dare not think about it.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Just then, a series of hurried footsteps approached.

They stood before Jiang Tian.

Mu Ya frowned slightly as she looked over, then looked down at Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian, ​​unhurried, wiped his mouth, his face showing no surprise, as if he had already anticipated these people.

That's right.

He had indeed anticipated it.

If it weren't for the fact that they didn't have any crime points, daring to follow him for several days would have been a death sentence. Jiang Tian hadn't acted because he wanted to see what these people were up to.

In fact, in Jiang Tian's eyes, their so-called surveillance was nothing more than child's play. For example, when Jiang Tian investigated Shimiao Village, these people knew absolutely nothing.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you've been caught."

Han Qi looked down at Jiang Tian with disdain.

As if looking at a piece of trash.

"Arrest me? Are you prepared to bear the consequences?" Jiang Tian said slowly.

When he returned to the city police station today, Jiang Tian re-investigated the two suspects who had tried to harm Zhao Qiuni and her group yesterday, the two who survived.

The results were shocking; these two were utterly despicable.

A simple investigation by a few officers yielded substantial evidence, including both physical and written proof.

What awaits them is predictable.

Before this, the sentence might have been three to seven years, but with connections, one could get out after two or three years.

But let's get this straight.

In this period of severe crackdown, those caught committing crimes, especially those with egregious offenses, are likely to be executed.

“The price? The price is to bring a more peaceful society to the people of Yunhai. I came to Yunhai not for anything else, but to purge black sheep like you, the cancer of the organization,” Han Qi declared openly.

The next moment, Jiang Tian stood up.

Looking down at Han Qi, seeing his righteous expression, Jiang Tian smiled, patted Han Qi's shoulder, and said calmly:

“This price, you cannot bear.”

Not just him.

Even those behind him.

Two thousand military and police officers launched an operation, and their commander was arrested. What would happen?

Jiang Tian didn't know.

But he knew Han Qi was finished.



Chapter 408 Are you crazy? Mu Ya has become a protector?


At this moment, Han Qi was filled with confidence and excitement.

On his way here, Han Qi had watched Zhao Qiuni fawn over Jiang Tian, ​​like a devoted follower, while he felt like a clown.

Of course, Han Qi thought none of this mattered, for he was righteous and fearless. Now, he believed that as long as Zhao Qiuni saw the true face of Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep, she would change her mind.

But how could he know?

Right now, it was only because Zhao Qiuni didn't know. If she did, she probably would have slapped him across the face long ago.

“Jiang Tian, ​​you’ve been exposed. Don’t put up a futile resistance, because every word you utter will become evidence in your defense.”

Han Qi was not in a hurry. This time, he was certain of victory.

It didn’t matter who came.

He even imagined that after Jiang Tian was arrested, Zhao Qiuni would see the true colors of this scumbag, and then she would understand who the real good person was, and who the man she could entrust her life to was.

As he spoke, Han Qi took out handcuffs.

To be honest, Han Qi was prepared for Jiang Tian to resist.

He even secretly hoped that Jiang Tian would resist arrest or take some extreme action; if he did, Han Qi would be pleasantly surprised.

Then Jiang Tian would be completely finished.

Even after handcuffing Jiang Tian, ​​Han Qi still had a hint of regret on his face, seemingly regretting that Jiang Tian hadn't resisted.

However, Han Qi quickly seemed to be overcome by a sense of righteousness, and a relieved smile appeared on his face as he said, "Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​making mistakes in the past is not terrible. As long as you admit your mistakes in time, there is still a chance to redeem yourself. Don't become a black sheep and a cancer in the organization."

His condescending tone was full of lecturing Jiang Tian.

These words, however, caused Jiang Tian to frown slightly.

Han Qi, is he worthy?

Is he worthy to say such things?

Jiang Tian has survived countless battles and near-death experiences to get to where he is today. He has made so many contributions and achieved so much; he, Jiang Tian, ​​has created a miracle.

Even going so far as to expose the entire criminal organization in Yunhai and Yun Province.

If Jiang Tian hadn't exposed it, would Han Qi have gone to great lengths to get here?

What is he here for? Isn't it just to scavenge his scraps?

"I don't think I've done anything wrong," Jiang Tian said, flashing the handcuffs with a chuckle.

These are easy to put on, but difficult to remove.

"Stubborn to the end, you black sheep, still unrepentant," Han Qi said, laughing angrily.

"Why waste time talking nonsense with him? This kind of incorrigible black sheep should be arrested and brought to trial by the state!"

A young girl following behind Han Qi pointed at Jiang Tian angrily.

Jiang Tian glanced at her.

She looked to be about twenty-seven or twenty-eight years old, her eyes were bloodshot, and she pointed at Jiang Tian as if he had committed an unforgivable crime.

Even now.

Mu Ya, who had been observing the situation from beside Jiang Tian, ​​also looked grim.

These people were trying to arrest Jiang Tian without any explanation.

What were they doing?

Putting everything else aside, just look at all the things Jiang Tian has done. Without Jiang Tian, ​​Yunhai wouldn't be what it is today.

Besides, even if his merits and demerits don't offset each other, Jiang Tian has only been in the group for a short time. To accuse him of embezzlement and bribery?

That's a joke.

Do you know how many times those criminals have attacked Jiang Tian, ​​even his family, in retaliation?

Yet you immediately launch into these baseless accusations.

"Mu Ya is alright," Jiang Tian reassured Mu Ya.

"I'm going to find my grandfather," Mu Ya said angrily.

Normally, Mu Ya's emotions are very stable, but that depends on the situation.

If it were anything else, it would be fine, but these people are accusing Jiang Tian of embezzlement and bribery without any evidence.

This is utter nonsense.

What kind of inexplicable accusation is this?

Oh no.

Perhaps, those criminal groups saw they couldn't deal with Jiang Tian from the outside, so they started from within?

Mu Ya's mind raced.

Perhaps Mu Ya had always been highly intelligent and perceptive, able to understand many things.

These people were clearly up to no good, but Mu Ya didn't immediately lash out.

However, her restraint didn't mean she wasn't furious.

"Your grandfather?"

Han Qi's heart skipped a beat.

Could it be that there was a protective umbrella in Yunhai?

Good, good, good.

Han Qi was overjoyed. He came to Yunhai for what? For fame, for merit, but most importantly, to uphold justice.

To crack down on the protective umbrella in Yunhai.

To resolutely eradicate evil.

To let the sunlight of justice shine upon Yunhai.

"Arrest them all. This person might be an accomplice," Han Qi decided decisively.

That's why he was known for his ruthless and efficient actions.

With just a single sentence, he was going to arrest Mu Ya as well.

"Fine, let's see if you still have any respect for the law." Mu Ya practically laughed in anger.

"Try it, let's try it then."

Han Qi sneered. He feared everything else, but threats were the least of his worries.

It was a joke.

Even with his grandfather gone, a fallen camel is still bigger than a horse.

In terms of background and connections.

Whoever came would die.

Moreover, if he could take down a protective umbrella, the bigger the commotion, the greater the reward might be.

Then, starting with Yun Hai, he would rise to prominence.

He would step onto the stage of history that belonged to him.

But the moment Han Qi finished speaking,

he instantly felt a bone-chilling cold.

This coldness was intense, a chill that emanated from the depths of his heart.

It even made Han Qi shiver involuntarily.

And that wasn't all.

The next moment, a shadow enveloped Han Qi's vision.

When Han Qi looked up, he was met with Jiang Tian's expressionless face and chilling aura.

You see...

Everyone, after killing someone, has a completely different demeanor.

If someone has only killed one person, an ordinary person might just feel that the person seems a little scary or fierce.

But for a professional detective, a veteran detective can immediately sense something is wrong—that murderous aura. A veteran detective can instantly smell it.

That's what's known as killing intent.

It sounds mystical, but the truth is, Jiang Tian has killed far too many people.

Several hundred, if not a thousand, drug dealers have died at Jiang Tian's hands.

The sheer number of people he's killed, and the indifference he exudes towards life, is truly terrifying.

"You... what do you want to do? Are you still trying to resist arrest?"

Just as Han Qi's body stiffened and chills ran down his spine, the girl standing next to him stepped forward and shouted fiercely.

Han Qi touched his pants; they were a little wet. But he breathed a sigh of relief; thankfully, it wasn't from fear, just a little bit of urine.



Chapter 409 A petty official with enormous greed, self-congratulatory sentimentality, and a rampage.


The next moment, Han Qi's face flushed slightly. Fortunately, everyone's attention was focused on Jiang Tian, ​​and no one noticed his disheveled appearance.

"I can cooperate with your law enforcement, but I hope you won't use the power entrusted to you by the people to flaunt your authority. What, do you still think you're the Embroidered Uniform Guard?" Jiang Tian sneered.

Besides.

Even if there really were an Embroidered Uniform Guard, it would be him.

Jiang Tian didn't take out that red identification card.

He just wanted to see who was behind it all and what they were planning.

As for embezzlement, bribery, and other illegal activities, that was a joke.

Fine.

Jiang Tian knew his assets, but his assets were system rewards, with a clean and honest origin, either rewards from the city bureau and higher authorities for apprehending criminals.

System-rewarded wealth always leaves traces.

All the money came from various investments, and even every transaction record was documented. How he did it, Jiang Tian didn't know.

But it was still very mysterious.

So Jiang Tian wasn't afraid of being investigated.

Just to prevent such problems from happening again, he figured, if he was going to cause trouble, he'd make a big one.

“You black sheep have no right to say that.”

The fierce-looking woman stepped forward again, pointing angrily at Jiang Tian.

“What’s your name?” Jiang Tian looked at her calmly.

“What, you want revenge?”

The woman said directly, “Let me tell you, I’m Ding Xiaoxiao, an investigator from the First Comprehensive Division of the Discipline Inspection Commission.”

“Okay, I understand.”

Jiang Tian didn’t say anything more; there was no need to say anything more now.

In fact, there was no need for Elder Mu to intervene, because it was completely unnecessary.

“Wait patiently, I’ll be fine,” Jiang Tian reassured Mu Ya.

He also gave Mu Ya a look, telling her not to do anything.

Jiang Tian believed Mu Ya understood.

Mu Ya did understand.

However, she also knew that Jiang Tian’s trip wouldn’t be pleasant.

“Let’s go, Officer Jiang. We’ve been waiting in the VIP box for ages. Don’t worry, it’s fully stocked with food and drinks, even exercise equipment. We’ll definitely fatten you up.” Han Qi gave a wink to those behind him.

Ding Xiaoxiao and her subordinates understood immediately.

No matter what, Han Qi had witnessed Jiang Tian’s rage before, and he was genuinely afraid Jiang Tian might take them down.

That would likely cause huge chaos.

To be on the safe side, Han Qi compromised and didn’t take action against Mu Ya.

Of course, this was on the condition that he didn’t discover that this woman was also suspected of being an accomplice to Jiang Tian.

Or rather, that she was his protector.

But that required careful consideration. Right now, the most urgent task was to determine Jiang Tian’s nature.

As for unexpected events?

Han Qi had never considered that.

When it came to background, Han Qi wasn’t afraid of anyone.

As for power…

With the Discipline Inspection Commission and the Procuratorate working together, how could they possibly fail?

Both the Discipline Inspection Commission and the Procuratorate's anti-corruption bureaus have already confirmed the case. What problems could a police officer like Jiang Tian possibly have?

How could that be? It's absolutely impossible.

Perhaps Jiang Tian has done a lot of good deeds.

But Han Qi is 100% certain that there is a huge corruption ring behind Jiang Tian.

Because Jiang Tian is even driving a Phaeton.

He could fool others, but he couldn't fool Han Qi about the car.

This was why Han Qi acted with absolute certainty. Good heavens, how could a mere police officer afford a Phaeton, a luxury car worth millions?

Did he really think they were fools?

Following Jiang Tian's lead, how much more could he uncover?

The worst thing in investigating a case is not finding a breakthrough; once you find one, it's like a flood bursting its banks.

Han Qi had considerable experience with this.

"We're from the Discipline Inspection Commission, conducting an investigation. Please enjoy your meal as usual. We apologize for the chaos caused," Han Qi apologized to everyone at the hot pot restaurant as he left.

He truly perfected the art of appearances.

Putting everything else aside,

at least the diners and onlookers all looked delighted.

"Leaders, we support you."

"The Discipline Inspection Commission? What does the Discipline Inspection Commission do?"

"You don't even know? The Discipline Inspection Commission is specifically for arresting corrupt officials. That young man looks young, but I didn't expect him to be a corrupt official too."

"He doesn't deserve to be called a corrupt official. At that age, he's at most accepting bribes while exercising his official power. He deserves it."

"That's right, such a black sheep should be shot."

For a moment, everyone seemed to be genuinely influenced.

A great deal of discussion arose.

Standing at the door, Han Qi suddenly felt as if his whole body was filled with power.

He stood on the side of the people.

No matter what, no matter how difficult the road ahead, he would forge a bright path.

Thinking this way, Han Qi was almost moved by his own words.

"Don't worry, everyone, we will absolutely not let any black sheep go unpunished. But we also hope that everyone will not spread rumors outside, especially false information. This is illegal. Okay, everyone, please continue with your meal." Han Qi waved to everyone.

"Boss, this is what the people want!" Ding Xiaoxiao exclaimed excitedly, her face flushed.

"This is just the beginning, Xiaoxiao. Our task is very challenging," Han Qi said with a pleased expression, showing his satisfaction at her potential.

"Don't worry, Boss, all the staff in our Comprehensive Office will absolutely obey your orders," Ding Xiaoxiao said excitedly.

This couldn't help but stir Han Qi's heart. Although he didn't show it on his face, he was already feeling quite smug.

"Get in the car. Our Discipline Inspection Commission's car is inferior to the Phaeton you, a petty official and a corrupt official, drive. We can only use a Santana. You'll have to make do," Han Qi said sarcastically as he opened the car door.

"A Santana is already pretty good. When I first joined the Municipal Bureau, I couldn't even get a Santana as an official vehicle. At first, I had to ride a motorcycle for official business and patrols," Jiang Tian said, not looking bitter or resentful as he got into the car and immediately closed his eyes to rest.

To be honest.

Since his rebirth, Jiang Tian has never had a proper rest.

He's often gone for days without sleep.

Even these past few days, Jiang Tian has been elusive, rarely visiting the city police station, but in reality, he's been investigating Shimiao Village.

This kind of crime, with its terrifying scale, requires absolute certainty.

Therefore, Jiang Tian dared not be careless or relax in the slightest. He was on high alert, waiting to wipe out the entire Shimiao Village.

It wasn't just the Shimiao Village issue; there was also the problem of Fang Tai and his two heinous scoundrels.

Jiang Tian investigated casually and discovered their monstrous crimes. Crucially, after committing their crimes, they used money and influence to coerce the women's families through various means.

Furthermore, these two scoundrels had even forced women into prostitution.

Most terrifyingly, Jiang Tian conducted further investigations into Fang Tai. The "Old Nine" was a member of organized crime, and Fang Tai's family background was surprising.

Fang Tai's father, Fang Jue, was actually a leader in the Discipline Inspection Commission—the very person who had previously questioned Jiang Tian.

Now that the Discipline Inspection Commission was arresting him, Jiang Tian couldn't help but wonder if Fang Jue was behind it.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's eyes grew increasingly cold.



Chapter 410 The big boss is frantic, but you've already gotten into a private room.


But for Jiang Tian, ​​thinking about it was pointless now; it was time to enjoy himself.

Or rather, to rest.

After getting into the car, Jiang Tian closed his eyes and remained silent.

Just then, his phone rang sharply in his pocket.

Jiang Tian, ​​handcuffed, struggled to retrieve his phone.

"Hand over your phone. From this moment on, you are not allowed any contact with the outside world."

Ding Xiaoxiao, sitting next to Jiang Tian, ​​quickly stopped him upon seeing his movement and snatched his phone away, then swiftly turned it off.

"Okay," Jiang Tian said without further explanation.

Meanwhile, as Jiang Tian was being taken to the VIP room for investigation by the Discipline Inspection Commission, outside the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau, Minister Tan stared into deep thought as his phone was disconnected.

He dialed again.

"Sorry, the number you dialed is currently switched off. Please try again later."

Hearing the busy tone on the phone, Minister Tan's eyelids twitched, and he said irritably, "That brat, not only did he hang up on me, he even turned off his phone."

"Officer Jiang is probably in a tactical meeting," Shen Han said with a smile, sitting beside him.

After learning that Minister Tan had arrived in Yunhai, Shen Han came to meet and accompany him immediately.

Although the provincial department is also very busy right now, assisting in the arrest of drug-related individuals in many cases at the municipal bureau.

Of course, on the other hand, and most importantly, the fact that people in Beijing only know about the municipal bureau and not the provincial department is a very serious problem.

The provincial department itself is holding its breath, especially the anti-narcotics team, which, under the leadership of Director Bai, is currently conducting a surprise investigation into a major case.

They've been monitoring it for a long time. What, you ask why so long?

"What a joke! The bigger the case, the more crucial it is. To ensure absolute success, you need to thoroughly investigate the supply and demand lines without alerting the suspects. Often, this even requires sending in undercover agents—that's the normal investigative procedure.

Who's as monstrous as Jiang Tian?

His moves are always decisive.

Good heavens! A case that others might have been monitoring for months or even a year, Jiang Tian solved in three days, wiping out the entire network from top to bottom.

Even Shen Han has been eyeing Jiang Tian for a long time.

If it weren't for the fear of making things look bad, and the presence of Gao Xiaolong—and most importantly, Gao Xiaolong—Shen Han would have already transferred Jiang Tian to the provincial department.

"Operational meeting?"

Minister Tan was somewhat surprised, then said, "That's right. With so many criminals arrested recently, we do need to do some summaries and finalize things."

Minister Tan's eyelids twitched, and he opened his mouth as if to say something.

"Just say what you have to say. Don't beat around the bush." ​​Minister Tan's brows furrowed deeply.

"Minister Tan, this isn't a summary, it's because..."

Shen Han then recounted Jiang Tian's discovery.

As she spoke, Shen Han couldn't help but feel a chill run down her spine.

It's only been a few days since the Wenye Group case, three days already?

That case hadn't even been closed for three days. In fact, just three days ago, the armed police were attacked because of the Wenye Group case, and they were currently working tirelessly to solve it.

And now, Jiang Tian had actually found another terrorist drug trafficking organization whose scale of influence, the scale of its crimes, and its devastating impact were no less than those of the Wenye Group.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian even infiltrated the area and took photos.

He also made a precise assessment of the approximate number of perpetrators.

Wow.

This is absolutely incredible.

Minister Tan even thought he was hearing things.

Was he hallucinating?

Minister Tan's eyes glazed over for a moment, then he looked blankly at the trays on the vehicle. Four police cars escorted the vehicle, with eight traffic police vehicles surrounding it.

In the middle was a large official bus.

Because the space was large, three trays could fit in front of Minister Tan.

And they were very solemn.

That's right.

Inside were Jiang Tian's two First-Class Merits, as well as a First-Class Hero Medal and certificate.

In addition, there were some collective merit awards, two Second-Class Merits, and more than ten Third-Class Merits.

Minister Tan brought them all, coming in person to show the headquarters' attention and commendation.

The recent actions of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau have been outstanding and brilliant.

Minister Tan personally came to present the award, and even used the fast track to deliver it ahead of schedule, all to encourage the bureau and inspire everyone to keep up the good work.

Unexpectedly, he's only on his way here.

Are you about to cause another big incident?

"This kid, so well-covered up?" Minister Tan was exasperated.

He had no idea.

However, after a moment's thought, Minister Tan seemed to understand.

"What are the plans for Yun Province?" Minister Tan inquired.

"We are mobilizing military and police forces to coordinate the operation, preparing to launch it at 8 PM," Shen Han quickly replied without hiding anything.

"Hmm." Minister Tan thought for a moment but didn't say anything.

Soon, the car arrived at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

They had only just approached the Municipal Public Security Bureau when Minister Tan saw it from afar. The huge compound was packed with people, and there were even military and police vehicles parked outside the bureau.

"Because we were afraid of alerting the enemy, we only hastily gathered five hundred people here, and the rest will assemble at night."

Shen Han continued to explain.

These five hundred people are for handling emergencies.

"Hmm, you've done a good job," Minister Tan nodded slightly.

At this moment, Minister Tan was extremely eager to see Jiang Tian.

After getting out of the car, he rushed inside.

Meanwhile, inside the Municipal Public Security Bureau:

"What, still haven't gotten through on the phone?"

Song Liguo was sweating profusely with anxiety.

"That kid probably went to investigate secretly," Jiang Peng thought, now possessing a strange confidence in his son.

After all, what if the phone rang while he was hiding? That would be incredibly dangerous!

"That's right, Xiao Jiang always has his own ideas," Song Liguo said, relieved.

Actually, at this very moment,

a large number of leaders had already gathered at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Military leaders and armed police leaders.

If Shen Han hadn't gone to greet Minister Tan, she would be waiting here right now.

After all, such a big case deserves a large presence and a grand setup.

And it wasn't until Minister Tan arrived that

"That kid, he kept it from me! I only just found out," Gao Xiaolong grumbled.

The more he thought about it, the angrier Gao Xiaolong became.

“That brat has changed. Before, whenever he encountered a big case, he'd come to me. Now he's become slippery. The Wenye Group case is one thing, but this case is the same old story.” Gao Xiaolong was furious.

Gao Xiaolong was shocked even after learning about this major case.

“Hahaha, Lao Gao, calm down. If I had told you about this case, I would have immediately sent the Beijing Armed Police to take over. Even if the local leaders don't say anything outwardly, they still have grievances against Xiao Jiang. Anyway, as long as the case is handled well, that's what matters,” Minister Tan comforted him.

The comforting continued until after 6 PM.

Jiang Tian's phone was still switched off.

The city bureau leaders all looked bewildered.

At 7 PM, two thousand military and police personnel were waiting at the city bureau.

Provincial Governor Huai arrived personally and immediately bombarded him with three questions: “Where is Xiao Jiang? Where is the target location? What is the detailed operation plan?”



Chapter 411 Under the Spotlight, the Protagonist Gone?


At the Municipal Public Security Bureau, a large number of leaders had gathered, and it was past 6 PM. Not only were there leaders from the Municipal Bureau, but also leaders from the Municipal Bureau headed by Zhou Zhao, and provincial leaders headed by Huai Sheng.

The entire conference room was nearly full of leaders.

Everyone was waiting.

They sat there for a very long time.

Until Minister Tan dialed Jiang Tian's number five times, but still received no news.

Minister Tan's brows furrowed deeply.

Not only Minister Tan, but also many other leaders.

At this moment, there was complete silence.

Why? Because everyone was waiting for Jiang Tian.

"What exactly happened to Xiao Jiang? Why haven't we heard anything from him yet? Did your Municipal Bureau assign Xiao Jiang a mission? Isn't this ridiculous?" Minister Tan's brows were deeply furrowed.

To say that something had happened to Jiang Tian was utter nonsense.

Although Minister Tan hadn't personally witnessed Jiang Tian in action, he was familiar with the details of Jiang Tian's past battles.

He even mentioned that TNT had been used in the past.

Following this, there was constant retaliation against Jiang Tian's family.

For example, were the attacks on Jiang Tian's family members over? No. In fact, they continued to target Jiang Tian's relatives and friends. The arrogance of these criminals was self-evident.

There was no need to worry about Jiang Tian.

Given Jiang Tian's past skills, even if those people wanted to attack him, it would have been impossible for them to do so silently.

At first, everything was fine, but then Jiang Tian remained completely silent, which immediately filled Minister Tan with a sense of foreboding.

"Minister Tan, we absolutely did not assign Jiang any missions," Jiang Peng hurriedly explained, cold sweat beading on his forehead.

Thank God.

They had only told Jiang Tian to go out for lunch, or even rest, to wait for the evening's operation.

According to the plan, the operation was supposed to begin at 8 PM.

But now, it was already past 7 PM, and over 500 military and police personnel had gathered at the entrance of the Municipal Public Security Bureau, with another 1,000 waiting to be deployed on the outer perimeter.

In other words, everything was ready, awaiting Jiang Tian's move.

However, Jiang Tian had remained completely silent. Not only Minister Tan, but even Jiang Peng felt a sense of unease.

After all, Jiang Tian had never acted in this manner before.

Moreover, even if they had gathered intelligence beforehand, Jiang Tian shouldn't have been completely without any information.

Thinking about this, Jiang Peng became even more worried.

This worry persisted until 8 PM, when everyone became restless.

At this moment, the municipal bureau's conference room was filled with a dazzling array of leaders.

That's why.

Although Minister Tan was involved,

ultimately, this matter, this case, was ultimately handled locally, that is, by Yunnan Province itself.

Minister Tan was present,

but ultimately, it was said that Minister Tan was not needed.

Even with their involvement in the operation, there's always a hierarchy. Yunnan Province has already launched its own operation. Minister Tan's role here is at most to supervise the process and analyze and plan the details of the mission.

The main focus remains on the local authorities.

"It's eight o'clock, why is there still no news?"

When eight o'clock arrived.

At the Municipal Bureau, all the leaders stared wide-eyed.

They were utterly bewildered. Even with the operation underway, at this moment, they had virtually no objective.

Because from beginning to end, they had almost no understanding or analysis of the case.

All the information they had came from Jiang Tian.

Even for the sake of secrecy,

they had no intelligence on the specific details or location; they were completely in the dark.

In this situation, everyone was somewhat bewildered.

Truly bewildered.

Everyone looked at each other.

But no matter how they looked...

Everyone could see the bewilderment in their eyes.

"So, you still can't contact Xiao Jiang?" Zhou Zhao asked in astonishment.
