"Can't contact him, completely can't contact him." At this moment, Jiang Peng listened to the busy tone on the phone, his eyes filled with confusion.

He had lost count of how many calls this was.

As for Elder Mu, the leader of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission, he was also completely bewildered.

"So, without Jiang Tian appearing and without any news, we're completely in the dark. We have no idea about the specific intelligence or mission details," Elder Mu said, tapping the table.

"That's quite accurate. To ensure absolute success, such a large criminal organization, even a drug cartel, can't be too cautious. To avoid alerting them, even we are completely unaware of the mission's details. Right now, besides Xiao Jiang, no one knows the specific situation," Jiang Peng quickly explained.

That's right.

It was all for secrecy.

Similarly, even within the city and province,

the more people know, the greater the risk.

Simply revealing the scale of the mission, its risks, the case details, and its severity is sufficient.

Jiang Tian didn't disclose the problems in Shimiao Village beforehand to prevent any unforeseen circumstances.

Who can guarantee that the internal workings are completely unified?

For example, Jiang Tian is currently staying in a private room, with only Han Qi behind him? That's impossible.

With Jiang Tian's intelligence and wit, he must know exactly what happened.

"The most crucial thing is, where is Xiao Jiang now? And if there haven't been any unforeseen circumstances, how come there's still no news by the time the mission is scheduled to begin?" Old Mu made his final judgment with absolute certainty.

Stunned.

At this moment, everyone was stunned.

Completely bewildered.

Because until now, Jiang Tian seemed to have vanished into thin air.

But the crucial issue is, without Jiang Tian, ​​they didn't even know the mission's objective.

Provincial leaders, primarily from Huai Province, and city leaders were all seated in their offices.

But without exception, everyone looked utterly bewildered.

Two thousand military and police personnel had been mobilized in a grand display.

All the leaders were in place.

"Could it be that Xiao Jiang is playing a trick on us?"

At this moment, Fang Jue suddenly stepped forward and spoke.

As the second-in-command of Yunhai, the position of the Discipline Inspection Commission was actually quite unique.

Unlike the third-ranking figure.

The third-ranking figure was the Political and Legal Affairs Commission.

The first was the Party Secretary, the second was the leader of the Political Affairs Commission, and the third was the leader of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission.



Chapter 412 Utter Panic, Comrade Xiao Jiang Actually...


Fang Jue's position in the Discipline Inspection Commission, while wielding considerable power, was also quite limited.

It largely depended on the top leader.

If you gained the top leader's approval and were to be promoted, then undoubtedly, you would be second in rank.

However, if the top leader didn't approve, or even if neither the top two leaders considered you a confidant, the Discipline Inspection Commission position would likely rank in the middle or even the bottom.

Without the support and backing of higher-ups, it was practically useless.

But with support, the power could be limitless.

Fang Jue was in this situation.

Fang Jue had always maintained a good relationship with Zhou Zhao, hoping to gain his trust.

If he received Zhou Zhao's approval, or if Zhou Zhao decided to investigate a leader to maintain his influence, then the Discipline Inspection Commission would undoubtedly be second in power, even surpassing the top official in the government.

Fang Jue simply hadn't had the opportunity before, but now he felt it had arrived.

"So many people, so many leaders. Putting everything else aside, the fact that Xiao Jiang didn't arrive on time makes me seriously doubt whether this case is even that serious. Perhaps it was a false report, and now that things have blown up so much, he's too scared to come," Fang Jue said, seemingly half-jokingly.

At this moment, the already tense atmosphere in the conference room grew even heavier.

"Comrade Fang, Comrade Xiao Jiang has handled so many cases all this time, has he ever been a slacker?" Old Mu narrowed his eyes.

"But it's already this late, and there's still no news. I seriously doubt whether this case is even that big," Fang Jue said with a cold smile.

A sharp confrontation.

He wouldn't lose his temper, but Fang Jue understood that the fact that so many leaders were waiting at this time presented a golden opportunity to launch an attack.

That's why, after Fang Tai was arrested, Fang Jue already harbored hostility and resentment towards Jiang Tian, ​​and now Fang Tai had virtually no chance.

Various pieces of evidence appeared like snowflakes; you wouldn't know until you investigated, and once you did, it was shocking.

The more they investigated Fang Tai's heinous crimes, the more unbelievable the cases became, filled with shocking details.

Jiang Tian even found a camera in Fang Tai's residence, containing various photographs.

It was too late for anyone else to help with this case.

Fang Jue felt utterly devastated.

The crucial point was that he was powerless to do anything.

Under normal circumstances, by pulling strings, using connections, and spending money, he could at most spend a short time in jail.

Two or three years at most.

Right now, it could be said that this was the most stringent and severe phase of the crackdown.

All crimes, regardless of who committed them, were almost all dealt with to the fullest extent of the law.

Those who could be spared the death penalty were all given the death penalty.

Even those with minor offenses were dealt with to the fullest extent of the law to ensure absolute justice.

In this truly severe phase of crime crackdown,

even Fang Jue could only watch helplessly, unable to take any action.

After holding it in for so long, Fang Tai finally couldn't hold back any longer.

Moreover, he was personally arrested by Jiang Tian, ​​and most importantly, he wasn't even given any prior notice.

This shows he wasn't taken seriously at all.

With witnesses and physical evidence all there,

a reversal is as difficult as ascending to heaven.

It's practically impossible.

In other words, Fang Tai was practically finished.

Under these circumstances, it would have been fine if Jiang Tian had obediently shown up.

The key issue was that with so many leaders waiting for him alone, Fang Jue instantly realized his opportunity had arrived and launched a direct attack on Jiang Tian.

However, what puzzled Fang Jue was, where exactly was Jiang Tian?

"Under normal circumstances, it would be difficult to touch you, but now, aren't you just courting death?" Fang Jue thought with a cold smile.

As Fang Jue set the pace,

a number of leaders appeared at the scene, their brows furrowing slightly, seemingly disapproving of Jiang Tian.

No matter what happened, the agreed-upon time had been reached.

You haven't made a sound. Even if you're on a mission, you should at least explain the situation clearly.

It was one thing during the meeting, but with layers of meetings, it's easy to leak information.

Everyone was extremely cautious.

They were all waiting for Jiang Tian to fully disclose the details of the case before taking action.

But who knew Jiang Tian would simply disappear?

"That's right, Xiao Jiang has gone too far."

"Yes, even if Xiao Jiang is investigating, at this point, there should at least be some news coming out. What is this? We've painstakingly mobilized so many people, and now there's not a single piece of information. This is outrageous."

"Are we all supposed to just wait here? This is ridiculous! No matter what happens, Jiang Tian can't do this."

Fang Jue also spoke up, "Everyone, you can't say that. What if Xiao Jiang has an emergency and can't attend to this? After all, he has made so many contributions."

While Fang Jue seemed to be speaking from a neutral standpoint, this very act put Jiang Tian in a difficult position.

In front of so many people, this one sentence practically solidified the first impression that Jiang Tian was arrogant, even utterly unreasonable.

Sure enough, many leaders present showed anger on their faces.

Minister Tan even checked the situation from time to time.

However, even starting a strategic meeting was now impossible.

Of course, without detailed information about Jiang Tian, ​​they didn't even know who the mission target was.

The mission's objective and deployment were completely baffling to everyone.

Even the smartest person couldn't figure out who the target was or what they did.

How could they not be confused?

By 9:00 PM, they couldn't sit still any longer.

All the leaders were practically on the verge of losing their composure.

He was in a panic.

Completely panicked.

Even if Old Mu wanted to say something in his defense, after looking around, he ultimately kept quiet.

The impact was too terrifying.

There were too many people and leaders present.

But just then.

Under everyone's watchful eyes.

Old Mu's phone suddenly rang.

Normally, leaders like Old Mu usually carry several cell phones.

Especially since most calls are made through their secretaries.

As for his private phone, it was a personal number he carried with him, known to almost no one except a few very close relatives.

Upon checking the caller ID and seeing it was Mu Ya, Old Master Mu hung up without answering.

However, almost two minutes later, the phone rang again.

"Could it be something urgent?" Old Master Mu wondered.

But at that moment, he answered the phone.

"Grandpa, Jiang Tian is in trouble..."



Chapter 413: The Full Exposed, A Terrifying Earthquake Erupts


All afternoon, Mu Ya spent her time pondering what had happened.

Should she contact Old Master Mu?

However, according to Jiang Tian's instructions, he had clearly told Mu Ya not to do anything, and had even given her hints. Jiang Tian's requirement was that Mu Ya completely pretend she hadn't seen anything and not tell anyone.

But by evening, Mu Ya had tried calling Jiang Tian several times, but no one answered.

She considered calling Jiang Peng to tell him.

However, after thinking about it, Mu Ya was worried. Was Jiang Tian plotting something? Would something uncontrollable happen if she notified him?

So, Mu Ya hesitated for a long time.

Finally, Mu Ya lingered at the entrance of the Municipal Public Security Bureau, hesitating whether to go in. When she arrived, Mu Ya was completely dumbfounded.

Because at that moment, a large number of armed police, special police, and even military police had gathered at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

A cursory glance revealed that the compound was full of people, and the lights of the police cars were dazzling.

Of course, such a high-profile operation wasn't necessarily out of fear of alerting the target.

Firstly, even if a criminal group found out, who would know who was being targeted or who the operation was about to take? After all, there were countless criminal groups in Yunhai.

Most importantly, as long as the target's identity wasn't revealed, there wouldn't be any problems. Therefore, even the mobile phones of all personnel involved in the operation were confiscated; no communication devices were provided.

This was to prevent any unforeseen leaks.

Of course, having so many military and police personnel wasn't the main issue; the crucial point was that Zhao Wei, who had just returned from a mission, happened to run into Mu Ya.

"Sister-in-law," Zhao Wei quickly got out of the car to greet her.

Actually, Zhao Wei had gone out to contact the victims' families. He had compiled statistics on many families and details of the Fang Tai case.

Other matters aside, the key issue was that Jiang Tian was involved in the execution of several people in this case, so the investigation needed to be thorough to prevent any unforeseen circumstances.

Fortunately, the case was now basically settled.

Fang Tai's father, Fang Jue, had also come to the Municipal Public Security Bureau and even reviewed the case files. He left without saying a word in front of Zhao Wei and his team, his face dark with anger.

But Zhao Wei knew this wouldn't end there.

"What the hell? His son committed such a heinous crime! He's utterly depraved, and he still has the nerve to act all high and mighty!" Zhao Wei muttered to himself.

But then he felt a surge of schadenfreude. With Fang Tai as the main culprit in such a serious case, how could his father, Fang Jue, be unaffected? Impossible.

He deserved it.

The case progressed well today, and with Comrade Jiang Tian's operation tonight, they rushed back, arriving around 8 PM.

"Sister-in-law, it's so late. Are you looking for the boss? It seems he has a mission today," Zhao Wei quickly greeted her.

"No, something happened to your boss," Mu Ya quickly explained, relieved to see Zhao Wei.

"Something happened?"

Zhao Wei's expression froze, and before he could react, Li Zijin jumped out of the car from the passenger seat.

"A group of people from the Discipline Inspection Commission came this afternoon," Mu Ya explained to the group.

After all, these people were Jiang Tian's team members. If Jiang Tian had any hidden agendas he wasn't allowing them to discuss, these team members should know some details.

Sure enough.

"Damn it, those people are crazy!" Zhao Wei exclaimed in disbelief.

"What do we do now?" Although anxious, Mu Ya maintained a basic level of composure.

"Let's go find the director," Li Zijin said hastily.

"No, sister-in-law, you should call your grandfather first." Zhao Wei also sensed something was amiss.

However, Zhao Wei had followed Jiang Tian for a long time, and after a brief mental review, he felt he understood the general situation.

He also guessed a little of Jiang Tian's intentions.

But Zhao Wei was also worried about the mission going wrong, so he waited for over an hour, until after nine o'clock, when the leaders at all levels of the Municipal Bureau were completely restless, before having Mu Ya call Old Master Mu.

Sure enough.

At this moment, the huge conference room was almost overflowing with people.

The enormous conference room, large enough to accommodate several hundred people, had the highest-ranking leaders seated at the front, followed by leaders of various ranks.

This was mainly because, aside from others speaking, this case was reported by Jiang Tian. The number of perpetrators was conservatively estimated at seven hundred, with the number of those involved impossible to count, and potentially even double.

Such a massive case was enough to cause social unrest and economic chaos; its impact was far too great and extremely damaging.

Of course, those present were mostly leaders implicated in the case.

For example, there were provincial leaders, specifically those from Huai Province, as well as Shen Han and Elder Mu, leaders in charge of law enforcement and policing.

And key leaders from Huai Province, while many more from the municipal bureau were present.

After all, such a major event had occurred in Yunhai.

In addition, military leaders were also present.

After Fang Jue finished speaking, a slight silence fell over the room.

As time ticked by...

The doubts grew louder. Even those who trusted Jiang Tian couldn't help but feel cheated as time went on.

Even if you're a high-ranking leader, making people wait this long is excessive.

Moreover, how can a mere policeman like Jiang Tian act so arrogantly?

Right under everyone's watchful eyes, Old Mu's rarely-rang private phone rang, instantly drawing everyone's attention.

Old Mu hung up the first time expressionlessly, finally answering the second time it rang.

Because Old Mu was in a prominent position, most people looked over.

"Leaving aside whether the case is real or not, whether the details were fabricated, just the fact that he didn't show up at the confirmed operation time, and didn't even arrive at the agreed-upon meeting to discuss the operation plan, what is he doing? Disorganized and undisciplined!" Fang Jue rebuked, feeling incredibly satisfied!

What goes around comes around.

His own son was in jail, his chances of survival slim, and now he's been given an opportunity to attack him.

At this moment, Fang Jue stepped forward and angrily rebuked them, because after this incident, Fang Jue didn't know if he would ever have another chance.

But the next moment...

A shocked voice echoed throughout the conference room, even drowning out Fang Jue's own.

"What did you say? Say it again! Who's in trouble? How did it happen?" Old Master Mu shouted.

Silence fell. Even Fang Jue unconsciously stopped speaking.

Then, in the next instant,

Old Master Mu's gaze suddenly fixed on Fang Jue.

Killing intent.

Fang Jue sensed the killing intent.

Bang!!!

The next moment, a loud crash resounded.

Old Master Mu slammed his fist on the table, his face contorted with rage as he glared at Fang Jue.



Chapter 414 The Big Boss's Shocking Fury, You Fangjue, You Deserve to Die


"Very well, very well, utterly audacious! Absolutely audacious!" Old Mu roared.

His voice was loud and clear.

However, many people were astonished.

Especially since, unlike Huai Province, Old Mu was a long-time leader in Yun Province, having served there for a considerable period.

While he hadn't achieved any major feats, Old Mu had undoubtedly made outstanding contributions to Yunhai's development and its steadily rising economy.

And played a major role.

Although he was only nominally the third-in-command, in terms of overall influence, even Huai Province lacked Old Mu's.

The true leader, however, was currently in a vacant position.

Of course, Old Mu had always given the impression of being calm and collected, aloof and unperturbed. This outburst of anger, even to the point of losing his composure, instantly drew everyone's attention.

"Old Mu, calm down. What's got you so angry? Especially since you've just recovered, don't let this upset you further." Gao Xiaolong, sitting next to him, was startled and quickly tried to comfort him.

His temper was already quite fiery, but Gao Xiaolong couldn't remember the last time he'd seen his old friend so angry.

"Calm down, hehe, how can I calm down, comrades..."

Bang! Bang! Bang!

Old Mu put down his phone, slammed his hand on the table, and grabbed the small microphone. His voice, though loud in the empty conference room, was especially clear.

The sound of him slamming the table made many people's eyelids twitch.

"Comrades, I never dreamed that something so big would happen today. Weren't you all just curious about Comrade Jiang, wondering why the key figure in this case, Comrade Jiang, hadn't shown up yet?"

"Just now I finally found out, but because I found out, I realized that there are bad people within our group."

Old Mu's voice grew increasingly angry and furious.

Actually, although Elder Mu hadn't said anything just now, he knew Jiang Tian was a man of principle.

Playing mysterious and disappearing at this time was utterly absurd.

He wasn't flustered because he knew that Jiang Tian wouldn't fail at this crucial moment unless something was absolutely impossible.

Now it seemed that was indeed the case.

Fang Jue kept attacking, while Elder Mu remained mostly silent.

He was also observing the situation.

Now Elder Mu understood.

As soon as Elder Mu's voice rang out, the scene erupted in an uproar.

The noise continued.

Fang Jue, under Elder Mu's intense gaze, couldn't stop his eyelids twitching.

"Secretary Mu, what's going on?"

Minister Tan, who had been silent until now, suddenly spoke up.

You see, he had come with honors, intending to personally present Jiang Tian with an award.

And so much had happened since he arrived, such a major event; even Minister Tan's brows furrowed deeply.

Boom!!!

After Minister Tan spoke, Old Master Mu, completely enraged, slammed his fist on the table, only to face Fang Jue: "Fang Jue, what have you done and you still won't admit it?"

Whoa!!!

As Old Master Mu's words rang out,

it exploded.

The conference room completely erupted, and Fang Jue, his face filled with panic, stammered, "Secretary Mu, what didn't I do!"

Fang Jue's scalp tingled, his breathing quickened, and an ominous premonition grew stronger.

"Nothing happened, Fang Jue. You've been relentlessly attacking Comrade Jiang. If Comrade Jiang had truly made a mistake, that would be one thing; if you were truly innocent, that would be understandable. But how do you explain the arrest of Comrade Jiang by your Yunhai Discipline Inspection Commission? Well, you, Fang Jue, do you really think you can act with impunity? Arresting people while simultaneously framing others, and you're still here attacking him!" Old Mu roared angrily.

He was genuinely angry.

If it were another system that made the arrest, that would be one thing.

The key point is...

Your Discipline Inspection Commission, Fang Jue's, arrested Jiang Tian.

Turning his head...

You're still here, relentlessly attacking Jiang Tian.

This goes beyond mere "excessive."

Jiang Tian had indeed made a mistake; say whatever you want, no one can say anything.

However, Fang Jue's unforgiving attitude had unknowingly incurred the displeasure of some leaders, especially those who favored Jiang Tian.

They were already dissatisfied.

But now...

It exploded.

A cacophony of voices erupted; almost instantly, half the people, stunned, jumped to their feet.

They stared at Fang Jue as if in a dream.

They hadn't expected Fang Jue to be so ruthless. While Jiang Tian was investigating the case, he had his men arrest Jiang Tian, ​​and then, when the leaders couldn't even wait for Jiang Tian, ​​he launched a frenzied attack.

Fang Jue's behavior just now had been far too obvious.

"He's insane."

"This is too ruthless. What grudge could he have?"

"I know. A few days ago, Fang Jue's son tried to rape someone. Comrade Jiang organized the killing of several of his accomplices, then investigated his son, and it turned out he had raped many people, even causing a death."

"Wow, such a thing happened."

"He's insane."

"Even so, he shouldn't do something like this at this time. Isn't he courting death?"

There weren't any secrets within the system itself, but Jiang Tian's secret profession was practically common knowledge.

After Jiang Tian arrested Fang Tai and investigated the arrest, Fang Tai's background was no secret in the police department.

The news had spread, but those who knew were still mostly within the political and legal system.

Now, through their mouths...

The scene exploded instantly.

They were stunned.

Even figures like Provincial Governor Huai and Minister Tan stared wide-eyed at Fang Jue, their faces filled with astonishment.

"I didn't, I didn't," Fang Jue hurriedly and frantically shook his head.

His head felt like it was about to explode, his scalp even felt like it was about to split open.

He hated Jiang Tian, ​​but he truly knew nothing about this matter; he was genuinely innocent!

But crucially, his accusations just now only confirmed the fact that Jiang Tian was retaliating.

Old Mu sneered and rebuked, "Fang Jue, do you think I don't know? I already knew about Comrade Jiang's investigation into your son's problems. As a leader, you've allowed your son to commit so many heinous crimes, and today you still dare to be so audacious! What do you take Yunhai for? What do you take the organization for?"

A furious shout.

*Thud!*

The pen in Fang Jue's hand fell to the table, his hands trembling.

His face was pale.

"Secretary Mu, this is impossible! Jiang Tian could never be in our Discipline Inspection Commission!" Fang Jue would never admit it.

Because he truly hadn't done it.

"Then let me tell you, Jiang Tian is right now in your Discipline Inspection Commission's private room, and he's cut off all contact with the outside world. As the leader of the Yunhai Discipline Inspection Commission, who would dare to do something this big without your approval? Delaying the mission, causing a terrible impact, resulting in serious consequences, deliberately retaliating against a heroic police officer—Fang Jue, what else wouldn't you dare to do?" Old Mu roared, questioning him.

Boom!!!

It exploded.

After those words were spoken,

it completely exploded.

The entire scene felt like it had been struck by lightning.

Just now, people could still criticize, but now, everyone was stunned speechless.

Bewilderment.

Because this was a bit too ruthless.

Too crazy.

At the same time, some of the leaders around Fang Jue subconsciously moved further away, as if afraid that getting even a little closer would implicate them in something.

The next moment, a muffled, thunderous voice rang out:

"You, Fang Jue, deserve to die!!!"



Chapter 415 The sky has fallen, who wants to make me die without a burial place?


Actually, whether Fang Jue is innocent or not is irrelevant, because even if he truly is innocent and unaware, it doesn't prevent the attack.

You're in charge of the Discipline Inspection Commission in Yunhai, and yet you've done this. This proves you have problems; your supervision was lax and ineffective. You bear the primary responsibility.

The key is, if you did this behind closed doors, that would be one thing, and if there was a misunderstanding, you could have just talked it out. There's no need to make such a big fuss.

But at this moment, who can cover it up?

Minister Tan from Beijing is personally attending the meeting here. From top to bottom, every leader in Yun Province, every single one of them, looks at Fang Jue with murderous intent.

This is a disgrace brought to Beijing.

From top to bottom, every leader in Yun Province is utterly humiliated.

Especially Huai Sheng's expression; at this moment, he looks like he's about to devour someone.

Boom!!!

A loud crash echoed as Huai Sheng stood up abruptly, angrily rebuking Fang Jue, "Fang Jue, what happened? I order you to explain yourself immediately! At this time, I don't care about your personal grudges, but is this how you use the power granted to you by the state and the organization?"

Huai Sheng's gaze was completely grim.

Not only had today's events made the entire Yunnan province a laughingstock.

Besides, what was Minister Tan supposed to be here for? To present awards! Now he's nowhere to be found—isn't this ridiculous?

Not only is he nowhere to be found, but they've also arrested him themselves.

And this happened despite the mobilization of so many people, with most of Yunnan's leadership gathered here.

This is a laughingstock, an absolute joke.

The most terrifying thing is, is only Yunnan province concerned about this?

Is only headquarters concerned?

Don't be ridiculous.

Minister Tan's personal visit was certainly partly for awarding medals, but on the other hand, did anyone really think his presence was merely a coincidence, that he was unaware of some underlying issue?

While this was indeed a combat mission within Yunnan Province itself,

the truth is, even Jiang Tian himself had to acknowledge his other identity.

Personal interests can never outweigh national interests.

Especially given Jiang Tian's current role in that department, such a significant mission would undoubtedly require communication with security department leaders.

And the higher-ups understood Jiang Tian's situation.

On the surface, there seemed to be no reaction from Beijing.

In reality, this mission had reached the highest levels of government.

Minister Tan's visit was also a message: "I'll observe your work and not interfere.

But if the results are unsatisfactory, then I'm sorry to say..."

In a sense, Minister Tan represented headquarters.

"Fang Jue, who gave you the audacity to do such a thing? Even if Comrade Jiang Tian has problems, our police system has its own internal oversight. Especially on the eve of such an important mission, you dared to arrest Comrade Jiang Tian. Do you have any organization or discipline? Now, immediately, I order you to release Jiang Tian at once."

Private room.

Jiang Tian was taken to a private room.

Isn't this a joke?

Even if Jiang Tian committed a heinous crime, do you have the right to arrest him?

Do you know how terrifying Jiang Tian's strategic position is right now?

Is it something you can even touch?

You, a leader of the Yunhai Discipline Inspection Commission.

You have such power.

As expected.

At this moment, Fang Jue looked at the angry glares of the crowd, and the sky collapsed.

The sky completely collapsed.

Regardless of whether Jiang Tian is finished from now on.

Whether there is real evidence of his crimes.

But taking action against Jiang Tian at this time is like setting off a huge landmine.

And if Jiang Tian is innocent, then it's even worse.

Jiang Tian's problems are confirmed. It only means that there are problems on the Yunhai side, and Fang Jue, with his undisciplined and unorganized personal revenge, has caused significant losses to the organization. His final fate will definitely not be good.

Jiang Tian's innocence proves that Fang Jue was deliberately retaliating, an extremely malicious act with unimaginable consequences.

Fang Jue wiped his forehead.

His face was covered in sweat. Fortunately, Fang Jue had been through a lot, and he managed to regain some composure, unlike his son Fang Tai who wet himself in fear.

However, even with his strong mental state, Fang Jue couldn't stand up in his current state.

He couldn't even stand upright with his hands on the table.

Extreme fear and shock can truly paralyze a person.

In fact, Fang Jue could only lean on the chair, his hands trembling as he tried to take out his phone several times.

After three attempts, he finally managed to pull the phone out of his pocket.

Fang Jue held the phone shakily.

"Hurry up!"

Huai Sheng slammed his fist on the table and roared.

*Bang!!!*

With a furious shout, Fang Jue dropped his phone onto the table, his hands trembling uncontrollably.

Finally.

Thanks to his exceptional mental fortitude, Fang Jue managed to steady the phone and dial the deputy leader of the Discipline Inspection Commission.

At this moment.

Although Fang Jue was extremely frightened, his mind wasn't completely broken.

At this crucial juncture, at this critical point, such a major incident had occurred.

Without a doubt.

Someone was definitely trying to harm him.

Who was it?

Thinking this, Fang Jue's fear subsided instantly, as anger burned within him.

Which beast dared to want him to die a horrible death?

Yes, a horrible death.

Leaving aside his own problems—after all, he'd been rooted in Yunhai for so many years, and even his son's issues were unbearable—plus this incident, Fang Jue knew he was likely doomed.

And it would be a very tragic death.

But turning around, Fang Jue couldn't figure out who would want to harm him.

After all,

he had always had a good relationship with the deputy leader in the Discipline Inspection Commission; they had cooperated for so many years, knew each other inside and out, and had no reason to harm him!

In other words, they were in cahoots.

So the question arises: besides Qi Huan, the deputy leader of the Discipline Inspection Commission, who else would want to frame him, even willing to pay such a terrible price? Didn't they consider that even if Jiang Tian were arrested and used to frame him, their own fate wouldn't be much better?

Or is there some terrifying conspiracy behind this?

Just as Fang Jue was thinking this and about to dial Qi Huan's number, his phone rang suddenly.

It was very sudden.

Fang Jue looked at the caller ID, and for a moment, he felt a surge of anger, especially seeing countless pairs of angry eyes staring at him. If looks could kill, Qi Huan believed he would have died countless times and would already be riddled with holes.

"This is Fang Jue." At this moment, because of the anger rising in his heart, Fang Jue's hand didn't even tremble.

Even if he died, Fang Jue wanted to know who wanted him dead.

Who wanted to frame him.

"Secretary, good news, great news!" Qi Huan was overjoyed and full of surprise.

"It's you?" Fang Jue's mind went blank.



Chapter 416 The wicked man's racking brains is no match for the fool's quick wit.


"What good news?" Fang Jue's eyelids twitched, a sense of foreboding rising from the depths of his heart.

"Hahaha, Secretary, you'll never believe it! We've discovered something! We've done a great job this time, hahaha!"

Qi Huan noticed the slight tremor in Fang Jue's voice but didn't think much of it, perhaps stunned by the big surprise he was about to announce.

That's how it is.

The Discipline Inspection Commission is different, unlike other arrest operations. They primarily deal with leaders within the system, and even at the very end, they have to give them some face.

Therefore, even interrogations take place in private rooms, never in a real interrogation room, preserving their final dignity.

Fortunately, that's how it is; otherwise, Jiang Tian probably would have suffered in the interrogation room.

Even in the private room, Jiang Tian's eyes were calm as he looked at the people opposite him, a half-smile playing on his lips.

The deputy leader of the Discipline Inspection Commission—in Jiang Tian's memory, he wasn't particularly familiar with him.

However, this didn't prevent Jiang Tian from knowing the malice this person harbored towards him.

And if someone could possess malice, Jiang Tian understood something in his heart.

“There are many who hate me in this world, and even more who want me dead. One more or one less makes no difference. But if you want to ruin my reputation, then I'm sorry, you're not qualified.” Jiang Tian spoke with a mocking smile.

“Still being stubborn. Even now, what can you do with your stubbornness?” Han Qi seemed to still have a face full of righteous indignation.

But seeing such a righteous person only made Jiang Tian want to laugh more.

It was a real joke.

“Have you ever heard a saying?” Jiang Tian asked.

“What saying?” Han Qi asked, puzzled.

“The cunning of a bad person is no match for the quick thinking of a fool.” Jiang Tian said unhurriedly, “And someone as stupid as you is the first person I've ever seen.”

Bang!!!

Han Qi slammed his hand on the table and stood up: "Jiang Tian, ​​please correct your attitude."

Even now, he's still stubbornly clinging to his ways.

"Why rush him? Let me report to the secretary first," Qi Huan waved his hand.

This Qi Huan was someone Jiang Tian had only met for the first time since his arrest.

To be honest, Jiang Tian had never met him before. After all, it was the Discipline Inspection Commission; unless absolutely necessary, how could Jiang Tian have any involvement with such a department?

Whether in his past life or this one, Jiang Tian was relatively unfamiliar with Qi Huan, but now he knew he was a deputy leader in the Discipline Inspection Commission.

This was somewhat unexpected for Jiang Tian.

Because in Jiang Tian's mind, the mastermind behind this should be Fang Jue, not Qi Huan.

Oh no, perhaps Qi Huan was just the one in the open.

Jiang Tian stroked his chin thoughtfully, completely unperturbed.

Now Jiang Tian understood very well who he was, what he was going to do, and what his importance was.

Jiang Tian genuinely hoped that all these people, and even those behind them, would be exposed so he could take this opportunity to eliminate them.

The saying "to pacify the internal before the external," still held considerable truth.

"So, the person on the other end of the phone was Fang Jue after all," Jiang Tian muttered to himself, stroking his chin.

As expected, Jiang Tian understood when Qi Huan mentioned "Secretary."

While Qi Huan was on the phone, Han Qi remained silent.

He simply sat on the sofa, his face full of righteous indignation, watching Jiang Tian's nonchalant demeanor. Han Qi felt an urge to punch him.

Even at his wit's end, he was still so arrogant.

"Kid, I've arrested so many corrupt officials. No matter who they are, once they're in the VIP room, they can't stand up straight. Regardless, your attitude is commendable. It's a pity you've gone astray, and you've knowingly broken the law, making your crime even more serious. You're facing death but show no remorse." Han Qi shook his head, seemingly genuinely lamenting for Jiang Tian.

In reality...

At this moment, Han Qi truly seemed confident of victory.

Fang Jue was completely wrong. No one had framed him, and no one wanted to get him.

In fact, Qi Huan's face was full of self-satisfaction.

Who is Han Qi?

Others might not know, but Qi Huan certainly did. Most importantly, after the operation, when it came time to arrest Jiang Tian, ​​Han Qi specifically sought Qi Huan's approval.

Qi Huan had initially considered reporting to the Party Secretary.

However, Han Qi thought it was making a mountain out of a molehill.

So he didn't bother him, planning to give Leader Fang Jue a huge surprise.

After all, the authentication was flawless.

That was a major achievement!

Moreover, by investigating Jiang Tian, ​​they might uncover a massive corruption ring—wouldn't that be an incredible accomplishment?

Thus, Qi Huan and Han Qi discussed it and ultimately decided to act without reporting to Fang Jue.

As for the major task from the Municipal Bureau...

What a joke.

Such a major task, such a significant matter, required strict secrecy from top to bottom. Even the provincial leaders didn't know what was happening.

Jiang Tian was needed to reconstruct it.

It was under these circumstances that a beautiful misunderstanding arose between Fang Jue and Qi Huan.

Qi Huan and Han Qi, completely unaware of what had transpired, simply rushed to arrest Jiang Tian and take him to the private room, just to prevent any unforeseen circumstances.

“After our rigorous investigation and detailed examination, Secretary, you have no idea what a pleasant surprise we’ve discovered! Jiang Tian, ​​yes, it’s Jiang Tian! We’ve successfully apprehended Jiang Tian and obtained evidence of his embezzlement and bribery.”

“That’s right, it’s Jiang Tian, ​​the Jiang Tian from the Municipal Bureau. He seems to have quite a few achievements to his name, huh? I never expected him to be such a big-time embezzler.”

“Don’t worry, Secretary, you can rest assured. We even arrested Jiang Tian discreetly beforehand to prevent any unforeseen circumstances. By the way, this is all thanks to our new guy, Han Qi. You should remember him, right? This young man from Beijing is incredibly promising. It was this young man who discovered Jiang Tian’s case.”

Qi Huan is a really good leader.

Such a great achievement, and he still didn't forget to boast about it to Han Qi.

But...

He was completely dumbfounded.

Qi Huan's gleeful report had no idea what Fang Jue on the other end of the phone was feeling.

He was struck dumb.

The sky had fallen.

"Alright, alright, I wasn't wrong about myself."

No.

Wait.

"A young man from Beijing?" Fang Jue's intelligence was now on full display.

"That's right, Secretary. You don't know this young man from Beijing is quite something. Upon arriving in Yunhai, he immediately and accurately located Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep, and successfully gathered a lot of evidence. He even collaborated with the anti-corruption bureau of the procuratorate. Don't worry, we also discovered Jiang Tian's financial dealings with Chief Gao's granddaughter and have already taken control of them. Chief Gao, for his daughter's sake, probably won't stubbornly protect Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep. Thanks to Xiao Han, he's so clever!"

Pfft!!!



Chapter 417 A Little Bit of Willful Power


"You've even taken control of Chief Gao's daughter?"

Shocked.

The sky had fallen.

His mind was reeling.

What kind of person is this?

Fang Jue's head was buzzing; he felt like his brain wasn't working properly.

"Insolence!"

Fang Jue roared the next moment.

Qi Huan, on the other end of the phone, was startled, her whole body jolting. She seemed completely unprepared for her leader's sudden outburst of anger.

But then Qi Huan thought to herself, Gao Yangyang is Chief Gao's daughter, and given Chief Gao's rank and status, he's probably afraid of Chief Gao's wrath.

However, the leader probably didn't anticipate that Chief Gao's upright character is well-known. Knowing that his daughter was involved in this matter, although he might have offended Chief Gao, he would definitely suppress his anger for Gao Yangyang's sake, to save face for himself and his daughter.

Therefore, Chief Gao absolutely had no time or opportunity to consider Jiang Tian.

Thinking this through, Qi Huan smiled: "Secretary, please rest assured, I understand your concerns. But rest assured, we will handle this flawlessly. We have their transfer records and evidence of their crimes. Chief Gao would never target us for this; otherwise, you would be underestimating him."

Pfft!!!

Fang Jue almost exploded with anger.

"As for Jiang Tian, ​​we didn't expect this black sheep to be so stubborn. Even at the last minute, he still refuses to confess. But Secretary, rest assured, you know our methods. No matter how high-ranking an official is, once they're in our hands, even if they're stubborn to the end, it's useless. We already have enough evidence to convict him. Even if Jiang Tian is stubborn, it doesn't matter; we have plenty of ways to deal with him." Qi Huan chuckled coldly.

He believed the Party Secretary would be overjoyed upon hearing his idea, perhaps even incurring a favor from Chief Gao.

However, a long silence followed.

After an unknown amount of time,

Qi Huan, awaiting a reply, heard the Party Secretary's voice on the other end: "Where is Comrade Jiang Tian being held?"

"In our VIP room at the Discipline Inspection Commission. Don't worry, we'll certainly treat him well," Qi Huan replied without hesitation.

"Now, do nothing. Just wait there."

Fang Jue hung up immediately after saying this.

Hearing the dial tone, Qi Huan frowned slightly.

It's true what they say, the cunning of a bad person is no match for the quick thinking of a fool.

Especially when several fools happen to be together.

But to say Qi Huan was stupid wouldn't be possible; otherwise, he wouldn't be in this position.

To be honest, it was Qi Huan who was influenced, because he trusted Han Qi too much.

This misled Han Qi into thinking that following Han Qi was the right direction and the right team. Jiang Tian, ​​who was being targeted by Han Qi, had no hope left.

After all, going against Han Qi was equivalent to going against the tide.

But now, Qi Huan vaguely sensed something was amiss. At first, he didn't think much of it, but after hanging up the phone, he fell silent.

He pondered the secretary's attitude and vaguely felt something was wrong.

"What's wrong?" Han Qi turned around, puzzled.

"Nothing, maybe I'm overthinking it, but I just feel the secretary's attitude isn't as happy as I expected," Qi Huan frowned.

After all, Jiang Tian had arrested Fang Jue's son, Qi Tai. In fact, under such tense circumstances, even Fang Jue was helpless, let alone finding a solution. He was now desperately trying to avoid being implicated.

At this moment, Fang Jue probably hated Jiang Tian to the core. Now that Jiang Tian was captured, Fang Jue's attitude wasn't as excited as he had expected.

"Perhaps the Party Secretary is just being cautious. After all, this black sheep still has some influence in Yunhai, and even got the favor of Chief Gao—he's got a bit of luck and has a few merits. That's understandable. But once the Party Secretary comes here and sees our evidence, Jiang Tian will have absolutely no chance of turning things around."

Han Qi still thought they were overthinking things, including Fang Jue.

They were being too cautious.

To the point that Han Qi's face showed a hint of sarcasm. These local people are just like that.

They're all so cautious, which is why the area is so chaotic. But we should also be grateful for their caution, as it leaves the credit for them to shine.

Isn't that the value they bring to the area?

"This black sheep still hasn't realized his mistakes. These people are the most hateful. These black sheep are even more harmful than criminals." Ding Xiaoxiao looked at Jiang Tian sitting casually on the sofa, a smile on his face, and the young girl was almost bursting with anger.

"These people, their small acts of abuse of power cause immeasurable harm to ordinary people. Xiaoxiao, just cuff him to the radiator and teach him a lesson," Han Qi said after a moment's thought.

"Boss, I've wanted to do this for a long time. If it weren't for the regulations, I would have slapped him twice already," Ding Xiaoxiao said, her face full of malice.

She dragged Jiang Tian's handcuffs, eager to cuff him directly to the radiator.

This method...

made Jiang Tian laugh.

"What are you laughing at?" Ding Xiaoxiao was filled with anger, and seeing Jiang Tian laugh only fueled her rage.

"Think carefully. Now you still have a chance to explain, but once you're cuffed here, you won't be able to take them off or explain yourself, and there will be no turning back," Jiang Tian said, not resisting, his face almost beaming with a smile.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was certain that Fang Jue knew exactly what had happened.

Jiang Tian knew that telling Qi Huan and the others to wait and do nothing meant that some leaders were probably already aware of the situation, and some might even be on their way.

As for the level of those leaders, Jiang Tian hesitated, considering it was the highest-ranking commander of today's operation.

Zhou Zhao might even be there.

With a Standing Committee member like Zhou Zhao, the Party Secretary of Yunhai, and even Shen Han, it was likely they wouldn't be able to sit still and would rush over.

This is a case of such magnitude that it's beyond the capacity of the city to handle alone; it requires province-wide coordination.

The province didn't intervene because the operation was so sudden and the local response so swift. Otherwise, a case this large would absolutely require a decision from headquarters.

However, given the numerous problems in Yunnan Province and the extremely rapid pace of this operation, local authorities were in charge of the operation and command.

Jiang Tian was certain that Zhou Zhao could arrive.

Therefore, Jiang Tian remained completely calm.

In fact, it was even possible that Chief Gao would come in person, and if Chief Gao knew, the situation would become even more serious.

Regardless of whether what these people were saying was true or false, trying to get Chief Gao to compromise was simply a sign that these people were out of their minds.



Chapter 418 With me here, the sky won't fall.


Anyone might compromise, but Chief Gao would never.

If Gao Yangyang was truly involved in corruption, Chief Gao wouldn't need anyone else; he could personally send her to jail.

To think they could use Gao Yangyang to get away with not interfering in Jiang Tian's affairs—these people are insane.

Especially Han Qi.

*Snap!*

Ding Xiaoxiao handcuffed Jiang Tian to the radiator. This was a common method, a torture where someone couldn't stand or squat.

Simply put, it was like being forced to stand in a horse stance; an ordinary person couldn't endure it for more than a few minutes.

Of course, Jiang Tian felt nothing.

He wasn't even angry.

There was no need to do anything now.

"What kind of look is that? Are you looking down on us, you black sheep?" Ding Xiaoxiao shouted angrily.

“Xiaoxiao, don’t waste your breath on such a black sheep. This kind of cancer will be oblivious to anything you say. He’ll only repent when he’s facing death. When the case is handed over to the court, we should recommend that they punish him severely. Ideally, we should take this opportunity to execute him outright. He’ll be more terrified than anyone else then.”

Han Qi, seemingly unfazed, pulled Ding Xiaoxiao aside and shook his head with a cold smile at Jiang Tian.

Upon hearing this, Ding Xiaoxiao’s eyes lit up slightly, and she looked at Han Qi with admiration, saying, “Boss, it has to be you! Black sheep like this should be executed. People who abuse their power deserve to die.”

Han Qi smiled slightly.

Jiang Tian noticed the confusion on their faces but said nothing.

Because in Jiang Tian’s view, it seemed that they were the ones abusing their power, using their authority, and causing huge problems because of their own willfulness.

“He deserves a lesson. Let’s wait here a bit. The secretary is on his way and will be here soon. It’ll be a real surprise when he sees him.” Qi Huan waved for the two men to sit on the sofa facing Jiang Tian, ​​who was handcuffed.

Looking down at him, Jiang Tian looked utterly pathetic in their eyes.

But wasn’t this precisely where their criticism should lie, and what they were naturally obligated to do?

“Okay, let’s wait for our Discipline Inspection Commission leader, Mr. Fang.” Han Qi sat down on the sofa.

Qi Huan nodded. “Little Han, this is a good opportunity to introduce you to the secretary. We’ll have dinner together tonight. Don’t worry, we’ll make sure you feel right at home.”

Han Qi said righteously, “A simple meal is fine. Don’t be like this black sheep who spent tens of thousands on a single meal. Who would believe he didn’t embezzle? If it weren’t for this meal, I really wouldn’t believe it. This cancer is hiding itself too well.”

“Yes, yes, that’s it. A simple meal.” Qi Huan smiled broadly.

After learning Han Qi's identity, Qi Huan treated him exceptionally well, granting his every request, all in an effort to curry favor with him.

Although Qi Huan was his subordinate, Han Qi knew his future was bleak. However, if he could gain the assistance of someone like Qi Huan, who seemed to have appeared out of nowhere,

his future might be brighter.

Perhaps even, he could experience a career resurgence.

Han Qi himself was also thinking that he would eventually leave Yunhai; Yunhai was merely where his dream began, but this place could absolutely become his base.

The more he thought about it, the more excited Han Qi became.

It could only be said that two people with similar quirks had somehow drawn together without any obstacles.

Time passed, second by second.

A few minutes had passed, roughly the time since Qi Huan hung up the phone, when a knock sounded at the door.

"Who is it?" Qi Huan stood up from the sofa in the private room.

The private room was very well-decorated, several levels better than ordinary hotels, even comparable to a five-star hotel.

It was built to guesthouse standards, with a bedroom and living room, and even a large space, complete with exercise equipment. The meals were all extremely light and healthy.

Putting everything else aside, retiring here would probably be something many elderly people dream of.

Qi Huan walked a few steps from the sofa to the door.

He pressed his ear against the door, trying to hear what was happening.

"Boss, something's happened." A voice rang out from outside.

"Something's happened? What is it?" Qi Huan was stunned.

"A lot of armed special agents have arrived at the door, all fully armed." The voice continued.

Buzz!!!

Qi Huan's mind exploded.

Han Qi on the sofa laughed in anger.

Only Jiang Tian's brow twitched.

Then, out of the corner of his eye, he noticed the radiator was right below the window, and Jiang Tian could conveniently see the low wall behind the building.

In the dim light emanating from that low wall,

the beams of tactical flashlights flickered.

Jiang Tian was extremely familiar with this; he immediately recognized it as tactical flashlights used by SWAT teams or something similar.

Such a large-scale operation.

Jiang Tian's brow twitched. He felt he might have been oversimplifying things. Such a large-scale mobilization, especially surrounding the private rooms,

even Zhou Zhao wouldn't dare to do this.

"Good, good, good, hahaha, someone's getting anxious! The protectors behind these troublemakers are starting to panic! They've jumped out themselves!"

"I thought these troublemakers were really waiting to die. No wonder they weren't flustered. Turns out, these people have become incredibly audacious."

Before Qi Huan could say anything, Han Qi almost burst out laughing with delight.

"Xiao Han, you..." Qi Huan was sweating profusely, feeling that things were spiraling out of control.

Han Qi, however, was currently in a state of ecstatic elation.

He mobilized men to surround the Discipline Inspection Commission.

He openly defied them.

This was practically tantamount to rebellion.

Not only was this black sheep finished, but he intended to uproot every single one of Jiang Tian's protectors.

Just to be on the safe side, Han Qi thought of his uncle.

With the old man gone, there was a lack of successors in the second generation.

Han Qi's uncle wasn't currently in an important department. Han Qi carried a heavy burden on his shoulders, and if it weren't for his urgent need for great achievements, even to be conservative, he would have focused on a simpler role, not Jiang Tian.

"Hehe, don't worry, let me call my uncle. He and your Huai Province were classmates during training. Whoever it is, they're finished today. With me here, the sky over Yunhai won't fall." Han Qi dialed the number with ecstatic joy.

Beside Han Qi, Ding Xiaoxiao was already filled with fanatical admiration.

But just as Han Qi dialed the number,

Qi Huan had already given the order: "Stop those people. Don't let anyone in. I want to see who's protecting Jiang Tian, ​​this cancer."

Han Qi was excited.

But before he could finish speaking, a furious voice rang out.

Boom!!!

A huge roar followed.



Chapter 419 This boy, whose subordinate is so brave?


Han Qi's face held a mixture of anger and a cold laugh, seemingly amused by Jiang Tian's ignorance.

These inexplicable expressions were almost incomprehensible to Jiang Tian, ​​a master of criminal investigation.

"It seems your protective umbrella is quite powerful!"

"To be able to mobilize so many people and resources."

"I must say, you've truly exceeded my expectations. After all, those who have made you famous and achieved so much must have immense influence operating behind the scenes, putting you in the limelight."

"If it were anyone else, perhaps they wouldn't be able to deal with you, but with me, Jiang Tian, ​​all I can say is that you, this black sheep, and your protective umbrella, are finished."

"Today, I'll see who it is, and who your protective umbrella is."

Han Qi's cold laughs, accusations, and anger seemed to stem from both disappointment and disapproval of Jiang Tian's protective umbrella.

His words left Jiang Tian speechless.

At the same time, even Huai Province, who had just arrived at the door, fell silent.

Today's farce was too much.

If Chief Gao and Minister Tan hadn't been here, it wouldn't have been so bad. But their presence meant that the capital was present.

Moreover, it meant that everything that had happened here was being witnessed by the capital. This was a farce beyond mere dereliction of duty.

It wouldn't be so bad on ordinary days, but the fact that such a major case had escalated into such a ridiculous situation and caused such serious problems today—what would the capital think?

How terrifying would that responsibility be?

To put it bluntly, even Huai Province might bear the blame.

"What? You say it has nothing to do with Huai Sheng? What a joke! In your position, would you dare say it has nothing to do with you? Everyone knows you might genuinely have nothing to do with it, and everyone else might not, but your biggest responsibility is dereliction of duty.

You couldn't even control your own subordinates, and you say you have no responsibility?

No one can shirk this responsibility.

Huai Sheng was already harboring terrifying anger.

He was relentlessly obstructed along the way, but once Huai Sheng revealed his identity and the armed police quickly secured the scene, all these disciplinary committee members were taken into custody.

As for Fang Jue, at this moment, he was sweating profusely, following behind Huai Sheng, his legs trembling uncontrollably.

The only reason he could still move was because, fueled by his boiling rage, Fang Jue wanted to ask these people directly why they wanted to harm him.

Was it intentional or unintentional?

Sure enough, after they arrived at the private room...

The disciplinary committee clerk who had just been talking through the partition was immediately and completely subdued before he could even react.

Huai Sheng was about to ask what was going on.

From inside the door.

A crystal-clear voice rang out.

"Your protector, this black sheep, has jumped out. I want to see who your protector is. But whoever it is, I'm telling you, you will all be punished by justice, and all your evil deeds will be exposed."

These were Han Qi's words.

And it was Han Qi's voice.

No one present recognized Han Qi.

That's why, while the social circles in Beijing aren't huge, they're not small either.

With each circle operating independently, it's impossible for everyone to know each other.

This is especially true between the older and younger generations.

Aside from close friends, most people are essentially part of a larger social circle. For example, if it weren't for his sister Han Lu, Han Qi would never have known Zhao Qiu Ni.

Because they belong to completely different circles; Zhao Qiu Ni's circle is at the very top.

In the past, their family might have had some connections, but now, with the loss of their breadwinner, they've fallen into complete decline.

This is why Han Qi is so eager to get things done.

But now…

Listening to the sounds inside the room, outside the door…

Huai Sheng's face almost contorted with rage.

That's right.

A black sheep?

A protector?

Representing justice to judge them?

"Who is this kid? When did our Discipline Inspection Commission send such a high-ranking official?"

Fang Jue stared wide-eyed, his face pale, his breathing rapid.

Even he didn't recognize the voice.

"Who said that? Who said that? Is this how your Discipline Inspection Commission operates?" Huai Sheng laughed angrily.

Fang Jue's forehead instantly broke out in a cold sweat: "No, no, no, I don't know this person. Oh, right, Deputy Secretary Qi Huan just said that a young man was transferred from Beijing, maybe from Beijing."

This wasn't about shifting blame.

But, Fang Jue seemed to see a glimmer of hope.

That's right.

This has nothing to do with them. It was all done by that damned Han Qi. It has nothing to do with Yunhai.

Han Qi was the one who initiated it.

I, Fang Jue, was kept in the dark. It wasn't our department's doing, so the responsibility is lessened.

"Alright, alright."

Fang Jue seemed to see a glimmer of hope.

As for whether he hated Jiang Tian, ​​how could that compare to his power?

As long as he had power, his son—he was only in his fifties this year, he could easily have more!

It wasn't like he couldn't have more children.

Besides, he didn't only have Fang Tai as a son; another son was studying abroad.

If one son was ruined, so be it. As long as he continued to shine in this position, what did it matter if Fang Tai was gone?

Fang Jue, who had thought there was no hope, was now incredibly excited.

Although he bore responsibility, it wasn't entirely hopeless, as long as he pushed all the blame onto Han Qi.

That's right.

The only thing to worry about was Qi Huan's involvement.

He just hadn't expected Qi Huan to be so stupid.

Fang Jue knew Qi Huan was doomed. As for Han Qi, it depended on the power behind him, but that had nothing to do with Fang Jue. Regardless of the power, that guy was doomed.

Just as Fang Jue was thinking this,

his vision suddenly went black.

When he looked up, he saw a figure rushing forward.

Then, a figure kicked open the wooden door.

Although the wooden door was sturdy, it hadn't been locked because Han Qi and his men were interrogating people inside, and it was kicked open easily.

After kicking open the door,

what came into view was...

Fang Jue, standing behind Huai Sheng, felt his head explode.

And it wasn't just Fang Jue whose head exploded.

Everyone who witnessed this scene was practically stunned.

Look who's here.

Minister Tan, Gao Xiaolong, and Shen Han.

These three are from the police system. Jiang Tian was arrested, and on the eve of such a major case, someone from their police system was arrested, so naturally all three of them came.

Huai Sheng, as the person in charge, naturally came too.



Chapter 420 Has Minister Tan become a protector?


Actually, the reason for such a grand display was because they were afraid of any unforeseen circumstances.

So, as soon as they learned the location, they all rushed over.

The entire Coaster bus was almost full.

However, what they didn't expect was...

The scene before them, let alone others...

Huai Sheng himself felt dizzy.

Gao Xiaolong had just kicked down the door, so Huai Sheng was truly the first to see what was inside.

It had to be said, the design of this one-bedroom apartment was quite nice.

The window faced the door directly, making it very well-ventilated and bright.

And below the window.

Jiang Tian was locked to the radiator, unable to squat down or stand up, only able to half-squat.

That sight was truly displayed before everyone's eyes and gazes.

They were stunned.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

At this moment, no one wanted to utter a single sound.

Jiang Tian rested his chin on his hand.

His gaze was utterly brooding.

He seemed unsurprised.

Shameful.

Indeed, quite shameful.

Even though Jiang Tian had anticipated this scene, he still felt somewhat ashamed.

Damn it.

Outside, Jiang Tian is a force to be reckoned with; he could take down hundreds of drug dealers single-handedly.

Now, he's locked up here.

It's not that he can't bear it, but he feels his dignity has been humiliated.

The key is, Jiang Tian can't really do anything.

After all, although Jiang Tian has another identity, he's not without scruples.

You know, even a weakened camel is bigger than a horse; if he resists, even if Jiang Tian is in the right, he'll be seen as unreasonable.

The crucial point is that Han Qi could then use this as an excuse to his advantage.

So if losing face and being humiliated can lead to his demise, Jiang Tian can only let it be.

Since Han Qi brought this on himself, he can't blame anyone else.

Therefore, while Jiang Tian isn't surprised by the situation, he just looks out the window, unwilling to turn around and look at the crowd outside.

Han Qi, who had just been berating and mocking Jiang Tian, ​​had an expression that was anything but simple.

He was still hurling insults, his phone in hand, dialing a number.

Yes, that's right.

Han Qi was dialing his uncle's number.

Because Han Qi had just sensed a powerful protector, he didn't dare delay after his outburst, immediately calling his uncle to ensure everything went smoothly.

If this matter succeeded, Han Qi felt his future was bright, a huge obstacle cleared away. He was going to deliver a devastating blow to those who abused their power.

He was heaven.

He was the law.

He was justice, capable of judgment.

And Han Qi's uncle was no ordinary man; he had once held a leadership position in an important department in Beijing, overseeing the nation's resource approval process.

Subsequently, after being promoted, although he was no longer in such a crucial department, and his current department had a somewhat retirement-like feel, the family's political legacy was almost entirely in the hands of his eldest uncle, who also had connections and entanglements with many leaders.

With such a huge problem arising in Yunhai, even with its protectors resorting to desperate measures, Han Qi naturally had to call his uncle for help.

But now that the call had been made, Han Qi swallowed hard.

“Little Han, what’s wrong?”

The voice on the phone broke the silence in the room, making time itself seem to flow.

“I…I…” Han Qi swallowed hard.

“Something’s happened?”

Han Qi’s uncle’s voice deepened as he asked.

It has to be said, having worked in the system for so long, Han Qi’s uncle instantly sensed something was wrong from that voice.

“What, calling your family to arrest me, their protector?” a deep voice rang out.

Accompanied by imposing footsteps, Minister Tan narrowed his eyes and approached Han Qi step by step, his voice unsurprisingly coming through the phone.

At this moment, even someone as worldly as Han Qi was somewhat flustered, because there were just too many people.

They were all wearing jackets.

Each person exuded an imposing aura, so much so that Han Qi initially couldn't recognize them all.

But when Minister Tan walked in first, his face was completely exposed to the light, the shadows on his features vanishing, revealing him clearly to Han Qi.

Buzz!!!

Han Qi's mouth gaped open.

It was already past nine o'clock at night, the sky outside was pitch black, and the corridor lights weren't particularly bright, so Han Qi hadn't recognized him at first.

Only after walking through the corridor and into the room, when Minister Tan's face was fully revealed, did Han Qi completely explode.

His eyes widened, his face filled with disbelief.

No.

Impossible.

Why.

Why is this person in Yunhai?

Han Qi's head was buzzing.

Even his legs trembled slightly, and his lips were dry.

Meanwhile, silence fell over the phone.

On the other end of the line.

A man in his fifties held the phone, his face filled with disbelief.

The voice he had just heard left this middle-aged man in disbelief.

“Brother, what’s wrong? What happened to Xiao Han? Why didn’t you tell me?” A man in his forties asked anxiously beside him.

The younger man tried to snatch the phone, but it was snatched back.

That’s right, the younger man in his forties was Han Qi’s third uncle, Han Xian, who was about to go to Yunhai to take over his position.

“Brother, what’s wrong?” Han Xian asked anxiously.

After all, Han Qi was the only one left in the third generation; he was the hope of their family for the next generation.

They had paid a heavy price to help Han Qi grow, sending him to Yunhai so that he could achieve great things and return with a meteoric rise to power.

"No, something's happened." Han Qi's uncle remained relatively calm, though cold sweat had inadvertently beaded on his forehead.

Han Qi recognized him, and even more so his uncle, who recognized him only by his voice.

Although the voice was slightly falsetto, Han Qi's uncle had heard that voice more than once.

Even if they weren't sure, they couldn't believe such a coincidence.

Then, Han Qi's trembling voice came through the phone: "Minister Tan..."

Buzz!!!

Minister Tan appeared in Yunhai?

And...

Something's happened.

That means...

Han Qi is Minister Tan's enemy.

He's gone mad.

What did Han Qi do?

Han Qi's uncle's head buzzed, instantly exploding.

Han Xian's head was also dumbfounded.

Was this the Minister Tan he knew?

Sure enough, the next moment, Minister Tan's unique voice rang out, but filled with anger: "My protector, this black sheep, is right here. Come, arrest me."

Buzz!!!

Han Qi's uncle and third uncle felt a golden light flash before their eyes.

They almost fainted on the spot.



Chapter 421 A newborn calf is not afraid of a tiger


The room was deathly silent, utterly still, so quiet you could hear a pin drop. Minister Tan's voice echoed throughout.

Regarding Jiang Tian.

A joke.

Even if it were embezzlement and bribery, it would require a reasonable and stable timeframe and channel to accomplish. How long has Jiang Tian been out of the police force?

You could say Jiang Tian doesn't even have time to rest, let alone time for embezzlement and bribery.

If he were truly embezzling and accepting bribes, why would drug lords and criminal groups be so eager to retaliate against Jiang Tian and his family?

That's one way to put it.

If Jiang Tian were truly embezzling and accepting bribes, Minister Tan would be suspected of protecting him.

With anyone else, Minister Tan would at least calmly investigate, but with Jiang Tian, ​​that's absolutely impossible.

So, since it's impossible,

why bring Jiang Tian here, and at such a crucial moment?

It's somewhat self-evident.

Is there a criminal group behind this?

Framed?

More than that.

Having exhausted all external means against Jiang Tian—assassinations, attacks, everything—they're probably trying to break him from within.

If that's the case, the problem is serious: framing a heroic police officer.

And a hero who has risked his life countless times, survived near-death experiences, and made immense contributions to Yunhai and even the entire Yunnan province.

The impact would be devastating.

Not just Han Qi, anyone here, regardless of their relationship,

would absolutely face the most severe punishment.

Following Minister Tan's voice, through a single door, followed by Governor Huai, entered.

After scanning the surroundings, his brow furrowed slightly.

Minister Tan could vent his anger without restraint, but Governor Huai couldn't.

Especially upon seeing Han Qi, Governor Huai's gaze became slightly heavy.

He seemed somewhat surprised.

More seemingly.

With disbelief.

Of course, after Minister Tan and his group entered, another person squeezed through the crowd from the back. Upon entering, they saw Han Qi and Qi Huan, along with Ding Xiaoxiao.

Behind these three were two clerks in suits.

However, all five of them, upon seeing such a terrifying scene, had a look of bewilderment in their eyes.

Without a doubt.

In their eyes, Jiang Tian represented an evil force, a criminal gang, and even more so, a protective umbrella organization.

They remained unperturbed.

That's why, although Han Qi's abilities and even his intelligence were questionable,

he had indeed handled one case since arriving from Beijing and taking office.

From the beginning, they had looked down on Han Qi, believing him to be too young, a nepotism appointee parachuted in from Beijing.

However, upon arriving at the Discipline Inspection Commission, he immediately led them through a significant case, investigating a deputy division-level official.

This is why they had such high regard for Han Qi.

Immediately afterward, Han Qi produced evidence of Jiang Tian's crimes.

As a member of the Discipline Inspection Commission, did he know Jiang Tian?

Of course he did. He was a hero in the eyes of many in Yunhai, given the recent frenzy of arrests of criminal groups within the entire Yunhai police force.

Initially, they would have absolutely no idea.

Even if they didn't know Jiang Tian completely, anyone who had heard of him, even just a little, would understand that Jiang Tian was practically a hero.

Just when they were still skeptical, Han Qi produced evidence of Jiang Tian's embezzlement and bribery.

That's right.

Evidence of embezzlement and bribery.

Who he traded with, they didn't know, but that wasn't important.

Because there's a charge against public officials called "possessing a huge amount of unexplained wealth."

This charge is actually linked to the charge of embezzlement and bribery.

Without a doubt, Jiang Tian was guilty of this crime.

Because the source of the enormous wealth was unclear, it was tantamount to embezzlement and bribery.

And what Han Qi produced was Jiang Tian's asset certificate.

He was holding asset certificates obtained from who-knows-where.

An entire CBD office building.

And he was driving a Phaeton worth over a million.

What? You're saying it was all embezzled by Jiang Tian? Not entirely. In their view, Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng, must have embezzled some of it. Jiang Peng was a major embezzler, while Jiang Tian was a minor one.

Just one Phaeton was enough to convict him.

A car worth over a million, how did you get it?

What? You can't answer? Sorry, that means you admitted to embezzlement.

Therefore, after understanding the situation, they were extremely convinced of Han Qi's abilities; even Qi Huan was directly persuaded.

These subordinates trusted Han Qi implicitly; after all, Jiang Tian's assets were undeniable.

"I have something to say."

Just then, in the silence, Ding Xiaoxiao suddenly stepped forward, seemingly fearless.

Others were afraid, even Han Qi himself was starting to panic, his convictions about justice wavering.

The sheer number of people they had mobilized was terrifying.

Minister Tan alone had already made him uneasy.

The arrival of Governor Huai made Han Qi's head spin.

This wasn't some clueless novice.

Faced with such a formidable lineup, he was a little flustered.

Thankfully.

He panicked, but Ding Xiaoxiao, like a newborn calf unafraid of tigers.

Facing the intimidating gazes of these powerful figures, Ding Xiaoxiao took a deep breath and stepped forward.

"You have something to say? Fine, say it."

Minister Tan nodded slightly, a forced smile.

He wanted to see what these people had to say.

"Jiang Tian, ​​this man is a corrupt official, and a major one at that," Ding Xiaoxiao said angrily, pointing at Jiang Tian.

She was still insisting on her own evidence.

His words,

instantly left everyone present bewildered.

Corruption.

At least corruption requires time, right?

Everyone who knew Jiang Tian knew how long he had been in the police force—only a few months, not even half a year. You're saying he's a major corrupt official?

"Do you have any evidence?"

Looking at the confident Ding Xiaoxiao, Minister Tan suppressed his anger and questioned her.

"Of course we have evidence, sir. If we didn't have evidence, how could we have arrested someone without authorization? We have evidence of Jiang Tian's embezzlement and bribery, which is why we took action to arrest him," Ding Xiaoxiao said righteously.

At this moment, Han Qi also recovered from his panic.

There were just too many leaders arriving.



Chapter 422 Fabricated charges, how dare you!


It's important to know that when Han Qi first arrived, his uncle specifically called Huai Sheng. It wasn't to offer any special favors, but simply to chat and subtly imply that his nephew had arrived in Yunhai and he hoped Huai Sheng could look after him. He also implied that this nephew was completely trustworthy.

Of course, Han Qi's uncle and Huai Sheng weren't actually very close; they were just somewhat acquainted.

They had worked in the same department before and had some minor interactions.

Of course, Han Qi had also met Huai Sheng when he worked in Beijing, thanks to his uncle.

This is why Han Qi recognized him at a glance.

Just now, Han Qi admitted he panicked, even feeling some resentment towards himself. He had almost been too intimidated to uphold his sense of justice.

He was even less capable than a young female subordinate.

Thinking this through, Han Qi gave Ding Xiaoxiao, who was speaking, an approving expression, seemingly quite pleased.

Han Qi calmly began, "Minister Tan, we naturally already have evidence of Jiang Tian's crimes. Because it involves a huge amount of unexplained wealth, that's why we arrested him."

He paused, seemingly giving people time to process his statement, then a confident smile appeared on Han Qi's face. "Since you leaders are here, I'll be frank. Forgive my bluntness, but while Jiang Tian appears outwardly righteous and upright, even seemingly having accomplished considerable merit, in my opinion, he's probably just collaborating with a criminal organization."

Silence!

At first, the crowd seemed to have some idea, but the more they listened, the more absurd it seemed.

These leaders present all knew Jiang Tian extremely well.

Precisely because they knew him, they understood the immense, almost unbelievable, achievements Jiang Tian had made.

Even later,

Minister Tan was almost unable to contain his anger.

Han Qi's meaning wasn't just questioning Jiang Tian, ​​but also questioning their headquarters. Was their headquarters being misled by Jiang Tian, ​​which was why they were awarding him so many credits?

"Han Qi, you just arrived in Yunhai from Beijing. Where did you get all this information?" Huai Sheng asked calmly.

Something was wrong.

Everyone among these people was shrewd.

They also keenly sensed the problem.

Han Qi had just been parachuted in from Beijing; this wasn't an attempt to absolve him of responsibility, because he couldn't.

After listening for so long, it was all speculation.

All conjecture.

All baseless accusations.

Even if what they said was true, Han Qi had only just arrived; how could he possibly know?

Upon hearing this, Han Qi smiled and said, "I happen to have an old classmate who works in the Anti-Corruption Bureau of the Procuratorate. They had been keeping an eye on Jiang Tian, ​​this bad apple, for a long time. After I learned about it, I launched a joint operation with the Anti-Corruption Bureau. So it wasn't just us, but the Anti-Corruption Bureau was also involved."

"At the same time, knowing all this, and knowing that Jiang Tian had made many contributions, I was curious how someone so young could have achieved so much. After seeing the huge amount of unexplained wealth of this black sheep, I understood that this black sheep must have cooperated with a criminal group, using their information to accumulate merits, while also eliminating dissidents for the criminal group. There must be a powerful protective force behind him to orchestrate this grand scheme."

"Of course, I don't mean to look down on Yun Hai, it's just that those involved are often blinded, and perhaps many have been misled by this black sheep."

Han Qi continued to state his speculations.

But in the end, it all boiled down to one thing:

Speculation.

All speculation.

They were stunned.

Many people looked at Han Qi with surprise and shock.

How dare you take action based on just this?

Do you know Jiang Tian's influence in Yunhai? Do you know that right now, not just in Yunhai, but throughout the entire Yun Province, it's practically Jiang Tian leading the charge, delivering a devastating blow to criminal groups?

Yet you're standing behind, making your own assumptions, just because Jiang Tian accomplished many seemingly impossible tasks, and you're concluding that others are connected to or collaborating with criminal groups?

Just as Han Qi was expecting everyone to be shocked and pleased with his investigative abilities, he found the room eerily silent.

A chilling silence.

Han Qi's eyes showed confusion, and his confident expression froze.

An ominous premonition crept into his heart.

"Finished?"

Huai Sheng took two steps closer and asked.

Han Qi was somewhat at a loss, because these people's reactions were completely different from what he had imagined.

Shouldn't these people be shocked?

Shouldn't they be incredulous?

Shouldn't they immediately investigate what he said and then interrogate Jiang Tian with furious expressions?

After all, Jiang Tian had deceived them!

But why were these people's expressions and reactions completely different from what they expected?

The next moment, Minister Tan said with a hint of anger, "Based on fabricated charges! How dare you!"
