"No, no, no, Minister Tan, this is the evidence we investigated regarding Jiang Tian's unexplained huge amount of assets. The Anti-Corruption Bureau gave it to us, and we immediately conducted further investigations. We found nothing wrong; it's absolutely true," Han Qi hurriedly said.

After Han Qi finished speaking, he quickly took out a stack of documents from a black leather briefcase on the sofa next to him. It was clear that an extremely thorough investigation had been conducted.

The contents were very detailed.

Including Jiang Tian's bank account statements.

Including the assets of Jiang Tian's subordinates.

And Jiang Tian also owned a car, a Phaeton.

"According to our findings, Jiang Tian's Phaeton, although not registered in his name, is a car worth over a million yuan. How could he possibly afford it on his salary? Not only him, but I suspect that those close to him are also highly suspected of embezzlement."

Han Qi explained, pointing to the documents. In fact, Han Qi had conducted a detailed investigation, trying to find out who the Phaeton's owner was.

But how could that be possible?

Where did Jiang Tian get this car? It was an official vehicle awarded to Jiang Tian by the head of the security department as a reward after his abnormally high performance during a mission with the security department across the Atlantic.

The car was directly registered under the security department's name.

However, the security department has the strictest and top-level secrecy; no matter what methods he used, he had no authority to investigate.

This made Han Qi even more certain that Jiang Tian had a powerful protector behind him.

To avoid any complications, a hasty arrest operation was launched. There was already enough evidence for the arrest; further detailed evidence could be obtained through interrogation and investigation after the arrest.

Once you're in here, you won't get out.

“Phenom?”

Minister Tan stared coldly at Han Qi.

“Do you know why you can't find out who owns the Phaeton? Well, let me tell you, this car…” Minister Tan's eyes flashed coldly.

Others didn't know.

But Minister Tan knew that Jiang Tian was already a member of the security department.

He also knew everything about the car.

He never expected that this idiot would use this as evidence to cause significant damage to the country and the organization. Indeed, the cunning of a villain can't match the quick thinking of a fool.

Minister Tan was almost laughing in exasperation.



Chapter 423 Rewrites History Completely: One Person Represents an Entire City


"Then let me tell you, that Phaeton was a reward from a special department above, because Comrade Jiang Tian performed exceptionally well." Minister Tan slowly exhaled.

He was suppressing his anger.

But no matter how hard he tried to suppress it, at this moment, Minister Tan was almost unable to hold back.

After all, who gave Han Qi the power to arrest Jiang Tian on a fabricated charge?

And who allowed Han Qi to act so recklessly?

Having ambition and a sense of justice isn't a bad thing; in fact, it's a good thing, and Minister Tan liked people like that.

But as the saying goes, the cunning of a bad person is no match for the quick thinking of a fool.

This Han Qi was that kind of fool.

Even more so, a fool completely blinded by his own achievements.

Minister Tan's voice was calm, but his words were like a thunderbolt, leaving Han Qi and Ding Xiaoxiao behind him stunned in place.

That's right.

Jiang Tian drives a Phaeton worth millions, yet he claims it's not bribery or embezzlement.

Of course, this kind of thing falls under the purview of the inspectors, who have jurisdiction over it. However, in the police system, the inspectors typically handle these matters independently.

Han Qi is under immense pressure in dealing with Jiang Tian's case, all to uncover the protective umbrella behind him. If he can gain merit, everything else will be minor.

Jiang Tian isn't Han Qi's primary target. Han Qi has grander ambitions. He sees the protective umbrella behind Jiang Tian in this case and keenly senses that a complete crackdown might bring him instant fame.

This crackdown could potentially sustain him for life.

That's why, as a disciplinary inspector, although he knows he has the right to intervene in the police system, very few people actually do so because it's too risky and offends too many people.

Han Qi, tempted by the lucrative rewards and the promise of instant fame, and feeling invincible after obtaining the evidence, finally took action.

The first piece of irrefutable evidence, Huiteng, turned out to be a reward from a higher-ups.

He was stunned.

Han Qi wiped the sweat from his brow; unknowingly, he was drenched in cold sweat.

But thinking of the other evidence he possessed, Han Qi breathed a slight sigh of relief.

"Even if Huiteng was obtained through legitimate means, but..." Han Qi began to speak.

"What 'even if'? Is your method of investigation based on fabricated charges? This was clearly a departmental reward, not the kind of speculation and fabrication you're talking about. Using baseless accusations, who taught you to conduct investigations like this? If everyone investigated like you, would the world ever be at peace?"

Before Han Qi could finish speaking, Minister Tan roared angrily.

Han Qi's forehead was covered in even more cold sweat.

Of course, at this crucial moment, Ding Xiaoxiao was filled with stubbornness.

Perhaps it was the fearlessness of youth, or perhaps in Ding Xiaoxiao's eyes, someone like Han Qi, who upheld justice and defied power, was the epitome of righteousness.

Now, seeing the justice she held dear crumbling because of tyranny, Ding Xiaoxiao glared intently at Jiang Tian.

In her eyes, Jiang Tian was the embodiment of evil.

He was a criminal.

They are a protective umbrella.

They are corrupt officials who have harmed countless people.

Small officials with enormous greed.

So Ding Xiaoxiao stepped forward, snatched the evidence documents from Han Qi's hand, and loudly proclaimed, "Okay, sir, the Phaeton was acquired legitimately. And this CBD office building under Jiang Tian's name, located in the city center, is worth over a billion. Is this definitely not a front?"

That's right.

This office building is currently worth over a billion.

But Jiang Tian understands that even considering the stalled development of Yunhai, it will double in value in ten years, perhaps even reaching two or three billion.

And considering the current grand strategy.

Jiang Tian understands that Yunhai's development into a first-tier city at this stage is driven by obvious intentions from above. It's impossible for Yunhai Province alone to achieve this.

It's thanks to resources from above, and even nationwide.

And why is the focus on the future? Undoubtedly, it's Southeast Asia.

This is a grand strategy: to develop Yunhai into a central city of Southeast Asia, a core economic hub for the future of the region.

In his previous life, Jiang Tian perhaps realized this development direction was flawed. Along with Yunhai's development, the economic bridges of the entire Southeast failed to be built; instead, crime reached unprecedented levels.

These criminals were making a fortune, which the authorities couldn't tolerate. Thus, the first-tier city stagnated for over a decade.

The hope of becoming a super-first-tier city not only vanished, but the economic standstill caused major companies to relocate from Yunhai, leading to significant economic problems. After more than a decade, it became a second-tier city.

But now, things have changed.

Because of Jiang Tian's appearance, hope has reappeared.

According to his previous life, at this time, the second bureau chief had already died unexpectedly, yet the criminal groups showed no signs of wavering. In fact, they had already begun to take action.

Could others see this?

Actually, Jiang Tian was deducing based on his perspective from his previous life, and it was largely accurate.

Few in the entire Yun Province could see this point. Jiang Tian didn't know if Huai Province could, but he understood that even if Huai Province didn't know the grand strategic direction, they could sense a lot.

This indirectly reflected Jiang Tian's importance.

Winning over Jiang Tian at this time meant winning over the entire Yunhai.

It meant winning over the future.

And if the grand strategic direction was followed, how much would this office building be worth if it developed into a super-first-tier city?

It was incalculable.

The annual rent would probably be tens of millions, even hundreds of millions.

Even now, over a billion, in 2003, it was an astonishing price.

Not to mention the high-ranking leaders who entered, those following behind all looked at Jiang Tian with astonished expressions.

Then their gazes turned to Jiang Peng in the crowd behind.

Actually, it wasn't Jiang Peng's fault for not showing up.

Because he couldn't show up.

They said Jiang Tian was corrupt, indirectly implying Jiang Peng was also implicated. Under these circumstances, Jiang Peng really needed to avoid suspicion, which made him feel incredibly aggrieved.

Now, seeing his colleagues looking at him strangely, Jiang Peng was completely bewildered.

An office building?

Over a billion.

Why didn't he, as the father, know this?

Meanwhile, Han Qi admired Ding Xiaoxiao even more.

Under the scrutinizing gaze of these leaders, even Han Qi himself couldn't withstand it, but Ding Xiaoxiao was able to bear such immense pressure.

Han Qi felt ashamed, feeling he had let down the justice he had always upheld.



Chapter 424 Fully Exposed, Making Heroes Cry No More Tears


Seeing everyone's stunned expression, Han Qi stepped forward and confidently stated, "That's right. According to our investigation, that office building was transferred to Jiang Tian's name not long ago. You see, we investigated Jiang Tian's background and found that he has absolutely no assets. He never had any before, and suddenly an office building appeared out of nowhere. Ha! And he says he's not corrupt? He's a black sheep. His so-called honors are nothing more than a front for those criminal groups and their powerful protectors."

The more Han Qi spoke, the more confident he became.

As he spoke, some of the leaders present looked at Jiang Tian with surprise.

Even Minister Tan frowned slightly.

"Given Jiang Tian's abilities, I find it hard to imagine how someone who's only twenty and recently graduated could own an office building. If it wasn't embezzled, how could he have acquired it? And given Jiang Tian's recent graduation, how could he possibly embezzle so much? It's impossible. Therefore, in conclusion, there's a huge criminal gang behind Jiang Tian, ​​and the people behind him..."

At this point, Han Qi's face was filled with righteous indignation, his eyes slightly bloodshot from excessive excitement. Pointing at Jiang Tian, ​​he said loudly and resolutely, "They are the biggest scourge of Yunhai, the biggest cancer. This isn't something a small force could accomplish. Once we can uncover them, the dark clouds over Yunhai will finally dissipate."

Flawless.

Han Qi was brimming with confidence.

This reasoning was logical and well-founded, even causing Minister Tan to look at Jiang Tian in astonishment.

His gaze was filled with doubt and shock.

Other things were fine.

But an office building.

"Xiao Jiang, what's going on?" Minister Tan asked, looking at Jiang Tian.

Minister Tan genuinely knew nothing about this matter.

And indeed, according to Han Qi's account...

Minister Tan believed Jiang Tian wouldn't embezzle, but even if Jiang Tian didn't embezzle, there was no guarantee that Jiang Peng might have, and then transferred the assets to his son, Jiang Tian.

Or perhaps, Jiang Tian had truly fallen from grace after receiving so many honors?

The more Minister Tan thought about it, the heavier his heart became.

His brows furrowed more deeply as he looked at Jiang Tian, ​​his gaze filled with deep questioning.

At that moment, a commotion erupted in the room.

A clamor instantly broke out.

"An office building worth over a billion."

"Wow, what's going on? Jiang Tian owns an office building worth over a billion."

"That's over a billion! How could Jiang Tian possibly have so much money?"

"Could he really have embezzled it? Seriously, with so many cases and so many achievements, could it really be a criminal organization pushing him out?"

"That's right, I knew it. How could someone escape from hundreds of armed gangs, and even go against hundreds of drug dealers? One person taking down hundreds of criminal gangs—if there's internal support, then it makes sense."

"I never imagined it would be like this."

Even the leaders who were initially confused but familiar with Jiang Tian's past achievements were instantly influenced by Han Qi's words.

And Han Qi smiled.

That's right.

This was the strongest evidence in his hands, and also crucial proof of the enormous criminal power behind Jiang Tian.

Although there was no concrete evidence of embezzlement or bribery.

However, as he had said before, the unexplained source of a huge amount of wealth was, without a doubt, the most important evidence for convicting a public official.

“Jiang Tian, ​​what else do you have to say?” Han Qi looked at Jiang Tian with a cold smile.

“That office building, don’t tell us someone transferred it by mistake and then it ended up in your name,” Ding Xiaoxiao also looked at Jiang Tian with a cold smile.

As a young public official, her heart was filled with a sense of justice.

Fighting for justice, even if it meant being shattered to pieces.

Perhaps the stability of life had smoothed out her edges, and the years she spent in the Discipline Inspection Commission had worn down her fighting spirit. Han Qi’s appearance rekindled the sense of justice in Ding Xiaoxiao’s heart, and now, seeing a bad apple fall from grace filled her with boundless satisfaction.

Accompanied by Ding Xiaoxiao’s voice.

Everyone stared silently at Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly awaiting his answer.

The next moment, surprisingly, Jiang Tian didn't deny it; he even nodded slightly and said, "That's right, that office building is mine."

Hiss!!!

Boom!!!

The scene instantly erupted.

Even Minister Tan stared at Jiang Tian in astonishment.

His eyes were filled with disbelief.

He thought there would be some emotional manipulation or a frame-up, but he didn't expect Jiang Tian to admit it so easily.

Then, wouldn't this office building prove Jiang Tian's embezzlement and bribery?

How foolish!

Minister Tan's chest heaved: "You really embezzled?"

Jiang Tian shook his head: "No, I didn't."

"Still saying you didn't? You've admitted to owning this office building, and you still say you didn't embezzle? What, if over a billion isn't embezzlement, then what is?" Ding Xiaoxiao sneered sarcastically.

"What, can't this office building be mine?" Jiang Tian asked, seemingly confused.

"Don't tell me you bought this building yourself, and earned the money yourself, right?" Han Qi looked down at Jiang Tian as if he'd heard a huge joke.

"Is that not allowed?" Jiang Tian continued to retort.

Pfft!!!

"Hahaha..."

Han Qi burst into laughter.

It has to be said that at first, he was shocked by so many leaders and such a grand display.

But now, Han Qi had calmed down, confident of victory. He was as steady as a rock, even feeling the excitement of having accomplished a great feat.

In reality, Han Qi didn't know that even if Jiang Tian hadn't embezzled, the consequences Han Qi would face if he acted at this moment were something Jiang Tian couldn't bear.

"You earned it yourself, hahaha, what? You earned over a billion all by yourself? You're about to die, and in front of so many leaders, do you think spouting nonsense will only make you a laughingstock?" Han Qi's eyes were bloodshot, seemingly genuinely enraged by Jiang Tian's embezzlement, and he rebuked Jiang Tian with righteous indignation.

For a moment.

Inside the room, even some leaders looked at Han Qi with astonishment.

And, a hint of admiration.

Putting everything else aside, just this performance—his insistence on justice, his attitude, his competence and willingness to act—is enough to earn him considerable goodwill.

“You’re about to die, and you still won’t confess? You earned it yourself! Look at these leaders here, do you think they believe you? Do you deserve their trust? Don’t you have any shame? Even now, you still refuse to show the slightest remorse?” Ding Xiaoxiao rebuked him angrily.

But just then…

The instant Ding Xiaoxiao finished speaking…

A calm voice echoed in the room.

“This money really was earned by Comrade Xiao Jiang. Its origins are crystal clear. Not long after Comrade Xiao Jiang purchased the office building, he reported it to the organization. The source of the money is also clearly recorded.”

Buzz!

Everyone instinctively looked at the speaker.

The next moment, Huai Sheng, hands behind his back, surveyed the scene before continuing, "So, all the charges are so inexplicable, convicted without even a thorough investigation? Comrade Xiao Jiang made a lot of money through his own investments during university, clearly possessing such terrifying business acumen. Yet, driven by passion, he resolutely joined the police force, starting from the bottom, fearlessly fighting criminals day and night, risking his life. And such a hero, you've wronged him so completely?"

"And you, you've made such a hero bleed and weep. Do you have even a shred of conscience?"



Chapter 425 He wielded absolute power over the underworld.


A buzzing sound!

Silence.

Deathly silence.

No one spoke, their eyes filled with horror.

They were stunned.

Completely stunned.

Is this some kind of nonsense?

Before anyone could react, Zhou Zhao, who was standing at the back, stepped forward.

Ignoring even Jiang Peng's bewildered expression, Zhou Zhao chuckled and said, "Actually, I reported this to Huai Province."

In fact, after Jiang Tian received the reward money for the special entry, he suddenly realized that if the God of Wealth's Blessing continued to reward money, a little less would be manageable, but a lot more would be impossible to hide.

And if it were exposed, it could cause a huge problem.

Under these circumstances, Jiang Tian thought that it wasn't a big deal now that his position was low, but once he rose in rank, he would inevitably give others leverage against him.

Actually, current civil servant regulations aren't as comprehensive, nor are they as strictly prohibitive of business activities as they are in later generations. Of course, very few people actually do it.

Because doing so easily leaves evidence against them, and it's considered inappropriate for someone within the system to engage in business. Most of the time, it's family members involved.

Furthermore, there are certain limitations: business activities cannot be conducted within the scope of one's authority—this is a red line.

Jiang Tian also considered this. Right now, it's not a big deal because any investigation can be explained away. But in the future, if he rises in rank and still has so much wealth, it will inevitably become a loophole.

Like now.

Although the excuse is somewhat ridiculous and involves much speculation, unexplained wealth is indeed a major charge for convicting corrupt officials.

Most arrests are made using this charge.

Because Jiang Tian considered this factor, he eventually found an opportunity to casually mention it during a meeting with Zhou Zhao.

Mainly, he made a record of it.

On the other hand, they also wanted to find an opportunity to file a record with the inspectors later.

However, the inspectors hadn't found a time yet. When Jiang Tian mentioned it to Zhou Zhao, Zhou Zhao was shocked.

It wouldn't matter if it were anyone else, but the key issue was who Jiang Tian was, how terrifying his influence was in Yunhai, and it could be said that Jiang Tian was practically controlling the underworld, wiping it out with his own hand.

Every member of Yunhai's underworld was terrified at the mention of Jiang Tian.

They were all afraid that one day, an unreasonable person would break into their homes and wipe them out.

Moreover, not to mention Jiang Tian's influence below, his influence above was enormous, even in the province and in Beijing, attracting the attention of many powerful figures. Under these circumstances, even a high-ranking official in Yunhai wouldn't dare make a decision.

They found an opportunity, conducted an asset investigation on Jiang Tian, ​​filed a record, and submitted it to Huai Province.

And Huai Province was shocked after reading it.

That's why the entry's efficiency is truly terrifying; those asset certificates can even be traced back to Jiang Tian's investments during his university years.

Moreover, every single transaction was traceable; every financial inflow and outflow could be tracked.

In other words, Jiang Tian earned over a billion yuan during his university years.

This directly shocked Huai Sheng at the time.

And if you were given a choice:

to become one of the wealthiest people in society, or to fight against criminal organizations in the shadows? Between these two choices, probably 99% of people would choose the former.

Being a tycoon—wealth, social status, gold and silver—you could have almost everything. Standing in the sunlight, enjoying everyone's flattery, reveling in all the good things.

Jiang Tian had almost succeeded on this path, but in the end, he chose to give it all up, using all his investments to buy an office building.

In two words: Great.

At that time, Huai Sheng's opinion of Jiang Tian completely changed; he gained a deeper understanding of him. Because of this, even if Huai Sheng put himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, he felt he couldn't do what Jiang Tian did.

He exchanged all his investments for a single building, then selflessly dedicated himself to contributing to social stability, risking his life for it.

Who could do that?

Now, Jiang Tian has done it.

Jiang Tian did it.

Even facing Jiang Tian, ​​Huai Sheng felt a pang of shame.

And such a great man.

Yet you still slander him so viciously, arrest him so cruelly, and even subject him to physical torture, making this hero bleed and weep.

When he was arrested, Jiang Tian was probably still desperately trying to find clues about the criminal group, and now, he was arrested from behind by his own comrades.

How much inner trauma would that cause a hero?

The more Huai Sheng thought about it, the angrier he became.

The more Huai Sheng thought about it, the harder it was for him to suppress his anger. This was because, from the very beginning, upon hearing of Jiang Tian's arrest, Huai Sheng's rage surged, leading to his furious outburst.

After all, people like Huai Sheng were the type to remain calm even in the face of a collapsing mountain; how could he possibly lose his composure so easily?

While everyone was bewildered, Huai Sheng continued, "Not long ago, Comrade Zhou Zhao did report this issue to me, and I eventually verified it. Comrade Jiang was already investing during his university years, accumulating hundreds of millions in wealth. Every single transaction is traceable, and the origin of every sum of money can be verified. Did you investigate this during your case?"

Huai Sheng spoke these words while suppressing his anger. Although Jiang Tian was under wraps, making many things difficult to investigate,

Han Qi's influence was such that with a little effort, he could directly verify every transaction from the head office.

But it was obvious.

As expected.

Han Qi moved his lips, his blank eyes flickering slightly.

No.

He didn't do that.

Because he believed all his actions were already very thorough, and that this stage of investigation was sufficient. The rest would be handled during the arrest, followed by a final interrogation to gradually verify the facts.

Actually, Han Qi couldn't really be blamed for this.

Whether it's the Discipline Inspection Commission or the Anti-Corruption Bureau, the investigations are all the same. Who the hell has the time to investigate every single penny of your unexplained wealth to determine if it's reasonable?

Normally, once they've found the assets under your name, they can directly convict you of unexplained wealth.

Since they can convict you, to avoid any complications, they'll just close the net immediately.

Investigating every single penny is just absurd.

Unless it's uncertain whether a small amount of money came from embezzlement, then they'll investigate the source.

But with assets exceeding one billion, plus Han Qi's preconceived notion, anyone would assume it's embezzlement.

"No, no," Han Qi's tone was no longer as assertive as before.

Cold sweat was pouring down his forehead.

Panic was evident in his eyes, and his face was pale.

The next moment, Huai Sheng suddenly roared: "You arrest people without any evidence, based on fabricated charges. Do you know how much damage and how bad the impact this has caused to the Party and the people? Can you bear the consequences?"



Chapter 426 A Great Past, He Was Truly So Great


Han Qi was stunned.

He couldn't imagine.

If Jiang Tian was truly innocent, what would be the outcome?

What terrifying consequences would it cause?

The more he thought about it, the more his heart pounded. He clenched his fists, trying to calm himself down.

Meanwhile, Ding Xiaoxiao, standing beside Han Qi, couldn't accept reality.

What a joke.

The most evil villain in her eyes, a great demon shrouded in darkness, a seemingly virtuous but actually evil protector—a white glove.

And in the end, he was innocent.

He was the most righteous person.

Who could bear this reversal?

"Impossible! Jiang Tian is a black sheep! How could he earn so much money? A billion-dollar office building—it's all his! A college student, without embezzlement..." Ding Xiaoxiao paused.

Because Ding Xiaoxiao suddenly realized.

How could a college student embezzle?

"He earns money in college, who's crazy enough to give money to a college student, let alone allow him to embezzle it?

So..."

Zhou Zhao's gaze sharpened. "I've repeatedly emphasized to the Party, especially the Discipline Inspection Commission, that it's crucial, often the most important position. This is because it supervises all comrades. When handling cases, there must be absolutely no bias or personal bias. If an irreparable tragedy occurs, it's a terrible loss to the Party and the people."

"And what did you do, Han Qi? What deep-seated hatred do you have for Comrade Xiao Jiang? You've just arrived in Yunhai, and in your quest for merit, you've become recklessly obsessed. Why Xiao Jiang can make money isn't something you need to concern yourself with. Just because he has a talent for business, you think you can arrest him?"

Boom!!!

Zhou Zhao's words were quite harsh.

And they directly targeted Han Qi.

That's right, directly naming Mi Hanqi is tantamount to completely exposing Hanqi.

Actually, by now,

the leaders present generally know the cause and effect. Fang Jue has a problem, but it's not a big one, because it's obvious that Fang Jue seems genuinely innocent.

And Qi Huan, the deputy leader of the Discipline Inspection Commission, is also an accomplice.

The main perpetrators are the anti-corruption team, and this Han Qi in front of them.

He's someone parachuted in from Beijing.

However, because of his parachuting in, many people look at Han Qi with a degree of hostility.

Huai Sheng took a deep breath, and seeing the stunned crowd, continued, "Faced with hundreds of millions in wealth and the glitz and glamour of a life of luxury, Comrade Xiao Jiang remained true to his original aspirations. He used all his wealth to buy a building, leaving it there, while dedicating himself to contributing to the nation's development. He risked his life for this cause, making outstanding contributions without seeking anything in return, bringing devastating blows to the criminal organizations of Yunhai and even the entire province. And yet, you have insulted such a hero like this? Do you have any humanity left?"

These words

completely shattered the illusion.

Or rather, they evoked extreme unease in everyone's hearts.

Why the unease?

Think about it: how did Jiang Tian rise to power? It was step by step, earned with his own life and blood, through countless perilous missions, infiltrating drug lords' lairs, and narrowly escaping death.

Even someone else carrying out so many dangerous missions would have died multiple times over.

With such abilities, no matter how much merit they accumulate, they feel it's all their due.

Even though most of the people here are leaders, if it were someone from the Municipal Public Security Bureau, they would probably be itching to kill Han Qi right now.

That's Jiang Tian's reputation within the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

After all, Jiang Tian shares many of his credits with others, even those he doesn't need himself, or those that could be shared.

He also delegates cases he can't handle.

In short, Jiang Tian has given everyone an easy opportunity to earn merit.

And thinking about it from Han Qi's perspective, what is his identity?

What has he done?

He had done nothing for Yunhai, not even a single glorious achievement.

No, absolutely nothing.

A man sitting in an office could so easily arrest a hero who had risked his life on the front lines, and ruin such a hero.

Therefore, the moment Huai Sheng finished speaking, the scene erupted in chaos.

"Comrade Jiang actually has such a past."

"Hiss, if you put yourself in his shoes, earning over a billion yuan through investments during university—that's an terrifying investment ability! But even so, he gave up business and joined the most dangerous anti-drug police force after graduation."

"Damn, I never imagined Comrade Jiang could be so great."

If there was no way out, risking one's life for a future is understandable.

But Jiang Tian clearly had a bright and comfortable future, yet he still chose to fight on the most dangerous path.

That's truly great.

And such a great person...

Look at him now.

Jiang Tian was handcuffed to a radiator, unable to stand or squat, only able to half-squat, his chin resting on one hand, his face expressionless.

This...

It left everyone stunned.

Putting themselves in his shoes, if they had given so much and were treated like this, calling it hatred might be an exaggeration, but they would probably have completely exploded by now.

Yet Jiang Tian maintained such composure.

Of course, the leaders present weren't entirely unfamiliar with Jiang Tian. After all, they had met him in Yanjiang a few days ago, where he had saved Su Tong and Elder Mu, averting huge losses.

They had already greatly admired Jiang Tian then.

Furthermore, the case with Wenye Group was still unresolved, and he had saved Yunnan Province from significant losses.

That had already earned him considerable goodwill from many leaders.

And now, it was simply incredible.

Great.

Hearing that Jiang Tian, ​​clearly capable and having already earned over a billion, had chosen to withdraw from business—wasn't that treating money like dirt?

And this great hero, a hero from their Yunhai, was being insulted like this.

Thinking this through,

many people in the audience were enraged.

Almost all eyes were fixed on Han Qi with fury.

Han Qi's head was buzzing.

Seeing those angry glares made him even more dizzy, and only one thought filled his mind:

It's over.

It's all over.

It's utterly over.

The sky has fallen.

On his third day in Yunhai, he arrested a hero of Yunhai, and a great hero at that, at a crucial moment before a case, leading to serious consequences and incurring the wrath of almost all the leaders.

What am I, parachuted in here, supposed to do?

The most terrifying thing was the next moment, when Minister Tan coldly and emotionlessly asked: "So, Han Qi, do you have any more evidence?"



Chapter 427 Shocking News: A Bright Future for Yunhai


Minister Tan's gaze was icy.

Meanwhile, Han Qi's hand, gripping the phone, was completely cold and devoid of warmth.

However, the sound from here carried clearly to a residence somewhere in Beijing.

Han Qi's third uncle, Han Xian, and his eldest uncle were both utterly bewildered.

Han Qi's eldest uncle was relatively unaffected, but Han Xian...

The sky had fallen.

They knew...

The Han family's political resources were limited. They had even sent Han Xian and Han Qi to Yunhai to gain experience and accumulate merit before their return.

This would pave a smooth path for their future.

For this purpose, they had sent Han Qi ahead as a scout, with Han Xian following behind, preparing to replace Jiang Peng as bureau chief.

But now...

They realized that all their calculations were no match for Han Qi's sudden inspiration.

The family's century-long plan, because of Han Qi's brilliant idea, was utterly ruined.

Listening to the voice on the phone,

they roughly understood the whole story.

They really did understand.

Han Xian sighed, "If it really comes down to it, let Han Qi come back."

Even now, Han Qi hadn't realized the seriousness of the situation. In his eyes, wasn't this just arresting the wrong person? And according to normal case-handling procedures, although there were some irregularities, everyone did it this way.

On the surface, there were indeed some minor issues.

But currently, it didn't seem like a big problem. It was just that Yunhai was making a fuss over it.

If it really came down to it, they could just leave.

Going to Yunhai was fine, they didn't need to go.

"Aren't you going either?" Han Qi's uncle asked expressionlessly.

"Han Qi is back. My going there is just to protect him. Now that he's gone, it doesn't matter whether I go or not," Han Xian said indifferently.

Bang!!!

The next moment, Han Qi's uncle roared in fury, "Foolish!"

Han Xian was startled: "Big brother, are you crazy?"

Han Qi's uncle took a deep breath, calmed down, and explained:

"There's a young man in Yunhai named Jiang Tian who, in a short time, turned the entire Yunhai, no, even the entire Yunnan province, upside down. For this, headquarters repeatedly broke precedents to award him significant merit. The 'Six Merits King' previously reported in the news was him; he's said to have wiped out several criminal groups."

"As for Yunhai, the higher-ups were originally prepared to give up on development, but Jiang Tian's appearance gave them hope and showed them the potential development opportunities in Yunhai's future. I used so many political resources to send you to such important positions; what do you think this is for? If Yunhai develops in the future, it could very well become a directly administered municipality."

Boom!!!

Hearing this news, Han Xian's eyes widened, and his breathing became rapid.

His face was filled with disbelief as he looked at his elder brother, wondering if his brother had gone mad, or if he himself had. Even he had never heard of such a thing before.

Moreover, it was almost entirely impossible.

There were only three municipalities directly under the central government in the entire country, but upon closer inspection, each one was a top-tier city—a true super-first-tier city.

And it's the most prosperous and influential city.

A directly administered municipality.

How is that possible?

Yunhai is already a provincial capital, how could it still be a directly administered municipality?

"Actually, if it weren't for the special nature of my department and the chance to have access to these things, I wouldn't know about these matters. Recently, there have been proposals regarding this, and high-ranking officials are already making plans. In short, you only need to know one thing: Yunhai's future development is sky-high." Han Qi's uncle slowly revealed this shocking secret.

This caused Han Xian's expression to change continuously.

His face was filled with disbelief.

But even with disbelief, seeing his older brother's completely serious expression, Han Xian's face gradually turned red.

And he started breathing heavily.

"Now you understand why I invested so many resources, why I poured in such terrifying resources, even risking the future of the entire family, using all available resources—all for this moment. It's because of my special position; otherwise, how could I have known this news beforehand? If the news had spread, heh heh, would we even have a chance?" Han Qi's uncle continued.

In a directly administered city, officials are two ranks higher.

If provincial capitals are already like this, imagine how much more so in directly administered cities.

It's like you initially got into a vocational college, but then the school, through its own efforts, becomes a university before you graduate.

Wow, after graduation, you're directly issued a bachelor's degree.

And Yunhai is the same.

Although, if Yunhai becomes a directly administered city, the fourth in the country, the officials in Yunhai, while their initial ranks won't change,

it's like giving you a high position, but the rank isn't high enough.

But that's okay; once the position is high enough, the rank will be quickly filled.

In this situation, what does it represent in Yunhai?

On the surface, people just thought it was about using resources and achievements to gain prestige, but who would have thought there was such a huge opportunity involved?

"So, if this news gets out, you should understand what this means," Han Qi's uncle said.

At this moment,

Han Xian finally understood why his older brother had used all the family's resources, even at any cost, to get them there.

But it was too late to understand now.

"Otherwise, why do you think I used so many resources and connections to get that bureau chief in Yunhai, when he was still controversial? It was a pledge of loyalty. Jiang Peng is Jiang Tian's father. I thought that with this connection, Han Qi would maintain a good relationship with Jiang Tian after he went there, but now..." Han Qi's uncle's eyes were red.

No wonder he was in such a hurry.

When he heard that Han Qi had arrested Jiang Tian, ​​he almost went mad.

Because others don't know, but he knows it perfectly well!

Originally, he planned to wait until Han Qi went to Yunhai. Anyone with half a brain would know Jiang Tian was a powerful figure. He figured he could just associate with Jiang Tian, ​​and even though the main target was criminal organizations, those organizations would surely have protectors. He figured he could easily gain all the credit by associating with him.

However, his plan was far from reality.

Han Qi went there and immediately saw Jiang Tian as an opportunity to make his mark.

Foolish.

This is simply inhuman!

Han Qi's uncle, at this moment, truly felt like the sky had fallen!

His head was spinning.

"Brother, what do we do now?"



Chapter 428 Showing No Mercy, Completely Gone Mad


Having learned the whole story, Han Xian instantly realized the gravity of the situation.

It was far more serious than he had imagined.

In other words,

By relying on Jiang Tian, ​​he could have gained countless merits.

But he had chosen to use Jiang Tian as an opportunity to make his mark.

Isn't this utter nonsense?

A joke!

"What do I do? What do you expect me to do now?"

Han Qi's uncle slammed the phone to the ground.

His face was filled with rage.

Yes.

What could be done now?

There was absolutely no way.

"Can we still repair our relationship with Jiang Tian?" Han Xian understood Jiang Tian's importance.

Of course.

In reality, Jiang Tian was far from being exposed, and no one knew about him except for the criminal groups in Yunhai and the bigwigs in Beijing.

In fact, only certain forces knew about the specifics of what was happening in Yunhai, through frequent exceptions granted by headquarters.

Of course, nationwide, Yunhai has also solved quite a few major cases.

If one deliberately looks into it, one can learn a great deal about the merits Jiang Tian had previously accumulated.

Han Qi's uncle was just a step too late.

Just a step too late.

But what he never dreamed of was that Han Qi would be so foolish.

Doesn't he know that the recent changes in Yunhai are all caused by Jiang Tian, ​​and how important Jiang Tian is to Yunhai?

And that fool, doesn't he really understand?

No.

How could that be?

Therefore, in his view, the most important thing is that Han Qi knew Jiang Tian's influence, yet deliberately targeted him. What did he want to gain?

Isn't it just coveting Jiang Tian's credit?

The influence gained after taking down Jiang Tian would allow Han Qi to rise rapidly in Yunhai.

But.

This action is utterly ridiculous.

"Fool, utterly foolish." Han Qi's uncle grew angrier the more he thought about it.

His anger was escalating.

It was completely outrageous.

This was tantamount to ruining the entire family's future.

"At this point, I don't know if Han Qi can still stay in Yunhai, but if you go there, don't even think about continuing to target him," Han Qi's uncle warned worriedly.

"Brother, I know how serious this is."

Han Xian nodded, but they had no idea about the implications.

Or rather, while they had overestimated Jiang Tian in Beijing, they were completely unaware of a far more important issue besides arresting him.

That was a massive operation.

A massive operation targeting seven hundred criminals, involving thousands of military and police personnel, had been completely ruined by Han Qi's arrest.

The consequences of this were far more significant than the arrest of Jiang Tian.

So, at that moment, Han Qi's uncle considered for a moment, went to his study, and picked up the landline.

He dialed a number, and the call was quickly answered after a few rings.

"Old Huai, long time no see! When are you coming back to Beijing? Let's have a meal together," Han Qi's uncle said with a broad smile.

He seemed completely unaffected by what had just happened.

"Thank you, but I probably won't have time. Your nephew... the whole of Yunnan Province is in chaos right now. I really have to thank your nephew!"

The next moment...

Huai Sheng's calm voice, however, made Han Qi's uncle's heart skip a beat.

He had already prepared for the worst.

That was to use resources, at all costs. In his view, keeping Han Qi in Yunhai wouldn't be a problem.

It was just that his future prospects might be questionable.

These people thought it was just for prestige, but Han Qi's uncle clearly knew that Yunhai's future development was about securing a foothold there in advance. He would never leave, even if it meant death.

Because of the allure of being a directly administered city.

It was terrifying.

It's like, you went from a vocational college to a top-tier university (985 Project), scored 200 points and got in—the school practically lifted you up to stardom.

Over there, your future path is practically guaranteed.

In fact, Han Qi's uncle was already wondering if he could go there himself.

If he could go and work things out in Yunhai, it might very well become an opportunity for their family, perhaps even a path to rise to power.

But reality is cruel. Before he could even begin to dream, the outcome seemed imminent.

"Old Huai, it's just a kid being ignorant. You've met Han Qi, why bother with him? When you come to Beijing, I'll treat you to dinner, and we can discuss this in detail, okay?" Han Qi's uncle's expression was somewhat frozen, but he still hadn't given up.

Even though he had overestimated it, Han Qi's uncle didn't understand how many problems were involved.

"Heh, I wouldn't dare. A child? Do you think a child can escape legal responsibility? The entire Yunnan province has suffered so much because of Han Qi! His impulsive actions have had such a terrible impact on Yunnan! Did you think I was joking when I said it was a complete mess?" Huai Sheng's tone grew increasingly cold.

A thud.

Han Qi's uncle's mood sank.

His breathing became heavy.

"Isn't this making a mountain out of a molehill? It's just the arrest of Jiang Tian, ​​a misunderstanding, all a misunderstanding. I'll have Han Qi treat Jiang Tian to a meal so he can personally apologize." Han Qi's uncle continued.

But Huai Sheng laughed at this.

He held his phone.

He glanced around.

Huai Sheng answered the phone without any hesitation.

He even moved closer to Han Qi, phone in hand.

Looking at Han Qi's pale face, Huai Sheng sneered mercilessly, "Look at this call, whose connections are these? You keep talking about cracking down on networks of connections and protective umbrellas, but before you've even gotten into trouble yourself, a call from Beijing is already hitting me."

Boom!!!

A bombshell dropped.

Some things can be done, but absolutely cannot be said.

Because if you say them.

This has caused very serious problems.

The impact is too terrifying and too terrible. Unless it's a complete and utter fight to the death, everyone tries to leave a way out, especially those within the system.

Giving others convenience is also giving yourself convenience.

Who knows if they might one day fall into someone else's hands? Therefore, no one will act too decisively.

But now, Huai Sheng has practically ripped the lid off the whole thing.

He's showing absolutely no mercy.

The impact of this...

Everyone looked at Huai Sheng in disbelief.

Including the person on the phone.

He thought Huai Sheng would be more discreet after seeing his call, but he never expected him to say it so directly, and to rebuke him without any mercy.

This is practically humiliating them.

"Are you crazy?"



Chapter 429 Almost struck by lightning, tragic Han Qi


Accompanied by Huai Sheng's voice,

the room fell almost completely silent. All eyes turned to Han Qi, each face bearing a cold smile.

A chill ran through him.

At that moment, Han Qi felt an icy coldness.

Even a fool would sense that this was an attempt to kill him.

Why?

All he did was offend Jiang Tian.

Why?

He hadn't committed any major wrongdoing!

Huai Sheng's blatant sarcasm made Han Qi blush and want to retort, "I did nothing wrong, Jiang Tian is a black sheep."

"How dare you."

"Still being stubborn."

"Well, do you know how much trouble you've caused today?"

"Still unrepentant."

"Arrest him immediately."

Some of the leaders who came with him immediately looked at Han Qi with disgust.

That's how it is.

Although Jiang Tian inevitably acts arrogantly and domineeringly, offending many people, and even having grievances against many leaders,

it cannot be denied that they have also benefited from Jiang Tian's contributions to Yunhai.

In this situation, it's a alliance of mutual beneficiaries, and this relationship will continue until Jiang Tian can no longer bring them benefits. At that point, many might turn against Jiang Tian.

But that outcome...

Jiang Tian understands his predicament, but he's confident in himself.

While many dislike Jiang Tian, ​​they genuinely admire his actions.

The dislike stems from the fact that while Jiang Tian has solved many cases and dealt devastating blows to numerous criminal groups, this doesn't equate to social stability. On the contrary, the collapse of the Wenye Group has left many leaders in a state of utter chaos.

Economic problems have arisen, and more importantly, illegal and criminal activities have proliferated.

With the ringleaders gone, the underlings, unrestrained, have gone wild.

Some, initially less powerful, have seized the opportunity of the larger groups' collapse to aggressively seize territory.

Thanks to the "Young and Dangerous" phenomenon, they are now heavily influenced by it.

Problems have arisen in society.

This is a clash of ideologies. However, without proving Jiang Tian wrong, if he can bring positive change to Yunhai, these people's perceptions will quickly improve.

Even at its worst, it's just an ideological conflict.

But Han Qi's actions are no less than a slap in the face to Yunhai.

What? Just because you're from above, you think you can act so recklessly? Who gave you the right? Who gave you the guts?

If it were a one-on-one fight, they might not offend Han Qi.

But now Han Qi has incurred public wrath.

These people are condemning Han Qi with vehement anger.

They're cursing Han Qi as a black sheep, a scourge that came to Yunhai.

And Han Qi's mind is blank, completely empty.

"It's hopeless. Yunhai is hopeless."

After regaining his senses, Han Qi felt a chill run through him, but he didn't think he was wrong. On the contrary, the more he felt this way, the more it proved the power behind the scenes, the stronger the protective umbrella must be.

It also proved that Yunhai was completely rotten.

The more he thought about it, the more resolute Han Qi's gaze became.

On the other end of the phone, Han Qi's uncle's face turned pale and then red.

"Are you crazy, Lao Huai? We don't have any deep-seated hatred, do we? It's just that the kids were being mischievous and made some mistakes. But nobody's perfect. In this position, you shouldn't be unaware that while this case-handling procedure does have problems, everyone does it this way. Why are you being so aggressive? Han Qi has been acting in the public interest; why are you pressing him so hard?"

Han Qi's uncle truly couldn't understand.

Lao Huai, why did you do this? Even if they didn't have a deep friendship, they had at least once maintained a gentlemanly relationship, and both sides had been satisfied with their interactions.

We'd shared a dorm room for a long time, so there was some lingering affection between us. Why was you, Huai, so ruthless, so determined to offend him?

It was clearly a matter of mutual understanding and compromise, but Han Qi's uncle obviously didn't realize the gravity of the situation.

"Heh, you're relentlessly pressuring me. Let me tell you, your nephew is finished. Still thinking about staying on? Dream on. Do you know the impact he's had on the entire Yunnan province today? Minister Tan personally came to Yunhai to direct this massive operation, and the most crucial figure in that operation was Xiao Jiang. Let me tell you, because of your nephew, this massive operation mobilizing thousands of people across Yunnan province has become a joke, causing irreparable damage and a terrible impact on society and the people," Huai Sheng said with a cold laugh.

If Minister Tan hadn't been here, perhaps Huai Sheng wouldn't have gone so far.

But Huai Sheng was here, and Jiang Tian had suffered such a great injustice. He had to give Jiang Tian an explanation. If he didn't handle the aftermath properly, and Minister Tan and Jiang Tian were both dissatisfied...

The entire Yunnan province will probably become a laughing stock.

Even higher-ups might be furious if things don't go well.

The more he thought about it, the angrier Huai Sheng became.

Everything was fine, the direction was right, but it's all because of this idiot.

"What?"

With a crash.

Han Qi's uncle stood up abruptly, his eyes wide and pupils contracting. He had thought he'd misheard when he heard Minister Tan's voice, but it was real.

Most importantly.

They'd already arrested the wrong person, and not only was the person a hero, but they'd also slandered a hero.

Furthermore, by slandering the arrest of a hero, causing a terrible impact, and this hero being a key figure in a major case, the consequences were even worse.

The most terrifying thing was that Minister Tan was present the entire time.

Bang!

Having figured all this out, Han Qi's uncle slumped weakly onto the sofa.

The next moment.

It wasn't over yet.

Han Qi's expression had completely frozen.

He said,

Why make such a big fuss over arresting Jiang Tian? Not only did the provincial governor come, but even Minister Tan came.

The case is solved, completely solved.

"You're still not convinced? Then let me tell you, because of you, the current negative impact has delayed the operation of thousands of military and police personnel by a whole day, causing incalculable consequences," Minister Tan said expressionlessly.

At the same time, Minister Tan felt ashamed as he spoke.

Ashamed of Jiang Tian.

What a great man he was! Just now, because of this brat, he had a fleeting moment of doubt about Jiang Tian.

Of course, this is for the best.

Because in this way, Jiang Tian will become even greater.

Indeed, outstanding people only become more outstanding at crucial moments; how could they possibly collapse?

"Impossible, this is impossible," Han Qi took two steps back.



Chapter 430 Final Handling: Still Want to Go Back? Dream on!


Then Han Qi turned his gaze to Ding Xiaoxiao. It was undeniable that Ding Xiaoxiao was Han Qi's most steadfast supporter and a staunch defender of justice.

She had a preconceived notion, because Ding Xiaoxiao remembered Han Qi saying before that the bigger the commotion, the stronger the protection behind Jiang Tian.

Especially Jiang Tian's records—who knew if they were true or false? What if Huai Province was also a protector?

The more Ding Xiaoxiao thought about it, the more alarmed she became.

Of course, if there was one mistake they made, it might be arresting Jiang Tian when he had an important mission—something they didn't know about.

But was it their fault? After being arrested, it was Jiang Tian's fault for not revealing anything. If he had, could they have forcibly detained him?

Even if he had, they would have still taken Jiang Tian in regardless, involved him in the operation planning, and then arrested him.

At this moment, Ding Xiaoxiao glanced at the briefcase. Upon seeing the photos, her eyes lit up instantly, as if she had found a glimmer of hope. She grabbed the briefcase and pulled out a stack of photos.

These included pictures of Jiang Tian having dinner with several policewomen, including Yu Hui from the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

There were also photos of Jiang Tian and Mu Ya.

Even more so, there were photos of Jiang Tian and Gao Yangyang, which, while relatively intimate, didn't prove anything, but served as their hope for a comeback.

They hadn't originally intended to use them as evidence, but now they had no choice.

"We're targeting Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep, not just for those photos, but also because he has intimate relationships with many women, is morally corrupt, and has a close relationship with a woman named Mu Ya..."

Old Mu's face darkened behind him.

But it wasn't over yet.

"Later, he went out to dinner with three female police officers from the Municipal Bureau in the early hours of the morning, behaving intimately with each other. Not only that, but Chief Gao's daughter was also involved. The Anti-Corruption Bureau even assisted in arresting Gao Yangyang. They must have solid evidence..."

BOOM!!!

The scene erupted.

The police system was in an uproar.

Chief Gao's face turned ashen.

Old Mu's expression was incredibly complex.

Even Jiang Tian was dumbfounded.

Good heavens.

Is this a self-destructive bomb?

If you have solid evidence, that's one thing, but just a close relationship? The key is, who's involved?

Regardless of whether it's true or not, the police system isn't looking good. These leaders' expressions are definitely not good, after all, the innocence of three female police officers is involved, and Old Mu's daughter is even more interesting.

After all, she's Jiang Tian's current girlfriend, practically his girlfriend.

There's nothing more to say.

Gao Yangyang's arrest was also due to an accident.

But you even arrested Gao Yangyang?

Is this self-destruction?

A secret investigation is one thing, but to bring it to her biological father's door—what will happen to this young girl's reputation?

Besides, it's slander without any real evidence.

Sure enough...

*Slap!*

Before Ding Xiaoxiao could finish speaking, a loud slap rang out.

Ding Xiaoxiao's face was visibly swollen and red.

Incredulous.

This slap made Ding Xiaoxiao's eyes widen; even her gold-rimmed glasses were shattered.

The pain on her face seemed to bring Ding Xiaoxiao back to her senses.

She looked at Minister Tan, whose hand had just landed on her.

Her chest heaved.

She was met with Minister Tan's face, now contorted with rage and a cold smile, and his furious roar: "Outrageous! Lawless! Are you trying to rebel? Making baseless accusations, arresting people without any evidence! Is there no law? Is the power entrusted to you by the Party and the people just for you to act so recklessly?"

It exploded.

It completely exploded.

Even Han Qi exploded.

He pulled out so many documents and pieces of evidence from his briefcase, but he didn't mention the photos at all.

Because, even if there were concrete evidence, in this kind of relationship—both unmarried—it could only be used for moral condemnation, and could be icing on the cake during a conviction, but it could never be decisive evidence, and no one had ever been brought down with such evidence.

Moreover, this involves the fact that the initial plan was to use evidence of embezzlement and mutual benefit between Gao Yangyang and Jiang Tian to force Gao Xiaolong to abandon Jiang Tian.

But now, with all this useless evidence, presenting it under these circumstances is tantamount to self-destruction.

It gives the impression that they are fabricating lies, acting recklessly and even lawlessly, resorting to any means necessary to slander.

This is simply self-destruction.

Han Qi's face was ashen.

It's over.

Han Qi knew he had no chance whatsoever.

He hadn't expected Ding Xiaoxiao to be so straightforward, not knowing what was acceptable and unacceptable.

The crucial point is that she's implicated him; he might not be able to stay in Yunhai anymore.

The chance for the family's revival is gone.

Han Qi's face was pale. How could he explain this to his uncle?

Sure enough, in the next moment...

Huai Sheng's voice rang out: "This incident fully demonstrates one thing: certain departments and comrades, abusing their power, act recklessly and unscrupulously. They even use their power to frame influential figures in order to gain merit and attention. The lawlessness has reached this point. This is due to our own inadequate supervision. We will hold a meeting immediately tomorrow, especially to address this issue and come up with a reasonable solution."

Huai Sheng's secretary immediately took notes.

Huai Sheng paused only slightly, glanced at Minister Tan, and seeing Minister Tan's silent demeanor after slapping Ding Xiaoxiao, Huai Sheng understood what he meant.

He immediately sighed and said, "Regarding the Yunhai Discipline Inspection Commission, such a major incident occurred today. As a leader, failing to detect and correct it in time, Fang Jue will receive a major demerit within the Party and be temporarily placed on probation. Any problems that arise will be dealt with accordingly. Secretary Zhao, you have no objections, right?"

After Huai Sheng finished speaking, he looked at Zhou Zhao, who hurriedly nodded, "No, no objections."

Hearing that it was only this punishment, Fang Jue's face lit up with joy.

He knew the impact of this incident and thought he was completely finished.

But unexpectedly, there was still a chance.

Of course.

The final result was also because he was indeed innocent.

Taking this matter down immediately would be excessive, especially given the current turmoil in Yunhai; a hasty decision would only create more chaos.

"Regarding Qi Huan, arrest him immediately, interrogate him rigorously, and place him under double regulation," Huai Sheng ordered ruthlessly.

Qi Huan slumped onto the sofa, completely exhausted.

"As for you..." Huai Sheng looked at Han Qi.

Han Qi sighed, "Sir, I know I made a mistake. Don't worry, I'll apply to go back. I won't stay in Yunhai."

Han Qi was stunned.

He knew Yunhai was a treacherous place, but he hadn't expected it to collapse on only three days after arriving.

However, looking at the smile on Huai Sheng's face, he suddenly had a bad feeling.

"Go back? Are you dreaming?"



Chapter 431 Touching the Red Line, You Only Have One Path: Death


Or rather, in Han Qi's eyes, almost everyone's gaze towards him at this moment was different.

Especially after he had spoken those words.

What else did these people want?

Han Qi's face darkened.

He needed to know the immense price he had paid to come to Yunhai, the immense price his family had paid, and for it all, it had been utterly wasted.

Heaven knew the immense pain Han Qi had endured, that because of him, the hopes of his entire family had been extinguished.

But this wasn't his fault; it was Jiang Tian's fault for hiding it too well, for hiding it too thoroughly.

The protective umbrella behind this black sheep was terrifying.

Yes, in Han Qi's mind, Jiang Tian was already a corrupt official, a petty official with enormous greed. Or rather, with this prejudice from the very beginning, no matter how he looked at it, he believed Jiang Tian was a black sheep.

Even at this point.

Han Qi still believed he was not wrong; the world was wrong.

Yunhai was wrong.

Yunhai's problems were far more terrifying than he had imagined. If there was one thing he hadn't considered, it was that he hadn't expected Jiang Tian to be able to embezzle so perfectly.

What? He made over a billion yuan in college, and instead of going into business, he wanted to join the police force?

Putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, Han Qi knew he could never do that. How could there be such a great person in this world, giving up a perfectly good billionaire career to become a lowly anti-drug police officer?

From this, it was clear that Jiang Tian had a truly enormous problem.

He himself couldn't do it, and he didn't believe anyone else could.

But...

This was just the beginning.

The next moment.

Minister Tan took a step forward.

Han Qi instinctively stepped back.

Minister Tan, accompanied by his secretary, simply walked past him without even glancing at him.

He trusted that Huai Province would give him a satisfactory explanation.

After all, this was a problem on the Yunnan provincial level, and with Huai Province present, Minister Tan couldn't be too forceful.

Sure enough.

Huai Province didn't disappoint: "Han Qi, because of your reckless actions, which have caused significant losses to the lives and property of the Party and the people, and have also had a huge negative impact on society, you are hereby formally punished as follows: expelled from the Party and placed under investigation."

Boom!!!

The scene fell silent.

Silence reigned.

Deathly silence.

Not a sound could be heard.

Regardless, Han Qi had connections and influence, and he came from the capital, yet now he was shown absolutely no leniency.

Of course, the local leaders of Yunhai, understanding the specific circumstances and the dire consequences, had largely anticipated this outcome, but their predictions were one thing.

This handling was still astonishing.

Because it meant Han Qi was completely finished.

Han Qi's political career, from this moment on, was declared over.

If Han Qi was truly clean, then it was the end of his political career; but if Han Qi was not clean, or had other problems, then Han Qi's life was utterly over.

Of course, there was another reason for this outcome.

From the moment Ding Xiaoxiao produced those photos and began with various unfounded speculations, it meant this wasn't just a problem in Yunhai and Yunnan Province; it was an insult to the entire police system.

They have inspectors; what problem could they possibly have that required your interference?

Even if you had the right to interfere, at least you should have presented a solid chain of evidence to make it plausible. Instead, you presented nothing but speculation, lacking any concrete evidence, and even further glorified Jiang Tian.

And that wasn't all; in the end, you even arrested Gao Yangyang.

Although it was the anti-corruption team that arrested her, it was still a collaboration between you.

This was the final straw.

It directly caused Han Qi, even with immense connections, to fall into complete ruin from this point on.

You can only say he was too young.

Jiang Tian wasn't surprised. From the moment Ding Xiaoxiao falsely accused him of having an affair, it meant these people were completely finished.

Even Fang Jue, who seems unmoved for now, is only temporarily so. The situation in Yunhai is too volatile right now; it's just not the right time yet. Once things stabilize slightly, he'll be the first to be eliminated.

This is what they call reaping what you sow.

Or perhaps, he's too politically naive.

Han Qi is completely dumbfounded at this moment.

He understands that if this really happens, he's finished. Returning to the capital might be the opposite; he might not even be able to get into a private room.

"Wait," Han Qi said hastily, backing away.

Panic. Han Qi is panicking now.

The next moment, two armed police officers squeezed in, one on each side, and immediately subdued Han Qi.

“You shouldn’t hold any hope. The mistake you made is too serious. No matter who’s behind you, or how powerful your connections are, you’d better not have any ideas, because it won’t help. Any illegal or disorderly conduct is absolutely unacceptable in Yunhai. No one is exempt.” Huai Sheng directly shattered all his hopes.

What a joke.

Is Jiang Tian just a kept man?

Does he know how many big shots in Beijing are watching Jiang Tian? Standing behind Jiang Tian, ​​even if you’re already past your prime, or even at the height of your power, Huai Sheng would dare to stand up to you at this point.

Of course, at this point, Huai Sheng had no choice but to step forward.

Because he didn’t know if the big shots in Beijing would find out about today’s events. If Minister Tan was dissatisfied and reported it directly, Yunhai would have made such a huge fool of itself, and at the very least, Huai Sheng himself would bear enormous responsibility.

“No, I’m not convinced.” Han Qi was still struggling.

*Snap!*

The next moment, Han Qi's arms were handcuffed behind his back.

Two armed police officers mercilessly pried at his arms, the excruciating pain almost making Han Qi scream.

"You're not satisfied?" Huai Sheng said expressionlessly, "You can appeal to anyone or any department if you're dissatisfied, but now, I'm telling you, in this Yunnan province, no one can rewrite this decision, because you've crossed the Party's red line."

Han Qi's eyes turned red at those last words.

His mind went completely blank.

Or rather, he wondered if Huai Sheng had gone mad.

Just because he arrested Jiang Tian, ​​he had crossed the Party's red line?

The more Han Qi thought about it, the more dizzy he became.

The more he thought about it, the more his whole body trembled.

Because he carried the hopes of his entire family; he couldn't just die like this!

If he died, all the enormous, terrifying sacrifices his family had made.

Even the near-total expenditure of political resources, all to help him achieve something in Yunhai—all of it would be for nothing.

The crucial point was that he was now the only one left in the three generations of the Han family!

If he died, it meant the family's demise; he absolutely could not accept that outcome.

What he found most unacceptable was that Jiang Tian, ​​the black sheep, the biggest cancer in his eyes, was being praised by everyone.

While he had fallen into the abyss, Jiang Tian remained high and mighty.



Chapter 432 Explosion and Medal Award: Changing History


He didn't realize that what he couldn't accept most wasn't the loss of his family's hope, but the utter trampling of his pride.

The contrast between Jiang Tian's lofty position and his own fall from grace was what he couldn't bear.

But unfortunately.

No matter how he acted, no one was looking at him anymore.

It's over.

The sky has fallen.

Han Qi glanced around.

At this moment.

Everyone's attention had shifted from him, or rather, no one was looking at him anymore.

That kind of indifference.

Or rather, Han Qi was completely finished from this moment on, and these leaders all knew it.

In his place was Jiang Tian.

There were even rumors circulating that Minister Tan had come for something.

Thinking this, many leaders looked at Jiang Tian with fervent eyes.

"Jiang Tian..."

Han Qi's eyes were bloodshot, his hands clenched tightly.

He twisted his head forcefully, his eyes bloodshot as he stared intently at Jiang Tian, ​​as if trying to imprint him into his mind.

Evil cannot prevail over good.

Han Qi believed that one day he would be proven right; he had done nothing wrong.

The only mistake was that the sky over Yunhai was too dark.

Even Minister Tan had been deceived. If Jiang Tian's true colors were fully revealed, Minister Tan would surely know.

Han Qi's mind raced wildly. Had he lost? No, he hadn't. As long as Jiang Tian had a problem, he couldn't possibly lose.

But then, in the next moment...

Click!!!

Minister Tan stepped forward, Fang Jue already having obtained the key, and ran up to unlock Jiang Tian's handcuffs.

"Little Jiang, have you been wronged?"

Unlike his attitude towards Han Qi, Minister Tan's demeanor towards Jiang Tian was terrifyingly different.

He spoke with a gentle expression.

Even with a hint of reassurance.

"It's alright." Jiang Tian glanced at Han Qi.

He was extremely sensitive to others' attention, or rather, his six senses were incredibly sharp. He could even sense bullets, let alone such a fixed gaze. Jiang Tian even suspected that if eyes could kill, Han Qi would have killed him countless times already.

Actually, Jiang Tian had another major question lingering in his mind.

He was certain that Han Qi's arrest of him wasn't because he had done anything wrong, but because this person harbored a deep prejudice against him.

It wasn't simply because he had hit Han Qi before; from the moment they started eating, this person had a grudge against him.

"What, are you dissatisfied with this outcome? You can tell us what your needs are, and we'll try our best to meet them." Huai Sheng stepped forward. At this point, Huai Sheng also had to make a statement.

"I obey the organization's arrangements." Jiang Tian's political talent, if he wanted to, could be maxed out.

Putting aside everything else, his status as a master detective had given him exceptional observation skills and logical thinking.

With both his intelligence and emotional intelligence nearly unparalleled, many times, Jiang Tian simply chose not to act that way.

At this moment, it's absolutely crucial not to voice any objections or make excessive demands, otherwise, it will be seen as pushing their luck.

What was originally a matter of principle, once brought up, would only make it more unpleasant.

As he spoke, Jiang Tian glanced at Han Qi out of the corner of his eye and saw him visibly relax.

Jiang Tian simply gave a cold laugh.

"Do they really think he's a nice guy?!"

Being treated like a little idiot was more unjust and humiliating than anything Jiang Tian had ever experienced in his past life.

"Jiang Tian."

Before Jiang Tian could even react,

Minister Tan's voice suddenly rang out.

Jiang Tian instinctively stood at attention: "Present."

Minister Tan looked at Jiang Tian's expression with considerable satisfaction.

The environment of this private room was indeed quite good. Although it was a one-bedroom apartment, the living room was very spacious, nearly a hundred square meters, to facilitate interrogation and questioning. This was far superior to the decor of a hotel.

After all, this was the final stop for a significant portion of the system's personnel, and the humanistic care was quite thorough.

Fortunately, it was over a hundred square meters, so although there were many people in the room, it wasn't too crowded.

A considerable number of armed police officers were also waiting outside.

"Sir, what should we do?"

Ding Xiaoxiao's face was pale.

In fact, Ding Xiaoxiao wasn't dealt with on the spot. After all, what level is Huai Province? Good heavens, a mere clerk, not even a section-level official, at most a sub-section-level officer. No matter how much Huai Province values ​​her, it's impossible for a mere high-ranking official from Yun Province to handle the resignation and punishment of a sub-section-level officer, right?

Isn't that absurd?

Even the fact that Han Qi was directly dealt with was because Han Qi had complicated connections, and there were calls from people in Beijing afterward.

Otherwise, this matter would have required Zhou Zhao to step in and handle it; his final decision would have been enough.

Of course, not being punished now doesn't mean Ding Xiaoxiao is off the hook. As an accomplice, Ding Xiaoxiao is undeniably culpable.

However, because of her low rank, and because if Ding Xiaoxiao truly had no problems, the final outcome would likely be expulsion from the Party and removal from the ranks.

Right now, Ding Xiaoxiao is relatively free.

She simply can't believe what she's seeing, especially since she's just been slapped by a high-ranking official.

"Xiaoxiao, can I trust you?" Han Qi still held onto hope.

"Yes," Ding Xiaoxiao nodded firmly.

"Reorganize all the information we've collected and send it to Beijing."

Even at this critical juncture, Han Qi retained his rationality. He understood that his only way to be rescued was to launch a complete counterattack.

Finding evidence of Jiang Tian's crimes, concrete evidence that definitively proves them.

Where did those nearly ten billion yuan come from? Han Qi absolutely refused to believe Jiang Tian earned it himself; it was more likely he had falsified accounts.

And Han Qi had no way to manipulate this matter. Now, he could only rely on his family.

If Jiang Tian fell, it would prove he was innocent.

Even being framed might bring him considerable political compensation.

"Let you be happy for a few days. This black sheep will eventually fall under the sword of justice." Han Qi gritted his teeth, trying hard not to look at Jiang Tian's triumphant appearance.

However, even if he didn't want to, Minister Tan's loud voice still reached his ears.

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​in Yanjiang, that forbidden zone of life, when Secretary Mu and the daughter of Tianxing Group fell into the water, disregarded his own safety and risked his life to save the Party and the country from enormous losses. After multiple votes at headquarters meetings, it was finally decided to award Comrade Jiang Tian the First-Class Medal of Merit."

Boom!!!

It exploded.

Han Qi was stunned.

His eyes were almost bleeding.

But it wasn't over yet.

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​in the past, demonstrated outstanding performance, was courageous in sacrifice, and made immeasurable contributions. His merits are almost unparalleled. After the final vote and after meetings of leaders at all levels, it was decided to award Comrade Jiang Tian the title of First-Class Hero."

Boom!!!



Chapter 433 Six First-Class Merits, Creating History


Six First-Class Merits.

Silence.

Those who knew Jiang Tian knew at this moment how many First-Class Merits he had accumulated. That's right, six.

Not only six First-Class Merits, but also one Special-Class Merit.

Besides that, the most terrifying thing was being selected as a First-Class Hero.

What does First-Class Hero mean? It can be said that in this society, in this era, very few people are alive enough to be selected as First-Class Heroes.

In fact, Jiang Tian understood another layer of meaning.

That is, First-Class Heroes are meant to be role models, even objects of learning and goals for many members of the Party.

Now, the question arises.

Jiang Tian belongs to a classified unit, specifically the anti-drug police force. This profession is classified, including all the merits Jiang Tian has previously accumulated.

In reality, only local leaders in Yunhai, including some bigwigs in Beijing, really know about Jiang Tian. Outside of Yunhai and Yunnan Province, very few people know anything about him.

If it were anyone else, their name would probably be everywhere by now.

They would be role models for the new era.

But Jiang Tian's position is rather special.

Of course.

Recently, a list of those who received commendations has been released, including those who sacrificed their lives, in which case their names could be published directly.

Anti-drug police officer.

If someone has sacrificed their life, even if their name is exposed, it doesn't really matter, because the person is gone, and no criminal would maliciously retaliate against their family.

But on the other hand, if someone hasn't sacrificed their life, but their name is exposed, that's a problem. It means you're still alive, especially with your name exposed, so sorry, many opposition groups will be targeting you.

This is a huge threat to both the person and their family.

And at the same time.

First-class hero.

This isn't just something the organization needs to learn from and emulate; it might even be announced on television, during prime-time news broadcasts.

What does that mean?

Jiang Tian himself is part of the classifieds.

Especially for first-class heroes, most are quite old, even if they're still alive, they're definitely over forty or fifty, and they've done many positive and absolutely honorable things. Everything they've done since joining the organization is traceable.

Even when awarding merit, their actions and impact are evaluated; the selection criteria are extremely strict, and a final investigation and review are conducted.

After all, if you have some problems, and they award you a first-class hero title, but later it's discovered you have issues, wouldn't that be a slap in the face?

Jiang Tian couldn't help but think, "I know the higher-ups have the idea of ​​awarding me a first-class hero title, but will the subsequent impact of doing so directly put me in the spotlight, or will they keep my name hidden?"

But is hiding my name even meaningful now?

Jiang Tian wasn't just boasting. In the entire Yunhai criminal underworld, anyone with even a little influence might not know who the police chief was, but they were definitely familiar with the name Jiang Tian.

What a joke.

He's taken down so many criminal gangs.

And he's racked up first-class merit awards one after another.

These criminal gangs aren't blind; they all have informants. While protective umbrellas are detestable, this problem can't be completely eradicated.

Ultimately, there are always some who can't uphold their principles and ultimately fall into depravity.

This information, once leaked even a little, is practically out in the open.

It was even leaked when Jiang Tian first performed meritorious service; that Mr. Yun had even arranged to use TNT to blow him up.

The subsequent actions of those criminal gangs have even led to retaliation against his family.

In short, Jiang Tian was mentally prepared for all of this.

In fact, whether he was exposed or not didn't matter to Jiang Tian.

Even if he were exposed, it might not be a bad thing; at least, Jiang Tian's invincible reputation would be established.

Therefore, what Jiang Tian is most curious about now is whether or not to keep his status as a first-class hero a secret.

"What's wrong? Is there a problem?" Minister Tan seemed to know what Jiang Tian was thinking and asked with a smile.
