"A first-class hero... will the name be announced, or will it remain a secret?" Jiang Tian scratched his head.

After all, Jiang Tian needed to be prepared beforehand if the name was revealed.

"Is there a difference?" Minister Tan countered.

This made Jiang Tian's lips twitch; it was indeed completely unnecessary.

Because, putting everything else aside, Jiang Tian looked at the already packed crowd behind Minister Tan, and this talk of secrecy? What kind of secrecy was this?!

“However,” Minister Tan paused slightly.

“The higher-ups have considered the issues. Exposure would indeed have some impact on you. For a narcotics officer, being in the spotlight is far too dangerous. The retaliation from drug dealers is ruthless. There are still some people who don't know you. If everyone knew, you'd be in too much danger. Therefore, for the time being, the decision has been made not to make it public,” Minister Tan explained.

Jiang Tian nodded in understanding.

In truth, he didn't care. He'd already given up, so it didn't matter anymore.

It was just that his superiors were concerned for his safety, which Jiang Tian understood.

As he spoke,

Minister Tan's secretary came up with a tray.

Soon, Minister Tan opened a box.

From it, he took out a First-Class Merit Medal.

The gleaming gold was pinned to Jiang Tian's chest.

It was dazzling.

Without pausing, he opened a second box and took out a First-Class Hero Medal to pin on Jiang Tian.

There were only two.

But each one felt incredibly heavy.

The sheer weight of it made Han Qi's eyes widen in disbelief.

The First-Class Merit Medal already made Han Qi's eyes almost explode with jealousy. Why? Why did Jiang Tian deserve it?

Why did this black sheep deserve a First-Class Merit Medal?

But after hearing Han Qi say that he had saved Old Master Mu, did Old Master Mu and Mu Ya have some kind of connection?

Han Qi's expression stiffened, because he suddenly remembered that the political and legal leader of Yunnan Province seemed to be Old Master Mu.

And what about Mu Ya, who had a close relationship with Jiang Tian?

Nobody would believe they weren't connected.

So, this was Jiang Tian who had gotten involved with Old Master Mu's daughter, even saving Old Master Mu, and incidentally rescuing the only daughter of the CEO of Tianxing Group, currently the most famous and largest development group in the country.

What incredible luck!

Fine, fine, damn it, pure luck! But why is he eligible for the title of First-Class Hero? Is this reasonable?

"Comrade Jiang has made outstanding contributions to domestic stability in the past, especially in the recent operation in Myanmar, where he risked his life to save dozens, even hundreds, of our comrades at the last minute. This First-Class Hero title is well-deserved. Initially, we considered Comrade Jiang too young, but after several discussions, if such significant contributions don't qualify, then who in the country is?"

That's right.

Jiang Tian is young, simply put, too young.

In fact, forcing growth isn't a good thing.

Besides, the saying "the tallest tree catches the most wind" isn't a good thing within the system.

Jiang Tian is too outstanding right now.

Whether for protection or other reasons, being low-key is actually a good thing.

But that's the problem.

Jiang Tian has made such a great contribution; if he's deemed unqualified for the selection due to his age, then what about those who were selected?

We can't discriminate against Jiang Tian just because he's young.



Chapter 434 Unbelievable! Everyone was stunned. Are you here to buy in bulk?


Jiang Tian's name appeared and disappeared from the list three or four times, ultimately due to the immense controversy surrounding it. The title of First-Class Heroic Model carries far greater influence than the First-Class Merit Citation.

Second only to the long-absent Special-Class Merit Citation.

Or rather, the Special-Class Merit Citation is so difficult to obtain that almost no one can achieve it in peacetime; the First-Class Heroic Model is essentially a scaled-down substitute for the Special-Class Merit Citation.

Jiang Tian had already received a Special-Class Merit Citation once, but the official propaganda at the time stated that it was awarded to a heroic police officer.

However, in 2003, the internet was not yet particularly developed.

Otherwise, it would likely have caused a huge uproar online.

Even so, the news only briefly mentioned it, yet it still sparked a frenzy of discussion on various forums.

Because the Special-Class Merit Citation is incredibly rare and difficult to obtain.

The reappearance of the Special-Class Merit Citation, unseen for decades, is nothing short of astonishing.

It's just a pity.

The secrecy was so tight that many people, even if they wanted to know, couldn't find out a single clue.

Only a portion of the criminal gang in Yunhai suspected it might be Jiang Tian.

However, they weren't certain.

Because the awarding of medals back then was completely confidential.

But now it's semi-public.

Having already received a Special Merit, being awarded the title of First-Class Heroic Model again in such a short period, and even receiving two First-Class Merits.

While the merits were sufficient.

The interval was too short, and Jiang Tian was too young, leading to considerable controversy. Ultimately, if a high-ranking leader hadn't intervened, Jiang Tian probably wouldn't have gotten in.

At this moment, Minister Tan approached Jiang Tian and whispered, "A high-ranking leader asked me to pass on a message to you—it's pressure, but also motivation; it's an encouragement, but also support."

A high-ranking leader?

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian's eyes flashed sharply.

Even Minister Tan mentioned a high-ranking leader—who was that?

What did it mean?

Jiang Tian didn't even dare to imagine it, but soon he realized that the things he'd done in Yunhai were unlikely to go unnoticed by the high-ranking officials, even if they wanted to.

Because it had caused such a stir.

But even so, his level shouldn't have caused such a sensation.

Unless…

A thought struck Jiang Tian.

Yunhai was far too important, or rather, it desperately needed a reform pioneer like Jiang Tian.

If Jiang Tian could do things well, Yunhai might very well become the third super-first-tier city in the country.

After all, Yunhai was close to various countries in Southeast China, possessing a natural geographical advantage. Although it was mostly mountainous, this terrain was suitable for development.

If it could become the economic center of Southeast China, it would be more than enough to support a complete transformation of Yunhai, making it a shining pearl of Southeast China.

The more he thought about it, the more eager Jiang Tian's eyes became.

All of this depended on one condition: the emergence of corrupt officials.

And even more importantly, the unchecked growth of rampant criminal groups.

Jiang Tian suddenly understood.

But at that moment, the room was in complete chaos.

Accompanied by Minister Tan's announcement:

"First-class hero, first-class merit?"

"Wow, so young and already a first-class hero, and a first-class merit award too! His future is incredible..."

"Heh, is a first-class merit award so rare? Comrade Xiao Tian has already received multiple first-class merit awards. For us, a first-class merit award is unattainable, but for Comrade Xiao Jiang, it's just one of many."

"Incredible! No wonder Comrade Xiao Jiang is so calm. If it were me, I'd probably be ecstatic. Comrade Xiao Jiang is still so composed; he's already used to it."

"More than that. As far as I know, he's received several recently, and he even has an Anti-Drug Vanguard Medal."

Numb.

Hearing the various compliments directed at Jiang Tian, ​​Han Qi was completely numb.

Multiple first-class merit awards?

Han Qi couldn't deny that his eyes reddened.

What does a First-Class Merit Citation represent?

It represents a bright future.

It represents a smooth and successful future.

After receiving a First-Class Merit Citation, your path will be completely different. In all promotion opportunities, you will have no rivals; you'll be promoted directly upon reaching the required years of service.

Who can surpass you in promotion with a First-Class Merit Citation?

If one First-Class Merit Citation is so significant, what about multiple First-Class Merit Citations?

At this moment, Han Qi felt a chill run through his entire body.

Because even now...

He suddenly realized something terrifying: if Jiang Tian was truly innocent, then he would have no chance of turning things around.

The greater Jiang Tian's reputation, the more dangerous his situation would become.

He regretted it.

Han Qi suddenly felt a pang of regret.

Just then,

After a slight pause, Minister Tan's voice rang out again: "Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​since joining the force, has been outstanding in his work, loyal to the people, and has made remarkable contributions. He has dismantled countless criminal gangs and repeatedly ventured into dangerous situations, achieving immense merit each time."

Minister Tan paused slightly at this point.

Looking at the gazes of the crowd, Minister Tan's lips curled into a slight smile.

Originally, Minister Tan had intended to personally present the award at a significant occasion.

However,

Plans can't always keep up with changes.

Today, while Jiang Tian was being slandered, he would let everyone see, let these leaders witness, Jiang Tian's achievements.

In the future, anyone who tries to harm Jiang Tian, ​​especially by fabricating charges, should think twice before facing the wrath of the police system.

"Recruiting talent without being bound by convention, and promoting younger cadres, are principles repeatedly emphasized by our Party. Seniority-based decisions are a thing of the past. Although Comrade Jiang Tian is young, his contributions are already beyond what many can achieve in a lifetime."

"Especially recently, Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​with his keen intuition and extraordinary action, discovered the massive criminal gang, the Wenye Group, that had been operating in Yunhai for decades. He obtained solid evidence and dismantled the criminal forces that the Yunhai Group had cultivated for decades, arresting numerous drug traffickers and rebel groups, and completely annihilating the criminal gang led by the Wen brothers of the Wenye Group. His contribution is immense."

"Therefore, after multiple meetings and discussions at headquarters, Comrade Jiang Tian's contribution is considered immense, and he is hereby awarded the First-Class Merit Medal."

After speaking, Minister Tan opened the third box.

And completely revealed the contents of the third box.

Sure enough, a First-Class Merit Medal.

Pfft!!!

Boom!!!

It exploded.

Did you buy First-Class Merits wholesale?



Chapter 435 It's a shame you've been given a first-class merit.


Two First-Class Merits?

Everyone present stared wide-eyed.

After all, initially, Minister Tan's secretary had three boxes on his tray, the first two already representing incredibly prestigious awards.

And the last one was another First-Class Merit!

Who awards two First-Class Merits at once?!

Of course, a normal First-Class Merit award wouldn't be issued so quickly. Going through all the procedures, to receive one, from the moment the merit is awarded, through layers of reporting, to various verifications, further investigations, and final selection, this process alone takes at least six months, and often one or two years.

But Jiang Tian was different.

Because Jiang Tian's merits were all evident to all, and most remarkably, many of his achievements even reached the highest levels of government, meaning headquarters received news of his merits almost immediately.

This eliminated the time spent on reporting and reviewing at each level. Subsequently, Jiang Tian's case received considerable attention from higher authorities, and was processed almost entirely through expedited channels.

Finally, the decision was made directly through a meeting, and the medal was awarded immediately after approval.

This explains why medals, which typically require a one- to two-year process, were issued so quickly, in less than a week.

"It's shocking, unbelievable. Two first-class merits awarded to one person. Everyone is stunned, feeling like they're dreaming. But reality is happening right before your eyes, leaving you no choice but to believe it. Most importantly, from now on, seniority will not be the sole determinant within the organization. The meritocracy of promoting younger cadres, repeatedly emphasized by the Party, is accelerating. So, everyone, I hope you won't rest on your laurels and think you can be complacent."

Minister Tan glanced at the reactions of those around him, expressing satisfaction.

In reality, even Minister Tan himself was astonished.

Because it was too abnormal, too outrageous.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​all of this seemed commonplace.

After all, a Special Merit Citation, unprecedented in decades and never before awarded in peacetime, had already been bestowed upon him—a historic achievement.

Two First-Class Merits were awarded simultaneously, which wasn't unusual.

After all, Jiang Tian now seemed to have five.

Hmm, five First-Class Merits and one Special Merit Citation.

Minister Tan clicked his tongue in astonishment.

Because, this round of awards brought Jiang Tian's total to seven First-Class Merits and one Special Merit Citation.

How many people throughout history have achieved this?

But the thing is, can you just brush off every single thing Jiang Tian has done, every single merit he has made?

For example, saving Elder Mu and Su Tong—given their importance and influence, if he didn't receive a First-Class Merit Citation?

If he didn't get one for that, what kind of merit could he possibly earn in the future?

Another example is the Wenye Group, a criminal organization that had been entrenched in Yunnan Province for decades. Jiang Tian uncovered almost the entire operation single-handedly.

And he carried it out entirely on his own.

Such a significant achievement—it's unacceptable not to award him a first-class merit.

A first-class hero, Jiang Tian saved the lives of dozens, even hundreds of comrades, averting significant losses for the Party and the organization. The first-class hero designation was undoubtedly deserved, especially considering all the merits he had previously received.

While it seemed like a lot,

Minister Tan, upon closer examination, suddenly realized how much danger and challenge lay hidden behind each merit.

The enemies he faced were increasingly terrifying and ruthless.

He and his family suffered constant retaliatory attacks from criminal groups.

What a heavy price he had paid!

All of this was well-deserved for Jiang Tian.

Minister Tan felt a sense of unease, including this time. He knew that if the intelligence was correct, a first-class merit was inevitable.

Because he had dismantled a terrifying and massive criminal gang, involving hundreds, even thousands of people—a truly terrifying scale of crime!

And Jiang Tian had infiltrated such a criminal base, even taking photos. How incredibly dangerous was that place?

Jiang Tian's greatness needs no further explanation.

Thinking this through, Minister Tan's gaze softened as he pinned the second First-Class Merit Medal on Jiang Tian.

So now, Jiang Tian wore three gleaming gold medals on his chest.

Each one represented a pinnacle of glory.

Each one was an honor almost everyone present dreamed of.

Because they all knew that if they possessed such a medal, their rank would be far higher.

Their future would be bright.

A single First-Class Merit Medal was enough to forge a hero, let alone the immense merit Jiang Tian had achieved.

Yet, this very merit—the hero who brought change to Yunnan Province, who eradicated the Yunhai criminal syndicates one by one—was now, for various fabricated charges, not only arrested and imprisoned in a private room by a parachuted-in second-generation rich kid, but also subjected to such humiliation.

"Pah, you beast."

One of the armed police officers holding Han Qi spat at him, his eyes filled with disgust.

Because these grassroots armed police officers actually knew Jiang Tian best, and had participated in numerous operations under his command.

It was quite a coincidence that this particular officer had only recently joined the force. Just a few days prior, when Wen Yulin was attacked, their unit suffered heavy losses, and they followed their captain into the search area.

Upon entering, they discovered that almost forty or fifty drug dealers and armed men had been completely wiped out.

And all of this was done by Jiang Tian alone.

From that moment on, Jiang Tian became his greatest idol.

Now, that idol had been insulted, even humiliated in this way.

The officer's face was filled with contempt and disgust.

He even wanted to slap him twice if it weren't for his position.

Especially since he seemed so righteous, yet did such disgusting things. If you portray yourself as so noble, ask yourself what you've done.

Where were you when they were in danger?

What right do you have to question Jiang Tian?

"Stop talking nonsense, this kind of beast should be sentenced to death." The other man holding Han Qi said coldly.

Listening to the conversation around him,

he looked at Jiang Tian standing in the middle of thousands of people.

At this moment, even the most resilient Han Qi felt his mind reeling, unable to stop himself from questioning himself: Was he wrong? Had he done something wrong?

No, these people were all deceived by Jiang Tian's appearance as a black sheep.

I did nothing wrong.

Han Qi kept telling himself this.

But reality told Han Qi that things were far from what he had imagined.

The next moment, Minister Tan spoke with a shocking statement: "Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​I know that awarding you a first-class merit in the Wenye Group case is unfair, but rest assured, we remember all your efforts, and all your merits are being observed by higher-ups. We will compensate you in the future."

Boom!!!

Han Qi's composure completely exploded.

What did this person say?

A first-class merit was being treated unfairly?

Is this even human language?

I'm not unfairly treated, give it to me then!



Chapter 436 gradually surfaced, the chicken feet leaked.


Even the Wenye Group's actions and resolution were so swift that the impact was limited, and many local leaders were not even fully aware of the situation.

Upon hearing Minister Tan's words, they were all utterly bewildered.

Jiang Tian didn't feign modesty regarding the two First-Class Merits, as they were well-deserved. In Wen Yulin's actions, it could be said that if Jiang Tian hadn't already received a Special-Class Merit, he would have been worthy of it.

The criteria for a Special-Class Merit are based on the magnitude of impact and destructive power an individual can exert, even the ability to single-handedly change the course of a crucial battle.

Clearly, in terms of influence, the Wenye Group controlled the economic lifeline of Yunnan Province; any wrongdoing would cause terrifying and unpredictable damage.

Secondly, the Wenye Group was deeply entrenched, having remained hidden for decades, yet Jiang Tian single-handedly uncovered it.

Thirdly, despite Wen Yulin being attacked and more than ten comrades sacrificing their lives, Jiang Tian single-handedly annihilated over fifty armed militants.

Giving him a First-Class Merit was indeed somewhat unfair.

But ultimately, it's all about serving the people.

Besides, the higher-ups have their own considerations, and Jiang Tian understood their meaning after a moment's thought.

Jiang Tian's current rank is too low, but these accumulated merits will allow him to rise rapidly.

The future compensation shouldn't be too bad.

"It's all about serving the people," Jiang Tian said calmly.

At least, he was already very satisfied.

Because in his mind, a voice only he could hear kept ringing out.

With two first-class merits pinned to his chest, the special achievement [God of Wealth's Blessing] was constantly resounding.

No surprises.

Especially after the recent days of sweeping, Jiang Tian's sin value had reached its maximum, a terrifying harvest.

The Wenye Group alone brought him a terrifying sin value income.

His current panel was extremely luxurious.

[Perfect Shot (Gold)]

[Scanning Radar (Gold)]

[Extraordinary Human (Gold)]

[God of the Ocean (Gold)]

[Medical Giant (Red)]

[Martial Arts Master (Red)]

Special Attributes

1: [Eye of Judgment]

2: [God of Body Shape]

3: [God of Wealth Blessing (Automatically generates 16 yuan per minute)] (Reserve Funds: 78000) (7/8) Next stage reward: one investment intelligence report. (Currently, the Rolls-Royce Phantom and Purple Mountain Villa have been successfully issued)

Hiss!!!

That's right.

The God of Wealth Blessing achievement system.

Although it's just a simple count of first-class merits, the God of Wealth's blessing doubled from an initial four yuan per second. The sixth first-class merit was eight yuan, and the seventh was sixteen yuan.

Don't underestimate the doubled amount; it actually brought Jiang Tian a monthly income that increased from 100,000 yuan initially to a million yuan annually.

Now, it's 400,000 yuan per month, nearly five million yuan annually.

This entry doesn't even require Jiang Tian to do anything; it's just there, bringing him five million yuan in income every year.

And this is in 2003—the concept is terrifying.

Besides...

There was also the Rolls-Royce Phantom. This car was irrelevant to Jiang Tian; according to the reward information, it was already inside the villa.

Clean and tidy.

Every penny, as always, could withstand scrutiny.

Moreover, Jiang Tian even had to thank Han Qi this time. After Han Qi orchestrated this incident, everyone knew that Jiang Tian had a knack for business and his investments were terrifying.

As long as he didn't make money within his authority, no one could say anything or offer any opinions.

Besides the multi-million dollar Phantom, he was also rewarded with the Zijin Mountain Villa.

Jiang Tian had a deep impression of this villa area.

It was newly built this year, nestled against the mountains and beside the water, with the Yanjiang River flowing in front and Zijin Mountain behind.

A tranquil oasis amidst the hustle and bustle.

The area not far from where the armed police were attacked was actually not far from Zijin Mountain.

But its location was by no means remote; in fact, it was almost in the heart of Yunhai City.

The location is excellent, and it just opened for sale, yet prices have already soared to 21,000 yuan per square meter. This is 30,000 yuan per square meter in 2003, with villas starting at nearly 1,000 square meters.

In other words, a single villa is already approaching 20 million yuan.

And that's now. Even with Yunhai's development halted, Zishan villas have continued to rise in price.

In later years, they've approached 100,000 yuan per square meter.

However, Jiang Tian believes that if Yunhai hadn't developed like it did in his previous life, the price per square meter for Huangpu Riverfront villas in Donghai would be the same as the price per square meter for Zishan villas.

The gains are substantial.

Especially the 160,000+ sin points.

What does that mean? Jiang Tian could max out many keywords.

Most importantly.

Jiang Tian can still make a big profit one more time.

That's Shimiao Village, with hundreds of drug dealers. If they were all arrested, hundreds of people, even if each contributed a few dozen crime points, plus the ringleader, would amount to at least tens of thousands.

Perfect.

As for what's next, there are still many unresolved issues.

For example, this guy's classmate.

That's right.

Han Qi's classmate arrested Gao Yangyang; they're with the anti-corruption team, and we don't know the situation yet.

"Xiao Jiang, don't worry. We've already sent people to the anti-corruption team. Yangyang will be fine. According to Han Qi, he just wants to reach an agreement with Old Gao to prevent him from interfering in your problem," Minister Tan said.

Gao Xiaolong sneered in the car.

Han Qi underestimated him.

Did he really think that Han Qi would show kinship in the face of the law? Or did he think he would protect Jiang Tianzhen if he committed a crime?

That's impossible.

All he could offer Jiang Tian was fairness.

"No," Jiang Tian shook his head instead.

"What other problems are there?" Minister Tan frowned slightly.

Because right now, all the most important issues still pointed to that drug village.

As for Gao Yangyang and the others, they could simply be dealt with by his subordinates.

"Go there, everyone. There's a problem there," Jiang Tian said directly.

The problem with the drug village was there.

It could be resolved sooner or later.

But regarding the anti-corruption issue, Jiang Tian suddenly remembered some details.

If all the evidence against Han Qi came from the anti-corruption unit,

that means they've been watching him for a long time.

Why were they watching him so closely, and how did they know so much about him?

In other words,

Han Qi's old classmate slipped up, and Han Qi, either because he was stupid, was being used as a pawn.

And what was the purpose of all this elaborate planning?

To prove that something was behind it all!



Chapter 437 Create Despair, Face Despair


The time was just a few minutes, before everyone could act.

Han Qi was immediately detained on the spot, right there in the private room. Regardless, Han Qi was someone of status, and although he would be severely punished and pay a heavy price for his actions,

the influence and power behind Han Qi also had to be considered.

Taking all factors into consideration, he wasn't directly detained at the Municipal Public Security Bureau or a detention center. Instead, he was temporarily held in a private cell, but not by the Discipline Inspection Commission; the Municipal Public Security Bureau personnel were directly in charge of his custody to prevent any unforeseen circumstances.

Before leaving, Jiang Tian specifically went to see Han Qi.

It must be said...

Han Qi's attitude towards Jiang Tian had completely changed.

Compared to Jiang Tian now, Han Qi was like a stray dog, utterly insignificant.

Han Qi understood his predicament.

Especially when Jiang Tian entered, the three gleaming gold medals jingling on his chest caused Han Qi's mentality to explode.

Forget about so many first-class merits; what Han Qi was pursuing in Yunhai was nothing short of a second-class merit.

Indeed, even a second-class merit would be a tremendous boost to Han Qi's future political career, let alone so many first-class merits.

"I really want them. I have many. Like these, I have seven first-class merits," Jiang Tian said, pointing to the first-class merits on his chest.

Because Jiang Tian himself also had a classified file, and the internal network wasn't connected to the anti-drug police.

While much of the anti-drug police's information had been leaked, the infiltration was only superficial; the overall security level was still very high. Even the disciplinary committee, with its authority, couldn't access the anti-drug police's data. In fact, Jiang Tian's classified information was even more secure than the anti-drug police's, since Jiang Tian had an identity file with the security department.

It was already included in high-level confidentiality regulations, but this was mostly for show. In many people's eyes, Jiang Tian had no secrets.

But Han Qi clearly hadn't conducted a detailed and thorough investigation of Jiang Tian.

"Seven?" Han Qi's mouth dropped open. Jiang Tian understood; Han Qi obviously didn't know about this.

Or rather, Han Qi didn't know anything about what Jiang Tian had done; any limited information he had was probably hearsay.

But then Jiang Tian thought about it.

If he truly had a comprehensive understanding of him and knew what he had done, Han Qi, unless he was an idiot, probably wouldn't know.

"You think you have connections? You don't even know something like this?" Jiang Tian sneered.

The tables had turned. Now, Jiang Tian stood ramrod straight in the room.

Although the leaders outside were anxious, Jiang Tian wasn't particularly worried. Only he knew the information about Shimiao Village; there was no possibility of leakage, and he hadn't given anything away.

But now, Gao Yangyang had also been arrested, and Jiang Tian needed to understand the situation.

"I..." Han Qi's head spun.

If he had known beforehand, would he have been out of his mind? Would he have been so eager to attack Jiang Tian?

"It seems you didn't know. Otherwise, you wouldn't be so stupid. Who told you to take action? And who gave you the information? If you confess now, you still have some room for maneuver. If you resist, you'll face severe consequences, and if it leads to even greater repercussions, even your family won't be able to protect you," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Han Qi didn't resist and told him everything.

And Jiang Tian understood completely.

"Yuan Guangliang from the Anti-Corruption Bureau?" Jiang Tian's tone rose slightly.

Han Qi nodded.

"Alright, I understand." Jiang Tian turned and left.

Watching Jiang Tian's figure recede into the distance, Han Qi slumped weakly onto the sofa.

Now he couldn't do anything, because even his phone had been confiscated.

Now he only awaited his fate.

But what panicked Han Qi the most was that, to this day, he still didn't know how the Yunnan Provincial authorities would deal with him.

"Given my uncle's relationship with the family, Huai Province should give me some face," Han Qi thought, still clinging to a sliver of hope.

Regret.

Han Qi wasn't stupid.

He'd come in such a rush, and his uncle hadn't explained things clearly—he wanted to toughen him up, to let him gradually adapt after arriving in Yunhai. Plus, his third uncle arrived right after, wanting him to adjust to Yunhai, but who knew he'd cause such a disaster?

Now, he realized Jiang Tian had already earned seven First-Class Merits.

What did that mean?

How many people throughout history could achieve that?

Han Qi's mind went blank.

His understanding was too limited, but at the same time, he wasn't stupid. He realized what had happened.

He'd been used.

That's right.

"Yuan Guangliang, you beast!" Han Qi's face contorted with rage.

After figuring it out, he understood he'd been used.

After the initial surge of excitement subsided, Han Qi's rationality prevailed. Even though he was reluctant to admit it, he had to acknowledge that Jiang Tian might genuinely be innocent.

Because he considered the lineup he'd assembled today.

If Jiang Tian truly had a protector, wouldn't that mean every single high-ranking official in Yun Province, from top to bottom, was a protector?

Even Chief Gao and Minister Tan were protectors?

Wasn't that utter nonsense?

"Right, Chief Gao has been in charge of Yunhai for so long; even if Jiang Tian wanted to fabricate his achievements, it wouldn't be possible."

Han Qi's head was buzzing, and the more he thought about it, the paler his face became.

Even though he was too jealous to admit it, he had to admit that Jiang Tian must have achieved enough to win over these high-ranking officials.

He could fool one or two leaders, but he couldn't fool all of them.

Such an outstanding individual as Jiang Tian couldn't possibly be unaware of his exploits in Yunhai. As a local strongman, Yuan Guangliang certainly knew what Jiang Tian had accomplished and how many merits he had made, yet he still colluded with him.

If it weren't for the presence of so many leaders today, and the fact that Jiang Tian had such a powerful backer,

anyone else would have been taken down without even realizing it.

But the coincidence lies in the fact that a major operation was taking place today, and even more coincidentally, Minister Tan had already received word of the operation and arrived discreetly under the guise of presenting an award.

Then things spiraled completely out of control. Otherwise, even if Jiang Tian were proven innocent, Han Qi would at most just return to the capital.

With a little effort, the matter could have been dropped.

In conclusion.

Jiang Tian, ​​pushing open the door and leaving, felt no sympathy whatsoever. He felt Han Qi deserved it all. He hadn't kicked him while he was down, only because Han Qi, though outrageously stupid and driven by self-interest, was also trying to gain merit.

After leaving and getting into the car, Jiang Tian finally understood the situation.



Chapter 438 Operation Suspended, Are You Crazy? Even Crazier Case Clues?


It has to be said that the title of "King of Criminal Investigation" provides Jiang Tian with terrifying assistance, especially in his field.

Take the case of Elder Mu, for example.

Although Jiang Tian wasn't actually involved, he had already obtained a fairly complete understanding of the case files through Miss Qiu.

In other words, Elder Mu's assassination, an extremely heinous case, had reached a dead end.

The difficulty in investigating cases in Yunhai lies precisely in this.

Yunhai's rapid development has brought countless illegal immigrants, who have smuggled themselves in from neighboring countries. Most work illegally, while some, unable to resist the temptation, have turned to crime.

These individuals lack household registration records and personal information, making investigations extremely difficult.

Elder Mu's case is a clear example of this.

The people behind it have found these illegal immigrants from neighboring countries.

Their personal information is completely unavailable domestically, possibly due to outdated technology.

However, according to their investigation, at this stage, the driver who attacked Elder Mu is clearly an illegal immigrant from neighboring countries.

In this respect, they did possess crucial evidence.

But here's the problem: finding information about this person requires collaborating with the neighboring authorities, but that area is a chaotic mix, almost entirely independent, and they can't even control one place themselves.

The current difficulty in the case is that they can't find information about the driver who's already dead.

Of course, when Wen Yulin was attacked, they did capture a surviving driver, but that driver has also reached a dead end.

Because they investigated the village and found information, they had absolutely no information about the mastermind behind it all; they only said that the group approached him and gave him money.

The case has gone cold.

However, the good news is that several of the attackers have been arrested, providing some leads and clues.

But this is far from enough.

"Jiang Tian, ​​do you know what you're doing? Over two thousand military and police officers are on standby, and now you want to suspend the operation?"

On the bus, Minister Tan looked at Jiang Tian. Clearly, if it weren't for the fact that it was Jiang Tian in front of him, Minister Tan would have already started yelling.

Because just now...

Jiang Tian proposed suspending the operation, suspending the operation against the drug village.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you at least need to give us an explanation." Governor Huai also frowned.

Nothing is more important than the drug village problem.

After all, it involves a large-scale crime, hundreds, even thousands of people.

Compared to this case, everything else can be put aside; even the most serious matter cannot wait.

"Little Jiang, you should know that with so many military and police officers assembled, almost every criminal group with any influence has received the news. The longer it drags on, the more unexpected things will happen. If anything goes wrong, it could even lead to a visible and terrifying chaos. The responsibility for that is something none of us can bear." Chief Gao also frowned slightly.

Ignorant.

Jiang Tian is still so clueless!

Why would he want to suspend the operation? Is that something he can just say so casually?

"No, I think the problem at hand is even more serious than the criminal organization I mentioned." Jiang Tian's words were shocking.

Everyone's eyes widened.

There were quite a few leaders on the bus, including military and police officials.

Upon hearing this, they all looked at Jiang Tian with surprise.

“Jiang Tian, ​​do you know what you’re saying? Don’t act recklessly at a time like this,” Old Master Mu quickly interjected.

His tone was slightly harsher.

However, Jiang Tian knew that Old Master Mu was anxious.

Because now, as the relationship between Jiang Tian and Mu Ya gradually repaired, Old Master Mu was beginning to treat Jiang Tian as family.

He definitely had high hopes for Jiang Tian.

In fact, their family was currently facing a dilemma of succession crisis, a common problem for many second and third generations.

That is, with the passing of the older generation, there is no successor.

And after the older generation leaves, like a lamp going out, the family naturally declines.

Old Master Mu was in this situation.

He only had one son, Mu Feng.

But Mu Feng went into business.

His granddaughter, Mu Ya, is now working at a news television station.

As for the collateral relatives, like his nephews, none of them are capable of taking on a major role.

What's lacking is someone with a bright future like Jiang Tian. All his political resources could pave the way for Jiang Tian, ​​but unfortunately, Jiang Tian seems to have no use for them right now.

The path he's taken is simply unrepeatable.

"Secretary Mu, don't be in such a hurry," Jiang Tian stood up.

Actually, Jiang Tian had thought about it for a long time; there were priorities.

It was obvious.

Yuan Guangliang was a crucial link in the plan.

Or rather, a key target for Jiang Tian to follow the trail.

For others, arresting Yuan Guangliang might not be of much use, and they might even end up like Han Qi—at worst, they'd be dismissed for arresting the wrong person.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​having grasped the key point, he had keenly sensed the scent of a major case.

"No matter how serious the matter, we must act first," Old Mu said directly.

"Secretary Mu is right, Xiao Jiang, you need to carefully consider the priorities," Minister Tan nodded slightly.

If it weren't for knowing Jiang Tian, ​​many leaders would probably suspect that Jiang Tian was deliberately exaggerating or trying to attract attention, and that there was no such major case at all.

"I suspect that Yuan Guangliang is connected to Secretary Mu's assassination attempt," Jiang Tian said.

Boom!!!

After these words, the carriage fell silent instantly.

Everyone stared at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

"Xiao Jiang, don't be ridiculous. Old Mu's case is being investigated by a joint task force of experts from headquarters, the provincial department, and the municipal bureau. So far, there have been no leads. Yuan Guangliang did illegally arrest Gao Yangyang, but how could that be connected to Old Mu's assassination attempt?" Shen Han was startled.

Shen Han couldn't even squeeze through the door.

The people inside were all big shots.

Shen Han didn't dare to squeeze into the VIP room either.

After all, even Jiang Peng was waiting at the door.

However, along the way, to prevent any accidents, Shen Han followed while simultaneously mobilizing the provincial security forces for protection.

After all, the situation in Yunhai was too serious; what if another case like Wen Yulin's happened?

Hiss!!

Shen Han simply couldn't bear to think about it.

It was as if the sky were about to collapse.

The world would be turned upside down.

Everyone in Yunhai, from top to bottom—no, the entire Yun Province—would likely be stripped of their positions.

As for Shen Han, he was the top person in charge, and he'd probably be going straight to jail.

Therefore, Shen Han dared not be careless in the slightest, with dense police escorts in front of and behind him.

He was constantly mobilizing forces for defense.

In front of all the leaders, Shen Han couldn't even utter a word.

The unprogressing Director Shen had unknowingly developed some obsessions.

But now, Shen Han stared dumbfounded at Jiang Tian, ​​almost thinking Jiang Tian had gone mad.

Does he know what he's saying?

If finding clues were really that easy, would the Major Crimes Investigation Team have taken so long without finding any clues, or even a direction?

You open and close your mouth.

So many experts couldn't find a direction, and you found a clue.

Isn't this a slap in the face to those experts?

"Exactly, you're in drug enforcement, do you even know anything about criminal investigation?"



Chapter 439 A Ray of Light in the Criminal Investigation Legend, Lost Hope


Despite the lack of fanfare following Elder Mu's assassination attempt, the patrol force in Yunhai increased several times over.

Moreover, a comprehensive investigation and screening were conducted from top to bottom.

Unfortunately, while numerous small-time drug dealers and users were apprehended, none were found to be connected to Elder Mu's assassination.

Of course.

On the other hand, the nationwide manhunt and search in Yunhai created a climate of fear.

Police officers were even stationed at every two intersections, checking IDs and other valuables.

But unfortunately, this seemed to be like searching for a needle in a haystack.

As a result, many undocumented immigrants were uncovered, and these were mostly deported.

Furthermore, border controls were tightened.

According to Jiang Tian, ​​the higher-ups seemed to be planning to erect barbed wire along the border.

While it couldn't completely prevent it, it would at least minimize the entry of undocumented immigrants from neighboring countries.

The prosperity of Yunhai has clearly led to a shortage of illegal immigrants from neighboring areas, who are impacting various industries with their cheap labor.

Although Yunhai's wages are still very low, even compared to first-tier cities, they are still many times higher than those in their home countries.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to make a shocking statement...

Provincial Department.

In the conference room sat numerous high-ranking officials.

Each of these individuals was a leader in the criminal investigation field.

They all possessed extensive experience in criminal investigation and a keen investigative instinct.

Especially Shang Mingyan, the leader of this special task force.

He was hailed as a criminal investigation genius.

In fact, back in the day, Shang Mingyan was even more of a genius than Miss Qiu.

Now approaching forty, Shang Mingyan's reputation alone was enough to make him an unattainable figure.

He pioneered many groundbreaking researches in criminal investigation.

In particular, he was a professor and mentor in criminal investigation at one of the top police academies in the country.

He had practically dominated the criminal investigation field for twenty years.

Rising to prominence in the 1980s, Shang Mingyan had virtually no unsolved cases and no unapprehended enemies.

Except for major unsolved cases that shocked the nation.

Shang Mingyan practically roamed the country; whenever a high-profile and heinous case occurred, Shang Mingyan's involvement was almost always present.

Compared to Shang Mingyan, Miss Qiu could only serve as his assistant.

"Mentor, the case still hasn't made any progress."

Miss Qiu stared blankly, holding the documents, lost in thought, until the sound of high heels filled the room. Then a shadowy figure appeared beside her, waving a hand.

Miss Qiu's blank eyes snapped back to reality. Looking at the newcomer, she spoke with an eerily calm expression.

Throughout the entire exchange, she showed almost no emotion.

Shang Mingyan seemed to be used to Miss Qiu's attitude. Hearing this, he sighed slightly and said, "This case can be listed as one of the top ten unsolved cases."

The case.

The worst thing is losing your way of thinking.

When all criminal investigation logic fails, relying on the human brain to fill in the gaps in a case that has virtually no clues,

naturally, the case reaches a stalemate and makes no progress.

"One of the top ten unsolved cases, huh? No wonder." Miss Qiu nodded slightly, showing no regret.

Or rather, for Miss Qiu, her only interest used to be solving cases, but now she had another: studying Jiang Tian's investigative methods.

Because Miss Qiu discovered that Jiang Tian's investigative methods were often completely different from what she had learned in class and from her mentor, Shang Mingyan.

Of course, perhaps Miss Qiu's interest had never truly changed.

She only liked solving cases; what was wrong with that? Studying Jiang Tian was also for the purpose of solving cases.

“You should smile more often. Staring at me with those dead fish eyes all day without any emotion isn't good,” Shang Mingyan shook his head.

Miss Qiu looked at Shang Mingyan with a puzzled expression.

Shang Mingyan touched his face, seemingly curious: “What’s wrong?”

Looking at the indifference in Shang Mingyan’s eyes, a slight glint appeared in the depths of her eyes as she said, “Before this, Professor, you’re the one who should change the most.”

“Whatever you want. But in my opinion, this case is hopeless, but it might help your investigative thinking. You can study it more and see if you can broaden your perspective.”

“What do you mean?” Miss Qiu looked up at Shang Mingyan.

What did Shang Mingyan mean by that?

He was giving up on the case.

But the importance and impact of this case—are we just going to give up on it so easily?

Miss Qiu's deadpan eyes finally showed some surprise.

Shang Mingyan shook his head and said, "Our approach to solving cases is to look at problems rationally. We've even mobilized the entire province's police force for a comprehensive investigation, deploying no fewer than tens of thousands of officers. But is it useful? No, it's useless except for wasting resources. Because, given the current level of technology, this is the limit of what we can do. Now, unless the murderer comes forward, we have no hope."

Shang Mingyan, as the leader of this special task force,

is responsible for reviewing this case.

That's right.

Miss Qiu is actually Shang Mingyan's student.

She's also the student Shang Mingyan has recently mentored, and in his eyes, the most promising genius student to succeed him.

She's also the only student who can stand shoulder to shoulder with him.

Ms. Qiu seems to be born for many investigative approaches and logics; she can almost perfectly apply them to various cases with remarkable ease.

While this case had a significant impact, Shang Mingyan remained remarkably rational.

This wasn't simply an individual's case.

It was organized and premeditated.

And the organization that orchestrated it might not even be located within the country.

How could they investigate?

Where could they even begin to investigate?

Continuing would only waste police resources and manpower, serving virtually no purpose.

"Professor, are we just going to give up like this? Just give up like that?" Miss Qiu still couldn't believe it.

Shang Mingyan nodded and said, "That's right. Currently, our case only has one common lead: the driver shares similarities with the driver who intercepted Jiang Tian from the Yunhai Narcotics Control Team. The only commonality is that both were drivers who committed the crimes. However, the difference is that one is from a remote mountain village within the country, while our investigation is overseas. We can't even find the driver's identity yet. Given the chaos overseas, do you think we still have a chance?"

We can't even find the driver.

Keeping him within the country is simply a waste of resources.



Chapter 440 They looked down on me then, now they have to kneel down.


"I've teamed up with so many experts, investigating almost day and night for over a week, but we still haven't even found a lead on the mastermind. You know what that means? Right now, all we can do is temporarily seal everything away. After all, Secretary Mu is alright, isn't he? There are more important things waiting for us now."

Shang Mingyan explained calmly.

This was her most prized disciple, whom she was grooming as her successor.

From now on, Miss Qiu will be her perfect successor.

If it were someone else, Shang Mingyan wouldn't have explained it in such detail.

Secretary Mu, in the end, didn't have anything serious, but they've mobilized almost the nation's most elite criminal investigation team to waste time here.

Then other major cases across the country will be left unsolved.

Even if Secretary Mu escapes unscathed, the sheer severity of the attack would still place it among the top three in terms of impact.

This is because, in decades, this is the first time such a high-ranking official has been attacked.

The severity necessitates bringing the perpetrators to justice.

"Therefore, from now on, we will gradually reduce investigative resources, but we will also reserve corresponding resources to maintain the investigation. I have already reported this to higher authorities, and they have already reassigned those experts," Shang Mingyan continued.

Of course.

As the head of the special task force, Shang Mingyan will not leave Yunnan Province for the time being.

Even if she knows she can't find the answer to Elder Mu's case, she still needs to make a statement.

Although reason tells Shang Mingyan that abandoning the case is the best course of action.

Unfortunately, sometimes her personal will couldn't influence anything.

"Furthermore, we investigated Secretary Mu's interpersonal relationships, but unfortunately, we didn't get any information. We also questioned leaders who disagreed with Secretary Mu, but the final results were unsatisfactory; there was almost no progress. And in my opinion, those people couldn't be the murderer."

No progress was made in personal relationships.

They even investigated those who suffered losses based on Elder Mu's policies.

For the past week, they've worked almost non-stop, sifting through all the files and investigating all the individuals. Anyone suspected has been thoroughly investigated.

However, as the case progressed, it wasn't just a deadlock.

It was that all the leads had completely dried up.

"There's another possibility," Shang Mingyan suddenly said.

"What possibility?" Miss Qiu knew this was her teacher teaching her.

"Secretary Mu is in charge of the political and legal system, and recently, he proposed reforms to maintain social stability. Some of these measures are quite targeted, especially his proposal to completely close the border. The culprit is obvious; the mastermind behind the operation is likely a foreign power profiting in Yunhai. There's no need to investigate this case anymore."

Cross-border investigation.

What a joke.

If it were a country with a stable government, that would be one thing, but overseas? Good heavens, a hundred different forces in a small territory.

They have to find a different force wherever they go.

What kind of alliance is that?

At this point, Shang Mingyan was somewhat helpless.

This was the real reason the case had reached a dead end.

“Teacher, regarding those foreign forces, criminal groups, and their crime-solving abilities, that Comrade Jiang Tian from the anti-drug squad…” Miss Qiu's tone became anxious when she mentioned Jiang Tian.

But before she could finish, Shang Mingyan interrupted her: “He’s an anti-drug officer, what does he know about criminal investigation? Don’t you understand? Without systematic training, do you think he knows more than us experts? That’s not genius, that’s treating us like idiots.”

This wasn’t arrogance.

It was the experience Shang Mingyan had gained over twenty years in the criminal investigation industry, becoming a beacon of light.

Her authority was unquestionable.

And no one could question it.

One of her books had even become a textbook in criminal investigation courses.

But just then, Shang Mingyan’s phone rang, interrupting her.

“Minister, what is it?” Shang Mingyan asked simply.

That’s right, it was Minister Tan calling Shang Mingyan.

However, Shang Mingyan's tone remained indifferent, seemingly cold and aloof.

Miss Qiu glanced up at Shang Mingyan, but quickly looked away. Meeting his cold eyes gave her the illusion that she was completely exposed, inside and out, leaving her feeling utterly naked.

Before those eyes, it seemed no one had any secrets.

That was how terrifying it was.

Of course, without this, Shang Mingyan wouldn't have become a milestone in the criminal investigation industry.

His position was so high that even Minister Tan sometimes felt a chilling sense of being seen through when facing him.

"Professor Shang, there's a breakthrough in the assassination case of Secretary Mu," Minister Tan said excitedly.

You see, the case of Secretary Mu's attack was no longer something that could be solved locally in Yunnan Province. Other cases might be able to be covered up, and if solved well, even if it meant undergoing painful reform, the higher-ups wouldn't blame them.

However, the influence of such a high-ranking leader was so pernicious that it had caused unrest throughout the entire national civil service system.

An earthquake even struck Beijing, demonstrating the terrifying extent of his influence.

Although Shang Mingyan was aloof, his pronouncements were almost always absolute.

In the national criminal investigation field,

Shang Mingyan believed that if a case could be solved, it could be solved.

And if he said a case couldn't be solved, it had little hope, and ultimately became one of the top ten unsolved cases in the country.

When Shang Mingyan arrived in Yunhai, his first words were that the chances of solving the case were slim.

Sure enough, a week passed with almost no progress. All possibilities were investigated, and all clues were exhausted without finding anything. The case was effectively closed.

Minister Tan also expressed his understanding. After learning that the driver was from overseas and the vehicle was a stolen, scrapped car, Minister Tan understood that the case's chances were dwindling.

But at this moment, Jiang Tianyu, determined to shock and provoke, intervened.

He actually claimed to have discovered a clue in the case.

The crucial point is that, in the end, Minister Tan discovered...

What Jiang Tian said might actually be true.

Minister Tan was thrilled.

He couldn't understand how Jiang Tian could be such a genius.

But none of that mattered.

"Do you know Jiang Tian from the Narcotics Control Unit? He discovered clues to the case, connected to the people involved, and according to Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​this case is definitely related to Yuan Guangliang from the Anti-Corruption Bureau," Minister Tan said directly.

Boom!!!

Hiss!!!

Shang Mingyan's cold eyes flashed with shock: "Where is he? I need to see Jiang Tian."

"Oh, he's handling an important case and can't get away right now."

Miss Qiu, standing to the side, raised an eyebrow, as if to say, "You looked down on him before, but now he's someone you can't afford to be near."

At this moment, Miss Qiu's soft voice rang out: "He's just a narcotics officer, what does he know about criminal investigation? After all, it's a completely different field. What he's saying is definitely false, Professor, don't believe him."



Chapter 441 The more they investigated, the more terrifying it became; they'd really stirred up a hornet's nest.


Meanwhile.

A bus was parked at the intersection, and not far from it stood a large group of military and police personnel.

That's right.

They were currently heading towards Shimiao Village.

"According to my investigation, there's a road behind Shimiao Village. As for whether there are other roads, I haven't found any yet, but one thing is certain: so far, besides the small paths at the front and back of the village, any other roads shouldn't be a problem."

Jiang Tian's eyes flashed. If these two roads were blocked, Shimiao Village would be practically trapped.

Especially since...

The original plan was to operate at 8 PM.

And hold a tactical meeting at 7 PM, ensuring everyone was home at night for easier operation and to prevent any suspects from escaping.

However, due to the unexpected events, after Jiang Tian came out and gave a detailed account of the situation in Shimiao Village on the bus,

and with the subsequent organization of manpower, it had already dragged on until midnight.

Of course...

Actually, it didn't affect the overall operation much.

"At midnight, most people are already asleep, which is a great help to our operation," Minister Tan said thoughtfully, stroking his chin.

Although Jiang Tian had just said to suspend the operation, an operation can't be suspended so easily.

In the end, there's no turning back once the arrow is released. Since manpower has been organized and the advance is underway, there's no way to stop.

Originally, Jiang Tian wanted to go in and set up radar tonight for detailed recording.

But because of this unexpected event...

Of course.

In fact, when Jiang Tian mentioned Shimiao Village, all the leaders turned pale.

Especially Huai Sheng, whose lips even turned purple.

Any other criminal issues could be put aside, but as mentioned before, this issue is absolutely non-negotiable. Drug crimes are the red line within the red line, a high-voltage line. Anyone who dares to cross it will be reduced to ashes.

After all, the disaster a century ago brought countless subsequent calamities to this land.

A lesson from the past.

And when it became clear that a village, perhaps entirely populated by people involved in drug trafficking, was involved, everyone was stunned.

They had considered it might be a large criminal organization.

They had also considered it might be a small criminal gang.

But they never expected it to be a village.

Especially after Jiang Tian produced the information.

“A village. If it’s a village, it’s difficult to operate. If we can’t identify the suspects, it’s easy to harm innocent people. Hiss, no wonder we had to suspend the operation. Xiao Jiang, you’re right, this operation was indeed impulsive.” Shen Han gasped.

Shimiao Village covers a large area.

It has a permanent population of over two thousand.

So, if it’s a drug village, how many criminals are there? How many people are involved?

It’s likely that everyone is involved in cover-ups; at least half the population is involved in the crimes, right?

"The problem in Shimiao Village is extremely serious."

"My God, Xiao Jiang, you should have reported this problem a long time ago!"

"What's the point of reacting? A drug village, how long has it been operating, how large is it, how many interest groups are involved? If we reveal this beforehand, what if word gets leaked? Wouldn't all our efforts be in vain?"

"But what do we do now? While we're certain most people are involved, we can't pinpoint the specific perpetrators. This poses a huge challenge to our arrests and operational procedures."

That's right.

This is the dilemma they're facing.

If they could identify the criminals, facing drug dealers—good heavens, if they resist or are in danger—they could just shoot them dead.

But if they can't identify the criminals, they can't just go into the village and kill everyone they see.

Besides,

Even in the largest drug village, it's impossible for everyone to be involved; that would be too outrageous.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, smiled slightly.

He had all of this under control.

"Xiao Jiang, I know how you do things. You don't fight battles you can't win. Stop hiding things," Gao Xiaolong stepped forward and said.

That's right.

Full force mobilized.

Given the scale of the case, these leaders were all surrounded and kept in secure locations.

Because of their presence, this operation didn't even require Jiang Tian's personal involvement.

Of course, such a large-scale operation involved far too many issues.

Jiang Tian needed to participate as a consultant and commander.

After all, no one present understood the problems of Shimiao Village better than Jiang Tian; he had been secretly investigating for a long time.

As Gao Xiaolong's voice rang out, everyone's gaze turned to Jiang Tian.

A hint of doubt lingered in their eyes.

A person's reputation is like a tree's shadow.

Jiang Tian, ​​after all, has a great reputation, but when it comes to witnessing his extraordinary feats of merit, they've only heard various rumors, some of which are quite bizarre, leading them to remain somewhat skeptical.

“I had anticipated this, so in the past few days, I have conducted a detailed investigation of Shimiao Village. The entire village has a total of 735 households, with a total of 2,300 people including adults and children. I have investigated the account transactions of these 2,300 people.”

Jiang Tian spoke calmly and unhurriedly.

Hiss!!!

Upon hearing this, many leaders looked at Jiang Tian in astonishment.

Jiang Peng, in particular, instinctively blurted out, "How come I didn't know?"

"I investigated this matter in secrecy," Jiang Tian said calmly.

As for the reason, most of the leaders present understood somewhat; a hint of embarrassment flashed across their faces, but they didn't say anything more.

But the next moment...

Those present finally reacted.

"One person investigated?"

"My God?"

"More than two thousand people?"

"Is it that insane?"

The leaders who finally realized what was happening were completely dumbfounded.

Actually, this kind of investigation is quite troublesome, because it involves screening all the people's information, marking any discrepancies in their bank account statements, and then labeling their names.

It must be said,

Although it's a crime, everyone in Shimiao Village has household registration numbers; if they really wanted to find someone, they could just arrest them by matching the numbers.

However, there were bound to be some oversights, but those weren't important.

"According to my investigation, the entire village is dominated by clan power, and everyone in the village shares the surname Shi, with the leaders among them..."

Jiang Tian recited the details with ease, thanks to his photographic memory.

To be honest, Jiang Tian felt a little ashamed.

The person who actually handled these documents was Sister Yu Hui, but Jiang Tian did all the review.

Jiang Tian even felt that Sister Yu Hui hadn't done much; he did most of the work, and he couldn't understand how she had become so exhausted.

After finishing the documents, he immediately took a vacation.

"Heartless! Absolutely heartless! Crimes involving the entire village! What is Yun Hai doing? Why didn't he notice anything beforehand?" Minister Tan's eyelids twitched violently.

This is a disaster!

This is a disaster!



Chapter 442 Doubting Life: Does Such a Perfect Prodigy Really Exist in the World?


He hadn't originally intended to get involved.

But the more he listened, the more serious and terrifying the problem became.

The scale involved was terrifying. Most importantly, this wasn't an isolated case. The sheer scale of crime in Shimiao Village clearly couldn't have been accomplished overnight, or allowed to continue undetected for years. The crucial question was, even if Shimiao Village was dealt with, how many more criminal groups like Shimiao Village were there in Yunhai?

The thought was chilling.

Thinking back to the criminal groups Jiang Tian had previously eradicated, Minister Tan felt increasingly terrified; it was truly breathtaking.

Minister Tan even had a premonition.

The ten most unsolved cases since the founding of the nation.

Could it be...

Most of them were going to be replaced by Yunhai?

Whether it was the Wenye Group, Shimiao Village, even the attack on the Party Secretary, the attacks by dozens of armed men, and the attacks by hundreds of armed men on Chief Gao... Good, good...

Don't even mention the future.

"Counting them up, it seems like none of the major cases in the last decade have been as numerous as Yunhai's one."

"Besides, I've prepared almost all the materials. I've marked those confirmed to be criminals with red labels based on bank statements, and those uncertain but high-risk have also been marked. Even innocent people can't prove their innocence; after all, 100,000 or 200,000 yuan in stolen money can be used in cash, not necessarily through bank cards. In short, the final estimate of 700 people is just a conservative estimate." Jiang Tian sighed.

This case is definitely the most impactful and terrifying one in recent times.

It will likely serve as a major deterrent for the next few decades, sounding the alarm for the entire country.

"There's no way around it, Yunhai is just that great. He'll blow up any potential threats first. You'll see how I got blown up; just be more careful in the future."

"700 people, conservative?"

Everyone present was stunned.

But thinking about it carefully...

The high-risk and confirmed cases both exceeded 700 people, but if you investigate through various channels...

wouldn't that easily involve thousands of people?

It's even possible that more than half the villagers were involved.

Zhou Zhao swallowed hard, glancing at Huai Sheng. He felt relieved that Huai Sheng had come; otherwise, even he wouldn't have dared to make a decision on such a horrific case.

No.

Even Huai Sheng didn't have the authority.

Because it involved something too terrifying.

Arresting over a thousand people at once.

Just thinking about where they would be detained after the interrogations and arrests was a huge problem.

Because the detention centers in Yunhai were already nearing capacity.

"Fortunately, the early discovery has mitigated this serious mistake in Yunhai. However, this doesn't mean it can be wiped clean."

Minister Tan's tone softened slightly, his gaze sweeping across the room.

Even Huai Sheng narrowed his eyes and took deep breaths.

At that moment, Yu Hui got out of a police car.

Then she quickly ran to the bus.

She was carrying a huge briefcase. Jiang Tian took it from her, glanced at Yu Hui's swollen eyes, and couldn't help but feel a pang of heartache.

But there was nothing he could do; Yu Hui was truly useful.

"Don't worry, Sister Yu Hui, I'll recommend you for commendation. You should go back and get some sleep," Jiang Tian said worriedly, waving his hand.

The main problem was that such competent workhorses were hard to find; if he got exhausted, who would compensate Jiang Tian?

"It's too late. It's such a big case. How can I possibly rest?" Yu Hui said, on the verge of tears.

She suspected she wasn't far from collapsing from exhaustion.

Continuing like this, although the bonuses were substantial, how could her flesh and blood possibly withstand it? Jiang Tian felt helpless about this.

With such a big case, it was probably impossible for Sister Yu Hui to take a break, given the follow-up issues.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian suddenly felt a pang of sympathy for Sister Yu Hui.

Who says only those standing in the spotlight are heroes?

The next moment, Minister Tan strode forward.

He took the documents.

Sure enough.

The more Minister Tan read, the more interesting his expression became, and the more surprised he looked at Jiang Tian.

Even if she didn't want to admit it,

the level of detail in this report, whether it was about major suspects or confirmed cases, was based on concrete evidence.

This work...

Jiang Tian was able to produce it in such a short time, simultaneously investigating the crime scene and completing it.

"Thank you for your hard work, Ms. Tan," Minister Tan said to Yu Hui.

But the next moment, Yu Hui hurriedly shook her head: "Minister, you misunderstand. I was only responsible for collecting information. Reviewing these materials and preparing the documents was all done by Team Leader Jiang himself."

That's right.

This was why Yu Hui worked overtime willingly.

This made Minister Tan even more surprised as he looked at Jiang Tian.

Even if he didn't want to admit it, Jiang Tian's impression was more about practical experience than strategic thinking.

Perhaps Jiang Tian's impressive track record easily made people overlook his intelligence in case investigation and criminal investigation.

Such potential...

Hiss!!!

Does such a perfect, terrifying genius really exist in the world?

Minister Tan was increasingly alarmed as he read through the documents.

The more he read, the more delighted he became.

Finally, Minister Tan slapped his thigh and exclaimed, "Excellent! Well done! With this evidence, we can now act without restraint!"

However, after arriving at the scene and preparing for the operation,

they discovered that

the complexity of the case far exceeded their expectations.

They had initially thought it was just another Wenye Group, but it turned out to be countless times more terrifying.

They had initially thought Jiang Tian was exaggerating, perhaps involving only three or four hundred criminals.

This was already a massive and terrifying case, and with all the unrelated individuals involved, it could easily amount to seven or eight hundred people.

But now...

the actual number of criminals involved was far more than a few hundred!

These thousands were definitely going to be arrested.

And all of them were major drug offenders.

After their arrest, they could be sentenced to death or life imprisonment.

"Comrade Xiao Jiang, you've truly exceeded my expectations. I thought that with such a complex case, further investigation would be pointless, but you've given me such a pleasant surprise."

Indeed, if they paused the operation to conduct an investigation, they wouldn't even get a chance to investigate further. However, if they proceeded directly, innocent people might be implicated. A few minor incidents wouldn't matter, but if their actions resulted in serious consequences, too many innocent people would be implicated.

Who could bear such a terrifying responsibility?

The information Jiang Tian submitted was tantamount to giving him a list.

All he had to do was infiltrate the addresses on the list, house by house, and make the arrests.

And for this achievement, Minister Tan showed no emotion whatsoever; on the contrary, his eyes shone brightly.

“Eight merits as kings aren’t enough; you want to be the king of nine palaces?”



Chapter 443 Another Shocking Thunderclap: You Had This Trick Up Your Sleeve All Along!


"Actually, it's not just that. I think we've also got some leads on another shocking case."

Jiang Tian smiled.

"What did you say?" Minister Tan seemed to haven't heard him clearly.

"The forces behind the group that attacked Wen Yulin should be showing some signs of life now," Jiang Tian said slowly.

That's what you call a bombshell statement.

It caused an uproar.

The moment those words were spoken, the room erupted in chaos.

Why?

The investigation into this case had actually been ongoing.

However, Jiang Tian's focus wasn't actually on the perpetrators.

Through his recent investigations, Jiang Tian had gained some understanding of the criminal organization.

They had a group of people operating overseas.

They were essentially mercenaries, but without the terrifying and professional combat skills of regular mercenaries, yet still possessing a bit more fighting ability than ordinary drug dealers.

They acted as substitutes.

Or rather, they acted as agents.

These people emerge when local criminal gangs are unable to handle their own affairs, or when there's a need for assistance.

Jiang Tian conducted a brief interrogation of those arrested.

Unfortunately, the results were somewhat unsatisfactory: this group was essentially hired thugs.

They were operatives for a foreign corporation.

The local Yunhai criminal group directly used its superpowers, paying exorbitant sums to hire thugs from that company to carry out their operations.

In other words, the mastermind behind the criminal group paid the foreign company, which then acted based on intelligence provided by the criminal group.

Regarding this case,

it's one of only two truly major cases.

Currently, major cases are constantly emerging in Yunhai, and the sheer scale of these cases is terrifying. If fully exposed, each one could easily become one of the top ten most shocking cases in the country, perhaps even ranking high.

After Jiang Tian conducted targeted interrogations of the few survivors he had deliberately kept alive, he discovered that this group had been hired by an overseas agency specializing in hiring hired criminals.

These kinds of cases...

generally involve extremely complex issues, far beyond ordinary difficulties. Finding the mastermind behind them is incredibly difficult.

Domestic companies are relatively easy to deal with, but these agencies are all located overseas; how could they possibly know who the killer is?

After reaching this point, subsequent investigations by criminal investigation experts yielded similarly unsatisfactory results.

In short...

the expert teams, after arriving in Yunhai, faced a series of setbacks.

They arrived with great fanfare, even displaying a degree of arrogance.

However, after arriving and handling two major cases, they began to question their abilities.

Or rather, the difficulties and challenges far exceeded their expectations.

One by one, the investigations became increasingly difficult, and most importantly, based on their experience, it seemed utterly unsolvable.

The attack on Elder Mu, in particular, was utterly unsolvable. The perpetrator was impossible to trace; they were an undocumented immigrant from overseas, and the only clue—the car—was stolen.

At this point, it was essentially a dead end.

No matter how much investigative resources were deployed, even a province-wide house-to-house search would be incredibly costly and difficult, and even if completed, nothing would be found.

Moreover, it was an unrealistic problem in itself.

And this case was even more so.

More than ten armed police officers were attacked.

When the criminal investigation team heard about the case, a special task force was immediately taken over.

After thoroughly understanding the details of the case, they fell silent.

Because they had reached another dead end.

To solve the case, they could only pursue the overseas criminal group.

But this presented another problem: pursuing those overseas required communication with them, but the opposing forces were too numerous.

It appears to be a single, unified entity, but internally it's divided into countless smaller factions with virtually no connection to each other.

Many are even hostile.

In that situation, only condemnation from higher levels is possible.

However, currently, this approach must consider the international situation, and most importantly, the mercenary group these individuals belong to is very powerful.

It's a highly professional armed force.

Eliminating them would require a large number of professionally trained personnel entering the country.

If things go wrong, it could even lead to international disputes.

Considering all these factors, using the criminal organization as a springboard to find the mastermind is essentially a dead end.

That means there are practically no leads.

The criminal investigation team is at a loss.

They came expecting to make a significant impact, but after investigating for so long, they've found nothing.

They can't solve Secretary Mu's case, and even the case of Wen Yulin and the deaths of over ten armed police officers has encountered such difficulties.

And now, you're saying you have a lead?

"Do you know what you're saying? Even the experts on the special task force, under Professor Shang Mingyan's guidance, have no clue what to do. And you actually have a lead on Wen Yulin's case?" Minister Tan asked in astonishment.

These are all major cases, terrifying cases that reach the highest levels of government.

And they are the kind of cases that can't be covered up, and no one can cover them up.

Otherwise, there wouldn't be a joint task force composed of headquarters, the provincial department, and Yunhai.

It's all for the purpose of investigating, but unfortunately, despite gathering almost all of the most elite criminal investigation forces, there has been no progress so far.

However, this also exposes a serious problem.

That is, the border lockdown is now extremely urgent.

"To be honest, regarding this Shimiao Village, according to my investigation, Shimiao Village has business dealings with Wen Yulin," Jiang Tian said with a grin.

Jiang Tian then conducted a detailed investigation into this situation.

It was discovered that Wen Yulin's account contained a business transaction related to Shimiao Village.

It wasn't much.

Only a little over 100,000 yuan.

Although the receiving entity was the village committee, Wen Yulin was only making a personal donation to Shimiao Village.

It wasn't a large sum of money, but over 100,000 yuan represented a lot.

Especially since, in many cases, transactions like this were almost always conducted entirely in cash.

Therefore, the fact that this sum of money was found was because Wen Yulin had leaked information about Shimiao Village to Jiang Tian before his arrest. Jiang Tian had specifically targeted this search, and naturally, he quickly found this sum of money among the vast amount of records.

Putting everything else aside, this sum of money alone was enough to prove the relationship between the two parties.

"This 100,000 yuan donation was made in March 1993, ten years ago. It was a donation made by Wen Yulin in his personal name and from his personal account to the Shimiao Village Committee. However, based on my investigation, this 100,000 yuan should be seen as Wen Yulin's attempt to subsidize and test Shimiao Village, or perhaps an expression of sincerity, or maybe a deliberately left loophole."

Jiang Tian's mind raced.

To this day, Jiang Tian still hadn't figured out whether the relationship between Wen Yulin and Shimiao Village was one of cooperation or collusion.

That makes the case more complicated.

If it were just a subordinate, and the Wenye Group was the only one helping with sales, the case would actually be simpler.

However, based on Jiang Tian's understanding, it's probably not as simple as just a subordinate.



Chapter 444 The shocking words of the legendary merchant: Why are you running?


Following Jiang Tian's words,

an uproar erupted instantly.

Yes, an uproar.

Everyone gasped.

Even Huai Sheng jumped to his feet, excitedly asking, "Are you serious?"

Heaven knows how frantic Yun Province was dealing with these two unsolved cases.

And who could have imagined this?

Jiang Tian's series of statements directly provided new directions for solving these two deadlocked cases.

Even the two major cases that the special task force had given up on were now listed among the top ten unsolved cases of the past few decades, sealed in archives, and it was recommended that Yunhai be severely cracked down on.

And the strictest restrictions be placed on entry and exit from abroad.

To prevent such situations and problems from recurring.
