And Jiang Tian, ​​at this moment, actually provided a reasonable solution.

"Damn it, why didn't you say so sooner?" Minister Tan was instantly stunned.

Completely stunned.

Since you found out, why did you only say so now?

The task force was already preparing to withdraw.

No, some of the experts had even gone home.

Jiang Tian shrugged and said, "I was going to talk to them about it, but the task force said, 'What do you think I, a narcotics officer, know about criminal investigation?'"

Huh?

Minister Tan was taken aback, slightly stunned.

He stared blankly at Jiang Tian.

He took a deep breath.

To be fair, Jiang Tian had been personally involved in both Minister Tan's and Wen Yulin's cases. Given his investigative abilities, joining the task force was perfectly reasonable.

"Is that so?" Minister Tan's gaze gradually turned serious.

"I don't know much about the narcotics aspects of their cases. Unfortunately, I couldn't provide any valuable clues before then," Jiang Tian shook his head.

The task force had a lot of power.

Even though Jiang Tian had responsibilities within the security department, he didn't have the right to interfere in the tasks of the task force.

They could mobilize all the resources and energy of Yunnan Province.

Even the few criminals who survived the Wen Yulin case were taken away by the special task force.

Jiang Tian knew nothing and was disliked.

Jiang Tian naturally had no intention of trying to curry favor.

"I understand," Minister Tan said without elaborating.

His brows, however, had unconsciously furrowed deeply.

This clearly reflected a discriminatory issue.

But Minister Tan couldn't say anything at this point.

"Furthermore, according to my investigation, Shi Hailiang, the village chief of Shimiao Village, is likely the organizer and leader. The first priority is to control Shi Hailiang."

He didn't elaborate.

Since the issue of Yuan Guangliang had just been discussed, he believed the special task force should have already taken action.

In other words, Gao Yangyang should be safe.

Therefore, Jiang Tian focused all his attention on Shimiao Village.

And as Jiang Tian expected...

At this very moment, in the pitch-black night, many staff members at the Anti-Corruption Bureau are still working overtime.

Many people have stereotypes about the system, such as the nine-to-five workday, arriving and leaving on time.

They think people are just slacking off all day.

Are there really such people? Actually, yes, and quite a few at that.

But those were basically people with no ambition for the future, people who had already given up.

Departments like anti-corruption are relatively relaxed on weekdays, but once they start handling cases, it's truly a matter of working day and night.

Once an operation begins, they almost work day and night to investigate clues and cases.

Working overtime is the norm.

Of course, if there are no goals or tasks, and it's just routine, it's relatively easy.

But recently, it's clearly been very busy.

Yuan Guangliang didn't leave.

Even though it was already late, Yuan Guangliang sat in his office, staring blankly at the landline in front of him, seemingly waiting for a call to come in.

His eyes were fixed intently on the phone number.

A moment later, his cell phone on his desk suddenly rang without hesitation.

Yuan Guangliang hurriedly took out his phone, glanced at it, and then answered hastily with a flustered expression.

"Is the matter settled?" a deep voice came from the other end of the line.

"Don't worry, I've acted according to plan, absolutely no mistakes," Yuan Guangliang said hastily.

"Idiot, Jiang Tian has been released. You, now, leave the Anti-Corruption Bureau immediately." The voice on the phone was deep and menacing, seemingly using a voice changer, sounding somewhat artificial.

"This...impossible." Yuan Guangliang's pupils contracted.

The voice on the phone was tinged with anger: "Han Qi has been arrested and detained in a private room, Jiang Tian has been taken away. If you don't leave now, you idiot, you won't be able to leave."

"Yes, I'll prepare right now," Yuan Guangliang said hurriedly and frantically.

As he spoke, Yuan Guangliang hurriedly stood up, but just as he was about to leave, the office door slammed open.

But what lay before him wasn't the path behind the door; on the contrary, a dark and imposing shadow stood before Yuan Guangliang.

Looking down at him, the figure was expressionless.

"Who are you?" Yuan Guangliang asked, forcing a calm demeanor.

"Yuan Guangliang, right? You've been arrested. Please come with us now."

Thump, thump, thump!

A series of heavy footsteps sounded.

Then...

A cold, aloof figure appeared before him.

At this moment, she was staring intently at him.

In truth, Yuan Guangliang had no idea.

The seemingly calm woman before him was experiencing a profound shock.

That's right.

The person who came was Shang Mingyan.

As the head of the special task force, she was an authority throughout the country, and even globally.

Mainly because Shang Mingyan's past achievements were too impressive.

Her professional expertise had also impressed many industry practitioners.

There's a saying: those who don't enter criminal investigation are like frogs in a well when they see Shang Mingyan; those who do are like mayflies.

"Unfazed even by a collapsing mountain," that perfectly describes Shang Mingyan.

A legendary woman.

A legendary woman with twenty years of experience in the criminal investigation industry.

At this moment, however, she stared at Yuan Guangliang with an expression as if she had seen a ghost.

Initially, Shang Mingyan thought Jiang Tian was just talking nonsense.

Or even deliberately retaliating.

But now.

Shang Mingyan had to admit that she had misjudged him, and even misunderstood Jiang Tian too much.

She had previously held a significant prejudice against Jiang Tian.

She didn't even think Yuan Guangliang was problematic, because after reviewing his detailed file, no matter how the investigation progressed, he seemed absolutely unrelated to the attack on Elder Mu.

But at this moment, she was completely stunned. This guy really did have a problem?

"Why are you running?"



Chapter 445 Roughly before dawn, dusk descended into utter chaos.


Shang Mingyan, a legendary figure in criminal investigation, possesses an almost supernatural ability to read minds, particularly regarding micro-expressions, emotions, and reactions. While "mind-reading" might be an exaggeration, he can achieve a similar effect through body language and micro-expressions.

Mastering these techniques is practically equivalent to mind-reading.

In this area, Shang Mingyan has reached a pinnacle. Even Miss Qiu feels uneasy and flustered in his presence, only able to mask her feelings with a deadpan expression.

However, Miss Qiu knows that her mentor has long seen through her.

Her aloofness is feigned; her mentor's aloofness is genuine.

Standing to the side, Miss Qiu glances discreetly at Shang Mingyan. Though he wears a faint smile, his eyes seem to pierce through all secrets.

No secret seems to be concealed before this legendary figure in the criminal investigation world.

Those eyes... Miss Qiu knew her mentor all too well. With just one glance, she understood: this case... was solved.

Or rather...

not necessarily solved.

But Yuan Guangliang definitely had a major problem.

Not to mention Shang Mingyan, Miss Qiu could see it with just a glance.

It could only be said that Yuan Guangliang's feigned composure was nothing more than that of a clown in the eyes of these elite criminal investigators.

"It's a pity, Professor's eyes are too frightening. He can even see through everyone's thoughts with a slight glance. That's why, at forty, he's never had a boyfriend." Miss Qiu's lifeless eyes drifted unsteadily, her thoughts wandering.

Because she suddenly remembered something.

Miss Qiu and Shang Mingyan had a close relationship; after all, she was his direct disciple. Miss Qiu knew Shang Mingyan well, and she understood the loneliness beneath the seemingly aloof exterior of this professor.

And because she saw through him so clearly, she understood him all too well.

Miss Qiu felt a pang of sympathy for the professor.

However, amidst her racing thoughts, a figure suddenly popped into her mind:

Jiang Tian.

That's right.

"That guy, he's so good at pretending, I can't see through him at all. I wonder if he can fool the professor?" Miss Qiu's heart stirred.

She suddenly realized that this case might be an opportunity.

Anyway, the professor is already over forty; if she doesn't seize this opportunity now, she might never have another chance in her life.

But finding a suitable partner isn't so simple.

Especially Shang Mingyan, whose eyes see right through people; almost no one can hide their true feelings from him.

Being too transparent is pointless.

Besides, whenever the man occasionally entertains any lewd thoughts, they don't escape Shang Mingyan's notice.

But only in Miss Qiu's imagination.

Jiang Tian was the most peculiar and mysterious man she had ever met, without a doubt.

At least, in her eyes, Jiang Tian was incredibly skilled at disguise—the kind of disguise that even she couldn't detect a trace of. He was also exceptionally good at putting on airs, and crucially, he was capable.

Perhaps, that guy could actually attract the professor.

Miss Qiu's heart skipped a beat.

However, Shang Mingyan, who was currently focused on Yuan Guangliang, didn't notice that her closed-door disciple, Miss Qiu, was already plotting how to betray her.

At this moment, Shang Mingyan's eyes seemed to penetrate people's hearts. Looking at the forcibly composed Yuan Guangliang, a slightly bright smile appeared on her almost snow-white and exceptionally delicate face.

It was a smile that could launch a thousand ships.

Yuan Guangliang, feigning composure, was instantly stunned.

Such a woman with such气质 (qi zhi, a kind of refined elegance), and such stunning beauty, even Yuan Guangliang couldn't help but be mesmerized.

Shang Mingyan's brows furrowed slightly, and a hint of disgust appeared on her bright face.

That's it.

Her filthy and disgusting thoughts were all visible in her eyes; her entire demeanor, inside and out, exuded a blatant lewdness.

"Excuse me, which unit are you from? My case... I don't know what case I have, or how it happened?" After a moment, Yuan Guangliang, regaining his senses, unconsciously glanced at Shang Mingyan's face out of the corner of his eye.

And his physique.

He thought he was being careful, only using his peripheral vision, but in Shang Mingyan's eyes, it was no different from naked observation.

To be honest...

Yuan Guangliang was, after all, an elite undercover agent.

Or rather...

Yuan Guangliang's mental fortitude was absolutely top-notch; even his every move was conveyed through micro-expressions. In reality, Yuan Guangliang's face was full of righteousness, and on the surface, he appeared absolutely reasonable and well-founded.

He even carried a hint of being wronged.

He really did seem convincing.

But Yuan Guangliang was completely unaware of how ridiculous all his thoughts and subtle actions were in the eyes of these criminal investigation experts.

"We are from the Joint Task Force of Headquarters. I am the team leader, Shang Mingyan."

Shang Mingyan expressionlessly produced her identification.

A person's reputation precedes them.

Although not part of the police system, she was still part of the civil service.

How could anyone not be familiar with the name Shang Mingyan?

"Shang Mingyan?" The previously strong and composed Yuan Guangliang instantly broke out in a cold sweat, his entire body turning ice-cold.

Why her?

And the Joint Task Force of Headquarters?

Hearing this name, and the team leader, Shang Mingyan,

Yuan Guangliang's breathing quickened, his mind completely blank.

"Are you afraid?" The next moment, Shang Mingyan's eyes brightened, even showing surprise.

If initially there was only a 20% chance,

now, it was 60-70%, no, even 80% if Shang Mingyan was bold.

In Yunhai, anyone with connections, or even just a little knowledge, understood what the Joint Investigation Team of Headquarters did.

There was even public news about it online.

[In response to the recent spate of major cases in Yunhai, due to their extremely negative impact, headquarters has decided, effective immediately, to establish a special task force stationed in Yunhai Province to jointly investigate these egregious cases with the Yunhai police.]

At first glance, this might seem like a sign of distrust from higher-ups.

In reality, that's partly true, but there's another layer of meaning.

The problems in Yunhai are increasingly exceeding expectations, the cases are becoming more complex and numerous, and the number and scale of the cases are enormous.

The local police force is facing a severe shortage of officers.

Moreover, don't be fooled by the recent cases where Jiang Tian leads the charge, with the anti-drug team following behind.

In reality, it's a chain reaction. With the collapse of the criminal syndicate, one chaotic small-scale criminal gang after another tries to seize the opportunity to rise up and seize territory. Those criminals who were previously suppressed and dared not show their faces are now also emerging one by one.

The chain reaction is that everyone in the other branches is also basically busy.

Large and small cases are popping up all at once.



Chapter 446 Giving the Legend a Small Shock: Has the Legend Gone Mad?


The saying goes, "Great order is preceded by turmoil."

In fact, this is a process of cleansing and evolution, or even a painful but necessary overhaul.

Yuan Guangliang naturally knew the main task of the joint investigation team, and even why he was there—because of the attack on Secretary Mu.

"Take him away."

Shang Mingyan, suppressing his excitement, hurriedly waved his hand.

Elite police officers rushed forward and instantly subdued Yuan Guangliang.

"Why? Why are you arresting me? I haven't broken the law! I haven't committed a crime! Why?" Yuan Guangliang tried to struggle.

His entire demeanor was that of someone innocent.

To the uninitiated, Yuan Guangliang would truly believe he was innocent; at least his outward appearance was impeccable. Even seasoned criminal investigators wouldn't detect anything amiss.

"What evidence do you have to arrest me?" Yuan Guangliang continued to plead.

Indeed.

Before arriving, through the joint investigation team, Shang Mingyan had obtained all of Yuan Guangliang's information in just a few minutes.

This included whether he had engaged in embezzlement, bribery, or other misconduct.

But unexpectedly,

according to Shang Mingyan's investigation,

Yuan Guangliang was spotless, almost without any blemishes. Since joining the Anti-Corruption Bureau, he had maintained a clean record, and his accounts were clean.

Even more detailed investigations revealed that Yuan Guangliang led a very simple life.

Initially, Shang Mingyan was somewhat skeptical, but this, in turn, became evidence that Yuan Guangliang had serious problems.

"Be careful not to alert him. Arrest him first and interrogate him slowly," Shang Mingyan waved her hand.

This operation...

Shang Mingyan didn't actually bring many people.

Because she understood that if Yuan Guangliang was even slightly a potential breakthrough in the case, it was absolutely crucial to avoid alerting him.

Even when they entered the Anti-Corruption Bureau, they directly contacted the highest-ranking leader of the Anti-Corruption Bureau.

Having reached an agreement with the highest leadership, the anti-corruption bureau's leaders were essentially following the orders of the joint task force.

To put it bluntly, the joint investigation team, in the entire Yunnan province, was now like someone in ancient times, wielding an imperial sword, able to unconditionally utilize all available resources.

"As for you, I hope you can honestly confess who was behind the attack on Secretary Mu. Since I'm here, you should be a smart man. If there wasn't evidence pointing to you, why would our busy joint investigation team mobilize all its resources to arrest you?" Shang Mingyan directly used psychological tactics.

Unfortunately.

Yuan Guangliang was also experienced and shrewd. Although Shang Mingyan could see through his eyes and analyze Yuan Guangliang's emotions through micro-expressions, Shang Mingyan wasn't a god.

He knew Yuan Guangliang was suspicious.

However, Yuan Guangliang refused to confess anything, only proclaiming his innocence.

"Where is Jiang Tian? Where is that Xiao Jiang?"

Interrogation room.

Shang Mingyan's face remained indifferent, though a faint, almost imperceptible, anger flickered in his eyes.

But Miss Qiu, who knew Shang Mingyan intimately, knew that he was truly enraged.

Enraged beyond measure.

Otherwise, such a significant psychological fluctuation wouldn't have occurred. Although very slight, it was still quite remarkable for Shang Mingyan.

"Team Leader, Minister Tan just called us back. Comrade Jiang Tian is currently undertaking an important operation. He's carrying out a very important mission and can't spare the time."

Just then.

Zhang Zhi, wiping sweat, stood before Shang Mingyan, not daring to even raise his head.

Because there was precedent for this. Anyone who knew Shang Mingyan's history knew how terrifying he was.

Rumor had it that if you made eye contact with Shang Mingyan for more than two seconds, he could easily know all your secrets.

Of course, that alone wasn't the problem.

The most terrifying thing was that Shang Mingyan was too stunningly beautiful.

At forty years old, his skin and face made him look like he was only twenty-five or twenty-six, only his demeanor was far too mature, like that of a noblewoman.

Such an alluring aura, coupled with those eyes that seem to read minds—good heavens, anyone who glances at him is bound to embarrass themselves.

No one wants to expose the ugly side of their heart.

As the captain of the Yunhai Criminal Investigation Detachment, how could Zhang Zhi not understand the super-big shot at Criminal Investigation Headquarters?

The legendary figure in the criminal investigation world, Shang Mingyan.

“Bullshit! What case could be more important than Secretary Mu?” Shang Mingyan exploded instantly upon hearing this.

These words instantly made Zhang Zhi gasp.

Because this was tantamount to saying that Leader Tan was talking nonsense. Such an insult was suicidal. Even knowing Shang Mingyan's legendary past, Zhang Zhi still felt terrified.

He didn't dare utter a single word.

After his outburst, Shang Mingyan calmed down.

Without making things difficult for Zhang Zhi, he let him leave and then took out his phone, found Minister Tan's number, and dialed it.

Because in Shang Mingyan's view…

Nothing is more important than this case right now.

"They're narcotics officers, they know nothing about criminal investigation. That Jiang Tian probably got his information from who-knows-where. He doesn't understand the importance of every bit of information right now. This could even be the biggest clue to solving Secretary Mu's attack, yet Jiang Tian is so careless."

Shang Mingyan felt a surge of anger, growing increasingly resentful.

Jiang Tian's ignorance is one thing, but why is Minister Tan joining in this nonsense?

"Team Leader Jiang is probably really busy with something important," Miss Qiu still believed in Jiang Tian.

After all, Jiang Tian had unknowingly become almost invincible in her eyes, probably because they worked in the same municipal bureau and had a lot of contact with him.

But the more she thought she understood Jiang Tian, ​​the more astonished she became. Every time she thought she had figured him out, he would always come up with something new and shocking.

"Criminal investigations are all about speed. Yuan Guangliang's endurance isn't up to par; he can't possibly confess in a short time. By the time he does, it'll be too late. What could be more important than catching the mastermind behind Secretary Mu's attack? This is utter nonsense." Shang Mingyan, though cold in demeanor, spoke calmly and deliberately, emphasizing the gravity of the situation.

However, he paused slightly and shook his head, saying, "I'm afraid Jiang Tian doesn't understand criminal investigations, and therefore doesn't understand the seriousness of the matter. I was being petty."

It was mainly Minister Tan's folly that caused Shang Mingyan to lose his composure.

After calming down, he regained his composure.

"Minister Tan, I need Jiang Tian to come over immediately." Shang Mingyan cut to the chase, without a single wasted word.

"He can't come." Minister Tan was equally blunt.

Buzz!!!

Hearing the calm voice on the other end of the phone, Shang Mingyan was completely dumbfounded.

She made sure she hadn't dialed the wrong number, but Shang Mingyan just felt like the world had gone mad.

Or perhaps...

"Minister Tan, are you crazy? This is crucial to solving the case of Secretary Mu's attack." Shang Mingyan's voice rose slightly as she regained her composure.

But what happened next?

A voice that made Shang Mingyan's mind explode even more mad came through: "What's the matter with Secretary Mu's attack?"

Boom!!!

"Crazy..."



Chapter 447 reveals another golden keyword, Fu Er becomes his underling.


"Are you crazy, Minister Tan?" Shang Mingyan's pupils contracted rapidly.

But she didn't know that Minister Tan, on the other end of the phone, hung up, put down his phone, and stared blankly out of the bus.

Jiang Tian was gathering the various team leaders and assigning tasks.

Jiang Tian's voice clearly reached their ears: "The time has come for the Party and the people to test us. Destroying and annihilating these drug traffickers will be our most perfect answer to the people."

No unnecessary words.

Just a simple sentence.

Minister Tan's gaze was deep. Shang Mingyan was indeed a genius, even a legend.

But Minister Tan couldn't help but sigh, in this world, new talents emerge in every generation.

Shang Mingyan might be a legend in the world of criminal investigation, even a myth. Throughout his life, he had solved numerous major cases. Without Shang Mingyan,

many major cases in the country would remain unsolved.

Shang Mingyan's talent was undeniable, but unfortunately, he encountered Jiang Tian, ​​a Jiang Tian countless times more monstrous than Shang Mingyan!

This guy not only possessed more terrifying criminal investigation and analytical abilities than Shang Mingyan, but also the terrifying ability to complete missions independently.

Everything Shang Mingyan could do, Jiang Tian could do too.

But what Shang Mingyan couldn't do, Jiang Tian could do as well.

Just at this moment.

Minister Tan was unaware that while Jiang Tian was giving a speech,

his mind was actually churning with numerous thoughts, displayed on the blue screen before him, a scene only he could see.

Jiang Tian was adding points, frantically adding points.

Jiang Tian's abilities were already almost sufficient.

Now, Jiang Tian's most important task was to fill in the gaps.

But Jiang Tian had a feeling that he had forgotten something.

Or rather, his current talent as the King of Criminal Investigation was almost insufficient for Jiang Tian to handle the complex information in certain cases.

Of course, this doesn't mean Jiang Tian's abilities are insufficient.

The King of Criminal Investigation—in terms of criminal investigation, he can be said to be comparable to the most terrifying criminal investigation masters in the world.

Master-level existence.

They seem terrifying, but the cases Jiang Tian has handled are incredibly terrifying.

And the cases involved are far too terrifying.

In conclusion.

Jiang Tian, ​​considering the current situation in Yunhai and the complex cases he's recently faced.

Ding!!!

【King of Criminal Investigation (Red) — Legend of Criminal Investigation (Gold)】(Your current criminal investigation abilities and knowledge are comparable to, no, perhaps, combined with other attributes, your criminal investigation abilities far surpass those legendary criminal investigators who have gone down in history, such as Sherlock Holmes. If you could go back in time and meet Holmes, you might become his idol and mentor.)

Good, good, good.

As his 100,000 sin points were cleared,

Jiang Tian's eyes instantly flashed with a terrifying light.

That's right.

A legend in criminal investigation.

More terrifying than the King of Criminal Investigation.

Even more terrifying than a concept.

In other words.

At this moment.

No one has any secrets in front of Jiang Tian; no one in this world can have any secrets in front of Jiang Tian.

Even with just a moment's thought, Jiang Tian instantly detected some flaws in the operation.

Legend of Criminal Investigation.

This skill boosted his abilities in every aspect, most importantly, with a terrifying increase in his thinking. If there were four individuals with the Intelligence of the King of Criminal Investigation, their Intelligence might only be 93 or 94.

However, after reaching the Legend of Criminal Investigation, his Intelligence could jump directly to 99. The remaining point was Jiang Tian's conservative estimate.

It was terrifying. At this moment, Jiang Tian's mind was flooded with countless new pieces of knowledge.

At a glance.

Jiang Tian could even directly see all the logical aspects of the thinking process and use this logic to tailor a perfect action plan.

Immediately afterwards, Jiang Tian didn't delay at all, quickly assigning tasks.

This was also to avoid any unforeseen complications.

It must be admitted that Jiang Tian's command skills had also improved terrifyingly.

Is this operation important? Absolutely.

However, many leaders didn't intervene, standing quietly behind the scenes watching Jiang Tian command from the front lines.

Especially Minister Tan.

At first, his gaze held some doubt, but then it grew increasingly shocked.

“A large contingent of armed police from headquarters, targeting the southeast direction, will infiltrate to apprehend…”

Jiang Tian stood in the crowd, continuously issuing orders.

Every task was flawless.

Every operation was almost without a flaw.

Moreover, the allocation of all police resources was rationally based on the size of the drug traffickers and the level of danger.

They were stunned.

At first, it was alright.

The more they listened, the more stunned they became.

This was originally intended by some leaders to test Jiang Tian, ​​to see if he had the ability to manage a larger-scale operation, that is, his command ability to control the entire situation.

But now…

They were just thinking about how to discuss the operation later.

And if Jiang Tian didn't perform well, they would step in to point out any shortcomings.

But now…

They were stunned.

Completely stunned.

"Good heavens, this is truly a well-organized plan."

The key point is that many high-ranking leaders suddenly realized that even though they were veterans of the police system and high-ranking officials, even with their deepest thinking and the assistance of numerous advisors, the best they could achieve was at best comparable to...

Oh no, comparable is an understatement.

Just this level of meticulousness in the operation.

They probably couldn't achieve such detailed task allocation even after a month of racking their brains.

Furthermore, based on the scale of the drug traffickers and the intelligence, this arrangement had no flaws.

"Does everyone understand?" Jiang Tian asked loudly.

"Understood!!!"

Everyone saluted in unison.

This place wasn't close to Shimiao Village, and Jiang Tian wasn't worried about Shimiao Village noticing, especially since all the roads to Shimiao Village had been blocked.

As for Shi Hailiang.

"Yunhai Detachment, follow me. We are responsible for apprehending the main criminal, Shi Hailiang," Jiang Tian finally gave the order.

However, no one else said anything.

Because of Jiang Tian's abilities, are they crazy? Surely no one would dare question how terrifying Jiang Tian is now?

It was even more so, a terrifying lead that seemed to leapfrog across levels, even precipitous.

"This kid is terrifyingly powerful."

On the bus, after Jiang Tian finished explaining all the tasks, reported inside, and confirmed there were no problems, he quickly led his men out.

The leaders didn't say anything.

It was Jiang Tian's presence that ensured everything went smoothly.

And those on the bus, watching Jiang Tian's retreating figure, could only sigh with such awe at this moment.

Terrifyingly powerful.



Chapter 448 Commanding a large-scale battle with two thousand men, earning the second Special Merit Citation.


"I can't even imagine how terrifying Yunhai would be without Xiao Jiang, these criminal groups hiding in the shadows, with no intelligence whatsoever. The most terrifying thing is that this isn't all of it, just the tip of the iceberg. Imagine how terrifying all these criminal groups combined would be!"

Minister Tan took a deep breath.

The interior lighting wasn't particularly bright; in fact, it was rather soft.

The bus was parked in a very safe location.

Therefore, there was no fear of an attack or someone sneaking in and opening fire.

That would only mean the world was too crazy, and these people wouldn't stand a chance.

With so many leaders here, the scale of the covert security was beyond anyone's imagination.

There were even bodyguards from across the country sitting in the bus.

Responsible for protecting the leaders.

The geographical location also prevented anyone from sneaking in and doing something truly heinous.

Of course, there was also a fear that someone might be desperate and do something rash, so the leaders' location was relatively far out.

As the operatives departed, only the leaders and their guards remained near the bus.

Silence.

A profound silence.

All eyes were on Minister Tan.

But almost none of them showed any sign of objection.

"Speaking of which, Comrade Jiang was truly unexpected. Even his command was flawless."

"To this day, I still don't know what Xiao Jiang can't do."

"The glory in public shouldn't overshadow the hard work behind the scenes. Genius only accounts for a small percentage. The reason Xiao Jiang has truly achieved this, I firmly believe, is because he has been diligently studying in places unseen by others."

"That's right, Chief Gao is absolutely right. I think so too."

Everyone nodded in agreement, a relieved expression unconsciously appearing on their faces.

If nothing unexpected happens.

Next, Yunhai can use this case to

recover some of their reputation.

Yes.

Although the existence of a drug village is inherently damaging to their reputation,

they can also admit their mistakes and correct them.

The impact of this case cannot be concealed; at the very least, it must be treated as such, handled as a national model case, and publicly disclosed within the police system for nationwide study.

Furthermore, the probability of such an incident occurring must be strictly prevented.

Compared to this case,

the attack on Secretary Mu seems less important.

Although it is still one of the most heinous cases in the country, it is still of similar importance.

Minister Tan, seeing the crowd's admiration, unconsciously smiled and continued, "Professor Shang just called, saying there's progress in Secretary Tan's case, but he needs to speak with Comrade Xiao Jiang for further information. He's very anxious, but I had to postpone it. Now, even if the sky falls, we must make way for the drug-using village case in Shimiao Village. Secretary Mu, I believe you feel the same way."

A collective gasp rippled through the crowd.

Everyone was immediately stunned.

A moment later, radiant smiles appeared on their faces.

God knows how much terrifying pressure Yunnan Province has been under these past few days.

The city of chaos truly lives up to its name.

Previously, unverified, egregious incidents were exposed online, or rather, spread through word of mouth.

Of course, much of it was likely true—where there's smoke, there's fire—but it always circulated only within a small circle.

Now?

Although Yunnan Province, or more specifically Yunhai, is constantly cracking major cases.

According to online announcements:

Today we seized 2,000 kilograms, tomorrow 3,000 kilograms, the day after 800 kilograms. We're a little unhappy because our city bureau only seized 800 kilograms today; the achievement is small, and we have a vague sense of dissatisfaction.

Add to that the continuous stream of major cases, etc.

With more cases solved,

the number of people paying attention online has naturally increased.

This hasn't all brought positive effects.

Because the number of cases you uncover every day is so terrifying, how terrifying are the cases you haven't uncovered?

What was originally announced as a victory to claim credit and enhance Yunhai's reputation has ironically solidified Yunhai's reputation as a "Little Gotham City of Sin," a truly laughable situation.

Now, several more major cases have emerged.

The crucial point is that they haven't been solved.

This has left them sweating profusely.

The pressure is immense.

They never expected Jiang Tian to be so capable.

"Damn, I knew it! Comrade Jiang has potential!"

"My god, Secretary Mu's case actually found a lead!"

"I thought it was just a possibility, but since Team Leader Shang called personally, it must be confirmed."

"Even Team Leader Shang's special task force returned empty-handed, and Comrade Jiang found a lead so easily all by himself! Is he crazy?"

"The key is, it doesn't even interfere with the investigation of the drug village case."

The more they talked, the more shocked they became.

Because they suddenly realized...

Jiang Tian wasn't involved in Secretary Mu's case.

In fact, while investigating the drug-infested village, he not only kept an eye on Secretary Mu's case but almost solved it as well.

The worst thing in investigating a case is when the leads go cold and the line of reasoning breaks; then it's all over.

As long as there's some clear information and clues,

solving the case is only a matter of time.

Unfortunately.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was unaware that his superiors back home were gossiping about him.

He was leading his men, approaching Shimiao Village.

After all, it was a large village with over two thousand inhabitants.

Over seven hundred households, each with one house.

Some households even had two or three plots of land.

The workload was enormous for everyone.

Even with over two thousand military and police personnel deployed,

if the workload was even evenly distributed, each household had fewer than three people.

Even including those who got married but whose household registrations weren't separated, there are many more.

One household might have two or three households registered under the same name.

"Kid, how do you know so much about this? How many times have you been here?"

Song Liguo squatted down beside Jiang Tian, ​​listening intently to Jiang Tian's voice. Jiang Tian was even holding a pair of binoculars—a night-vision device with excellent clarity; after all, it was for internal use, definitely top-tier for a department-level operation.

Jiang Tian, ​​his face serious, continued issuing orders into the walkie-talkie.

If Song Liguo was shocked that Jiang Tian knew all the action teams in such a short time, then the details Jiang Tian assigned to each person—even down to specifics like assigning one person to lead two others to be responsible for a particular residence—were even more astounding.

Sometimes Jiang Tian would reveal the identities of the criminals inside the residence, sometimes he wouldn't.

Because, regardless, no one would be allowed to escape.

"Enter quietly, try not to arouse suspicion, and avoid any further accidents or chaos."

Jiang Tian gave the orders.

Dressed in plain clothes, he led his men and infiltrated the village.

This command role was actually a new experience for Jiang Tian.

While anyone else in the group held a higher position than him, tonight, Jiang Tian was the one most familiar with the target's situation.

In other words, Jiang Tian had volunteered.

Having learned his lesson from Zeng Fan's previous experience, Jiang Tian was genuinely hesitant to entrust the task to anyone else.

Last time, Jiang Tian could still cover it up, but this drug-infested village involved two to three thousand people. A single misstep could lead to consequences that Jiang Tian couldn't possibly manage.

Fortunately,

Jiang Tian didn't let Minister Tan down.

Using his achievements, he seized command of the operation in one fell swoop.

Because this time, Jiang Tian wouldn't relinquish the credit.

He wanted to make history, to earn his second Special Merit for his work on the drug-infested Shimiao Village. To ensure absolute success, he even upgraded his rank to King of Criminal Investigation.

And this time, he would directly command two thousand people at once.

Although countless leaders were monitoring him from behind walkie-talkies.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​this was undoubtedly a terrifying and enormous leap forward.

After all, this operation involved four to five thousand people. Commanding two thousand—except during wartime, Jiang Tian never imagined he'd ever have such a day.

"The Jiang family is emitting auspicious smoke."



Chapter 449 The moonlight is beautiful, but murderous intent is lurking.


Shimiao Village.

The village chief's house.

The scenery here is beautiful.

"The moonlight is bright tonight, and the scenery is even more beautiful. I must say, this is truly a wonderful place."

A voice of praise rang out.

Village Chief Shi Hailiang.

He swirled the teacup in front of the stone table, and after a series of practiced tea-making movements, the aroma wafted from the table.

The aroma of the tea was invigorating.

In the pitch-black night, Shi Hailiang looked at the man across the stone table, a hint of emotion and nostalgia in his eyes. "Can you imagine what this village was like twenty years ago—no, even ten years ago?" he said. "Broken bricks and tiles, people trekking over mountains every day to plant cheap tea trees, and the resulting income couldn't even guarantee a basic living."

Shi Hailiang stood up as he spoke. He was wearing a black suit, his hands behind his back, looking up at the stars.

He was right.

Ten years ago.

This village wasn't always like this.

From their vantage point, standing on the second-floor balcony of a villa, they could see much of the village's scenery.

While "every family a villa" might be an exaggeration, there was no doubt that almost every house in the village was incredibly luxurious.

But who could have imagined?

Ten years ago, Shimiao Village was a notoriously impoverished village in the area.

Because of its proximity to the border, it had little development potential, and they were practically forgotten.

If it weren't for him.

If it weren't for Shi Hailiang, this village would still be the same as it was ten years ago, how could it be like it is now, with everyone striving for a better life?

No.

In this year 2003.

He led the entire village towards prosperity.

"Hahaha, good, well said."

The man sipping tea stood up abruptly, a bright smile spreading across his face.

Following Shi Hailiang, the man walked to his side, his gaze filled with admiration as he looked at the bustling scenery of Shimiao Village.

Indeed.

In this era, 2003, it was still a developing country. Even in many areas of developed provinces, the villages were dilapidated, and simply being able to make ends meet was a challenge.

Let alone a backward province like Yunnan.

This village was like a miracle: "But being so open and aboveboard, aren't you afraid of being targeted one day?"

Shi Hailiang paused slightly, then replied, "I wasn't afraid before."

"Oh, and now?" the man next to Shi Hailiang asked, puzzled.

"Now I'm afraid," Shi Hailiang said without hesitation.

That's right.

Thinking back, Shi Hailiang felt a pang of helplessness. In the past, he truly wasn't afraid.

Back then, their relationship with the Yunhai police was like two lines maintaining friendship and peace; as long as they weren't crossed, they coexisted almost peacefully.

They committed crimes, but not openly or excessively; within their capabilities, they tried their best to minimize the impact.

They also wouldn't disrupt Yunhai's development, since Yunhai's growth was a win-win situation for the criminal group.

However, not long ago, everything changed.

Before, their relationship wasn't exactly friendly; it was more of an unspoken understanding.

As for those who broke the tacit agreement, they have all been eliminated from this world through various means.

“You mean Jiang Tian?” the man next to Shi Hailiang suddenly asked.

“That’s right.”

Shi Hailiang nodded: “Jiang Tian’s appearance completely shattered a tacit agreement and disrupted the balance of our criminal forces. Now, the underworld is in chaos.”

The man fell silent.

Shi Hailiang also fell silent.

Over the years, some of the development relied on foreign forces, while some rose from the ground up independently. Regardless, the entire Yunhai underworld maintained a certain balance.

If Jiang Tian understood, he would surely think of the Four Emperors from a certain anime in his past life.

They had their own set of rules and principles.

But now…

With Jiang Tian’s appearance, the structure of the underworld has been disrupted.

“We’ve always maintained a friendly cooperative relationship with our partners, but not long ago, our biggest partner, Wen Yulin, unfortunately, was arrested. Even the Wenye Group has collapsed, let alone our Shimiao Village.” Shi Hailiang shook his head.

“What do you mean?” the man beside him asked anxiously.

“My meaning is clear: after this business deal, we’re going to retire from this business. From now on, Shimiao Village will no longer engage in this.” Shi Hailiang’s gaze was deep and unsettling.

“Then what will you do?” the man asked, puzzled.

“I don’t know.”

To be honest, Shi Hailiang himself didn’t know what to do.

This poor and backward mountain village.

Even whether he could successfully retire from this business and stop in time, Shi Hailiang himself wasn’t sure.

When he could bring benefits to Shimiao Village, the villagers listened to him and obeyed his every word.

But when Shi Hailiang can no longer bring benefits to the village, then who knows who he is?

Now, Shi Hailiang is leading Shimiao Village to prosperity.

But as the saying goes, it's easy to go from frugality to extravagance, but difficult to go from extravagance to frugality—that's the point.

When villagers who had tried earning hundreds of thousands, even millions, a month suddenly left the industry, they were nothing. Having grown accustomed to lavish spending, could they possibly endure a few hundred yuan a month for hard work?

How could that be possible?

But now, Shi Hailiang had no other choice.

“So soon?” the man asked, surprised.

“That’s right. I’ve already had the drug-making equipment destroyed. After you take this last batch of drugs, we’ll completely forget about each other. If you get caught, I hope you won’t implicate us, although even if you do, it won’t matter much.” Shi Hailiang spoke meaningfully.

“You’re leaving Shimiao Village?” the man instantly understood.

“After I’ve taken care of everything, what happens to Shimiao Village will be none of my business.”

Shi Hailiang’s gaze was complex as he spoke. Fortunately, he had brought tremendous change and wealth to this village; it was time for him to leave.

Besides, he already had a considerable amount of money.

This was a path of no return.

Shi Hailiang had anticipated this; fortunately, he had a backup plan.

"Shi Hailiang, are you really willing to abandon this prosperous village you've built with your own hands?" The man hadn't expected Shi Hailiang to show such courage.

As he spoke, the man gradually turned his head.

A face gradually became clear.

This was a man about the same age as Shi Hailiang, but with a completely different demeanor.

Shi Hailiang had gleaming hair, wore gold-rimmed glasses, and looked refined, though his eyes were deep-set, and he had a prominent, hooked nose.

He gave off an impression of both refinement and wisdom.



Chapter 450 The whole truth is revealed, criminal history


The man standing next to Shi Hailiang was completely different, his body a mass of solid muscles.

He had a huge scar on his face, a fierce expression, and was dressed in camouflage.

“I have no choice. Now, I strongly suspect we’re being targeted,” Shi Hailiang sighed.

But the man beside Shi Hailiang suddenly erupted.

“I’ve investigated. Wen Yulin is now in a vegetative state, still in intensive care.”

The man paused slightly, then lunged forward, grabbing Shi Hailiang’s collar and roaring, “Ten years ago, we left this village. We swore we’d make something of ourselves in this world. Now I’ve established myself in Myanmar, built a huge corporation, and you’re telling me you’re quitting?”

It was clear the man had been suppressing his anger, and now it had completely exploded.

His eyes were bloodshot.

“Hai Chao, stop,” Shi Hailiang said calmly.

Bang!!!

Shi Haichao punched Shi Hailiang in the face, knocking him to the ground. “How can I stop? I lost over fifty brothers trying to clean up your mess. Now you want me to stop? Where were you before? Can we turn back? No, we can’t. We’re done for. Shi Hailiang, you’re dreaming! I knew you had ulterior motives, and I was right.”

“Is this why you risked everything to come back personally?” Shi Hailiang wiped the blood from his mouth.

“That’s right.”

Shi Haichao now squatted menacingly beside Shi Hailiang, his previous friendly demeanor completely gone.

More than ten years ago.

Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang left the village.

The two went to Myanmar to make a living, and there they discovered that drug trafficking was relatively underdeveloped. This gave them an idea.

They decided to have Shi Hailiang return to the village.

In Myanmar, they initially lacked a base of operations.

Furthermore, wanting to help their fellow villagers prosper, Shi Hailiang returned and officially established the village as a drug-producing village.

They even set up their own research and development base in the village.

This was used to research and improve a new type of flour.

And surprisingly,

their village did have a talented person—a chemist. Given that drug trafficking in Myanmar was mostly opium-related, their newly developed flour was a surefire way to succeed.

Successfully, they made it a success.

More and more families in the village joined in.

Meanwhile, Shi Haichao, with his cheap and competitive flour, successfully expanded and grew his business in Myanmar.

Later, they even formed a conglomerate.

For example, the group that viciously attacked Wen Yulin was Shi Haichao's subordinate.

Over the years, the two sides have become indispensable to each other.

Besides Shi Haichao, the village itself also has other sales channels. Because of its good reputation, and through Shi Haichao's promotion, more and more people are involved, even turning exports into domestic sales.

In other words, the drugs were shipped from their village to Shi Haichao, who then resold them to other criminal organizations.

This cooperation had been conducted very discreetly over the years, which is why Shimiao Village had been committing crimes for over a decade without being discovered.

And the starting point for all of this, or rather, their first benefactor and buyer, was none other than...

It was Wen Yulin's sponsorship that provided them with their initial funding.

It was also their first source of capital.

Wen Yulin was a benefactor in their lives, and it was through Wen Yulin that their business grew bigger and bigger. Now, Shi Haichao has even recruited many young people from the village to work with him in Myanmar.

They now have their own armed force.

And their power was growing increasingly terrifying, even capable of turning against Wen Yulin.

Wen Yulin wasn't worried about the others; what worried him were precisely these two from Shimiao Village.

"You've forgotten our agreement! We've just gotten everything on track, and you're telling me you're quitting? If you quit, how am I supposed to develop further? How am I supposed to support all my men?" Shi Haichao roared.

Although Shi Hailiang appeared refined, he possessed a keen sense for the heroin trade.

Most importantly, Shi Haichao's forces were in a somewhat awkward position. If they built a factory in Myanmar, they feared it would be blown up the very next day.

There were too many enemies, too many people who wanted them dead.

In other words, if Shi Hailiang quit, Shi Haichao would lose his best source of drugs.

He absolutely wouldn't allow such a thing to happen.

"If you walk by the river long enough, it's bound to happen sooner or later. While everyone's got plenty of money now, do some small business, and you'll be set for life, living a happy and carefree life. If you continue like this, none of us will have a good ending. The winds are getting more and more dangerous, you know that?" Shi Hailiang had a keen sense of smell.

But in Chao Haichao's eyes, Shi Hailiang was just a coward.

*Click!*

Shi Haichao cocked a bullet, pointing it at Shi Hailiang's forehead: "No one can stop me, including you. Don't push me."

With a bullet pointed at his head, Shi Hailiang's emotions exploded: "I can't lead the village into the abyss. Continuing will only lead to destruction. Haichao, we've already made enough, and you too, aren't you satisfied yet? What else do you want?"

"Now you're talking to me about morality and righteousness? If it weren't for you and Wen Yulin in the first place, would I have done these things? Hahaha, you, Shi Hailiang, have actually reformed? Hahaha..." Shi Haichao seemed to have heard the biggest joke in the world.

The two men's emotions grew increasingly intense.

Just then,

a clicking sound rang out.

Although they were arguing fiercely, their years of criminal activity and constant presence along the riverbank meant that even as their interest escalated, they remained highly vigilant.

When the clicking sound rang out,

both men simultaneously stopped talking, especially Shi Haichao, who immediately turned his gun towards the window from which the sound came: "Who's there? Come out! If you don't come out, I'll shoot right now!"

"It's probably just some brothers from our clan."

Their presence here at this moment, though their hiding methods were somewhat cowardly and covert, didn't raise any serious concerns.

After all,

their clansmen had indeed been finding Shi Hailiang quite often lately.

However, they maintained a degree of vigilance.

They didn't fire rashly, but they also didn't blindly trust him.

"Come out," Shi Hailiang shouted.

The next moment.

Crack!!!

Accompanied by the sound of shattering glass.

A moment later.

A thud.

A dark figure fell to the ground through the broken window.

"No, this is..." Shi Haichao roared, his eyes bloodshot, his face contorted.

The next moment.

Bang!!!



Chapter 451 Let's go down a bit and meet up.


It must be said, Shi Hailiang's villa was indeed well-built.

In fact, the overall design was extremely luxurious.

However, it also had a touch of retro style, with tiles on the second floor.

Jiang Tian led his group to the rooftop.

Although the villa wasn't tall, only two stories, it was very large.

And the interior structure was also very high.

For this operation, Jiang Tian brought many people, who were spread out in the surrounding area to lie in wait. Jiang Tian himself led the people who had been carrying out the mission.

Lu Chun, Zhao Wei, and Chu Jing, the three of them, infiltrated the rooftop, using a simple ladder to climb up from the back.

That's why Jiang Tian's gaze was so intense as he looked at Zhao Wei.

"Boss, I, I didn't mean to," Zhao Wei said, sweating profusely and forcing an embarrassed smile.

That's right, it was Zhao Wei who had stepped on and knocked over the tiles.

In fact, as Jiang Tian's strength has grown, the security personnel who have followed him have been perfectly capable. They can keep up with the intensity of missions and won't hold him back, especially in dangerous ones.

Recently, Zhao Wei's skills have even surpassed those of his menial workers.

Although he's been undergoing intensive physical training, it's clear that such skills can't be improved in a short time, even with Jiang Tian's help.

Under these circumstances, Jiang Tian rarely takes Zhao Wei on dangerous missions anymore. However, today's mission is practically a sure thing, and Jiang Tian isn't the heartless type.

Within his capabilities,

in this kind of mission—one that brings honor to his family, even a major case that could become a textbook example—Jiang Tian is willing to give Zhao Wei a stage to showcase his abilities.

Considering this mission, Jiang Tian's current achievements, and the impact on Shimiao Village, this is, in short, a case worthy of a top-tier merit.

Moreover, this is a classic case of a top-tier merit award.

Unlike the previous top-tier merit award Jiang Tian received, where although his actions were outrageous and extreme, his achievements were terrifying, to some extent, he even reshaped the landscape of neighboring Myanmar.

He established a terrifying rule and deterrent effect on various forces.

To be precise, the main reason Jiang Tian received a top-tier merit award last time was the impact he generated during that operation.

It wasn't because of rescuing hostages, simply killing a drug lord, or escaping from an encirclement by hundreds of armed drug traffickers.

No, it should be said that he escaped from an allied encirclement by a vast network of criminal groups.

These all have their reasons, and they are all objective. The main reason was the combined impact of all these factors, which greatly deterred all criminal forces in Myanmar, instilling fear and awe in them towards their neighbor.

The impact was actually greater than the actual significance.

But this time, it's completely different.

A massive case involving thousands of people, and the horrific crimes and impact of years of drug trafficking.

This is a case that can be publicized.

However, the previous special-class merit case cannot be publicized.

Frankly speaking, Jiang Tian causing trouble overseas is simply unacceptable.

If it were explained clearly, his neighbors would probably publicize it all over the world.

This kind of thing can only be understood implicitly.

Even when Jiang Tian received the special-class merit, there was some publicity and promotion, causing quite a stir. Through television media, many people knew that a special-class merit had reappeared in China, a merit that hadn't been seen for decades.

But what exactly he did, and what merit he earned, remained a mystery to many, fueling their intense curiosity.

Taking down Shimiao Village, barring any unforeseen circumstances, is a case that can be directly publicized.

A one-man investigation.

A lone hero.

No.

This is a miracle. One person discovered the evidence, perfectly preventing the future harm this criminal group would cause.

Another person infiltrated the village and discovered the problems within Shimiao Village.

Even more, one person reviewed all the records, finding information on all potential criminals in Shimiao Village.

And now, Jiang Tian is directly commanding all the military and police to surround the entire Shimiao Village.

This is like a closed loop in the operation, the final link, the final link in achieving a top-tier merit.

Jiang Tian understands how many leaders are watching him, testing him.

Whether he's a reckless brute charging blindly or a decisive general with strategic foresight, all will be revealed in this operation.

If successful,

Jiang Tian will have completely proven his abilities, without any flaws.

And after this operation, if he performs perfectly, he might very well be promoted exceptionally.

Jiang Tian hadn't initially had any plans, but now, if Jiang Peng were to be promoted—a one-person-one-position system—Jiang Tian might very well reap enormous benefits from this transfer and rise to a higher level.

From leader of the special emergency response team, he could successfully transition to captain.

Jiang Tian took a deep breath.

His gaze remained unchanged, deep and unwavering.

The plan was quite good, but Jiang Tian hadn't expected the criminals to discover it ahead of time.

However, Jiang Tian's face showed no anger; instead, it was tinged with excitement.

That's right.

Jiang Tian should have been angry about the unexpected turn of events, but he couldn't resist the fact that he had overheard so much useful information.

He hadn't expected that besides Shi Hailiang, there was another big fish here.

"Never seen him before, but that makes sense. He left the village more than ten or twenty years ago and has almost never openly appeared in the village. It's normal not to have any information on him."

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

Although he had been caught, it didn't matter.

In this situation...

With just the two of them, even though Jiang Tian sensed there were many bodyguards outside the villa, he felt no pressure whatsoever.

Even if there were only a few people, and with his reinforcements outside, Jiang Tian wouldn't feel any pressure even if those numbers doubled or tenfold, without any reinforcements outside.

"Alright, nothing serious, go downstairs and meet them," Jiang Tian smiled.

Then, with a somersault from the rooftop, he kicked through the villa's glass and flew into the second floor.

*Crack!!!*

As soon as Jiang Tian landed, the sound of two guns being cocked rang out.

But this wasn't the end of it.



Chapter 452 Accidentally, Another Major Case Was Solved


Following closely behind Jiang Tian, ​​several people jumped in simultaneously. Jiang Tian glanced around; everyone was there. The only one missing was Zhao Wei.

Zhao Wei was panicking. He swallowed hard, watching the others move nimbly, leaping over two meters into the villa.

The second floor was actually quite high, roughly seven or eight meters. A fall could be fatal, or at best, a broken leg. But the crucial point was, Zhao Wei couldn't jump over it!

There was a huge difference.

Zhao Wei was, after all, just an ordinary drug enforcement officer. Compared to Jiang Tian—no, not even compared to Jiang Tian, ​​but even compared to Jiang Tian's other subordinates—he was a million times weaker.

Because the security personnel were the elite of the elite.

These people possessed unique skills, honed from childhood. Someone like Zhao Wei, even if he worked himself to death, couldn't possibly keep up.

Of course, Jiang Tian wasn't looking at Zhao Wei anymore.

His gaze was fixed on the two people in front of him.

Jiang Tian recognized Shi Hailiang; he'd seen him in files, and because he was the village chief, Shi Hailiang's information was quite comprehensive, including his biography.

According to the files, over ten years ago, Shi Hailiang had worked as a farmer outside the village for a period. Shortly after, he returned and became the village chief.

It was around this time that Wen Yulin transferred 100,000 yuan to the village.

100,000 yuan ten years ago, 100,000 yuan ninety years ago—a household with 10,000 yuan was already incredibly wealthy, let alone 100,000 yuan.

And it was presented as a donation, seemingly to help a poor mountain village.

But now, Jiang Tian finally understood that the 100,000 yuan was actually for initial startup capital, and even more so, for the development of drugs.

“Who are you?”

Shi Haichao put Shi Hailiang down, holding a pistol, and looked warily at the several people who had suddenly jumped in through the window.

A moment later, Shi Haichao abruptly turned to look at Shi Hailiang on the ground: "Such impressive skills, Brother Liang! I never expected this. When did you cultivate such an outstanding young man in the village? Not a single word leaked out."

Indeed.

Shi Haichao had assumed it was one of Shi Hailiang's men, and for a moment, he had even relaxed his guard.

"Cough cough cough..."

"What men?"

Shi Hailiang shook his head.

"Not your men?" Shi Haichao turned sharply.

No wonder, when Jiang Tian and his men infiltrated, they were all dressed in plainclothes.

But there was no doubt about it.

When Shi Haichao realized what was happening, he shouted loudly: "Cops?"

"What cops? Don't be so rude. We are righteous law enforcers." Jiang Tian brushed the shards of glass off his clothes.

It must be said, the villa's interior was quite nice; the glass was double-glazed, and someone else really couldn't break it.

But Jiang Tian's words...

This instantly enraged Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao.

Although they had misunderstood Jiang Tian and his group, their subconscious alertness made them immediately raise their pistols.

"Did you hear what we just said?"

Shi Hailiang's face was grim.

"I heard you perfectly clearly. I never expected there to be another big fish here besides you." Jiang Tian's eyes gleamed as he looked at Shi Haichao.

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian was astonished.

Shi Haichao, the culprit who attacked Wen Yulin, causing the deaths of over ten armed police officers, was also here.

Actually, Jiang Tian couldn't just leave the country easily anymore.

In fact,

the authorities in neighboring Myanmar had already listed Jiang Tian as a dangerous individual, prohibited from entering the country except under special circumstances.

What a joke.

He saw through it but didn't say anything. The first time, he took down a major drug lord, fighting his way through hundreds of miles of armed men, even with his dependents, amidst a vast horde of them.

He truly fought his way over hundreds of miles with a single shot, killing countless armed men.

The second time was when he wiped out the Yun family.

Although it was to assist in the investigation, Jiang Tian was too ruthless, killing far too many people.

Even though those people were criminals, the Burmese authorities were frightened and issued a strict order prohibiting Jiang Tian from entering their territory at will.

To prevent damage to relations between the two sides, it would be difficult for Jiang Tian to enter Myanmar to investigate the Wen Yulin attack.

But unexpectedly, a pleasant surprise came so suddenly.

The Wen Yulin attack was an undeniably major case.

The severity of the case was beyond imagination—a public attack, a bizarre battle even urban crime films couldn't depict, and the use of high explosives.

Due to the large number of witnesses, despite the blockade, numerous rumors still circulated, causing extremely negative consequences.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, never expected that this unintentional act would lead to such a positive outcome.

His plan was simply to eliminate Shimiao Village, and incidentally, he solved the Wen Yulin attack case as well.

Then, Jiang Tian thought of the special criminal investigation team.

It seems they've taken over Wen Yulin's case and, after reviewing the details, have included it in their plans alongside Secretary Mu—the leads have reached a dead end, a completely dead end.

They've even begun sealing off evidence, planning to investigate again later when there's a breakthrough at the section level; now, it's just a waste of resources.

If...

the special task force knows the case has been solved, I wonder what they'll think.

Jiang Tian's thoughts raced.

He then looked at the two men, especially Shi Haichao.

His face was fierce, his eyes truly filled with murderous intent.

Jiang Tian was too sensitive to this.

That murderous aura, that fierce demeanor—it couldn't be there without ruthless killing.

"Why? Why are you forcing me? I'm going to retire from crime, our whole village is going to retire, why are you still forcing me?"

At this moment, Shi Hailiang was already quite agitated, almost collapsing, shouting.

That's right.

Shi Hailiang was truly preparing to retire from his life of crime; everyone had become wealthy, the entire village had prospered.

Shi Hailiang felt he had done his best.

He was about to live a secluded life in the mountains.

Why, why did these people come looking for him at this time?

Why did they find him at this time?

“Shi Hailiang, I told you long ago, you can never retire from this life. Now, what can you do? There's only one way: kill these cops and go all the way down the criminal underworld. I don't know why they barged in to die, but since it was their own choice, don't blame me for being ruthless.” Shi Haichao's face was contorted with rage.

If these people had overheard this or been discovered, and had frantically tried to escape, perhaps they would have had a chance to survive.

But now, there was only one path: certain death.

Bang!!!

Thud!!!

A gunshot rang out, followed by the sound of a bullet piercing flesh, but it was masked by the gunshot, so only Jiang Tian's keen hearing could detect it.

The next moment.

Shi Haichao looked at his arm.

Thud!!

The pistol fell to the ground.

Shi Haichao looked at his arm, which was now soaked in blood.

The tattered clothes, the mangled flesh, and the excruciating pain in his arm after the initial numbness were constant reminders to Shi Haichao.

"When?" Shi Haichao looked at the person opposite him, completely bewildered.

A black gun muzzle was smoking.

"Drawing a gun in front of the boss, how adorable!" Lü Chun took two steps closer, a bright smile on his face.

These two broken guns, let alone the boss, even he could kill them before they could fire.



Chapter 453 There are so many criminals, why are they chasing me relentlessly?


"Who exactly are you?"

Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao were both deathly pale.

They hadn't seen Jiang Tian make a move; they just felt a blur before their eyes, and then Shi Haichao was shot.

It was too sudden.

So sudden that they hadn't expected it at all.

They had assumed these men had come in looking for death.

After all, when they jumped in through the window, they weren't carrying any weapons, at least not in their hands. They could have easily taken out their pistols, even disengaged the safety, and simply pulled the trigger to easily finish off Jiang Tian.

But reality was too sudden.

Even if Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao were fools, they would clearly understand now.

The terrifying power of the man before them was definitely not simple.

"Let me introduce myself. I don't know if you've heard of me before. Um, my name is Jiang Tian."

Jiang Tian made no attempt to hide anything. These two men were basically finished; Jiang Tian wasn't afraid of any retaliation or threats.

And when the two men heard this name...

Shi Hailiang even lost the courage to fire.

*Thud!*

The pistol in Shi Hailiang's hand fell to the ground, his face blank as he stared at Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian.

A person's reputation precedes them.

Outside of Yunhai, and even further away, outside of Yunnan Province, the greater the distance, the lower the profile. No matter how many rumors circulate, Jiang Tian's secret identity cannot be faked.

Even though it's an unspoken rule, many criminal groups, including those in Yunhai, know Jiang Tian, ​​there are still certain precautions. At least, if keeping his identity secret can bring Jiang Tian even a little more safety, the higher-ups will try their best to suppress information about him.

But clearly, Shimiao Village is not among them.

The moment the pistol in Shi Hailiang's hand finally fell to the ground, both Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao knew they were finished.

Especially Shi Hailiang, who stared at Jiang Tian in shock, asked, "Why are you personally dealing with us?"

"After all, it's a drug village, a drug village with thousands of people involved. Any scale of action is worthwhile. Don't even mention me; with your scale of crime, no matter how high-ranking your leader is, you deserve it," Jiang Tian sneered.

The crimes they committed...

They deserve to die a hundred times over.

"There are so many drug dealers in Yunhai, countless drug dealers, why are you relentlessly targeting our Shimiao Village?" Shi Hailiang shouted emotionally.

That's right.

Shi Hailiang's composure crumbled.

Everything was fine; he had already begun transferring his assets, made all the preparations, the plane was taking off, he was leaving this village, leaving this country forever, seeking development abroad.

The village was going to completely retire from drug trafficking, a complete severing of ties with the past.

Why, at this crucial moment?

He was discovered.

Where did the evidence come from?

When was it discovered?

One question after another popped into Shi Hailiang's mind.

"Fine, Jiang Tian, ​​I know you, and I know your capabilities, but what makes you say we're involved in thousands of cases? Are you just making baseless accusations?" Shi Hailiang wouldn't give up until he saw him in person.

Or rather...

Even now, for these two seasoned veterans, Jiang Tian's appearance was simply too sudden, catching them completely off guard.

But only for a moment.

Mainly, Jiang Tian was too terrifying, frightening them. His terrifying track record was something even boasting couldn't replicate!

It was simply outrageous.

Because of this, they immediately lost their ability to think.

But after regaining their senses, they returned to their rationality and their immense resilience.

Now, the two barely exchanged a glance before understanding each other's meaning.

Caught red-handed.

"Do you think you can escape legal punishment by being stubborn? The net of justice is vast, and its meshes are wide, yet nothing escapes it. If we didn't have evidence, would we have taken action?"

Jiang Tian's face was cold.

These people were truly ruthless.

To what extent were they pure?

How much drug trafficking had they committed over the years?

And how many people have been harmed by these drug crimes? How terrifying are the indirect consequences?

“Shi Hailiang, for so many years, you led the entire village in a frenzied drug production spree, reaping enormous illegal profits. Countless families have been torn apart and lives lost because of these drug crimes. For your own selfish gain, how can you face the countless people of Yunhai?” Jiang Tian said coldly.

Hearing this, they were horrified.

That's right.

The entire village's drug production capacity, combined with Shi Haichao and the Wenye Group…

Can you even imagine the terrifying scale of drug crimes they caused?

Can you even imagine it?

Shi Hailiang's face froze.

He even turned somewhat pale.

"And Shi Haichao, although your forces are committing crimes overseas, you are still a citizen of this land. All your illegal activities warrant your arrest. Moreover, you've gone so far as to cause the deaths of over ten armed police officers to eliminate witnesses. Such ruthlessness—how dare you still try to justify your actions here?"

Jiang Tian didn't want to continue.

Because under the Eye of Judgment, nothing could be hidden.

When Jiang Tian scrutinized Shi Haichao with his Eye of Judgment, the recent crimes included the heinous attack on Wen Yulin.

The combined sin value of these two individuals...

truly exceeded Jiang Tian's expectations.

It was over 30,000.

Just these two people alone had over 30,000 sin values. How many people in the entire village were involved?

Even averaging fifty sin values ​​per person, seven hundred people would still amount to nearly 40,000.

And this is a minimum estimate; the actual number is definitely over 40,000.

Including these two, their sin value totals a full 70,000.

With good luck, it's even possible the sin value could reach 80,000 or 90,000, or even close to 100,000. Besides this, Jiang Tian smiled as he looked at Shi Haichao.

Compared to the trapped Shi Hailiang, this unexpected joy from Shi Haichao is what truly captures Jiang Tian's attention.

Because Jiang Tian is already considering whether he can use Shi Haichao to further control this overseas criminal group.

Even if not control,

through Shi Haichao, it would still be considered a complete annihilation of a huge overseas criminal group.

Especially the hired criminal groups, the threat they pose is immense.

Especially within the country, it's a terrifying and uncontrollable force, as seen in the Wen Yulin case.

*Crack!!!*

Accompanied by a crisp sound.

Even if Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang wanted to resist, they were powerless against them.

Shi Haichao even tried to turn and run, but Jiang Tian rushed forward and kicked him from behind, slamming him against the wall with a cracking sound; it was unclear which bone had broken.

"I told you, I told you, you can't walk by the river without getting your shoes wet, but I never expected..."

Shi Hailiang, being held up, looked at Jiang Tian with a very complicated expression.

This time, being arrested, Shi Hailiang knew he was completely finished.



Chapter 454 The leaders were all dumbfounded. Are you trying to fly to the sky?


Shi Hailiang understood perfectly.

When these people began their operation, it meant one thing, one thing: they had definitely obtained all the evidence.

Just now, he had only clung to a sliver of hope.

When the cold handcuffs were slipped onto his wrists, Shi Hailiang felt a momentary daze, as if he were dreaming, but the dream felt far too real.

So real that Shi Hailiang could clearly feel the coldness of the handcuffs.

And the two main criminals had already been apprehended, and Jiang Tian hadn't delayed anything.

Just now.

The operation's communication channel was completely silent.

In fact, when they began their operation, the walkie-talkies were generally on the same channel. Unless there were special circumstances, they wouldn't speak normally, so the walkie-talkies were basically silent except for Jiang Tian's initial task assignments.

This left the leaders, heavily guarded and in a relatively safe place not far from Shimiao Village, sitting in the bus, watching the silence outside.

In the middle of the bus, there was a small speaker.

Any movement would be clearly transmitted to everyone's ears through the speaker.

How much time had passed was unknown.

The atmosphere inside the bus was tense.

"Could something have happened? Why isn't there any sound?" one of the leaders suddenly asked worriedly.

"Actually, the silence is a good thing. It proves everyone is hiding and hasn't been discovered," Shen Han explained.

Sure enough, with Shen Han's words, the atmosphere in the room instantly relaxed a bit.

However, there wasn't much relaxation.

Even for these seasoned individuals, this was the first time they had encountered a case like this.

A moment later.

A deep voice suddenly came from the speaker.

"Attention everyone, attention everyone! Shi Hailiang has been successfully apprehended. I repeat, the main culprit, Shi Hailiang, has been successfully apprehended at his residence. Everyone else, begin operations immediately."

Jiang Tian's voice came from the speaker.

Actually.

Before this order came out...

Jiang Tian's firing of his gun had essentially exposed his position.

Of course, this wasn't important.

Because the entire arrest operation was carried out in an orderly fashion.

As expected.

Following Jiang Tian's order, over two thousand military and police personnel swiftly sprang into action.

Each team had been specifically assigned their assigned area of ​​responsibility by Jiang Tian before the operation.

In other words, each team only needed to arrest and guard what was under their jurisdiction.

Even if problems arose, or if they were discovered beforehand, it wouldn't be a problem.

And following Jiang Tian's order,

almost instantly, intense gunfire erupted throughout the village.

That's right.

A barrage of gunfire rang out instantly.

And at the very moment the gunfire began,

in a nearby house, a middle-aged man who was sleeping suddenly jumped out of bed, pulled open the bedside table, and took out a pistol.

That's why I say it.

Why is drug enforcement the most dangerous and deadliest job in peacetime in this new era?

Because the enemy they face is so ruthless. Faced with death, they will fight desperately, even knowing they can't win, they will fight for survival.

Countless people have been caught off guard and perished.

Various cases have even become cautionary tales.

Ultimately, these people are all facing death. After being arrested, they know they will most likely face the death penalty.

Besides, double-crossing among those involved in this type of crime is common.

Additionally, this place is too close to the mainland and has too deep ties with foreign countries.

As a result, too many people here possess firearms.

When the gunshot rings out.

Inside the bus.

Almost instantly, the silence exploded.

"Unbelievable."

"These people are practically courting death."

"Why didn't Yunhai discover such a terrifying criminal gang for so long?"

Minister Tan listened to the gunfire erupting from the village outside the car; it was like firecrackers.

Although they weren't close to the village, in the silence of the night, the rapid-fire gunfire was far too loud and obvious.

Enraged.

Minister Tan's face was filled with rage.

Because Minister Tan suddenly realized.

If...

this village hadn't been discovered and the situation hadn't been investigated beforehand...

how terrifying the chaos that could have been.

Hundreds of armed people.

what a terrifying security threat that would have created.

And those involved in this kind of crime...

they're capable of anything.

The more he thought about it...

the more enraged Minister Tan became.

The impact was too terrifying.

Now...

The so-called attack on Secretary Mu was completely irrelevant. Compared to the crimes committed in Shimiao Village, this was utterly unbelievable.

"We never imagined that such a terrifying criminal organization was lurking in Yunhai."

As the secretary of Yunhai, Zhou Zhao immediately admitted his mistake.

Zhou Zhao was also internally turmoil.

He had initially thought it was just drug production, at most a few ringleaders carrying weapons.

He never dreamed this would happen.

That's right.

Even from a distance, they weren't fools. The dense gunfire clearly indicated a large-scale armed resistance, if not every criminal was armed.

Conservatively speaking, there was at least a series of gunshots, like firecrackers, in an instant.

What were they doing?

A rebellion?

Minister Tan took a deep breath, pulled out his walkie-talkie, and immediately spoke, "Jiang Tian, ​​please respond."

"Roger, please speak," Jiang Tian's voice came through the walkie-talkie.

"Report the situation." Minister Tan could no longer sit still.

"We have just infiltrated Shi Hailiang's house and arrested him. The arrest was successful, but there was a slight mishap during the process," Jiang Tian reported.

"A mishap? What mishap?" Minister Tan's heart skipped a beat.
