“I never imagined that there was another criminal in Shi Hailiang’s family. He’s the founder of an overseas mercenary group and also the perpetrator of the attack on Wen Yulin. He had a long-standing working relationship with Wen Yulin. I never expected this person to also be from Shimiao Village. He just happened to be there today, and I arrested him on a whim.” Jiang Tian’s voice rang out.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

This silence lasted only a moment before the leaders erupted.

“Holy crap, are you crazy?”

Because of this one whim.

The special merit award that was already somewhat precarious was now firmly secured.

Thinking about this,

many leaders were stunned, because this was an enormous achievement!

This was almost entirely Jiang Tian’s doing!



Chapter 455 The old era has passed, now a new era begins


"Good, well done."

"Excellent."

Firing was inevitable.

Even in the dead of night, these leaders understood that arresting so many criminals would inevitably create a significant commotion.

Many would react, launch an operation, and resist.

To subdue thousands so easily without any disturbance would be a pipe dream.

What was needed now was Jiang Tian's ability to command on such a large scale.

Previously, Jiang Tian had demonstrated individual combat skills and criminal investigation abilities, but these two abilities alone were insufficient.

As a leader, command ability was paramount. Individual combat, no matter how exceptional, was still just that of a soldier charging into battle.

The most important thing was command.

And a strategic vision.

These were the essential qualities of a qualified leader.

Furthermore, while the country strongly advocates for a younger cadre, its implementation has always been difficult because even when young people are promoted, their abilities and experience are often insufficient.

Sometimes, seniority-based promotions and consideration of seniority in everything are unavoidable, as seniority equates to extensive experience.

In this regard, experience and seniority are precisely Jiang Tian's greatest weaknesses.

But now,

what is presented to these leaders is Jiang Tian's terrifying strategic vision.

He commands two thousand men in battle without even needing their intervention; all arrangements are meticulously planned, and they are completely unable to supplement the troops.

The arrangements are too perfect.

This includes the timing of the operation, the immediate blocking of possible escape routes for the criminals, and the deployment of controls on roads.

In addition, compared to the number of people in the village, the actual number mobilized is insufficient.

The number of people who needed to be controlled due to potential criminal involvement was roughly two to three thousand, although only seven or eight hundred of them were involved in serious crimes, while the others committed minor offenses.

But all two to three thousand people needed to be controlled.

Compared to the number of people they deployed, it was almost a one-to-one ratio.

To some extent, this was also a test for Jiang Tian.

Especially after the unexpected happened, when gunshots rang out in the village.

"To be honest, two thousand people isn't a large number compared to this case. I just never expected Comrade Jiang to bring me such a big surprise right after the operation. Not only did he crack this drug village case, but he even cracked previous major cases along with it."

Minister Tan was beaming with delight.

And most importantly.

The group was leaderless.

Jiang Tian directly carried out a decapitation operation, arresting the village chief, Shi Hailiang, who was clearly the leader of the operation.

The remaining villagers were a mob to begin with, and now that their leader was dealt with, there was even less of a threat.

"It's insane! My god, they even arrested the perpetrator of Wen Yulin's attack!"

"Good heavens, that's incredible!"

"This achievement is terrifying."

"Holy crap, is it going to be another First-Class Merit?"

"First-Class Merit? Are you kidding me? Just the contributions Comrade Jiang made in this drug village case alone already qualify him for a Special-Class Merit. Now, with the Wen Yulin case added to the list, a Special-Class Merit is no problem at all."

"Pfft, Special-Class Merit?"

There were quite a few leaders present.

At this moment, quite a few leaders were engaged in a heated discussion on the bus.

For example, leaders from both the military and police.

There were also city leaders represented by Zhou Zhao, and provincial leaders represented by Huai Sheng, along with relevant responsible leaders, such as Elder Mu from the political and legal department, etc.

Each one of them was a big shot.

But at this moment, these people were all unsurprisingly shocked and gasping for breath.

Mainly because...

Damn it, Jiang Tian just received two First-Class Merits and a First-Class Hero award!

And now you're telling me this is just the beginning?

What's coming next is even more exciting and thrilling.

"A Special Merit, that's historic!" Shen Han exclaimed excitedly.

"What historic? Didn't Comrade Jiang just receive a Special Merit before this?" A voice rang out the next moment.

At this moment, everyone suddenly remembered.

That's right.

Although the Special Merit was kept secret, these leaders had all heard of it!

So...

This is Jiang Tian's second Special Merit?

They were stunned.

Everyone's gaze turned to Minister Tan. The matter of the Special Merit was mainly based on Minister Tan's opinion and that of headquarters.

And Minister Tan did not disappoint: "Our organization has always adhered to the principle of rewarding merit and punishing wrongdoing. The era of seniority-based promotion is over. If Comrade Jiang performs exceptionally well in this mission, I will personally petition the higher authorities to make an exception and apply for a second Special Merit for Comrade Jiang Tian."

Minister Tan, remembering the high regard of the bigwigs, smiled.

No pressure, absolutely no pressure.

Although this was historic.

Although it could easily be sensational,

and could easily cause too much impact,

while adhering to the principle of prioritizing stability, Minister Tan wasn't genuinely worried about this issue.

Other places were irrelevant; Minister Tan knew the importance of Yunhai.

And Yunhai's most important aspect was its strategic values ​​for the foreseeable future, and the planned economic "iron triangle"—a top-tier municipality within the country—that would radiate across the nation's regional development.

Yunhai was involved in future economic development, and even more so in the grand strategic development of the next few decades.

Its importance was self-evident.

Jiang Tian's outstanding contributions to Yunhai, even ignoring the hidden contributions, were already sufficient, not to mention the other significant hidden contributions.

This special merit award was undeniable.

"Sir, I have apprehended and controlled the suspects Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao. The operation in the village is also progressing smoothly," Jiang Tian said, standing on the rooftop with a walkie-talkie.

After a sharp look around, he had a general understanding of the situation.

It must be said,

the environment inside Shimiao Village is very complex.

Moreover, due to the suddenness of the operation, there was actually no opportunity to find any spies to lead the way.

Spies.

Wait...

Jiang Tian's gaze suddenly sharpened.

Because he remembered something.

That is, given the terrifying scale of the case in Shimiao Village, is it possible that there are spies within their police system?

Jiang Tian's gaze hardened.



Chapter 456 [Micro-management Master] has completely exploded, is he even human?


However, he didn't dwell on it further. For Jiang Tian, ​​the most important thing right now was addressing the immediate problems.

Then, in the following time, Jiang Tian even took the time to demonstrate his masterful micro-management skills.

That's right, masterful micro-management.

[Arrest and trial of Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao on site, successfully obtaining the skill "Micro-management!"]

[Micro-management (White)] (The further away you are when giving commands, the more your command ability is enhanced.)

What an insane skill!

It's different from other skills.

This skill dropped from Shi Haichao.

To be honest, it was a pleasant surprise, and quite unexpected.

Jiang Tian has now captured so many skills, all kinds of skills, skills from almost every industry have dropped.

Most of them are not very useful to Jiang Tian.

Only the "Micro-management" skill is a huge surprise for Jiang Tian.

Mainly because this skill ability is absolutely necessary for Jiang Tian.

As Jiang Tian's position rises, he certainly won't be able to always be on the front lines; his most important role will be commanding the overall situation.

And without a doubt, what Jiang Tian needs to develop most is his command ability.

However, command ability is still too difficult for those criminals who have no organization or discipline.

Unexpectedly, this time, such a bizarre keyword was directly exposed to Jiang Tian.

[Give me some more!]

Jiang Tian looked at the 30,000 additional sin points he gained after capturing the two people.

Adding to this the remaining 60,000+ sin points after Jiang Tian upgraded the "King of Criminal Investigation - Legendary Criminal Investigation" attribute, his total sin points reached nearly 100,000.

What does 100,000 mean? It's enough for Jiang Tian to upgrade a red attribute to a gold one.

Jiang Tian rubbed his hands together.

Ding!!!

[Host has successfully upgraded the attribute by spending 11,100 sin points.]

[Micro-management (White) - Micro-management Master (Red)] (Micro-management Master, equipment attribute. You will possess terrifying command abilities, and the more subtle your commands, the more terrifying your command abilities become. If a major battle comes, your command abilities will even be enough to micro-manage an army of 100,000, and the battles you micro-manage will increase your side's win rate and combat power by 30%.)

Good, good, good...

After seeing the explanation of the Micro-management Master attribute...

His eyes widened instantly.

Red meant a force of 100,000; if it were gold, wouldn't that mean Jiang Tian could directly launch a million-strong army-wide battle?

And this wasn't just commanding from a grand strategy; the instructions were clear: it was micro-management.

What is micro-management?

It means being thousands of miles away, receiving intelligence about a regiment's battle situation on the front lines, and issuing an order to micro-manage the regiment, or even a single regiment, at the squad level. To be even more precise, Jiang Tian could control a platoon-level ambush from thousands of miles away.

Of course, this wasn't important to Jiang Tian. In peacetime, this ability would likely be rarely needed.

The key point was that for Jiang Tian, ​​micro-management command would increase his troops' combat effectiveness and win rate by 30% when leading operations against criminals. This was the most monstrous boost for him.

After being enhanced by the Micro-Management Master's guidance,

Jiang Tian, ​​in that instant,

felt many flaws in his command, even with the Master's assistance.

These flaws might not be immediately apparent to the superiors, who, lacking a clear understanding of the situation and its complexity, had yet to notice.

However, during a post-mortem review, many details and loopholes would become apparent.

Some of these loopholes, in particular, could potentially lead to a significant crisis.

While the chances were slim, why allow a crisis to occur when it could be completely prevented?

Especially since...

With the Micro-Management Master's guidance, Jiang Tian's mind was practically filled with a realistic map, a detailed topographical map,

and small flags planted across the terrain, each representing a different action team.

"Third Battalion, attention! Seven armed men are preparing to escape on your right. Send one company out. Second Battalion, attention! They are behind you..."

"Fifth Battalion..."

Then, Jiang Tian unleashed his incredible maneuvering.

Micromanagement.

His micromanagement was so precise, even down to the individual actions within a battle involving two thousand men.

Meanwhile,

Jiang Tian, ​​consulting the "Micromanagement Master" app, discovered flaws in his previous orders. He not only corrected them but also continuously directed these individuals.

He's insane!

The leaders on the bus initially thought Jiang Tian had just happened to see something and was giving off micromanagement commands casually.

Minister Tan even smiled, saying, "Comrade Jiang's micromanagement skills are quite impressive!"

He finished with a slight smile.

It was just a little joke from Minister Tan.

Others might not notice, but Minister Tan did. He saw some flaws in Jiang Tian's initial command—not exactly flaws, but errors stemming from inexperience.

For example, he hadn't considered what countermeasures would be taken if the operation were discovered beforehand and the villagers rioted.

Normally, these aspects should have been considered.

Because if such a large number of people were to launch a horrific riot, the consequences would be unbearable for everyone.

The reason he remained calm upon hearing gunfire was because Jiang Tian's command abilities so far hadn't made any fundamental errors, only some lapses in experience.

Minister Tan had also mentally assigned Jiang Tian a score.

That was:

In terms of command ability, Jiang Tian had performed very well, even exceeding Minister Tan's expectations.

However, compared to Jiang Tian's achievements in other areas, this slightly flawed, even barely passing, score caused Minister Tan a subtle sense of disappointment.

Minister Tan quickly recovered, thinking, "What am I thinking? My performance so far is already satisfactory. No one is perfect."

Besides, he'd already commanded to this extent on his very first attempt.

With a little training and development, once he gains experience, he will definitely be a terrifying commander, only slightly weaker compared to the abilities others have demonstrated.

But Minister Tan hadn't even finished comforting himself.

Then...

Jiang Tian instantly demonstrated to Minister Tan what master-level micro-management truly meant.

It wasn't a coincidental demonstration by Jiang Tian.

It was genuine micro-management by Jiang Tian.

"Fourth Battalion, pay attention to the four o'clock position, provide immediate support. Fifth Battalion, proceed to the three o'clock position. Suspected criminals have gathered in courtyard number seventy-seven. Also, Second Company..."

This was already getting a bit outrageous.

But what happened next was even more terrifying.

"Third Battalion, First Company, First Squadron, you are responsible for..."

Buzz!!!

The scene became increasingly chilling.

Micro-management down to the squad level?

This...

Who the hell said his command abilities were lacking?

A force of two thousand, directly commanded down to the individual level...

The most terrifying thing was the result that followed.

The chaotic, almost firecracker-like gunfire, after Jiang Tian began his micro-management, in less than ten minutes, the gunfire simply... disappeared?

*Thud!*



Chapter 457 The Net Closes Completely, A Terrifying Harvest


"Impossible."

"Over two thousand people, how could they possibly command with such precision?"

"Even with the full map open, it can't be this outrageous."

While some were shocked, others were incredulous and skeptical.

Of course, not towards Jiang Tian himself, but rather doubting the reality of such micro-management.

Because it was simply too illogical.

Who could pull off such a feat?

Even a seasoned general with countless battles under his belt probably couldn't.

Even micro-management has its limits.

At first, they thought Jiang Tian had simply spotted a squad or battalion and was directly controlling them on the spot, since he could observe the situation.

But the subsequent absurdity left them utterly bewildered.

Isn't this a joke? Commanding one battalion on one side, simultaneously commanding another battalion on the other, and then, wow, temporarily changing the initial battle plan, mobilizing all resources in a continuous operation.

The key point is, the further it went, the more outrageous it became.

Two thousand+ military and police personnel—how many battalions, companies, and squads? It's already impressive if he can remember the battalions, let alone the subordinate units?

The most terrifying thing is…

Does this mean Jiang Tian knows the location of every single squad, allowing him to conduct live drills?

Especially…

The top military and police leaders are all on the bus. Why are they here?

This brings us to, for example, independent units like the Armed Police.

Although the Armed Police appear to be part of the police system, they are not actually under its management. Their mission is simply to assist the police in apprehending and combating crime.

To some extent, the Armed Police is an independent combat unit, but not part of the military or police; it is completely independent.

Its primary responsibility is maintaining internal stability.

And do Jiang Tian even recognize the various battalions, companies, and squads within it?

Okay, fine. I recognize some of them. After all, it's common for the armed police to assist in arrest operations. Cooperation between anti-drug forces and the armed police is quite frequent and routine.

But this time, they were directly dispatched from the provincial level, and even armed police from Beijing were involved. The police system also included support personnel from headquarters.

In total, it exceeds two thousand people.

That number is already terrifying.

Distinguishing between the major brigades is already difficult, let alone the command of the squadrons.

The most absurd part is the division into individual squads—is this some kind of nonsense?

And what are these high-ranking military and police leaders doing on the buses? Why are they there? There's only one purpose: to assist.

To assist in commanding operations and to quickly direct their subordinate units to act promptly.

Of course, since Jiang Tian had initially gained a basic understanding of the various operational units and his command was quite good, they didn't interfere.

They planned to wait and see what happened next before taking further action.

But there was no doubt about it.

This was a test for Jiang Tian.

In other words...

Two thousand people apprehending two thousand criminals—how was your command ability?

Do you deserve this second Special Merit Award?

If you did well, without any mistakes, then naturally the achievement would be perfect. But since you directly took command, if any major mistakes occur, or if you don't perform well, it's hard to say whether you'll definitely get the Special Merit Award.

Of course, this doesn't actually diminish their high regard for Jiang Tian.

The main reason is that they didn't expect Jiang Tian to actually dare to do this. Originally, even without taking command, Jiang Tian still had a high probability of getting this merit, but if he made a mistake, that's uncertain.

They have to admit, they admire Jiang Tian for choosing to do this.

The key is...

At first it was fine, but why did things gradually become so absurd later on?

The gunfire started like firecrackers.

But after Jiang Tian activated his "Micro-Management Master" skill, he truly demonstrated what crushing dominance meant.

Do you understand what crushing dominance from a God's-eye view means?

Wearing the "Micro-Management Master" attribute.

Jiang Tian practically stood in God's-eye view, and his passive skill increased his combat power by 30%, meaning a 30% chance of winning.

These criminals were originally just a rabble, not much of a threat at all.

After being micro-managed by Jiang Tian, ​​they launched a devastating attack.

"Report, First Squad has achieved its strategic objectives; the relevant area has been completely cleared."

"Second Squad..."

As the crackling sounds gradually subsided, similar voices continued to come from the walkie-talkie.

The distant Shimiao Village also gradually quieted down.

Less than three minutes passed.

All the gunfire had ceased.

And at that moment.

Jiang Tian, ​​listening to the voice on the walkie-talkie, and confirming through his omniscient perspective that no one had escaped,

“Close the net,” Jiang Tian said with a grin.

Thus,

The massive operation, from start to finish, lasting less than half an hour, came to an end.

They arrived.

The spacious grounds in the center of the village. The grounds were large, paved with cement, and spacious, even equipped with some fitness equipment and a basketball court.

Putting everything else aside, the infrastructure of this village and the prosperity of the surrounding area made it hard for anyone who came here to believe that this was a village in the mountains in 2003.

Even twenty years later, it's hard to imagine that a mountain village would have such comprehensive infrastructure.

It was just a pity.

What was supposed to be a fitness area was now filled with people.

The elderly, the weak, women, and children.

The entire village was now completely under control.

After the main criminals were apprehended, killed, and brought under control, the others—innocent individuals, those with minor offenses, and those who were too cowardly or unwilling to resist—posed virtually no danger.

They wouldn't, like the most heinous criminals, knowing that capture meant certain death, desperately resist to escape.

Once they escaped, they could simply head to the neighboring village and be completely safe.

But unfortunately…

This operation was commanded by Jiang Tian, ​​who had taken command from Minister Tan. Of course, it could also be seen as a test deliberately given to Jiang Tian by Minister Tan.

However, the answer Jiang Tian submitted was somewhat unacceptable.

Mainly because...

It was utterly outrageous.

It had exceeded Minister Tan's acceptable range.

The further it went, the more outrageous his command became.

The further it went, the more insane Jiang Tian's micromanagement skills became.



Chapter 458 Are you out of your mind to deal with this battle casualty? Are you really aiming for a special-class merit?


For example, they even combined a small squad with a squad from another large unit, seemingly from a god-like perspective, directing them to attack from two directions.

That wasn't all.

Sometimes, they even seemed to be on the scene, directly directing the attack via walkie-talkie.

There were even instances where they directly ordered someone to fire from the three o'clock position, someone else to sneak in from the six o'clock position, and the others to draw fire from the front, and so on.

Of course, these were just isolated cases. Jiang Tian didn't have that much time, and besides, not every drug dealer was as difficult to deal with or as dangerous as they seemed.

Most of them were fleeing in panic. Jiang Tian mainly mobilized everyone to launch a comprehensive encirclement and suppression of the village.

With almost no mistakes, he easily eliminated everyone.

The roar of car engines filled the air.

Less than a moment later.

A large bus, escorted by several police cars, arrived at almost the most open area in the village.

As the bus stopped, one after another, leaders dressed in Zhongshan suits, their faces stern and imposing, disembarked.

It must be said...

Even though they were worldly-wise and mentally prepared,

the shock of seeing two or three thousand people, almost crammed together with their hands behind their heads, was unimaginable.

Especially since...

Before they arrived, Jiang Tian had even specifically categorized them.

Those suspected of major crimes, and those clearly possessing a red aura, were practically invisible to Jiang Tian.

All the guilty parties were selected and placed under strict guard, with a large security force assigned to them.

Therefore, when these leaders disembarked, they saw approximately five hundred people, all handcuffed, hands behind their heads, huddled together.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian didn't even need to review any files.

These were the truly innocent ones, those without a single red dot on their bodies, or with very faint red dots and minimal red glow, and were thus more relaxed under guard.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Just as the leaders were stunned, and the surroundings were silent, no one uttering a sound, Jiang Tian quickly ran over, stood at attention before Minister Tan, and said:

"Reporting, Minister, the mission was successfully completed. In this operation, we eliminated 102 armed and resisting suspects, killing them on the spot, seriously injuring 231, and brought the entire village under control. The village's population is approximately 2,873; excluding children, about 2,300 remain and are now under complete control."

Boom!!!

As soon as Jiang Tian finished speaking, everyone's expression turned to a complex mix of emotions.

As leaders, they had to admit that none of them hadn't experienced much in their lives, none of them weren't shrewd and experienced countless events; few things had ever caused such a stir.

But this was completely different from the sheer shock of what was happening before their eyes.

That's all there is to it.

Two or three thousand people, brought under control immediately—this scale, this overwhelming sense of oppression, aside from wartime, in recent decades, who has experienced anything like it?

Then, at that moment, Jiang Tian revealed the exact number again.

Hiss!!!

Even Minister Tan gasped.

What shocked Minister Tan most was the sheer scale and terrifying speed of the operation.

Putting himself in the other's shoes, Minister Tan realized that even if he were in charge, he could never have resolved everything so quickly and perfectly.

It was explosive.

These leaders were truly stunned after receiving the exact number.

Everyone stared at Jiang Tian with a complex expression.

While they, especially the more professional leaders like those in the military and police with some knowledge of such matters, wouldn't dismiss Jiang Tian's micromanagement as mere showmanship, deep down they absolutely refused to believe someone could be so monstrous.

Isn't this utter nonsense?

The battlefield is ever-changing. Of course, while this wasn't a real war, it wasn't a battle of the same level, considering that a significant portion of these two thousand men were elite troops sent from Beijing.

But the entire village, nearly three thousand people—a massive village—was in constant flux, every corner shifting rapidly.

How could Jiang Tian control the entire situation?

Even with prior preparation, it couldn't be this monstrous. Even the situation at the six o'clock position was initially directed on the spot, yet he then micro-managed the situation at the twelve o'clock position as well.

This micro-management ability was ridiculously over-the-top.

Especially some of the team leaders who were being commanded; they almost thought Jiang Tian was right beside them, but after searching extensively, they couldn't find him.

Only then did these team leaders realize, to their utter astonishment, that Jiang Tian was actually remotely directing everything.

The crucial point was that the escape routes in the rapidly changing crime village, or the methods of hiding and escaping based on familiarity with the terrain, were crucial. Being unfamiliar with the terrain, they initially experienced some chaos.

But all of this ended after Jiang Tian's command.

They didn't need to think about anything; they only needed to blindly follow Jiang Tian's orders to achieve the greatest results with the least cost and in the shortest time, achieving the most perfect victory.

"Are all the personnel confirmed to be gathered?" Minister Tan was still in a daze.

Even though he had already taken Jiang Tian very seriously,

Minister Tan realized at this moment that he still knew far too little about Jiang Tian.

This was simply a bottomless pit. Every time he realized Jiang Tian was at his limit, he could unleash terrifying abilities he could never have imagined.

It was as if there was no end in sight.

Sure enough, Jiang Tian was extremely knowledgeable about the details of Shimiao Village: "All the people in the village have been apprehended. The total registered population of Shimiao Village is exactly 3,300. Of these, about 400 are working in agriculture or have gone out on business. The rest are all here; not a single one is missing."

Jiang Tian spoke with great confidence.

Just now, he found an elderly person who knew the village well, and after a brief interrogation, he easily obtained information about the entire village and matched it with all the people in the village.

All the people in the village had been successfully apprehended.

As for those not in the village, we'll see how they fared during the interrogations, to see if any of them were criminals.

If so, we'll just issue an arrest warrant.

"Besides that, everyone performed exceptionally well in this operation. No one was killed; only three team members were unfortunately hit by stray bullets—two in the arms and one in the leg. The rest suffered no losses," Jiang Tian continued.

Boom!

This sentence

instantly stunned all the leaders.

This left everyone utterly astonished.

Those unaware of just how outrageous this record was could never fathom the terrifying magnitude of these numbers.

After all, over a hundred people were killed.

Currently, the investigations have uncovered approximately seven or eight hundred individuals involved in serious crimes.

Such a significant operation against such a large group of people…

“Impossible, absolutely impossible. Only three were injured. Against such an enemy, this is absolutely impossible.”



Chapter 459 Sensing a Shocking Change in the Subtle Details


Of course, there was another reason: everyone was wearing bulletproof vests, including Jiang Tian.

Ever since he was shot once, Jiang Tian had been wearing a bulletproof vest almost always during operations.

Jiang Tian didn't like wearing a bulletproof vest over an outer coat, as it felt like wearing a thick cotton-padded coat, hindering his movements.

However, for safety's sake, even Jiang Tian couldn't guarantee he wouldn't be shot.

And some of the operations personnel were certainly hit, but it was clear that the bulletproof vests provided significant protection.

After all, guns don't have eyes. In such intense and chaotic conditions, with criminals firing indiscriminately, even with such elite forces, a few were bound to be unlucky enough to get shot.

They were just lucky.

Jiang Tian was prepared for a few casualties.

But without a doubt, in this operation, not only was no one killed, but they even achieved such a brilliant victory.

This was truly astonishing.

“What about Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao?” Minister Tan took a deep breath.

He clearly wasn't interested in continuing to investigate how Jiang Tian had done it.

Both Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao were undoubtedly key criminals.

And the absolute masterminds behind it all.

Hearing Minister Tan's question, Jiang Tian didn't keep him in suspense.

Of course, at this moment, in this kind of operation, even though Minister Tan was just an observer, he held a dominant position, while Huai Sheng could only watch helplessly from the sidelines.

After all, everyone has their own expertise.

In this situation, Minister Tan was the professional.

Huai Sheng clearly chose to remain an observer.

"This bespectacled man is Shi Hailiang, the village chief of Shimiao Village. For over ten years, he's dragged the entire village into making flour for him—his crimes are heinous."

As Jiang Tian waved his hand, the two men, who had been under strict control by four police officers, were pulled to their feet, their hands handcuffed and bound behind their backs.

Jiang Tian, ​​in particular, pointed at Shi Hailiang first.

That's how it is.

At this moment, Shi Hailiang's feelings were incredibly complex.

Ten years of hard work.

Completely collapsed in an instant.

At this moment, Shi Hailiang suddenly remembered something someone had told him.

His actions would bring disaster to the entire Shimiao Village.

But at that time, Shi Hailiang was blinded by the profits from the flour.

He couldn't hear any advice.

Especially later, it was through his own planning that he successfully dragged that young man down with him.

Now, thinking back, he truly brought it all upon himself.

No, he deserved it.

But the entire village had been dragged down by him as well.

Looking at the once bustling Shimiao Village, now utterly silent, only the occasional bark of a dog seemed to indicate that some life still existed within the village.

This brought Shi Hailiang, lost in thought, back to reality. He and Shi Haichao were now standing before Minister Tan.

Of course, Minister Tan's gaze was primarily focused on Shi Hailiang.

At a certain moment, Shi Hailiang suddenly spoke, "I feel like I've seen you somewhere before?"

Minister Tan spoke abruptly.

Shi Hailiang's expression changed slightly, a very subtle change, and in the darkness, only Jiang Tian, ​​who was constantly watching and ready to act, noticed the fleeting unusual emotion in Shi Hailiang's expression.

Something's not right.

This is completely wrong.

Shi Hailiang's expression clearly showed some guilt, even fear of being discovered.

But why would Shi Hailiang show this emotion now? By now, Shi Hailiang should be completely finished.

There's no way he has a chance to turn things around.

His secrets should be about to be revealed, but Shi Hailiang is clearly not like this, in other words.

Jiang Tian pondered.

Jiang Tian now possesses the golden attribute of "Legendary Detective," allowing him to control a criminal's mindset. Not even the slightest expression escapes his notice, and he can judge a criminal's psychological state with just a glance.

"They have a backup plan. Interesting. That makes sense. Although they were supported by Wen Yulin, after more than ten years of development, they dared to try to kill Wen Yulin. Their protective umbrella is probably not a small force." Jiang Tian instantly understood.

This is the terrifying boost that the golden attribute, "Legendary Detective," has brought to Jiang Tian.

It doesn't mean Jiang Tian has reached the level of Forrester, but rather that he has surpassed it, even being able to make accurate judgments based on the national context and what is happening.

Understanding this, a meaningful expression appeared on Jiang Tian's face.

However, it was very subtle. The biggest improvement that "Legendary Detective" brought to Jiang Tian was that all his body language and micro-expressions that could express his emotions had completely disappeared.

Before, although he was discreet and deliberately restrained, there were still some small gestures that revealed his emotions.

But now, they are completely gone.

In other words, even a psychology professor standing before Jiang Tian wouldn't be able to see through him at all.

"Perhaps I'm mistaken," Minister Tan shook his head.

Just now, Minister Tan had a subconscious feeling that he seemed familiar, but after thinking about it carefully, he wondered what level Shi Hailiang was at, and what level he was at.

Most importantly, Minister Tan had never even been to Yunhai before.

Even with his rich experience, Minister Tan thought he was overthinking things, but Jiang Tian clearly saw that Shi Hailiang's micro-expression relaxed after Minister Tan's words.

He guessed right, he was absolutely right just now.

Now Jiang Tian was even more certain.

"Minister Tan, this Shi Hailiang is fine, the main thing is that Shi Haichao, the guy with the fierce face. He has considerable influence overseas, and even some of his own territory," Jiang Tian explained softly.

These were all things Shi Haichao himself confessed.

It seemed he was trying to demonstrate his value.

Shi Haichao hurriedly said, "That's right. I've already established a foothold overseas. Although it's not large, our group is quite large, with hundreds of armed men under our command, enough to cause considerable chaos. Of course, I know my crimes are heinous, but I'm willing to become an undercover agent for my country, contributing to the investigation of major cases."

"Of course, not for anything else, but simply to atone for the crimes I've committed."

Oh?

Minister Tan glanced at Shi Haichao with surprise.

Then, his face icy, he said, "Shi Haichao, you were ruthless enough to launch an attack on our comrades in Yunhai. Do you think you can erase everything by offering compensation?"

What a joke.

Given the crimes Shi Haichao committed, execution ten times over wouldn't be an exaggeration.



Chapter 460 The major case is over? No, this is only the beginning of a bloody affair.


"Let me tell you, executing you a hundred times wouldn't be enough. The crimes you've committed are enough to warrant a hundred, a thousand executions." Minister Tan's face was as cold as ice.

The attack on Wen Yulin.

was not only a provocation to the entire police system, but also a provocation to the motherland.

At this moment, Jiang Tian's eyelids fluttered.

He glanced at Shi Haichao with a slight frown.

It has to be said.

The legendary criminal investigation experience had brought Jiang Tian a comprehensive improvement.

His control over human nature and micro-expressions was almost ingrained in his instincts; he could easily detect any subtle change.

Shi Haichao seemed to have suddenly realized his mistake.

However, like Shi Hailiang, it was clear that Shi Haichao, who seemed physically strong but simple-minded, couldn't have built such a large empire overseas simply through brute force alone.

Shi Haichao knew best the crimes he had committed; no amount of execution would be excessive.

He's still alive, just one step away from a judicial trial.

It's impossible for him not to understand that if Shi Hailiang confessed and performed meritorious service, he might have a chance of a suspended death sentence, but that's absolutely impossible for him.

The sacrifice of more than ten comrades, the case with such a huge and egregious impact—the initiative involved is too much.

He's 100% certain to die.

Even for Shi Hailiang, unless the intelligence he contributed is no less significant than the problem in Shimiao Village,

his merits and demerits would offset each other.

But that's possible.

Jiang Tian believes it's impossible. The Shimiao Village case is already a terrifyingly large-scale case.

It might not even happen once every few decades.

The number of people involved is too exaggerated, the scale too enormous.

Moreover, the valuable information contributed is not much, because most of Shimiao Village's sales channels are through Shi Haichao taking it overseas and selling it at high prices.

Sometimes it's even exported and then resold domestically.

Because of this extra procedure involving Shimiao Village, such a major case remained completely undetected for over a decade. If it weren't for Wen Yulin's intelligence, Jiang Tian wouldn't have even noticed the problem in Shimiao Village. Even with scanning radar, Jiang Tian couldn't have found such a remote mountain village.

"Take them all away and interrogate them one by one."

At this point, Jiang Tian didn't need to intervene; Minister Tan issued the order with a wave of his hand.

Jiang Tian's task was almost complete.

However, it was clear that interrogating so many people would be a major challenge.

Fortunately, Minister Tan had previously emptied several detention centers, which were barely usable.

However, this would only be enough for the approximately eight hundred people Jiang Tian had selected.

There were still nearly two thousand people remaining.

At least according to the data, there were no problems. Jiang Tian's scanning radar had only scratched the surface; most of them would likely only receive sentences of two or three years.

In conclusion, these two thousand-plus people would still need to be kept in the village.

There was nowhere to put them.

Therefore, the personnel stationed here cannot even be evacuated yet.

“Xiao Jiang, what do you think?”

After arranging everything, Minister Tan walked to Jiang Tian's side and asked.

What do you think?

Jiang Tian pondered for a moment and said, “I feel something is wrong. Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao are suspicious; they don't seem as honest as they appear. We should strengthen our defenses.”

Minister Tan glanced at Jiang Tian with slight surprise.

“Jiang, you're overthinking it. The entire Shimiao Village has been apprehended. We can't possibly suspect someone else attacked the detention center; that would be incredibly audacious.” Shen Han believed Jiang Tian was simply overthinking things.

However, Jiang Tian's performance today was still astonishing to Shen Han.

So much credit—was he envious? Absolutely.

The credit Jiang Tian earned today would have been enough for Shen Han to achieve her long-desired advancement.

Of course, it was just pure envy.

Shen Han understood that Jiang Tian had barely needed their intervention in the entire case today.

Aside from providing basic police force and personnel, from finding the clues and evidence to leading the arrests, Jiang Tian had planned almost the entire operation himself.

In this situation, Shen Han couldn't even claim a share of the credit.

“Fortunately, if our provincial department solves this case, it should be no less impactful than this one. While it might not have the same level of shock, its influence and reach will be just as significant,” Shen Han thought to himself.

He even calculated the timing; the operation would likely close down soon.

As for what Jiang Tian said, Shen Han thought it was pure overthinking.

Right now, Shen Han couldn't even fathom what opportunities they had left.

What other backup plans could they possibly have?

“It's impossible that Shi Haichao had planted men in an ambush,” Shen Han shook his head.

Although he had seen a lot, he absolutely refused to believe in such a fantastical, unheard-of possibility.

“Who says it's impossible?” Jiang Tian retorted.

“Hmm?” Shen Han suddenly paused.

Because…

If there really was an ambush, it was highly unlikely. After all, with such a large-scale operation tonight, if there were an ambush, why wouldn't those people have acted?

“Of course, I might be overthinking it, or he might have other ideas, but regardless, safety is paramount. Hopefully, we can get more manpower,” Jiang Tian said.

Minister Tan unconditionally agreed with Jiang Tian's statement.

Manpower was already insufficient.

Even at the scene, the remaining personnel seemed honest and law-abiding; the villagers all seemed obedient, but who knew if some major criminals were hiding among them?

“I'll arrange it,” Shen Han said, opening his mouth before finally nodding.

He then quickly issued orders via walkie-talkie: several more police cars for escort.

After all, transporting nearly eight hundred people was far beyond what the current fleet could handle; a large number of vehicles were needed.

Even before the operation, no one had anticipated such a terrifying number of criminals being captured.

Nearly eight hundred people! Even with ordinary five-seater cars, it would require one or two hundred vehicles.

Of course, Shen Han couldn't possibly bring all sedans; he only called for several buses.

Moreover, it was a prison bus, specifically used to transport criminals; even the windows were welded shut.

After Shen Han left.

Minister Tan sighed.

It must be said.

If Minister Tan were to give Jiang Tian a score today, he would definitely give him a perfect ten.

Flawless.

In every aspect.

Is this the end? Jiang Tian looked at the silent village. No, this wasn't the end; it was perhaps just the beginning. The situation in Yunhai, as the excavation progressed, revealed far more serious than he had imagined. At this moment, Jiang Tian finally understood why he was willing to change the overall strategy—because the crime problem was far more severe and terrifying than he had ever imagined.

Just as Minister Tan was about to say something, the phone rang suddenly.

Minister Tan took out his phone, glanced at it, and answered, his smile freezing slightly: "You've arrived?"

Meanwhile.

While the aftermath work was being carried out intensively in Shimiao Village.

Three official vehicles were driving along the smooth cement road leading to Shimiao Village.

Especially inside the middle vehicle.

Shang Mingyan frowned slightly: "What's going on? Why are there so many police cars here?"



Chapter 461 Madness and Shock: Have They Broken a Heavenly Law?


To be honest, most people who know Shang Mingyan understand that he's a workaholic and a case-solving fanatic.

Initially, many assumed that Shang Mingyan was simply too engrossed in his work to find a partner.

However, those close to him know best. Ms. Qiu, who at least understands Shang Mingyan, understands the real reason behind her mentor's behavior.

He simply doesn't find anyone appealing.

Or rather, he doesn't feel anything special about them.

Or perhaps, ordinary men no longer stir any emotions in Shang Mingyan, which explains why he hasn't found anyone he's been attracted to all these years.

Since he feels nothing, and can see right through someone, why force himself?

Besides, while Shang Mingyan works within the police system, he's more of an expert than a civil servant.

Therefore, being married or not is essentially irrelevant.

That evening, after receiving a call from Minister Tan, Shang Mingyan couldn't sit still.

Good heavens! A workaholic to begin with, Shang Mingyan was already extremely frustrated after learning of a lead on the killer of Secretary Mu and immediately launching a swift interrogation, only to find nothing of interest. He desperately wanted to see Jiang Tian and learn more about the case.

But then Minister Tan called, and Jiang Tian was unavailable.

What case could be more serious than Secretary Mu's attack?

Shang Mingyan was dumbfounded.

He couldn't understand it.

Later, unable to sit still any longer, Shang Mingyan called Minister Tan directly.

Actually, by then, the mission was basically over, or rather, the operation had been completed.

Plus, this case was significant.

So, the special task force's involvement wasn't a problem. They told Shang Mingyan the location of Shimiao Village and asked him to come over himself.

"Today, the provincial department mobilized most of their police force; it seems they're conducting some kind of operation," Miss Qiu said thoughtfully, watching the police cars drive by.

Miss Qiu had been paying attention to this that afternoon.

"Why didn't you say so sooner?" Shang Mingyan suddenly exclaimed. How could she not know about something so important?

"It was a task assigned by the provincial department. I didn't think much of it at the time," Miss Qiu said indifferently, her eyes lifeless.

Shang Mingyan took a deep breath, inwardly reassuring herself. This was her beloved apprentice, the heir to her mantle; she couldn't be angry.

"Next time something this important happens, you must tell me promptly, so I can at least be prepared," Shang Mingyan said.

Mainly, Shang Mingyan desperately wanted to know what case it was.

As they drew closer,

Shang Mingyan then witnessed something incredibly outrageous.

Police cars, their lights flashing, silently passed by their official vehicles.

One, two...

After more than ten police cars had passed, Shang Mingyan clicked her tongue: "This is definitely a major mission!"

For Shang Mingyan, such a crucial mission, which she hadn't been involved in, only fueled her anger as she looked at her apprentice.

If I had paid attention beforehand, I might have been able to get involved in this case.

After all, for business magnum opus...

It seemed he possessed a profound understanding of human nature, including psychology and micro-expressions. His mastery of these skills, coupled with an innate, heightened sensitivity to emotions, caused Shang Mingyan to lose interest in many things.

Shang Mingyan even realized he was experiencing some psychological issues, so he deliberately poured all his energy into the case.

"No wonder. But even a major case can't compare to the importance of Secretary Mu's case," Shang Mingyan frowned deeply.

Unless…

Shang Mingyan was thinking to himself.

Just then.

Through the window, Shang Mingyan suddenly saw rows of flashing lights.

A moment later.

Police cars roared by, seemingly endless, and occasionally military vehicles could be seen passing by.

Twenty vehicles.

Thirty cars.

Fifty cars!!!

Shang Mingyan was stunned.

His expression was blank as he stared at the police cars, his eyes glazed over.

Shang Mingyan wasn't a rookie; he knew the procedures for determining the level of a case and the scale of police deployment.

This was a terrifyingly large-scale deployment.

Shang Mingyan had been a police officer for over twenty years, building a legendary career for over two decades, but he had rarely witnessed such a scene.

Even though he had handled countless major cases, this was still the case.

"How many police cars have passed by?" Shang Mingyan snapped back to reality, realizing he had been lost in thought.

"Probably over a hundred," Miss Qiu said uncertainly.

"Over a hundred?" Shang Mingyan exclaimed.

A hint of surprise appeared on his usually cold face.

Soon.

As Shang Mingyan drew closer to Shimiao Village, the number of police cars nearby increased dramatically, seemingly endless. Shang Mingyan even seriously suspected that all the armed police in Yunhai had been mobilized that night.

Then, Shang Mingyan arrived at the village entrance.

At this moment, dozens of buses came into view.

Nearly eight hundred people needed to be detained and strictly guarded. These people couldn't be detained in Shimiao Village on the spot; there were too many uncertainties. Jiang Tian had already scanned them all to ensure that not a single one was innocent.

Moreover, each one was a grave criminal.

Each bus could only hold about fifty people, and at least ten fully armed police officers were needed to guard them to prevent any accidents.

That is to say, eight hundred people, exactly twenty buses.

Of course, twenty buses weren't actually enough; the remaining important criminals were transported directly by armed police and special police vehicles.

In short...

The buses were well-lit.

So, the details inside could be seen from the outside.

More than a dozen armed police officers stood in the aisle of the bus, with crowds of people sitting on both sides.

And Shang Mingyan's gaze was incredibly sharp.

At a glance, almost everyone sitting there shared the same expression: ashen-faced.

In that instant...

A chilling thought instantly rose in Shang Mingyan's mind.

These people...could they all be criminals?

And not ordinary criminals?

Have these people broken some cardinal law?

Shang Mingyan was shocked. She had handled countless major cases in her life, but the scale of this case was terrifying. Even just a cursory glance at the number of people inside the bus alone, she calculated it to be close to eight hundred.

Over eight hundred prisoners under custody.

What kind of horrific case could have such a terrifying scale?

She was dizzy.

Shang Mingyan felt a little lightheaded.

"No wonder Minister Tan said this case was even more important," Shang Mingyan thought, taking deep breaths.



Chapter 462 Operation Success: A Great Moment That Made History


Just then, upon arriving at the entrance to Shimiao Village, the three official vehicles were unsurprisingly stopped at the intersection for inspection.

Coincidentally,

the person guarding the village entrance was an old acquaintance.

Thump, thump, thump!

The crisp sound of high heels echoed on the concrete.

Shang Mingyan got out of the car, glanced around, and couldn't help but breathe slightly faster.

There was no other reason than that, because at this moment, the entire Shimiao Village was practically under tight surveillance, with guards every three steps and sentries every five.

Although the police cars had left, some military vehicles had taken over the area.

The surroundings were almost completely sealed off.

Overall, the case was far too serious.

If the punishment were to be life imprisonment or execution for more than fifty grams of drugs, then apart from the children, almost no innocent people in Shimiao Village would escape unscathed.

Of course, some women were forced into this situation, and that needs to be considered as well. The specific sentencing standards will depend on the judgment of the prosecutor and the court.

The Municipal Bureau can only offer its suggestions. Generally, in such cases, the prosecutor and the court will conduct the trial according to the needs of the Municipal Bureau.

At this moment, Shang Mingyan had no idea what kind of horrific case this was.

“Zeng Fan, what are you doing here?” Shang Mingyan asked, her face full of surprise.

That’s right, Zeng Fan was guarding the checkpoint at the village entrance.

“Professor Shang.” Zeng Fan hurriedly and respectfully greeted Shang Mingyan.

“Answer my question.” Shang Mingyan’s expression was cold, and her words were unusually simple.

“This case is too big. Minister Tan has transferred a large number of personnel from headquarters, so I came along.” Zeng Fan said, forcing himself to be brave.

“Why didn’t I know?”

Shang Mingyan’s breathing was already rapid beneath her cold expression.

Good heavens.

Such a big case.

So she was the only one who didn’t know?

Even Miss Qiu had sensed it beforehand, but said nothing.

Zeng Fan looked completely innocent.

In reality, although Zeng Fan wasn't a member of the joint investigation team, he was still part of the operational force, assisting with some basic work in the investigation.

Or perhaps, he was doing supervisory work.

Generally, in these kinds of investigation teams, especially joint investigation teams, the headquarters' responsibility is basically to supervise the local investigation.

After all, coming down from Beijing, they were practically strangers to everyone.

Zeng Fan's lack of reporting was also somewhat innocent, because his entire team was directly mobilized from above, and he didn't know Shang Mingyan wouldn't know.

So, when he learned that Shang Mingyan didn't know, Zeng Fan was somewhat surprised and astonished.

But Zeng Fan didn't know.

At this moment, Zeng Fan's expression instantly ignited Shang Mingyan's inner rage a hundredfold.

Zeng Fan's ignorance meant that she didn't know, which meant that Zeng Fan had been directly transferred without her prior notification. What did this mean? Was he guarding against her or something?

Did he not trust her?

"Okay, I understand. Take me to Minister Tan and the others." Shang Mingyan, used to his aloof demeanor, spoke with a cold expression despite his inner rage.

Zeng Fan shrank back, not daring to say a word, and led the way.

This caused Shang Mingyan to frown slightly.

In fact, Shang Mingyan was in criminal investigation at headquarters, while Zeng Fan was in drug enforcement. Although they weren't in the same system, Zeng Fan had previously been a key figure in their development program. Even after committing a serious offense and being suspended for a few days, he eventually returned to work.

It's just a pity.

Zeng Fan had lost the organization's trust and was no longer of any value to them.

In other words, unless Zeng Fan made a significant contribution, his political career was over.

Shang Mingyan had met Zeng Fan before. From his micro-expressions, he could tell that Zeng Fan was definitely arrogant, even somewhat disdainful of others. Shang Mingyan didn't have much of a liking for him.

But now.

Shang Mingyan was stunned to find that.

He had changed.

From the inside out, Zeng Fan had completely changed. He was timid and hesitant, as if afraid of making a mistake.

He was ruined.

This kid was ruined.

What happened to cause such a drastic change in him?

Oh, it hasn't been that long since we last met.

It seems like it's changed since I first came to Yunhai and returned.

Is Yunhai really that scary?

We're not from the same system, but we often cooperate. Of course, this isn't when we cooperate with Zeng Fan. When we cooperate with Shang Mingyan, it's mostly on major drug cases. Zeng Fan's teacher had cooperated with us for a long time back then, and we're quite familiar with each other.

Shang Mingyan's gaze pierced through Zeng Fan's back, but she wasn't interested in what had happened to him. For her, this village was what truly interested her.

"Professor, something's wrong with this village," Miss Qiu noticed, following behind.

Shang Mingyan nodded calmly, "Indeed, it's too prosperous."

Like a crane standing among chickens, in an era where most people are struggling to make ends meet, this village has almost every household living in a small villa.

It's easy to imagine that something's not wrong here.

Thinking of the people who were just arrested...

Shang Mingyan felt a chill run down her spine.

Zeng Fan, standing ahead, answered their questions: "This is a drug village; almost every household manufactures drugs."

*Whoosh!*

Shang Mingyan's eyes widened, her pupils contracted.

No wonder.

The next moment.

A voice approached them.

"According to the on-site investigation, a total of twenty sets of large-scale new drug-making equipment and about one hundred sets of small-scale drug-making equipment were seized. Approximately three thousand kilograms of drugs were seized on-site, with extremely high purity, giving it considerable competitiveness in the market."

*Boom!*

Upon approaching, Shang Mingyan gasped.

Three thousand kilograms?

And that was seized on-site.

What does that mean?

Good heavens, even if divided among everyone in the village, that's over a thousand grams per person—twenty times the death penalty.

What a terrifying concept.

This isn't an account book, nor is it a historical ledger.

Not the total.

It's the quantity and scale found during the on-site search.

This quantity.

Hiss!!!

What the hell is this case?

At this moment, Shang Mingyan finally understood why Minister Tan had been so dismissive of Secretary Mu's case.

Compared to this case...

The attack on Secretary Mu was utterly insignificant.

Three thousand kilograms of a new, extremely high-quality heroin, once it entered the market...

Good heavens.

It's chilling.

If this gets into Yunhai, the entire city will be in serious trouble.

"Three thousand kilograms?"

At that moment...

Minister Tan thought he'd misheard. Looking at Jiang Peng, who was holding a notebook and giving a serious report, a moment of confusion flashed across Minister Tan's face.

Then he instantly snapped back to reality.

"That's right, three thousand kilograms. If I'm not mistaken, this batch should be sold through Shi Haichao." Jiang Peng confirmed again.

"What Shi Haichao just said, that they're selling it overseas, seems like no threat to us, but that's a joke. Exporting and then reselling domestically is commonplace. This scale, once the transaction is successful and it reaches Myanmar, will be a huge blow to Yunhai." Minister Tan said through gritted teeth, almost enunciating each word.

That's right.

When massive quantities of heroin appear on the market, there's bound to be those willing to take the risk of smuggling it into the country to profit from the price difference.

Leaving aside these objective factors, just consider the 3,000 kilograms seized this time.

Seizing 3,000 kilograms in one go—that's already historic.

"Besides that, we also uncovered a large-scale underground drug manufacturing plant in the village, and arrested a group of researchers there. The system was extremely sophisticated. Especially noteworthy is that the heroin we seized this time is a new type that has recently appeared on the market; this type was developed by these researchers."



Chapter 463 A breathtaking scene, a case that has stood the test of time?


An underground drug lab?

As footsteps sounded, the entire underground drug lab, under the control of the armed police, revealed over twenty researchers in white lab coats, seemingly researchers, crouching on the ground with their hands behind their heads.

They appeared to be awaiting their fate.

When Jiang Tian arrived, he was immediately hit by a pungent odor. Although the underground drug lab had good ventilation, the overwhelming smell was practically assaulting his senses.

Especially for someone like Jiang Tian with sensitive senses, this nauseating odor was a huge test of his tolerance.

"What's wrong?" Minister Tan stopped and asked.

"Nothing," Jiang Tian shook his head.

The case was solved. Of course, the most important thing was that so many criminals and criminals needed to be resettled. The leaders couldn't stay indefinitely. Apart from the leaders within the police system and the armed police—the leaders of the combat units—who remained to command, the remaining civil service leaders had already returned home.

Of course, it wasn't about going home. How could they go home after something this big? Their primary concern was resettling the criminals, handling the aftermath, and dealing with the village's problems.

Such a large case, not to mention anything else, just the aftermath alone was a massive undertaking.

This required a short timeframe for interrogation within the police system, followed by prosecution and sentencing by the prosecutor's office and court.

Of course, the initial interrogations were also crucial.

This wasn't a small number; it was a terrifying scale of seven or eight hundred people being processed all at once. And it wasn't just seven or eight hundred; nearly two thousand remained at the scene. Who could guarantee there wouldn't be any problems?

Although Jiang Tian prioritized those with strong red light for classification, the others, whose crimes were less serious, and who remained in the village—nearly half of them—also emitted a red light, though much weaker than those taken away.

But even though it was much weaker,

with the red light, Jiang Tian had figured out the effectiveness of the scanning radar.

These people committed minor crimes, and the "eyes of judgment" couldn't discern the specifics, but based on the consistent data from the scanning radar...

Of the remaining two thousand people, at least seven or eight hundred had a crime value that might seem small, but compared to those with more serious crimes, legally speaking, a sentence of ten years to life imprisonment would be sufficient.

However, if they were to be prosecuted, a problem arises.

The entire Shimiao Village has nearly three thousand people; arresting more than half would leave very few. In other words, the elderly, women, and children in the village would be left without care.

Even though this is a drug-ridden village, the children are innocent, and those who didn't participate are also innocent.

These people all need a follow-up plan and treatment.

At the very least, the children's normal lives must be guaranteed.

These are all major problems.

After the leaders return, they will have to solve these problems and quickly resolve the overall aftermath.

In short, this has presented them with a difficult challenge.

However, no one dared to say anything more.

Such a major case, if not handled properly afterward, would leave no one with the responsibility if held accountable by higher authorities.

For these leaders,

Jiang Tian's actions were like defusing a fatal bombshell for them; they could forget about any credit, at most they could atone for their mistakes.

At this moment,

those who came to the underground drug manufacturing base, besides Jiang Tian and Minister Tan, were Shang Mingyan, Miss Qiu, and Jiang Peng.

As for Shen Han, she had already led her men to escort those who had been arrested.

Entering the underground drug manufacturing plant, not to mention Jiang Tian, ​​even ordinary people like Shang Mingyan and the others couldn't help but frown slightly.

Because the environment was too terrifying.

Huge drug manufacturing equipment.

And...

Standard bags of heroin, each weighing fifty kilograms, piled up on the ground.

Dozens of bags of heroin were piled together.

It was a truly chilling sight.

“The heroin we just seized, all of it, was seized here, totaling three thousand kilograms. Besides that, we discovered that these people are researching a new type of heroin here. This new heroin has two characteristics: it's more addictive, and most importantly, compared to the old type, it causes a much stronger hallucination. Of course, it also causes more severe damage to the central nervous system and other systems,” Jiang Peng said.

Anti-drug experts were already on site examining the heroin.

Jiang Peng had a preliminary understanding of the results of the tests: “Of course, there may be other characteristics, but more detailed testing is needed. But without a doubt, some of the new heroin we've recently found in the market is almost identical to the heroin here. We can basically confirm that it originated here.”

No one spoke.

Minister Tan's face was grave.

Besides Minister Tan, Gao Xiaolong was also present.

Gao Xiaolong's gaze was constantly scanning the equipment and specialized research instruments on site.

Finally, Gao Xiaolong's face darkened. "These are extremely advanced equipment, and..."

After saying this, Gao Xiaolong's gaze fell upon the researchers crouching on the ground.

These people were undoubtedly highly educated.

But at this moment, they were committing heinous acts.

"Most of this group are chemistry graduate students from top universities, some were recruited, and the leader is a local recruit from Shimiao Village. A significant portion of them are likely his classmates," Jiang Tian explained.

He didn't care about the others, but the leader was Jiang Tian's primary focus.

He had found information on a young man from Shimiao Village who had obtained a chemistry graduate degree from a top university.

Jiang Tian recognized the leader immediately.

"Beast!" Gao Xiaolong was furious.

However, he restrained his anger for the time being.

The scene was shocking.

After visiting the underground drug lab, Gao Xiaolong had a grasp of the scale of crime in Shimiao Village.

A shocking concept.

Even though they had overestimated the scale of crime in Shimiao Village, the discoveries kept pushing the boundaries of what was acceptable.

Even Minister Tan was taken aback.

The equipment was incredibly sophisticated.

If it weren't for the pungent smell, he would have thought he'd stumbled into a pharmaceutical technology company's laboratory.

"Hello, my name is Shang Mingyan."

Just as Jiang Peng was showing Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong around the underground drug lab, Shang Mingyan approached Jiang Tian.

On the way, Shang Mingyan had already learned the entire story.

Especially knowing that Jiang Tian had almost single-handedly cracked the entire case.

Even though she had already overestimated the scale, after learning about the terrifying scale of crime and its impact in Shimiao Village, Shang Mingyan couldn't deny that she was truly shocked.

She had handled all sorts of major cases, but this was the first time she had encountered one like this.

And this case...

could be called the number one major case.



Chapter 464 First Contact, Shang Mingyan Completely Breaks Down


The case was terrifying.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian's abilities surprised Shang Mingyan the most.

From discovering clues to infiltrating and photographing evidence, and finally leading his men to wipe out the entire Shimiao Village.

Shang Mingyan had to admit that reputation precedes him. He had previously held some prejudice against Jiang Tian, ​​assuming he was just a elite soldier with formidable combat skills but limited understanding of criminal investigation.

However, from the events surrounding this case, Shang Mingyan witnessed Jiang Tian's demonstrated investigative abilities.

Therefore, Shang Mingyan proactively approached Jiang Tian, ​​extending his hand.

Mainly, he wanted to understand Jiang Tian more deeply.

To see if he truly possessed such terrifying abilities in criminal investigation.

Jiang Tian's eyes flickered slightly upon seeing Shang Mingyan.

The distance was close, and Jiang Tian could see Shang Mingyan clearly. Of course, this name was absolutely legendary to Jiang Tian.

Before his rebirth in his previous life, Shang Mingyan had become a titan in the entire criminal investigation industry.

Moreover, he was a world-renowned figure.

He stood at the very top of the field.

He even frequently held seminars at top universities abroad.

Now, seeing Shang Mingyan, who looked only about twenty-five or twenty-six years old, though not yet forty, Jiang Tian knew that Shang Mingyan's true age was thirty-seven.

His features were exquisite, but his aloof demeanor gave him an unapproachable air.

Besides that...

Jiang Tian reached out his hand without making a sound.

During this time, Shang Mingyan's gaze remained fixed on Jiang Tian, ​​as if trying to discern something from his face.

No.

She couldn't see anything.

Shang Mingyan frowned deeply. That's right.

She couldn't see any micro-expressions from Jiang Tian, ​​not even a subconscious, habitual gesture that revealed his inner state.

Nothing, none of that.

How could this be?

Even Shang Mingyan herself, though aware of the emotions conveyed through micro-expressions, was utterly unable to control them.

From the eyes to the expression, no one, being human, can escape this realm.

No matter how strong one's mental fortitude, no matter how well one hides it, micro-expressions cannot be concealed. Even with special training, one can only hide the surface; any change in behavior or facial expression can reveal the wearer's inner emotions.

Shang Mingyan was observing Jiang Tian's behavior.

But what she saw left her dumbfounded.

Because whether Jiang Tian heard her name or shook her hand, he always wore a smile.

However, this smile remained completely unchanged; his facial expressions and actions showed absolutely nothing that revealed emotion or inner thoughts.

Shang Mingyan was shocked.

Compared to the shock of the case itself, the shock of Jiang Tian's demeanor upon first meeting him was far more terrifying.

Because this was the first time Shang Mingyan had ever encountered someone like this.

No, this was simply not human.

Only robots could accomplish this; humans simply couldn't, Shang Mingyan firmly believed.

Compared to robots,

Shang Mingyan preferred to believe he was mistaken, that he must have been mistaken.

"I'm Shang Mingyan from the Criminal Investigation Department," Shang Mingyan reiterated, intending to directly trigger Jiang Tian's emotions through her name. Her initial assertive self-introduction was designed to make Jiang Tian lose his composure first.

Now she assumed Jiang Tian hadn't heard of her name or didn't recognize it.

"I just heard it. Professor Shang's name is very well-known. I read your theories on investigation in university; they were very insightful and greatly helped me," Jiang Tian said.

His face was still full of smiles.

Still the same.

His expression showed no displeasure, no shock, and certainly no admiration as he claimed.

Absolutely nothing.

Shang Mingyan's eyelids twitched.

In reality, Shang Mingyan didn't see Jiang Tian's micro-expressions, but her emotions were unsurprisingly laid bare before him.

From shock to disbelief, then numbness.

Even though Shang Mingyan was outwardly feigning it, she couldn't escape Jiang Tian's eyes.

【Legend of Criminal Investigation (Gold)】(Legend of Criminal Investigation, in the field of criminal investigation, you have reached the pinnacle. Throughout history, even the most brilliant detectives, like Di Renjie of ancient times and Sherlock Holmes of modern times, can only look up to your brilliance. You have perfect control over every detail of criminal investigation and can uncover numerous case clues that no one else can detect. In your eyes, there are no unsolved cases.)

That's right.

That's the terrifying aspect of this entry.

The most terrifying aspect.

This isn't just about finding clues in criminal investigation; it includes everything related to criminal investigation and solving cases.

For example.

Psychology.

Unbeatable.

Micro-expressions, unbeatable.

And various kinds of mind-reading, etc.

In fact, Jiang Tian understands these things more deeply than these famous figures in history.

And Jiang Tian has reached the pinnacle in these areas, naturally, he has complete control over his micro-expressions, including many small habits.

Although Shang Mingyan was trying to hide her true feelings, in front of Jiang Tian, ​​it was as if she was simultaneously trying to conceal them while loudly proclaiming her innermost thoughts.

Jiang Tian chuckled inwardly.

She knew what Shang Mingyan wanted to do.

“Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​you’re quite different.” Shang Mingyan stared intently at Jiang Tian’s expression.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, asked, “Professor Shang, is there something on my face? Why are you staring at me?”

“No.” Shang Mingyan’s mood grew increasingly strange.

More and more bewildered.

She couldn’t see through him.

Good heavens, there wasn’t a single subtle movement.

All her body language was the same.

Shang Mingyan was somewhat bewildered, and even questioned her own existence. Was this really human?

So much so that Shang Mingyan’s attempt to take the initiative by introducing herself upon meeting Jiang Tian hadn’t caused any reaction from him; instead, it had directly thrown Shang Mingyan into a state of inner turmoil.

Jiang Tian knew that at this moment, she should even press her advantage.

This woman, from the very beginning, wanted to control his mind and manipulate him at will.

Shang Mingyan, however, was unaware of this.

She had no secrets.

"Professor Shang, you seem quite shocked. Are you so shocked to see me?" Jiang Tian inquired.

Perhaps because Shang Mingyan was used to seeing others like this, he subconsciously hid himself, putting on a protective shell of aloofness.

In reality, it was nothing more than a subconscious attempt to conceal all his inner thoughts.

“Oh, Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​you solved such a big case, isn't it only natural that I'm shocked?” Shang Mingyan replied coolly.

“No, what I meant was, when you said your name, you seemed somewhat shocked. I've seen many shocked people, but this is the first time I've seen someone who was shocked before introducing themselves.” Jiang Tian still smiled and shook his head, the smile natural, yet it gave off a very fake feeling.

This was actually a form of micro-expression control.

Of course.

This sentence.

directly broke Shang Mingyan's defenses.

“Kid, what are you saying?”



Chapter 465 Clash: Utter Defeat, Professor Collapses


"My name is Jiang Tian. It seems Professor Shang has heard of me. Nice to meet you."

Jiang Tian spoke slowly at this moment.

Shang Mingyan's composure crumbled.

It was their first meeting, but Shang Mingyan swore that Jiang Tian was unlike anyone she had ever met.

This difference was evident from the inside out.

The most terrifying thing was that so many people, or rather, anyone, made no pretense in front of her.

Shang Mingyan had even developed an instinct to see the true nature of others.

Even though Shang Mingyan didn't want to, because it caused her boredom with life and greatly affected her personality.

However, this was no longer something Shang Mingyan could control.

Because she was too proficient in psychology, Shang Mingyan had never had any friends, or rather, everything she had focused on was solving cases.

Until now, meeting Jiang Tian.

She couldn't see through him.

Completely unfathomable.

He was like a robot; his inner thoughts were completely hidden from Jiang Tian's outward appearance.

Even his self-introduction was a ploy to shock Jiang Tian, ​​observe his behavior, and ultimately see through him.

In reality...

Before coming, Shang Mingyan had wondered who Jiang Tian really was, and had developed some interest on the way. After all, Shang Mingyan didn't really value Jiang Tian, ​​considering him just a muscle-bound guy.

They probably wouldn't have many opportunities to cooperate.

However, after learning a little about the tasks Jiang Tian had done, Shang Mingyan became somewhat interested in him, mainly wanting to know why her student held him in such high regard.

And now...

She'd been caught off guard.

She had to admit it.

This meeting had brought Shang Mingyan immense shock and completely shattered her defenses.

Not only had she not seen through him, but Jiang Tian had seen right through her with a single sentence?

Good grief, she'd been completely outmaneuvered.

Jiang Tian's next sentence, in particular, instantly enraged Shang Mingyan, who had already broken down.

"Those who peer into the abyss never consider that the abyss is also peering into them," Jiang Tian said.

Shang Mingyan's anger surged.

This was unacceptable to Shang Mingyan from any perspective.

Although Shang Mingyan was adept at observing people's hearts and had even become accustomed to it, she also subconsciously restrained her micro-expressions and controlled her emotions.

She was unwilling to expose her most authentic self.

Even more unwilling to be nakedly seen through by others.

Because she saw through others, she was even more afraid of being seen through herself.

Not only had she failed to see through Jiang Tian, ​​but she had been completely outmaneuvered by him.

Finally, Jiang Tian uttered those words, which sounded almost like a lecture.

"You're angry because you feel seen through, aren't you? You use a cold exterior to hide your inner self. Because you're always watching others, you're even more afraid of being seen through. You're very angry; I can feel your fury, and even your shock. You've also developed a bit of interest. In this short minute, you've glanced at me thirteen times out of the corner of your eye. You're curious, not understanding why this is happening?" Jiang Tian continued.

Pfft!!!

She exploded.

Shang Mingyan completely exploded.

She swore she had never been so vulnerable in her life.

Of course, more than anything, she felt a chill run down her spine and the embarrassment and anger of having her inner thoughts exposed.

And immense shock.

Because Jiang Tian was absolutely right.

It was as if she hadn't hidden anything in front of Jiang Tian, ​​completely exposed for him to see.

Before, she didn't feel anything when she looked at others.

But after being seen through, that feeling came from within.

Most importantly...

How did Jiang Tian do that?

No.
