"The only explanation is that this Jiang Tian has reached an extremely terrifying level of understanding of psychology, even surpassing my own expertise." Shang Mingyan shuddered at this thought.

Although unwilling to accept it, Shang Mingyan realized that this was indeed the only explanation left.

Otherwise, there was no other way to explain what was happening.

Interesting.

Shang Mingyan suddenly calmed down, looked at Jiang Tian, ​​and smiled, saying, "You're right. I never expected that after all these years, you'd have taught me a lesson."

Jiang Tian, ​​without any modesty, said, "I've actually met many people from Beijing, and I've found that many of you have this problem, this common ailment: a superior attitude towards those from other places. But without exception, none of them have a good ending after coming to Yunhai."

Jiang Tian's words were tantamount to an insult.

And he said it to Shang Mingyan's face.

Shang Mingyan's expression remained unchanged. "If you're talking about people from the 21st Bureau, that might be an issue, but not our 5th Bureau, you've probably misunderstood."

The 21st Bureau is in drug enforcement.

The 5th Bureau, however,

is in criminal investigation.

While speaking, Shang Mingyan continued to secretly observe Jiang Tian, ​​trying to understand his behavior more deeply, even just his tone of voice was enough.

"Somewhat arrogant," Shang Mingyan analyzed inwardly.

"I think your problem is the most serious," Jiang Tian said.

"You're very bold."

Shang Mingyan hadn't expected Jiang Tian to be so bold, but thinking about the dangerous life Jiang Tian led, Shang Mingyan had a deeper understanding of him.

This made Shang Mingyan smile inwardly.

However, Shang Mingyan didn't know that from the very beginning, her inner thoughts had been under Jiang Tian's control.

"Do you know why I dare to say this? Because I know you'll calm down on your own, and I know that once you've calmed down, you'll want to understand me more deeply. I also know that you'll think you understand me better based on what I've said."

If Jiang Tian felt pressured before...

But what does it mean to be a living legend in criminal investigation?!

It's no exaggeration to say that he stands at the very top of the pyramid throughout history, an inhuman being whom no one can surpass.

Even if Shang Mingyan is incredibly talented, his abilities are terrifying, and his experience is even more terrifying, sorry, Jiang Tian has opened up.

As expected.

Shang Mingyan's face remained calm, but he felt like vomiting.

As if he had eaten shit.

His heart felt instantly blocked.

Discomfort.

Nausea.

He was completely crushed in the confrontation.

"You were stunned for two seconds, your eyes somewhat vacant. Although you were trying to hide it, you're almost unable to accept reality right now. But your dazed eyes kept glancing at me out of the corner of your eye. You have some admiration for me. Your behavior shows that you've never seen a man like me before. I've aroused your curiosity. More than inner shock, you want to understand me. I've pierced your heart. Your eyes flickered; you're panicking."

Micro-expressions have no effect on someone like Shang Mingyan.

But I'm sorry, Jiang Tian has opened the door.

All your actions and movements, seemingly meaningless, actually reveal your inner panic.



Chapter 466 Recruiting, promoting? No, he has already made progress.


Standing to the side, Miss Qiu watched her teacher, her professor, being utterly and completely annihilated by Jiang Tian in their very first exchange.

Even knowing Jiang Tian was a monster,

Miss Qiu couldn't help but be shocked.

She knew Jiang Tian was monstrous; it seemed there was nothing he couldn't do.

But psychology! Shang Mingyan had almost pushed the boundaries of what his predecessors had achieved, pioneering his own path and holding a certain academic standing in the field of psychology worldwide.

Yet, in their very first exchange, the psychology professor was utterly crushed by Jiang Tian.

Pfft!!!

Miss Qiu spat out her drink.

Of course, most importantly.

"Team Leader Jiang, Professor Shang meant no harm," Miss Qiu quickly stepped forward.

Because if she didn't speak up, she feared her mentor would be so devastated that he might do something drastic.

This was too much of a bullying.

The professor was being bullied so badly by Jiang Tian; Miss Qiu could hardly bear to watch.

“I’ve said it before: those who gaze into the abyss, the abyss also gazes into them; those who skillfully manipulate hearts will ultimately reap what they sow,” Jiang Tian shook his head.

Even with his reputation as the King of Criminal Investigation, Jiang Tian’s daily life, aside from solving cases, automatically filters out psychological elements.

In short…

In his daily life outside of missions, there needs to be some unknown enjoyment; otherwise, life would be far too lonely.

Just like this poor Shang Mingyan.

“I know, if there were malice, it wouldn’t be like this,” Jiang Tian said.

From the very beginning, Shang Mingyan’s condescending attitude had already displeased Jiang Tian.

If he had intervened earlier, the case might have been solved much sooner, and Jiang Tian wouldn't have even needed to be arrested.

Although being arrested resolved the problem, who would want to stay in a private room?

Especially since they started with psychological hypnosis, it thoroughly enraged Jiang Tian. Otherwise, he wouldn't have gone so far as to torment Shang Mingyan so badly.

"The professor didn't want this either, but it's just his nature. He's too sensitive to emotions and those around him. When psychology reaches its limit, it becomes uncontrollable. Otherwise, who would want to do this?" Miss Qiu quickly explained.

Jiang Tian's mood eased slightly.

His expression remained unchanged.

Has he broken down? Not completely. Jiang Tian realized that Shang Mingyan was still observing him, only more discreetly, but still not escaping Jiang Tian's notice.

This almost made Jiang Tian laugh.

Before meeting him, Jiang Tian still held some respect for Shang Mingyan.

After all, before his transmigration in his previous life, Shang Mingyan had reached a level that was beyond the reach of most people.

After his rebirth.

Even now, Shang Mingyan hasn't reached his peak in his later years; he's only in his thirties, yet he has already created a legendary career and made significant contributions.

Not a single one less.

This person is also a recipient of multiple First-Class Merits.

And because of his travels all over the country.

Solving even a single major case earns at least a second-class merit.

Some very serious cases, those with significant impact and difficult to handle, have also garnered first-class merits.

Shang Mingyan's achievements, aside from the special-class merit, are arguably no less than Jiang Tian's.

This is mainly because Jiang Tian rose to prominence too quickly, accomplishing far too much in a short period.

Otherwise, considering the cases Jiang Tian has handled...

If things had gone a little slower, he might have even received one or two more first-class merit awards.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​it was all too insignificant.

If Shang Mingyan hadn't been so domineering, so arrogant before, even refusing Jiang Tian entry into the special task force, especially giving the impression that letting Jiang Tian understand the case was superfluous and risky...

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​you truly have impressed me."

After all, it was Shang Mingyan. Her composure had almost been shattered by Jiang Tian, ​​but only for a short time.

Now, Shang Mingyan understood.

Jiang Tian had just been deliberately provoking her emotions.

She had originally wanted to cause Jiang Tian's emotions to fluctuate, but unexpectedly, she had been directly provoked by him instead.

And without her even realizing it.

Shang Mingyan had completely abandoned her contempt and looked at Jiang Tian with seriousness.

She had realized it.

Jiang Tian chuckled inwardly, but realizing his mistake didn't bother him. With Shang Mingyan's inherent skill, it would be difficult to see through her if she deliberately concealed her abilities with a cool and aloof demeanor.

But Jiang Tian was different.

No matter how Shang Mingyan tried to hide it, it was all under Jiang Tian's control.

"To have reached this level in psychology, I have to admit, even if I don't want to believe it, your psychological expertise is already at the top level in the world. With such outstanding psychological skills, your criminal investigation abilities must also be considerable."

Shang Mingyan exhaled.

After regaining her composure, Shang Mingyan instantly understood.

She had underestimated Jiang Tian, ​​and at the same time, she had previously held a prejudice against him.

Or rather...

Jiang Tian's assertive teaching finally made Shang Mingyan realize.

So, his mention of Beijing at the beginning was a veiled reference to her.

Thinking this,

Shang Mingyan's feelings were complicated.

Mainly, who could have imagined that this young man was so monstrous?

After all, the records showed Jiang Tian was only in his early twenties, and he'd already gone so far in the anti-drug field. Even if he'd been trained from birth, it was hard to imagine him achieving such success in criminal investigation.

No wonder her student was so highly regarded.

"Of course, after all, studying hard is for serving the people," Jiang Tian replied flawlessly.

"Since you have such talent in criminal investigation, why not come directly to the Fifth Bureau and make a name for yourself? Why settle for being a lowly anti-drug officer in Yunhai?" Shang Mingyan had to admit she was tempted.

He was definitely more talented than her student, Miss Qiu.

In other words,

If Jiang Tian joined the Fifth Bureau,

it would mean that

the nation's criminal investigation capabilities would, in the future, undoubtedly occupy a leading position globally.

"Your smile is no longer concealed; is it because you're tempted?" Shang Mingyan was already doubting her own psychology.

"No, I have no interest whatsoever," Jiang Tian shook his head decisively.

Shang Mingyan was surprised: "There are so many major cases in Yunhai now. With you joining the Fifth Bureau and our special task force, you can not only help us solve the major case of Secretary Mu's attack, but you can even get involved in the Wen Yulin case."

At this point, Shang Mingyan's mature and refined face showed undisguised confidence as he said, "Moreover, after you come, I can guarantee that if you complete these tasks and achieve merit, I will ensure that you will be promoted even further in a year."

She couldn't fathom why Jiang Tian could refuse; the conditions were simply too generous.

Especially the further advancement.

She knew Jiang Tian had been promoted multiple times without prior recognition, which wouldn't have been possible without extraordinary achievements recently. However, Shang Mingyan believed she could still manage to move him to a different position within a year.

Just as Shang Mingyan finished speaking,

a voice suddenly rang out.

Behind Jiang Tian, ​​Gao Xiaolong's voice, tinged with surprise, went directly: "Comrade Jiang has already found clues for Secretary Mu's attack case, hasn't he? Also, Wen Yulin's attack case has been solved; the murderer has been arrested. Are there any other cases involving Wen Yulin?"

Buzz!!!

Wen Yulin's case has been solved?

Oh no.

Not only solved, but he's been arrested?

The murderer has been caught?

Is this a joke?

"Chief Gao, your joke isn't funny. The Wen Yulin case only happened a few days ago, and there haven't been any leads yet." Shang Mingyan simply couldn't believe that a cold case the special task force hadn't found any clues about could be solved so easily by someone else catching the murderer so quickly.

"Oh, Xiao Jiang might have forgotten to tell you, Wen Yulin had vested interests with Shimiao Village. The mastermind behind the murder acted through an overseas hired force, but the leader of that force happened to be in Shimiao Village and was arrested by Comrade Jiang Tian. The case was solved in the process. The next step is interrogation, and the murderer will definitely not be able to escape." Chief Gao explained.

Boom!!!

It would have been better if he hadn't said that.

After hearing this, even with Shang Mingyan's ability to control his emotions, his eyes became vacant and blank.

His mouth was slightly open, staring blankly at Jiang Tian.

The next moment, Gao Xiaolong's voice rang out: "Furthermore, after careful consideration, Comrade Jiang has been added to the list of those to be promoted, and he will be officially promoted soon."

Boom!!!

Shang Mingyan almost choked.

A further step.

Jiang Tian now seems to be a First-Class Superintendent, oh no, a Police Inspector, that's right, a Police Inspector.

A kid in his early twenties is already a Police Inspector, what's the next step?

A Second-Class Police Inspector?

Is this a joke?



Chapter 467 Yunhai cannot lose him, just like...


The sheer scale of this impact is terrifying.

The sheer terror of this crime-solving capability.

Their special task force mobilized resources across the entire province for the investigation, spending over ten days, and almost all the cases remained unsolved—yes, all of them became unsolved cases.

This situation actually dampened Minister Tan's and his team's spirits.

Specifically, it was for this reason.

After all, they had deployed so many resources to investigate Secretary Mu's case, and incidentally, they were also checking for any leads on several recent major cases.

In the end, after more than ten days, they hadn't uncovered anything of substance.

Not only that.

In the end, all of them ended as unsolved cases.

What are unsolved cases? They are cases that have been sealed away, to be unearthed ten or even twenty years later, when the currently sealed evidence is retrieved.

Then, future technology is used to investigate the existing evidence.

But with these kinds of cases, it's considered good if one or two out of ten are solved; it's all down to luck.

In other words, both the Wen Yulin case and the attack on Secretary Mu were essentially closed. The entire province mobilized resources, conducting a massive crackdown, even imprisoning many people.

However, there were absolutely no leads in either case.

Finally,

The only remaining clue—the lead and clue from the final investigation into the attack on Secretary Mu—lies with Yuan Guangliang.

The crucial point is that there's no conclusive evidence against Yuan Guangliang, nor can any connection be found between him and the mastermind.

The only certainty is that Yuan Guangliang is indeed involved.

On the other hand, there's an investigation into Yuan Guangliang's life, but so far, no valuable leads have been found.

Overall,

The expectations were very low.

It even made Minister Tan momentarily suspect that Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan had switched identities.

Two unsolved cases.

resolved by Jiang Tian in the blink of an eye, even in one fell swoop.

"Professor Shang, Lao Gao is right. It's obviously very difficult to investigate the murderer domestically. After all, the murderer acted through foreign forces, and directly targeting foreign forces is unrealistic. It was just a coincidence that the leader of that foreign force happened to be here conducting a transaction and was arrested incidentally. Xiao Jiang was just lucky. Professor Shang, please don't overthink it. We won't have any complaints about you. After all, we all recognize your abilities, Xiao Shang," Minister Tan quickly interjected.

It would have been better if he hadn't said it.

After saying those words, Shang Mingyan felt even worse than if he had eaten shit.

It would have been better not to say anything at all.

However, Shang Mingyan had this advantage: he was less skilled than others.

If he made a mistake, and it was his own fault, he would never be stubborn.

So Shang Mingyan immediately said, "Minister Tan, Chief Gao, I was wrong. I apologize. After coming to Yunhai, I did make mistakes. I shouldn't have been prejudiced, and I shouldn't have underestimated others. This is my problem, and I will make a deep self-criticism later."

This case...

The most crucial person was Jiang Tian.

But Jiang Tian wasn't involved.

No, in fact, Jiang Tian's family was already prepared, but because of her biased perspective and prejudice against him, these problems arose.

No.

In fact, if Jiang Tian had been involved, these cases would probably have been solved long ago.

Thinking this, Professor Shang felt a sudden tightness in his chest.

Professor Shang didn't believe it was a coincidence. Solving cases, especially many cold cases, requires countless details to deduce.

Luck?

That's just an outsider's perspective.

In criminal investigation, there's no such thing as luck. If you can solve it, you can solve it; if you can't, you can't solve it even if you search high and low.

Besides, the clues to the murderer in Secretary Mu's case and the arrest of the murderer in Wen Yulin's case—once could be a coincidence, but twice? Could it really be a coincidence?

To call it a coincidence is simply ridiculous.

The only explanation is that Jiang Tian definitely found something.

He originally wanted to join the special task force, but because of her biased perspective, Jiang Tian discovered it himself.

This was a grave mistake.

Shang Mingyan wasn't depressed at all; on the contrary, she felt an unusual admiration and admiration for Jiang Tian.

After all, this man had brought far too many surprises.

He could single-handedly defeat a hundred men, invincible in battle.

He could also master criminal investigation.

He possessed terrifying expertise in both criminal investigation and psychology; he was a genius without weaknesses.

How far could such a genius grow in the future?

Shang Mingyan simply dared not imagine.

And most importantly, Jiang Tian was the first, and absolutely unique, man she could not understand at all, and even more so, the man who could completely crush her in the very area she was most proud of.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​his expression towards Shang Mingyan was somewhat strange.

Mainly because of...

Shang Mingyan's personality.

While not outwardly completely unfathomable, even seemingly capricious, such behavior would leave even a regular psychology expert completely clueless.

But Jiang Tian could analyze and see through it all with remarkable ease.

Shang Mingyan.

This legend of the criminal investigation world.

Jiang Tian had thoroughly understood his personality, and this understanding deeply shook him.

Before this, aside from interrogations, Jiang Tian had always avoided using psychological micro-expressions and other analytical methods. Once you got used to them, life lost all its joy and surprise.

Jiang Tian only used them when investigating cases and facing suspects.

This was the first time Jiang Tian had used them on Shang Mingyan.

The result truly shocked Jiang Tian.

He discovered that Shang Mingyan was looking at him with a strange kind of pleasure.

This…

He could understand everything else, but what was this pleasure?

“Xiao Jiang, don’t worry, this case is completely and successfully concluded. Now we just need to proceed with the investigation step by step,” Minister Tan said, his expression instantly becoming amiable.

"As for the General Administration, your focus should be more on Yunhai right now."

At this point, Minister Tan's lips twitched.

Because Shang Mingyan didn't know much about politics, he wasn't aware of the importance of Yunhai, or Jiang Tian's importance to Yunhai.

In other words,

the current situation in Yunhai...

besides Jiang Tian being here, no one else could make a difference.

This isn't an exaggeration.

After all, before this, it wasn't just one leader who ran into trouble. Those who were seriously investigated were almost all inexplicably and secretly killed by criminal groups, dying mysteriously—they were incredibly audacious.

The key is that the investigations haven't uncovered anything.

Even if there were a serious purge, the scoundrels here would be like rats.

Some might even have simply fled the country.

Especially since Yunhai is a first-tier city, a major city in China, with a population in the tens of millions, and many remote mountainous areas.



Chapter 468 Taking the Greatest Power, Completely Unleashing Oneself


Before Jiang Tian appeared, there weren't even any clues to be found.

On the surface, it seemed fine, but the entire Yunhai area had been completely covered by the underworld.

He went to headquarters.

What about Yunhai?

Although unwilling to admit it, what would happen to Yunhai without Jiang Tian?

Isn't this ridiculous?

"Don't mention Xiao Jiang going to headquarters anymore. However, Xiao Jiang can temporarily join your special task force to investigate these two cases," Minister Tan said.

These two major cases were both orchestrated by Jiang Tian, ​​and both solved by him.

Although Jiang Tian had already received his share of the credit, who would complain about having less credit?

At least, by joining the special task force, Jiang Tian could at least gain two more collective credits.

"Okay," Shang Mingyan said coldly.

However, Jiang Tian could clearly sense the disappointment in his tone.

No.

Does this guy have some kind of masochistic tendency?!

Jiang Tian was dumbfounded.

Having been so thoroughly humiliated by himself, how could he suddenly realize that this Shang Mingyan was actually quite dependent on him?

No, it wasn't dependence.

It was a deep sense of reluctance to part with him.

"Xiao Jiang's mission in Yunhai is very arduous. In other words, Yunhai still can't do without Xiao Jiang," Minister Tan said with a slight sigh.

Although the situation at the highest levels in Beijing was still under wraps, Minister Tan, at his level, had heard some rumors. He wasn't entirely certain, just whispers, but they were gradually spreading.

At least Minister Tan had heard some of it.

He understood even more clearly the importance of Yunhai's strategic goals.

In the future, it was to become a strategically important city on par with Mingzhu.

Once the dark forces and criminal groups were dealt with, the entire Yunhai would become a crucial pillar for national investment in the next phase.

Moreover, compared to Beijing and Donghai, Yunhai had one crucial advantage: abundant natural beauty.

No one dared to imagine Yunhai's future.

From a political perspective, staying in Yunhai is clearly the best choice for Jiang Tian, ​​especially if it becomes a directly administered municipality.

Jiang Tian's future will be limitless; there's no need for him to maneuver around.

Following Yunhai will allow him to rise with the tide.

Letting Jiang Tian leave at this crucial time is utter nonsense.

“The special task force is also an option,” Shang Mingyan said, his eyes lighting up.

His gaze was fixed on Jiang Tian, ​​his eyes gleaming.

Without a doubt.

Shang Mingyan was now incredibly interested in Jiang Tian.

He practically wanted to know everything about Jiang Tian right now.

This man was like poison.

And just how capable Jiang Tian was, Shang Mingyan couldn't wait to find him a few cases to examine.

"Besides these major cases, the task force has actually taken on several other cases, all of which are extremely important and still under investigation. I just don't know what positions they'll be assigned to," Shang Mingyan inquired.

The task force is at a very high level.

It can be said that the task force has the authority to mobilize resources across the entire province.

This gave Jiang Tian even less reason to refuse.

Because after joining the task force, Jiang Tian discovered that he seemed to have an even more terrifying space for advancement.

Although there are many major cases in Yunhai, continuing here would allow for the filing of numerous more.

However, joining the special task force would provide access to more information channels, and wouldn't be limited to Yunhai; it would even grant access to major cases across the province.

With the golden tagline of "Legend of Criminal Investigation,"

it's as if...

the sky's the limit.

Jiang Tian's ears perked up with slight curiosity about his assigned position.

That's right.

Before this, the special task force consisted almost entirely of the nation's top experts.

However, before this, due to the lack of progress in the case, most of the experts had already taken some case materials to various parts of the province to solve major cases.

And to investigate some cold cases to see if there were any clues.

In short,

their authority is vast.

But without a doubt, almost everyone in it is at the very top level of the General Bureau of Criminal Investigation.

Especially those experts, each wearing police rank insignia, completely overshadow Jiang Tian; even the lowest-ranking officer in a white shirt is someone Jiang Tian is nothing!

He's currently only a Level 3 Police Inspector, but if there's a real opportunity, he could be promoted.

A Level 2 Police Inspector at 22 years old.

"That's pretty silly, but it's probably historic," Jiang Tian thought, his heart pounding.

Of course, even a Level 2 Police Inspector is nothing compared to those experts and big shots.

Just being sent in as a lowly soldier with no power is the same as not going at all; it's practically the same. It's better not to go at all; he'd have more freedom and wouldn't be a lackey. Jiang Tian isn't asking for trouble.

Besides, Jiang Tian lacks ambition.

He wouldn't stoop to that level.

Minister Tan clearly considered this aspect and said, "For now, let's send him in as a special advisor, giving him all the necessary investigative powers."

Minister Tan had high hopes for Jiang Tian.

He also wanted to know whether, given a bigger stage, Jiang Tian would shine like a meteor or fall into oblivion.

"That's what I meant too." Shang Mingyan spoke quickly, her expression seemingly cold.

But based on Jiang Tian's brief but already superficial understanding of Shang Mingyan, this rapid tone told him that Shang Mingyan was probably breathtakingly surprised.

This woman...

Jiang Tian felt that there might be something fishy about her.

However, Jiang Tian quickly realized he wasn't entirely normal either, and immediately calmed down. After all, geniuses always possessed some extraordinary mental qualities.

Jiang Tian temporarily put the rest of the matter aside.

"Boss, let me take you home to rest. You haven't had time to rest for days," the chubby Zhao Wei quickly stepped forward to offer.

To be honest, Zhao Wei's emotions were practically exploding at that moment.

The chubby Zhao Wei had seen everything that had just happened.

Abandoned from a prominent family in Beijing, disregarded by everyone and denied any political opportunities, doesn't mean the chubby boy is truly an idiot.

Besides, being from Beijing and part of the police system, he naturally understands the system well, especially Shang Mingyan, the legendary criminal investigator.

The clash between the two just now...

completely stunned Zhao Wei.

Then Minister Tan appeared and dropped a bombshell: Jiang Tian was getting promoted again?!

Zhao Wei realized he was almost out of sight of Jiang Tian.

Finally,

Zhao Wei was even more stunned to hear that Jiang Tian was going to join the special task force, even as an advisor.

My God!

This was a three-way law enforcement task force, but it was actually led by experts from Beijing. Local experts only played a supporting role; the decisions were made by the Beijing experts. Undoubtedly, each of these experts was a nationally renowned figure in the criminal investigation field.

The youngest was probably in their forties or fifties, with Shang Mingyan being the most talented and also the youngest.

Zhao Wei knew this very well. He also knew that, according to his understanding, there was a second-generation heir from Beijing in the police system, holding a high position, but lacking professional skills, who was ruthlessly rejected.

And then, Jiang Tian...

He became an advisor to the task force?!

"Are you crazy?"

"Why are you sending him off? Comrade Xiao Jiang's problem is none of your concern. I'll get him home safely. You can get lost."



Chapter 469 This devilish woman, she smiled.


"Huh?"

Zhao Wei, who had just arrived, stared in astonishment at Shang Mingyan, whose aura was cold yet whose peripheral vision seemed to radiate a terrifying, menacing aura.

Gulp!

Zhao Wei swallowed hard.

A person's reputation precedes them. In the police system, one might not know Minister Tan, but who wouldn't know this woman, this terrifying woman?

According to sources, anyone standing before Shang Mingyan felt a terrifying sense of dread, as if all their secrets were laid bare before her.

It was as if everything they thought about was completely exposed to her.

That terrifying feeling.

The sheer shame was so overwhelming that almost everyone who saw Shang Mingyan once would never want to see her again.

But, ironically...

Shang Mingyan's fame also stemmed from another crucial reason: she led a special task force from the General Bureau, and her influence was felt in almost every major case across the country.

This is why her reputation was so terrifying and widespread, practically known throughout the nation.

Zhao Wei had only glimpsed Shang Mingyan once before, from afar, back in Beijing, and that single encounter had left a terrifying impression.

If it weren't for the increasingly tense atmosphere between his boss and Shang Mingyan, and the fact that it was already quite late, Zhao Wei wouldn't have dared to step forward.

Now, suddenly seeing the terrifying look in Shang Mingyan's eyes...

Zhao Wei touched his pants.

Then he touched his head.

Thankfully, he wasn't so scared he broke out in a sweat, otherwise it would have been too embarrassing.

It was just a little bit of peeing.

“Your boss, I’ll personally take him back. This young comrade, I’m taking your boss. Why are you blocking the way? What’s going on?” Shang Mingyan demanded condescendingly.

The next moment, Zhao Wei’s face broke into a smile more like a grimace: “Boss, the boss is already very tired. He’s been working overtime for several days without sleep.”

Others might not know, but Zhao Wei certainly did.

He had witnessed firsthand how exhausted Jiang Tian was, running around tirelessly for the recent investigation. Even just seeing it made Zhao Wei’s scalp tingle.

In just a few days, Jiang Tian had single-handedly thoroughly investigated the accounts of Shimiao Village over the past few years and uncovered everyone with problems.

Some of them even had huge sums of money deposited two or three years ago, and their accounts hadn’t shown any abnormalities in the last two or three years. Jiang Tian was able to uncover even those.

One can imagine the terrifying workload Jiang Tian undertook, including conducting on-site investigations and reconnaissance in Shimiao Village.

Of course, besides these reasons, the number of cases within the Municipal Public Security Bureau was also staggering. It's safe to say that once someone entered the Municipal Anti-Drug Bureau, there was no going back.

They were practically ready to conscript anyone who went there.

Especially with so many cases, Jiang Tian sometimes had to lend a hand.

In recent days, Jiang Tian hadn't even had time to go home.

Especially.

Before the operation, Jiang Tian arranged for his cousin, Jiang Ning, to stay at home; he needed to see what was going on.

After confirming that the cases were progressing smoothly, Jiang Tian relaxed.

"Boss," Zhao Wei said, looking at Jiang Tian.

To be honest...

Shang Mingyan was terrifying. Zhao Wei was trembling even standing there. But despite his fear, Zhao Wei remained steadfastly by Jiang Tian's side.

This pleased Jiang Tian greatly.

It must be said...

Although Zhao Wei lacked ability, Jiang Tian didn't mind helping him.

At least, he wanted Zhao Wei to get what he deserved. Although Jiang Tian didn't know what had happened to Zhao Wei, he could tell from the attitude of the second-generation rich kids from Beijing towards him and the events that had transpired that Zhao Wei's family situation was complicated, even miserable.

He was truly unwanted and unloved.

"It's alright, you can go back first. Professor Shang and I still have a case to discuss," Jiang Tian finally said.

Jiang Tian glanced at Shang Mingyan with slight displeasure.

Even when beating a dog, one should consider its owner.

"Although you're the leader, you have the power, but..."

"I knew you'd say that, Comrade Jiang." A slight smile appeared on Shang Mingyan's face.

It was faint, and fleeting, quickly replaced by a cold, aloof expression.

But Zhao Wei, standing beside her, clearly caught it, and his pupils immediately contracted. Heaven knows, in Zhao Wei's eyes, Shang Mingyan was practically a demon.

She was practically a legendary figure.

Most importantly, Shang Mingyan had always been stern and unsmiling.

But now, she smiled at Jiang Tian?

She smiled.

She smiled!

"My team leader, you're amazing!"

Zhao Wei was filled with even greater admiration, almost kneeling before Jiang Tian.

Especially that smile—it perfectly embodied the phrase "a smile that could launch a thousand ships."

Shang Mingyan, in her thirties, was at the peak of her youth, usually as aloof as ice, unapproachable. Whether in legend or reality, this was the first time Zhao Wei had ever seen Shang Mingyan smile.

"Brother Jiang, be careful!"

Zhao Wei felt as if a tremendous burden had been lifted, a sense of relief washing over her.

In her mind, Shang Mingyan was like a demon; she dared not say another word, and didn't even dare linger—she ran away in a flash.

Before leaving, Jiang Tian, ​​Minister Tan, and Chief Gao analyzed the case and made some follow-up plans.

The case seemed closed.

The answer seemed to have solved everything, but they all knew the real challenge was just beginning.

The first step was to find the most professional interrogation experts to work day and night to process these criminals as quickly as possible.

Minister Tan didn't even return to Beijing; he went directly to the Municipal Public Security Bureau with Chief Gao. Minister Tan decided to stay in Yunhai for the next few days to prevent any trouble.

He also wanted to see how Yunhai handled cases,

and assess their skill and capabilities.

After seeing it, Minister Tan was instantly moved, or rather, greatly disappointed.

Of course, that's a story for later.

At this moment, Jiang Tian had already followed Shang Mingyan and carefully got into Shang Mingyan's car.

As for the driver...

Miss Qiu, with her deadpan expression, was already driving in the front.

"What, are you afraid I'll eat you?" Shang Mingyan's cold voice rang out in the car.

"Why do you say that?" Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

"Otherwise, why would you avoid me so much? Are you afraid I'll eat you?" Shang Mingyan asked condescendingly.

This was a woman who used a strong exterior to mask her domineering and extremely proud nature.

But in reality, Jiang Tian had long seen through Shang Mingyan and knew her vulnerable nature. He couldn't help but smile slightly.

"No, I'm just afraid of trouble." Jiang Tian genuinely didn't want to get involved with Shang Mingyan.



Chapter 470 Emma smells so good, coming home, the taste of home


Shang Mingyan, without any hesitation, plopped down next to Jiang Tian. Both of them ended up in the back seat, making the normally spacious sedan feel somewhat cramped.

“I’m really curious about something right now: how did you do that?” Shang Mingyan stared intently at Jiang Tian, ​​making no attempt to hide her feelings.

She was completely open in front of Jiang Tian.

Pretending was exhausting, but since Jiang Tian could see through her anyway, there was no point in pretending. Now, Shang Mingyan made no attempt to conceal her expressions or actions.

“What? How did you do that?” Jiang Tian feigned ignorance.

“Yuan Guangliang is connected to the case of Secretary Mu’s attack. It’s one thing that you discovered it, but how could you coincidentally also discover the murderer in Wen Yulin’s case at the same time?” Shang Mingyan pressed closer.

They were practically touching.

Jiang Tian’s gaze remained calm.

What a joke. He had already experienced rebirth, and his understanding of psychology was already terrifying. Jiang Tian controlled his emotions far too easily.

Judging from Shang Mingyan's demeanor,

while it seemed almost unbearable, and few could resist it,

Jiang Tian not only held back, but even wanted to laugh.

His acting was so convincing.

"It's simple. Before Wen Yulin was attacked, he mentioned a crucial place: Shimiao Village," Jiang Tian stated without hesitation.

"That simple?" Shang Mingyan was somewhat incredulous.

Jiang Tian nodded.

"Aren't you afraid of losing some credit for the case? From what I understand, your contribution in Shimiao Village, plus the capture of the perpetrators of Wen Yulin's attack, should earn you at least a Special Merit. Aren't you worried about some credit being shared, turning a Special Merit into a First-Class Merit? You'd have nowhere to cry then," Shang Mingyan warned.

"I also captured Wen Yulin. The information I obtained from Wen Yulin, how can I attribute it to someone else?" Jiang Tian shrugged.

*Buzz!*

Shang Mingyan was dumbfounded.

Only then did she suddenly remember.

That's right.

The Wenye Group was single-handedly destroyed by Jiang Tian. A terrifying pillar industry, with assets worth billions.

It was also the largest developer in the province, and its diversified operations involved many other sectors.

"What about Yuan Guangliang?" Shang Mingyan persisted.

"Yuan Guangliang and I have no political conflicts, nor any personal disputes. His sudden request to join forces against me is suspicious. Of course, I've offended too many people, but after thinking it over, I only thought of a few recent major cases. I randomly guessed it was related to Secretary Mu's case, and asked you to investigate the clues to see if it was true. I didn't expect it to be the breakthrough."

Jiang Tian was very modest.

But every word he spoke felt like a blow to her soul.

This actually worked?

Shang Mingyan was speechless.

This was the first time she had encountered someone like Jiang Tian; he seemed like a ghost.

"Comrade Jiang, I'm becoming more and more interested in you." Shang Mingyan didn't believe him for a second.

The entire province's resources were exhausted, and after more than ten days of investigation, there wasn't a single clue. And you, damn it, solved two cases in just a few days.

And you still say it's a coincidence.

This is ridiculous.

Click!!!

"I've arrived." Jiang got out of the car.

To be honest, Jiang Tian didn't even know how to face Shang Mingyan now, because he realized that Shang Mingyan definitely had a mental illness.

And it was definitely serious.

Especially since he had directly crushed and defeated her in her most professional area, which was also her greatest pride, exposing the mental illness that Shang Mingyan had been hiding.

“Get to the provincial department early tomorrow,” Shang Mingyan said, her eyes shining.

“I’ll try,” Jiang Tian’s lips twitched.

Although he was about to be promoted, and although he had made meritorious contributions, Jiang Tian only felt exhausted.

Joining the special task force gave Jiang Tian considerable control over his career development.

But at the same time, it also added a terrifying workload to Jiang Tian, ​​after all, it wasn't just the special task force.

The Shimiao Village case that the Municipal Bureau had just solved required Jiang Tian to give a detailed report on many details. Thinking about this, Jiang Tian sighed speechlessly.

"It's already past midnight, everyone should be asleep."

Jiang Tian walked to the door and glanced at the time, realizing it was almost 1 a.m.

When Jiang Tian opened the door...

"Brother!"

A cheer rang out, followed by a powerful impact that slammed into Jiang Tian's chest, causing him to stumble backward and absorb the force.

As for why...

Jiang Tian's lips twitched as he looked at Jiang Xiaoyu, who was now crouching by the door, her hands covering her head, tears welling in her eyes as she pouted at him.

If Jiang Tian hadn't retreated quickly, Jiang Xiaoyu would have been badly injured, her head bleeding profusely.

"Brother, are you wearing a helmet?" Jiang Xiaoyu cried out in pain.

"Who told you to be so reckless? You asked for it." Jiang Tian said helplessly.

"Waaah, I don't care, it's all your fault!" Jiang Xiaoyu cried out angrily, baring her teeth and claws. Jiang Tian could only helplessly raise his hands to show his innocence.

He absolutely did not fight back.

"I don't care, tomorrow's Sunday, you have to take us out to play!" Jiang Xiaoyu sobbed.

?

Jiang Tian covered his head.

To be honest, if it weren't for his cheat-like abilities, Jiang Tian suspected he might have died of overwork.

That's why, while the higher-ups were all working overtime, Jiang Tian was given a day off. He didn't complain, because even with his robust physique, while he wasn't physically exhausted, the mental fatigue from several days without sleep was unbearable.

If even his "superhuman" cheat code died from overwork, that would be ridiculous.

"Stop it, your brother hasn't rested for days. Xiaoyu, calm down and let your brother rest," Jiang's mother scolded sternly.

"Okay!"

Jiang Xiaoyu reluctantly got up from the ground.

"Mom, haven't you all rested?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Your dad called to say you're back. He asked me to make you some soup. They were both worried you hadn't slept," Jiang's mother said.

Jiang Tian was touched and comforted Jiang Xiaoyu, promising to take them out next week if they had the chance. This reassured Jiang Xiaoyu.

Then Jiang Tian's gaze shifted to Jiang Ning on the sofa.

Compared to Jiang Xiaoyu, Jiang Ning truly seemed like someone living under someone else's roof, sitting timidly on the sofa with his head down, occasionally glancing up cautiously.

Seeing Jiang Tian return, he hurriedly stood up, bowing his head and calling out, "Brother."

Jiang Tian casually ruffled Jiang Ning's hair.

“Xiao Ning, just stay here peacefully for now. We can talk about anything after your college entrance exam. Of course, this isn't just because of you, but also because I've offended a lot of people, and many want to target me, and they'll come after you too. This isn't just to help you, but also to help myself,” Jiang Tian added at the end.

“Brother, you need to be careful too,” Jiang Ning nodded cautiously.

Look, what a good girl.

Just then, the phone rang.

Jiang Tian looked up.

It was Jiang Ning calling.

It has to be said that although Uncle's family is poor and can't afford a nice place, Uncle has truly given everything for Jiang Ning, and even bought her a second-hand phone to ensure her safety when she goes out.

“It's Nana, worried about me,” Jiang Ning explained.

At this moment, Jiang Ning also felt for the first time the hardships of being a police officer, having just finished working overtime until the early hours of the morning.

But she had to admit that after not seeing him for a while, Jiang Ning found that her cousin had really changed, inside and out.

Jiang Tian smiled and nodded. He remembered Guo Na vividly; she was a nice young woman, loyal and kind, and most importantly, she was the daughter of Captain Guo.

"Your sister's room is too small for a nap, so I've arranged for Jiang Ning and your sister to sleep in your room. If you're sleeping alone, you can sleep in your sister's room or on your sofa, it's up to you," Jiang's mother said, serving Jiang Tian some porridge.

Jiang Tian was really hungry, and he nodded nonchalantly as he ate.

"Wow, this smells so good." Jiang Tian hadn't had such a warm and familiar bowl of porridge in days.

But just as Jiang Tian finished reminiscing, a rapid phone call rang.

Jiang Tian frowned slightly. He thought it was a problem with a case at the Municipal Bureau, and answered the phone without looking.

The next moment, Jiang Tian jumped up, shaking violently, and exclaimed:

"What did you say? Captain Guo is in trouble? Have those CS gone mad?"



Chapter 471 Behind every hero lies a deeply etched memory.


"Your brother's back already?"

"Damn it, my dad's still not home, and I can't get through to him on the phone."

"Your brother's back, he should be off work by now. That old man, I'm going to ask him what he's been doing when he gets home, why he didn't answer the phone."

Hearing the incessant chatter on the other end of the phone, Jiang Ning didn't know how to answer.

"By the way, ask your brother if my dad got off work with him," Guo Na said.

This...

Jiang Ning stood in the room, looking at Jiang Tian outside the door, phone in her hand, her face showing a hint of shock, and a burning anger.

That's right.

Jiang Ning was a little scared.

This kind of anger was something Jiang Ning had never seen before.

What happened?

It could be said that Jiang Tian had given her a completely different feeling today. His attitude towards her, even Jiang Ning's feelings towards him, were constantly being rewritten.

At least, she even felt a little bit of security in Jiang Tian's heart.

And a feeling of dependence.

But seeing Jiang Tian's angry expression, Jiang Ning vaguely recalled that when he was attacked, Jiang Tian's face was only cold, never this out of control.

"I wouldn't dare," Jiang Ning whispered.

That's why.

While Jiang Peng was still worried about Jiang Tian and asking his mother to cook when he called back, Jiang Ning and Jiang Xiaoyu hadn't actually slept.

Not only had they not slept, but although they weren't very familiar with each other before, neither of them were particularly aloof. After introducing themselves, they found themselves surprisingly talkative.

Especially after Jiang Xiaoyu learned that Jiang Ning was about to take the college entrance exam, she even patted her chest and promised to tutor him.

In any case, Jiang Xiaoyu, as a top student at Yun University, was definitely among the best students.

The two chatted excitedly, and with Jiang Peng's call, they were even less sleepy and stayed up waiting.

"Brother, where are you going?"

Just then, Jiang Xiaoyu suddenly called out.

Jiang Tian was getting dressed, draping his jacket over his shoulders. It was late, and the weather outside was getting chilly.

“Oh my god…”

Jiang Tian paused slightly, as if remembering something. His gaze suddenly shifted to Jiang Ning, who stood behind the door, and he asked directly, “Were you on the phone with that Guo Na?”

Jiang Ning nodded blankly, unsure why this man was looking at her, but still somewhat afraid.

Perhaps the psychological trauma Jiang Tian had inflicted on her was too great, leaving her with a post-traumatic stress disorder. Clearly, in less than a day, Jiang Ning had only slightly changed her attitude towards Jiang Tian, ​​but deep down, she still felt terrified in his presence.

Jiang Tian's ruthless side back then.

The way he mercilessly trampled her and her father into the ground, humiliating them, was still vivid in Jiang Ning's memory.

Seeing Jiang Tian looking at her made Jiang Ning turn pale with fright.

Especially with Jiang Tian's angry face, she even wondered if he was going to kick her out again.

Where could he go?

Was the rented apartment safe?

Could he even go back?

Jiang Ning felt panicked.

"I...I'm using my own phone, the one my dad bought," Jiang Ning explained hastily, afraid Jiang Tian would misunderstand.

Phone calls are expensive these days, and she rarely makes calls.

It's only when Guo Na calls that it's a little cheaper to answer.

"What are you saying?" Jiang Tian didn't have time to comfort Jiang Ning; his eyes were bloodshot as he asked, "Is Guo Na home?"

"Nana, yes, she's home." Jiang Ning took two steps back in fright.

"Come with me right now." Jiang Tian grabbed Jiang Ning's wrist without further ado.

"Brother, brother, what are you doing?" Jiang Xiaoyu thought Jiang Tian was going to kick Jiang Ning out.

After all, there had been a previous incident, but now, through their conversation, Jiang Xiaoyu suddenly felt a deep sympathy for Jiang Ning.

Such a young girl, going to that dark, damp, and extremely messy rented room?

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't give Jiang Xiaoyu a chance to speak, dragging her downstairs with lightning speed.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian's Phaeton was parked downstairs. Jiang Tian helped Jiang Ning into the car and quickly started it.

"I...I haven't grabbed my things yet," Jiang Ning stammered in the back seat, thinking Jiang Tian was trying to kick her out.

But she just silently endured it.

Unfortunately.

At this moment, Jiang Tian had no intention of talking to Jiang Ning.

This made Jiang Ning feel even more aggrieved, her head bowed.

Why did her cousin, Jiang Tian, ​​treat her like this, giving her hope and then crushing it?

Sure enough, old habits die hard. Perhaps everything before was just a facade because there were too many people at the Municipal Bureau; once home, her true colors were revealed.

But Jiang Tian had saved her after all.

This left Jiang Ning's eyes lifeless, as if devoid of some color, staring blankly out the window.

But soon, as time passed.

Jiang Ning was surprised to find...

“This isn’t the way home. It should be at that intersection just now,” Jiang Ning said hoarsely.

“Your home? What about your home?” Jiang Tian retorted.

“Weren’t you supposed to be taking me home?” Jiang Ning asked.

“What do you mean, taking you home? What are you thinking? Give me directions! Where does Guo Na live?” Jiang Tian asked urgently.

He actually knew the general location of Captain Guo’s home; it was near the city center.

However, Jiang Tian had never been there.

Moreover, due to the special nature of his work, even with his colleagues, Captain Guo had never revealed or disclosed his residential information or address.

All of this was for security reasons.

It could be said that behind every regulation and rule in the anti-drug campaign lay a terrifying, bloody lesson.

Even his colleagues, although he was certain they hadn’t been corrupted, the price was too high; he couldn’t risk his family.

Therefore, even Jiang Tian didn’t actually know where Captain Guo lived.

"Guo Na, Nana, what happened to her?" Sure enough, Jiang Ning was completely bewildered when she heard this.

Going to Guo Na?

Going to Guo Na's house? Why Guo Na's house?

But Jiang Tian quickly provided the answer.

"It's in this neighborhood, Jinhua Residential Area, Unit 1, Room 602."

Jiang Ning brought Jiang Tian directly to the door. Although Jiang Ning didn't know what Jiang Tian wanted or what his purpose was, she knew he must have his reasons.

Fortunately, Jiang Ning had been there a few times before, and she and Guo Na were extremely close, best friends among best. They shared almost everything and were inseparable during their school years.

*Knock knock knock!*

Jiang Tian knocked directly on the door.

"Who is it?" Guo Na's voice rang out.

Almost instantly, a voice came from behind the door. Jiang Tian gave Guo Na a look, and Guo Na hesitated before saying, "It's me."

Hearing Jiang Ning's voice, Guo Na immediately opened the door.

Upon seeing Jiang Ning, Guo Na's face lit up with surprise: "Jiang Ning, what brings you here?"

After the initial surprise, Guo Na remembered something and her gaze fell on Jiang Tian. With a slight blush, she greeted him, "Brother, you came too?"

Looking at the smile on Guo Na's face, Jiang Tian felt a little heavy-hearted.



Chapter 472 Taking over the First Brigade, there will be no more scruples from now on.


"Where's your mother?" Jiang Tian asked.

Guo Na's face showed surprise. "Brother, my mother passed away two years ago."

"Passed away?" Jiang Tian's heart sank even further.

Jiang Tian didn't know much about Captain Guo, and he had only met Guo Na today. For their profession, work absolutely forbids mentioning family.

Even with their own people, they maintain absolute secrecy, never revealing anything unless absolutely necessary.

But now...

Jiang Tian's heart was heavy. "In that case, come with me."

"Me?" Guo Na's face still showed confusion.

After all, even if they wanted to go out, it shouldn't be until tomorrow, right?

After going downstairs, even after getting into the car, Guo Na still asked with a face full of surprise, "Brother, it's so late, where are you taking us?"

Jiang Tian didn't answer, just kept driving.

They arrived at the hospital.

A hospital at night should normally be eerily quiet.

But now, everything was completely different.

The chaotic, incessant wailing of sirens echoed erratically.

When Jiang Tian drove to the emergency room entrance,

Guo Na was almost completely paralyzed.

Ever since going downstairs, Guo Na had a vague premonition of what was to come.

Upon arriving at the hospital, the fear in Guo Na's heart grew stronger.

The moment she got out of the car, if Jiang Ning hadn't reacted quickly, Guo Na would have collapsed to the ground.

At the emergency room entrance, two ambulances were parked.

Several police cars on the road had their sirens flashing.

The sirens were particularly piercing in the quiet of the night, but Jiang Tian's gaze swept across the scene. When he saw Wei Xun beside the ambulance, Jiang Tian walked over: "Captain Wei."

"Xiao Jiang, you're here." Wei Xun looked at Jiang Tian with bloodshot eyes.

"Where's Captain Guo?" Jiang Tian asked gravely.

Wei Xun didn't speak, only his gaze fixed on the ambulance. Jiang Tian rushed over, and one glance sent a shiver down his spine.

A doctor was frantically pressing on Guo, performing CPR, while a nurse assisted with medical equipment.

But at this moment...

Guo lay there, completely still, eyes open, unresponsive to any pressure.

Jiang Tian took deep breaths.

Even though Jiang Tian had mentally prepared himself, the moment it actually happened still sent a powerful shock through him.

A person he knew so well, his captain so well.

His mentor, so well-known.

What Jiang Tian found most unacceptable was that in his previous life, Guo hadn't been in any trouble. In fact, before his rebirth, the already promoted Guo had invited him to dinner and promised to help him transfer if the opportunity arose.

But now, Guo lay lifeless on the stretcher.

"Officer Jiang, you're here."

Liao Ying, the last to get out of the ambulance, rushed towards Jiang Tian the moment she saw him.

That's how it is.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian's incredible performance earlier, it wouldn't have been a big deal.

But Jiang Tian's terrifying medical skills completely shocked the entire hospital. Good heavens, his medical skills were terrifyingly advanced, practically unheard of.

"How is the injured man?" Jiang Tian asked.

"He was already dead when he was put in the ambulance. We only provided humanitarian aid to the best of our ability," Liao Ying said silently.

Jiang Tian could easily see it.

Captain Guo had been dead for some time.

He hadn't even seen his only relative, Guo Na, before he died.

Jiang Tian felt deeply saddened.

His heart was heavy.

"Captain Wei, what exactly happened?" Jiang Tian asked in a low voice, his eyes red.

Because when he left, everything was fine; in fact, the entire operation had gone exceptionally smoothly.

"I don't know, I have no idea. We were attacked halfway through. Those people went on a suicidal attack, especially targeting the important criminals they were escorting, attempting to kill them all. Captain Guo was unfortunately shot while resisting," Captain Wei explained.

Although the explanation was simple,

Jiang Tian almost instantly rehearsed what had happened in his mind and roughly understood what had happened.

“Didn’t you say to be careful, very careful, when you left?” Jiang Tian said in a low voice.

“We didn’t expect those people to be so crazy. Some of them were even committing suicide attacks, and it happened just as we were about to reach our destination. It’s absolutely insane, utterly depraved,” Wei Xun said, still shaken.

But just then, a painful cry suddenly rang out.

“Dad…”

“Dad, sob sob, look at me, move, move!”

Guo Na clung to Captain Guo, her cries loud.

But at this moment, no one spoke.

Even the doctors performing emergency resuscitation stopped, silently standing aside and bowing their heads.

This was the most sublime farewell a doctor could offer to someone beyond saving.

Moreover, they had already done everything they could along the way, and even when it was clear the person was beyond saving, they had done all they could.

Jiang Tian watched this scene and sighed deeply.

After Captain Guo was wheeled into the emergency room, Jiang Tian stood at the entrance of the emergency department. At that moment, he was about to greet a terrifyingly powerful figure.

*Click!*

The bus door opened.

Minister Tan stepped down.

"Minister Tan!" Jiang Tian greeted.

"Little Jiang."

As Minister Tan shook hands, Jiang Tian could feel that his hand was icy cold, almost chilling.

"Heartless! You already know about the case?" Minister Tan was about to unleash a torrent of abuse when he suddenly looked at Jiang Tian and asked.

"I know a little, but not entirely." Jiang Tian shook his head.

The details over the phone weren't very clear, and Wei Xun hadn't explained them in much detail either.

Minister Tan lit a cigarette and offered one to Jiang Tian, ​​who took it and lit it as well.

Along the way, Jiang Tian had encountered many similar incidents recently, with one comrade after another sacrificing their lives. Today's events, especially the incident involving Captain Guo, had a significant impact on Jiang Tian.

And it wasn't just the First Brigade.

"The sacrifices were heavy. Although we took precautions beforehand, we didn't expect those thugs to be so reckless, and we certainly didn't expect them to attack so close to their destination. I suspect some of them went completely mad as soon as we attacked Shimiao Village, and they might be the next targets." Minister Tan's eyes flashed with murderous intent.

Through the swirling smoke,

Jiang Tian's face was also indistinct.

"Those people were carrying a large amount of TNT. The First Brigade suffered heavy losses. Captain Guo was just one of them. Pull yourself together and avenge the First Brigade," Minister Tan said.

After a moment, Minister Tan continued:

"Xiao Jiang, from now on, you are the acting captain of the First Brigade. I hope you won't let us down."



Chapter 473 was a terrifying and life-altering event for the young girl.


The dim lighting, the wide-open eyes, the lifeless face—grotesque, yet not frightening; only shocking.

At least.

For Jiang Ning, it was a terrifying shock.

Jiang Tian only felt a vague sadness at the deaths of his colleagues, superiors, and a leader who had been kind to him.

But for Jiang Ning, the shock was absolutely terrifying.

Because tonight, she had just seen Captain Guo. In fact, Jiang Ning had seen Captain Guo more than once; she had met him before.

When the city bureau wasn't particularly busy, Captain Guo actually had a relatively leisurely schedule, spending a lot of time at home with Guo Na. Therefore, Jiang Ning often saw Captain Guo when she visited.

In Jiang Ning's memory, Captain Guo was a kind and approachable, very familiar, and highly regarded uncle.

A familiar person.

A figure without a sound.

Especially the face faintly visible in the darkness; even with frantic CPR, it was all too late.

There was no going back.

"I'm sorry I couldn't let you see your father one last time."

"The mission was going smoothly, only the final prisoner escort was left, but unexpectedly, someone launched a frenzied attack. Perhaps, if I were here, things would have been better."

Jiang Tian bowed his head in silent mourning, approaching the stretcher. His medical skills were already very strong; a single glance told him there was no hope.

The injuries were horrific.

Even if Jiang Tian had now upgraded his medical skills to gold, being able to save someone didn't mean he could revive them, much less that he could bring back someone whose bodily functions had ceased for a long time, and whose injuries were so severe they were practically sieves.

In a daze, Jiang Tian's voice jolted Jiang Ning back to reality.

Unconsciously.

Jiang Ning's legs were already trembling, his face deathly pale.

But...

actually, the one most hurt right now is Guo Na.

Already from a single-parent family, losing her father meant her only pillar of support had collapsed. The impact wasn't tears, screams, or any irrational, heart-wrenching grief.

There was only profound sorrow, a deathly despair, and a feeling of being like a walking corpse, not even realizing it, not even accepting the loss.

That's the greatest pain—the future, the everyday moments of life, will never see that again.

That's when the pain will truly erupt.

"Don't apologize. You're all great people, aren't you?"

Guo Na silently wiped away her tears, then looked at Jiang Tian.

Yes!

Drug enforcement!

When there are no missions, it's one thing.

But in peacetime, the most dangerous team—everyone is great. Captain Guo is just one of many great people.

“Don’t say that only my dad sacrificed his life. You must have lost many comrades-in-arms. I’m so glad my dad was able to contribute his strength to defending the peace of our motherland and protecting the safety of people’s property. I’m proud of my dad,” Guo Na continued.

That sentence.

Silence.

Even Jiang Tian stared at Guo Na in astonishment. He hadn't expected her to utter such noble words at this moment.

Jiang Tian was taken aback, then his gaze shifted to Captain Guo.

He took a deep breath to suppress his inner turmoil, a sudden surge of immense respect welling up within him for Captain Guo.

Respect for Captain Guo's approach to raising his children.

Such values.

"Brother, will you avenge my father?" Guo Na asked Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian's heart was heavy, but without hesitation, he nodded emphatically: "Yes, definitely. I will personally ensure that the true culprits who killed your father receive the punishment they deserve."

As he spoke, Jiang Tian clenched his fists, his eyes burning with fury. If this case wasn't thoroughly solved, if...

those murderers went unpunished.

Even Jiang Tian himself couldn't overcome this inner conflict.

Just then, the sirens of ambulances began to blare.

At this moment, Liao Ying hurriedly said to Jiang Tian, ​​"The scene is horrific, the casualties are extremely heavy, many comrades have died on the spot, and a considerable number are seriously injured. Those criminals seem to have come prepared. I have to go now."

Seeing Jiang Tian, ​​Liao Ying felt as if a day apart had lasted three years.

Or rather...

Liao Ying's recent experiences had been exhausting. First, she discovered her brother was using heroin; then, he tried to kill her for money.

Finally, she was implicated in drug trafficking.

It could be said that with so many crimes, she deserved to die.

Such a drastic change, yet Jiang Tian appeared at this time, and it was Jiang Tian's presence that saved her. If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​she couldn't imagine what the consequences would have been if she had faced her crazed brother.

Unconsciously, Liao Ying, who already had feelings for Jiang Tian, ​​found herself increasingly unable to control herself.

Although she was happy to see Jiang Tian, ​​it was clearly not the time for reminiscing.

As expected.

As Liao Ying finished speaking,

in the distance, ambulances, escorted by police cars, rushed into the emergency room, their sirens blaring. The entire emergency room was in a frenzy of activity.

There were bodies of fallen comrades, and wounded people screaming in pain.

Clearly, at this moment, with limited time and medical resources, those who had already lost vital signs had been abandoned; priority was given to those who still had a breath left and a chance to survive.

Especially those like Captain Guo, except for a nurse who directed the armed police as they were wheeled out of the emergency room lobby and parked outside.

At this moment, it seemed cold and ruthless.

But now there was no other choice.

"Brother, I'm fine. I can manage here by myself. I'll stay with Dad. You go ahead and do your work," Guo Na said, wiping away her tears.

Although she spoke calmly, tears streamed down her face uncontrollably, no matter how much she wiped them away.

Jiang Tian felt a pang of heartache watching the scene unfold. Looking at the people coming and going in the corridor, he knew this case was extremely serious, its impact so great that it couldn't be allowed to spread.

How could it be spread?

If it were to spread, it wouldn't be considered a meritorious deed anymore.

This was practically a rebellion; the negative impact was unparalleled, even compared to Wen Yulin's.

But now, leaving Guo Na here, with the lifeless Captain Guo, was unbearable even for Jiang Tian, ​​a man accustomed to many trials. Faced with this separation of life and death, this situation, he couldn't help but feel deeply distressed.

"Brother, the case is so big. You go and do your own thing. I'll take care of Nana," Jiang Ning said quietly.

At this moment.

At this instant.

She suddenly realized how terrifying and dangerous her cousin's profession was.

This harm wasn't just to herself, but also to her family.



Chapter 474 The sky collapsed, a complete explosion occurred, and Yunhai will become famous throughout the country.


Seeing this horrific scene, seeing the injured people coming and going, this shocking sight truly left a deep and fatal wound on Jiang Ning's young heart.

And that's not all.

This time, it was just that Jiang Tian was lucky to return.

So, what if Jiang Tian had happened to be working overtime tonight? Would he have been one of them?

If Jiang Tian knew, he would definitely tell Jiang Ning.

If he had been there, this wouldn't have happened; it would never have happened in his life.

Not to say that everyone would have survived, but at least, even if someone had died, it couldn't have been more than three people. How could it have turned out like this?

"Thank you for your trouble."

Jiang Tian looked at her deeply, then said without hesitation.

Indeed.

No one has special privileges.

Even he, Jiang Tian, ​​if he were to fall ill one day, it would probably be the same.

After all, priority is given to those who can be saved.

Even with all medical resources, can someone who has lost vital signs be brought back to life? Hospitals only treat the sick, provide emergency care, and prevent wounds from worsening.

They don't possess magical powers.

With limited medical resources, they might be able to save more patients.

Jiang Tian understood this harsh reality perfectly. Even the emergency room was full, and Captain Guo could only be placed in the corridor. Even with an armed police officer guarding the area, the feeling of being abandoned by the world, sacrificing oneself without receiving proper treatment, ending up like this, would break anyone's heart.

However, this handling was clearly ruthless, but without any problems.

Jiang Tian turned and left with a heavy heart.

The case was too serious; Jiang Tian didn't have time to stay here.

He had more important things to do: revenge.

But just as he reached the entrance of the emergency room, he bumped into an acquaintance—Shang Mingyan.

At that moment, Shang Mingyan, leading his entourage, rushed into the emergency room. Upon seeing Jiang Tian, ​​he instantly stopped in his tracks.

“Xiao Jiang, you’re here too.” Shang Mingyan’s expression was aloof, as if he were a completely different person from the one in the car.

Despite his seemingly cold exterior, Jiang Tian could immediately see the surprise and delight in Shang Mingyan’s heart.

That’s why he said that.

Generally, Jiang Tian didn’t like to use this ability to see through people.

Human nature… who doesn’t have a few selfish moments? There’s a saying that goes, never try to see the true face of a person’s heart, because that face is far darker than you can imagine.

“Professor Shang, you’ve arrived.” Jiang Tian nodded, his gaze turning to Miss Qiu.

So, Miss Qiu came along too?

Yes.

They came together.

“The situation is too urgent. The special task force has been reassigned throughout the province to review major cases and verify other cases, so they won't be back for a while. I came to get the details.” Shang Mingyan only knew the general outline on the way.

“The incident occurred twenty minutes ago.”

“Specifically, it happened on Anxin Road, only about two intersections from the detention center, in a busy downtown area. The attackers were carrying high explosives and blocked the detention center entrance, bombarding us and launching a gun attack.”

"The other side acted very swiftly, and many of their attacks were suicide attacks. They seemed to have no intention of leaving alive. Several we captured committed suicide on the spot, leaving no survivors."

"The total combat time was only about thirty seconds. They didn't linger, and seized an opening to attack the vehicle transporting Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao."

"There were about twelve people involved. Two escaped. Because it was so sudden, many people didn't have time to react. The two escapees jumped into the Yanjiang River after the attack, and their bodies are nowhere to be found. Now, everyone, tell me, how do we solve this case?"

City Police Bureau.

Even after several days without sleep, Jiang Tian's spirits remained high.

He had been somewhat fatigued before, but this case, this whole affair, had completely enraged Jiang Tian.

The more he listened, the more Jiang Tian frowned.

Damn it.

The problem is serious.

Jiang Tian's heart sank.

Good heavens.

One case unresolved, another arises.

Most importantly, the escorts were anti-drug personnel from the municipal bureau.

Of course, Jiang Tian, ​​as a member of a special operations team, wasn't part of this formal structure. He could join the operation and act freely, especially since he hadn't expected any problems after the case was closed and simply went home to sleep.

Unexpectedly.

If Jiang Tian remembered correctly, before leaving, he and Minister Tan had repeatedly reminded Director Shen.

Director Shen couldn't possibly be unprepared!

So Jiang Tian glanced at Director Shen but said nothing, turning his gaze back to Minister Tan.

It was clear that Minister Tan was on the verge of exasperation during the case presentation.

The main problem.

Although the number of personnel involved in the opposing operation wasn't as large as in the Wen Yulin case, the impact of this case far surpasses that of the Wen Yulin case.

As the saying goes, once is enough, twice is not enough, three times is not enough.

That's exactly what it means.

This case is somewhat similar to Wen Yulin's, but the two are completely different, with entirely different backgrounds.

The similarity lies in the fact that both were suicide attacks, seemingly rescue operations, but in reality, intended to kill their targets. Why would they do this?

Clearly, it's to prevent being exposed after being captured.

They definitely possess information about the killer.

Therefore, after hearing the case analysis, Jiang Tian immediately asked the most crucial question: "What about the safety of Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang?"

Minister Tan had already learned all the details beforehand; it could be said that the local authorities no longer had the authority to intervene in this case.

Minister Tan had taken full control.

At this point, even Huai Province had no right to speak.

In the last Wen Yulin case, Beijing had given Yunhai some face, dispatching a special task force and granting the local authorities some autonomy.

But now.

What autonomy? The last case wasn't even over, and now this one has arisen.

This is blatant, unequivocal, and direct provocation against the entire motherland.

If this still can't be completely resolved, and a swift deterrent is needed, the consequences are unpredictable.

Just then, Minister Tan quietly spoke up: "Captain Guo sacrificed himself, riddled with bullets, while trying to save them."

Jiang Tian looked up abruptly, unable to believe it. Was this a dream?

Even though Jiang Tian wasn't present, he knew the size of the escorting force.

Moreover, they couldn't have been unprepared along the way. How could Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao, inside the vehicle, have been cornered by a mere dozen or so people?

Even if the other side had high explosives, it was impossible.

"This is the crucial point. According to the investigation, after the attack, Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao rushed out in the chaos. Captain Guo, in an attempt to stop them, tackled them both in the first round of gunfire. Our comrades reacted immediately and fought back, but were killed by the enemy's high explosives. Captain Guo, in that situation, was already dead on the spot."

The sky had fallen.

After understanding the case description, Jiang Tian had only one thought in his mind: those people had completely gone mad.

And Yunhai, Jiang Tian needed to know.

This time,

it was going to explode nationwide.



Chapter 475 A Test? No, for me it's just an opportunity.


Based on the case details, Jiang Tian had a fairly complete understanding.

Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao were crucial to the entire interrogation and subsequent conviction, a fact that Captain Guo clearly understood.

At the time, these two even contemplated suicide, preferring death to capture.

So, when the criminals attacked, they frantically ran towards them, and Captain Guo was the first to rush in, even shielding them from bullets.

Was it out of a sense of saintliness?

No.

Precisely because, through these two, they could uncover more crimes, maintain greater peace, and save more lives.

Even if the price was his own sacrifice.

"Captain Guo has lost his mind!" Jiang Tian sighed heavily.

As a veteran anti-drug officer, Captain Guo could easily have ensured his own safety in that situation, but he was willing to die to save two heinous and depraved criminals.

But this could have been planned more carefully!

It's not that losing those two means we can't find anything at all.

We can investigate further later!

He sacrificed himself for two scumbags.

But regardless, Captain Guo's actions are admirable.

At least, putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, he himself would find it very difficult to do such a thing, especially sacrificing himself to save two scumbags.

"Captain Guo's sacrifice has served as a wake-up call for us—no, recent cases have all served as a tremendous wake-up call. Time and again, we have witnessed the lawlessness, the depravity, and the ruthlessness of criminals. Therefore, in future cases, no matter what happens, ensuring your own safety must be your top priority."

Minister Tan leaned on the table, his gaze sweeping across the room.

There weren't many people in the room.

Besides Minister Tan, there was Chief Gao and the special task force with Ms. Qiu's business partner, Shang Mingyan, and Jiang Peng.

At this moment, Jiang Peng was visibly dejected.

Song Liguo remained silent, head bowed, seemingly lost in thought. Captain Guo was Song Liguo's old comrade-in-arms and colleague of many years; this incident might have dealt Song Liguo a devastating blow.

As for Wei Xun, he was also there, a participant in the operation, now looking disheveled and dejected.

Clearly, the sacrifice of Captain Guo had instilled a sense of shared grief in them.

The recent pressure had been immense; the successive sacrifices had meant that nearly half of the anti-drug squad had been replaced, either killed or hospitalized.

The constant loss of comrades was a terrifying pressure for anyone.

“Sir, I made a mistake this time, and I deeply regret it.”

Just then, Shen Han stepped forward to take responsibility.

“Remember the mistake for now; we’ll settle accounts with you after the case is closed,” Minister Tan waved his hand.

He had just learned the details, and it wasn’t entirely Shen Han’s fault; after all, Shen Han had indeed given instructions to be careful.

However, because they had just completed a mission, the team was somewhat lax in their vigilance. Even with the increased alert, they still entered the city as they approached the detention center. Given the location and the early morning hour, their complacency naturally led to a slow response when the enemy appeared, resulting in such a significant problem.

Otherwise, such a large casualty wouldn't have occurred.

After finishing speaking, Minister Tan paused before continuing, "Regarding the sacrifice of the captain of the First Battalion of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment, a vacancy has arisen. We must always look forward, and missions must continue. Leaving vacancies is not good. Comrade Jiang's ability and contributions are sufficient. For now, Comrade Jiang Tian will assume the position of captain of the First Battalion."

That's right.

Originally, this matter wasn't urgent.

Jiang Tian's rank isn't high enough either.

Normally, a captain in a provincial capital like Yunhai would need at least a second-level police inspector, or even a first-level police inspector, generally a rank higher than in other prefecture-level cities.

Jiang Tian is currently a third-level police inspector; ultimately, his rank is insufficient.

This could easily lead to other problems. There might be senior officers under his command, or a squad leader with a third-level police rank, or even a senior officer with a second-level police inspector rank.

A squad leader with a higher rank than a battalion commander? That's absurd.

According to the original plan, after Minister Tan returned, considering the current situation in Yunhai, he would have emergency measures in place for such a critical moment. Given Jiang Tian's indispensable role, he should be promoted as much as possible.

Jiang Tian's performance has never disappointed. With some maneuvering, leveraging the special merit he received in Shimiao Village, he could be promoted exceptionally.

This way, even if someone objects, they can't say anything. They can't just ask for such a high merit themselves.

With his rank increased, he can easily fill any vacancy later.

Or perhaps after Jiang Peng's promotion, a small opening up in the lower ranks would create an opportunity.

As for Song Liguo, the branch captain, his recent performance, while not particularly outstanding, has benefited greatly from Jiang Tian's promotion, with many rising through the ranks. As Jiang Tian's direct superior, Song Liguo naturally benefits as well, and with Jiang Peng's promotion, he's a strong contender for deputy director.

Meanwhile, Captain Guo, Jiang Tian's former superior, will also be promoted, a strong contender for branch captain.

Jiang Tian would then smoothly take over.
