But no one expected that plans would change so quickly. However, this was also a good thing, as it happened at a critical moment, a time of great uncertainty, directly promoting Jiang Tian.

This was absolutely like snatching chestnuts from a fire for Jiang Tian.

A twenty-two-year-old battalion captain?

What does that mean?

Twenty-two years old is the age when most people haven't even graduated from university!

Jiang Tian is already a battalion captain.

This is practically history.

This reputation isn't a good thing either. Standing out from the crowd is a challenge for Jiang Tian; if he doesn't handle it well, the problems will be serious.

It will jeopardize Jiang Tian's entire political career, and the first thing that will likely cause problems is his ability to manage his subordinates.

Jiang Tian remained silent, but the room instantly erupted in anger. Was Jiang Tian's contribution really that great?

It's a joke.

In terms of merit, who has been able to compare to Jiang Tian in recent decades?

But ultimately, it comes down to insufficient rank and seniority, making it difficult to gain the respect of the masses. Jiang Tian, ​​however, clearly doesn't have this problem.

Because in the past, those people have always been very familiar with Jiang Tian, ​​and he always brings them along when he achieves merit.

And the specific reaction at the scene was that everyone's eyes were fixed on Jiang Tian in astonishment.

"Minister, isn't this... a bit too fast?" Shen Han was indeed astonished.

Indeed.

Twenty-two years old, and already a captain?

And in the municipal bureau, and not just any ordinary prefecture-level city, but the provincial capital.



Chapter 476 A Military Order: Clearing the Name of a Notorious Figure


"Comrade Jiang has already achieved enough in terms of both merit and reputation. The organization always highly values ​​and promotes comrades with merit, regardless of background," Minister Tan said, confident that Jiang Tian wouldn't disappoint.

"Alright, now, the focus is still on the case. Everyone is already familiar with the details. What are everyone's thoughts on this case?" Minister Tan tapped the table and asked.

This case is too serious.

The impact is too terrifying, too egregious.

Once exposed,

the entire Yunhai will become the focus of the nation.

"First, we must block all information. The impact on Yunhai is far too severe. Second, we must track down the murderer, starting with Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao," Shen Han said after a moment's hesitation.

"What reputation does Yunhai have left?" Minister Tan interrupted, asking directly.

The meeting room fell silent instantly.

Jiang Peng stared blankly, his eyes vacant.

That's right.

The entire Yunhai area is now infamous.

Good heavens, one big case after another has surfaced.

The most terrifying thing is...

Before this, everything was calm. Cases were consistently kept to a minimum standard of completion.

In other words, they barely passed the mark, just barely scraping by. Everything seemed peaceful, but numerous firsthand accounts online repeatedly revealed that Yunhai was like a mini-Gotham.

The saying goes, "No amount of praise is as valuable as public opinion."

Through word of mouth from migrant workers from other provinces, it's clear that Yunhai has a terrible reputation nationwide.

At night, walking through the alleys and streets, you can smell a nauseating, pungent odor. Those who know, know what it is.

Some have even witnessed scenes of people being beaten to a pulp and dragged away.

There are too many similar scenes.

Some online are even saying, "Yunhai is a big stage; come if you dare."

Similar incidents like gang fights and territorial disputes are far too numerous to mention.

Actually, what caught the attention of higher authorities was a journalist's report on Yunhai's nightlife, which was then exposed online.

At first, it seemed like watching a violent movie, even absurd.

That wasn't the main issue. The key was that, with this initiator, countless people who had experienced the ravages of Yunhai came forward to give their firsthand accounts, confirming that it was real.

This caused a complete uproar.

Shortly afterward, under scrutiny from Beijing, it was discovered that Yunhai's crime rate was unusually low, revealing a serious problem.

In fact, before Jiang Peng, a police chief had already died unexpectedly.

Jiang Peng himself had acted relatively law-abiding.

Although no one was directly involved, everyone understood that these issues couldn't be resolved; reporting them would only bring punishment upon themselves. Therefore, these problems were temporarily shelved.

It wasn't until Chief Gao arrived that Jiang Tian suddenly appeared.

Good heavens, this was incredible! One major case after another emerged.

And each case was more terrifying than the last.

A major case solved in a month, even rivaling the national average.

The city's resources could rival the entire nation's.

This further fueled the media frenzy, thrusting Yunhai into the spotlight.

Concealing it would be difficult.

"Comrades, we can silence the media, but can we silence public opinion, can we silence the people's judgment?" Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table, shouting.

No one spoke. No one opened their mouth.

Even Jiang Tian had never seen Minister Tan so enraged.

A series of major cases.

How could they conceal it?

Could they hide it forever?

"Regarding this case, I contacted headquarters and decided to give the special task force and Yunhai ten days to solve it at all costs. Do you all understand?" Minister Tan continued.

"Understood."

Everyone's face was grave.

Good heavens.

On the surface, the only person seemingly unrelated to this case is Shen Han, Shen Han from the provincial department.

But to claim complete disassociation is utterly absurd.

Good heavens, how could a provincial department handle such a massive matter? Why did they allow Yunhai to become like this? And to think they can sever ties? That's a pipe dream.

It's impossible in this lifetime.

"Professor Shang, do you have any thoughts?" Minister Tan looked at Shang Mingyan.

This case is very important.

As he said, it can't be hidden for long. It can be hidden for a short time, but if it goes on for too long, it will likely cause serious trouble.

Once it's exposed, and the case hasn't been solved yet...

The public attention will be terrifying; the entire Yunhai will be bombarded with criticism.

Actually, Minister Tan is giving people time.

Such a major case, the Shimiao Village incident, and the subsequent attack—no matter how much suppression they try to maintain, it'll likely be over in ten days or so before the news spreads.

These ten days are time for Yunhai, and even more so for headquarters; they must solve the case at all costs.

And what is the source of Minister Tan's confidence?

That's right.

It's Professor Shang Mingyan's words.

"I wasn't at the scene to discuss the specific details, but here's the on-site investigation report, which gives us a rough idea of ​​about 70-80%. Actually, my opinion is to wait until after we've interrogated Shi Haichao and the other man. Right now, the only clue is on them, at least based on the information we have. As for other issues, I have no leads yet," Shang Mingyan sighed.

Putting everything else aside,

the extremely disciplined actions, the suicide attack, and the final escape—all were executed flawlessly.

This was an organized and premeditated attack.

Shang Mingyan's eyes blazed with fury, his gaze fixed on Jiang Tian.

That's right.

Compared to other cases where there are no leads, this case offers more avenues for breakthrough.

Although no one was captured alive, the fact that the other party was willing to take such a risk means that Shi Haichao and his companion are now of paramount importance; they must possess some crucial clues.

This presents a significant challenge, especially since it falls within Shang Mingyan's area of ​​expertise.

Shang Mingyan is extremely confident this time.

"Minister, rest assured, our task force will definitely uncover the truth within ten days," Shang Mingyan asserted immediately.

After a pause, Shang Mingyan looked at Jiang Tian and asked, "Comrade Jiang, what's your opinion? Do you have a better solution?"

This was merely a polite remark made by Shang Mingyan, after all, Jiang Tian was not a party involved, and the dossier records were countless times more detailed than the oral account.

But ten days is more than enough.

After learning about Jiang Tian, ​​Shang Mingyan wanted to see his true colors and get a direct demonstration of his criminal investigation abilities.

What happened before was all speculation.

Now, however, is the time for the real test.

Sure enough, after those words, everyone's eyes turned to Jiang Tian.

They wanted to see how Jiang Tian would respond.

Jiang Tian simply smiled…



Chapter 477 Jiang Peng doubts life: Do I deserve such a son?


"How did Comrade Jiang offend Professor Shang?"

"This case has only just begun, and Comrade Jiang is still quite young!"

"To be honest, based on the information revealed so far, getting accurate feedback is difficult. Only veteran criminal investigators can uncover some details. Comrade Jiang will likely find it hard to answer!"

"That's not quite right. Comrade Jiang was recently promoted, almost a historic one. It's normal for Professor Shang to test him at such a young age, but the test is a bit too difficult."

Indeed, it's too difficult.

Most of those present were from the police system, and frankly, based on the leaked information, it's unlikely they'll make any further discoveries. They need to find new clues through Shi Haichao and his colleague, the two who fled the scene, and those killed.

Then they can follow these clues to uncover the truth.

To make inferences without any leads is just making things difficult for him.

Of course, it's not about deliberately making things difficult or forcing him to reveal anything; it's about testing Jiang Tian for any fresh investigative ideas.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​after glancing at Shang Mingyan, he knew perfectly well what Shang Mingyan was thinking, and that Shang Mingyan had his own ideas.

However, regarding these criminal investigation issues and reasoning, Jiang Tian could say with certainty that in the current world, no one had more experience or more terrifying investigative logic than him.

The next moment, Jiang Tian stood up, a smile on his face, and slowly said:

"The criminals' operation this time appeared very meticulous and well-planned, with highly organized actions between them, but in reality, many problems have already been exposed."

Everyone's gaze turned to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Peng, Jiang Tian's father, also had his eyes twitching wildly.

Mainly because Jiang Tian's confident demeanor was too intimidating, and more importantly, did Jiang Tian have any fresh ideas, especially in front of so many veteran criminal investigators and even police bigwigs?

"What problems?" Minister Tan asked with slight anticipation.

To be honest, they had only witnessed Jiang Tian's operational efficiency before; they had never seen Jiang Tian's diagnostic logic and thinking in solving cases on the spot.

"The fact that Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang didn't have time to die in this operation raises a huge question. First, on the surface, it seems there's nothing they value more than their own lives. After all, with such a massive case, especially with Shi Hailiang's entire team wiped out and the whole Shimiao Village wiped out, what could possibly be more important to Shi Hailiang than his life?"

Shocked.

The people present were somewhat stunned.

Shang Mingyan, in particular, instantly stood up. Yes, Shang Mingyan had just thought of this question; she hadn't even considered that Jiang Tian could answer it or see this aspect of the issue.

But she hadn't expected Jiang Tian to see it. Even Miss Qiu, at this moment, clearly hadn't considered this stage and looked somewhat confused.

"That's not all. Actually, the two escaped murderers are valuable, but not particularly so. I'm almost certain that these two, along with the people who carried out the operation, are similar to the murderers in the Wen Yulin case—all from foreign forces. Catching them wouldn't be of much use. The key issue is that this case actually reveals deeper problems."

Jiang Tian's expression gradually turned serious.

To be honest, this level of analysis was child's play for Jiang Tian.

Take the Wen Yulin case, for example. Didn't you see how Jiang Tian acted back then? Didn't Chief Gao remain silent?

Because Chief Gao had witnessed firsthand how abnormal Jiang Tian's analytical abilities were, how terrifying he appeared—almost omnipotent.

And now,

everyone understands something.

As expected, Jiang Tian continued, "That's right. Only a more powerful, threatening, and terrifying force could have instilled fear in Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang. Perhaps there were prior warnings, or perhaps someone leaked information, passing them secret messages during the arrest that led them to escape and commit suicide. As for why, I think, besides their families, there's nothing else that could threaten them. In other words, the lives of their families are all under that force's control."

Hiss!!!

Upon hearing this, many people in the audience were shocked.

Given time, they could also figure it out through investigation and speculation, but definitely not now, and certainly not in such a short time.

In other words, Jiang Tian's criminal investigation and reasoning skills were unmatched by 90% of those present; even Shang Mingyan swallowed hard.

She guessed it, but not with the same level of detail Jiang Tian had deduced in such a short time.

Jiang Tian continued, "Whether they had given them prior instructions, or were able to secretly pass messages through a possible mole after our arrest, neither of these is something a small force could accomplish. The fact that they responded so quickly and decisively means that their power is local, and definitely not small; it's a local force."

Upon this analysis, the people present gradually fell into deep thought.

Because with this analysis, the problems became increasingly complex.

Who is behind this?

Who is in charge?

Is there any connection between them and Wen Yulin?

Are Wen Yulin involved with Shimiao Village, Shi Haichao's overseas mercenary group, and that mysterious force? What kind of financial dealings exist between them?

But without a doubt, the problems revealed this time are more serious.

Especially Jiang Peng, whose expression froze.

He seemed to be questioning his own intelligence—how could he have given birth to such a monstrous son?

"It seems the case analysis is almost complete, Professor Shang. From now on, your task force will be handling this case entirely. I will be here to supervise on-site. I hope you can solve the case as soon as possible." Minister Tan, feeling it was sufficient, gave a final summary.

In short, all local police forces and all available resources must obey the task force's orders at all costs and without question.

In other words, the task force essentially possessed an imperial edict from ancient times, allowing them to investigate relentlessly.

However, the problems exposed in Yunhai were becoming increasingly serious.

“Don’t be so discouraged, everyone. What’s the point? This is a good thing. The fact that these things have come to light means they’re at their wits’ end, they’ve reached a dead end. The more they come forward, the greater the credit will be. Cheer up! No matter how powerful this force is, I believe that with our sincere cooperation, we will eliminate them one by one, no matter the cost,” Minister Tan said, tapping the table.

“Furthermore, I’m only giving you ten days. Whatever the method, after ten days, I want to see the murderer caught.”

Minister Tan’s last sentence was addressed to Shang Mingyan.



Chapter 478 It's time to let the Jiang family's crime-solving methods shock the world.


This case is far too serious, and a lot of evidence needs to be gathered as soon as possible; the longer it drags on, the more difficult the case will become.

After the meeting room adjourned, Jiang Tian, ​​though somewhat tired, still went to the crime scene.

After looking around, he regrettably didn't find much valuable information. However, the only piece of information he could confirm was that these people came from overseas.

Still overseas.

Although he had expected it.

But the border issue still needed to be addressed.

Otherwise, the crime problem would never stop.

"In my previous life, it was about a year later, after the unexpected deaths of two bureau chiefs, that the higher-ups realized they couldn't solve the problem and completely shut it down. Now, with several cases in succession, the higher-ups should have realized the seriousness of the situation, but I don't know how they will solve it. After all, closing the border goes against Yunhai's grand economic strategy, but if they don't close it, the crime problem will never end," Jiang Tian thought to himself.

"Have you found anything?"

Just as Jiang Tian was deep in thought, a fragrant breeze wafted by, and Shang Mingyan stood before him, asking.

“A little.” Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

The crime scene.

The intersection was completely blocked off. Even the forensic team was searching for remaining evidence. The entire section of road was completely sealed off.

Of course, since it was early morning, there weren't many vehicles around.

Jiang Tian scanned the area several times before finally arriving at the Yanjiang River.

The Yanjiang River is a major branch of the Yangtze River that runs through Yunhai, with a main channel and numerous tributaries.

The river in front of Jiang Tian was the main stream of the Yanjiang River, winding through the mountains of Yunhai.

“What did you find?” Shang Mingyan’s eyes lit up.

“The killer’s scent.” Jiang Tian smiled mysteriously.

Jiang Tian’s sense of smell was incredibly sharp. His legendary-level golden attribute not only gave him various investigative logical thinking skills, but most terrifyingly, it gave him a terrifying sense of smell.

He could detect anything unusual at the scene.

Especially…

The scanning radar.

This deadly weapon.

This is where his expertise came in handy.

It's common knowledge that criminals typically return to the crime scene immediately; this is a criminal psychology trait.

Therefore, when dealing with a crime scene, criminal investigators usually conduct a thorough search of the surrounding area.

While they might not find the perpetrator often due to luck or the criminal's strong mental fortitude, the rate of on-the-spot arrests is not insignificant.

"The criminal group's actions towards our operation were highly provocative, or rather, mocking. This indicates that the criminals have extremely arrogant personalities; otherwise, they wouldn't have committed this crime. Therefore, the mastermind behind it all must be watching us from somewhere," Jiang Tian said with a smirk.

Shang Mingyan was astonished.

He was genuinely surprised.

If Jiang Tian hadn't looked so young, Shang Mingyan would have thought he had encountered a veteran criminal investigator with decades of experience.

Especially this line of thinking—it truly astonished Shang Mingyan; he could even analyze the psychological state of the perpetrators.

That's perverted.

The key point is, in such a short time, doesn't that mean Jiang Tian's crime-solving skills are even better than hers? How is that possible?

Shang Mingyan remained expressionless, maintaining a calm demeanor despite his extreme surprise.

His outward appearance remained aloof and indifferent as he looked down at Jiang Tian with praise: "Not bad, I didn't expect you to think of that. But don't be arrogant. These are things that veteran detectives like us can easily think of. This is just the minimum level of our task force. Don't even mention me, even Xiao Qiu would have thought of it long ago."

"Huh?"

Miss Qiu was completely bewildered. She had thought of that?

How could she not know?

"The crucial problem is, look at the surrounding neighborhoods. How are we supposed to search? The area is full of residential buildings. Searching door-to-door in this time of year is simply unrealistic." Even if the case is big, they can't go crazy, searching house by house in the middle of the night.

Moreover, those who can see this area are all high-rise buildings. Searching will be even more troublesome.

“And even if your analysis is entirely correct, it's enough to prove the perpetrator is arrogant and condescending. They'll probably only observe the horrific scene while committing the crime, and leave long afterward,” Shang Mingyan analyzed.

That's true.

If the perpetrator is arrogant, they wouldn't even have the cat-and-mouse mentality; at most, they'd just observe the spectacle during the act.

“What if I said I smelled the killer?” Jiang Tian remained noncommittal.

“Where?” Shang Mingyan's heart pounded, but outwardly, he forced a calm response, if only his voice didn't tremble even slightly.

Pretending, Shang Mingyan absolutely acknowledged Jiang Tian's extraordinary analytical abilities and logical reasoning. If Jiang Tian said there was something there, then it probably really was.

But, is it really that perverted?

"We'll have to see the specifics; I'm not entirely sure, but it's probably close to the truth."

Jiang Tian spoke slowly, his gaze lingering on the distant lights.

The more prosperous a city is, the richer its nightlife tends to be.

Take Mingzhu, for example; it's even known as the city that never sleeps.

Yunhai is no less impressive, at least in this era and at this time. It has benefited from the era's dividends, coupled with nationwide support, the full support of major strategic directions, and the green light of all resources, allowing it to develop rapidly.

It's like a small village that has undergone a complete transformation overnight.

Yunhai was no exception. With strong support and development, it flourished, especially at night when its lights shone brightly.

Under this high-pressure development, countless migrant workers and laborers, exhausted from their daytime jobs, sought places to completely relax at night.

And the more developed and prosperous a place was, the more such places there were.

These were nightclubs, bars, and so on...

A main road, spanning five or six intersections, stretched a considerable distance, nearly two or three miles in a straight line.

Jiang Tian removed his white gloves, donned a jacket, and, accompanied by Shang Mingyan, who was also dressed casually in a suit, crossed four or five intersections and stood under the dazzling lights.

“YS Bar!”

"Is this the place you said had clues?"

Shang Mingyan looked back at the crime scene, then at the huge bar sign in front of her.

The dazzling scrolling lights on it flashed incessantly.

Jiang Tian, ​​seeing Shang Mingyan's indifferent attitude, knew this woman's character all too well.

She was just putting on a brave face, refusing to admit defeat.

That's true.

After all, Shang Mingyan had been a genius and authority figure her entire life, a legend for half her life, suddenly being overshadowed by a younger generation. This was just Shang Mingyan's last act of defiance.

And the more arrogant she was now, the more interesting her expression would be later.

It was time for this Shang Mingyan from Beijing to experience the Jiang family's crime-solving methods.

"Scanning radar, turn it on!" Jiang Tian's eyes flashed red.

"Do you two have an appointment?"



Chapter 479 Reunion with an Acquaintance: A Cataclysmic Encounter After a Long and Changing World


In the slightly dimly lit environment, Jiang Tian led Shang Mingyan to the front desk. A dull, rhythmic melody emanated from the soundproof doors.

Even standing at the doorway, one could feel the floor vibrating with the music.

Shang Mingyan stood beside Jiang Tian, ​​her brow slightly furrowed. To be honest, this was Shang Mingyan's first time in a place like this, especially while it was open.

Of course, it wasn't that Shang Mingyan disliked such places. It was mainly that bars and nightclubs were often associated with organized crime and the old three vices: gambling, prostitution, and drugs—these were hotbeds of criminal activity.

What disgusted Shang Mingyan wasn't coming here, but rather, why, despite all the trouble in Yunhai and the constant crackdowns, could this kind of place still be so thriving?

Hadn't it been shut down?

Fortunately, Jiang Tian didn't know Shang Mingyan's thoughts, otherwise he would have told her with disdain that the prerequisite for a crackdown was maintaining social and economic stability.

"Good heavens, we can't shut down all entertainment venues because of a crackdown. If we turn a first-tier city into a second- or third-tier city, what's the point of the crackdown?"

"No, huh?"

Jiang Tian was speaking when he suddenly looked at the receptionist in surprise through the dim light.

That familiar face.

Good, good, that's it.

And when Jiang Tian recognized her, the receptionist recognized her too.

"Jiang Tian?" Xiao Bo exclaimed.

That's right.

It's Xiao Bo.

After some time apart, Xiao Bo had left the police force.

Or rather, Xiao Bo clearly understood that there was no place for him in the police station. Although he hadn't been directly fired, he had been marginalized, or rather, he wasn't even given the chance to collect a salary.

He was assigned to cleaning every day.

And he had received several major demerits, his rank had been repeatedly lowered—well, well, it wasn't high to begin with.

Later, he was practically relegated to the role of a cleaning lady.

This was unbearable for the proud and ambitious Xiao Bo. Eventually, while venting his anger, he got into a fight with the cleaning lady on duty, and the already hot-tempered Xiao Bo physically assaulted her.

The cleaning lady ended up in the hospital. Good heavens, Xiao Bo not only had to pay a large sum of money, but he was also permanently fired.

Yes, permanently fired.

Within the system, a job is generally considered a secure, stable position. Unless you commit serious offenses like breaking the law, once you're in, you can easily become complacent and give up.

But this requires you to have completely lost your drive. And who would choose to give up when they've just entered the system?

After a few years of settling in, things might get better.

Clearly, Xiao Bo hadn't reached that point yet.

Even though he was unwilling, Xiao Bo left the Municipal Public Security Bureau in a sorry state, not daring to tell his family.

Fortunately, he knew quite a few people in society, and his connections led many to cultivate relationships with him. The manager of YH Bar was one of those people; through that manager, he successfully landed a job as a receptionist at YH Bar.

The salary was even higher than in the police system.

This was the only thing that could console Xiao Bo.

He thought the past was behind him, a clean break from the past, a fresh start in his life in Yunhai, a new beginning, determined to find a way out.

But he never expected that on his third day at work, he'd run into Jiang Tian, ​​the very culprit who had ruined his life.

Not only was he badly treated at the city police station,

but the worst part was what happened later. Wang Yifei and her family went completely mad. Because of Wang Yifei's serious injuries, medical expenses exceeded 200,000 yuan.

The child survived, but the medical bills were staggering.

The crucial point was that because of the case, although the hospital had used medical methods to treat them without payment, they still had to pay for the subsequent treatments.

Good heavens, this was tantamount to killing Wang Yifei's entire family.

And then Xiao Bo was in deep trouble.

Now, Xiao Bo doesn't even dare to show his face, fearing being cornered by Wang Yifei's family.

His biggest fear is running into acquaintances,

especially enemies.

The saying goes, "Enemies meeting are especially hateful," and the moment Xiao Bo recognized Jiang Tian, ​​his eyes turned bloodshot.

"You know him?" Shang Mingyan asked calmly.

If he couldn't see through Jiang Tian, ​​he could surely see through Xiao Bo. Seeing Xiao Bo's expression, Shang Mingyan looked at Jiang Tian with confusion. Such deep-seated hatred, such hatred that makes one's eyes blaze with fury upon meeting—this was no ordinary hatred!

"An acquaintance, someone from our team. He did some shady things, then made a serious mistake and got fired. I just didn't expect to see him working as a receptionist after a few days," Jiang Tian explained casually.

"What are you doing here, you bastard?"

Xiao Bo suppressed his anger, not daring to make a move. Although he hated him, he was still wary of Jiang Tian's god-like battle record.

Making a move would be suicide.

However, after Shang Mingyan spoke, Xiao Bo saw Shang Mingyan beside Jiang Tian, ​​and damn it, his envy intensified.

It has to be said...

Anyone who sees someone they once looked down on suddenly get promoted and receive a raise, while they themselves, after a period of utter misfortune, have succeeded while they're stuck working as a waiter, and this person is accompanied by an extremely beautiful, mature woman.

That's right.

When a woman's beauty reaches a certain level, it's actually difficult to determine a winner. Mere appearance is superficial; what truly attracts people after beauty reaches a certain level is her aura.

Shang Mingyan is exactly like that.

His legendary life over the years has exuded the oppressive aura of someone in power, his aura extremely cold, especially his eyes, which seem to see right through people with a chilling intensity.

He evokes a feeling of fear, attraction, and dread all at once.

Shang Mingyan, even with shoes on, is probably close to 1.7 meters tall. Wearing a suit jacket and looking young, standing next to Jiang Tian, ​​they truly look like a couple.

The age difference is completely imperceptible.

And thinking about this bastard, he already has Mu Ya, and now he has such a beautiful woman.

Xiao Bo's face darkened, but soon, a thought struck him.

"This kid's really asking for trouble!" Xiao Bo suddenly realized he had a chance to win Mu Ya back.

If Mu Ya knew what had happened here, and saw Jiang Tian bringing such a beautiful girl to the bar, perhaps she would be heartbroken and he could win her back.

And with Mu Ya's background, how could he possibly worry about not being able to turn the tables?

The more Xiao Bo thought about it, the more excited he became.

"What should we do now?" Shang Mingyan asked Jiang Tian.

Although he didn't know much, based on his previous observations, Shang Mingyan suddenly realized that there might really be some clues here.

Jiang Tian didn't answer, but gave me a look that said "Look at me."

"Jiang Tian, ​​I never thought you would be so audacious. Having Mu Ya, you still bring women to bars to fool around. Mu Ya must be blind to have fallen for you," Xiao Bo sneered.

In reality, Xiao Bo was almost driven mad with jealousy. In his eyes, everything Jiang Tian had now should be his; Jiang Tian had stolen his life.

He wished Jiang Tian were dead.



Chapter 480 I just wanted to scare you, and you killed me?


"I heard from Mu Ya that you've been harassing her lately?" Jiang Tian asked slowly.

To be honest, Jiang Tian hadn't forgotten about it since Mu Ya mentioned it last time. However, he'd been too busy with cases lately, working for days without rest, so he hadn't had time to deal with Xiao Bo.

He just hadn't expected Xiao Bo to be so persistent, waiting until he had some free time to deal with him.

But now that he'd run into him...

"Harassment? I'm just pursuing her normally," Xiao Bo said shamelessly.

Bang!!!

Pfft!!!

Before he could finish speaking, a muffled thud rang out.

Jiang Tian's punch, delivered with lightning speed, sent Xiao Bo spitting out blood. His temple slammed hard against the bar, and he coughed up more blood in the process.

"Annoying..." Jiang Tian sneered.

Killing this bastard outright would be too easy; Jiang Tian intended to make him suffer a fate worse than death.

It was also revenge for what he had done to him in his past life. No matter what Jiang Tian did to him, it wouldn't be excessive. After all, the harm done in his past life meant that even killing Xiao Bo would be too lenient on this beast.

“You…”

Xiao Bo nearly died from the impact, his eyes wide and his blood-stained mouth seemingly trying to say something to Jiang Tian.

But his pupils gradually lost their luster, and then he collapsed limply to the ground with a loud thud, remaining motionless.

“You killed him?” Shang Mingyan asked, his face pale with fright.

“Killing him would be too easy. Don't worry, he won't die, he's just temporarily unconscious.” Jiang Tian casually explained, preventing Shang Mingyan from misunderstanding further.

Jiang Tian knew perfectly well the force he had used.

“That's good.” Shang Mingyan breathed a sigh of relief.

She had genuinely feared that Jiang Tian might have killed the man indiscriminately. Should Shang Mingyan handle the situation according to strict regulations, or leave it alone?

Fortunately,

After all, the other party hadn't posed a fatal threat, far from reaching the level of assaulting a police officer or self-defense.

“Now?” Shang Mingyan asked.

“Let’s go inside and have a drink.” Jiang Tian led the way towards the soundproof door.

Shang Mingyan followed behind. Once inside the soundproof door, the world outside was completely different.

A deafening roar engulfed everything.

Even speaking, the two could only whisper in each other’s ears.

The immense noise forced Shang Mingyan, who had always been self-disciplined and had never been in such a situation before, to stay close to Jiang Tian.

Shang Mingyan had solved various cases before, but this was the first time she had personally visited a bar while it was being operated.

"Where are we going?"

Following Jiang Tian down the corridor, Shang Mingyan even had to shout loudly into Jiang Tian's ear to be heard.

The fragrant breeze, however, did nothing to disturb Jiang Tian's peace of mind.

What a joke.

For Jiang Tian, ​​after his rebirth, beautiful women and temptations were all meaningless.

In his past life, Jiang Tian had truly experienced betrayal by a woman, causing him immense trauma. While it didn't completely extinguish his interest in women, it certainly gave him an exceptionally high level of resistance and self-control.

He had also long since seen through women.

"Find the killer," Jiang Tian said, looking at the scanning radar.

The crimson light was so dazzling; there was no doubt about it.

Actually, Jiang Tian discovered this bar while using the scanning radar on his way here. He had sensed something amiss about the bar while searching with the radar on.

"Are there any clues about the killer here? We should call for backup immediately," Shang Mingyan said urgently.

This was also why Jiang Tian often preferred to act alone, even when it was difficult to explain.

Calling for backup—he couldn't very well say he had scanned a criminal through the scanning radar.

"I'm not entirely sure right now. Let's wait until we see him," Jiang Tian replied.

"Alright," Shang Mingyan didn't resist the prospect of finding a suspect.

But as they climbed the stairs, Jiang Tian seemed to realize this wasn't his first time here.

Accompanied by dull, deafening music, Jiang Tian led Shang Mingyan to a private room on the second floor. But he didn't stop there; he led the bewildered Shang Mingyan to the third floor.

On the third floor, the music was much quieter.

"Is the clue here?" Shang Mingyan asked, confused.

She was genuinely confused.

In fact, if it weren't for the shock Jiang Tian had caused earlier, Shang Mingyan would have turned and left.

The case had just occurred.

The related suspects hadn't been interrogated yet.

The escaped killer hadn't been apprehended.

Jiang Tian had solved the case by bringing her directly to a bar.

Especially, what Shang Mingyan saw at this moment: a large row of women, at least a dozen, all heavily made-up and dressed flamboyantly, standing at the door of a private room.

Every now and then, the voices of the next woman could be heard.

“That’s right, it’s always good to take a look.” Jiang Tian gave an ambiguous answer, then led the way towards the private room.

The female models queuing in the corridor all looked at Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan with curiosity.

However, Jiang Tian bypassed them and went straight to the door of the private room.

Two bodyguards stood at the door.

“Next.”

Just then, a voice came from inside again.

The female model who was called first glanced at Jiang Tian curiously, then turned to go in.

“Wait.” Jiang Tian called out.

The female model looked at Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan with curiosity.

It has to be said that the moment she saw Shang Mingyan, the female model was full of vigilance, her face showing both wariness and disappointment.

Jiang Tian understood her emotions at a glance and thought to himself.

“Kid, I haven’t seen you before, are you a newcomer? Not bad, the one the boss found is good.” One of the bodyguards at the door had his eyes light up when he saw Shang Mingyan.

"Damn, she's gorgeous! I've never seen such a beautiful girl before. Kid, you've done a great job this time," another bodyguard exclaimed, clicking his tongue in amazement.

These comments also sparked discussion among the female models in line.

"It's over. She's so vixen-like, and so elegant. The boss definitely won't like us this time."

"Yeah, damn it, I thought we had a chance. Every time the big boss comes, we get at least 20,000 or 30,000 in tips just for drinking with him. This time, I guess we'll miss out."

"So pretty, and she's working as a hostess."

The whispers were quiet, but Jiang Tian heard them all clearly.

"What are you all dawdling for? Next! Oh my god, why didn't you let someone so pretty come over sooner? Damn it, you're wasting time. You, hurry up and come in with me."

Just then, the half-open door to the private room opened, and a madam in her forties came out cursing, but her face lit up with surprise when she saw Shang Mingyan.

"You're asking for it!"

Shang Mingyan took deep breaths, suppressing his murderous intent. In all his life, when had Shang Mingyan ever suffered such humiliation?

He, being treated like a hostess?

At this moment, even Shang Mingyan, in her mindset, would immediately place a hand on her hip, ready to draw her gun and shoot these bastards, then, regardless of any problems, seal off the place first.

“You want to stop me?” Shang Mingyan’s expression was indifferent, but her eyes were deep. She was suppressing her anger, her hand trembling slightly. Jiang Tian knew she was furious.

However, Jiang Tian smiled faintly.

Then, calmly, he drew a gun from his waistband and pointed it at the forty-something-year-old brothel madam in front of him.

Bang!!!

“No, I’ll kill her for you!!!”

“I just wanted to scare her a little, you really killed her?”

Shang Mingyan: ???



Chapter 481 I solve cases by being straightforward.


"The quality has been getting worse lately. Our bar needs to improve in this area."

Inside the private booth.

The view was excellent, with huge floor-to-ceiling windows offering a clear view of the bar's dance floor below and the women dancing above.

In the center of the booth, a man sat with his legs crossed, a slight dissatisfaction on his face.

"They're all just common, low-class women. I don't understand what you're after. But you can have your fun, the boss is watching. I hope you don't mess things up in the future, or the boss won't let you off the hook," said another man sitting next to him.

"Don't worry, boss. I'm not like that good-for-nothing Shi Hailiang. I'm not careless at all. You can rest assured, those cops probably won't be able to find us anywhere," the man on the sofa couldn't help but say.

When Shi Hailiang was mentioned, a man's face flushed with anger. "That good-for-nothing Shi Hailiang! Nobody expected him to be so unreliable."

"But you went a bit too far tonight. Be careful," another man cautioned.

"Shi Hailiang was careless. I've hardly ever sold anything in Yunhai. To prevent this kind of accident, I guess that bastard Shi Hailiang, being careless, secretly went out to expand his sales network. Otherwise, given how discreet we are, the cops would never find us. Shi Hailiang deserves to die." This was accompanied by a click.

In the dim light of the private room, a lighter burned, illuminating a cigar and the speaker's grim face.

His face hardened with murderous intent when Shi Hailiang was mentioned.

"Be even more careful outside of Yunhai. If we get caught out of town, and this source of income is cut off, the boss will be furious. You won't be able to handle the consequences," the man opposite warned expressionlessly.

Upon hearing this, the man with the cigar seemed to recall the BOSS's terrifying nature, and a hint of fear rose on his face.

However, he quickly smiled and said, "Our sales channels are foolproof. Don't worry, even if they investigate, they won't be able to find out."

"That's for the best. If you get caught doing something underhanded, think about your family," the other man nodded in agreement.

Seeing the man's expression soften, Xiang Tiannan took a puff of his cigar, his expression relaxing as well, and he seemed to let out a deep sigh of relief.

But as he breathed a sigh of relief, Xiang Tiannan's brow furrowed slightly, and he looked towards the corridor.

The private room was large, and there was a corridor several meters from the entrance. From inside the room, the scene outside was completely invisible.

But no one had yet entered, which was already making Xiang Tiannan increasingly impatient.

"Tiannan, it seems your management skills aren't very good. You can't even train your subordinates properly, and they're not attentive to your work. How can the BOSS trust you in the future, is that it?"

Xiang Tiannan's eyes were dark and brooding, especially after giving the man who spoke a deep look. His gaze was icy, but his face was full of fawning obsequiousness as he said, "These bastards are getting bolder and bolder. Don't worry, Ah Xun, I'll teach them a lesson later."

The man called Ah Xun nodded slightly: "Just be careful yourself. Our line of work, while profitable, is also extremely risky. Everything must be done with utmost care, especially your subordinates. You must keep a close eye on them; often, setbacks start with these underlings."

Xiang Tiannan outwardly agreed, but inwardly he was cursing.

Xiang Tiannan had gotten to where he was today entirely on his own, step by step. Although the BOSS had initially supported him, Xiang Tiannan was already resentful.

He did everything himself.

He did all the dangerous work himself.

While he and his men were living a life of leisure abroad.

His rebellious intentions had long been present.

If it weren't for the recent unfavorable winds in Yunhai, Xiang Tiannan would have already been preparing for a rebellion.

“It’s wrong of me not to discipline my men. They’ve been gone for so long, what the hell are they doing?”

After waiting a while, Xiang Tiannan couldn’t sit still any longer.

He stood up.

Ah Xun took a sip of his drink. Having just been reprimanded, Ah Xun’s attitude had softened. He said, “Don’t worry, the BOSS asked me to ask you how the shipment is going.”

At this, Xiang Tiannan quickly nodded, “The shipment has arrived at the warehouse and is ready to be shipped. Luckily, nothing went wrong with it, otherwise, I would have wiped out their whole family.”

“That’s good. This shipment is extremely valuable. Be careful and make sure everything goes smoothly. The BOSS has been keeping an eye on this shipment. I’ll give you half a percent of the profit.” Ah Xun seemed to be the BOSS’s mouthpiece, speaking with an air of superiority.

It was as if giving Xiang Tiannan half a percent was a huge favor.

This nearly broke Xiang Tiannan's composure, but thankfully, he maintained his composure, his face remaining impassive, though the murderous intent in his eyes grew increasingly intense.

Half a percent.

One million is equivalent to only fifty thousand.

Ten million is only five hundred thousand.

The profit from this batch of goods is only around twenty million. Although the total value is forty million, half of that goes to costs and delivery to the buyer.

There are risks and costs involved.

But he did all the work, and in the end, he only gets one million. Taking such a huge risk on such a big deal, and only getting one million?

How could Xiang Tiannan be satisfied?

However, thinking of the BOSS's power, Xiang Tiannan fell silent.

The BOSS's terrifying influence made him almost completely unsure if he could successfully betray him.

"One day, I'll kill that old bastard," Xiang Tiannan cursed inwardly, but on his face, he seemed overjoyed, constantly thanking the BOSS and so on.

"It's good that you think that way. Just don't do anything that will make the BOSS misunderstand, or you can't imagine the consequences," Ah Xun said threateningly.

"Of course," Xiang Tiannan nodded, then sensed something was wrong.

Damn it, where is he?

Xiang Tiannan stood up, his face grim.

An accident?

No, no, no.

How could an accident happen?

In this Cloud Sea, everyone knows the background, the power, and what YS Bar does.

If there's nothing wrong, who would dare come here looking for someone, come here to die? Besides, this is the third floor, the top floor; no outsiders would come here unless something was wrong.

Is there no one to accompany them?

While Xiang Tiannan was still pondering this,

Bang!!!

A deafening roar suddenly rang out.

Accompanied by a gunshot.

Xiang Tiannan's composure faltered; his calm emotions finally crumbled.



Chapter 482 You still say you're not crazy? You've gone mad with killing!


That's why Xiang Tiannan is so clever.

He also understands one thing perfectly well: pure organized crime can never last.

And Xiang Tiannan is clever enough.

He owns several large bars in Yunnan Province, and also has a security company. Ostensibly a security company, it's actually run by thugs.

But these thugs don't usually engage in any illegal or criminal activities.

They appear to be respectable, legitimate businessmen, with nothing particularly unusual about them, so Xiang Tiannan has no worries about being discovered.

And his sales channels are even less likely to be discovered.

If he were discovered, there would only be one possibility: international arrest.

But even if the international community wanted to arrest him, it would be very difficult to trace him.

From every angle, it's the safest place.

And someone like Xiang Tiannan, who appears to be no pushover, even having connections in both the legal and illegal worlds, yet still maintains a clean image,

is someone no one wants to mess with, because any underworld forces they've offended in the past have been taken down.

So now.

Why were there gunshots?

"What happened?" Xun jumped to his feet as if he'd exploded, shouting.

"What's the rush? What could possibly happen? Stay calm, nothing's wrong. I'll go check." Xiang Tiannan stood up calmly.

"Do you have a secret passage here?" Xun asked hastily.

Mainly, Xun genuinely didn't want anything to go wrong.

Xiang Tiannan gave him a look as if he were an idiot. If it weren't for the fact that Xun was the BOSS's confidant, Xiang Tiannan would never have given this fool a second thought.

But now wasn't the time for Xiang Tiannan to worry about that. He stood up and walked towards the door, reaching the corridor.

The smell of blood?

Xiang Tiannan's heart skipped a beat; his composure wavered.

But just then...

Bang!!!

Another gunshot rang out.

No, not just one.

Two gunshots rang out in quick succession.

Accompanying this were the sounds of bodies collapsing one after another.

In Xiang Tiannan's view, the bodyguard who had been following him at the main entrance fell straight to the ground with the sound. He could even see, with his eyesight, the bullet piercing the bodyguard's skull the moment the gunshot rang out, tearing through a large patch of flesh, and then, without losing momentum, grazing past his ear.

A warm sensation came from his ear.

Xiang Tiannan subconsciously touched his ear; blood, crimson blood.

"Ah!!!"

"Murder!"

"Run! Murder!"

"No, don't kill me!"

"Run!"

At this moment, finally, a deafening scream rang out from the doorway.

A piercing scream, and the sound of hurried footsteps.

Xiang Tiannan knew that the hostesses had gone mad with fear, or rather, the first gunshot had left them in a state of mental blank. Now, after the two more gunshots, they could no longer hold on.

They fled like madmen.

Only then did Xiang Tiannan's expression finally change.

He stared at the doorway, his heart pounding. In the dim light, he could even make out a shadowy figure—a young face.

At this moment, the two were only three to five meters apart, almost face to face.

Young, too young.

But Xiang Tiannan was frantically racking his brains, desperately trying to recall his enemy.

He racked his brains, even remembering how many times he'd committed crimes, but he couldn't remember knowing this person.

No.

"Somewhat familiar." Xiang Tiannan's first impression was that this was an enemy he'd offended somewhere in the past.

But as the saying goes, a rabbit doesn't eat the grass near its burrow. Aside from his actions tonight, he rarely did anything illegal or criminal. Tonight's actions were out of necessity, and also, on the other hand, a testament to his absolute confidence.

His actions tonight were absolutely impossible to detect.

"An enemy? Where? Why can't I remember? Who is it?" Xiang Tiannan's mind raced.

Meanwhile, as the two faced each other...

Shang Mingyan, standing next to Jiang Tian, ​​was completely bewildered.

Yes, utterly bewildered.

"Crazy."

"Is Jiang Tian crazy? Without even asking what happened, he just shot and killed three people. The thing is, those people didn't even recognize him, they didn't know who he was, and he just shot them for no reason?"

"And he says this isn't crazy?"

"Insulted, Shang Mingyan was furious. Drawing her gun was just a way of expressing her anger and dissatisfaction with Jiang Tian bringing her here; she even knew he would stop her."

"It wasn't that she really intended to draw her gun; she was just angry and trying to scare Jiang Tian."

"Who the hell brought you here for no reason? And now you've insulted me, making me look like a hostess?"

The result was completely unexpected.

He vented his anger, yes.

But why vent it like this?

Even with Shang Mingyan's extensive experience, seeing Jiang Tian immediately shoot three people dead was utterly overwhelming.

He didn't even know how to express his complex and bewildered feelings.

He felt a flood of words, yet had no idea how to express them.

"You killed someone?"

After a moment of silence, Shang Mingyan asked, bewildered.

Even Shang Mingyan's masterful control of his expressions crumbled at this moment. His trusted young subordinate had turned into a serial killer?

This transformation was too sudden!

"Ah, isn't killing normal?" This was Jiang Tian's confusion.

But in an instant, Jiang Tian suddenly remembered that Shang Mingyan was different from him.

Or rather, Shang Mingyan came from a criminal investigation background. Criminal investigation is about solving cases; it doesn't actually involve direct contact with criminals.

Even when apprehending criminals, criminal investigators rarely get to handle the actual work; they are usually assisted by armed police or special police.

The primary task of criminal investigators is actually to investigate clues at crime scenes, track down the perpetrator, and ultimately apprehend them.

As for Shang Mingyan, he seems to be a seasoned veteran, experienced and knowledgeable, and has indeed handled various cases.

However, the opportunity for him to personally take action is practically zero.

Especially considering he immediately executed someone, especially in a case involving the killing of three people in succession—isn't that the serial killer from so many past cases?

“It’s alright, you’ll get used to it soon.” Jiang Tian didn’t explain anything further.

This was how Jiang Tian solved cases.

Evidence was needed, but not really.

Because when the crime was too heinous, it no longer followed the usual criminal investigation procedures; it was treated as terrorism and subjected to devastating attacks.

Don’t say that under the radar, there are no innocent people; even if there were innocent people connected to the case, they could be killed on the spot.

So even though Shang Mingyan couldn’t accept it, Jiang Tian wasn’t flustered at all, and even felt a little amused.

“Get used to it?” Shang Mingyan was even more confused. How could one get used to killing?

You still say you’re not crazy?

Actually, Jiang Tian was mainly exhausted, mentally exhausted. He hadn’t rested for days, and there were too many things to do, leaving him with no mood to waste time with these criminals.

And at this moment…

Xiang Tiannan's questioning voice finally snapped out of his daze: "Who are you? What kind of hero are you, coming here to kill my men? What did my men do to offend you?"

Shang Mingyan also suddenly realized, in a flash, that's right.

Could this guy be here to settle a personal score?

But why did he bring him along to witness this guy's criminal activities?

"No grudge, no hatred," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"Then you?" Xiang Tiannan asked, somewhat bewildered.

"To rid the people of a scourge."



Chapter 483 Pfft, it's been leaked, the murderer is really here?


"Holy crap!!!"

Xiang Tiannan almost couldn't hold back and cursed.

Putting himself in Jiang Tiannan's shoes, imagine sitting peacefully at home when suddenly a madman appears, indiscriminately shooting everyone around you.

Then he tells you he's ridding the world of a scourge.

Hmm, okay, Xiang Tiannan thought about what he'd done. "Rectifying the world of a scourge" did seem quite fitting, making him even more speechless.

Shang Mingyan was even more on edge.

Luckily, Shang Mingyan's facial expression control was unparalleled; he held his nerve at the crucial moment. Otherwise, he really wanted to crack open Jiang Tian's skull to see what Jiang Tian was up to.

Is this a madman?

Are you a murderer?

A serial killer?

Shang Mingyan reached up and wiped the sweat from his brow. Long hair at his temples was plastered to his skin and near his mouth. Unbeknownst to him, even this seasoned criminal investigator, experienced and seasoned with countless high-profile cases, had been significantly affected mentally.

“Are you crazy?” Shang Mingyan asked in a low voice.

He couldn't pretend he didn't see it. Shang Mingyan now wanted to know what Jiang Tian was trying to do.

A madman?

It didn't seem like it!

Solving a case?

Shang Mingyan was over thirty years old, and this was the first time he'd seen anything like this. He'd never seen a case solved like this before. No analysis, no evidence, just killing the person right off the bat.

No questioning what happened, no regard for whether the victim was the murderer or if they had any connections.

Even if there was a possibility of suspicion, shouldn't you at least ask if the victim would resist before taking action? Who shoots three people for fun right off the bat?

"You've chosen the wrong person to rid the people of this scourge. Everyone in Yunhai knows I, Xiang Tiannan, have never done anything illegal or criminal. 'Rectifying the people' is a joke," Xiang Tiannan scoffed.

He had indeed committed crimes.

But, in this world, it's hard to say. At least in Yunhai, he was absolutely innocent.

Most importantly, there was absolutely no way anyone could trace him back to him.

Xiang Tiannan had carefully considered many things in his mind. Even if Shi Hailiang confessed everything, he still wouldn't be able to trace it back to him.

Because Xiang Tiannan's channels involved contacting and collecting money overseas. That's right, the person collecting the money was the BOSS, and the BOSS would definitely not have any problems.

The delivery channels were also Xiang Tiannan's people overseas.

In other words, his men, in those lawless areas overseas, only appeared when delivering goods and then secretly left the country immediately afterward, making it absolutely impossible for them to be discovered or detected.

Even if those overseas subordinates were arrested within the country, it wouldn't matter much, because they were all orphans raised by the boss from a young age overseas, nurtured for over a decade. None of them were cowards; even if they betrayed him, it wouldn't matter, as they didn't know much.

They had only one belief: to carry out the boss's orders.

As for Xiang Tiannan here, his role, in essence, was to maintain the smooth operation of the transactions and to conduct oversight and management.

In other words, he was a covert surveillance agent.

After the transactions were completed, even with Shi Hailiang's confession, they couldn't find where the goods were stored, nor did they know who their trading partners were.

Even Xiang Tiannan himself didn't know who his trading partners were.

In fact, it was precisely because of this trading model that the boss behind the scenes had such a high degree of control over Xiang Tiannan.

Because Xiang Tiannan's own power was limited to the assets in Yunhai, and most of the profits had to be handed over.

And most of the transactions within the territory were actually coordinated by Xiang Tiannan.

This was the most dangerous step.

But he believed that he hadn't been exposed, at least not now.

Therefore, Xiang Tiannan scoffed at the absurdity.

"If you want to rid the people of evil, there are so many criminals in Yunhai. After sunset, go for a walk on the streets; crime is everywhere. Why are you targeting an innocent, ordinary merchant like me?" Xiang Tiannan said calmly.

Shang Mingyan remained silent.

This was all thanks to Shang Mingyan's incredible composure; otherwise, anyone else would have likely lost their temper.

He remained silent, though his face showed disbelief, and he looked at Jiang Tian with a strange sense of dread, managing only to observe from the sidelines.

Jiang Tian believed that very few people could do that.

Of course, this also revealed Shang Mingyan's abilities and self-cultivation through these small details.

Jiang Tian also developed a certain interest in Shang Mingyan.

A criminal investigation legend.

Sorry, his is a genuine criminal investigation legend.

He just didn't know if Shang Mingyan was just exaggerating.

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

He walked into the room.

"You hid it very well."

Jiang Tian's voice came out calmly as he walked.

"What do you mean?" Xiang Tiannan's face showed a suitable look of confusion.

"You're a good actor. In fact, finding you would be incredibly difficult for most people. But don't worry, I never kill indiscriminately. I only kill those who deserve to die. I've killed so many people, and I've never made a single wrong judgment," Jiang Tian said calmly, his face expressionless.

"Are you crazy? If you're breaking the law, what crime have I committed?" Xiang Tiannan sneered.

A madman.

In Xiang Tiannan's eyes, Jiang Tian was a madman. Damn it, he shot three people right off the bat.

That's right.

That's right, the three blood-soaked corpses truly made Xiang Tiannan blush.

Damn it, is this guy insane?

Xiang Tiannan swore that after this was over, he would find someone to kill this bastard, no, he would kill his entire family.

"You're very angry and want to kill me, right? But you still don't understand my purpose. You're still hesitating, aren't you? Let me help you make the decision," Jiang Tian said, standing face-to-face with Xiang Tiannan.

At this moment,

Shang Mingyan was startled.

Although Xiang Tiannan's acting was good, it was only good. To a professional, especially someone as serious as Shang Mingyan, it was impossible to hide the truth.

Xiang Tiannan's reaction to his subordinates being killed was already somewhat problematic. An ordinary person, witnessing three dead bodies and the murder of three close associates for the first time, would absolutely not be able to speak so calmly.

Jiang Tian's words and Xiang Tiannan's response were also problematic.

At this moment, Shang Mingyan suddenly recalled Jiang Tian's earlier analysis: "The murderer is an arrogant and conceited person. In his eyes, the cases he committed are flawless. Such a murderer would definitely observe the crime scene. Such a perfect crime is an absolute must-see for any arrogant and conceited murderer. Arrogance and conceit are the murderer's only flaw."

Jiang Tian spoke with extreme arrogance back then.

And the sneer on Xiang Tiannan's face was a typical manifestation of arrogance. Why was he arrogant?



Chapter 484 Execution, Solving Another Shocking Case


A normal person, faced with this situation, would only feel panic. Jiang Tian's words would only elicit anger and resentment at being wrongly accused.

But the sneer in this man's eyes

could only mean one thing:

it perfectly matched the mastermind behind the murder.

Buzz!!!

Shang Mingyan's mind went blank.

She stared in horror at Jiang Tian, ​​whose back was turned to her.

Shang Mingyan's heart pounded faster and faster, almost uncontrollably. Even if she didn't want to believe it, she had to accept what she was seeing.

Minister Tan's ten-day deadline for solving the case.

Jiang Tian was going to solve it in ten minutes?

The more she thought about it, the faster Shang Mingyan's heart raced.

The more she thought about it, the more it felt like a dream. She herself was a legend in the world of criminal investigation, having seen all sorts of bizarre cases and all sorts of investigative logic, but she had never seen anything so bizarre.

Sure enough, what happened next perfectly confirmed her suspicions.

“Xiang Tiannan, you've hidden yourself very well, almost committing the perfect crime. However, the net of justice is vast, and though its meshes are wide, nothing escapes it. You did commit the perfect crime, but didn't you consider that the person in your house has already been exposed?” Jiang Tian said with a calm smile.

This nonchalant smile froze Xiang Tiannan's expression, and he visibly panicked.

Yes, Xiang Tiannan committed the perfect crime.

But what Ah Xun did, Xiang Tiannan didn't know.

Or rather...

Ah Xun, exposed?

Or...

The assassin Ah Xun hired, the boss no longer trusts him?

Xiang Tiannan's mind was in turmoil.

“I don't understand what you're saying,” Xiang Tiannan vehemently denied, but his hand had already swiftly drawn his gun from his back.

In this situation...

Regardless of the reason, regardless of what happened, whether it was related to Ah Xun, whether they were enemies, or whether there was some other purpose...

Xiang Tiannan would never sit idly by and wait to die. He had built his empire from scratch over the years, and it wasn't solely due to the BOSS.

Without his resources, the BOSS wouldn't have even noticed him.

At this point, Xiang Tiannan's face hardened.

"Watch out!" Shang Mingyan, watching from behind, felt her heart leap into her throat at the sudden change, her delicate face instantly contorting.

But before she could warn him, Xiang Tiannan had already drawn his gun.

His expression was filled with murderous intent.

"Your mistake was talking too much. Die." Xiang Tiannan wasn't one to waste words.

The instant he drew his gun, he aimed at Jiang Tian.

His action was swift and decisive.

Because at this moment, Jiang Tian didn't have a gun.

In fact, for Jiang Tian, ​​if it weren't for the dazzling red aura emanating from Xiang Tiannan, he wouldn't have been babbling on and on.

Such a serious crime, such a major criminal.

He was involved in countless shocking and major crimes; killing him would be far less valuable than keeping him alive.

If he were dead, his deterrent effect would be insufficient, and many cases couldn't be decided by death.

But if he were alive, then his existence would be terrifyingly valuable.

If the special merit award for Shimiao Village still had some uncertainty, then Xiang Tiannan alone was absolutely enough; this case was absolutely sufficient.

The second special merit award.

Jiang Tian was eagerly anticipating it, and the difficulty of obtaining a Special Class Merit was immense. The first one was already an exception, and the second might be even more difficult.

However, with Xiang Tiannan involved, Jiang Tian was certain of absolute success.

For this reason, Jiang Tian even slowly holstered his gun as he spoke.

The two people in the room were of increasingly higher value.

In fact, Jiang Tian had shot three people upon entering because those three possessed a dazzling red aura, but compared to the people in the room, they were insignificant.

Keeping them alive would be troublesome, since Jiang Tian was alone.

And there was Shang Mingyan, this burden.

Having eliminated some insignificant henchmen, the two in the room possessed terrifyingly abnormal red auras.

Jiang Tian even seriously suspected that each of these two, individually, was worth over ten thousand sin points.

After upgrading his "Legendary Detective" gold attribute, Jiang Tian is now desperately short of crime points.

"Although your move was decisive, unfortunately, your opponent is me."

*Crack!!!*

Before Jiang Tian finished speaking,

a terrifying sound of bone breaking rang out.

Extreme pain.

Within three steps of Jiang Tian,

it's safe to say that no one can be a match for him, regardless of the weapon.

Moreover, with them so close, drawing a gun in front of Jiang Tian is practically suicide.

But Shang Mingyan sees things differently.

In her view, Jiang Tian placed his gun at his waist, his confidence so overwhelming that Shang Mingyan couldn't understand it at all. Then, Xiang Tiannan quickly pointed the gun at Jiang Tian's head, which terrified Shang Mingyan even more.

But the next moment...

She hadn't even seen how Jiang Tian made his move.

Almost in the blink of an eye, Jiang Tian's hand had already gripped Xiang Tiannan's burly wrist.

Without even a hint of force.

A terrifying cracking sound rang out; yes, the bone broke.

The entire wrist was snapped.

Then, with a clatter, the pistol fell to the ground.

"Hiss!!!"

The excruciating pain, this terrifying pain, almost made Xiang Tiannan scream out loud, only able to alleviate it by constantly gasping for breath.

Thump!!!

The next moment, Jiang Tian mercilessly kicked Xiang Tiannan in the stomach.

The terrifying pain, almost the instant Jiang Tian released his grip, caused Xiang Tiannan to collapse to the ground like a shrimp, convulsing uncontrollably.

Pain!!!

Jiang Tian didn't even glance at the crippled Xiang Tiannan; his gaze turned to the room. Compared to Xiang Tiannan, the person inside actually intrigued Jiang Tian.

In the past...

When Jiang Tian wiped out Tucha, Tucha's crime value abroad was only around ten or twenty thousand.

These two people, either one of them is comparable to Tucha.

Two people with almost the same crime value, then there is no leader.

In other words.

This case is far beyond imagination.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to turn around and walk in, he suddenly stopped: "I must say, your willpower is impressive. To be able to overcome such terrifying physical pain and still grit your teeth and continue fighting."

*Crack!*

Jiang Tian turned and stomped on Xiang Tiannan's other hand.

Putting everything else aside, Xiang Tiannan's willpower was incredible. One hand was crippled, then Jiang Tian kicked it, and yet Xiang Tiannan was still crawling on the ground, trying to pick up his gun.

"Who...who are you?" Lying on the ground, Xiang Tiannan was completely useless, both hands were useless, but he was unwilling to accept it.

He had built his life from scratch and was unwilling to accept this.

His eyes were bloodshot as he stared at Jiang Tian.

He had spent most of his life struggling, finally reaching this point, only to be inexplicably destroyed?

He didn't even know who had done it to him.

Who was this person?

He was in his prime, his fighting ability was definitely not bad, yet he was so easily crippled?

"Yunhai Narcotics Control Team, Jiang Tian," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"Jiang Tian?"

Xiang Tiannan's pupils contracted.



Chapter 485 A shocking crime method, solved.


"Looks like you've heard of me, but you should know that no matter how you resist, it's futile." Jiang Tian glanced at him and then walked into the house.

At this moment.

Inside the house was another criminal, no less formidable than Xiang Tiannan.

Especially Jiang Tian's Judgment Eye.

Upon seeing Xiang Tiannan, Jiang Tian could already see the man's recent crimes.

[Recent intelligence: A batch of heroin worth 40 million RMB was purchased from Shimiao Village a few days ago. It's currently stored in the Dongri warehouse and will be shipped out in one day.]

40 million RMB worth of heroin.

What kind of quantity is that?

At least three or four thousand kilograms! Good heavens, that's incredible!

Jiang Tian gasped.

Nearly four thousand kilograms of heroin!

What kind of haul is this?

If there's one regret about Shimiao Village, it's that they couldn't find the village's account books even after searching the entire village thoroughly.

That's probably the biggest regret.

Although Jiang Tian's contribution was already substantial, and the capture of Xiang Tiannan made his second Special Merit Award a foregone conclusion without any surprises,

if the account books of Shimiao Village could be accessed, given the village's over ten years of continuous sales, even if the heroin had already been sold, the buyers and subsequent purchasers would still be unknown.

While much might be difficult to verify, given the passage of time, it would definitely be possible to uncover the truth.

But the most important thing, however, lies in what's recorded in the ledger.

In other words, the ledger records how much heroin was seized and how much was sold. Once verified, this can all be recorded as part of his achievements.

For an anti-drug officer, what is the greatest honor? Is it a medal?

No, no, no.

The most important thing is how much heroin was seized and how many criminals were apprehended during their career.

Without the ledger, the achievements would be sufficient, but for Jiang Tian, ​​not being able to record them in his achievements, not being able to record them in his career, would be a regret.

In fact, so far, besides the ledger found earlier, plus the heroin seized, the amount of heroin recorded on Jiang Tian during this period has already exceeded ten thousand kilograms.

You read that right.

Adding the batch of heroin found in Shimiao Village, in terms of Jiang Tian's achievements alone, what's recorded can already be considered a case solved.

Ten thousand kilograms—what a terrifying concept.

It's safe to say that in a year, the total amount of heroin seized nationwide would probably be less than 100,000 kilograms.

Jiang Tian alone had already seized over 10,000 kilograms.

And now, another three or four thousand kilograms.

For anti-drug police, heroin is an undeniable and glaring fact, a contribution no one can deny.

Plus, none of these criminals escaped, so this success, along with the assets of Shimiao Village, is practically a done deal.

"They're here."

Ah Xun hid around the corner, gun in hand, taking deep breaths to calm himself down.

It must be said that compared to Xiang Tiannan, Ah Xun's mentality and resilience were ultimately somewhat lacking.

At this moment, terrifying pressure swept over him. He had already cursed Xiang Tiannan a thousand times in his mind. Not only had he been caught courting death, but he had also been dragged down with him.

*Thud!*

Ah Xun pressed himself against the wall, enduring the terrifying pressure.

Holding his pistol, he took deep breaths and wiped away cold sweat.

Until a drop of sweat fell to the ground, Ah Xun instinctively looked down, but in that instant, a shadow flashed before him.

The next moment, Ah Xun lay unconscious on the ground.

Before losing consciousness, his vision was blurry; he saw a young face, its features indistinct, squatting in front of him.

The next moment, all consciousness completely vanished.

Until Ah Xun opened his eyes.

The large villa he had always been accustomed to was gone.

Trying to turn over, he felt aches and pains in his back and waist, and his whole body felt restrained, a tingling and numb sensation rising within him.

Ah Xun looked up, his expression one of shock, but only for a moment. Staring at the blinding light, his head spun.

The handcuffs rattled loudly.

Arrested?

He was destined for endless prison.

The villa he'd lived in all his life, gone?

His carefree life, surrounded by beautiful women, gone forever?

He was going to spend the rest of his life in prison. No, that was probably the best outcome. Compared to prison, a peanut was probably his final fate.

The more he thought about it,

Ah Xun's heart grew colder.

When he finally saw the dim surroundings, and in the faint, dim light, he saw two police officers sitting opposite him, silently watching him. They didn't urge him, and he didn't know how long they had been waiting.

A man and a woman stood nearby. Seeing him regain consciousness, the male police officer wore a triumphant expression, saying, "Looks like I won this time. I told you, he'd wake up here in half an hour."

Compared to Jiang Tian's smile, the woman beside him left Ah Xun utterly stunned.

Indeed.

Ah Xun had seen a lot, both domestically and internationally, but he had never seen anyone as beautiful as that woman.

It wasn't that she was conventionally beautiful, but her cold, aloof demeanor combined with her striking features was breathtaking.

A greedy glint flashed in his eyes; for a fleeting moment, Ah Xun wanted to possess this woman at all costs, but he quickly regained his composure.

However, it was clear that both Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan could see right through Ah Xun.

This caused a hint of disgust on Shang Mingyan's face.

"Why did you arrest me?" Ah Xun feigned confusion.

His attempt to bluff his way through was utterly laughable, whether in front of Shang Mingyan or Jiang Tian.

"Hong Xun, born in Yunhai in 1975, is twenty-eight years old this year. His father, Hong Shiwu, made his fortune through smuggling in his early years and immigrated to Windmill Country fifteen years ago," Jiang Tian said calmly, holding the documents in his hand.

"That's right, I'm a Windmill person, not under your jurisdiction," Xun suddenly exclaimed excitedly, as if he had just remembered something.

Jiang Tian sneered, "You think you can escape legal punishment just because you immigrated? Sorry, according to the law, regardless of nationality, anyone who commits a crime on this land has priority in trial, and foreign countries have no right to request extradition."

It has to be said, the "Windmill Country" is absolutely a paradise for criminals.

Take Hong Kong for example. So many gangsters and thugs back then, but where did they go after returning home?

Some were wiped out.

Others, the powerful and wealthy tycoons, simply immigrated to the Windmill Country and settled down there to retire.

Hong Xun's father did just that; after making his fortune early on, he immigrated.

As for the follow-up...

[Recent intelligence: Hong Xun, through a dark web trading platform with the domain name "FishBuy.com," completed a drug deal worth 40 million RMB seven days ago, involving a female drug dealer, and is about to transfer the drug to Tian Nan.]

Good, good, good.

This case is getting more and more terrifying; the water is getting deeper and deeper.

In this day and age, cracking such a crime is virtually impossible.



486 Octopus Shopping Network, its defenses have been breached, they even managed to find this.


Jiang Tian was slightly surprised.

The dark web—this was Jiang Tian's first encounter with it, but it was certainly not unfamiliar.

It was a platform for online transactions that had rapidly gained popularity in recent years, rife with illegal and criminal activities. Within the dark web, there was virtually no light or oversight; various illegal transactions could be conducted with impunity.

These transactions could be conducted openly, but it was extremely difficult to trace the forces behind them; everything was elusive.

Especially since the dark web was a relatively new feature, even the country's response strategy initially caught off guard, lacking professional means of containment.

The internet had only existed for a few years; in fact, let alone the dark web, even direct sales through internet communications were difficult to detect.

In other words, the investigative department's technology needed updating.

"I don't know what crime I've committed. I don't know what Xiang Tiannan did. He just invited me over because he knows Jia Fu. Even though I smuggled myself in, there's no need for such a fuss, is there?" Hong Xun continued to feign ignorance.

Even though Shang Mingyan saw through Hong Xun's feigned ignorance, his brows furrowed deeply.

What truly baffled Shang Mingyan now was Jiang Tian's methods. How did he uncover the other party's illegal activities, and how could he pinpoint the location so precisely?

Could it be that Jiang Tian had already tracked down the information?

But that couldn't be!

With so many serial cases lately, and Jiang Tian investigating the Shimiao Village case, how could he have time to connect them to this group? No matter how you looked at it, the two sides seemed unrelated.

But just as Shang Mingyan's brows furrowed,

Jiang Tian spoke up: "In the Dongri warehouse, there are 40 million worth of heroin stored. Do you admit to this?"

Buzz!!!

What did you say?

40 million?

That's several thousand kilograms of heroin?

Actually, the so-called 40 million was the market price; the purchase price was clearly less than 10 million.

It is precisely because of such exorbitant profits that people constantly risk terrible dangers to reap such huge gains.

Shang Mingyan's breathing quickened.

He stared at Jiang Tian, ​​taking deep breaths. Forty million!

At least, that quantity must be close to four thousand kilograms. A single operation to crack four thousand kilograms of heroin?

Whoosh!!!

Shang Mingyan's eyes widened.

No, wait, are all the cases in Yunhai this thrilling?

That's why, as a legendary figure in criminal investigation, and at headquarters no less, anyone who knows Shang Mingyan would know that his legendary life is no exaggeration. He first made a name for himself in Beijing.

Then he cracked several major cases in Beijing, one of which was a heinous crime that shocked the entire nation. Based on these achievements, he flew directly to headquarters.
