At just over twenty years old, he was already serving as an assistant in the Criminal Investigation Bureau.

Following this, various major cases were solved across the country.

Thus, Shang Mingyan's name gradually became known throughout the nation.

During this period, Shang Mingyan also handled various major cases, especially heroin cases. He made outstanding contributions in many joint enforcement operations that led to their cracking.

But precisely because of this, Shang Mingyan knew that in such cases, the normal seizure quantity—30-50 kilograms in a large-scale drug trafficking case—was already considered a major case.

At most, 100-200 kilograms—that was truly remarkable, a collective second-class merit award was guaranteed.

If it was 300-500 kilograms, and an individual made an outstanding contribution, even suffering some injury, it would be enough for a first-class merit award.

But how much did this stuff amount to?

In Shimiao Village alone, was the seizure 1,000 or 2,000 kilograms?

This time it's even more outrageous and absurd. Jiang Tian casually opened his mouth, and good heavens, 4,000 kilograms?

Are you sure you didn't misread it?

Are all the cases in Yunhai this outrageous? Are all the cases this high-level all the time?

Shang Mingyan has seen the world.

But with each major case, he felt that the frequency of such cases in Yunhai seemed to exceed the combined total of the entire country.

In reality, Shang Mingyan was mistaken.

The primary reason for Yunhai's situation was the decades-long problem of oversight.

Another reason was that Yunhai was located near a lawless zone, attracting undocumented immigrants who acted with impunity, lacking basic legal knowledge and capable of anything.

This, combined with the growing autonomy of criminal gangs over the years, contributed to the situation.

This has led to the current lawless behavior of criminals in Yunhai, who are now committing major crimes.

Shang Mingyan was shocked.

Ah Xun was completely dumbfounded.

He's gone mad.

Who said that?

Who exactly said that?

Ah Xun tried to stand up, but with his hands restrained, he couldn't. His shocked eyes couldn't hide his disbelief.

A look of utter bewilderment perfectly captured his expression.

He confessed?

That's right.

The Dongri Warehouse was located in an extremely secluded place, guarded entirely by loyal soldiers. There was absolutely no way anything could have gone wrong.

In other words.

How was it discovered?

That is...

He confessed. Who confessed? Who else could it be?

Very few people knew about the Dongri Warehouse. Besides the loyal soldiers, only he and Xiang Tiannan knew.

He couldn't have said it himself, so it must have been Xiang Tiannan who leaked it.

But Xiang Tiannan must be crazy.

Does he think he can gain merit by confessing these things?

He's utterly stupid, a complete idiot. Confessing only adds to his own crimes, what else can he do?

Hong Xun's expression confirmed Jiang Tian's words, which further shocked Shang Mingyan.

The case is solved.

It's confirmed.

That's right.

Damn it.

Jiang Tian wasn't joking; this was real.

This wasn't just a bluff during interrogation.

"What Dongri Warehouse? Whose warehouse? Xiang Tiannan's? Sorry, I don't know anything about these things. As for Dongri Warehouse, I've never heard of it before. What kind of nonsense is this? Officer, can you let me go?" Ah Xun calmly denied everything.

"Admitting it would be the end of you."

"Yes, the goods worth forty million in the Dongri warehouse, you might only be covering it up, it's not really your fault. A mere charge of concealing something might only get you ten or eight years out of prison. But, the FishBuy website on the dark web, are you still going to deny it?"

Buzz!!!

The dark web is a deep pool.

Jiang Tian knew that some cases, even in later generations, remain unsolved, and not only unsolved, but transactions continue to occur normally on the dark web.

These often require international cooperation to solve.

Especially in this era, if it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​this group would have been indiscriminately killing people.

It's just a pity.

Indiscriminate killing, but they encountered Jiang Tian.

So!!!

They went mad.

Ah Xun broke down.

He could handle everything else, but this kind of transaction method, even Xiang Tiannan kept it a secret.



Chapter 487 A business genius a decade ahead of his time, actually did this?


"Fishbuy.com? The dark web?"

Not to mention Hong Xun, even Shang Mingyan jumped to his feet, staring at Jiang Tian in utter astonishment.

Shang Mingyan had naturally encountered cases involving the dark web, especially assassination cases. Many missions were issued through the dark web, and payments were settled directly there.

Of course, virtual currencies hadn't taken off yet, but they still had their own clandestine payment processes, just a bit more difficult than the virtual currency transactions of later generations.

Covered investigations were incredibly difficult. Almost all the dark web cases Shang Mingyan had encountered were unsolved, extremely difficult to investigate.

In this era, aside from those across the Atlantic, Shang Mingyan had heard that there had been some efforts to target dark web crimes, with some success, but investigating dark web cases remained fraught with difficulty.

Because the dark web is untraceable, it's very difficult to investigate.

This person actually used the dark web.

Oh no.

That means...

"FishGo.com, if I'm not mistaken, was founded by your father. Over the past decade, it has accumulated a large number of international clients, including but not limited to domestic clients; there are similar operations overseas," Jiang Tian said with a sigh.

Actually, he hadn't expected this unexpected surprise to come so suddenly.

This was an absolutely major case.

And a case beyond imagination.

Simply put,

FishGo.com was a transaction-based criminal platform. It allowed for shipping through the founder and also provided a platform for direct transactions.

In short, if Jiang Tian remembered correctly, he had seen this case in his later life.

FishGo.com's existence was discovered ten years later during an accidental case on the other side of the Atlantic. As the investigation deepened, it was revealed that FishGo.com was actually a global drug trafficking platform.

Of course, it hasn't developed to the same terrifying scale as it has over a decade later.

But this model...

was absolutely groundbreaking in the original Taobao shop. When Jiang Tian first saw this case, he exclaimed in astonishment.

The existence of FishBuy.com was simply outrageous.

Their business acumen...

It was more than a decade ahead of Taobao!

Unfortunately, they didn't use it for good.

In short, when it was uncovered, more than ten years later, the seized criminal funds reached nearly 10 billion US dollars.

Of course, that was more than ten years later; it's not on that outrageous scale now, but FishBuy.com has undoubtedly grown significantly.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.

What were the previous cases compared to this?

That's right.

Compared to this case, the previous cases were like children playing house. This was a pure, transnational, major terrorist case!

Moreover, it was a major case that, once exposed, could shock the world.

If handled well...

Global fame.

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't particularly value fame. Having been reborn, Jiang Tian only pursued three things: money, power, and social responsibility.

We must not compromise ourselves, but we must also absolutely safeguard social stability and peace.

Especially since this incident could significantly improve Yunhai's reputation and standing nationwide, which is crucial for its future development. Furthermore, it would deter foreign criminals from acting with impunity in Yunhai.

The impact would be enormous.

Of course, the most important aspect is the deterrent effect it would send to the nation globally.

This would be the first case ever cracked, involving the largest scale, the widest scope, and the most egregious nature of the crime.

And it would bring billions of US dollars in foreign exchange to the nation.

“No, what is Yugou.com?” Hong Xun retorted, but his expression was already betraying him.

“What is Yugou.com? We’ll know once we arrest your father, the founder, in Windmill Country.” Jiang Tian stood up and stood before Hong Xun.

Looking down at him, Jiang Tian exuded immense pressure.

Unconsciously, Hong Xun's psychological defenses were on the verge of collapse. Jiang Tian knew it was just one step away.

"If you atone for your crime by performing good deeds, you can be considered an accomplice. If you confess and demonstrate significant merit, you can become a witness against the criminal record, and you could even get a suspended death sentence or life imprisonment. If you behave well in prison, you can get out in a little over ten years. But if you remain stubborn, and we have concrete evidence against you, you should understand that I have an undercover agent with your father. I'm giving you a way out now. Whether you talk or not won't make much difference. Are you still going to remain stubborn?"

Jiang Tian's last sentence was a sudden, harsh rebuke.

This caused Hong Xun, whose will was already wavering, to instantly break down: "I'll talk, I'll talk, I'll confess!"

Hong Xun was a spoiled young master, pampered since childhood. Jiang Tian's presence was suffocating, and with so much Jiang Tian was saying, how could he possibly know so much without an inside man?

Naturally, his mental defenses completely crumbled.

Ten years in prison and out, a hero again, versus facing execution—even a fool would choose the latter.

The reason these drug lords were so reckless and brutal, resorting to suicide attacks when resisting arrest, was because they had no way out and no way back. Now, Jiang Tian had provided that way out.

In fact, Jiang Tian wasn't lying.

This case was so huge.

Most importantly, its influence and the rewards were so immense.

Jiang Tian could only rely on recent intelligence to obtain a limited amount of crucial information, and he revealed everything he knew.

Hong Xun's confession was detailed enough, and the rewards were substantial enough that, while it might not secure Hong Xun's release for over ten years, there was a real chance he could be released in twenty.

Meanwhile.

Outside the small, dark room.

Minister Tan rushed over in a panic.

Behind Minister Tan was Gao Xiaolong.

Thrilling.

Absolutely thrilling.

Damn it.

The task had just been assigned, with a ten-day deadline. Half an hour had passed, and Minister Tan, after issuing the order, went back to sleep, temporarily resting at a guesthouse not far from the city bureau.

He had just gotten back and was halfway through his shower when the phone rang.

It was Shang Mingyan calling.

Shang Mingyan remained silent for a long time before quietly uttering a few words: "The case is solved. The attacker has been apprehended, and the mastermind behind it has been caught."

Minister Tan immediately exploded, demanding more details. In truth, Shang Mingyan was also quite confused.

It was solved so easily. The key point was that, having witnessed the entire process, she had no idea how Jiang Tian had solved the case in such an unscientific way.

Immediately afterward, officers were dispatched to the scene to control the situation.

Coincidentally, it was someone Jiang Tian knew who went—Song Liguo.

Upon arriving, Song Liguo was somewhat bewildered by the three corpses. Jiang Tian then arranged for a seriously injured person to be taken away, placed under strict guard, and then sent to the hospital for treatment.

As for Ah Xun, Jiang Tian knocked him unconscious and took him away on the spot.

When Jiang Tian returned, Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong were already anxiously waiting in their upstairs office.



Chapter 488 Old Tan is dumbfounded, Old Tan questions, Xiao Jiang gives his acceptance speech.


From any perspective, this case is shockingly outrageous.

The speed at which the case was solved was indeed fast, but it's simply absurd!

And such a major case at that.

Before solving it, there was a flurry of analysis, with experts from various fields discussing it for two hours. And then, in the time it takes to drink a cup of tea after the meeting, the case was solved so easily?

It all seems like a dream.

Therefore, Minister Tan couldn't sit still any longer and personally led Chief Gao to the crime scene, YH Bar. After understanding the details, they rushed over. Meanwhile, Jiang Tian had already returned to the Municipal Police Bureau with Shang Mingyan and A Xun.

Chief Gao only found Song Liguo standing at the scene.

So, as Minister Tan pushed open the door, Jiang Tian was still on the phone giving instructions: "Chief Song, the Dongri warehouse has several thousand kilograms of heroin. Go there immediately, and bring plenty of manpower."

There weren't many guards there, and Hong Xun's first instructions were also directed to the Dongri warehouse. Jiang Tian knew, but only that the Dongri warehouse stored heroin worth forty million.

After Hong Xun's instructions, it was finally confirmed.

"This batch of goods weighs about four thousand kilograms. The delivery is scheduled for 8 PM tomorrow night. However, the specific delivery plan is directed to Tiannan. I don't know much about it. The buyer is a drug lord from the largest drug trafficking group in the neighboring Hanxi Province, and the seller we're buying from is Shimiao Village."

It's a second-hand platform, basically just making a delivery fee.

However, the profit from this delivery fee is terrifyingly outrageous.

"The total purchase price was around ten million, but the selling price is about thirty-five million. I don't know how much they'll sell it for. Dongri's warehouse is located in a large warehouse at No. 108, South District Commercial City. Previously, all domestic business was transshipped through there."

Good, good, good.

Jiang Tian was shocked.

Numb.

Like being struck by lightning.

Good heavens, this operating model... Jiang Tian suddenly realized, isn't this just JD.com's warehousing system from later generations?

Damn it, he thought it was just a quick search and it was amazing, but unexpectedly, they've already evolved into a high-end local warehousing model?

Talent.

This is absolutely the most terrifying top-tier business talent.

But unfortunately, such talent has used this business acumen on the path of illegality and crime.

Two trillion-level ideas, and they've used them to build a drug trafficking network.

Jiang Tian didn't know whether to laugh or cry, but suddenly it dawned on him why this platform was thriving. Because of its business model—who could compete?

"We have three similar warehouses in China, but they're rarely used. They're only activated when we receive large orders; otherwise, they're mainly for risk management."

These goods are like a hot potato—holding them is like handling a time bomb. It's terrifying to even think about, because if you don't sell them, there's no profit.

Not selling them means that every fifty grams could be exchanged for a bullet aimed at your head.

Jiang Tian listened and nodded, then used his phone to instruct Song Liguo, who was maintaining order at the bar, to act quickly, seize the Dongri warehouse, and ensure no one escaped, lest the major drug lord of Hanxi Province become aware of the situation.

Previously, Jiang Tian might have been tempted, but now, he was only interested in the dark web platform.

So, when Minister Tan pushed open the door,

Jiang Tian was still communicating with Song Liguo: "The Dongri warehouse has approximately 4,000 kilograms of goods, with a market value of 40 million."

40 million was just a base price, a second-hand price.

However, in the hands of addicts, it wouldn't be a second-hand price, but a third or fourth-hand price.

From 40 million, double that, meaning 4,000 kilograms could potentially sell for over 100 million. 10 million to make over 100 million—that's a concept.

Moreover, the cost of producing this 10 million worth of goods in Shimiao Village would probably only be around 4 or 5 million.

This alone shows just how exorbitant the profits were.

Jiang Tian then gave the detailed location.

“Aren’t you coming to join the operation?” Sweat beaded on Jiang Tian’s forehead, given the scale of the case.

He had never handled a case this big in his life.

However, in Jiang Tian’s view, the case wasn’t particularly dangerous. To avoid suspicion, there were only two or three security guards.

If Jiang Tian were free, he would naturally go and participate, then plan the arrest of the drug lord in Hanxi Province.

But at this critical moment, Jiang Tian didn’t even have time to sleep; he didn’t have time to make a special trip.

“There aren’t many security personnel. Tell the brothers to ensure their own safety. Be careful, nothing will happen,” Jiang Tian said.

Then Jiang Tian hung up the phone.

He turned around.

He saw Minister Tan standing in the open doorway, staring at him in disbelief.

His eyes were utterly vacant.

There was even a hint of confusion.

Yes, it was that kind of dazed, confused state, a loss of the ability to think.

“Minister Tan?” Jiang Tian waved his hand.

Minister Tan, also in a daze of self-doubt, suddenly snapped back to reality.

“What did you just say?” Minister Tan thought he had misheard.

“An unexpected windfall. A drug lord in Yunhai has a long-term cooperative relationship with Shimiao Village. Fearing a leak, he took the risk. I followed the trail and found them. There happened to be about four thousand kilograms of heroin, just traded from Shimiao Village, stored in the Dongri warehouse.” Jiang Tian spoke briefly.

But this simple sentence almost made Minister Tan stumble.

Four thousand kilograms.

That's four thousand kilograms!

Eight thousand jin (approximately 4000 kg), a whole eight thousand jin! Are you trying to solve all the drug crimes cracked across the country in just one case?

That's right.

Nationwide, over several months, the total amount of drugs seized in drug-related crimes cracked would probably only amount to tens of thousands of kilograms.

Jiang Tian's case, solved in less than half an hour, yielded 4,000 kilograms.

Oh no, Jiang Tian himself had already confirmed seizures of 10,000 to 20,000 kilograms.

"We just seized two thousand kilograms of heroin from the drug village, and then, while investigating an attack, you managed to uncover four thousand kilograms. Since when has seizing heroin in drug trafficking become so simple?"

"You know, in the anti-drug profession, the most reliable measure of merit is the quantity of heroin seized."

"Are you sure you're not mistaken?" Minister Tan's first reaction was that there was a mistake.

"No, the suspect just confessed, it's indeed around four thousand kilograms, which can probably be resold for forty million." Jiang Tian said.

Buzz!!!



Chapter 489 Emergency Breakthrough Interrogation: A Horrific Case Fully Revealed


"Damn it!!!"

I asked you to investigate a major attack case, and you casually uncovered about four thousand kilograms of heroin?

What kind of achievement is that?

This achievement alone is worthy of a first-class merit.

Just consider how much heroin Jiang Tian has seized. Even without calculating, the amount Minister Tan knew about was already over ten thousand kilograms. Adding this four thousand kilograms, that's well over twenty thousand kilograms!

Minister Tan couldn't help but feel a headache coming on.

Of course, there's an even more crucial issue now.

After speaking, Hong Xun seemed to have completely come to terms with it. Jiang Tian offered him a cigarette, which Hong Xun took a drag with a look of enjoyment.

Then, as if he had come to terms with everything, or perhaps resigned himself to his fate, he slumped into his chair and looked at Minister Tan who had entered, saying, "Sir, because Xiang Tiannan—oh, the Xiang Tiannan who was injured by Officer Jiang and is currently receiving treatment in the hospital—has a direct connection to Shimiao Village, this attack was launched. This shipment is to be delivered to the biggest drug lord in Hanxi Province tomorrow."

Wait.

There's another shipment?

There's even an expert involved?

Minister Tan couldn't help but gasp. Admitting it was one thing, but the murderer was right in front of him. Minister Tan felt like he was dreaming.

Especially since Hanxi was involved too.

This...

This was an unexpected windfall. If it hadn't been leaked, with proper surveillance, they could have wiped them out in one fell swoop!

But then Minister Tan remembered the crucial point.

"Wait, what's your relationship with that murderer, Xiang Tiannan?" Minister Tan suddenly grasped the key point.

That's right!

From beginning to end, it was always the injured Xiang Tiannan. How could Jiang Tian know so much about this person he arrested? What's their relationship?

“My father sent me,” Hong Xun said.

“Your father?” Minister Tan was bewildered.

No.

Who is your father?

This case…

How is it getting more and more complicated?

Minister Tan hasn't even figured out Xiang Tiannan yet, and what's your father's relationship?

Isn't this just Xiang Tiannan being a middleman, getting goods cheaply from the factory, Shimiao Village? These goods are not only cheap and good, but also of extremely high quality, then reselling them at several times the profit to earn the middleman's gains, right?

Then who is the third party, this person in front of me?

Hong Xun didn't keep him in suspense: “Xiang Tiannan is my father's subordinate. My father doesn't trust Xiang Tiannan, so he sent me to supervise him.”

Hiss!!!

Minister Tan gasped, "Who is your father?"

Good heavens, this is incredible! The mastermind behind the attack, and the 4,000 kilograms of drugs—this is practically a top-tier drug trafficking case—wasn't the ultimate culprit.

There's someone else behind this?

"That's right, my father is the founder of Yugou.com," Hong Xun explained.

"Yugou.com?" Minister Tan frowned deeply.

Actually, right now, the regulation of illegal activities on the dark web in China is, well, practically nonexistent.

In reality, in this era, countless illegal and criminal activities are already being conducted through transactions on the dark web.

Unlike ten or twenty years later, when the dark web was already infiltrated by spies—who appeared and disappeared without a trace, but if you leaked even a little information about yourself, someone would immediately come to your aid.

Now, it's not that the higher-ups don't care, it's just that the technology isn't there.

Not only domestically, but even the major power across the Atlantic is the same, but all they can do is disguise themselves as traders to lure people to offline meetings and then arrest them.

"For over ten years, the dark web trading platform, Fishbuy.com, has been conducting large-scale heroin transactions globally. And because of its extreme secrecy, coupled with the fact that most of its partners are powerful drug lords, it has remained undetected. Fishbuy.com initially operated through direct transactions between buyers and sellers, with the platform taking a service fee. Later, we took over warehousing and offline operations," Hong Xun explained.

Minister Tan: "???"

This trading model was completely new to Minister Tan.

Even Shang Mingyan had never seen this trading route before.

Online transactions.

Offline transportation.

It was easily achieved—ordering from home and having it delivered to your door.

This model, though unprecedented, was already imaginable. Just how wildly popular was it?

"How much are these transactions?" Minister Tan's voice trembled slightly.

Damn it.

He'd never seen anything like it either!

It wasn't that he'd never dealt with the dark web before; in fact, he'd seen transactions there, but this kind of platform taking a cut was a first, even having its own warehousing.

Is this something a human would do?

With your talent, why did you choose to run this kind of criminal platform?

"Last year, the total transaction volume was around two billion US dollars, our platform's total profit was around two hundred million, and our self-operated offline profit was about three hundred million," Hong Xun explained.

Buzz!!!

Everyone present instantly paled, followed by gasps of shock.

Insane! The sky has fallen.

Oh no.

It should be said...

An unprecedented international case.

This far surpasses the terrifying impact of major domestic cases.

Minister Tan initially thought it was some small platform, but upon hearing the transaction amount, he had to admit he was stunned.

Five hundred million US dollars in net profit a year—what terrifying profits!

That's five hundred million in pure profit!

Jiang Tian wasn't surprised at all, because he had seen the case files; it was a globally shocking case, even attracting significant attention and influence domestically, all because the owners were overseas immigrants.

The roots were still in China.

Years later, it was discovered that this father and son had amassed a fortune of tens of billions of US dollars—if it hadn't been for the coincidence of the discovery.

Most importantly, the father and son were brought down by money laundering. After the investigation of FishBuy.com on the other side of the Atlantic, the father and son were found to have laundered a large sum of money.

The crucial point is that the organization had an undercover agent on the other side of the Atlantic, and then their information was leaked.

After the arrest, they happily confiscated tens of billions of US dollars.

Although the timeline was a few years earlier, even if the amount wasn't tens of billions...

So, there must be billions of US dollars involved, right?

This achievement...

Is a special-class merit award too much to ask for?

Jiang Tian suddenly realized something: a special-class merit award might not actually be enough.

“Minister Tan, this transaction model is roughly the same as the Taobao app that just launched this year,” Jiang Tian explained.

Taobao.

It was launched in 2003, that is, this year.

Although it became popular immediately after its launch, it also had limitations and wasn't fully popularized. The newly launched Taobao didn't have much influence, and its brand awareness was low.



Chapter 490 A Global Milestone: A Great Glory That Will Be Remembered


Thinking of this, Jiang Tian suddenly realized something.

He should remind Zhao Qiuni when he had time. Given her status, he could mortgage his own commercial building to secure a loan and invest in her investments.

Although Jiang Tian didn't need much money, he couldn't be without it.

"Taobao, I know it. I just bought something from Taobao, they deliver to your door," Shang Mingyan interjected.

"Good business sense, just misplaced," Jiang Tian said with slight regret.

This was practically a combination of Taobao and JD.com, and with a concept more than ten years ahead of theirs.

"Where is your father now? Who are his accomplices?" Minister Tan asked impatiently.

"Windmill Country, he immigrated there a long time ago. I brought people here to keep an eye on things because my father felt Xiang Tiannan might have ulterior motives," Hong Xun explained.

"Windmill Country?" Minister Tan paced back and forth.

Even someone as seasoned and knowledgeable as him was utterly shocked by this case, though he managed to remain calm.

He suppressed the turmoil within him.

Good heavens! Do you know what that means? Do you know how terrifying this case is?

Do you know the profits from this case?

The most terrifying aspect of this case is the billions of dollars involved and its terrifying international influence.

Dark web crime has been a significant dark force in the global internet landscape for years.

While various countries are struggling to combat it, they haven't yet found a truly effective solution.

And this website was completely wiped out.

It can be said that it has made history globally.

It's made history globally!

How terrifying is this influence?

"Wait, where did those people come from? The ones who carried out the attack?" Jiang Tian tapped the table, pressing for an answer.

Hong Xun's gaze darted away.

"Tell me the truth. You've already confessed everything. If you dare to lie, you know the consequences. If the investigation finds out, you won't just get no credit, you might even be executed for your bad behavior," Jiang Tian warned.

Jiang Tian knew these people from the underworld all too well. Talking to them about sentiment was like casting pearls before swine.

But if they had to choose between their own death and someone else's, they would definitely choose the latter without hesitation.

"My dad sent me to do this. It was Xiang Tiannan who did it. It has nothing to do with me!" Hong Xun raised his hand and swore.

"Don't worry, even if you said you ordered it, I wouldn't believe you, looking like a useless piece of trash," Jiang Tian comforted him.

Although Hong Xun felt humiliated, a genuine smile appeared on his face.

"At Windmill Company, officially called Hong Yu, it's ostensibly a shell company at the department level, but actually just used to run a website. And every year we allocate a portion of our assets to bribe the leaders of Windmill Country, so it's always been safe and peaceful. No one has investigated us, and no one can find us online."

In this era.

Many people say that power is more valuable than money.

This statement is actually true, but it doesn't hold true in the real world.

In other countries, money is often equivalent to power.

Would the Hong family, with their billions in assets, be powerless?

They're so afraid of death that they'd even bribe their superiors.

"Not only that, we've been supporting and cooperating with a local gang, one of the top forces in Windmill Country."

Hong Xun confessed honestly, bit by bit, and he wasn't lying.

Windmill was incredibly chaotic.

Everyone knew about it. For example, after the gangsters returned from there, most of the gang leaders fled, all going to Windmill to retire.

The entire place was rotten to the core.

And cooperating with the top forces—no, Jiang Tian even believed that this top-ranking gang was probably the Hong family's henchmen, kept like dogs.

There, money truly can buy everything.

From the gangsters down to the bigwigs who made decisions, they probably had their people too.

This…

“We have many, many people working closely with us. I don’t know about other places, but on our side, almost no one dares to lay a hand on us. We have connections in both the legal and illegal worlds. Also, I’ve written down my father’s address. You can find him by looking here.” Hong Xun was desperate.

He pushed open the interrogation room door and walked out. Even now, the group was still in a daze, unable to recover.

All because this case was far too big.

It had exceeded their capacity to handle it; no, it had exceeded their imagination.

“Do you think he’s lying?” Shang Mingyan asked Jiang Tian.

With an excited heart and trembling hands, Shang Mingyan had handled and solved countless major cases, traveling all over the country to crack them. Behind almost every influential case that was solved, her figure was present.

But this kind of terrifying case was something Shang Mingyan had never seen before, because the things that came out later were simply too numerous and terrifying.

"He wasn't lying, but he's also very cunning. I know why he surrendered so easily and without restraint—because his power is too great," Jiang Tian said quietly.

That's right.

The further they went, the more terrifying the things Hong Xun revealed became.

In other words, according to Hong Xun's confession, it was impossible to take down this dark web company through official means, because everyone there was already under their control.

Even the area around them was their kingdom.

Everyone depended on them for their livelihood.

"Little Jiang is right," Minister Tan suddenly stopped and agreed.

At this moment, Minister Tan also wanted to curse inwardly.

The horror of this case, the scale of the terror involved, and the scope of its impact were simply catastrophic.

Most importantly, it was fortunate that they discovered it.

If another country had investigated this website, arrested them, and then discovered that the founder was someone they had immigrated with.

That would have a negative impact on the entire group and the outside world.

And ignoring it now is even more impossible.

"But this matter is too terrifying. I need to report to my superiors now. You go ahead and do your work; I can't make a decision about this myself." Minister Tan said hurriedly and rushed to his office.

This matter...

Good heavens, who could sit still?

Even Chief Gao was in a daze. Damn it, in all his life, he had never seen such a terrifyingly high-IQ crime, and with such business acumen.

Because this case is the kind that would cause a huge sensation globally, no longer limited to the domestic market.

Once exposed, it will attract global attention, and this will be the most serious, unprecedented single drug-related case in global history.

While Jiang Tian could enjoy this great honor alone, he wouldn't truly keep it all to himself.

"Xiao Jiang, you've been busy for days without a proper rest. You probably won't have any more rest in the future. Go home and rest. You can't keep working like this," Chief Gao instructed.

This suited Jiang Tian perfectly, as he was indeed quite tired. Physically, he could manage, but mentally, he was exhausted, constantly yawning.

“Boss.”

Just then, a series of crackling sounds rang out.

It turned out that many officers who had gone home to rest had been urgently recalled.

The issues in Windmill Country needed further consideration.

However,

the Dongri warehouse within the country and the major drug lord in Hanxi Province needed to be apprehended urgently.

Although the cases in Windmill Country were on a completely different level, and the Hanxi drug lord was insignificant, each case alone was worthy of a first-class merit.

At this point, Jiang Tian himself dared not imagine the terrifying reward his superiors would bestow upon him if the Fishbuy.com case were solved.

“Boss, I heard you caught the murderer?”

Zhao Wei, who entered the room, hurriedly asked.



Chapter 491 A Complete Explosion: A Feast for the Senses


That evening, Zhao Wei received a call from home. To be honest, this was the first time he'd heard his family treat him with such warmth and unexpected kindness.

This made Zhao Wei incredibly flattered.

Ultimately, his situation was, in some ways, even worse than Jiang Tian's; his life experiences were truly tragic.

Faced with his family's greetings and acceptance, Zhao Wei instantly realized that on his own, could he have survived all these years, even recently being relegated to this place where they despised him?

But now?

His family was gradually accepting him.

No, it wasn't acceptance of him; it was acceptance of their boss, Jiang Tian.

In other words, Jiang Tian had even entered the family's radar, and the family valued him highly.

What a terrifying concept!

After all, their family's power and status were almost at the top level.

Immediately afterwards, the city bureau held an emergency operational meeting.

Zhao Wei rushed back as soon as he received the call.

Now, because Jiang Tian was temporarily promoted to acting captain, the special emergency response team, which was merely a transitional structure for him, was naturally canceled, and all of Jiang Tian's former team members were merged into the main force.

Now, Jiang Tian can truly say that at twenty-two years old, he has achieved something truly historic.

Moreover, in most prefecture-level cities, a third-level police inspector is sufficient for a captain, while Yunhai, being a provincial capital, typically requires a first-level or second-level police inspector.

Although Jiang Tian hasn't had a chance to be promoted yet, it's practically a done deal.

Given his current achievements, he's already more than qualified.

Under these circumstances, at twenty-two, a captain about to become a second-level police inspector is, within the system, equivalent to a section-level cadre.

If he were to enter politics, he could already serve as the top leader or second-in-command in a typical township.

Of course, the future prospects of Jiang Tian as a captain in the provincial capital are incomparable to these two. Most importantly, who has ever seen a twenty-two-year-old, fresh out of university, already in this position?

This level of promotion is so absurd it's unheard of.

"Boss, I heard there's been a major case. Captain Guo was killed, and the escort team was attacked?" Zhao Wei said hurriedly.

He heard this on the way.

Actually, after the mission, Jiang Tian's men, along with Jiang Tian himself, were given a day off. While Jiang Tian didn't rest, these men hadn't had time either.

Since everything seemed fine, they all went straight home after the operation.

They hadn't even had time to sleep when they received news of the attack.

"The case has been solved. You found out too late. We've also uncovered another major case. You'll be under Chief Song's command for now," Jiang Tian said.

There are still operations to be arranged at the Dongri warehouse, and tomorrow a large-scale drug lord in the neighboring Hanxi province will also be targeted for capture. In short, the entire municipal bureau will be busy tonight.

However, as mentioned before, Jiang Tian had already had his fill of meat. At this point, no one would object to his rest.

They might even thank him for giving them this opportunity.

It was a joke; Jiang Tian had solved the case single-handedly.

What credit could they possibly gain then? Without Jiang Tian present, they might have been able to perform well, at least earning a third-class merit, and with luck, a second-class individual merit wasn't out of the question.

Even the unluckiest among them could at least secure a collective merit.

This was a feast for gaining credit.

Upon learning of the upcoming operation, the entire city police bureau was in an uproar.

Even the branch offices weren't notified for secrecy. At times like these, the fewer people who knew, the better. The bigger the case, especially the operation itself, the more crucial secrecy was—this was a consensus everyone understood.

While the entire city police bureau was in an uproar, eager to launch an operation and working overtime to set up surveillance, Jiang Tian had already quietly and successfully retreated.

Back home, seeing that everyone was already resting, and Jiang Peng wasn't home due to overtime, Jiang Tian wasn't surprised. He drank some water and lay down on the sofa to rest.

And without any surprises.

While Jiang Tian was sound asleep, it exploded.

It completely exploded.

First and foremost was Minister Tan. Even though it was three or four in the morning, a time when everyone should undoubtedly be resting, Minister Tan could still confidently call his superiors to report even the most serious matters.

That's right.

Over the years, there have been very few cases that Minister Tan couldn't handle.

But this time was different.

Truly, Minister Tan is one of a kind.

The impact and the horror of this case were simply suffocating.

Even Minister Tan was completely bewildered after understanding the situation.

It was too big.

It was unbearable, utterly unbearable.

After Minister Tan reported to his superiors, the situation exploded, and a meeting was immediately convened.

First, there's a crucial question.

Why do many underworld figures like to retire to Windmill Country after achieving their goals?

Because what's illegal elsewhere is often legal in Windmill Country.

For example, drugs. There are two types: soft drugs, like narcotics and sleeping pills, and hard drugs like heroin.

In Windmill Country, soft drugs are legal, but heroin is illegal.

This is a rare piece of good news.

Twenty years from now, the situation outside will be increasingly unpredictable.

Right now, at least there's some control, albeit not very strong.

"You're not joking?"

While Jiang Tian was resting, Minister Tan sat in his office, phone in hand, communicating with someone on the other end. The person on the other end questioned him with an air of authority and pressure.

"Absolutely true. Just now, we did find this website on the dark web," Minister Tan replied definitively.

You don't know until you investigate, and what you find is shocking.

In reality, many times, when you're not paying attention or lack access, you won't notice anything for a long time.

Furthermore, while the investigation and control of the dark web in China isn't as comprehensive as one might imagine, once you have concrete information and the website, investigating it is incredibly easy.

Minister Tan made a call to headquarters, and within ten minutes, the information returned, almost entirely consistent with what Hong Xun had given him.



Chapter 492 The Highest Honor, Shocking, Does He Deserve It? The Path to Heaven


FishGo.com definitely exists, and its business model is indeed that one.

The key issue is that there's more to it than Hong Xun described. For example, FishGo.com has three self-operated warehouses across China; how many bases must it have globally?

Billions of US dollars in transaction volume annually.

To put it bluntly, FishGo.com's existence has even suppressed the Mexicans.

This can be considered the first case of its kind in the new era, the most widespread, the largest in scale, and the most egregious in nature globally.

It's destined to be recorded as a landmark case in global drug trafficking.

It might even be documented and studied by law enforcement systems worldwide as a key case.

"Hiss, all of this was done by Xiao Jiang?" The voice on the phone sounded visibly bewildered.

"That's right. I just came to present an award to Xiao Jiang, but I didn't expect that he would not only uncover the Shimiao Village case, but also solve it in ten minutes after being attacked during transport. He not only cracked the case of the mastermind, but also arrested the main person in charge of Yugou.com, and incidentally brought this behemoth of the dark world, Yugou.com, to the surface. Oh, and the buyer of that batch of goods was the biggest drug lord in Hanxi Province. We'll be setting up an ambush and arresting him tomorrow." Minister Tan's voice was somewhat numb.

As he spoke, Minister Tan suspected he was going insane, completely influenced by Jiang Tian.

Because for a moment, when he mentioned the transaction with the biggest drug lord in Hanxi Province,

this would have been a major case in the past.

It would have been earth-shattering, alarming the entire headquarters, even requiring constant monitoring from headquarters.

But as Minister Tan spoke, he suddenly realized that this case seemed insignificant.

He was ill.

Even such a major case seemed insignificant.

"No, no, no, it's just that the major cases that Xiao Jiang uncovered are too outrageous," Minister Tan quickly reassured himself.

After all, such cases are rare, occurring only once every few decades. It's only because Jiang Tian is exceptionally capable that he couldn't have uncovered them.

Because this business model—Taobao—has only been around for less than a year, a few months. It hasn't even had time to expand its market; it's only popular within a small circle of people. It will take at least several more years to gradually occupy the national market before it becomes the behemoth it will become in later years.

This fantastical, highly threatening, and crucially, extremely secretive method left even the top officials speechless.

The key is that Jiang Tian solved the case so easily.

"Did Xiao Jiang do all this?" The voice on the phone sounded somewhat dreamy.

After understanding all the details of Yugou.com, this bigwig felt somewhat bewildered.

Then came a surge of surprise.

The main point is the impact of this case, its global impact.

In the year 2000, what was most important? International recognition.

At a time when the economy was not yet fully developed and taking off, the importance of international recognition to the domestic economy was self-evident.

Most importantly, if they were discovered and the Hong Xun father and son were arrested, it would have a devastating impact on their compatriots abroad.

This makes them arguably the world's biggest drug lords, bar none.

While the impact isn't as outrageous as the Mexican drug lords' open-air gunfights, the damage is still considerable.

Mexican drug lords, no matter how devastating, can only harm their own region. But the Hong Xun father and son are truly wreaking havoc globally through their online drug trafficking network!

"Yes, that's why I was thinking of applying for a special merit award for Comrade Jiang based on this brilliant case in Shimiao Village, which uncovered over two thousand kilograms of heroin. But I never expected Comrade Jiang to give us such a wonderful surprise. I even feel that a special merit award isn't enough for Comrade Jiang now," Minister Tan sighed.

How should they be rewarded?

Such a great achievement, even a special merit award feels like an injustice.

If this got out, everyone would be in an uproar.

Look at what he's saying!

The greatest honor, and he's even being treated unfairly.

Most importantly, after a moment of silence, the person on the other end of the phone said with great admiration, "You're right. If this case is true, a special-class merit is completely insufficient to express Comrade Jiang's contributions. Our principle is that merit must be rewarded; there can be no precedent of suppressing merit or haggling over its value. Otherwise, wouldn't that dishearten our comrades? Although this year's August 1st Medal selection is already complete, Comrade Jiang is fully qualified to be included as an exception."

August 1st?

Hiss!!!

Not to mention Minister Tan.

Even Chief Gao, sitting to the side, jumped to his feet.

You know, even Chief Gao coveted this medal.

But unfortunately.

This is the highest medal.

That's right, you read that correctly.

It's an award even greater than the Special Merit Award.

Actually, obtaining the Special Merit Award is more difficult than obtaining the August 1st Merit Award.

However, in terms of honor, it can't compare to the August 1st Merit Award.

It's just that in peacetime, obtaining the Special Merit Award is practically a dream come true, but in wartime, it's not particularly difficult; this is simply a limitation of the era.

In reality, in terms of the prestige of the August 1st Merit Award:

If given to someone within the system, it's practically paving the way to the heavens.

It's the highest form of recognition.

It's like an ancient get-out-of-jail-free card.

It's not just awarded to those with limited influence; it's awarded to great figures who can change the overall landscape and wield enormous power.

Even for Minister Tan and Chief Gao, this kind of merit is something they dream of obtaining.

"Damn it." Minister Tan was a little exasperated.

"Of course, it mainly depends on the nature of this case and whether there's any exaggeration involved. We need to expedite this case, as delays can lead to unforeseen changes. Keep an eye on things in Yunhai. Xiao Jiang has lifted the veil over Yunhai, and the darkness at the bottom is being exposed step by step. Things should get quite chaotic in the near future," the voice on the phone continued.

"Yes, what's the next step in this case?" Minister Tan inquired.

"Comrade Jiang has joined the security department. He has the responsibility and the right to investigate cases like this. After all, the August 1st Army isn't so easy to obtain. Although we will officially intervene in negotiations, for something of this scale, it's best to strike first. After all, with assets worth billions of dollars, the Windmill won't release him so easily. Let's see how well the mission is completed. If it goes well, what harm is there even if we give him the medal?"

The voice on the phone was full of nonchalance.

Minister Tan sighed upon hearing this, thinking that this was more like it. After all, this medal was a great medal!

Jiang is too young.

With this medal, Old Tan can't be so cold anymore.

This kid's status will be elevated countless times.

He's only twenty-two!

Minister Tan thought about Jiang Tian's youth and couldn't help but wonder what he was doing at twenty.

Just moments after he hung up, the phone rang again.

Minister Tan quickly answered the call.

"Minister, a huge discovery! We arrested four criminals at the Dongri warehouse and seized a terrifying 4,000 kilograms of heroin. This is the largest seizure in a single case in recent years."



Chapter 493 One night, ten thousand kilograms—a history no one can break.


The news was just news, but the actual seizure—the actual confiscation of the mountain of heroin—truly shocked them.

Song Liguo felt dizzy.

No.

Have drug dealers become this brazen? Have they become a veritable plague without their knowledge?

Four thousand kilograms! What a terrifying number! For a moment, he even wondered if Jiang Tian was exaggerating.

But the reality was that when they arrived, they found only four guards. After subduing them and entering the warehouse to see the four thousand kilograms of heroin, they finally believed it.

"Hara hara hara!!!"

Police dogs circled the mountain of heroin, while Wei Xun organized his team to examine the scene.

His eyes trembled with increasing intensity.

Just two hours earlier, they had seized 2,000 kilograms of heroin and several large-scale drug-making facilities in Shimiao Village.

Then, in the blink of an eye, another 4,000 kilograms appeared. This isn't an exaggeration; it truly happened in the blink of an eye, and this wasn't just one case.

Strictly speaking, these should be considered two separate cases, merely an extension of the Shimiao Village case.

The saying "birds of a feather flock together" perfectly describes this situation. Corrupt individuals inevitably form powerful groups, and the same applies to criminals.

Shimiao Village brought Xiang Tiannan to the forefront, but behind Xiang Tiannan, and his partners,

are numerous and significant cases.

Moreover, Xiang Tiannan is not merely a major drug lord in Hanxi Province.

He owns three warehouses across the country, arranged in a triangular pattern.

This significantly reduces risk and many dangers during transportation, while also lowering costs.

For this type of crime, transportation is, to some extent, the biggest challenge.

However, on the other hand, in reality, 80% of normal criminal investigations are basically uncovered through investigation.

In other words, it's a passive approach.

They receive information, then set up a stakeout, then lure the suspect into the trap, and finally make the arrest.

Most of the time, it's based on certain clues, followed by in-depth investigation and tracing back to the source.

On the other hand, a small portion involves using police dogs and other methods to check high-risk areas.

Similar to Jiang Tian's method of solving cases.

Song Liguo was astonished.

What is this?

This is all credit!

"Comrade Jiang Tian is amazing!"

"Wow, another four thousand kilograms."

"What 'Comrade Jiang Tian'? That's not the title you should use. Now you should call him Captain Jiang."

"Oh, oh, right, mine, mine, that's right, Captain Jiang."

Voices constantly rang out from the team members searching the scene.

The idea that youth is distrusted only shows a lack of ability.

Who here would dare to challenge Jiang Tian?

That would be a joke.

These drug addicts, although they were merely executors and didn't make any significant contributions themselves, their achievements were undeniably substantial.

In such a major case, they faced no danger whatsoever; simply participating guaranteed them a share of the credit.

While their lives were previously leisurely, who joined the anti-drug squad truly for a life of ease?

Who didn't enter with fervent patriotism?

The previous leisure was unavoidable; given a choice, no one would willingly languish in the squad. Otherwise, there were far better opportunities to simply coast along.

They had no choice before.

Now, Jiang Tian has given them an option to bring glory and fame.

It's fair to say that within the entire municipal anti-drug squad, every single member, even without individual achievements, has been reaping enormous collective credit lately.

Especially the various bonuses, which have exceeded their monthly salaries by several times.

They all understand who brought them all this—Jiang Tian!

These four thousand kilograms.

They represent the foundation of the Yunhai Municipal Bureau, allowing them to proudly display their achievements to everyone.

"Chief Song, look what we found!"

Just as Song Liguo was reporting to Minister Tan, a voice suddenly rang out.

"What is it?" Song Liguo turned and asked.

Wei Xun, beaming with excitement, came out holding a stack of documents. "It looks like an inbound and outbound inventory list."

A ledger?

No.

This was a more direct representation than a ledger.

Song Liguo hurriedly took it, opened it, and sure enough, it contained detailed inbound and outbound records.

For example, the first sheet of paper detailed that 160 items were received on the fourth day.

And what were these 160 items? Song Liguo looked at the mountain of white powder, all in bags, and each bag, based on his experience, weighed approximately 25 kilograms.

"What about the inspection department? Have they checked the specifications of all this white powder?" Song Liguo asked impatiently.

"The results are in. After inspection, there were 160 bags of heroin, each weighing approximately 25 kilograms. According to statistics, the total is 4,000 kilograms."

4,000 kilograms.

What does that mean?

This kind of large-scale transaction is terrifying.

It's safe to say that once shipped, the social harm caused by this shipment will be unimaginably terrifying.

That drug lord in Hanxi is simply insane.

Wait...

Song Liguo suddenly remembered that not long ago, when he went to Hanxi, the Hanxi director summoned him and General Manager Bai to his office and gave them a severe dressing-down.

Yan Ming, he couldn't even manage Yunnan Province properly, allowing these criminals to harm their Hanxi.

"Drug lords in Hanxi are probably rampant by now." Song Liguo's heart sank.

Then Song Liguo opened the document and read through it page by page, each page more shocking than the last.

Knowing the specifications of the records, this is irrefutable evidence of recent sales.

As expected.

[Thirty days, thirty items shipped.]

[Twenty-nine days, thirty items received.]

[Three days.....]

These are the warehouse entry and exit records. These documents, even without ledgers, are sufficient to prove the quantity.

"They only record the shipment volume for the last six months, roughly 500 to 1000 kilograms per month."

And this record proves that the quantity of drugs involved exceeds 10,000.

Moreover, this is 10,000 kilograms with sufficient evidence, enough to convict someone.

"Minister Tan, we found the warehouse entry and exit records, which confirm that the total amount received has exceeded 10,000 kilograms," Song Liguo hurriedly said.

Crazy.

Completely driven to madness.

Jiang Tian slept soundly that night.

Because Jiang Tian understood that the problems were now solved.

The next step was simple.

That is to continue following the leads of Xiang Tiannan and Hong Xun.



Chapter 494 Post-Battle Summary, the Whole Audience Boils Over


The downsides of drug enforcement are obvious and well-known: dealing with criminals often results in terrifying retaliatory acts.

However, the advantage is that they operate in large groups; arresting one person allows for further surveillance and arrests based on their leads.

If criminal investigation is about solving cases and finding the perpetrators,

then drug enforcement is essentially a process of continuous, in-depth investigation and uncovering the truth through sting operations.

This often requires a significant amount of time, and the final outcome is uncertain.

But now...

When a massive drug trafficking ring finally revealed a weakness,

the entire Yunhai, no, the entire Yunnan province, was shaken.

Four thousand kilograms!

Upon confirming the news, the protection of Xiang Tiannan was immediately raised to the highest level.

This was to prevent Xiang Tiannan from escaping and, more importantly, to prevent any problems from arising.

Following this, surveillance of tomorrow's transaction was deployed—this is essentially the core work of drug enforcement.

Meetings were held one after another.

Jiang Tian slept soundly until dawn.

When Jiang Tian woke up, his mother had already prepared dinner, and Jiang Ning and Jiang Xiaoyu were sitting at the table chatting.

They were mostly talking about what had happened the previous night.

Shock.

Undoubtedly, what happened last night had a huge impact on Jiang Ning's worldview.

After her father was arrested without any explanation, Jiang Ning even had a momentary misunderstanding about them. Her father was innocent; why was he arrested?

But after seeing the horrific scene from the previous night, Jiang Ning fell silent.

After accompanying Guo Na to the hospital, she witnessed firsthand the horrific state of the place.

How many people were seriously injured?

And it wasn't just Captain Guo who had died.

There were far too many others who had sacrificed their lives.

"This is the first time I've realized how dangerous things can be," Jiang Ning said, her voice heavy with sadness.

This made Jiang Xiaoyu, who was listening nearby, look worried. Seeing Jiang Tian stand up, Jiang Xiaoyu thought of her brother's days and nights of working overtime, and her concern grew even stronger: "Brother, is your anti-drug work very dangerous?"

"A month ago, our anti-drug squad had about two hundred people. In the past month, about seventy new comrades have joined, so the number is still around two hundred."

"Do you think it's dangerous?"

Jiang Tian asked casually.

It's normal.

Meritorious service often comes with sacrifice.

But the sacrifice of Captain Guo truly weighed heavily on Jiang Tian's mind. In fact, Jiang Tian had already gotten used to it.

One must always look forward. He could only arrest more criminals to avenge Captain Guo.

"Brother, could you perhaps transfer to another position?" Jiang Xiaoyu's face turned pale with fright.

“Some things just have to be done. If I don’t do them, someone else will. If no one does them, how will you make a living? The whole world will be in chaos,” Jiang Tian comforted her.

But his words only made Jiang Xiaoyu look more melancholy.

Jiang Ning stood by, head bowed, silent.

However, Jiang Tian was thinking of Guo Na: “How was that classmate Guo Na last night?”

“Nana is very strong. She won't be easily defeated. She said she'll study hard to serve her country, and even wants to follow in her father's footsteps and join you,” Jiang Ning replied.

This surprised Jiang Tian.

Thinking of Guo Na's tragic situation—her mother had just passed away a few years ago, and now her only family, her father, had also sacrificed his life—it was a devastating blow for a young girl still underage.

“Does she have any relatives?” Jiang Tian asked worriedly.

“No,” Jiang Ning shook her head.

“Spend more time with her when you have free time. She's a very strong girl. If she encounters any difficulties, let her contact me anytime. Although Captain Guo has sacrificed his life, our entire anti-drug squad will not stand idly by if she encounters any difficulties in the future,” Jiang Tian said heavily.

“Thank you.”

Jiang Ning gave Jiang Tian a completely different look.

Jiang Tian didn't say anything. After washing up and eating, he returned to the city bureau.

Since arriving at the Municipal Public Security Bureau, almost everyone has been working overtime, some even working non-stop.

Too many cases happened last night.

Many cases, even those already compiled, still need to be submitted to the procuratorate for appeal.

Of course, those are the cases that are clear and closed.

But over 90% are still piling up in the drug enforcement department.

Mainly because the cases Jiang Tian has created are far too numerous and complex; without a complete case, they can't be submitted to the procuratorate.

Overall, because of Jiang Tian, ​​not only is the entire municipal police system incredibly busy, but the entire public security, procuratorate, and judicial system has seen its workload increased many times over.

Although 90% are not yet closed, even the 10% of cases that have been closed are enough to keep the entire public security, procuratorate, and judicial system working overtime alongside the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

"First, on behalf of the entire police system, I express my deepest condolences to the comrades who sacrificed their lives last night. These were heroes who made outstanding contributions to the motherland. Even though they have died, they will receive the honors they deserve. I will specifically apply for special awards and medals for them. As for the families of the fallen comrades, all compensation will be provided to the fullest extent possible, and any difficulties they may face in the future will be given priority."

Early morning.

The morning meeting was underway.

Almost everyone in the entire city bureau was present.

This was the largest conference room in the entire city bureau, large enough to accommodate over a thousand people.

However, even with so many seats, it was already full. Jiang Tian arrived and casually found a place to stand near the back.

Clap clap clap!!!

As soon as Minister Tan finished speaking, enthusiastic applause erupted.

"Secondly, and right here, I want to especially commend Comrade Xiao Jiang for successfully uncovering the criminal activities of Shimiao Village, this drug-ridden village. Furthermore, without giving the criminals any time to recover, he swiftly and decisively apprehended the mastermind during their retaliatory attack, delivering a resounding counterattack to all criminals. Is Comrade Xiao Jiang here?"

Minister Tan, seated on the lower center of the platform, looked around, but unfortunately, there were too many people, and he couldn't find him.

Most morning meetings are departmental meetings, but for joint operations like last night's, a debriefing meeting is necessary.

To analyze the problems and acknowledge the mistakes.

Jiang Tian was just there to listen, never expecting to be called out by name.

Even Jiang Tian was stunned.

Wow.

This is absolutely incredible.

Not only was Jiang Tian shocked, but even the leaders who knew him well stood up in disbelief.

Praising Jiang Tian is normal.

But...

Who were the people present? Leaders from various departments, police officers from various departments, special forces, and armed police.

And their superiors, etc.

And among the police officers present were leaders from the branch bureau, and even leaders from headquarters.

In short, there were many people.

Jiang Tian was famous before, and many people knew him, but only within a small circle.

Now, Jiang Tian was directly brought into the public eye.

In other words,

Jiang Tian's previous actions, due to the nature of his position, were always kept confidential. While the results weren't great, at least he adhered to the rules.

But now, Minister Tan has broken those rules.

What does this mean?

Targeting Jiang Tian? That's utter nonsense.

In other words, Jiang Tian is being used as a role model.

A positive role model, and what does that represent?

Even Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat, because these words, at this time and in this period, also represented another layer of meaning.

This meaning, even for Jiang Tian, ​​was the kind that made his heart race.

And then, a scene that instantly took everyone's breath away unfolded.

"Just last night, our Comrade Jiang Tian successfully cracked a drug trafficking case involving tens of thousands of kilograms in one night, a truly outstanding achievement."

Minister Tan's opening statement in the post-battle summary was like a magnitude 18 earthquake, leaving everyone's heads spinning.



Chapter 495 The Big Shots' Approval, All in One Go


This was less of a morning meeting and more of a credit-sharing session.

What? You're saying the case hasn't even been solved yet, and you're already assigning credit? Isn't that a bit premature and ridiculous?

What a joke.

Look at Yunhai's reputation these days, look at his public image.

Especially as time goes by, while Jiang Tian has actually accomplished something, he hasn't salvaged Yunhai's reputation; in fact, he's made it even worse.

Why specifically?

Because recently, major cases have been frequently featured in the news, and they've been solved so often.

A major case involving Yunhai appears, and within two days, it's solved; then there's the overseas joint enforcement operation, boasting about its great achievements.

Then domestically, there's the arrest of a huge criminal organization, the seizure of vast amounts of heroin.

It's too frequent.

Recent news and online discussions about suspenseful crimes have almost entirely focused on Yunhai.

Goodness, let's put it another way.

Why is it that others don't have as much crime, but you have so much? What, isn't solving cases your job? On the contrary, why do you allow so many drug crimes to occur in your jurisdiction, and in such a concentrated manner?

Does this mean that only so many have been caught, and there are many more who haven't been caught?

Yunhai has recently experienced a crisis of trust.

This crisis, if not handled properly, could easily cause a huge and devastating blow to the entire development of Yunhai. This is not an exaggeration, because companies will not put their own interests at such risks.

Farmers from other places, as well as talents from various industries, will be unwilling to come to Yunhai due to social unrest.

In short,

In fact, Yunhai's current situation is, in some ways, much more difficult than before Jiang Tian took action.

And what is needed at this time is morale boosting.

Last night, Minister Tan had a long and detailed discussion with the higher-ups.

This issue was discussed at length, and it was ultimately decided that facing such difficulties, this gripping case provided an opportunity to bring Jiang Tian forward.

In other words,

the moment Minister Tan called Jiang Tian out, it represented an attitude, and more importantly, a transformation for Jiang Tian.

He would no longer be an ordinary anti-drug police officer.

He would even be surrounded by a certain aura.

Actually, having an aura isn't always a good thing. When exposed to the spotlight, especially in such a dangerous profession, like Jiang Tian's, his actions would be somewhat restricted.

But as the saying goes,

when the country needs him.

Amidst the watchful eyes of the crowd, Jiang Tian walked steadily yet powerfully down from the center of the stage.

At that moment, with Jiang Tian's entrance, Minister Tan and all the leaders of the police system present stood up in unison and applauded enthusiastically.

Jiang Tian first saluted them one by one.

And these leaders, one by one, returned the greeting with solemn expressions.

Leaving aside everything else.

Some of the onlookers below might not know Jiang Tian, ​​but these leaders were perfectly aware of what Jiang Tian had done.

Among those present were high-ranking leaders from the Armed Police, Jiang Peng, Director Shen, and Shang Mingyan. The usually aloof Shang Mingyan smiled upon seeing Jiang Tian. The director of the Narcotics Control Headquarters was also present, which Jiang Tian understood; given the magnitude of the drug case and the presence of Minister Tan, it wouldn't make sense for the director not to attend.

However, Jiang Tian was somewhat surprised to see General Manager Bai seated on the edge.

Of course, Jiang Tian suddenly realized that General Manager Bai was qualified to be there, but seeing him still surprised him, as it seemed like it had been a while since he'd seen him.

Actually, Jiang Tian had learned about General Manager Bai's situation from his previous life. He hadn't forgotten that case, but the time hadn't come yet, and Jiang Tian had been quite busy.

However, judging from his memory, that operation had been completed quickly.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​a day apart feels like three autumns," Bai Qiu said, shaking Jiang Tian's hand.

"It's all part of my job," Jiang Tian replied to Bai Qiu.

Then he met with Xu Poye, the head of the 21st Bureau.

It hadn't been long since their last meeting, but seeing Jiang Tian again, Xu Poye couldn't help but feel a surge of emotion.

"Good lad, last time we met you you only had a few first-class merits. In the blink of an eye, I didn't expect that was just the beginning," Xu Poye quipped.

In terms of quantity, Jiang Tian's achievements couldn't compare to his, but in terms of prestige, Xu Poye admitted that he hadn't accomplished as much in his entire life as Jiang Tian in such a short time.

Wow.

Nearly 20,000 kilograms of heroin seized!

The 21st Bureau was the head office of the entire anti-drug bureau, and also a crucial department leading the national anti-drug efforts, something Xu Poye understood well.

In the anti-drug field, the only hard standard for measuring merit was how many heroin cases you seized and solved.

Jiang Tian's exaggerated credit left Xu Poye dumbfounded. He realized that, in his entire life, in terms of solving cases independently, Jiang Tian was almost surpassing him in total number.

"This is all thanks to your guidance, Director Xu," Jiang Tian said modestly.

This made Xu Poye's eyes light up as he looked at Jiang Tian.

Next came Shang Mingyan and others.

In short, Jiang Tian saluted and greeted everyone regardless of rank, leaving no one speechless. Even Minister Tan watched with admiration.

He knew how to get along with people.

Even better at getting things done.

Most terrifyingly, his efficiency was terrifying.

Even Minister Tan was secretly astonished.

Besides, there were two other people on the stage whom Jiang Tian didn't recognize. After introductions, he learned they were from the Fifth Bureau.

"This is Director Tao of the Fifth Bureau," Shang Mingyan introduced.

Jiang Tian looked at the man in his fifties, with an upright face, and inwardly gasped.

All the Fifth Bureau personnel are here?

Is it really that big of a commotion?

Actually, Jiang Tian had no idea how much of a sensation the events of last night had caused.

Especially the cases Jiang Tian had solved, each one more terrifying than the last, not to mention the sudden progress in the attack on Secretary Mu, and the resolution of a previous major attack case.

The attack on Wen Yulin, known as the "411 Case," involved dozens of thugs. Although Jiang Tian killed almost all of them, the case reached a dead end due to foreign-hired forces. Just then, Jiang Tian solved a case in Shimiao Village and, incidentally, apprehended the mastermind behind it.

Immediately afterward, this mastermind was attacked.

As a result, Jiang Tian solved the case again, ultimately arresting Xiang Tiannan, a major drug lord in China. Accompanying Xiang Tiannan was the founder of Yugou.com, a globally notorious drug trafficking platform.

This type of platform, operating on the dark web, is extremely difficult to investigate. Even if discovered on the dark web, it's hard to pinpoint its location due to its complex architecture; even tracing the server's country of origin is extremely difficult.

Even twenty years later, arrests would only be possible through sophisticated phishing schemes and luring offline transactions. As long as one doesn't leak information online, such crimes are difficult to target even twenty years later.

Now, however, they're going to take them all down at once.

This is no longer just a major case; it's a global case.

"Young man, I have high hopes for you," Director Tao said with a broad smile.



Chapter 496 The Path to Godhood Ultimately Insults the God


Putting everything else aside, he had already learned from Shang Mingyan about Jiang Tian's speed in solving cases and his terrifying investigative logic.

Sometimes, even Shang Mingyan couldn't understand how Jiang Tian solved cases, how he investigated clues, or even how he managed to do it while standing right beside him.

This caused Shang Mingyan immense emotional distress.

At the same time, Shang Mingyan wasn't one to deny it. She had indeed been thoroughly outmatched, and she accepted the defeat. However, she had developed a strong interest and curiosity in Jiang Tian.

"Thank you," Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

As for Shang Mingyan's position, from what Jiang Tian knew, it seemed to be the deputy director of the Fifth Bureau. To be honest, Jiang Tian was slightly taken aback when he found out.

Looking to be twenty-five or twenty-six, but actually thirty-five or thirty-six—a deputy director of the General Bureau at thirty-five or thirty-six, what a terrifying future that was!

However, Jiang Tian also realized that Shang Mingyan seemed only interested in criminal investigation, not in the system. He was purely driven by hard work and had risen through the ranks. Although young, his potential was probably limited; at most, he could succeed Director Tao.

The other two experts in the special task force held important positions in the Fifth Bureau, and Jiang Tian nodded slightly to them.

At this moment,

Minister Tan stood up, picked up the small microphone on the table, and calmly but explosively announced how Jiang Tian had solved a case in half an hour the previous night, then quickly uncovered the drug storage location.

He had successfully dismantled a drug storage site with a confirmed amount of over ten thousand kilograms, actually seizing four thousand kilograms.

Boom!!!

Sure enough, as Minister Tan finished speaking, almost ninety percent of those present, unaware of the inside story, erupted in shock.

The case, which had just been solved in the early hours of the morning, meant that no one present, not even those from the branch bureau, had received any prior notice.

Even the branch bureau chiefs sitting below were all utterly shocked and incredulous.

Their eyes were all fixed on Jiang Tian.

It was an explosion.

It had to be admitted that Minister Tan's high-profile announcement had completely blown things up.

"In addition, last night we raided a drug-producing village, seizing 2,000 kilograms of drugs, along with a quantity of drug-making equipment. Then, within two hours, we uncovered another major drug trafficking case, seizing 4,000 kilograms. Not counting past transactions, the total seizure volume is now 6,000 kilograms."

Minister Tan slowly recounted Jiang Tian's achievements from the previous night.

Six thousand kilograms.

What does that mean?

Six thousand kilograms in one night.

You have to understand, even one kilogram is enough to get the ringleader executed. Strictly speaking, over fifty grams is enough for a life sentence or more.

And ringleaders with over one kilogram are almost always executed; a suspended death sentence is considered good behavior.

Six thousand kilograms—what does that mean?

"This major drug lord's name is Xiang Tiannan. You might not have heard of him before, and frankly, I hadn't either. But after investigating, I discovered that this Xiang Tiannan is no ordinary person!"

Minister Tan tapped the table and looked around.

You don't know until you investigate, and what you find is shocking.

In fact, many things cannot withstand investigation.

"According to the interrogation, last night, one of our branch leaders had dinner with this Xiang Tiannan."

Besides that, investigating Xiang Tiannan wasn't actually difficult. He maintained a very positive image, a kind of "good guy" in the underworld and a social butterfly in the legitimate world.

In other words,

Xiang Tiannan was very clever. He almost never participated in the power struggles within the underworld, and the forces he built existed as companies.

Even his henchmen were all affiliated with security or bodyguard companies.

His assets mainly consisted of operating nightclubs and bars, focusing on building his bar business. However, investigations at the bars revealed they were completely clean.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian, ​​frankly, even Minister Tan had to admit that without this lead, it would have been impossible to catch Xiang Tiannan. His methods were too meticulous, which is precisely why Jiang Tian's terrifying investigative skills were so evident.

As Minister Tan's words were spoken, a commotion erupted in the room.

A deathly silence was quickly broken by a deafening roar.

Various voices of discussion erupted in the massive conference room.

The branch office leaders, seated at the front, exchanged bewildered glances, wondering who Minister Tan was referring to.

“The capital has repeatedly emphasized that officials must resolutely avoid drinking culture at banquets. This is a serious violation of rules and regulations. As for who it is, I won’t say, but don’t think I’m letting it go. After the meeting, I expect you to write your own resignation letter, leaving everyone with some dignity,” Minister Tan began, delivering a stern warning.

This sent a chill down everyone’s spine.

In reality, Minister Tan had conducted an investigation and found that while the branch office chief had issues, they were within certain boundaries.

Regarding Xiang Tiannan, although he had accepted some money from Xiang Tiannan, he hadn’t done anything for him. It seemed Xiang Tiannan was simply trying to curry favor.

Otherwise, resigning would have been a pipe dream.

"And now, standing before you is Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​who, in just a few months since joining the anti-drug squad, has successively completed major combat missions, making outstanding and indelible contributions to the peace and stability of Yunhai. During this time, he has been awarded multiple First-Class Merit Medals, the First-Class Hero Medal, and even more..."

"The Special-Class Merit Medal, which has been almost extinct for decades—yes, the Special-Class Merit recipient who has recently been the subject of much discussion—the person standing before you is Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​who holds the title of Anti-Drug Vanguard, multiple First-Class Merit Medals, the Special-Class Merit Medal, and the First-Class Hero Medal. After receiving so many significant honors, Comrade Jiang Tian not only did not stand still, but even last night, he cracked two major cases in succession, dismantling a drug village and the case of the drug lord Xiang Tiannan in one night. Let us give a warm round of applause and congratulate Comrade Jiang Tian!!!"
