Minister Tan did not delve further into the details about any particular branch leader. After all, he was well aware of the reasons behind those bad apples, and most importantly, the purpose of today's meeting.

Last night's detailed conversation with the bigwig, and after receiving his advice and approval, this meeting marks the first step on Jiang Tian's path to fame.

Since Yunhai is on the verge of chaos, a stabilizing force needs to be planted in its path.

And that stabilizing force is Jiang Tian.

It's a bit of a sacrifice for Jiang Tian. Actions that were convenient before are now inconvenient, and his previously safe environment is now under the spotlight. But there's no other way; the top priority now is stabilizing the situation in Yunhai.

So, the only option is to create a god.

Or rather, not create a god, because Jiang Tian is already a god.

"Little Jiang, I'm sorry to have to put you through this," Minister Tan said with some guilt. Little Jiang is full of passion, and for the sake of Yunhai's economy, this is the only way now. Although this creates an unbreakable golden image for the future, Comrade Jiang's actions will be restricted.

Jiang Tian was stunned, almost blurting out a slogan that seemed to only exist in the future: "I am a brick of the motherland, I will be moved wherever I am needed."

Minister Tan: "???"

All the leaders: "???"



Chapter 497 Promotion to Second-Level Police Inspector, Creating Unprecedented History


Almost instantly, a deafening clapping erupted.

Especially some of the newly recruited officers, some of whom were Jiang Tian's classmates from police academy, stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

Was this reasonable?

Looking at their own trainee officer status, and then at Jiang Tian's third-level police inspector rank, was this reasonable?

"The captain of the First Brigade of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment unfortunately sacrificed his life in last night's operation. However, the responsibility of narcotics control is arduous and immense. We have no time for mourning. After careful consideration, Comrade Jiang Tian will temporarily assume the position of captain of the First Brigade of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment. Let's give him a round of applause," Minister Tan continued.

Boom!!!

After a moment of silence, deafening applause erupted.

Leaving aside all his other merits, that one Special Merit alone left no room for doubt or objection.

That's why it's said that the Special Merit Award, after being broadcast on television, caused a huge sensation.

Even the internet exploded with discussion, wondering what earth-shattering event had occurred in Yunhai that led to the fabrication of a Special Merit Award.

It's similar to the August 1st Army Day Award.

In terms of prestige, the August 1st Army Day Award is the most prestigious and highest honor, far surpassing the Special Merit Award. However, only one or two people receive it every few years.

The Special Merit Award, on the other hand, has been undeserved for decades.

This is because the Special Merit Award is confined to the police or military systems. In peacetime, it's equivalent to one person single-handedly changing the course of a war, which has prerequisites. It might be possible in wartime, but in peacetime, it's practically a pipe dream. Therefore, the Special Merit Award has gradually disappeared.

And the August 1st Army Day Award...

This represents the highest honor, open to all sectors of society, regardless of background, profession, or social status. Anyone who has made a significant and impactful contribution is eligible for selection.

With a wider range of professions and groups involved, naturally a few are selected every few years.

Of course, Jiang Tian hadn't considered this at all; it seemed too far off.

What was happening right now that surprised Jiang Tian was...

Minister Tan gestured for the applause to subside before speaking: "At this moment, I have another important announcement to make. In recognition of Comrade Jiang Tian's numerous meritorious deeds and outstanding contributions in successfully saving lives and property for the Party and the people, Headquarters has specially promoted Comrade Jiang Tian to the rank of Second-Class Police Inspector."

Boom!!!

If the previous announcement was just a minor shock—a battalion commander, a temporary battalion commander, in his early twenties—something seemingly unbelievable, still within the realm of what these people could accept.

But now...

Second-Class Police Inspector.

That's not it anymore.

Jiang Tian was previously at a low-end, high-end level, but now even his equipment has been upgraded in one go.

Not to mention others, even Jiang Tian himself was somewhat surprised.

Because Jiang Tian had expected to receive a lot of credit and possibly be awarded a reward, but there was absolutely no option for a promotion in police rank.

Jiang Tian had only been in this position for a few months.

Strictly speaking, Jiang Tian is still in his internship period—yes, his senior year internship.

He even has to return to school periodically, but because of his special case schedule and numerous cases, this has been put on hold.

Even the school wouldn't dare say anything.

What a joke! Who at the school would dare say anything, who would dare criticize Jiang Tian?

Moreover, if it weren't for the special nature of Jiang Tian's position and the need for secrecy, the police academy was prepared to publicize him extensively, making him a famous alumnus.

After all, in all these years, the school had never had a student like him before; this was the first time.

Heaven knows what the principal's reaction was when Jiang Tian returned to school last time and revealed his police rank.

Good heavens!

He just graduated!

Oh no, it should be during his senior year internship! You haven't even received your diploma yet, and you're back at school as a third-level police inspector?!

What the hell? Who would believe this? But the principal, refusing to believe it, inquired and was dumbfounded to discover that Jiang Tian really wasn't lying.

Although detailed news and information about Jiang Tian couldn't be found in the police system, his rank of Third-Level Police Inspector was undeniably in the system.

The principal of Jiang Tian's alma mater almost instantly and completely changed his attitude towards him, becoming extremely accommodating, even to the point of being willing to come and go as he pleased, like returning to the school for registration.

They even asked Jiang Tian if he needed any help, urging him to let them know immediately, promising to provide it.

Although they didn't know what Jiang Tian had done, the principal knew that Jiang Tian had definitely done something earth-shattering, and not just one thing.

And now, the Third-Level Police Inspector rank was history; what stood before everyone was...

Minister Tan personally produced the rank of Second-Level Police Inspector, tore off Jiang Tian's Third-Level Police Inspector rank, and personally replaced it with the Second-Level Police Inspector rank.

Quickly.

This is too fast.

Not even a day.

It came too suddenly, leaving Jiang Tian somewhat bewildered. This surprise came a little too quickly.

After all,

Jiang Tian had already skipped ranks several times, achieving a five-level leap from trainee police officer, each time a promotion made through exceptional procedures.

This speed is unprecedented, even nationwide, in the past few decades.

Moreover, the higher the rank, the greater the difficulty of each promotion, and the more terrifying the power associated with it.

In other words, from now on, even if Jiang Tian does nothing, following the three-year promotion principle, if he doesn't fall behind, he can simply relax and reach Shen Han's position before the age of thirty.

Of course, this is under normal circumstances. Is it possible for Jiang Tian to do absolutely nothing?

Following that, Shen Han gave a brief account of Jiang Tian's glorious past. Of course, it wasn't very detailed; she simply mentioned his achievements, such as the fact that he had proactively discovered and seized nearly 20,000 kilograms of drugs to date.

For example, the number of drug dealers Jiang Tian had already arrested and killed had long since exceeded several hundred, even a thousand. In fact, Jiang Tian himself couldn't remember how many he had arrested and killed, and even Minister Tan was being conservative.

Shimiao Village alone had over seven hundred people. Adding everything else, the number of drug offenders killed and arrested—those convicted of crimes involving more than fifty grams of drugs, meaning those sentenced to death or life imprisonment—whether dead or alive, at Jiang Tian's hands, had already exceeded 1,500, and that was a conservative estimate.

The figure of over a thousand was only because even Minister Tan himself had lost his sense of proportion.

A wave of uproar swept through the audience.

After being stunned by the award ceremony, Jiang Tian quickly recovered and delivered a speech without a prepared script, thanking the leaders, and so on…

This was entirely Jiang Tian's impromptu reaction. Even many seasoned veterans within the system sometimes make fools of themselves when giving speeches without a script. Therefore, many high-ranking leaders even have their departments prepare numerous prepared speeches beforehand, and then choose the best one to deliver.

Jiang Tian's performance, however, left the leaders on the podium somewhat taken aback.

Clearly, it was entirely Jiang Tian's improvisation.

But this improvisation made even these seasoned officials feel ashamed of themselves.

"The Party has entrusted us with..."



Chapter 498 Getting serious, going all out to completely dismantle it?


"What a joke! Regardless, Jiang Tian had over a decade of experience in clerical work in his previous life. He was all too familiar with how leaders' speeches, as time went on, became increasingly elaborate but ultimately meaningless.

These rhetoric spells left the leaders completely bewildered.

Especially since Jiang Tian went from discussing the harms of drug crimes and the other social harms they spawned, extending to the necessity of combating crime.

"Someone once asked me why I chose to join the most dangerous and arduous anti-drug police force, and why I work so hard—wasn't I risking my life? This is how I answered him: I'm just doing what I'm supposed to do. In the end, I summarized it in eight words: With great power comes great responsibility."

Boom!!!

Things were polite words, pretty words, that's what Jiang Tian said at first, but later, he couldn't help but reveal some genuine emotion.

Indeed.

The economy of Yunhai, the safety and stability of millions of people.

A simple statement, yet it was an explosive outburst.

Especially the last eight words—they were the finishing touch.

Twenty years ago, who had ever heard such beautiful words? Amidst all the empty rhetoric and grandstanding of speeches, Jiang Tian's words were like a thunderclap from a clear sky.

The leaders on the stage applauded wildly.

Especially those like Bai Qiu.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​their eyes trembled with excitement.

Their crystal-clear eyes were fixed intently on Jiang Tian.

At that moment, Jiang Tian also turned to look at Bai Qiu, smiled slightly after his speech, and turned to leave the stage.

Actually, Jiang Tian knew about the case Bai Qiu had been investigating recently, or rather, the case that the provincial department had been monitoring.

Jiang Tian didn't know much about this case; the only thing he vaguely remembered was that it was a drug trafficking case involving foreign countries.

They had connections with criminal gangs on the Korean Peninsula. During Bai Qiu's raid, these gangs suddenly appeared, heavily armed, and, in a crucial moment, outmaneuvered Bai Qiu and his team, ultimately surrounding and eliminating them.

The operation resulted in heavy losses for the team, and Bai Qiu himself fell into their hands.

After Jiang Tian left, Minister Tan immediately issued orders.

This time, he summoned all the branch bureau chiefs of Yunhai, as well as anti-drug leaders, and members of the armed police, among others—a large number of people.

Operational personnel, investigators, reconnaissance personnel, and so on. Minister Tan's purpose here was clearly not simply to hold a meeting.

"Chaos also represents opportunity—an opportunity to make a name for yourself, an opportunity to safeguard social security. Although there is danger, I believe that from the moment you joined, you will not be cowards."

This wasn't just empty rhetoric from Minister Tan; he genuinely believed it.

While things might be manageable in inland areas, being an anti-drug police officer in Yunhai is a truly risky and high-risk profession.

Here, every day, all sorts of drug dealers come and go, almost all with various means of transportation.

They are frantically smuggling drugs every day using all sorts of methods.

Moreover, as the saying goes, when a force disappears, the market doesn't disappear; instead, it becomes more chaotic because the force that governs the market has disappeared—this is the great chaos before great order.

The same applies to Yunhai.

When a large number of drug lords are arrested and large quantities of heroin are eradicated, the market for addicts doesn't disappear, nor will it.

In fact, due to stricter enforcement and more difficult transportation, a state of scarcity will emerge.

Prices will then skyrocket.

“Under these circumstances, the entire Yun Province will face a severe test, and Yunhai is of paramount importance. Director Xu, Director Shen, and General Manager Bai are here because of this. Next, the 21st Bureau will take the lead in addressing the foreseeable counterattack from drug lords. Therefore, the 21st Bureau will primarily focus on severely cracking down on smuggling crimes in Yunhai. Furthermore, a portion of police force will be deployed to establish an airtight border blockade.” Minister Tan's words were shocking.

These words immediately caused an uproar in the room.

The relationship with the neighbors has always been like this.

Furthermore, doesn't the higher-ups know that almost half, if not more than half, of the crimes occurring in Yunhai and even Yunnan Province involve undocumented immigrants? This creates enormous difficulties for investigations and prosecutions, often leaving these types of cases unsolved.

But even if they knew, why hasn't there been any action taken before?

First, there's the issue of the international situation.

Second, it would have a significant impact on the power structure of the neighboring region, making them more prone to unrest.

Third, stationing troops on the border would greatly damage the overall image of Yunhai and affect future investments.

After all, think about it, which company would dare to invest in a controlled area?

But without strict control, smuggling persists.

Some even openly smuggle in through checkpoints, hiding drugs inside vehicle rims, tires, and even frames; some even go so far as to smuggle drugs into the fuel tank.

A large portion was seized on the spot, but a significant amount was still smuggled in through various means.

“After successively dismantling large-scale drug manufacturing plants, many criminals will inevitably take the risk. In response to this counterattack, the 21st Bureau, with the participation of the Provincial and Municipal Bureaus, will implement stricter controls on border smuggling and illegal immigration. Furthermore, a temporary task force, tentatively designated as a provincial-level unit, will be established, with Xu Poye as the leader, Bai Zong as the deputy leader, and the heads of various branch bureaus as clerks. During this period, all branch bureaus in Yunhai must unconditionally cooperate and obey command.”

Minister Tan then issued the operational orders.

The world has changed.

Jiang Tian, ​​back in his seat, couldn't help but click his tongue.

“The world has changed. The higher-ups have finally made up their minds!”

Beside him, Gao Yangyang couldn't help but click her tongue as well.

During this time, Gao Yangyang also learned about the situation in Yunhai. When the two were alone before, Gao Yangyang had asked Jiang Tian this question: why, knowing about the border issue, had they remained indifferent? Jiang Tian had given her a detailed explanation.

However, he also understood that the higher-ups probably wanted to press their advantage and completely eradicate all illegal activities and drug traffickers, or at least severely weaken them. Jiang Tian's attitude, however, was rather pessimistic.

"This is just treating the symptoms, not the root cause. The border is so long; there will always be those who slip through the net. Besides, this number of people is negligible," Jiang Tian shook his head, unconvinced.

"So many big shots have come. How many criminals are there in Yunhai?" Gao Yangyang's heart sank. Even with the higher-ups taking it seriously, it still didn't work?

Jiang Tian remained silent. He knew what his superiors were thinking, but unfortunately, Yunhai was truly different from other places. This plan was destined to fail. Such a large-scale operation clearly indicated that the higher-ups wanted to wipe them out completely, but smuggling in Yunhai was impossible to stop unless all channels between the two sides were completely severed.

"Why are you so agitated?" Jiang Tian asked.

Gao Yangyang, who had been eager to make a big splash, froze, her gaze shifting to Jiang Tian ingratiatingly, "I'm back in my post now, as the assistant to the bureau chief. Brother Jiang, do you have any good case leads to share?"

"There really is one..."



Chapter 499 Breaking Through Extraordinary Skills Again? No, I Want to Break Through World-Class Extraordinary Skills!


"You seem to have known about this all along?" Jiang Tian said unmoved.

What a joke.

Although he had that kind of relationship with Gao Yangyang, their relationship was still quite delicate, shrouded in a veil. Besides, for Jiang Tian, ​​a woman couldn't shake his path of fighting crime and pursuing power.

"I only found out before I came. I was planning to go all out, but you poured cold water on my plans before I even started." Gao Yangyang looked instantly dejected.

That's really true.

Actually, Gao Yangyang's personality was incredibly high in emotional intelligence. If it weren't for that, she wouldn't have gone undercover in the first place.

As for her crime-solving abilities, Jiang Tian thought they were just so-so.

Drug enforcement isn't about blindly rushing in. Many cases require keen insight to spot fleeting clues, and it's the kind of game of wits and courage with drug dealers.

On the other hand, it often requires criminal investigation skills.

Gao Yangyang was clearly lacking in that area.

"If you're looking for something, there is indeed a case you could participate in," Jiang Tian said, changing the subject.

"Really?" Gao Yangyang's face lit up with surprise.

There's a saying that goes, once you've tasted the sweetness, you'll understand how much you crave it.

Just like Gao Yangyang, after infiltrating abroad, Jiang Tian rescued her and conveniently eliminated Tucha, earning a Special Merit, while she also received a First-Class Merit.

That's right, because of this, Gao Yangyang was directly promoted and is now serving as the assistant director, which is the director of the General Bureau, a position of great power and influence.

If assigned in the future, she could easily be promoted another level in rank and position.

Jiang Tian being a second-level police inspector in his twenties is outrageous, but Gao Yangyang being a third-level police inspector at twenty-six is ​​also quite outrageous.

"That's right," Jiang Tian's eyes gleamed.

The last collaboration went very smoothly, and when Jiang Tian prepared to go to the Windmill Country, he took Gao Yangyang with him.

The dark web is now the top priority; everything else is secondary.

This is especially true for the mastermind, Hong Shiwu. Jiang Tian returned to the city police station and briefly inquired, only to find that the station was making astonishing progress.

Under Minister Tan's personal supervision, they had begun to implement Hong Shiwu's plans. Unfortunately, Hong Shiwu had no intention of returning to China. According to Hong Xun's instructions, bringing it up would only arouse Hong Shiwu's suspicion.

Speed ​​was crucial.

As instructed by Hong Xun, the transaction with Hanxi had to be resolved beforehand; otherwise, Hong Shiwu would begin contacting those back home.

Fortunately, back home, at least in Yunhai, Xiang Tiannan had essentially taken control, and Jiang Tian had also managed to kill Hong Shiwu's planted cronies.

In other words, barring unforeseen circumstances, Jiang Tian would soon be leaving the country.

"However, this mission requires going abroad, to the Windmill Country. The journey will probably take three or four days round trip," Jiang Tian pondered.

"A cross-border mission?" Gao Yangyang asked, somewhat surprised.

Jiang Tian nodded.

"Is it a government-sponsored mission or one where we're hiding our identities?" Gao Yangyang pressed.

“Initially, we'll probably be hiding our identities,” Jiang Tian pondered.

Clearly, although the case hasn't started yet, the higher-ups' plan wouldn't involve openly contacting the Windmill Nation from the outset.

After all, if they did reveal it, wow, with Hong Shiwu's assets, those people would probably go crazy. They'd be lucky if they didn't drain his wealth immediately.

And right now, billions of US dollars in foreign exchange!

That's a significant influence on the country's development at this stage.

So, most likely, after arresting the suspects and gaining complete control, they'll then publicly announce the case, making contact with the remaining peripheral details.

It will definitely be a clandestine operation from the start.

Hiding identities?

Gao Yangyang frowned. These kinds of missions are usually extremely dangerous; once you go out, you almost certainly won't return.

The key is that even if you sacrifice yourself, you can't use your real identity, and even rescue would be difficult.

The last undercover mission left her with a huge psychological scar.

“You can think about it first. We'll probably be taking action after the meeting,” Jiang Tian replied.

“Okay.” Gao Yangyang did need to think about it.

While the two were talking, Minister Tan and his team finally finished their meeting.

The meeting mainly focused on a few key points. First, Jiang Tian's identity was revealed without reservation, and Jiang Tian had no objections.

In fact, it was very successful. A special merit award alone would silence everyone.

Second, it addressed the counterattack by criminals. Therefore, the heads of various branch bureaus would join the anti-drug task force and take turns investigating border smuggling, illegal activities, and other crimes.

Third, it summarized the upcoming operations and distributed operational tasks.

Recently, there have been too many major cases and too many minor details to handle.

Often, the collapse of a major case or criminal group is accompanied by a long period of surveillance, arrests, and wanted notices.

Too many fugitives have slipped through the net, and these all require focused follow-up and arrest.

The meeting was short, lasting only a little over an hour.

However, the impact was absolutely sensational.

Jiang Tian's rise to power was too brief, and due to the secrecy surrounding the anti-drug system, his name hadn't actually been leaked.

Aside from the criminal groups that had been constantly harmed and harbored deep resentment towards Jiang Tian, ​​even units like the branch bureau, except for a few leaders, hadn't openly publicized his activities. In reality, they didn't know much about Jiang Tian.

But now, they knew him completely.

It was precisely because of this understanding that things exploded.

Before this.

Whenever a first-class merit or higher was awarded, although the identity was kept secret, the news of a comrade being awarded a first-class merit was never concealed.

Therefore, recently, a series of first-class merits had been awarded, all to people from the municipal bureau, and later even special-class merits appeared, causing a huge uproar, with countless people speculating about who was receiving them.

And now the case is solved.

Seeing Jiang Tian's familiar face, those who were seeing or knowing him for the first time all erupted in shock.

An uproar erupted.

The moment the meeting adjourned, Jiang Tian was surrounded by a crowd.

"Captain Jiang, holy crap, a Special Merit Citation! How did he get that?"

"Captain Jiang looks so young! To achieve such an incredible feat at such a young age, what was I doing when I was his age?"

"It's infuriating to compare yourself to others!"

"What do you mean infuriating? Captain Jiang is my classmate! I'm a senior in college, in my internship, and he's already a Second-Level Police Inspector, while I'm still a trainee officer! Who can I complain to?"



Chapter 500 The Ordinary Jiang Student Drops Another Big Bomb


Just then, a voice suddenly rang out from the crowd.

Everyone present fell silent instantly, turning to look at the source of the voice. It was a similarly young man, his shoulder bearing the rank of probationary officer, indicating he was still in his probationary period and hadn't yet been promoted.

This was normal. Most police academy students begin their probationary period in their senior year. Generally, as long as their performance isn't particularly poor and they don't commit serious misconduct, they can usually be promoted to Third-Class Superintendent after about a year, by graduation.

But Third-Class Superintendent is just the beginning. To advance further, it's a step-by-step climb, with opportunities every three years. Miss one opportunity and wait another three years. But how many three-year periods does one have in a lifetime?

There's a limited pool of candidates, and so many are waiting for promotion, some even failing several times, waiting for over a decade. Why should a newcomer get a chance?

If you miss the first wave of promotions, becoming a Second-Class Superintendent won't be until at least six years later.

And further up, the difficulty doubles again.

The competition doubles again.

As for the promotion from Superintendent to Inspector, it's a qualitative leap; many people spend their entire careers stuck at this threshold.

Years of service are merely the most basic competitive ticket.

Therefore, their mentality collapsed.

But the one whose mentality exploded the most was undoubtedly Liu Zhong.

Liu Zhong's family was actually quite well-off, possessing considerable influence. When they were assigned to their positions after graduation, Liu Zhong was the only one in his entire class to join the General Police Department.

At that moment, everyone instantly understood Liu Zhong's hidden talent, so much so that many classmates constantly tried to reconnect with him after graduation.

Liu Zhong maintained a cool, aloof approach, always smiling at everyone, though in reality, he looked down on these classmates.

Although Liu Zhong joined the Narcotics Control Unit, which is inherently dangerous, as the saying goes, are there any small cases in the General Police Department? They're all major cases. Even from the very beginning, he was already ahead of everyone else.

With resources like these, just a little involvement in major cases could easily secure a position, and with a bit of luck, a special merit award would be a huge boost – these were his future promotion assets.

Now it seems insignificant, but ten or twenty years from now, the difference would be terrifyingly vast. Some classmates, no, most of them, would never become police inspectors.

But Liu Zhong stared at Jiang Tian, ​​dumbfounded.

Jiang Tian led the team in the last operation, achieving tremendous merit, but unfortunately, they were held back during the operation, preventing them from reaping the rewards.

But Liu Zhong knew that Jiang Tian had made a crucial contribution in that operation; he even heard shortly afterward that someone in the city bureau had awarded him another first-class merit.

At that time, he suspected Jiang Tian.

Liu Zhong could still handle Jiang Tian's special and first-class merit awards, but now, Liu Zhong stared longingly at his own probationary police rank, then at Jiang Tian's second-class police inspector rank.

Good heavens, this is unbelievable.

Second-level police inspector.

Recently, the provincial anti-drug squad planned a major operation, even involving overseas operations. Liu Zhong knew his opportunity had arrived.

He was eager to give it his all, hoping to earn significant credit in this operation and secure a promotion. With that credit, his first-stage promotion was practically guaranteed.

However, he hadn't expected to encounter someone even more terrifying—no, he already knew Jiang Tian was monstrous; he knew during their last joint operation, but back then Jiang Tian was only a first-level police superintendent!

Now, he was already a second-level police inspector. Don't underestimate the difference of just two ranks; in the police system, it's a terrifying difference of heaven and earth—a true meteoric rise.

Especially at Jiang Tian's position, Jiang Tian himself understood that future promotions would be extremely difficult.

Even this promotion was beyond Jiang Tian's expectations; he hadn't expected Minister Tan to give him such a terrifying surprise.

After all, at his age, a third-level police inspector was already absurd; a second-level inspector—was this going to create a terrifying history?

For Jiang Tian, ​​however, this wasn't necessarily a bad thing.

To others, this behavior would be like forcing growth, drawing everyone's attention. If any problem arose in handling the case, it could instantly cause a huge uproar, potentially ruining a genius.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​it was simply stress-free.

"Holy crap, just graduated this year?"

"Not even graduated yet? Is the world this crazy?"

"Special Merit Citation? I've never even seen a Special Merit Citation before! He got one before graduation?"

"Compared to this, I'm practically stupid. At this age, I was probably still doing odd jobs. Comparison is the thief of joy."

Jiang Tian wasn't a naive fool when it came to those around him who fawned over him. He handled social interactions well, always wearing a smile, unchanging towards everyone.

In fact, Jiang Tian always adhered to one principle: no matter how strong or invincible he was, unnecessarily making enemies was a brainless act.

Having invincible strength and a large circle of friends—wouldn't that make one virtually flawless?

However, Jiang Tian didn't have much time to talk to these people right now. He only gave a few perfunctory replies before the intersection was quickly pushed open.

Minister Tan, accompanied by his assistant, walked silently.

He stopped abruptly beside Jiang Tian, ​​and the surrounding area fell silent at his arrival.

"Little Jiang, you haven't had breakfast yet. Come with me to the cafeteria later, I'll treat you. I also want to talk to you about something." Minister Tan warmly placed his hands on Jiang Tian's shoulders from behind, then familiarly pushed him away.

Meanwhile, at the scene...

BOOM!!!

It exploded.

After Minister Tan and Jiang Tian left, everyone at the scene erupted in shock.

At this moment, everyone was stunned, then stared at Jiang Tian with utter disbelief.

Especially Liu Zhong.

He felt a chill run down his spine.

You know who that is, it's Minister Tan!

Within the system, including policing, there's a rule: everyone has two faces—one on stage, one off stage.

This applies to every leader. For example, no matter how many mistakes or transgressions a lower-level employee makes, a higher-level leader will always maintain a gentle smile and show no disapproval.

However, if you think your leader is too kind, you're sorely mistaken. When this high-ranking leader is out of sight, your immediate superior is in deep trouble.

Similarly, on stage, praising Jiang Tian and encouraging him for completing the task well is understandable. But why are you two so familiar with each other offstage?

This is Minister Tan!

Liu Zhong almost vomited blood watching this scene. Back in school, that brat Wang Hao said Jiang Tian was just an ordinary, worthless piece of trash, and Liu Zhong believed him wholeheartedly.

And now, you're telling me he's just an ordinary piece of trash?!

Liu Zhong truly regretted not cultivating a good relationship with Jiang Tian back in school. Although he hadn't offended him, he had now lost his best chance to gain Jiang Tian's friendship.

Liu Zhong took out his phone and snapped a photo of Minister Tan and Jiang Tian's backs. In the photo, the two looked like longtime friends. He then posted it to their classmates' group chat: 【Congratulations to Jiang Tian on his promotion to Second-Level Police Inspector! Minister Tan personally invited him to dinner. Please protect him!】

"???"



Chapter 501: Pressure Enough to Destroy a Genius, Can You Bear It?


"Second-level Police Inspector?"

"???"

"Minister Tan, which Minister Tan? It can't be the Minister Tan I'm thinking of, right?"

Liu Zhong: "That's right, the Minister Tan you're all imagining."

After he finished speaking, the entire chat group exploded.

Everyone was sending messages in rapid succession.

It's insane.

Yes, it's insane.

This world is too crazy, especially with people they used to know and who were once mediocre. Many classmates were completely devastated.

You have to understand, everyone in this group, all the classmates, were interns at the same time as Jiang Tian. Those with connections might be in the provincial department, those with weaker connections might be in various localities.

Of course, most of them were assigned jobs.

The situation in Yunhai is different from other places. Other parts of the mainland are relatively peaceful, with fewer cases and less demanding work, but the downside is that opportunities for meritorious service are extremely rare.

Yunhai is known for its high volume of cases, dangerous and busy work, but also offers a significant opportunity for meritorious service. Therefore, most students with even a modicum of ambition choose to stay in Yunhai. However, unfortunately, not everyone can stay in Yunhai simply because they want to.

It's like graduating from medical school and being assigned to a top-tier provincial hospital – is it likely that the average student will be accepted?

Even if they are, most will leave for smaller hospitals after their internship.

The same rule applies to police academy graduates; unless you are exceptionally outstanding, you must abide by it.

Undoubtedly, the Municipal Public Security Bureau and the Provincial Public Security Department are the two most coveted departments for all police academy students.

In fact, some of this batch of students chose to become narcotics officers partly due to mandatory assignment, and partly because other departments wouldn't accept them, leaving only narcotics as an option.

Those who couldn't stay at the Municipal Public Security Bureau mostly chose to remain in branch offices rather than go to other prefecture-level cities.

There's no other way; Yunhai offers too many opportunities, and being the provincial capital, no one wants to leave if given a choice.

Jiang Tian's reputation had already spread in the classmates' group.

They were already shocked at first, but little did they know, that was just the beginning.

"Wang Hao, didn't you say Jiang Tian was just some ordinary good-for-nothing from an ordinary background?"

"Damn, I got fooled! I never expected Jiang Tian to be a hidden big shot."

"My god, is it really Minister Tan? He's so close to Minister Tan?"

Liu Zhong: "More than that! Minister Tan just personally promoted Jiang Tian to Second-Level Police Inspector. Now Jiang Tian is the captain of the First Brigade of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau's Narcotics Control Detachment. And all the leaders have high hopes for Jiang Tian. He's about to take off! Please protect him!"

Yes.

Liu Zhong wasn't just watching Minister Tan; he was also watching Jiang Tian on the platform, chatting intimately with all the leaders there, exchanging pleasantries with each one.

Clearly, most of them seemed to be acquaintances of Jiang Tian.

This completely infuriated Liu Zhong.

Especially for Liu Zhong, there was the unattainable superior of his superior's superior's superior...

For example, General Manager Bai had a terrifying reputation throughout the entire provincial department.

For example, Director Shen's smile towards Jiang Tian was incredibly friendly, even to the point that Liu Zhong witnessed Jiang Tian withdrawing his hand, yet Shen Han still wouldn't let go.

Please, you're Director Shen!

How can you act like this?

It's only because Shen Han doesn't know what this guy is thinking, otherwise he would have slapped him already. What a joke! Now, Shen Han is absolutely afraid to offend Jiang Tian, ​​even going so far as to frantically curry favor.

In front of Jiang Tian, ​​Shen Han doesn't dare to put on airs in the slightest, because Shen Han desperately wants to advance his career.

But advancement requires conditions. Shen Han's former boss is Secretary Mu, but Secretary Mu and Jiang Tian have a very close relationship. He knows that Secretary Mu's granddaughter has a close relationship with Jiang Tian, ​​and Secretary Mu values ​​Jiang Tian highly.

His biggest backer has a terrifyingly close relationship with Jiang Tian.

Furthermore, his direct superiors at headquarters, Minister Tan and Chief Gao, also have very close relationships with Jiang Tian.

Good heavens, these two can both decide Shen Han's future!

They're practically surrounding Shen Han from all sides.

So now, when Shen Han sees Jiang Tian, ​​he even calls him "Young Master Jiang" in private. Regardless of what others think, Jiang Tian is secretly terrified.

Of course, Jiang Tian ignored the shocked expressions of the people behind him.

As Minister Tan dragged him away, Jiang Tian casually pulled Gao Yangyang along. However, Gao Yangyang followed silently, head down, acting like she was invisible. Clearly, Gao Yangyang has good emotional intelligence; she knows when she can speak and when she has no right to speak, just like now, when she's following her grandfather, Chief Gao.

Chief Gao followed slightly behind Minister Tan and Shen Han, about half a step away.

"Comrade Jiang, we apologize for exposing you this time without informing you beforehand. We know the pressure on you has increased dramatically, which is unfair to you. But given the current situation in Yunhai, we have no choice but to make you sacrifice yourself temporarily," Minister Tan apologized, trying to comfort him on the way.

The apology was sincere, so sincere that even Jiang Tian couldn't handle it.

Isn't this a good thing?

But Jiang Tian quickly realized why his superiors were acting this way.

Clearly, on the surface, exposing Jiang Tian was like creating an invincible golden image for him, even shaping him into a mythical figure. They wanted to make Jiang Tian a calling card for Yunhai.

In short, it was about creating a god.

And clearly, Jiang Tian perfectly fit the criteria for being a god.

However, without the exposure, Jiang Tian had much less pressure and it was much easier to do things. But after the exposure...

Everything seemed to be under the spotlight, seemingly infinitely glamorous.

But consider Jiang Tian's age—just over twenty. He's already achieved so much under pressure and danger, and his continued advancement is already a surprise to his superiors.

Being exposed to the spotlight, with every move scrutinized by countless people, the pressure on a young person, especially someone so young, is unimaginable.

One wrong move could easily destroy a genius.

"I know it's very unfair to you, and the pressure is immense, but after numerous meetings, our headquarters and higher-ups have ultimately decided to trust you. Because you've never let us down, and this time is no exception. We've seen all the contributions you've made," Minister Tan said directly, without any concealment.

Minister Tan knew that hiding anything from Jiang Tian was futile, because he already knew how terrifying Jiang Tian was through Shang Mingyan.



Chapter 502 The Horrific Plan: Stepping into the Spotlight, Stepping into the World


From the moment Minister Tan met Shang Mingyan today, he sensed something was amiss. This woman was seriously off. Minister Tan couldn't quite put his finger on it before, but after his detailed questioning, Shang Mingyan finally revealed that besides the awe she felt from Jiang Tian's crime-solving abilities,

Jiang Tian's expertise in psychology and other fields was even more terrifying than hers. In Jiang Tian's presence, she was like a greenhorn.

The proud Shang Mingyan even uttered this statement.

She went on to say that in their psychological battle, she was utterly crushed. She couldn't see through Jiang Tian, ​​yet he had completely exposed her, inside and out, leaving her utterly exposed.

During this time, while chatting, what made Shang Mingyan most embarrassed and vulnerable was when, during their exchange on the sofa, she slightly raised one side of her buttocks. Jiang Tian bluntly stated, "From a psychological and physiological perspective, with almost 100% certainty, suddenly raising your buttocks during a pleasant conversation is like secretly farting."

Shang Mingyan was completely devastated by this remark.

Sincerity has always been known to hurt, and now Shang Mingyan felt a mixture of love and hate towards Jiang Tian.

But being less skilled was undeniable, and Shang Mingyan wouldn't hide Jiang Tian's abilities.

This is what shocked Minister Tan, because he was well aware of Shang Mingyan's formidable abilities and profound knowledge of psychology, yet she felt inferior to Jiang Tian.

However, precisely because of this, Minister Tan and the higher-ups made a decision: with such strong psychological skills, surely Jiang Tian could handle this kind of pressure.

"Hey, with great power comes great responsibility. I have the ability, so this is what I should do," Jiang Tian said modestly.

"Xiao Jiang, you're good in every way, but you're a bit too modest. Sometimes too much of a good thing is bad. We've all seen your excellence. We didn't plan to put you in the spotlight so early; it seemed like we were pushing you too hard. But now it seems plans can't keep up with changes. Your abilities are constantly exceeding our expectations. We all agree that you're capable of handling this pressure," Gao Xiaolong suddenly said.

After saying this, without giving Jiang Tian a chance to speak, Minister Tan sighed and said, "Xiao Jiang, regarding the dark web, the higher-ups have made a decision. They've decided to secretly arrest Hong Shiwu and bring him back to China before contacting Windmill. I know this is a bit demanding, but you know how important billions of US dollars in foreign exchange are to our country at this stage."

Soft drugs may not be strictly prohibited in some Western countries, but heroin is absolutely prohibited everywhere and in every country.

Hong Shiwu's case is an absolutely major case of global significance.

Most importantly...

"If we succeed, we can use this case to stabilize the situation in Yunhai," Jiang Tian said, seeing through the situation immediately.

Minister Tan's face showed an appreciative expression as he said, "That's right. Once you are successfully apprehended, we will immediately arrange for an evening news reporter to conduct an exclusive interview with you. Moreover, through this case, we plan to launch you onto the world stage."

*Hiss!*

Jiang Tian's expression changed.

Minister Tan's words caused Jiang Tian's face to shift repeatedly.

Even Jiang Tian hadn't anticipated this step. It seemed the higher-ups were planning three steps ahead, or rather, the decision made at today's meeting had already predetermined what was to come.

Since the new era, the motherland has entered a phase of rapid development, but it still lags far behind in terms of public recognition.

Especially now, it leaves an impression of backwardness on both the West and developed countries.

Without a doubt.

This isn't creating a god of the cloud sea; this is creating a world-class god.

Jiang Tian naturally couldn't object to this.

This was a responsibility, and even more so, a terrifying pressure. Insufficient ability meant bringing shame to the motherland on the international stage; lacking real strength meant truly bringing shame abroad. And undoubtedly, once the reputation was established, all sorts of powerful figures would jump out to express their dissatisfaction and challenge him.

Ordinary people, even those with ability, would probably find it difficult to withstand this terrifying pressure.

But sorry.

For Jiang Tian, ​​pressure has always been a source of motivation; he's never known what pressure is.

If he were truly afraid of pressure, he wouldn't need to do anything; he could just lie down and relax.

"Pack your things and get moving as soon as possible. Whomever you need, how many you need, we can accommodate you. This time you're traveling by commercial airline, so you can't reveal your identities," Minister Tan inquired.

"I do need two people over there. Just Yangyang, Lü Chun, and Zhao Wei—that's enough. Too many would be inconvenient," Jiang Tian had already decided on his manpower.

"Are these enough?" Minister Tan asked, somewhat worried.

"They're enough," Jiang Tian explained.

"Are you sure you don't need to find a translator who understands the local language to accompany you?" Chief Gao reminded him, his face darkening.

He thought Jiang Tian was so nervous that he'd forgotten something so important. They were unfamiliar with the area, and the language was different—what kind of capture would they make?

How could this mission proceed?

"It's alright, I understand," Jiang Tian replied, his words surprising.

"Actually, I forgot to mention, I'm fluent in eight languages, including Windmill Language," Jiang Tian explained casually.

*Whoosh!*

Minister Tan and Chief Gao were completely dumbfounded.

[Consuming 11,100 Sin Points]

[Ding, Initial Understanding of Eight Languages ​​(White) - Hemisphere Expert (Red)] (You have successfully upgraded from knowing only simple colloquialisms in eight languages ​​to becoming proficient in all major global languages ​​and most non-major languages. Except for some extremely rare regional dialects and few languages, you are now more fluent in their languages ​​than the native speakers. You have a thorough understanding of 95% of the languages ​​used by humanity.)

After adding points, Jiang Tian was unsurprised. This skill point was actually quite funny. It was revealed by one of the college students Jiang Tian arrested at Yunnan University. One of the students frequently watched foreign movies and TV shows, and over time, while he didn't learn anything else, he could say a few swear words in eight languages.

There aren't many, at most just one or two sentences.

And they're all swear words, like "shiba," "baka," "fuck," etc.

But who says understanding just one sentence can't result in an entry?

In short, Jiang Tian was surprised to discover this skill entry. It just so happened that he had already foreseen that if the plan went according to plan, he would likely have to deal with foreigners frequently in the future, and foreign languages ​​would come in handy at this point.

After equipping the skill entry, Jiang Tian was suddenly overwhelmed by countless memories, as if they were embedded in his body. They were incredibly natural, far more fluent than many locals, even surpassing their language skills by a great deal.

“Damn, is there anything you can’t do?” Minister Tan asked, completely bewildered.

No.

It’s just that Jiang Tian is always so incredibly capable, seemingly omnipotent and perfect, to the point that even Minister Tan finds it hard to believe.

Why does the world have such talent? Oh, you mean our police system? Never mind then.

“Windmill Language isn’t a mainstream language, and you know it?” Gao Yangyang asked, her face full of shock.

This wasn’t just empty talk; she was genuinely shocked.

Windmill Language is a minority language; many locals even speak English. Why was she so skeptical?

"Isn't this normal?" Jiang Tian replied in windmill language.



Chapter 503 A Master Strategist's Counterattack Against Jiang Tian? The Mysterious Confidence of a Tycoon


The Land of Windmills.

This is a country that appears economically highly developed, particularly in electronics, chemicals, water conservancy, shipbuilding, and agriculture.

However, no matter how developed it is, it cannot conceal its chaotic nature, nor its inherent evil.

Beneath this developed economy lies a dark world of unparalleled terror, where horrific crimes occur daily.

And here,

a vast number of criminals from around the world find a peaceful retirement.

The overall environment is excellent.

However, the officialdom is extremely corrupt, with bureaucratic collapse creating a chaotic and declining society. The most obvious consequence is that when certain powerful figures come here to retire, they naturally seek security.

Especially individuals and organizations, almost all establish gangs here, to ensure they have their own power base for retirement.

On the other hand, the beautiful scenery, relatively developed society, and corrupt bureaucracy have made this a retirement paradise for criminals, their preferred destination.

And it was in this environment, on this day, that the overcast sky was filled with a light drizzle, like a cool breeze blowing in the cooler heat of summer, yet it still drew crowds of tourists to the streets.

Indeed, besides being a retirement paradise, this place is also a tourist hotspot, a beautiful and scenic area, and a highly developed destination.

In fact, the authorities in Fengche warmly welcome those bigwigs who come to retire, and sometimes even provide them with conveniences.

Nothing lasts forever, only interests. These bigwigs from the underworld often bring substantial funds and assets to invest in Fengche when they retire. Moreover, these bigwigs don't cause trouble after retirement, unlike their former gangster friends who are prone to violence. They are essentially retired, bringing money and investing in the local area, and they don't cause trouble.

As a result, more and more retired bigwigs are choosing to retire here, and their reputation has spread naturally.

Hong Shiwu is one such example.

In the brightly lit office, beside a huge floor-to-ceiling window, Hong Shiwu stood with his back to the door and his henchmen behind him, his gaze lingering on the bustling street below. The first impression was of a prosperous and beautiful place.

Hong Shiwu's favorite thing to do every day was to sit in this office, watching the masses below, which always brought him pleasure.

But today, Hong Shiwu's extremely square face and seemingly upright eyes held an astonishing wisdom. If he were outside, no one would believe that this man was a criminal; rather, he resembled a scholar, exuding a refined air. His eyes gave the impression of a clever scientist, brimming with intelligence.

At this moment, Hong Shiwu, gazing silently at the masses below, stared at his phone, lost in thought.

After an unknown amount of time...

A female voice sounded behind Hong Shiwu: "Boss, should we go for the rescue now?"

"Rescue? How? Go to Yunhai to rescue him? Don't you know that Tucha lost his life trying to rescue his own brother?" Hong Shiwu's voice was calm.

Standing behind Hong Shiwu, Su Su, for some reason, felt a sudden calm in her anxious heart upon hearing his voice. It seemed to possess an astonishing magic—no, perhaps it was Hong Shiwu's personal charisma.

"From a small mountain village, I, Hong Shiwu, have risen step by step to where I am today. I relied on no one but myself. I single-handedly created Fishbuy.com, built the business into an ever-expanding empire, and created this vast dark empire. He, a mere brat, thinks he can destroy my empire? He's not qualified." Hong Shiwu suddenly exuded an icy aura of dominance.

That's right, unworthy.

In reality, many people underestimated Hong Shiwu, or rather, lacked sufficient understanding of this international drug lord.

This is a true, global, terrifying drug lord, with assets worth billions of dollars. Did Hong Shiwu rise from nothing to where he is now through brute force or luck?

That's all nonsense. It's all thanks to Hong Shiwu's intelligence and intellect.

Or rather, Hong Shiwu received the news the moment Jiang Tian attacked the YH bar. Not only did he receive the news, but he even gathered all the details.

A moment later.

Hong Shiwu swiveled his chair to face his desk. Through the bright light from the large floor-to-ceiling windows, a document could be seen on the desk.

If Jiang Tian were here, he would definitely recognize him.

The person in this photo is him. Not only him, but the document also contains detailed information about Jiang Tian.

"This Jiang Tian probably never dreamed that I was already paying attention to him when Wen Yulin was arrested," Hong Shiwu sneered.

That's right.

Hong Shiwu had been gathering information on Jiang Tian since he arrested Wen Yulin. Hong Xun's arrest was an unexpected turn of events for him.

He hadn't expected Jiang Tian to find out so easily.

He had only intended to sever ties with Yunhai; killing Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang, who knew the inside story, would have been enough.

But why?

"Susu, how many years have you been with me?" Hong Shiwu suddenly looked up at the person opposite him.

A woman around thirty years old stood in front of the desk and quickly replied, "Boss, sixteen years. I've been working for you since I was eighteen."

"Yes, it's taken me sixteen years to go from nothing to where I am now, and now some greenhorn in open-crotch clothes wants to wipe me out." Hong Shiwu seemed to be lamenting to himself.

The file on the desk had a special note on the age: twenty-two.

A twenty-two-year-old greenhorn, who was lucky enough to solve some major cases, cannot be denied that he has the ability. However, Hong Shiwu never thought he would lose, because over the years, he has faced far too many genius opponents, but without exception, he has outsmarted and defeated them all.

"Boss, what do we do now? Young Master's crimes are probably enough to get him executed," Su Su said anxiously.

"What are you afraid of? That kid will confess eventually if he can't withstand the interrogation. But it's okay, this was just a smokescreen I threw out. I even suspect that kid has probably already arrived in Windmill Country."

Hong Shiwu stood up confidently and sneered.

But those words instantly made Su Su's mind reel, not far away.

Cold-blooded.

Su Su felt a chill run through her body. She'd been with him for sixteen years, but only now did she truly grasp the extent of Hong Shiwu's coldness and lack of emotion.

He'd even schemed against his own son.

Was all of this a calculated scheme?

"Someone wants to use my brain to establish their authority, but don't I also want to use someone else's brain to establish my authority? A recipient of the Special Merit Award, interesting. A twenty-two-year-old recipient of the Special Merit Award—that's worthy of being a trophy for me to collect." Hong Shiwu's words grew increasingly sinister and terrifying.



Chapter 504 A Prodigy Scientist? Hero Saves the Beauty


Cold-blooded and ruthless?

Hong Shiwu never cared about such comments; they were a joke. If he were truly loyal and righteous, he would have died countless times over.

“Special Merit, twenty-two years old?” Su Su was shocked.

“That’s right.” Hong Shiwu’s lips curled into a smile.

“Spread this news. Also, have people keep an eye on all the airports. Report immediately if Jiang Tian is spotted, and mobilize manpower to go and entertain him as soon as possible. After all, no matter what, we should show him the proper hospitality.” Hong Shiwu’s voice gradually turned cold.

“Yes!!!” Su Su hurriedly left.

After Su Su left, Hong Shiwu turned around and looked at the bustling traffic below. In this land of windmills, one thing had to be said: due to various restrictions on cars, very few people actually drove.

Even though it was quite developed, most people still rode bicycles.

The whole society gave off a sense of complacency. Otherwise, why would this be the first choice for many bigwigs to retire to? It was because the living environment here was so good.

Aside from the slightly higher prices, none of that mattered to the retired bigwigs.

It was around the time Hong Shiwu was about to take action.

Jiang Tian was still flying in the sky. After landing, he looked around at his surroundings, much like Zhao Wei on his first trip abroad—everything was new and exciting. He looked left and right.

“Boss, where do we go next?” Zhao Wei asked.

Mainly, surrounded by blond-haired, blue-eyed people, Zhao Wei felt lost. Being in an unfamiliar place, if he were to run away now, he might easily get lost.

“Let’s find a place to get some weapons first,” Jiang Tian said after a moment’s thought.

That’s right. After all, they needed to go through security checks. Actually, there are only twenty-four countries in the world that allow citizens to legally own guns, one of which is the United States.

That’s quite a high number. The country is already a source of various problems, and then they loosened gun ownership, resulting in widespread social chaos.

However, because of the security checks and the need to maintain identity confidentiality, firearms could not be carried.

Because carrying firearms requires various official documents for legal transport, Jiang Tian and his group were using fake identities.

The three men and one woman walked together, dressed very casually. Gao Yangyang even clung excitedly to Jiang Tian's arm, skipping and jumping as they left the airport, clearly treating it like a vacation.

"Where's the boss going to get weapons?" Zhao Wei scratched his head, feeling his scalp glowing; the feeling of being a third wheel was getting stronger.

But it had to be said.

Looking at the surrounding environment and the large crowd—yes, a large crowd—

Many tourists come this season. Despite the hot summer, the atmosphere in Windmill Country was surprisingly pleasant.

This made Gao Yangyang sigh, "No wonder so many people like to travel here. Even some people who have retired from crime like to come here to retire. The environment is indeed nice, and the pace of life seems quite slow."

However, the next moment, Jiang Tian sneered, "You said it yourself, it's just a feeling."

"What's wrong?" Gao Yangyang looked up and asked.

They discovered that, unknowingly, they had followed Jiang Tian off the main road and onto a secondary road, a road with very few people.

Moreover, this road was more like an alley than a secondary road.

As the four of them entered,

“There’s a smell.” Gao Yangyang coughed, choked by the odor.

“Damn, in broad daylight, are these people crazy?” Zhao Wei frowned slightly.

They were all in the drug enforcement field, and they were all too familiar with this smell.

“It’s drugs.” Jiang Tian glanced at it and frowned slightly.

“What do we do? Should we take a detour?” Gao Yangyang complained.

Damn it.

To be honest, they hadn’t been abroad much, but as drug enforcement officers, this was the first time they’d seen people using this stuff and having to take a detour.

It just goes to show that anything can happen abroad.

However, their identities made it even more difficult to intervene in this kind of situation.

Jiang Tian hadn’t expected to cause so much trouble, but after taking just one step, he suddenly stopped.

Just then, Jiang Tian's ears twitched slightly.

"What are you doing? Don't come any closer..."

It was a cry for help, but what made Jiang Tian stop was that the cry was spoken in Mandarin.

This caused Jiang Tian to abruptly halt his turn, and he quickly rushed into the alley.

The three men following Jiang Tian, ​​Lü Chun and Zhao Wei, saw his actions and, though confused, rushed in after him.

Actually, while Jiang Tian was acting...

Inside the alley, Li Qingqing, pale-faced, retreated. After reaching a corner, she looked at the three men before her with despair.

They all reeked of a nauseating stench. Others might not have noticed, but Li Qingqing, coincidentally a chemistry student, was all too familiar with the smell, especially since she had some knowledge of it. However, by the time she realized what was happening, it was too late; she was already deep in the alley.

As a top student at Wudaokou High School, and a terrifyingly high achiever at that, she was even on exchange with students from Fengche High School during her junior year. Everything had been peaceful until now.

She rarely even went out.

Tonight, hearing that many tourists from all over the world were visiting, and since she hadn't been out and about for a while, she went out with a friend—her first time venturing out at night.

As it got late, Li Qingqing, wanting to get back to school, took a shortcut along a secondary road. It was actually just a quiet alley, but upon entering, she saw several menacing individuals, reeking of a nauseating odor, blocking her way from both sides.

As a top-performing exchange student, she found it hard to believe that the night abroad could be so dangerous.

There were so many people outside the alley, yet the people inside were committing a crime.

"Don't come any closer! I've already called the police," Li Qingqing said, clutching her phone.

But the three men smiled mockingly.

Instead of backing down, they moved even closer after hearing that the police had been called.

One of them continued spewing insults.

"Hahaha, Qin Qiongqiong..." The three exchanged glances, their insults growing increasingly emboldened.

Although Li Qingqing quickly recounted the events in English and Mandarin after realizing what had happened, the three men had lost all fear.

*Smack!*

One of them flung Li Qingqing's phone away.

The three men's smiles were almost manic; clearly, they had been so high they were nearly out of their minds.

This made Li Qingqing's face grow even paler. She thought of her ambitions, of the relentless pursuit of knowledge she had put into her studies.

Would her achievements in basic chemistry be wasted, before she could even put them to use, ending up silently dead here?

Even if they found out later, what difference would it make?

In this country with less than 20 million people, so many die every day; who would care about a foreign student?

Li Qingqing looked at the three men, drawing ever closer, her face filled with utter despair.

But just as she was in despair...

Just as one of them reached out to touch her...

She saw a light.

Was it an angel?

Li Qingqing's eyes were blurry; the dim light reflected a tall, slender figure that seemed frozen in her vision.



Chapter 505 Instant Kill, Shocking Li Qingqing: "Mom, I saw a god!"


Boom!!!

Just as Li Qingqing was watching, the drugged thug stood before her, getting closer and closer, almost touching her.

He truly descended from the sky.

Jiang Tian's running speed was such that even an Olympic champion would be left in his dust. He had already surpassed human limits several times over.

You could even say that the superhero Captain America from across the Atlantic would only be able to go toe-to-toe with Jiang Tian. Of course, that's just a fictional character; Jiang Tian's true strength had reached this level, his physical abilities genuinely exceeding human limits, far beyond the realm of humanity.

Sometimes, Jiang Tian even wondered if his genes were no longer those of a normal human.

Running at a terrifying speed, from a distance of five or six meters, Jiang Tian shot out like an arrow.

So, in Li Qingqing's eyes, leaning against the wall, she watched helplessly as the nearest thug became her first target.

That kick landed squarely on the bandit's head.

Boom!!!

A terrifying sound rang out. The bandit almost instantly lost consciousness. Jiang Tian's whip-like kick, coming from nowhere, struck the bandit's temple, sending him flying without a second thought.

And in that instant...

Bang!!!

A deafening crash. The bandit's head slammed into the body of the bandit beside him. The moment their heads collided, blood spurted out instantly—literally spurting.

Blood splattered everywhere. In that instant, both bandits were gone in a flash.

Their heads collided without pause, both flying five or six meters before crashing heavily to the ground, completely unconscious.

Only their bodies twitched occasionally, seemingly from the intense pain, or perhaps from the body's primal instincts.

“Qin Qiangqiong, Qin Qiangqiong…”

The thug, still high on drugs, was spewing insults.

His face was contorted with madness, a hint of irrationality remaining, reduced to mere biological instinct, a brutal instinct.

Thump!!!

Just then, Jiang Tian's foot touched the ground, regaining his footing. The insulting words around him instantly filled him with a grimace.

This word was actually highly offensive and discriminatory, used only because it was difficult to pronounce.

Perhaps due to his high, the thug's reaction was slow. Only now, seeing Li Qingqing's unperturbed demeanor, did he suddenly remember hearing a sound beside him.

His reaction was sluggish; he looked around blankly, and when he saw the empty space beside him, a slight look of confusion appeared on his face.

He seemed to be wondering how his two companions had suddenly disappeared.

Huh!!!

Wait, why are there two people lying there?

His already sluggish brain seemed unable to grasp what was happening.

Unfortunately, no one answered his question. The next moment, he felt a sharp pain in his scalp, as if it were being ripped off, causing him to scream in agony.

But before he could even utter a sound, a searing pain shot through his mouth.

Gulp!!!

Blood, along with teeth, flew everywhere from Jiang Tian's punch, some of which the thug swallowed.

Before the thug could even regain consciousness, he felt the terrifying force gripping his scalp, pulling his head and slamming it against the wall at incredible speed.

"NONONO, Fuck, NONONO..." the thug screamed in agony, but the speed didn't stop.

Boom!!!

The horrific impact, blood splattering, left the thug limp and lifeless on the ground.

At this moment, Jiang Tian calmly reached out his hand. Zhao Wei, who had been waiting nearby, hurriedly pulled a wet wipe from his pocket and handed it to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian took the wipe, his expression calm, and carefully wiped his palms a few times.

This was a habit Jiang Tian had gradually developed, and those who frequently followed him had recently become completely accustomed to it.

Actually, it wasn't because Jiang Tian was a germaphobe; he wasn't. However, since his advancement to the non-human level, he could often see things that others couldn't, and smell odors that others couldn't, especially the foul stench emanating from criminals. This was torture for Jiang Tian; after any contact or physical altercation, he would immediately wipe his hands and nose to alleviate the smell.

The entire movement was fluid and elegant.

Li Qingqing was stunned.

Mythology?

No, kung fu?

What's the most comforting thing in life? That's right, it's meeting an old friend in a foreign land.

Seeing familiar faces from her hometown in this unfamiliar foreign land filled Li Qingqing with a sense of warmth, especially since he had saved her during this crisis. This was enough to make her completely lower her guard.

Even towards Jiang Tian, ​​she felt a strange connection.

The story of the hero rescuing the damsel in distress is cliché precisely because the trope is classic and therefore enduring.

“Mom, I saw a fairy!” Li Qingqing murmured, her face blank.

“Are you alright?” Jiang Tian asked, wiping his hands before looking at Li Qingqing.

At first glance, Jiang Tian was somewhat surprised. She was wearing a white lab coat, glasses, and appeared quite young. This attire surprised him. As for her face, although she was beautiful, Jiang Tian had seen too many beautiful women lately, so she didn't particularly stand out.

“A fellow townsman?” Li Qingqing was overjoyed when Jiang Tian spoke.

Just moments ago she wasn't sure, but now, Li Qingqing was absolutely certain.

"Thank you, thank you so much! Without you, I don't know what would have become of me." Li Qingqing looked at Jiang Tian with overwhelming emotion, as if she were a fangirl.

Her eyes were practically sparkling with admiration.

Being cornered by three high-spirited thugs who had lost all reason, Li Qingqing didn't know what terrible consequences would have occurred if Jiang Tian hadn't appeared.

But without a doubt, Li Qingqing knew she was in a terrible situation.

They were extremely grateful to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian, ​​after briefly learning about their situation, couldn't help but be astonished.

The same was true of the three people following Jiang Tian.

A top student.

This was a true top student!

To be able to obtain a government-sponsored scholarship to study abroad in Wudaokou proved that even in a school teeming with geniuses like Wudaokou, Li Qingqing was a genius among geniuses, one of the best.

"These bastards, damn it, luckily we arrived in time."

Zhao Wei looked at the three thugs who had been brought to the ground, his heart burning with anger. So close! A talent of the nation almost met with misfortune here.

Zhao Wei stretched out his foot and kicked the three thugs several times.



Chapter 506 He's gone mad, every move he makes kills. You say you're on a business trip?


"Don't believe how great things are outside, especially what's said online—it's all lies. Even if you go out, try to go out during the day. Don't go out at night, no matter how urgent the situation, especially in places like this; it's dangerous." Jiang Tian glanced into the alley.

Although shrouded in darkness, there were still quite a few drug addicts inside.

"Even if you don't think about yourselves, think about your country. How can you serve your country if something happens to you?" Jiang Tian added.

Hearing this, Li Qingqing looked terrified. "This was my first time going out at night, and I never expected to encounter something like this. I'll never do it again."

"By the way, what are you wearing?" Jiang Tian looked at Li Qingqing's white lab coat.

"I just came from the research lab at school. Today, I was so engrossed in a new research idea that I didn't notice the time. Once I develop this drug, it will be a terrifying feat. It can even achieve an effective sedative effect without side effects or addiction." Li Qingqing's face lit up with excitement as she mentioned her research.

It seemed like she'd finally met someone from her hometown, and she especially trusted Jiang Tian completely, like a child bursting with unspoken joy, eager to share.

Jiang Tian, ​​who was about to take his leave, suddenly stopped.

He understood the first part, but putting the rest together, Jiang Tian froze.

It was common knowledge that both painkillers and sedatives inevitably carried significant side effects; the stronger the effect, the more terrifying the side effects, and many were illegal substances.

But what if there were sedatives without side effects?

Wait a minute.

Jiang Tian suddenly remembered something.

“Could we use it on these addicts when they're experiencing withdrawal symptoms?” Jiang Tian asked abruptly.

“It should, maybe, be possible,” Li Qingqing said uncertainly.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian hadn't expected that the person he rescued abroad would possess such terrifying abilities.

This wasn't just a treasure, it was an absolute gem!

Most importantly, Jiang Tian suddenly realized that a drug with this ability, if it were to appear, would cause a huge sensation, perhaps even replacing 60-70% of painkillers and sedatives. Why hadn't it appeared in later generations?

There were only two possibilities.

One was that it failed, but it was clearly a success, so that's unlikely.

The other was that Li Qingqing had an accident tonight.

This was the most likely possibility. Thinking of this, Jiang Tian's anger flared, and he kicked the thug at his feet hard.

"Let's take you back to your place," Jiang Tian offered.

The mission could be put on hold for a while, but this talent, and definitely a national talent, absolutely had to be protected.

In fact, Jiang Tian could already imagine that even if the development of sedatives and painkillers failed, it could still be used to treat addicts during their addiction flare-ups.

Even if it couldn't completely eradicate addiction, even if it could alleviate it by 10-20%, it would still be a miracle drug.

Although it was impossible to reform a large number of addicts.

Jiang Tian knew perfectly well that expecting addicts to reform was like asking a sow to fly—a pipe dream. Even if they could completely suppress their addiction, getting them to quit was an insurmountable challenge.

After all, the psychological addiction was far more terrifying, a lifelong nightmare.

Once hooked, getting rid of it was nearly impossible.

However, the existence of a suppressant drug could offer a glimmer of hope to those genuinely wanting to escape the torment of heroin.

“By the way, are you here for tourism? Your kung fu is amazing! Are you, sir, one of those incredibly skilled martial artists?” Li Qingqing chatted animatedly, like a curious child, trying to liven things up.

Lu Chun and Gao Yangyang were usually quiet, preferring to work silently. Gao Yangyang, in particular, rarely spoke except in front of Jiang Tian.

Although Zhao Wei wanted to say something, Li Qingqing completely ignored him, showering Jiang Tian with questions like a curious child asking a million questions.

This made Jiang Tian question his life choices. Shouldn't these academic geniuses be aloof and uninterested in anything but studying? Why was she acting like a curious child?

No, that couldn't be right. Perhaps a strong thirst for knowledge was the driving force behind her studies. Jiang Tian sighed silently, but in his mind, Li Qingqing's reputation had already been elevated to an unparalleled level.

Such talent, if successfully developed, could even make a significant and great contribution to the entire anti-drug effort.

"It's not a vacation, just work," Jiang Tian shook his head.

"Are the four of you on a business trip?" Li Qingqing seemed as curious as ever, but a thoughtful look crossed her eyes.

Because this group of four seemed off, very off!

Li Qingqing silently looked at the pistol tucked into Jiang Tian's waistband, then at Gao Yangyang and Lü Chun; those two also carried guns.

That's right. While no one was watching, Zhao Wei frantically looted the corpses, and all three men were carrying weapons.

Unfortunately, there were only three guns. In the end, Zhao Wei silently contributed the three guns he had found. As for himself, he knew his place.

Killing and looting corpses—no matter how you look at it, it doesn't seem like a business trip.

"Don't look at us like that. We're all good citizens. Our favorite thing to do is help others," Jiang Tian explained, afraid of scaring the young girl.

"Those three?" Li Qingqing, who had reached the alley entrance, silently pointed behind her.

"Oh, they should all be dead," Jiang Tian said quietly.

Pfft!!!

Li Qingqing was still calm, but Zhao Wei and the other two behind Jiang Tian instantly burst out laughing.

They had thought Jiang Tian had only knocked them unconscious, but they hadn't expected him to kill them instantly—a true instant kill.

"Just kidding, I held back. They won't die, but they'll probably be in a vegetative state for the rest of their lives. Don't worry, I held back."

"Pfft!!!"

"Boss, you're too ruthless!"

Jiang Tian didn't want to kill anyone before even starting anything while he was abroad.

But those three thugs were asking for it.

Killing them outright, taking three lives, would easily attract the attention of the authorities at the Windmill Police Station, potentially causing trouble. But a vegetative state wouldn't be a problem; as long as they're still breathing, they're alive. Without death, the case's severity level is lower.

Besides, with the Windmill Police Station's speed in handling cases, Jiang Tian would probably have already made his escape long ago.

"Boss?"

Just then, Li Qingqing suddenly looked at Jiang Tian's back, her heart skipping a beat.

She hadn't misheard, had she? That fat man seemed to be calling Jiang Tian, ​​"Boss?"

What boss?

Li Qingqing's heart raced.

While Li Qingqing was thinking this, she wasn't paying attention and suddenly bumped into Jiang Tian's back. It turned out that Jiang Tian had suddenly stopped walking.

Pain made Li Qingqing cover her nose, tears welling in her eyes, and sob, "Why did you stop?"

For a moment, Li Qingqing thought she had bumped into reinforced concrete.

Jiang Tian, ​​with his back to her, suddenly stopped walking out of the alley and said quietly, "Qingqing, it seems you can't go back to your place for the time being."

Li Qingqing looked somewhat confused.

The next moment, Zhao Wei asked, "Boss, what's wrong?"

Jiang Tian, ​​still with his back to them, said calmly, "It seems that old fox Hong Shiwu knew all along and has been watching us."

Ever since the Windmill came, Jiang Tian felt that his scanning radar had been rendered useless.

Because once the radar was activated, good heavens, blood-red dots were everywhere, almost filling the entire screen.

There were very few empty spaces, which shocked Jiang Tian.

The Windmill was going a bit too far.



Chapter 507 Cunning and calculating? Not worth mentioning.


"He knows?"

"Boss, what do we do? Should we retreat now?"

"Retreat? How can we retreat? If this mission fails, the losses will be enormous."

"But we've already been discovered. Hong Shiwu is on guard. Success is a pipe dream for us. Wait, how could Hong Shiwu have discovered or detected us? Didn't we immediately block the news during the operation?"

Zhao Wei and the others standing behind Jiang Tian were all stunned.

Their faces showed disbelief.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, remained calm. These men had been working with him and naturally knew the background of the mission. Ultimately, it was because when Jiang Tian wiped out Shimiao Village, he happened to arrest two important figures.

These two important figures happened to know Xiang Tiannan, the agent of Yugou.com.

After Xiang Tiannan made his move, when he tried to kill the two key criminals to silence them, Jiang Tian followed the clues and arrested Xiang Tiannan and Hong Xun, who was with Xiang Tiannan, thus implicating Yugou.com.
