After interrogating Hong Xun, they finally obtained all the information and details about Hong Shiwu. Overall, it was a very unexpected yet logically logical case of following the clues.

There was no so-called surveillance, and Jiang Tian didn't use any other methods, only one: whoever made a move, their claws were chopped off.

The entire operation and the digging process were known to Gao Yangyang and Zhao Wei, among others.

Therefore, it was precisely because they knew this that they found it hard to believe they had been discovered.

"Boss, could there be a mistake? How could we possibly be discovered? Otherwise, wouldn't Hong Shiwu be a god?" Zhao Wei's smile was somewhat forced.

A surprise attack was possible, but if they really went all out, this was someone else's stronghold.

Especially since Hong Shiwu was different from other reclusive gangsters who had retired from their criminal activities; when their power was at its peak, they would choose to come to Windmill to expand their influence, gangs, or markets.

However, they would never invest much energy in it, nor did they have the ability to grow it into a powerful force. Therefore, most of these gang leaders' branches only operated here to ensure the basic safety of their leaders after retirement.

They didn't even consider making a profit, mainly because the competition was too fierce and the costs too high.

But Shi Hongwu was completely different. From the very beginning of his rise, Shi Hongwu had been focusing on building his power here.

Of course, the most terrifying thing about Shi Hongwu at this time was that he was incredibly wealthy. In this world, money truly can make the impossible possible.

Here, money can buy almost anything.

With focused development, Shi Hongwu's influence here was undoubtedly widespread, both in the legal and illegal worlds. They were unfamiliar with the area and had no one or any power that could help them.

Whatever problems they encountered, even their hometowns would find it difficult to intervene. Ultimately, it was because their identities were kept secret and couldn't be brought into the open.

The operation was a success. Even if the windmill team finds out what they did, there are no problems at the scene since they weren't caught red-handed.

But if the authorities intervene, then we'll have no way to defend ourselves.

“Our entire operation was flawless, from the operation at the Dongri warehouse to the subsequent information blockade, and most importantly, our entire operation proceeded without any problems. There were absolutely no survivors at the Dongri warehouse. Those we encountered in the bar that day were directly controlled and apprehended. Last night, no one had the time or ability to pass on intelligence, so how could there have been a leak?” Gao Yangyang asked, her brows furrowed.

Jiang Tian glanced at Gao Yangyang, then at Lü Chun. Lü Chun, however, didn't know if his initial treatment of Jiang Tian had affected his mentality.

Of course, perhaps Lü Chun was simply this kind of person. Since joining the anti-drug squad, Lü Chun's performance had always been that of an operational member.

As for case analysis, Lü Chun never chimed in or offered any analysis.

At first, Jiang Tian thought Lü Chun's mentality had been affected by his treatment, but later he realized that wasn't the case; he was just a typical muscle-bound guy.

However, the advantages were also obvious: their combat prowess during operations was absolutely terrifying.

Of course, Jiang Tian understood this; after all, everyone in that organization had a special skill.

Like this squad, Lü Chun was a standard core combatant.

Ignoring Lü Chun, Jiang Tian calmly said, "Hong Shiwu was able to build such a large fortune from nothing; don't underestimate him."

Although he said this, Jiang Tian's expression remained calm and indifferent.

What a joke.

If it were someone else, they might have really failed. After all, such a seemingly perfect operation, with no possibility of intelligence leaks, had such a major flaw.

But this operation was Jiang Tian's, and Jiang Tian never considered failure when undertaking a mission.

"Prepare for battle. As for Zhao Wei, take Miss Li and find a safe place to hide." Jiang Tian didn't give anyone a chance to speak, directly issuing the combat orders with a wave of his hand.

Among them, Zhao Wei's combat strength was undoubtedly the weakest.

Even as a woman, Gao Yangyang was far stronger than Zhao Wei.

Leaving aside Lü Chun, Gao Yangyang, despite being a second-generation rich girl, was actually trained in martial arts by professionals since childhood, as Jiang Tian later learned.

Her killing skills were superb.

Her combat abilities were formidable.

Killing Zhao Wei was child's play for her.

Zhao Wei wasn't disappointed: "Boss, be careful, Miss Li, let's find a place to hide."

"Brother Zhao, what's going on?" Li Qingqing's heart pounded, her blood rushed, and her face showed some confusion.

Because she was very intelligent, she understood what these people were saying.

She had even keenly sensed something amiss from their brief conversation.

These people.

"Our identities are confidential, but to be precise, we're from our hometown, so Miss Li can completely trust us," Zhao Wei quickly explained.

"I trust Brother Jiang," Li Qingqing said, her heart pounding.

From the conversation just now, she sensed that enemies were approaching.

And it seemed to be targeting them.

Just as Li Qingqing's mind raced, she saw a large group of people gathering and approaching from the alley entrance.

Zhao Wei tried to lead Li Qingqing to retreat from the other side of the alley.

But Jiang Tian stopped them: "Find a blind spot in the alley; there are people there too."

Whoosh!!!

Cold sweat poured down Zhao Wei's forehead.

Human beings are driven by the fear of the unknown.

They'd been discovered, and in someone else's stronghold, and most importantly, they didn't know how many enemies there were.

But there was no doubt.

Jiang Tian's tense demeanor suggested that there were quite a few enemies.

Sure enough, the next moment, more than ten people suddenly appeared at the alley entrance, unchaining themselves and pulling out AKs from their bags as they ran.

They moved towards the alley.



Chapter 508: A Thirty-Second Instant Kill Shocks the World, a Record-Breaking, Terrifying Speed


Fortunately, the alley's secondary road layout was rather complex; it was narrow towards the end, but widened considerably once inside.

The sides offered some concealment.

The entrance was three meters wide; after walking about ten meters, the road widened to five meters – this was where Jiang Tian and his group were.

"So many people."

A moment later, Li Qingqing swallowed hard, her eyes widening as she looked at the dozen or so men carrying AKs who had already crossed most of the entrance path.

Luckily, it was late at night.

Jiang Tian and his group were hidden in the darkness behind a corner, so when Li Qingqing saw the dozen or so thugs with AKs, she instinctively covered her mouth with both hands, afraid to scream.

But her heart was pounding at its peak.

For Li Qingqing, who had spent most of her life studying, facing this situation, her body subconsciously sensed a terrifying threat, and her legs trembled.

An ordinary person would probably be terrified in this situation.

Li Qingqing had never witnessed anything like this before, and the fact that she managed to remain silent was already beyond the capabilities of many.

After all, a dozen or so men armed with AK-47s were coming to kill her.

How many people could withstand that?

Just as tears welled up in Li Qingqing's eyes, and despair welled up within her,

Jiang Tian, ​​who had initially saved her and whom she had found somewhat strange, suddenly struck from the darkness without warning.

Or rather, even if Li Qingqing had made a sound and been discovered, it wouldn't have affected Jiang Tian.

In Li Qingqing's eyes, Jiang Tian's actions were swift and decisive, as fast as lightning. He reached out from the darkness and grabbed a large, white man cautiously advancing against the wall.

This man was very tall and extremely burly, at least 1.9 meters tall, and weighed close to 200 pounds. Yet, despite his imposing physique, Jiang Tian had seized him by the throat.

One hand gripped his throat, and the next moment, a crisp, loud snap echoed through the darkness.

The man didn't even have a chance to struggle; he was dead on the spot.

This sound also completely alerted the others.

Bang! Bang! Bang!!!

Intense gunfire erupted as these people rushed through the narrow passage, spraying bullets in Jiang Tian's direction.

But Jiang Tian, ​​along with Gao Yangyang and Lü Chun behind him, reacted even faster.

Before Gao Yangyang and Lü Chun could even react, Jiang Tian, ​​still holding the corpse in one hand, caught the AK rifle that had fallen from the corpse's hands with the other and opened fire on the men.

The gunfire came from Jiang Tian's hands.

Six men facing Jiang Tian died instantly, bullets flying aimlessly into the sky.

Several bullets were fired towards Jiang Tian, ​​but were blocked by the corpse in his hand. Without giving anyone a chance to react, Jiang Tian kicked the white man's body, sending it flying into the narrow passage.

The dozen or so men following behind were clearly panicked, especially as the corpse flew towards them; they instinctively opened fire wildly.

But Jiang Tian seized the opportunity, holding his rifle in one hand, and opened fire on them during their moment of confusion.

Lu Chun suddenly darted out from around the corner, performing a somersault, and fired his pistol at them, a series of splattering sounds ringing out.

By the time the surviving thugs reacted, Lu Chun had already rolled around the corner.

But as they looked at Lu Chun, Jiang Tian had already abruptly turned to the side, his AK long since discarded, replaced by a pistol he had just seized.

The crackling of gunfire echoed through the night sky.

Keep in mind, this was peak tourist season, a time when countless tourists from all over the world, struggling in the sweltering heat, flocked to this place.

Of course, the air was definitely cooler, but not as exaggerated as rumored; the weather at this time was still quite hot.

After emptying a magazine,

Jiang Tian touched his head; it was damp. This prompted an unconscious, self-deprecating smile from him.

In this kind of weather, strenuous exercise easily causes sweating.

However,

the effect was also significant.

Through recent collaborations with Lü Chun, Lü Chun, with his fearless spirit, excellent physical abilities, and terrifying combat instincts, had even managed to, barely, keep up with Jiang Tian's offensive pace.

But it was merely keeping up.

Compared to Jiang Tian's previous tendency to act alone, having Lü Chun undoubtedly relieved a little of the pressure.

This little pressure, however, was enough to make Jiang Tian lose his temper.

One person would need at least three minutes to completely resolve the situation, but with Lü Chun...

Jiang Tian checked his watch. Good, thirty seconds.

The corpses lay scattered all over the ground.

The gunfire that erupted here naturally threw the crowd of tourists on the main street outside the alley into complete panic.

Some called the police, some fled, and a few fearlessly stood there watching the spectacle.

Corpses lay scattered on the ground. Lü Chun, hands on her knees, panted heavily. This wasn't fear; it was the sheer tension and focus she'd been under, causing her adrenaline to surge and unleashing her fastest speed and fighting power ever.

Now that the battle was over, Lü Chun naturally felt utterly exhausted.

Zhao Wei had witnessed combat a few times before, but this time, with Lü Chun drawing fire, it was simpler and more terrifying, though still largely within his tolerance.

Gao Yangyang remained silent. She had initially thought she could offer some assistance.

But she hadn't expected this at all!

She hadn't even had a chance to fire a single shot. The moment she raised her pistol, the thugs were already on the ground.

But the one whose worldview was most shaken was undoubtedly Li Qingqing.

She had witnessed everything clearly from behind—how Jiang Tian made the first move, grabbing one thug and then instantly killing so many others.

That was over twenty people!

Thirty seconds later, they were all shot dead. The horrifying stench of blood constantly pushed Li Qingqing to her limits.

Actually, the fact that she had managed to hold on this long was a testament to Li Qingqing's strong physical and mental fortitude.

Anyone else would probably have completely collapsed by now.

"We won?" Li Qingqing asked blankly.

"Ah, we won, but not completely," Jiang Tian said with a quiet smile.

After a pause, Jiang Tian's deep, resonant voice sounded in Li Qingqing's ear: "Outside, more and more people are gathering."



Chapter 509 The sound that echoed through the windmill city was about to explode.


At this moment, Li Qingqing was a complete treasure in Jiang Tian's eyes!

Her talent for scientific research.

So young, and already capable of researching that kind of thing. Of course, whether she would ultimately succeed was uncertain; he was confident, but many had failed.

However, Jiang Tian had never doubted Li Qingqing in this regard, because without a doubt, someone who could use a laboratory couldn't just make things up.

Being able to use a laboratory meant that the research project was 70% likely to be successful.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian hadn't revealed any information about her research.

In fact, how many of the people Jiang Tian arrested had a university education?

Except for the Yunnan University case, but even that didn't reveal any academic prowess, let alone those arrested overseas.

As for the drug addicts in Shimiao Village, it's a pity they didn't provide Jiang Tian with that information either.

Of course, even if it were exposed, Jiang Tian wouldn't have the time or interest for research. His goal after his rebirth had never changed; forcing him to spend time in a lab doing research would be torture.

The fact that Li Qingqing could produce results, and at such a young age, was enough to prove one thing.

"They're all dead?" Li Qingqing swallowed.

"Probably all dead." Jiang Tian nodded.

This time, with Lü Chun's cooperation, Jiang Tian's operation was infinitely easier. You have to know how terrifying Jiang Tian's marksmanship is; if he really put on a show, every shot would hit its mark, he could use a Gatling gun like a sniper rifle.

An AK burst could take down an enemy with a single shot.

Killing these nearly twenty thugs was a piece of cake. As for keeping any alive, leaving them alive at this point would be insane.

But Jiang Tian's calm demeanor completely chilled Li Qingqing.

Having killed so many people, this man remained completely calm, as if he had merely crushed an ant. Li Qingqing was utterly astonished and stammered, "So you're saying it's not entirely safe?"

Indeed.

Jiang Tian gave Li Qingqing an appreciative look.

Actually, Jiang Tian had been worried that after witnessing the killings, Li Qingqing would rush in and accuse him without any explanation. He even considered whether he should just throw her away.

It wasn't that Jiang Tian was overthinking it; it was simply that there were too many overly compassionate people these days.

At a time like this, her first concern was for her safety—what a normal young woman!

"Because, there are still people, many people."

As Jiang Tian spoke, he picked up an unused AK from the ground and casually tossed one to each of Lü Chun, Gao Yangyang, and Zhao Wei.

Jiang Tian took one for himself.

"There are still people?" Li Qingqing's heart pounded wildly.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't say another word for the rest of the time, and Li Qingqing could only hurriedly follow behind him.

At that moment, Li Qingqing's intelligence told her that among so many people, Jiang Tian was clearly the leader, the head of this group.

Furthermore, given that Jiang Tian's actions had been the most stylish and terrifying just now, it was only natural that standing beside Jiang Tian, ​​and only by his side, could she seem to find a strong sense of security.

Jiang Tian didn't leave through the alley filled with corpses; instead, he went down the other side.

The main reason was that there were too many people there—too many tourists, and also too many criminals mixed in with ordinary people. Frankly, unless Jiang Tian went on a killing spree, it would be difficult to distinguish them.

For a normal person, the more chaotic the place, the easier it is to slip through the mud and escape.

However, that only applies to those in a vulnerable position.

For Jiang Tian, ​​it wasn't certain who the hunter and the prey were.

Jiang Tian had his scanning radar activated, displaying the movements of almost all criminals within a kilometer radius.

This was essentially like having a god's-eye view.

"There's an ambush at this alley entrance too. Be careful," Jiang Tian said as he walked.

He had to admit that Hong Shiwu, who had built his empire from scratch, was definitely capable.

To react before he could act and quickly pinpoint his location to surround him, Jiang Tian had to admit that this man was highly skilled.

And with Yugou.com involved, it's safe to say...

If Hong Shiwu had followed the proper path, Taoyitao and Gouyigou wouldn't have been involved at all.

This guy can really handle everything at once.

Jiang Tian was thinking about things, but his actions didn't falter. Now, Jiang Tian was leading his team, bidding farewell to solo combat.

Even though Jiang Tian could often instantly kill or crush opponents, acting alone was often severely limited. With coordinated action, if someone could keep up with Jiang Tian's pace and cooperate, it would be far more efficient and smoother than acting alone. For example, just now, Lü Chun only attracted a little attention, and Jiang Tian was able to wipe out the entire enemy team in half a minute.

Sometimes, it's just a matter of a few seconds.

Now, Lü Chun was by Jiang Tian's side, learning from him, and her combat skills had improved rapidly, especially in coordinating with Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian led the way with Lü Chun, while Li Qingqing followed closely behind.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian and Lü Chun quickly disappeared from her sight, while she followed behind, led by Zhao Wei.

Gao Yangyang, meanwhile, held her gun, vigilantly watching for anyone appearing in the alley behind them.

In short, their overall coordination was already showing initial signs of integration. With all the members being elite and adept at teamwork, the combination looked just like something out of a movie.

At least, that's how Li Qingqing perceived it.

Just as Li Qingqing was thinking these things, a burst of intense gunfire suddenly erupted from ahead.

The startling gunfire, initially sounding like firecrackers, wasn't particularly frightening. But when one's subconscious realized it was the sound of deadly gunfire, it was when fear truly rose from the depths of their being.

The fear of death.

"Don't be afraid, little girl. We're here. You're absolutely safe," Zhao Wei reassured her, standing beside her.

However, Zhao Wei's gaze kept darting towards Li Qingqing, his face showing a thoughtful, confused, and hesitant expression.

Li Qingqing's reaction was unusually calm, or rather, although she was afraid, her behavior far exceeded that of any normal person.

"I trust your boss too," Li Qingqing replied.

At this point, Li Qingqing actually had some guesses in her mind.

However, she wasn't entirely sure yet.

"By the way, what's your boss's name?" Li Qingqing asked.

"Why are you asking that?" Zhao Wei's face instantly became wary.

"Nothing, I just wanted to get to know him. After we go back to China, I want to invite your boss to dinner." Li Qingqing wanted to gather some information.



Chapter 510 The gunfight was broadcast live worldwide! Oh my god, this is insane!


“Our boss’s name is Jiang Tian,” Zhao Wei stated bluntly.

What a joke.

Normally, revealing his name would be utterly ridiculous, but things were different now.

Before taking action, Zhao Wei clearly knew, and had even received word from his aunt, that CCTV was going to conduct another exclusive interview with Jiang Tian, ​​and this time without blurring his face.

Good grief, he was about to be featured nationwide; was there any need to hide his name now?

On the other hand, what could a name possibly represent?

“May I ask what you do in China?” Li Qingqing pressed further.

“You already know it’s presumptuous, and you still ask?” Zhao Wei was exasperated. This was going too far, absolutely too far.

If Li Qingqing had asked for his name earlier, he definitely wouldn’t have given it.

Jiang Tian could openly reveal his identity because he had the power. Now, who would dare to retaliate against Jiang Tian? They were afraid of not dying fast enough. Those who knew Jiang Tian wouldn’t dare act, and those who didn’t, naturally, would be even less likely to retaliate.

But Zhao Wei was different; he was afraid of dying!

He didn't possess Jiang Tian's terrifying fighting power. If he were cornered and retaliated against, losing his life would be a minor matter.

"But if you want to find the boss, you can go to Yunhai," Zhao Wei explained.

However, he didn't continue talking to Li Qingqing, because the situation was urgent; the gunfire was gradually intensifying.

After the initial intense gunfire, followed by sporadic shots, a terrifying, firecracker-like barrage of gunfire echoed continuously through the alley.

This was getting out of hand.

Zhao Wei was actually starting to panic.

Before this, Zhao Wei had only worked on some major cases with Jiang Tian, ​​mostly playing a minor role or assisting Jiang Tian.

But this time, the intensity had increased terrifyingly.

Let's talk about the chaotic situation.

Furthermore, while the Windmill Country has many criminals, it's still a relatively developed country; at least on the surface, it maintains a respectable image.

Especially since some things haven't been exposed, in many people's minds, it truly is a scenic, environmentally friendly, and civilized tourist destination.

However, according to their plan, they were secretly arresting Hong Shiwu.

Now, this terrifying gunfight has erupted on the street, and on a crowded street at that. The impact is terrifying.

To put it simply...

As Zhao Wei cautiously led Li Qingqing towards the alley entrance, the alleyway was now filled with flames.

Zhao Wei tripped over something and fell to the ground. In the darkness, this startled him. He quickly stood up and realized that he had tripped over a corpse.

Not only under his feet, but also around him, Zhao Wei noticed many more corpses.

This sent a chill down Zhao Wei's spine. Aside from accompanying Jiang Tian on a rescue mission in Myanmar, this was practically his first time abroad, and he'd already encountered this situation.

Especially since the road outside the alley was wide and brightly lit, Zhao Wei could even see a steady stream of thugs arriving as reinforcements.

"Are they crazy?" Even Zhao Wei couldn't sit still anymore.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian, ​​hidden behind cover, AK in one hand, would occasionally peek out and unleash a barrage of bullets. After each burst, the thugs outside would fall to the ground with a series of thuds.

They were like dumplings being dropped into a pot, instantly losing their lives. Each time Jiang Tian hid behind cover, the thugs seemed to be further stimulated, firing even more frantically, as if driven to madness by witnessing their comrades fall.

At this moment, Zhao Wei was unaware.

The thugs blocking the alleyway outside were a thousand times more terrified and fearful than he was.

One of the thugs, hiding behind a car, looked at the three corpses lying beside him. Almost all three were like copies, all dying in the same way.

“Calling for backup! We need backup! Enemy firepower is too heavy! The intelligence is wrong! We need a lot of backup!”

This thug shouted frantically into the walkie-talkie. Clearly, from his voice, it was evident that he was on the verge of a mental breakdown.

How could they not be devastated? This was utter madness!

Every time someone peeked out, they were instantly killed. The remaining thugs were forced to hide behind cover, firing wildly and aimlessly, only their hands sticking out of the cover. What they were shooting at was nobody's guess.

But there was no doubt that getting them to peek out now was a pipe dream.

"Fuck, bastards, how many are left?" one of the thugs cursed, his expression fierce, but his forehead was covered in cold sweat.

"We still have twenty men," a henchman nearby immediately replied.

"Twenty men gone in one fell swoop?" The leader was completely dumbfounded.

To be honest, Hong Shiwu had really gone all out for this operation.

Unlike others, Hong Shiwu never underestimated his enemies. In fact, it was precisely because of this that he had been able to operate on such a terrifying scale for so many years without being captured—no, without even being discovered.

According to history and the files Jiang Tian had reviewed, Facebook only discovered and took action several years later.

Whether it was due to the heinous crimes and the need to rid the people of a scourge, or simply greed for the victim's wealth, remained unknown.

Because of Hong Shiwu's caution, after seeing Jiang Tian's file, regardless of whether the information was true, false, or a smokescreen, Hong Shiwu firmly believed that even a lion would use its full strength to hunt a rabbit.

He didn't even consider showing mercy and sending them away one by one; he immediately dispatched all the forces he could mobilize.

In reality, no one knew this.

Hong Shiwu appeared calm, but he was actually somewhat panicked.

His ability to remain so calm stemmed from the peculiarity of the dark web: no one could find him through it.

To avoid being exposed, he even automatically isolated himself from everyone who could contact him.

For this, he paid a considerable price.

For example, granting his subordinates enormous autonomy and reducing his control over them, etc.

Xiang Nantian was a prime example; the lack of control over him even fueled his rebellious thoughts, leading him to consider going solo.

Upon confirming Jiang Tian's arrival in Windmill Country, Hong Shiwu instantly understood everything. The other party was targeting him, and he mobilized almost all available resources to eliminate Jiang Tian there.

"Boss, there seems to be someone over there."

The leader had just called for backup on the walkie-talkie when his underling hurriedly and anxiously shouted.

"Fuck, there's someone, so what's the big deal?" The leader slapped him.

The underling, without even having time to call, blurted out, "No, boss, she's carrying a camera on her shoulder."

"What?"

The leader stiffened and turned his head, only to see a shocking scene.

They were firing shots, but a burly man was carrying a camera, and in front of it, a woman was holding a microphone, frantically narrating something: "Fuck, friends in America, did you see that? We're in the Land of Windmills, broadcasting live to you from these historic streets, but oh my god, what am I seeing? Gunfight, intense gunfight! Look at the corpses on the ground! Oh my god, more than twenty people are dead, and they haven't stopped! Oh fuck, where am I?"

The leader: "???"

"Boss, are we about to become famous worldwide?"

It exploded.

It really exploded.

"Oh my god, friends, we're broadcasting live from the Windmills' capital, Amnesty, to you. It's insane! As you can see, this group of unidentified men, about forty in number, are already half dead on the ground! Oh my god, it's insane! There must be many people in the alley! It's crazy, oh my god!"



Chapter 511 Exploring Reality: In the Face of a Global Live Broadcast, You Actually Did...


Unlike the infamous Mexican city, Windmill, regardless of its hidden agendas or the rampant power of its gangs,

has always maintained a public image of a beautiful, peaceful, and praiseworthy city.

Some say it's a tranquil country.

Some say its people live leisurely lives, cycling through the fields and city every day.

Others say it's the most beautiful country in the world.

Furthermore, some say it's the most peaceful, beautiful, and happy country in the world, a top tourist destination, and boasts the highest happiness index.

Regardless of how this narrative was cultivated, regardless of the amount of praise garnered, and regardless of how many blindly lauded it,

using countless filters to lavish praise, etc.,

at this moment, without a doubt, it has completely exploded. Yes, it has exploded.

It's important to understand that there's not much of a time difference between the US and China. It's currently evening, a prime time for many, and their busy professionals, having finished a long day of work, are returning home.

They're then turning on the TV, using their leisure time to watch the currently popular and highly anticipated evening news broadcast.

This program is a hit show on a prominent US television network called "Exploring Reality."

The core selling point of this program is its authentic exploration of famous or popular tourist destinations, or widely circulated beautiful places.

This program aligns perfectly with a core value of the US: they love authenticity and enjoy expressing their uninhibited opinions.

Coincidentally, the US is very popular, or rather, it ranks first among many tourists' choices.

Many people say that this land of windmills boasts a long and rich history, where developed cities and rural life are perfectly integrated, making this country stand out globally. It's unclear who made this claim, but now it seems like anyone who visits feels obligated to praise it.

Exploring Reality, in this context, visited the Land of Windmills to see if it truly lived up to the hype.

Exploring Reality seems to be constantly going against the grain.

Places others praise are criticized, while places they deem unpleasant are praised to the skies.

The host is particularly interesting, continuously belittling the place during their visit.

The host even has a comment section on their phone for Exploring Reality, allowing viewers to watch the live broadcast, leave comments, and interact with the host in real time.

This model, in this era, has caused a huge sensation, making Exploring Reality an overnight sensation.

Because of their pursuit of truth and lack of social skills, the show became a hit, but it also brought immense pressure. After all, many countries criticized them after seeing the show, severely damaging their tourism industries, and they were not about to let it go.

The same applies to windmills.

After host Annie arrived in Windmill Country, what she saw and experienced wasn't quite as amazing as the rumors had suggested. However, the scenery was undeniably beautiful, and most importantly, the cities lacked the hustle and bustle of big cities, giving the whole place a relaxed, laid-back feel.

Annie was just starting to think she'd had a bad experience, that this exploration was truly as wonderful as everyone had described.

Then, after exploring the main streets and arriving at a less crowded secondary road, she tried to salvage the situation.

“Fuck, Annie, stop spreading rumors! We know what you're up to.”

“Annie, don't make another mistake. Be careful, if you keep spreading these rumors, Windmill will sue you for defamation!”

“It's so beautiful, so safe! There are so many tourists here! Damn America, those ugly administrators, they can't protect us ordinary people like Windmill does.”

“Yeah, so many tourists! The scenery is beautiful, and they can even go out at night. Ladies and gentlemen, would you dare to go out so openly at night in America?”

“Oh fuck my God, unless I want to die.”

Annie held a microphone in one hand and watched the comments section of the discussion.

Annie's lips curled slightly.

Indeed.

Annie's first impression of everything she saw and heard along the way was very good. Even on secondary roads, she occasionally saw lone people strolling around, seemingly carefree and safe.

Putting everything else aside, this safe environment gave Annie, who lived in America, a strange sense of peace.

The program "Exploring Reality," in order to be immersive, has no rehearsals. It doesn't praise any place, nor does it maliciously vilify anything.

It analyzes the situation from a completely neutral perspective.

Annie was about to give up, ready to go along with everyone's wishes and praise the windmill, even questioning why, despite both places having open fire, there were no police officers on the streets of the windmill, yet everyone dared to go out at night.

Why can't we in the US even leave our homes at night?

What exactly is wrong? Why are we inferior to the US? Annie felt the eagle's thoughts erupting, preparing to praise the windmill to launch a fierce critique of the US.

Bang bang bang!!!

The loud, crackling sounds continued. Annie pondered, "It must be firecrackers."

But after getting closer...

Annie looked at the scene before her. Behind the cover of three vehicles, more than twenty corpses lay scattered haphazardly.

There were also figures behind cover, AKs firing rapidly.

The fire was incredibly bright in the darkness.

Bullets, spitting flames, fired wildly into the alleyway.

It exploded.

It really exploded.

You know, "Exploring Reality" is practically the most popular show in the entire United States.

Every night, anyone planning a trip or going abroad watches it first thing.

And then, it really exploded.

The message board was completely filled with "Oh my god," "Fuck," "This is a windmill," and all sorts of other question marks.

"This is insane."

The impression of Windmills from the outside world has always been one of extreme peace.

And yet, such a serious street gunfight has occurred.

Although it happened on a secondary street, it was still a public exchange of fire between dozens of people in broad daylight.

"Oh my god, this is countless times crazier than what we see in the US!" Annie exclaimed excitedly, clutching her head.

That's right.

Their investigative reporting is all about this kind of contrast.

But this contrast is terrifying!

And the moment Annie's live stream showed this scene, it wasn't just the US that was in an uproar; in that instant, the whole world was in an uproar.

In the US, there are also international students and people from all over the world who come to the US for various needs. This is a program they love to watch. After seeing this horrific news, they immediately contacted their relatives, who then spread the word frantically.

It was after this scene was shown...



Chapter 512, now for an urgent news briefing.


First, the police station, upon receiving the news, erupted in chaos, mobilizing all available personnel.

Of course, Jiang Tian wasn't idle either.

After noticing Annie, Jiang Tian froze, inwardly cursing his bad luck.

There's a saying that's very true: the people at Windmill, influenced by a "laissez-faire" mentality, are almost universally like this, from top to bottom. But this is only true if it doesn't cause too much of a stir.

If this gunfight hadn't been exposed, Jiang Tian would have had ample time to resolve it, leave, and still apprehend Hong Shiwu without delay.

But once it's exposed, it's a completely different story. Once it's out in the open, Windmill will be in complete turmoil. This is a scandal, a massive scandal.

Imagine, in a country booming with tourism, and with tourism accounting for a huge portion of their economic revenue, suddenly becoming known like Mexico—unsafe, universally hated—would have a terrifying impact on Windmill.

They would absolutely explode; even the most laid-back people wouldn't be able to sit still.

So Jiang Tian showed no mercy in his subsequent actions, instantly killing anyone who peeked out.

Or rather, simply being incredibly accurate wasn't invincible for Jiang Tian.

For example, when enemies hid behind cover, occasionally peeking out to fire, Jiang Tian often felt he could easily eliminate them, but his body simply couldn't react in time.

In other words, while he was aware of the enemy, his body couldn't keep up with the reaction.

By the time he fired, the enemy had often already retreated back behind cover.

However, after Jiang Tian's physical abilities were upgraded, especially after becoming superhuman, when enemies peeked out, they didn't even have time to see clearly. In those fractions of a second, Jiang Tian's bullets had already fired.

Instantly, bursts of headshots erupted.

"Now! Run!" Jiang Tian, ​​guns in hand, stood in the shadows.

Good heavens, thank goodness it's dark. Otherwise, Jiang Tian really wouldn't know how to handle this. If it were exposed, other things wouldn't matter to him.

But if it were confirmed, Jiang Tian's political career would be completely over.

Regardless of why the fight broke out today.

Regardless of what happened here, more than twenty corpses, more than twenty lives—if Jiang Tian's face were exposed to the spotlight, especially at this time, it would be troublesome for him to climb to a higher position in the future.

Everyone would know that Jiang Tian is a thug who illegally engaged in a street gunfight in a foreign country. Who knows what kind of public condemnation he would receive.

Fortunately, it's the dead of night, making it difficult to see faces clearly, let alone have a clear camera recording.

Of course, to be on the safe side, Jiang Tian didn't go out immediately but provided cover from the rear.

"There are probably about five enemies left. After you exit the alley, turn left immediately and run away as fast as you can. Don't ask any other questions," Jiang Tian immediately gave the order.

"Boss, I'll stay," Lü Chun immediately shook his head and said.

Since ancient times, no matter what you do, being a rearguard has never been a good job, because the phrase "rearguard" literally means being on the defensive!

Few who are responsible for rearguard action have a good ending.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, seemed to think he was an idiot. Was he capable of killing the enemy when they made their move?

If he couldn't, they were doomed.

Even if Jiang Tian were a god, if the enemy hid behind cover and fired wildly, they would be in danger.

"Stop talking nonsense. After you get out of the alley, don't look back. Absolutely do not look back," Jiang Tian instructed.

Good heavens.

There was a surveillance camera recording that.

And time was of the essence; they had to complete the operation before the police arrived.

This was somewhat exciting for Jiang Tian, ​​because before, he was always the one arresting people in a police car; this was the first time he had been pressured into an arrest by a foreign police car.

"Yes, sir!!!" Gao Yangyang, the highest-ranking officer besides Jiang Tian, ​​immediately saluted.

"Retreat."

Gao Yangyang took the lead. Regardless of the danger outside, she chose to trust Jiang Tian completely, so she was the first to rush out without any protection. Once outside, she instantly turned left, didn't look back, and ran frantically without looking at anything.

After seeing Gao Yangyang leave the alley, Zhao Wei and Lü Chun's trust in Jiang Tian increased even more, and they rushed out as well.

"Wait for me!"

At this moment, Li Qingqing was terrified, her heart pounding wildly. She watched the three of them run away without hesitation. The crucial point was that outside the alley was open plain, with no cover whatsoever. In other words, once outside the alley, they were directly exposed to the guns of the criminals.

These men actually trusted Jiang Tian so much that they obeyed his orders without hesitation.

This seemed to ignite Li Qingqing's fighting spirit. Although she rationally cursed the three men as madmen and insane, she dared not linger and rushed out with them.

Sure enough, just as Li Qingqing ran into the alley,

Bang! Bang! Bang!

The last five thugs couldn't hold back any longer.

They began firing wildly at the men emerging from the alley.

But they didn't know that to Jiang Tian, ​​they were like sitting ducks. The moment their fingers were on the trigger, ready to fire, a bullet whizzed past and pierced their foreheads.

"The last five are done," Jiang Tian breathed a sigh of relief.

Seeing the thugs were all dealt with, Jiang Tian leaped up and instantly dashed out of the alley.

He didn't even glance at the bodies, much less turn to look at Annie, who was filming the scene, and quickly made his way away.

But Jiang Tian's choice was the right one.

He didn't know...

At this very moment, countless eyes around the world are watching this scene.

Outside, the world is in utter shock.

"Fuck, what did you say? Several more have died! Nearly thirty are dead now! We still don't know the situation inside the alley?"

Woo-hoo woo-hoo!!!

A loud siren blared.

The police chief in charge of this area, having rushed as fast as he could, finally arrived with his team. But as soon as he got out of the car, his eyes fell upon...

He happened to see it: over twenty corpses lay scattered across the street, some of whom Jiang Tian had dealt with beforehand during the assembly.

That's why the bodies were so scattered. Because of this, the bureau chief's head buzzed, he staggered and almost fell, and waves of dizziness washed over him. He had only one thought: it's over.

Completely over.

What kind of shootout could kill twenty or thirty people on the spot?

"Station, station, station, now for an international breaking news broadcast..."



Chapter 513: Under Direct Command from Headquarters, Hometown Phone Call, Utter Explosion


It was a little past 8 PM. In distant Tokyo, with a twelve-hour time difference, this time was exactly 8 AM, during the morning news.

While morning news doesn't garner as much attention as evening news, the sheer number of viewers is still considerable.

"This is CCTV-6. We're interrupting our morning news broadcast with a breaking news report. According to the latest news, a fierce gunfight has just broken out in the capital of Windmill Country, suspected to be a gang shootout. Many people have died in the streets. We solemnly remind everyone, especially those traveling to Windmill, to be aware of their safety."

"The Ambassador to Windmill has issued a safety notice, advising everyone to be mindful of their safety while traveling and avoid going out at night."

...

It exploded.

It really exploded.

The US already has a very high level of attention on this, and the fact that it happened on one of the most popular television programs in the US, coupled with the stark contrast...

The problem stems from the sheer number of tourist hotspots and picturesque landscapes touted as tourist destinations. The stark contrast between these portrayals and the reality, presented in unfiltered footage, creates a dramatic surge in popularity.

Windmill is a prime example. With a strong global reputation as a tourist hotspot, its tourism revenue constitutes a significant portion of its GDP.

The sudden appearance of a violent clash in the streets of such a tourist destination, and a horrific one at that, might not seem unusual in an occasional occurrence.

But the crucial point is that it was broadcast on television, and crucially, it occurred not far from the office area—the very heart of the area.

This is insane.

This caused a complete uproar.

Especially for the University of Tokyo, who follows the news closely, the news reports of the clash were met with widespread shock.

Furthermore, some observant individuals noticed...

"Holy crap, didn't you see the live stream? Five people ran out of the alley."

"My god, if I'm not mistaken, there were two women in there too, both with long hair."

"That's right, I just looked closely. Look what I found! My god, after the first four people ran out, it was the last one who fired. All the people outside who were about to fire were taken down by that last guy."

It has to be said, the camera angle was excellent, causing a huge uproar outside.

What was supposed to be a close fight turned out to be a crushing victory for only a handful of scattered individuals.

That's terrifying fighting power.

It's explosive.

Of course, because they were running in the opposite direction, their faces weren't shown in the recording, only their clear backs.

Of course, that was enough.

For Jiang Tian, ​​as long as his face wasn't captured on camera, it wasn't him, no matter how eloquently you explain it, it wasn't him.

Meanwhile...

A deathly silence fell over the city bureau's conference room.

And the faint sound of a telephone ringing in the room was further silenced.

"After we arrived, we didn't expect to be targeted by Hong Shiwu. He probably discovered we were there and cornered us on the secondary road, but we've broken through now."

Jiang Tian's voice came through clearly on the speakerphone.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong in the conference room fell silent.

That's right.

Jiang Tian has really made something of himself now, working for the high-level bureau.

This high-level bureau has even excluded Shen Han and Jiang Peng, assigning them their own tasks: handling the aftermath of cases, and apprehending escaped criminals and wanted criminals.

With the outbreak of major cases, a large number of criminals were exposed. Some were caught, but most were not apprehended in time.

Mainly because of Jiang Tian's solving of cases, many major cases emerged too suddenly and too quickly.

After they broke out, the scope was too wide, leading to significant oversights, primarily a shortage of manpower.

Jiang Peng and Shen Han were either resting or working overtime on cases.

Command of the case has been completely transferred to headquarters.

In other words, Jiang Tian is now directly under the jurisdiction of headquarters.

Minister Tan, Xu Poye, and Gao Xiaolong are monitoring the situation almost constantly.

This lineup—it's safe to say—is unprecedented in history; such a lineup personally overseeing the operation is unparalleled.

Minister Tan's position is self-evident, Xu Poye is the director of the 21st Bureau, responsible for the nation's anti-drug work, and Gao Xiaolong's position is even higher than Xu Poye's; he's part of Minister Tan's team.

However, at this moment, the three of them, though huddled together, looked extremely grim.

The news had exploded.

The crucial point is that they only received the news after it exploded, barely having time to do anything. They didn't even have time to wash up, only sleeping for four or five hours before rushing to the municipal bureau.

The main concern was Gao Xiaolong, the oldest of the three. As people get older, their sleep quality suffers, and coupled with his restless mind, and the fact that his granddaughter had gone abroad on a mission, Gao Xiaolong wasn't particularly worried.

Around 8 PM, Gao Xiaolong, unable to sleep, was woken by the television. It turned out the TV had been left on all night.

Or rather, it had been on the whole time. He hadn't had time to watch TV; it had been on when Gao Yangyang left, and Gao Xiaolong hadn't even noticed. He was so tired when he got home that he fell asleep immediately.

After being woken by the television, Gao Xiaolong was stunned to see the words "Windmill." These two words almost reflexively woke him from his deep sleep.

Upon waking, he saw a horrifying scene.

Fierce fighting in the capital?

Dozens of people already dead?

The opposing side.

As soon as he saw the group leave, Gao Xiaolong recognized their backs immediately; he knew those backs all too well.

Seeing this, Gao Xiaolong was instantly jolted.

His mind went blank.

He was told to secretly and quietly arrest Hong Shiwu, and you went to the windmill, went overseas, and put on a show of fighting in front of the whole world?

No…

Gao Xiaolong suddenly realized, was this a show of fighting in front of the whole world? Something terrible had happened.

Such a huge commotion was caused on someone else's turf.

The most terrifying thing is that they've been publicizing it everywhere, spending enormous sums to promote their tourist destination, which relies heavily on tourism to boost its GDP every year.

Half of their prosperity depends on the tourism industry.

And now, you're going to destroy their livelihood.

Putting himself in their shoes, Gao Xiaolong fell silent.

This incident has probably caused a huge uproar. If no one had broadcast it live, if it hadn't been reported, or even if someone had seen it, it wouldn't have mattered. But it was broadcast anyway.

Gao Xiaolong immediately went to the Municipal Public Security Bureau to assemble.

Xu Poye and Minister Tan arrived later, but after learning the whole story, their faces instantly darkened.

They've really stirred up a hornet's nest.

"It's too much of a coincidence, why is it such a coincidence?" Xu Poye exclaimed.



Chapter 514 Bearing the sole hope of everyone, a heavy burden on his shoulders


Everyone knew Xu Poyue meant to ask why the TV station staff were so conveniently there, and whether there was some kind of conspiracy.

But then he thought about it again and realized he was overthinking it.

"They just happened to run into us. It's too late to say anything now. The entire Windmill (a TV station) is probably going to explode right now. Xiao Jiang and his team are not only facing pressure from Hong Shiwu, but also from the entire Windmill, and from the entire nation," Minister Tan said, his heart pounding.

Even he couldn't imagine how to proceed with the mission.

No, this wasn't just about the mission anymore; it was about whether Jiang Tian and his team could safely evacuate.

It's insane! From now on, the entire Windmill is probably going crazy. At this point, once the news spreads, countless furious citizens will inevitably take to the streets to exert pressure.

"The entire Windmill organization can't remain silent; otherwise, they can't withstand everyone's wrath. But this response hinges on capturing Jiang Tian and his team. In other words, they must capture Jiang Tian to give everyone an explanation and, more importantly, to deter others. Otherwise, like Yunhai, similar incidents could explode in the future." This was Minister Tan's real concern.

They held high positions, and the terrifying burden this incident placed on them terrified them.

Therefore, they barely consulted and immediately established one-way contact with Jiang Tian.

In fact, Jiang Tian did carry communication equipment.

However, this equipment wasn't for two-way communication; it was only for one-way communication in emergencies, precisely to avoid detection.

Most importantly, if they were discovered to be behind it and evidence was found, they would be truly finished.

This concerned core interests.

Therefore, the operational method they adopted was one where they could contact Jiang Tian unilaterally, while Jiang Tian could only passively wait.

To ensure absolute certainty, even though they trusted Jiang Tian, ​​the interests of the nation could not be compromised in the slightest.

Jiang Tian's voice came through immediately after the connection was established.

The three men couldn't help but breathe a sigh of relief, but Jiang Tian's voice immediately shattered their newly relaxed hearts.

"I now authorize you to abandon the mission. Your first priority is your own safety. Find a safe place and hide immediately. Next, the entire Windmill will be turned upside down," Minister Tan issued the order in an uncompromising tone.

"No, I believe the most dangerous place is actually the safest place," Jiang Tian said slowly.

"What do you mean?" Minister Tan was startled.

"At this moment, nowhere is safe, but I actually feel that Hong Shiwu's office is the safest place," Jiang Tian said calmly and unhurriedly.

Sure enough.

After hearing Jiang Tian's words, the three men froze for a moment, then gasped in shock.

Although it was shocking...

However, it must be said...

"Hiss, Xiao Jiang's mind! That's right, Hong Shiwu wields terrifying power within the Windmill Group, practically operating in both the legal and illegal worlds. Plus, this time, it was his underlings who died. He'll definitely shift all the blame onto Xiao Jiang. He's definitely already orchestrated this. With his connections, absolutely no one will investigate their company." Xu Poye jumped to his feet, his face flushed with excitement.

This is actually a reverse line of thinking.

But at this moment,

it's the optimal choice.

Even returning home is a luxury at this point.

However,

"Hong Shiwu has so many men; he must have his own people, and who knows, weapons. It's too dangerous to be on guard. I disagree," Gao Xiaolong said cautiously.

Other things are negotiable, but if anything happens to Jiang Tian, ​​it would be a major blow to the entire country.

"No, at this moment, this is the only way," Minister Tan's heart pounded.

It has to be said,

this is a crazy decision.

It was an absolutely insane choice.

But without a doubt.

At this moment, what Minister Tan feared most was being smeared. Hong Shiwu, through his connections, was constantly throwing mud at them—that was the most disgusting thing.

Therefore, the dark web case was of paramount importance.

Once they had Hong Shiwu in hand and consolidated the assets, no matter what the other side did, they would be in an invincible position, and could even easily turn the tide.

“Xiao Jiang, the next heavy responsibility is entrusted to you.” Minister Tan’s heart pounded wildly.

He had to admit.

This idea was insane, but wasn’t it precisely because it was insane that success was possible?

“Yes, I will definitely complete the mission.” These were Jiang Tian’s last words, followed by complete silence on the communication device. No one spoke again.

The entire conference room was silent; no one spoke.

Those present were all seasoned veterans, and they all understood how much filth existed outside. They would never let go of an opportunity to smear others.

And at this moment, Windmill desperately needed an outlet for its emotions.

Sure enough.

Just minutes after the call ended.

Minister Tan, who was closely monitoring Windmill's actions, received the news from the scene.

"Windmill has already broadcast a news report about tonight's events, clearly stating that all the problems were caused by us, and demanding a response from us. Otherwise, they won't let it go, nor will the innocent victims. They even said that those who died were sacrificed in the capture of the perpetrators and that they should be awarded medals."

Minister Tan's face grew increasingly grim. He hung up the phone and fell into a long silence.

Because he realized.

Now, all the turning points rested on Hong Shiwu. Completely dismantling Fishbuy.com—that reputation and achievement were enough to shed light on everything.

As for the process, it didn't matter anymore.

The only downside was that Windmill was likely to go even further insane.

Because the headquarters of Yugou.com is located there, and the higher-ups were considering this, they didn't even bother to notify them. Notifying them wouldn't only prevent cooperation, but they might even secretly use some means to obstruct them.

"This is a complete distortion of the truth," Minister Tan gritted his teeth.

"Now we can only place our hopes on Xiao Jiang," Gao Xiaolong sighed deeply.

But just then, something they never dreamed of happened.

In a spacious, enormous office in Windmill Capital, there was a deathly silence.

"Hong Shiwu, you have the right to remain silent, but every word you utter will be used against you in court."



Chapter 515 Unparalleled Powerhouse? A 60% Chance of Victory Lost in an Instant Kill at the Start?


Let's rewind a little.

Hong Shiwu sat in his office, his face expressionless, and dialed the bureau chief's number.

Actually, Hong Shiwu's subordinates were not easily investigated. Unlike Yunhai's personnel transferred from overseas, although many of these subordinates had come secretly, if a thorough investigation were conducted, even if Hong Shiwu had immense abilities, he wouldn't be able to oppose the entire Windmill Group.

However, Hong Shiwu had his own methods, or rather, he wasn't worried at all. Although the operation had taken an unexpected turn when a television reporter appeared.

The key question is, what do we do if it happens? If it happens, we just accept our bad luck. What else can we do but eliminate the windmills? Then the whole world will know what kind of place they are.

Who would dare to visit the windmills then?

The broadcast is already over.

What else could be done?

Although the plan had taken an unexpected turn, Hong Shiwu remained completely unfazed. Even though those men were all his subordinates, lower-level investigations might require evidence, but once the higher-ups knew these people were Hong Shiwu's men, no further evidence was needed to resolve the matter.

Before the machinery of the state, all evidence was meaningless; confirmation was enough.

However, Hong Shiwu did have his own solution to this problem.

While he was far from capable of blatantly distorting the truth, he could easily shift the blame using his connections.

Therefore, on the phone, Hong Shiwu unceremoniously pinned all the blame on Jiang Tian, ​​and ultimately, all the dead were identified as Hong Shiwu's men.

More than thirty of his men were wiped out.

Not a single enemy corpse was left.

Hong Shiwu directly framed himself as the victim.

The police chief, whether bribed or receiving instructions from above, immediately stated, "We understand the situation, Mr. Hong. Rest assured, from now on, our police will spare no effort to apprehend the criminals. If you have any leads, we hope you will notify us immediately."

Hanging up the phone, Hong Shiwu let out a deep breath.

His eyes still held undisguised horror, as if waves of turbulent emotions were churning within them.

Thirty men—just those on the street.

Nearly twenty more were on the other side of the alley.

More than fifty of his men, all wiped out—even for Hong Shiwu, this was an absolutely devastating loss.

Most terrifying of all was that so many had died without obtaining a single clue; this was the most unacceptable aspect.

"Useless, a bunch of useless trash," Hong Shiwu overturned everything on the table.

However, he then looked at the two people standing before him.

These two were Hong Shiwu's core subordinates. One was Su Su, who had followed Hong Shiwu from the beginning and had always helped him manage the dark web operations.

Next to Su Su stood a white man in his forties, wearing sunglasses and a suit, with his chest puffed out and head held high.

This man had always been Hong Shiwu's top enforcer and his most powerful fighter.

For a long time, Hong Shiwu managed to expand his business so large without being discovered, steadily advancing and expanding offline. He had Su Su managing the online operations, and his top enforcer, his most formidable subordinate, Kroder.

Kroder was a terrifying legend. In his early years, he served in the special forces of Big Cat, later joining the internationally renowned Blackwater company.

After several years, he initially wanted to go solo, but an unexpected turn of events led him to join Hong Shiwu, becoming his top enforcer—no, more accurately, his top bodyguard.

But in reality, he was the top commander responsible for the entire offline operations and organizing combat power.

Not only that, Kroder even recruited several other highly skilled comrades to join him.

What, you ask why they came? As the saying goes, money makes the world go round.

Thus, thanks to the stability of both online and offline operations, coupled with Hong Shiwu's astounding intelligence, the entire Yugou.com network grew stronger and stronger, eventually reaching its current scale.

But now.

“Boss, I have to admit, we've encountered a formidable opponent this time. Even if I were to face him, this is roughly the best I could do,” Kroder said seriously.

“If you were to face him, what are your chances of winning?” Hong Shiwu asked.

In truth, Kroder was Hong Shiwu's greatest source of confidence.

Kroder thought for a moment before cautiously replying, “Judging from this operation, the opponent is very strong, definitely top-tier special forces elites. If I were to face him, I'd probably only have a 60% chance of winning.”

Kroder gave a conservative estimate.

“What? Even you only have a 60% chance?” Hong Shiwu gasped.

He admitted that he had completely underestimated Jiang Tian.

Hong Shiwu's mind had always been terrifying. From the moment Jiang Tian made his move, Hong Shiwu had received intelligence and also found Jiang Tian's intelligence information.

Finally, Hong Shiwu became certain that if Dongda took action this time, there was a 90% chance it would be Jiang Tian, ​​the one who initially discovered the case.

Because in terms of both combat power and overall strength, no one in past cases was more suitable than Jiang Tian. But this time, Hong Shiwu was determined to bury Jiang Tian in the windmill.

However, he hadn't expected that despite his high level of attention, he would ultimately underestimate Jiang Tian's combat power.

Jiang Tian had a 40% chance of winning against Kroder, which was already terrifying combat power. At least over the years, Kroder and his comrades had faced many world-class, terrifying combatants and had always held their own.

The fact that this young man, Jiang Tian, ​​could have such a terrifying chance of winning was already a terrifying assessment.

"Of course, this is just a conservative estimate, but if we are well-prepared, we can act just as quickly against that rabble, and perhaps even faster. This is a worthy opponent, perhaps even more interesting than the opponents we've faced before," Kroder pondered for a moment, a hint of interest appearing on his face.

Hong Shiwu breathed a sigh of relief.

But he never expected this.

Just then.

BOOM!!!

A terrifying roar suddenly erupted from the direction of the door.

Everyone in the room was startled, then reacted swiftly, especially Kroder, who leaped up and tried to dive behind the table as cover. In mid-air, Kroder had already drawn his gun.

In reality, even someone as strong as Hong Shiwu, who was far superior to the average person, could only manage to duck and dodge.

Compared to Kroder, there was no comparison whatsoever. This demonstrated Kroder's terrifying reaction speed and combat prowess.

But unfortunately...

He was fast, but someone was faster.



Chapter 516 Office Murder: It Ended Before It Even Began, My Mind Was Exploding


Kroder's reaction was absolutely top-tier.

In the fraction of a second that the sound appeared, he had already reacted.

"Who is it? The door's been kicked down! Who in the company would dare kick down the BOSS's door? Is it an enemy? Is it Jiang Tian? Then why is there no sound outside? If that's ruled out, who could it be? Could it be some traitor trying to attack?" In those fractions of a second, countless thoughts flashed through Kroder's mind.

But the next moment, while he was in mid-air...

Bang!!!

A muffled gunshot rang out suddenly, barely audible amidst the sound of the shattering door.

Thud!!!

Kroder, airborne at incredible speed, had never imagined he would be instantly killed under such circumstances.

Even when the bullet struck him, the adrenaline surge left Kroder completely numb.

After landing, Kroder was already behind Hong Shiwu's desk, a step away from him.

"A long-awaited surge of passion... what an exciting opponent! So, who is it?" A smile crept onto Kroder's lips.

He seemed pleasantly surprised to have encountered such an opponent.

Just then, he felt some water on his skin, and a slight itch.

Without thinking much of it, Kroder scratched it off, quickly trying to stand up to retaliate. But as he tried to stand, his legs inexplicably gave way, and he almost fell to the ground. He barely managed to stay upright by holding onto the desk.

"Just being kicked in the door, and you're so scared your legs are shaking and you can't stand? An interesting opponent. Even I probably couldn't kick down a door like that. Is it because I'm too hot-blooded that I can't stand?" Kroder chuckled.

But just then, a terrified and panicked voice rang out beside him: "No, you've just been shot."

"Boss, are you joking? Shot? How could I be shot?"

Upon saying the last sentence, seeing the panic creep into Hong Shiwu's otherwise calm expression, Kroder's expression froze slightly. He looked down and finally saw the scene he least wanted to see.

Blood.

He had just casually scratched an itchy spot, ignoring the dampness. Now, looking down,

his hand was covered in blood.

In other words,

he had been shot.

"Hiss!!!"

At that moment, his body seemed to only just react. A sharp pain shot through his thigh, but the physical pain was nothing compared to the shock in his heart.

He'd been shot.

When?

From the moment he dodged?

But how could this be?

Just now, Kroder was certain that he had used every ounce of his strength, instinctively dodging in a fraction of a second.

In those fractions of a second, he had moved, while the killer, from the moment he kicked down the door to the moment he saw him, should have already found cover and taken refuge.

Therefore.

Kroder was utterly baffled.

Thump thump thump!!

Just then, unhurried footsteps gradually entered from outside the door.

Shadows loomed, and Jiang Tian stared blankly into the room.

"I just heard someone say they have a 60% chance of winning against me. Well then, I invite the gentleman with the 60% chance of winning to come forward and test his skills with me." Jiang Tian cracked his neck.

His voice slowly carried to everyone's ears until Jiang Tian reached Su Su's side.

In truth, Hong Xun had already given Hong Shiwu's men a fairly complete overview of his forces. Even if there were occasional oversights, Hong Shiwu himself knew but hadn't told his son, Hong Xun.

Clearly, Su Su and Kroder weren't among them. As the cornerstone of Hong Shiwu's power, his two most important subordinates, Hong Xun naturally gave them orders immediately.

While fighting in the street, Jiang Tian had been observing to see if Kroder was there, but unfortunately, after exchanging fire for a while, to Jiang Tian's surprise, Kroder wasn't among them.

However, this had no impact on Jiang Tian.

“This must be Miss Su Su. It's a pleasure to meet you. No need for introductions; you should know who I am, right?” After stopping, Jiang Tian immediately noticed the first page of the documents on the table, which contained all his detailed information.

Upon seeing this, Jiang Tian smiled slightly and turned to look at Su Su.

Without a doubt.

Compared to Kroder's speed and Hong Shiwu's quick dodge, Su Su had nowhere to run. Moreover, the technicians' reaction speed was much slower. By the time she tried to move, Jiang Tian was already standing beside her.

But at this moment, Su Su dared not move.

Beads of nervous sweat poured down her face.

Not only Su Su, but Hong Shiwu and Kroder, these two unfortunate brothers, were even more nervous.

“What, Mr. Hong, do you need me to invite you out?” Jiang Tian looked at the desk. He had to admit, the desk was quite large; two grown men could completely hide behind it.

"How did you get in?"

Hong Shiwu's face was more ashen than if he'd eaten shit. His assistant's numerous subordinates, Kroder's men, were all elite.

Each one was terrifying in combat.

But what was most terrifying about Jiang Tian was that he'd managed to get in so silently?

"Ah, those guys...what a formidable group. Taking them out so quietly to give you a surprise certainly wasted a lot of my time and effort," Jiang Tian said calmly, seemingly with a hint of感慨 (gan3kai3, a Chinese expression of deep emotion or reflection).

This left Kroder behind the desk utterly incredulous.

"Impossible. They couldn't have been taken down." Kroder's face was filled with a cold smile.

No one knew better than him how powerful his men were. Taking down one was already impressive, but to do it so silently and without any warning was even more impossible.

That was even more impossible.

"Fine, if you're not coming out, I'm coming over." Jiang Tian didn't have time to waste. He understood the adage that villains die from talking too much, even though he represented justice.

"Now."

At that moment, Kroder gathered all his strength and suddenly raised his gun, firing at Jiang Tian.

"Kid, I'll teach you a lesson today: don't spout so much nonsense before you've achieved complete victory. Too bad you realized it too late; you'll only be able to use it in your next life," Kroder muttered to himself.

"Die!!!"

Bang!!!

The gunshot was swift, but it was Kroder who fell.

Kroder, with a 60% chance of winning, lay dead on the ground, his forehead facing the hiding Hong Shiwu. He watched helplessly as this man with a 60% chance of victory was instantly killed by a headshot, his feelings indescribable.



Chapter 517 Terrifying Storm, Scapegoating


Kroder was dead, and Hong Shiwu's heart seemed to die with him.

Was this the 60% chance of victory Kroder spoke of?

He was killed instantly without even seeing him?

As Hong Shiwu stood there stunned, he looked up and saw a young man standing expressionlessly a short distance away, silently watching him.

The pistol in his hand—at this moment, under that gaze, Hong Shiwu found himself lacking even the courage to fire.

What courage did he need?

"Where are the people protecting them outside?" Hong Shiwu's voice was hoarse.

But deep down, he seemed to still retain some hope. After all, those outside were Kroder's strongest comrades.

"Didn't I just say that it took me a little bit of time to take them out silently?" Jiang Tian's voice was calm.

But as Jiang Tian said, it only took a little extra time.

This was, after all, the Windmill Country.

At this moment, Jiang Tian definitely couldn't reveal his whereabouts.

However, it must be said that Hong Shiwu's men did possess some fighting ability, but when facing Jiang Tian, ​​they were no different from ordinary people.

Jiang Tian's fighting power was terrifying now.

Especially if he were to kill in the shadows, he would be like a dragon entering the sea, appearing and disappearing without a trace.

Thump thump thump!!!

At this moment, Jiang Tian's footsteps slowly approached, while Hong Shiwu's breathing became increasingly heavy. He held a pistol in his hand, and at this moment, he desperately wanted to fire.

But at this moment, his hand seemed to be out of control.

The next moment, a hand rested on his pistol, and then slowly took the pistol from his hand.

"I must say, you've really caused me quite a bit of trouble. Among all the major drug lords I've encountered, I acknowledge you," Jiang Tian said slowly.

Only at this moment did Hong Shiwu seem to wake up from a dream.

He looked at his empty hands, a pair of cold shackles on his wrists.

“You won’t be leaving Windmill. The entire Windmill is after you. If you show your face, countless officials will be there to arrest you,” Hong Shiwu’s voice was terrifyingly hoarse.

Jiang Tian smiled indifferently.

He really didn’t care.

If it were Jiang Tian alone, he would even dare to jump into the sea and swim to the nearest country, but now, he’d rather take his men.

Because he naturally had backup.

Never underestimate the power of a nation; even in Windmill, there are a large number of support personnel.

“Boss, everything outside is almost done.”

Just then, Zhao Wei pushed open the door and quickly said to Jiang Tian.

Even after watching it countless times, the shock Jiang Tian brought with each action was still breathtaking.

As for Zhao Wei and his men, it was naturally simple.

When Jiang Tian entered, he had killed many people; the corpses lay scattered on the ground. For Jiang Tian, ​​no matter where you hid, if you appeared within a kilometer radius of him and had a certain amount of "crime value," he could easily find you with his scanning radar.

Zhao Wei and the others followed behind, handling the cleanup.

The most dangerous place is the safest place. The entire Windmill organization probably never dreamed that Jiang Tian, ​​instead of retreating, advanced. While they were sealing off the airport, investigating transportation facilities, and checking vehicles, Jiang Tian had actually arrived at Hong Shiwu's headquarters.

That's why.

Hong Shiwu's entire office building was about five stories high, and it actually belonged entirely to Hong Shiwu. Especially the floors below the fifth floor, which should normally house many people.

For example, normal website operations, etc. After so many years of development, everything at Yugou.com was on track. However, because it was after get off work hours, only security personnel were still there; the other staff had already gone home.

Even so, clearing the entire building still took Jiang Tian a considerable amount of time.

That's why Jiang Tian's silent elimination of them left Hong Shiwu in a huge shock.

Besides.

Also in Yunhai, at this very moment, the atmosphere was extremely tense.

Minister Tan, holding the phone, repeatedly agreed with a grave expression.

After Minister Tan hung up the phone, he looked at Gao Xiaolong and said in a deep voice, "The higher-ups are already paying close attention, and Windmill has already held them accountable."

"I knew those bastards at Windmill! Hong Shiwu mobilized a large number of people to find Comrade Xiao Jiang and was killed instead, yet they're pushing all the blame onto Xiao Jiang!" Gao Xiaolong said angrily.

Xu Poye lit a cigarette, took a deep drag, and continued, "Windmill is clearly using this as an excuse to shift all the blame onto us. To maintain their facade of peace, they're blaming us for everything. Windmill is still Windmill; they've really pulled off a clever scheme."

That's exactly right.

Clearly, this incident has caused enormous damage to Windmill's reputation, resulting in terrifying losses globally.

At this point, if Windmill wants to reverse the situation, they must frantically shift the blame.

Perfect.

The Windmill organization had officials with close ties to Hong Shiwu. After their cooperation, they shifted all responsibility onto Jiang Tian, ​​who had been passively taken over.

As for whether they could actually capture him,

that wasn't within the Windmill's consideration.

Because with the entire nation's resources mobilized, if they couldn't catch this group within their borders, the Windmill organization would be finished.

"The Windmill has already issued an official condemnation, but the only good news is that so far, they haven't failed to catch Xiao Jiang," Minister Tan said quietly.

This was the only good news for them.

As long as Jiang Tian wasn't caught, the blame wouldn't be pinned on them. But if Jiang Tian was caught, things were uncertain; they might actually have to pay the price for the Windmill's reputational damage.

The Windmill had forcefully pinned the blame on them.

"Should we contact Comrade Xiao Jiang?" Xu Poye asked.

Minister Tan pondered for a moment and slowly nodded. Because it was a one-way encrypted communication method, Jiang Tian would never be able to contact them unless they took the initiative to do so.

However, its effectiveness was now minimal.

It had already caused terrifying consequences, but blaming Jiang Tian was impossible. It was simply a case of poor planning, allowing Hong Shiwu to anticipate the situation.

Moreover, it was Hong Shiwu who initiated the attack.

When Minister Tan contacted Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian had already wiped out all of Hong Shiwu's armed men.

The only two survivors were Hong Shiwu and Su Su.

As for the others, those still in the company who could be eliminated had already been taken care of by Jiang Tian.

"You said they were all wiped out?"

Minister Tan's astonished voice came through the phone again.



Chapter 518 Safe Evacuation, An Unexpected Encounter with the Nation's Goddess


"Although there were some setbacks along the way, the final result was excellent," Jiang Tian said, glancing at Hong Shiwu beside him.

"Where are you now?" Minister Tan asked hurriedly.

"We're driving Hong Shiwu's car to the evacuation point," Jiang Tian continued.

That's right.

The entire operation was planned from the beginning, including the final evacuation point.

They would then head to the harbor, board a fishing boat to the neighboring country, and then leave by plane from there.

And undoubtedly, the airline in the neighboring country had already received the news.

The evacuation route was also smoother than expected.

Or rather, even Jiang Tian himself hadn't anticipated that the whole operation would be all talk and no action. Although it seemed like there were blockades everywhere, after boarding the fishing boat at the harbor, they encountered almost no strict checks.

More accurately, it was like they were just going through the motions.

Smoother than expected.

At 3:00 AM European time, Jiang Tian and Hong Shiwu finally arrived in the neighboring country, only to encounter a minor incident.

To prevent any unforeseen circumstances, Jiang Tian and his group had booked almost entirely first-class tickets.

There were about seven people in the group, mainly including Li Qingqing.

Clearly, Li Qingqing had also left with Jiang Tian. Given the magnitude of the event, as a participant, Li Qingqing couldn't possibly remain with Windmill Entertainment. Otherwise, who knew what Windmill might do—for example, using Li Qingqing to coerce her into helping them cover up their wrongdoing?

Don't underestimate what Windmill Entertainment is capable of doing to reverse its negative image.

Moreover, Li Qingqing had indeed been with Jiang Tian. As a direct participant, if an investigation were conducted, Li Qingqing would definitely be found out.

"Finally safe." The moment she stepped into first class, Li Qingqing seemed to lose all her strength and slumped into her seat.

Heaven knows what Li Qingqing had gone through today.

She could swear that in her twenty years of life, nothing had been as thrilling as this day.

She had actually escaped the entire Windmill manhunt.

Thinking about it, it truly felt like a dream.

Zhao Wei held down Hong Shiwu, who was handcuffed but whose hands were covered by clothing, as he sat in a seat.

Gao Yangyang was also holding down Su Su.

Actually, Jiang Tian had gone to great lengths to bring Su Su along because she was in charge of the entire online operation of YuGou.com, making her the undisputed second-in-command of the entire criminal group.

In just a few years, the transaction volume of YuGou.com, and the amount of heroin it handled, far exceeded several thousand to tens of thousands of tons.

It was already a terrifying number.
