Jiang Tian looked out the window at the scenery, his gaze deep and unsettling. But then, his eyes suddenly swept behind him, his gaze unusually sharp and filled with a cold light as he scrutinized the movements behind him.

"Ah!!!"

The next moment, a startled female voice rang out.

"Yu Yu, what's wrong? What happened?"

A flurry of footsteps followed, along with anxious, worried voices.

"Sister Sun, I'm fine," a soft voice rang out the next moment.

Jiang Tian scrutinized the two women who had suddenly appeared before him, especially the one in the lead, called Yu Yu. Perhaps seeing no danger, he averted his gaze.

"Ugh, why is first class so crowded? Yu Yu, let's just make do for now, it'll be better when we get back home," the woman called Sister Sun complained incessantly.

Yang Yu swallowed hard, quickly pulled down her mask, lowered her hat, and hurried forward, head down, settling into her seat, her hands still nervously clutching her phone.

It was terrifying.

Yang Yu's heart pounded. Jiang Tian's gaze echoed in her mind—so fierce. Yang Yu swore she had never seen such a terrifying look in her life.

It was a look, like that of a murderer who had killed countless people.

Yes, it seemed like murderous intent.

And, just now, she had caught a glimpse of someone whose hands were covered by clothing.

This group of people made Yang Yu's heart pound.

Especially since, Yang Yu could clearly feel the illusion that her back was being scanned.

That's right, coincidentally, her seat was right in front of Jiang Tian.

"Yu Yu, it's safe once we're back in China. Honestly, it's too chaotic out there. I saw on the international news that there was a fierce street fight in a neighboring country tonight. It's safer back home. It's better to avoid going abroad unless absolutely necessary. Everyone says the windmills are a tourist hotspot, but even this kind of thing can happen here," Sister Sun said, sitting next to Yang Yu.

Yang Yu nodded nervously.

That's why, when Jiang Tian is restrained, he really does seem like a harmless, sunny young man. It's only because he's killed so many people that the occasional glimpse of his expression and murderous intent is a little frightening.

Sister Sun hadn't seen the murderous look in Jiang Tian's eyes just now. She glanced at Jiang Tian behind her; he was unexpectedly young and looked very sunny.

"Young man, are you here for tourism or studying abroad? You're so young, I guess you're a student?" Sister Sun asked Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow, glanced at Sister Sun, and said calmly, "Business trip."

"So young and already working? What do you do for a living?" Sister Sun's interest was piqued.

But just as she finished speaking, Yang Yu cautiously reached out and tugged at Sister Sun.

"Yu Yu, why are you pulling me?" Sister Sun asked, somewhat confused.

Yang Yu was incredibly nervous!

That look she'd just seen had left a terrifying psychological scar on her.

She could swear that look had definitely killed someone.

Her intuition was spot on.

But at this moment, she couldn't say it aloud, and sweat beaded on her forehead.

Just then, Jiang Tian's voice suddenly rang out: "Defend world peace."

*Pfft!!!*

Li Qingqing couldn't help but burst out laughing. Actually, Jiang Tian had always struck her as ruthless and decisive, the kind who kills without blinking an eye.

She mainly didn't expect Jiang Tian to be so humorous. But the key point was that Li Qingqing thought of what Jiang Tian had done, and she fell silent for a moment, as if what he said wasn't entirely wrong.

Yang Yu and Sister Sun also fell silent for a moment.

Finally, Sister Sun coughed awkwardly and said, "Young man, you're really funny."

Jiang Tian ignored her, having already sensed her identity and feeling she was troublesome, so he didn't want to speak.

The woman named Yu Yu, wearing a mask, was someone Jiang Tian didn't recognize in real life.

However, this year, a wildly popular martial arts drama, adapted from a classic martial arts novel, became a nationwide sensation. The female lead, Yang Yu, subsequently became a national sensation, hailed as a national goddess.

And this was just the beginning. Jiang Tian remembered from his previous life that Yang Yu had burst onto the scene, adapting three different works in quick succession, firmly establishing herself as the leading actress in the domestic film and television industry.

Of course, this had nothing to do with Jiang Tian, ​​and he didn't think he would ever have any interaction with her. Besides, in Jiang Tian's profession, he generally kept his distance from such celebrities.

For the rest of the time, until the plane took off, the first-class cabin fell silent. After the flight attendant left, Jiang Tian closed his eyes, feigning sleep, but actually listening intently.

At this moment, no matter how exhausted he was, he couldn't afford to make a mistake.

Especially Hong Shiwu; although he hadn't resisted much along the way, who knew what he might do in the end?

And indeed, he did.

While everyone was tired and resting after takeoff, Yang Yu got up to go to the restroom.



Chapter 519 The celestial beings are right before our eyes, even the goddess is shocked.


After getting up, Yang Yu cautiously glanced at Jiang Tian. Of all the people in first class, Jiang Tian had left the most terrifying impression on her.

Moreover, this group seemed strangely intertwined.

They seemed familiar with each other, yet they barely interacted.

Yang Yu walked with a pounding heart.

After seeing Jiang Tian feign sleep, Yang Yu breathed a deep sigh of relief.

Only after entering the restroom and locking the door tightly did Yang Yu pat her chest heavily, still feeling a lingering fear.

The return journey was even more nerve-wracking.

Because she had a feeling that the young and sunny man sitting behind her was the scariest person in first class.

Her intuition was accurate.

Fortunately, the plane reached its destination.

To be honest, this flight was a direct flight to Yunhai. Frankly, being able to fly directly from abroad to Yunhai was something Jiang Tian couldn't even imagine ten or twenty years from now.

But in this era, in Yunhai today, all of this is perfectly normal.

First-tier city.

Even now, compared to Donghai and Beijing, it's much weaker, mainly due to the support of the past decade or so. This support, not only from the central government but also from the local government, has poured the entire southeast region into the region, implementing the "let the rich get rich first to help the poor" philosophy. Ten years of development have propelled Yunhai onto a terrifying fast track of growth.

However, this developing city, because it hasn't integrated with the economies of the southeast, is essentially forcibly propelled. This is the real reason why Yunhai quickly declined after the central government stopped providing support.

As for Yang Yu.

Although their voices were very soft, Jiang Tian could still hear them clearly.

"Yu Yu, Yunhai is very turbulent, but this martial arts drama absolutely needs to be filmed here. Once we're in Yunhai, let's try not to leave the set unless absolutely necessary. Besides, no matter how turbulent Yunhai is, there's still the entire crew here," Sister Sun comforted her.

At the same time, she was also informing Yang Yu of the dangers in Yunhai.

Jiang Tian, ​​sitting in the back seat, raised an eyebrow and inwardly sighed.

To be honest, Yunhai's influence has indeed grown significantly recently. The higher-ups' concerns are justified; this media storm will only intensify and become more ferocious over time.

While Jiang Tian doesn't know the details, he can clearly sense that Yunhai's economic environment has undergone tremendous turmoil.

But some things must be done, and some malignant tumors must be eradicated.

For the sake of future secure development, Yunhai can withstand this temporary pain, and the higher-ups can certainly withstand it too.

"I heard that in Yunhai, there have even been incidents of thugs attacking armed police in the streets, and the police are making arrests every few days. Isn't it dangerous for us to come here at this time?" Yang Yu was still somewhat worried.

"Silly girl, what's there to worry about when we're with the film crew? At most, we'll just stay home as much as possible outside of filming. Besides, this film is very important to you!" Sister Sun reassured her.

Yang Yu seemed slightly relieved: "Sister Sun, you're right. Compared to overseas, our country is definitely safer."

Filming?

Indeed.

Jiang Tian closed his eyes to rest.

Throughout the journey, Jiang Tian remained constantly vigilant against Su Su and Hong Shiwu causing trouble. After all, the closer they got to the end, the more cautious he couldn't afford to be.

The period before the plane came to a stop was arguably the most dangerous.

Although they couldn't cause any trouble in front of Jiang Tian, ​​he was still afraid of creating any chaos.

Fortunately.

Hong Shiwu seemed to have resigned himself to his fate, his head bowed, his expression somewhat blank.

As for Su Su, she seemed rather restless.

In fact, for a fleeting moment, when Yang Yu went to the restroom, Su Su appeared to want to do something, but after Jiang Tian stood up, Su Su completely gave up the struggle.

However.

As the plane approached the taxiway at Yunhai Airport.

Yang Yu witnessed a scene he would never forget.

"Sister Sun, look! So many police cars!" Yang Yu exclaimed.

She was sitting by the window, pointing out the window. Sure enough, the taxiway was still in flight, but there was a police car every few dozen steps outside the airport taxiway.

How long was the entire taxiway?

Several thousand meters long!

The police cars gleamed with dazzling lights.

It has to be said that time difference makes adjusting to another time zone very difficult for ordinary people.

They arrived at three or four in the morning, and when they got here, it was still three or four in the morning—a full twelve-hour time difference.

In the pitch-black night, the police cars flashed their lights.

"Police cars," Sister Sun said, her heart pounding.

The moment the plane landed, a flurry of fully armed police officers instantly surrounded the area below, intently and nervously watching the runway.

Especially in the next moment...

"Everyone assemble!"

A voice suddenly rang out in the first-class cabin. Yang Yu, already cautious, was startled by the sound. Turning around, she saw the voice belonged to Jiang Tian.

**Whoosh!**

The next moment, everyone in the first-class cabin except for the two of them quickly stood up.

This included Hong Shiwu and Su Su. Facing Jiang Tian, ​​the two seemed truly terrified, or perhaps resigned to their fate. Hearing Jiang Tian's voice, they stood up without hesitation.

"Maintain vigilance. Disembark!" Jiang Tian ordered.

"Yes, sir!!!"

Then, led by Jiang Tian, ​​the two serious criminals were escorted off the plane.

Only Yang Yu and Sister Sun remained, stunned. They looked at each other, mouths slightly agape, seemingly filled with disbelief and shock as they watched Jiang Tian and his group's retreating figures.

Especially Sister Sun, who seemed to be from the Beijing social circle and possessed terrifying knowledge.

"Wait, isn't that... the legendary minister?" The well-informed Sister Sun exclaimed in shock, pointing down from the window.

Indeed.

Even in the dead of night, in the dead of night.

Minister Tan, Xu Poye, and Chief Gao—the three top officials—were all gathered here.

Behind them, a large number of police officers, armed police, and special police were on guard.

Personnel were stationed on all sides.

"Minister, I have accomplished my mission."

After disembarking, Jiang Tian saluted, a smile on his face.

"Everyone, salute!"

The next moment, Minister Tan raised his hand in salute, followed closely by a swift convoy of police officers behind him. The scene was incredibly impressive.

This scene was deeply etched into the mind of Yang Yu, who was still on the plane, an image she could never erase.

That young man, whom she sensed was not a good person, but definitely a vicious criminal, had just stepped off the plane, and even a legendary terrorist had to personally salute him.

That's right.

Minister Tan was a legendary figure to them.



Chapter 520 The Sea of ​​Clouds Changes, Jiang's Father Gets Promoted, Double Happiness for Fellow Disciples


Yang Yu fared somewhat better; she became a national sensation upon her debut, and with protection from others, she remained unscathed.

However, most others weren't so lucky.

While they appeared glamorous on the surface, they knew best what their reality was.

For them, someone of Minister Tan's caliber was like a god, unattainable.

"Sister Sun, that person, isn't his name Jiang Tian?" Yang Yu pointed out the window, swallowing hard.

"Wow, I didn't expect that when this person got off the plane, Minister Tan personally brought his men." Sister Sun gasped, relieved that she hadn't done anything too harsh to Jiang Tian.

"Don't overthink it. Maybe Minister Tan is just here to receive criminals." Sister Sun seemed to understand.

"That's true. This person is too young. The criminals must be very important. How could someone so young receive a meeting with someone like him?" Yang Yu finally breathed a sigh of relief.

Seemingly to confirm their suspicions, sure enough, the next moment, as Yang Yu boarded the plane, she saw a man and a woman, their hands covered, surrounded by a large number of armed police officers, who then handcuffed them.

What happened on the plane, and whether it was spying, was of no concern to Jiang Tian.

Because, barring any unforeseen circumstances, he would likely never cross paths with anyone on the plane in his future.

These were two parallel professions, with no possibility of intersection.

"The arrest must have been extremely dangerous," Minister Tan thought with relief.

Fortunately, it was Jiang Tian who was dispatched this time. If it had been someone else, the outcome was uncertain.

However, with someone else, Hong Shiwu would probably never have been caught.

"Although there were some mishaps along the way, they were minor issues, not particularly difficult. I think this mission was easy and not dangerous," Jiang Tian replied calmly.

Indeed, for Jiang Tian, ​​there was no danger whatsoever.

The problem was solved with ease.

“Xiao Jiang, although you've done a good job, you can't get arrogant. According to the interrogation, that gang called Kroder is incredibly powerful, at the top-tier terrorist level globally. You were lucky they weren't around; otherwise, you might not have made it back. Don't take such risks next time. You know, because of this risk, we've even been used against us internationally.”

Just then, a voice suddenly came from behind the three leaders.

Jiang Tian looked over curiously, only to find a completely unfamiliar face. However, the police uniform and rank insignia seemed strangely familiar.

“Who are you?” Jiang Tian frowned slightly, looking at the man who had suddenly stepped forward.

His intuition was incredibly sharp. Besides his physical abilities, the enhancements given to Jiang Tian by his superhuman abilities were terrifying—terrifying in every way.

For example, his sixth sense and intuition allowed Jiang Tian to clearly feel that there was absolutely no one in this world stronger than himself.

There is malicious intent.

Although malice was subtly present, the man's gaze made Jiang Tian uncomfortable, and Jiang Tian never tolerated anything that made him uncomfortable.

"Comrade Jiang, let me introduce you. This is our new director, surnamed Han, named Han Xian. You can just call him Director Han," Gao Xiaolong introduced.

"Han Xian?"

Jiang Tian suddenly thought of Han Qi. Han Qi was currently detained in the Zhongcheng Detention Center, and his case had been transferred to the procuratorate and was under investigation.

However, for some reason, it was still being dragged on.

Han Qi, Han Xian—what was the connection between these two? Jiang Tian recalled what he had heard before: Han Qi's third uncle was about to take up a post here and solve a case. Jiang Tian hardly needed to think too deeply.

He had put the man's only son in jail; it would be strange if he had any goodwill towards him. But Han Qi had brought this upon himself.

It was purely his own doing.

Now he was getting his comeuppance.

Ultimately, due to personal reasons and grudges, Jiang Tian was inexplicably arrested and falsely accused. His subsequent imprisonment in a private cell had extremely serious consequences and a very negative impact on the case.

Even when the matter reached the highest levels of government, contacting anyone was futile.

On the other hand, even if countless connections were used to get him released, Han Qi's entire political career would undoubtedly be over.

Given this situation, the Han family couldn't possibly invest all their remaining connections and resources in someone who was completely useless, especially to prevent any unforeseen circumstances.

The Han family further expended political resources to promote Jiang Peng ahead of schedule, elevating him to a leadership position within the municipal bureau.

"Your father has been transferred to the city, and he has decision-making power in a crucial position managing law enforcement. He's practically his superior, so you can rest assured. As for Director Han, he was just transferred here too. With us backing you up, you have even less to fear," Chief Gao whispered in Jiang Tian's ear.

Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow, looking at the man who appeared before him, who looked to be under forty.

And Han Xian...

was also sizing up Jiang Tian, ​​the man who had sent his nephew to prison.

This was the first time Han Xian had seen Jiang Tian.

But even though it was the first time...

Jiang Tian showed him no reverence or respect. Even setting aside the issue of his nephew, this displeased him, but unfortunately, as Chief Gao had said, with the three leaders present, he didn't dare utter a sound.

He could only suppress his dissatisfaction.

"Who told you we didn't encounter Kroder?" Jiang Tian retorted with a cold smile.

Han Xian was initially surprised, then seemed to joke, "After we interrogated Kroder, we reviewed his file. He was active on the international battlefield multiple times and made numerous and significant contributions, making many countries hate him to the core, but they can't do anything about him. He's not only powerful but also incredibly slippery. Defeating him, especially on his home turf, is simply impossible."

Han Xian didn't express contempt or overt mockery, but the meaning behind his words was clear.

They were relentlessly nitpicking Jiang Tian's words, attacking any errors in them, and solidifying his image as arrogant and boastful.

After learning the details, Han Xian was utterly astonished.

In fact, after Jiang Tian left Yunhai, the Han family realized that Yunhai was becoming increasingly sought-after; any delay would mean missing out entirely. With some additional resources, Jiang Peng's promotion would be effortless. Taking advantage of this opportunity, Han Xian quickly transferred over.

Upon arriving, he learned about this terrifying case.

He was overjoyed; he truly hadn't expected such a terrifying operation to occur so soon after arriving.

As for his attitude towards Jiang Tian:

He simply didn't believe it was too extreme.

But Han Xian believed that Jiang Tian was capable enough, possessed formidable combat skills, and had his own methods for handling cases, but he was definitely not as abnormal as the rumors suggested.

Because that was simply beyond human capability; the boasting was far too exaggerated.

Kroder's reputation is undeniable; his achievements are solid, he's experienced, and he led his team in home-field advantage.

To have encountered Kroder and survive is simply an unbelievable dream.

"If the Kroder you're referring to is the man in the photo, then perhaps I can tell you, Director Han, that man has already been killed."



Chapter 521 Mental breakdown, Zhao Wei alone crushed him?


As the person in charge of handling the aftermath, Zhao Wei took photos of everyone. This was a necessary measure, facilitating future investigations.

Especially overseas, killing so many people in such a muddled way would be impossible to remember, even if it were a meritorious deed!

So, following Jiang Tian, ​​they eagerly followed him. While he took down the enemy, Zhao Wei handled the cleanup, first photographing the bodies, then moving them to concealed locations.

And not just this one.

At this moment, Zhao Wei pulled out a stack of photos. Except for the initial thirty-odd people, who hadn't been photographed due to the urgency, all the others killed, including all the security personnel encountered during the raid on Hong Shiwu's company, were photographed.

Thankfully.

Kroder was a special case; he was the last person Jiang Tian killed, appearing in the office.

Zhao Wei smiled, took out the photo, and showed it to Han Xian.

The photo was brightly lit, but the scene was extremely gruesome. A bullet hole, about the thickness of a finger, was lodged in the center of the skull, and the person died with their eyes wide open in disbelief.

Han Xian, a man of experience, wouldn't be intimidated, but looking at Zhao Wei's smile, he felt a blatant mockery.

Yes.

It was definitely mockery.

"Although I don't know if Director Han knows this person, you can investigate anytime to see if I'm lying. If you haven't met Kroeder, it doesn't matter if you find Kroeder's accomplices, because I have photos of their accomplices being killed. Director Han, would you like to see them?" Zhao Wei pressed his advantage.

Zhao Wei didn't give Han Xian any way out.

Zhao Wei wasn't unfamiliar with Han Xian. He knew him from his time in Beijing and had naturally heard of him.

Of course, due to the age gap, they rarely met and had little interaction, but they were definitely acquainted with each other in Beijing.

Although Zhao Wei, who followed Jiang Tian around, lacked professional knowledge, his ability to read people was top-notch. He immediately noticed Han Xian's smiling, treacherous demeanor.

Han Xian was blatantly belittling Jiang Tian while pretending to be acting in Jiang Tian's best interest—it was utterly disgusting.

"Director Han, why aren't you saying anything? Don't you recognize him? Let me introduce you. This photo shows a key member of the Krodl gang, and this is..."

As Zhao Wei rambled on, Han Xian raised his hand to interrupt him: "Well done, Comrade Jiang, well done! Although the situation has been turned around, the international situation remains far from optimistic. Overall, while the mistakes were significant, everyone has seen the merits, which are also considerable. Comrade Jiang did a very good job."

As he said this, Han Xian's smile was forced; inwardly, he was gritting his teeth.

Because he had seen something.

Zhao Wei, that guy, somehow started wearing two medals on his chest. Upon closer inspection, wow, one was a second-class merit and the other a third-class merit.

That's right.

Zhao Wei, this guy, somehow started wearing his medals on his chest. Even Jiang Tian was astonished; this was an international operation, and he hadn't expected Zhao Wei to carry those two medals even abroad.

Actually, Zhao Wei was quite lucky. Under Jiang Tian's leadership, following closely behind, he'd smugly snagged two credits. But it couldn't be called smuggling; he'd genuinely earned merit on two previous occasions.

It could be said that while Jiang Tian took 80% of the credit for targeting and apprehending numerous criminal groups, even the smallest contribution he made was immense.

But Zhao Wei's action struck a chord with Han Xian.

Back in Beijing, Zhao Wei was someone no one even despised.

Not just Han Xian, even Han Qi looked down on him.

But now, Zhao Wei has a second-class merit and a third-class merit. Judging from the situation, he's definitely already been promoted. Good heavens, his future is incredibly bright.

But what about Han Qi, on whom they placed so much hope?

He's still in jail.

So angry.

What to do when you want to explode?

Han Xian's face flushed red and then paled. He originally wanted to establish order and authority in front of Jiang Tian, ​​to show Jiang Tian who was in charge in the city bureau.

But instead of establishing order, Zhao Wei gave him a direct rude awakening.

His face was practically slapped to the ground.

Having learned about the case, Han Xian knew about Kroeder and his accomplices. He immediately realized these people had really been killed.

"Impossible, this can't be, just these few people..." Han Xian roared internally.

Countless eyes were on him, and Han Xian felt a burning pain on his face.

Most importantly, even if Han Xian were to lose his temper, he couldn't bring himself to do it. He considered confronting Jiang Tian and had countless ways to counter him.

He thought that since Jiang Tian was young, he could make him lose his temper in front of so many people, and then, regardless of whose fault it was, Jiang Tian, ​​being the underdog, would win.

But Jiang Tian didn't make a move; Zhao Wei's few words had already thoroughly humiliated him.

However, this wasn't the end.

Minister Tan turned and frowned, looking at Han Xian with displeasure. "Director Han, if you don't understand the case and its development, then don't spout nonsense in front of so many people. You're blaming Comrade Jiang based on Feng Che's one-sided account. I don't understand what you're trying to do, but whatever you do, you can't wrong a comrade who bravely took responsibility and made great contributions."

His tone gradually became harsher as he spoke.

And the scene erupted in cheers.

There were people from the Municipal Bureau.

They were even more excitedly watching the spectacle.

You know, those who stayed at the Municipal Bureau, like Li Zijin, were practically having enough these days.

This Han Xian, for some unknown reason, was aggressively cracking down on drug trafficking. He wasn't trying to cause trouble, but he was constantly running to their branch, holding meetings and preaching his values, saying the branch wasn't just one person's branch, but the whole branch's.

And the credit wasn't just one person's, but everyone's.

Impulsive mistakes are huge; Jiang Tian made a terrible mistake, and everyone shouldn't follow his example, etc.

There were still some cases being processed, and he insisted on interfering, making reforms, changes, and adjustments. They were already incredibly busy, and now Han Xian had brought them an enormous workload.

The key is that almost all of this work was worthless, sometimes even detrimental, turning simple tasks into complex ones.

But as the saying goes, no matter what, they had to give Han Xian, the new bureau chief, face.

Moreover, he had just taken office, and even the provincial organization department had come along.

For them, the short time had felt like an eternity.

Minister Tan was about to lose his temper, causing a stir, but many people from the municipal bureau looked on with amusement.

It was as if they wanted to see Han Xian suffer, and they would be ecstatic.

*Clap clap clap!!*



Chapter 522 The Three Giants' Massive Disappointment: Han Xian Was Completely Stunned


Han Xian felt a series of resounding slaps on his face at Minister Tan's words.

Even though nothing had actually happened, his face was completely flushed red.

Now, Han Xian even wished Minister Tan would slap him twice to make him feel better, because it was obvious that Minister Tan's words, spoken in front of everyone, were a sign of disapproval.

His face was red, but Han Xian felt a chill run through him.

There's a saying that goes, there are no secrets in the system. What you think is a secret can spread throughout the entire organization within a day.

Especially something as explosive as today's incident.

Minister Tan's disapproval of Han Xian's abilities as the newly appointed Director of Yunhai and his belief that Han Xian's work performance was poor—Han Xian was certain that this would reach the provincial level by the next day.

When the local leaders heard this rumor—no, it wasn't even a rumor; Minister Tan had indeed publicly expressed his dissatisfaction with him—what opportunities would he have left?

But how could that be?

How could Minister Tan speak to him like that? Han Xian's mind went completely blank.

He couldn't even believe the consequences.

But what shocked him even more was that Minister Tan, as the most powerful and senior officer in the police force, couldn't possibly be unaware of the devastating impact such a statement would have on someone of Han Xian's position.

In the short term, he hadn't achieved his goal of establishing authority; instead, he'd been trampled on.

How could he possibly lead the entire city bureau after this?

Minister Tan couldn't possibly be unaware of this.

In the long term, if word got out, it would be a terrifying disaster for Han Xian's political career.

"You have no right to speak without understanding the situation. What have I been saying in meetings all along? No one understands anything, just makes assumptions. Do you know how much Comrade Xiao Jiang has sacrificed? You're just relying on what you think you understand, your subjective interpretation of the situation, and you even believe it to be true. If that's the case, I'm very worried about whether you're capable of being the bureau chief," Minister Tan said bluntly, his brow furrowed.

What a joke.

Let alone Han Xian, even Jiang Peng—to be precise, Minister Tan has never thought highly of him, even now, always believing Jiang Peng lacks ability.

In fact, Minister Tan did pull strings behind Jiang Peng's promotion. After all, the position of Director Yunhai is too important; Jiang Peng's abilities have always been insufficient there.

Everyone can sense that Jiang Tian is handling more and more major cases, exceeding Jiang Peng's capabilities. But this is just the beginning; who knows how many more dangerous and perilous cases await in the future.

Replacing him is something Minister Tan is happy about.

In fact, no one knows that Minister Tan initially had high hopes for Han Xian.

After all, at the very least, he shouldn't be worse than Jiang Peng.

Promotion is reasonable; on the other hand, it's also giving Jiang Peng a higher but easier position, a reward for his many and significant contributions to Jiang Tian.

And Han Xian—Minister Tan was hoping for some surprises from him.

And this is the kind of surprise he got?

"I'm telling you, Comrade Jiang has made outstanding contributions. No matter who it is, I won't allow anyone to frame him. Otherwise, I'll be the first to punish them. Director Han, you've disappointed me greatly." Minister Tan didn't seem to understand the exchange just now.

But Jiang Tian was certain that Minister Tan, this old fox, had absolutely seen through it.

"Do you understand?" Minister Tan raised his voice.

However, disappointment was undeniable in his tone.

In reality...

Jiang Tian didn't know that Han Xian's grandfather was very familiar with Minister Tan, and Minister Tan greatly admired him.

He had thought that Han Xian, upon arriving in Yunhai, would be different from Han Qi; at least he would have some of his grandfather's charisma, able to prioritize and focus on handling cases.

But before he even arrived, there was already backstabbing and infighting.

This utterly disappointed Minister Tan.

Whoosh!!!

Han Xian's face instantly turned deathly pale, and his heart sank into a chilling abyss.

No, even his feet felt icy cold.

His entire body felt cold, as if it had lost its temperature.

"I..." Han Xian tried to say something.

But Minister Tan had already walked past him without even glancing at him.

Chief Gao also shook his head, a hint of disappointment on his face.

"No, I understand."

Suddenly, a thought flashed through Han Xian's mind. He wasn't the young Han Qi anymore; he suddenly remembered what had happened today.

He had been specifically called here today, initially thought to be just a casual call to receive prisoners.

But now he understood.

The real culprit, even Shen Han, hadn't come. Why had he been called here? This seemed to be a test, a way to see if Han Qi would act impulsively or harbor personal grudges against Jiang Tian after he went inside.

But clearly, this test...

He acted madly.

"I..." Han Xian's face was pale.

Especially when he saw the three giants surrounding Jiang Tian, ​​praising him highly.

As for Hong Shiwu and Su Su, they had already been taken away by escorts.

“These people are too crazy, extremely vicious. There are many dangerous elements hiding in Yunhai. We can't be sure if anyone got wind of this and took them hostage, so we brought a larger group, and naturally, the scene was a bit larger,” Minister Tan said to Jiang Tian with a smile.

Although, after two previous raids, most of the criminals had been dealt with, and those with real power, seeing how tragically their predecessors died, would definitely not dare to continue doing this.

But Hong Shiwu was too important.

In other words, Hong Shiwu's existence was the most crucial link in reversing public opinion and shattering the prevailing narrative.

The large number of escorts was understandable.

This time, clearly having learned from the two painful lessons, the security forces around the escort vehicle were terrifyingly excessive.

Dozens of police cars were providing full protection.

They escorted Hong Shiwu to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Jiang Tian glanced at Han Xian but then ignored him.

It's too late to understand now.

To Jiang Tian, ​​Han Xian posed absolutely no threat. From this very first encounter, it was clear that Han Xian had suffered a complete defeat.

Moreover, after tonight's lesson, Jiang Tian knew even more that Han Xian would be completely quiet for quite some time to come.

"Little Jiang, are you hurt anywhere?" Chief Gao, even forgetting his own granddaughter, was concerned for Jiang Tian first.

"I'm not hurt. This kind of danger is nothing to me. It's just like going on a trip abroad," Jiang Tian said casually.

It really was like a trip. In both his past and present lives, this was Jiang Tian's first time abroad. Overall, he broadened his horizons.

"By the way, who is this person?" Minister Tan suddenly looked at Li Qingqing.

Jiang Tian's face lit up, and he recounted what had happened.

Minister Tan was shocked, feeling a chill run down his spine: "Are you serious? Is it really possible to suppress and calm without side effects?"



Chapter 523 Li Qingqing's Secret Identity, Mysterious Phone Call


Talent.

What's most lacking in this era? Undoubtedly, it's talent.

Clearly, Li Qingqing is the cream of the crop. As for whether her discovery is genuine, it's simple; a few experts can easily verify it.

Moreover, it's obvious that staying in Windmill Country is out of the question. At this point, Windmill Country is frantically investigating everywhere, so it'll be easy to find out.

Following this, Jiang Tian didn't get much rest. Although he should have been given a break at this time, the situation was urgent.

Minister Tan pulled Jiang Tian directly onto the bus, his face flushed with anger. "Damn it, those bastards from Windmill are blatantly lying, constantly smearing us, saying we crossed the border, condemning us, and claiming all the chaos is our fault."

"If you hadn't caught Hong Shiwu, you would have really suffered this in silence," Chief Gao added angrily.

After all, this has huge international repercussions; one wrong move could easily lead to serious consequences and problems.

Building a good reputation is difficult; it requires gradual, step-by-step effort. But ruining it can happen in an instant.

With Hong Shiwu, they now have a decisive weapon.

The Windmill criminal den harbors world-class criminals.

Anyone with influence knows what kind of place the Windmill is; it's practically a retirement home for organized crime, with various underworld bosses residing there.

Think about it, can such a place be safe?

"It's just a coincidence that we were caught on camera and exposed, and now all the blame is being pinned on us." Minister Tan's temper flared.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, remained silent. He only needed to listen to the bigwigs.

Although Jiang Tian had accumulated many merits and now held an extremely important position, he clearly understood his limitations.

He could be arrogant when dealing with criminals, but within the system, and with powerful figures, Jiang Tian always maintained a humble attitude.

As long as he didn't provoke anyone, Jiang Tian almost always remained humble.

It was precisely because of this performance that Jiang Tian held immense weight in the hearts of these powerful figures, who all held him in high regard.

Now, Jiang Tian had done everything he could.

And everything he couldn't do, he had done.

Actually, Han Xian's words weren't entirely wrong. It's unrealistic to say he was completely without fault, because the entire case was uncovered by Jiang Tian. Although the cross-border arrest had higher-ups' opinions, overall, he had performed meritorious service, and the credit belonged to Jiang Tian. Any mistakes, naturally, were also his own.

If Hong Shiwu had died, there would have been no way to verify the truth, and the damage to the country's reputation would have been immense. This huge mistake could have become a stain on Jiang Tian's entire political career.

In other words, Jiang Tian's perfect resume had also been marred by a major blemish.

This would have been a huge blow to Jiang Tian's future path.

Fortunately, all the mistakes had been made up for.

He had even been able to directly confirm that Windmill Country was harboring a world-class criminal organization.

It can only be said that the windmill project backfired spectacularly.

However, Jiang Tian couldn't help but consider that his actions this time were definitely due to carelessness. If he had been more careful, this problem would never have occurred.

This made Jiang Tian constantly warn himself that he must never make such a mistake again.

"Qingqing, where are you from?" After complaining about the windmill, Minister Tan suddenly remembered that there was an outsider, Li Qingqing, and quickly stopped his complaints, asking her with a puzzled look.

Minister Tan felt that he had heard the name somewhere before.

Furthermore, Minister Tan looked closely at Li Qingqing, and there was an inexplicable familiarity between her brows, which made him even more curious.

"From Beijing," Li Qingqing replied.

"Beijing?" Minister Tan's tone rose slightly.

But Li Qingqing didn't say anything more, remaining silent, showing no interest in talking to Minister Tan, and instead excitedly pulled Jiang Tian aside to talk about something.

That's right. After Li Qingqing got into the car, she barely gave anyone time to react, pulling Jiang Tian to sit beside her.

Her eyes seemed to be completely filled with Jiang Tian's image.

Indeed.

After all, it's the old saying: the hero rescuing the damsel in distress may be cliché, but it's precisely because of its terrifying power that it has become a classic trope passed down for thousands of years and still plays a significant role.

Jiang Tian's sudden appearance, followed by her charging ahead and then setting up fire so they could escape while she covered their retreat.

Li Qingqing witnessed all of this firsthand, and especially for a young girl like her, the impact was terrifying.

As for Minister Tan?

What did her position matter to her?

She had absolutely no interest in paying attention.

This, however, made Jiang Tian slightly thoughtful. Looking at Li Qingqing's exquisitely beautiful features, dressed in what appeared to be an ordinary dress, yet possessing an elegant air that was far beyond the reach of ordinary people.

Could it be that Li Qingqing's background was also extraordinary?

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue inwardly.

Especially since he could tell that Li Qingqing wasn't pretending; she genuinely couldn't be bothered to acknowledge Minister Tan.

Most importantly, when Minister Tan tried to say something or ask something, Li Qingqing didn't even turn her head, instead whispering to Jiang Tian about the scenic spots in Yunhai.

Jiang Tian helplessly shrugged. After all, if her identity wasn't simple, Li Qingqing's research seemed more authentic.

Although...

This kind of drug, even if it could calm the mind without side effects and had significant effects in countless fields, its impact on drug control wasn't as great as imagined.

The most harmful aspect of this kind of thing is its psychological addiction.

Physical addiction is secondary; psychological addiction is what you'll have to overcome for a lifetime.

Once you're hooked, your life is over. You'll be tormented for life. Even with forced withdrawal, official statistics show the relapse rate is terrifyingly high.

But the advantage of this is that if Li Qingqing develops a method, it could reduce physical pain during forced withdrawal—and reduce it indefinitely.

This is a breakthrough.

For those truly wanting to quit, it's a true miracle drug.

After arriving at the Municipal Public Security Bureau, Jiang Tian first had Yu Hui take Li Qingqing to rest. He then immediately began giving his report on the mission and meticulously documented the entire process.

However, Jiang Tian was unaware that...

While he was giving his report, in the break room, Li Qingqing, finally having some free time, was on her phone, talking to someone on the phone.

"Dad, I'm back in China."



Chapter 524 Drawing a Line, Political Rewards


"Ah, why are you back so suddenly?" A stern voice came through the phone.

Li Qingqing sighed softly, "Your daughter almost didn't make it back."

"What happened?" The voice on the other end of the line instantly became anxious and explosive.

"When I was at the windmill, I was surrounded by several thugs when I went out. They were all armed." Li Qingqing's voice became calm.

The person on the other end seemed completely unsurprised by Li Qingqing's calm attitude.

No, perhaps this was simply Li Qingqing's true nature.

Her eyes were cold and indifferent, like still water.

"And then, daughter, are you alright?" The voice on the other end of the line was clearly on the verge of exploding with anxiety.

"I was rescued." Li Qingqing said calmly.

"Who? Did he come back with you? I want to treat him to dinner." The voice on the other end of the line said.

"Jiang Tian."

"Jiang Tian?" Li Qingqing's father was clearly bewildered.

Especially bewildered by those two words.

"Which Jiang Tian? Is he one of our compatriots?"

"Jiang Tian from Yunhai," Li Qingqing replied.

Hmm?

On the other end of the phone, in a cozy apartment building, Li Xianying stood by the window, her heart pounding, listening to her daughter's indifferent voice.

Li Xianying was all too familiar with her daughter's attitude.

When Li Qingqing was very young, she was frail and sickly, spending her time at a Taoist temple taking medicine and recuperating until she started school at six, when she began to experience the outside world.

However, Li Qingqing's personality had always been like this—detached from worldly affairs, seemingly uninterested in many things.

As for her studies, perhaps her intelligence was truly extraordinary; she learned things effortlessly, which gradually led Li Qingqing to show less and less interest in people or things.

Li Xianying worried that things would go wrong if this continued, so she sent Li Qingqing abroad as an exchange student to broaden her horizons. She never expected such a terrible thing to happen.

So close, so very close.

Li Xianying nearly collapsed.

Of course, most importantly, Li Xianying heard a different tone from Li Qingqing.

Before this, Li Qingqing had never repeatedly mentioned anyone like this, especially not as a key figure, showing an unusual level of attention and concern. This filled Li Xianying with overwhelming joy.

However, as the name appeared, Li Xianying gradually frowned.

Then his frown deepened.

Until, Li Xianying exclaimed, "You mean, Jiang Tian from the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau?"

"I'm at the City Public Security Bureau right now," Li Qingqing confirmed.

"Hiss!!!"

Li Xianying sat bolt upright, as if from a nightmare.

Today's news had blown the whole world up; how could he not know? That meant...

If Li Qingqing was right, Jiang Tian had initially rescued his daughter from those armed drug addicts, and then faced a large-scale attack by armed men.

But Jiang Tian, ​​with his daughter, had single-handedly wiped out dozens of enemies.

And after taking his daughter with him, he wiped out the enemy's stronghold, then, for safety, returned home with his daughter.

The most terrifying part is...

Li Xianying wasn't unfamiliar with this case; he'd even paid special attention to it today. There was no way around it; it had become such a huge scandal.

Even the most popular TV stations were interrupting their broadcasts with this news.

But he never expected his own daughter to be involved.

"No wonder I saw someone who looked like you in that video from the US program. I thought I was mistaken," Li Xianying muttered to himself, seemingly lost in thought.

Then he quickly snapped back to reality. It really was his daughter.

That meant that dangerous shootout...

"Not just a shootout, Brother Jiang also successfully captured a world-class international drug lord," Li Qingqing said.

Hiss!!!

Li Xianying gasped in shock, feeling a chill run down his spine.

"You were with him too?" Li Xianying asked incredulously.

“Dad, with Brother Jiang here, I'm safe. If I hadn't come with him, I probably would never have returned from the Windmill,” Li Qingqing explained calmly.

Li Xianying's heart pounded wildly, a feeling of relief washing over him.

And, incredibly, a shock.

It was indeed shocking.

He knew about the Hong Shiwu case.

Although he was a local official in Beijing, as the head of a directly administered municipality, Li Xianying held a very high position and rank.

He wouldn't normally pay attention to normal cases.

However, the Hong Shiwu case was enormous, the kind that would shake the world, the kind with terrifying international influence. Li Xianying learned about it immediately.

He also knew that Minister Tan was personally planning an operation against Hong Shiwu in Yunhai.

And the news reports of the street brawl, followed by the Windmill's rapid condemnation of them, etc., undoubtedly indicated that it was their people sent there—that is, Jiang Tian—who had been exposed as soon as he arrived, meaning the plan had most likely failed.

Now, he realized that not only had the plan succeeded, but they had even managed to rescue his daughter as well.

Li Xianying's feelings were incredibly complex.

"Wait, you just called him 'Brother Jiang,' are you two that close already?" Li Xianying continued, still incredulous.

"Does it matter?" Li Qingqing replied calmly.

But Li Xianying clearly remembered that just now, when Li Qingqing, his precious daughter, spoke of 'Brother Jiang,' her tone wasn't so calm!

He had supposed to be used to the calmness, but why this sour feeling inside?

"That's it." Li Qingqing hung up the phone without giving Li Xianying a chance to speak.

Li Xianying lowered the phone from his ear, his expression gradually becoming silent. He looked at the moonlight outside and slowly exhaled.

"Jiang Tian, ​​although that kid is indeed good, but..."

Li Xianying shook his head, not wanting to think about it further. Perhaps he was overthinking it.

He took out his phone, still uneasy, and called Minister Tan directly. After inquiring about the details, Li Xianying said directly, "This case involves serious errors. Although it's not a case of merits and demerits canceling each other out, the impact is too bad and too great. Being reported in the street for fighting is a terrible influence. However, I will help you as a reward for this."

Li Xianying's words were brief, only touching on the surface.

However, Minister Tan on the other end of the phone showed a mixture of surprise and understanding.

That's right.

They often don't speak so explicitly.

Li Xianying's directness meant one thing: she wanted to repay a favor.

She owed them a favor, and at the same time, it seemed to be telling them that Li Qingqing's problem was over.

It also seemed to be saying that she hoped the relationship between Jiang Tian and Li Qingqing would also end there.

Many things are understood implicitly; a single sentence suffices, too much is undesirable.

Both sides understood each other's meaning.

"Xiao Jiang, it seems your August 1st position is secure. However, try to avoid contact with Qingqing in the future..." Minister Tan's gaze was complex.

Jiang Tian looked up, his eyes deep and unfathomable.



Chapter 525 Jiang Tian, ​​Leader Tan publicly declares himself his successor


"Xiao Jiang, don't overthink it," Minister Tan sighed.

"There's nothing between me and Li Qingqing."

Jiang Tian wanted to curse inwardly.

As a psychology expert, how could he not know what Li Xianying meant? She probably knew the whole story and was afraid of having that kind of relationship with Li Qingqing.

But wasn't this utter nonsense?

From beginning to end, Jiang Tian had never considered these things.

Especially since Jiang Tian originally wanted to introduce Li Qingqing to Zhao Qiuni, so that if Li Qingqing had any research achievements, he could use Zhao Qiuni to promote them.

But now he realized that her connections were incredibly powerful.

Also, she was probably afraid that Jiang Tian would try to take advantage of her kindness.

But this was too much of an insult to him.

Jiang Tian's anger flared. His current achievements were already remarkable enough, and even his family background was among the highest in society.

After all, Jiang Peng is currently a political and legal leader in Yunhai, and barring any unforeseen circumstances, he will remain in this position until retirement.

However, if Jiang Tian works hard before retirement, and with the help of higher authorities, elevating Yunhai to a directly administered municipality is still a realistic possibility within his lifetime.

This is already the absolute ceiling for an ordinary family.

As for Jiang Tian...

He already has numerous achievements: seven first-class merits, one special-class merit, a first-class model worker award, the title of "Progress Pioneer," and recently, the international cases involving Shimiao Village and Hong Shiwu and his son.

And this case, especially Hong Shiwu's case...

To be precise, Jiang Tian made a mistake.

But at the same time, Jiang Tian completely rejects this mistake.

At least Jiang Tian himself has done a perfect job.

"Minister Tan, although there were twists and turns, it was ultimately resolved perfectly. Aren't they just using this achievement, using my own achievements to repay a favor to themselves?" Jiang Tian sneered.

Although he never intended to exploit this relationship to gain anything through Li Qingqing, her actions infuriated Jiang Tian.

Minister Tan sighed slightly, "Jiang, you need to understand one thing: the essence of politics is a process of compromise. This weakness of yours is fine if it's not brought up, but if it's blown out of proportion, things will be very difficult for you. Even if this achievement is extraordinary, if you're given an exceptional special merit, no one can say anything."

"That sounds nice. If they didn't interfere, I would have easily taken this credit. But they're standing in the middle, simply remaining silent, which means they've repaid me a favor. Although I didn't consider it a favor, just something I did casually, do they really think I'm a beggar?" Jiang Tian sneered.

If the situation weren't so dire, Jiang Tian would have wanted to shout, "Thirty years east of the river, thirty years west of the river!"

But Jiang Tian understood even more clearly...

His terrifying achievements—a mere second-level police inspector at twenty—while seemingly terrifying and even godlike, were actually at a monstrous level.

However, within the system, Jiang Tian was acutely aware of his own limitations.

Li Qingqing's background was something he couldn't possibly exploit, but that wasn't a reason for her to insult him like this.

"I know, but politics is about compromise, Xiao Jiang. I know you understand all this, you know what's going on, but this is reality. If one day you succeed me, those who look down on you now will become your most loyal followers in the future," Minister Tan said earnestly, looking at Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian's face froze instantly.

This...

Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.

Seeing Minister Tan's completely serious expression, Jiang Tian couldn't stop his heart pounding.

"But not now. We all know what sacrifices you've made. Youth is your greatest asset, but also your greatest weakness. If you were older, with a few more years to recover, not only Li Xianying, but even her whole family opposing you wouldn't be able to stop you," Minister Tan said regretfully.

Hong Shiwu is very important.

It was so significant that after being detained at the city police station, he wasn't interrogated immediately. Instead, he was given a luxurious private cell, nutritionally balanced food, and two prison guards stood by, observing him closely.

Hong Shiwu's meals were countless times better than those of these two guards.

In other words, due to the time difference, Hong Shiwu was provided with the best accommodation and, when hungry, the best and most abundant food in the entire city police station.

Even cooking was done by two professionals who accompanied him throughout the entire journey.

The security was absolutely top-notch.

Ultimately, Hong Shiwu's appearance will be the most effective means to refute the rumors circulating online.

No wonder, since Hong Shiwu's arrest, the rumors have become increasingly agitated, smearing the motherland in various ways and constantly clamoring internationally for the handover of Jiang Tian.

As for how they know Jiang Tian...

That's because of Hong Shiwu, who provided all the intelligence from the very beginning.

The motherland, of course, wouldn't admit it, but the key issue remained unrefuted; they simply kept shifting the focus to international crime.

Windmill, naturally, wouldn't just swallow its anger. Having finally found an opportunity to suffer a loss, it was determined to recoup everything it had lost, with interest.

But all of this was contingent on Hong Shiwu's arrest.

"In other words, Windmill itself didn't know that Hong Shiwu possessed such terrifying power and skill, as well as such staggering assets. Otherwise, Windmill would have been countless times more reckless, and would have pursued the arrest without hesitation."

Outside the lounge, Jiang Tian and the three giants stood watching from the outside. It was a one-way mirror, a private room.

However, there was a single-sided window. This window allowed a bright view from the outside, but the inside couldn't see anything outside.

Now, the four of them—Jiang Tian and the three giants—stood outside the window.

As for Han Xian, what he's doing now is no longer Jiang Tian's concern.

Or rather, Jiang Tian never considered Han Xian a true rival.

Originally, Jiang Tian thought that if Han Xian was extremely cunning and ruthless, his future would likely be even more turbulent.

However, he never expected Han Xian to deliver such a huge surprise.

Or rather, they never considered Jiang Tian's standing in the eyes of these three giants. To personally greet him at the airport, thinking it was all because of Hong Shiwu, would be a grave mistake. It was all because of Jiang Tian; they personally went to welcome him.

Because they misjudged his importance, the three giants immediately abandoned him.

In other words,

Given the opportunity in the future, Han Xian would probably leave Yunhai immediately.

Therefore, Han Xian's fate is essentially sealed. Unless there are particularly significant changes, his future is essentially over.

"Billions of US dollars can indeed drive everyone and even powerful forces crazy," Minister Tan sighed inwardly.



Chapter 526 Another earth-shattering explosion, breaking historical records with 140 tons.


This achievement is already phenomenal.

Anyone else would have easily won.

They wouldn't have been so easily manipulated.

Li Xianying's words were more of a threat.

"I'm giving you a chance, and I'm giving you a reward. I hope you won't be ungrateful, and that you understand your place and position, and don't have unrealistic expectations."

"Although the achievement is enough, the mistakes you made also make it easy to control you."

Unless..."

"Heh, unless you present an even more shocking and impactful achievement," Gao Xiaolong sighed slightly.

But how easy is that?

It's almost impossible.

At the very least, there needs to be a nationally significant landmark case.

At this point, Gao Xiaolong had already done his best, or rather, they had done their best, but many things aren't things they can do just because they want to.

It's not a one-man show.

There are many things, especially those like the August 1st Incident, whose rigorous evaluation process makes it difficult even for them to intervene.

"Alright, Xiao Jiang, you'll have plenty of time and opportunities in the future," Minister Tan offered a slight word of comfort.

After all,

the other party doesn't belong to this system. Although they know how much effort Jiang Tian has put in, how much he's contributed, and how significant his achievements are,

knowing this doesn't really help them.

They can easily exploit even the smallest mistake.

This anger has to be swallowed.

Even Minister Tan felt angry for Jiang Tian.

"Alright, Minister, I know the gravity of the situation. I'm not angry. I was just a little flustered. I don't really need to win the August 1st Incident. If I lose it, I'm lucky. It's mainly what that person said that upset me," Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

He admitted that he had indeed been furious.

His hard-earned achievements were treated as if they were a handout.

So, not interfering is considered handouts.

Perhaps they were simply used to this way of interacting, and Jiang Tian could only force himself to endure it; what else could he do?

Do something illegal?

Jiang Tian would never do such a thing.

“Xiao Jiang, if there’s nothing else, you can go back and rest. You’ve done such a dangerous and arduous mission abroad; you probably haven’t slept in a long time. I’ll give you time to rest today,” Minister Tan comforted Jiang Tian, ​​but advised him to come if he were to interrogate Hong Shiwu.

After all, Jiang Tian had handled the entire Hong Shiwu case, from investigation to discovery and finally, arrest.

If it wasn’t absolutely necessary, Jiang Tian coming would allow him to gain a deeper understanding.

Hong Shiwu was too important, and Jiang Tian needed to keep an eye on him, so he nodded slightly, preparing to leave.

But just then, a series of hurried footsteps sounded.

Bang bang bang!!!

The crisp sound of leather shoes clicking on the ground suddenly rang out at that moment.

Sure enough.

Jiang Tian stopped abruptly, because right in front of him, Miss Qiu's deadpan expression remained unchanged, but her skin was now flushed, as if it could emit smoke.

"Miss Qiu, is something the matter?" Jiang Tian asked, still somewhat bewildered.

"Is there something you need?" Miss Qiu's face was somewhat dazed, seemingly taken aback by Jiang Tian's calm question, unsure of where to begin.

"Miss Qiu, please continue with your work. I'll be going now." Jiang Tian knew that many cases from the Municipal Bureau had recently been submitted to the special task force.

Especially Miss Qiu, who was currently busy with numerous cases, probably reporting to Minister Tan, or perhaps having made some major discovery. Jiang Tian himself had no interest in such matters.

Right now, Jiang Tian just wanted to go home and rest.

Since his rebirth, his mind had been constantly tense, almost entirely focused on handling various cases, and he hadn't had a proper rest.

"Wait." Miss Qiu stood in front of Jiang Tian, ​​blocking his way.

"Aren't you looking for Minister Tan?" Jiang Tian asked in surprise.

Since Miss Qiu worked for Shang Mingyan, essentially being Shang Mingyan's assistant, Jiang Tian assumed she had some major discovery to report to Minister Tan.

"No, it's about you," Miss Qiu said calmly.

This wasn't because Miss Qiu had a problem with Jiang Tian; it was simply her personality—always aloof and reserved, a fact known to many.

Shang Mingyan and the others who came with Miss Qiu were almost all like this.

“Me?” Jiang Tian pointed to himself.

Miss Qiu took a deep breath. Although her expression was cold, it didn't mean her heart was.

“Just now, after our tireless interrogation, the Shimiao Village case has finally been solved. Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang have both confessed, especially Shi Hailiang,” Miss Qiu quickly said.

Because the Narcotics Control Unit clearly didn't have enough interrogation experts, and many renowned interrogation experts were with the special task force, many ringleaders or criminals in major cases were basically handed over to the special task force for interrogation.

“What did they find?” Jiang Tian knew that although the special task force was currently reorganizing, they didn't have many major cases on hand, so they wouldn't have come to him without results.

Most likely, they had already uncovered clues and found evidence.

In other words, the evidence is already conclusive.

Although Jiang Tian no longer handled the transfer of Shimiao Village, he was the one who resolved the Shimiao Village case.

“Comrade Qiu, what did the two confess?” Minister Tan raised an eyebrow.

Shimiao Village has nearly three or four thousand people; almost every household is involved.

However, the information we have so far only shows a large-scale drug manufacturing facility and a few small-scale drug-making devices. Most of the equipment was found in family-owned facilities.

Although the equipment was seized, the amount of heroin seized on-site was not high, only two thousand kilograms, even less than the amount of heroin swept from Hong Xun's Dongri warehouse.

“According to the ledgers and their transactions over the years, based on the concrete evidence we have now, the amount of heroin sold in Shimiao Village over the past ten years has now exceeded ninety tons,” Miss Qiu said without hesitation.

Ninety tons.

That's right.

This is the amount we already have.

But the three giants immediately erupted in anger.

What does ninety tons mean?

One ton is one thousand kilograms, ninety tons...

Boom!!!

It exploded...

Even the three giants nearly stumbled and fell to the ground.

“Ninety thousand kilograms?”

“This, is this a joke?”

“Holy crap.”

The three giants’ heads exploded simultaneously.

But Miss Qiu dropped another bombshell: “Not only that, this is the transaction record we’ve obtained so far, between Shi Hailiang and Shi Haichao, excluding Wenye Group’s records. Based on our current estimation, the sales amount between Shimiao Village and Wenye Group is at least fifty tons. The ledgers haven’t been found yet, but we have a pretty complete grasp of the situation.”

One hundred and forty tons?

Boom!!!

“Holy crap!!!”

“No wonder all the heroin in the world comes from here. I always thought it was an exaggeration, but now it seems this is more than that, and I even underestimated it.”

“One hundred and forty tons, a record-breaking amount, this is a complete record-breaking amount.”

“Who else?”



Chapter 527 What an outstanding comrade he was, who made him become like this?


Prior to this, a large proportion of cases uncovered in mainland China, when followed through with leads, invariably led directly to Yunhai.

Moreover, Yunhai's unique geographical location, coupled with persistent but persistent problems, insufficient attention to the issue, and the rampant presence of local criminal groups, ultimately led to a complete and widespread epidemic.

Ultimately, it stemmed from previous issues, even a degree of laxity.

The vested interests were far too large.

While developing the economy, these issues were neglected.

However, no one had ever imagined the scale would reach such a terrifying level.

A drug village.

A village called Shimiao.

It directly involved a terrifying amount of 140 tons. And is this the final figure? No, no, no, this is only the current terrifying scale of transactions with the two major powers.

As for whether the 90 tons from Shi Haichao were sold domestically or internationally, it doesn't matter anymore, because there is already concrete evidence.

“We interrogated Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang overnight. So far, apart from the Wenye Group (because Wen Yulin is seriously injured and unconscious, the exact details of the Wenye Group haven't been finalized; we've only confirmed a small portion through Wen Yulin's brothers, with the majority being business handled by Wen Yulin),”

Miss Qiu's face remained expressionless, but her voice trembled slightly and her tone was noticeably hesitant.

Clearly, her inner turmoil was far from what she appeared to be; in fact, she was struggling to suppress her shock.

Minister Tan glanced at Hong Shiwu, and seeing that he was still unconscious, he turned and led his team towards the Criminal Investigation Division.

Recently, with so many arrests, the Narcotics Control Division is severely understaffed.

Furthermore, there's another problem.

The major issues that occurred in Yunhai before mean that even transferring personnel from other branches can't guarantee the safety of personnel.

Later, when the anti-drug squad was replenished, some personnel were directly recruited from other provinces by Evergrande. Therefore, many police officers in Yunhai are actually from other provinces.

At least, after Jiang Tian returned, he noticed many unfamiliar faces among the city bureau's personnel.

"Let's go take a look." Minister Tan's pace quickened unconsciously.

One hundred and forty tons!

What does that mean?!

What a terrifying quantity!

The biggest case?

A super terrifying case?

Even though Minister Tan had considered that the amount involved in Shimiao Village over the years had reached an extremely terrifying level,

he never imagined that it would break the terrifying amount of one hundred tons.

Although it was four or five in the morning, the entire Criminal Investigation Detachment was still brightly lit.

Upon arriving at the Criminal Investigation Detachment, he saw Shang Mingyan already waiting there with his men.

"Minister Tan."

Shang Mingyan greeted Minister Tan upon seeing him, his face noticeably flushed from excitement and exhilaration.

Such a terrifying case, such a terrifying amount of money involved—even Shang Mingyan had rarely encountered anything like it in his entire life.

One hundred and forty tons!

It's safe to say that the sheer scale of the 140 tons already sold will have an enormous impact worldwide.

In fact, Jiang Tian, ​​following behind, had already considered this question, and his emotions weren't overly agitated.

Why was he agitated?

One hundred and forty tons seems outrageous.

However, there's a comparison.

And there's a very serious issue to understand: Shimiao Village and Shi Haichao's overseas employment group are actually part of the Yugou.com industry.

In other words.

One hundred and forty tons seems outrageous.

But compared to Yugou.com, it's nothing.

Now, Jiang Tian is genuinely looking forward to seeing how much transaction volume Yugou.com has accumulated over the past ten years after Hong Shiwu's full confession.

Even Yugou.com itself would probably have a hard time calculating it.

Because many offline transactions are no longer entirely controllable online, but there's no doubt that the sheer volume that Yugou.com can track is staggering.

“After Shi Haichao and Shi Hailiang gave their statements, we arranged for them to rest,” Shang Mingyan said, immediately getting to the case.

“Thank you for your hard work,” Minister Tan said.

Shang Mingyan led them directly to the conference room, where various documents were already prepared.

Thick stacks of documents—all evidence.

“Unfortunately, although there are ledgers for the transactions between Shimiao Village and Shi Haichao, there aren't any for the transactions with Wenye Group. While we can basically confirm the transactions through the sales personnel, it's still not perfect,” Shang Mingyan said with a hint of regret.

“This is already very well done,” Minister Tan said, his face beaming with delight.

There's a saying.

Just now, they were saying that Jiang Tian had enough credit, but unfortunately, not enough decisive credit.

Or rather, now all that's missing is that final push.

Now...

It's happened.

In other words...

Li Xianying's words were utter nonsense.

It could be said that Li Xianying and her group, while inside, might use Jiang Tian's medals as a form of condescension, bestowing them upon him because of Jiang Tian's mistakes or the mistakes he corrected.

Let alone Jiang Tian.

Even Minister Tan was furious in this situation, but as the saying goes, politics is a process of compromise.

"Now we don't need to be subservient to anyone. Xiao Jiang, you've done a great job," Minister Tan said, looking at Jiang Tian.

Shang Mingyan stood silently to the side.

In truth, all the credit ultimately belongs to Jiang Tian.

For Shang Mingyan, there was no sense of imbalance. She had already accumulated enough merit; she had too much to ask for.

In reality, the overnight interrogation was more of a favor.

Even if the interrogation results are out now, it'll all be for nothing.

However, Shang Mingyan really didn't feel any resentment or anything like that.

Especially after hearing Minister Tan's words, Shang Mingyan's eyes darted back and forth with a hint of doubt, but he said nothing or asked anything.

"This is all thanks to Minister Tan's excellent leadership," Jiang Tian said with slight humility.

But these words instantly made Minister Tan's heart sink.

Look at him.

Look at what a fine comrade he was!

What a spirited comrade he was, what a comrade with such sharp edges, yet his edges had been smoothed out.

What made such an excellent comrade smooth out his edges?

Ultimately becoming so smooth and worldly?

Because of certain comrades.

Minister Tan suddenly felt something he had to say.

He was very frustrated, which overshadowed his joy at solving the case.

The shocking case had been solved, but Minister Tan wasn't happy.



Chapter 528 Are you crazy? Have you torn off all pretense?


"Comrade Jiang, don't worry. You've helped our police system solve numerous major cases. Our own comrades don't need others to interfere," Minister Tan said slowly.

That's right.

Previously, this medal wasn't something Minister Tan could decide on. Besides, there was another important factor.

That is, this medal is rarely awarded by the police system; it's mostly awarded to people from various professions, mainly because the competition is too fierce.

For example, those with social influence or those who have made significant contributions internally.

There are too many like that.

So, Jiang Tian receiving this medal is extremely encouraging news for their police system.

Minister Tan remained silent in front of August 1st.

Jiang Tian endured some grievances, but to avoid further complications, he let them pass.

But now.

Just as Minister Tan was thinking this, his phone suddenly rang.

After glancing at it, Minister Tan's face remained expressionless as he looked at the name displayed on the screen. Yes, it was Li Xianying.

He called again.

As soon as Minister Tan answered, Li Xianying's voice came through the phone: "Minister Tan, Qingqing is an excellent child with a bright future, so I hope you won't overthink it. You need to understand the feelings of parents. But don't worry, I will lend you a hand."

At that moment, Li Xianying sat on the sofa, lit a cigarette, and looked at the hazy sky outside the window. Holding the phone, Li Xianying spoke with a sigh.

The hearts of parents are always full of love and worry.

Li Xianying sighed slightly.

From childhood to adulthood, Li Xianying had never seen Li Qingqing treat anyone with such special affection.

Benefiting from growing up in a Taoist temple, although she only went there periodically for recuperation later, entering society had instilled in Li Qingqing a general indifference to many things.

This was the first time Li Qingqing had shown such feelings towards a man.

But for Li Xianying...

It was clear that Jiang Tian, ​​in terms of both profession and background, was not a suitable match for Li Qingqing.

"A hero rescuing a damsel in distress, what's the difference between that and taking advantage of someone's vulnerability? Whether it's a coincidence or not, this situation cannot continue," Li Xianying scoffed inwardly.

If...

Jiang Tian were in the government system, achieving his current position at such a young age, becoming a section chief in his early twenties, Li Xianying might have given him a second thought, enough to consider him.

But it was clear...

The dangerous nature of Jiang Tian's profession, and his family background, were not factors Li Xianying considered.

Li Qingqing was young, but he wasn't. He understood that the best choice was to act decisively and cut things off as soon as they began to escalate.

He wasn't at the age where he acted impulsively or was easily moved.

He had to consider his daughter's happiness and future.

He couldn't control Li Qingqing, but he could guide Jiang Tian to make a wise choice.

If he wanted that medal.

“Comrade Xiao Jiang has already been exceptionally awarded a Special Merit. You applied for an August 1st Medal for her in the drug village case, but it's clear she's not qualified yet. Even with the later case of the fish-buying network, although the arrests were successful, the consequences were extremely serious. It could easily have led to international disputes. But Minister Tan, rest assured, if Comrade Xiao Jiang is smart enough, we are also moved by her efforts and will help her.” Li Xianying hesitated, still uneasy, before making another call.

Because Li Qingqing was his lifeline, he was afraid that what he had just said would be too explicit and the other party wouldn't understand, so he chose a simpler and more tactful approach.

After speaking, Li Xianying hung up the phone.

He trusted that Minister Tan understood his meaning.

He also knew that Minister Tan would convey his message to Jiang Tian: don't have any inappropriate thoughts.

What is yours will be yours.

But don't covet what isn't yours.

"What a pity. Such a young man, a recipient of the Special Merit Award, and multiple First-Class Merits, now poised to receive the August 1st Merit Award. Since the founding of the nation, such an outstanding young man has been extremely rare. It's truly a pity."

Li Xianying felt a pang of regret, but regret aside, he was ultimately just someone on the front lines.

While Li Xianying deeply respected and understood the greatness of those on the front lines, this was definitely not a suitable match.

Moreover, Li Xianying knew all too well how dangerous it would be for family members to be with Jiang Tian, ​​given his background.

Even if there were no other prospects, just friendship, would being Jiang Tian's friend be without danger? Jiang Tian had offended so many criminal groups; what about their retaliation?

Li Xianying himself wasn't afraid of retaliation, but he didn't want his daughter involved in these matters.

As for Jiang Tian's thoughts, they were no longer important.

In the end, Jiang Tian was just an outstanding young man who hadn't yet reached his full potential, even if he was currently exceptionally talented, even if he had achieved something truly historic.

But how far could he ultimately go? Like Shang Mingyan?

At a higher level, it's no longer about being good at solving cases or getting promoted based on that.

This requires a bureaucratic mindset.

It requires a broader perspective. Working alone will at best only get you a position at the bureau level.

Just as Li Xianying was about to hang up,

Minister Tan's voice suddenly rang out on the phone: "Don't bother worrying about it. We can handle this ourselves. If Comrade Jiang is good enough, what's his will be his, and what's not, he won't force. Besides, Comrade Jiang is an outstanding talent in our entire system, and even more so, an outstanding talent for the Party and the people. Using connections would be an insult to him. So, if he gets it, I'm lucky; if he loses it, it's fate. I really don't need your concern!"

Minister Tan's voice was calm.

Li Xianying jumped up, her face filled with disbelief.

You're actually being sarcastic to me?

Are you crazy?

Oh no.

Why are you being sarcastic and sarcastic towards me?

Li Xianying was completely baffled and deeply shaken.

He had actually communicated with Minister Tan beforehand, mainly to give Minister Tan face, asking him to simply inform Jiang Tian that he would cut off all contact with Li Qingqing in the future.

But he didn't consider that Jiang Tian had never been threatened by anyone.

Furthermore, within the same circle, everyone held the belief that having more friends is worse than having more enemies; they almost never offended anyone if they could avoid it.

They maintained a certain balance among themselves.

But he couldn't understand why Minister Tan's reaction was so intense.

Why?

Just because of Jiang Tian?

Li Xianying's eyes widened, her face filled with shock and astonishment.

At this level, these words were tantamount to a direct break in relations.

In his view, such a small matter—wasn't this insane?



Chapter 529 is terrifying; even Li Xianying is scared. Yunhai is too dangerous.


"Something's not right, something's not right."

Li Xianying paced back and forth in the room, his hands behind his back, his brows deeply furrowed.

"Xianying, what's wrong?"

Just then, a beautiful woman emerged from the bedroom, standing in the doorway and asking with concern.

"It's nothing, you should go back and rest." Li Xianying didn't want his family to worry.

Although this matter involved Li Qingqing.

"Never mind, I won't rest either. Qingqing's had such a big incident, I can't sleep if I don't go home. You go ahead with your work, I'll go make you some breakfast." The beautiful woman said, heading towards the kitchen.

Li Xianying's expression softened considerably. He was grateful that although their families had arranged the marriage, the two of them had maintained a respectful and happy relationship for so many years.

Therefore, the arranged marriage wasn't necessarily a bad thing.
