Especially for Li Qingqing, Li Xianying had a clear plan, and although some problems had arisen with the plan, the impact wasn't significant.

He couldn't accept that Li Qingqing and Jiang Tianzhen had any connection.

Jiang Tian's identity was too dangerous.

As a member of the Party, he admired Jiang Tian, ​​but as a father, he couldn't allow his daughter to have any contact with him.

Moreover, Jiang Tian seemed to have a bright future, but who could predict it? He had seen too many political upstarts fall from grace.

Too many young people were short-lived, especially in such dangerous positions.

Mediocrity, or unexpected mishaps—there were too many uncertainties, especially given the high level of danger.

In conclusion, Li Xianying didn't believe that having a relationship with Jiang Tian would be a good thing.

The disadvantages outweighed the advantages.

However, he had to admit that Jiang Tian was indeed a capable person, and his achievements were considerable. Although he didn't know much about the fish-buying website and the drug village, he knew how terrifying those achievements were.

Plus, the exceptional special merit he had previously received had already caught his attention.

So he instantly understood that Jiang Tian was a strong contender for the August 1st Merit Award, but his support didn't guarantee a significant advantage.

Moreover, the August 1st Merit Award was open to the entire society, and the police system received very little. The inclusion of Jiang Tian in the August 1st Merit Award signified recognition of the entire police force, transcending the limitations of a mere special merit rank.

The competition was incredibly fierce.

Especially for someone as young as Jiang Tian.

His own qualifications, with a few factors increasing his chances of success, were simply to prevent two people he'd only known for a day or two from contacting each other.

In Li Xianying's eyes, this was nothing at all.

He simply wanted the two to cease all contact and interaction; it didn't imply any romantic feelings between them—that was nonsense.

But Li Xianying feared his daughter would become increasingly entangled.

Why was Minister Tan not only interfering in such a small matter but also escalating the conflict?

Li Xianying took deep breaths.

However, he never considered how much influence Jiang Tian now wields in the entire police system, or how much support the three leaders provide for him.

It's fair to say that the level of support Jiang Tian received from the top three leaders within his system was far beyond his comprehension.

Having stayed in Beijing, he was even less able to comprehend it.

Jiang Tian's influence over Yunhai, and the immense sacrifices and dangers he had endured for Yunhai's current state.

The few words on the achievement report were like bullets aimed at Jiang Tian; anyone else, regardless of who they were, would likely have died countless times over.

Only Jiang Tian survived, and only Jiang Tian was special.

"Is this insane? This is utterly absurd," Li Xianying thought, his mind in turmoil.

Especially thinking of his daughter still in Yunhai, his anxiety deepened.

"The food is ready, aren't you going to eat?"

Just then, the beautiful woman came out of the kitchen and saw Li Xianying already wearing a Zhongshan suit jacket and heading towards the door.

"I'm not going. I'm going to Yunhai," Li Xianying couldn't sit still any longer.

The situation in Yunhai is incredibly complex. Leaving aside everything else, the recent major cases alone are terrifyingly heinous, each one more horrifying than the last.

It's unimaginable how dangerous Yunhai is right now.

The beautiful woman seemed to know something as well, nodding worriedly, "That Comrade Jiang did save Qingqing after all; don't make things difficult for him. But as far as I know, the situation in Yunhai is far more complicated than it appears on the surface. There are many criminals involved, and while it seems like one major case after another has been solved, the criminal organizations in Yunhai are actually far more numerous than that."

"What do you know?" Li Xianying turned around, his face full of surprise.

Because he knew that his wife worked in an internal affairs department, and some things she knew even more than he could.

Although he was in charge of the government affairs in the capital, in reality, there was a world of difference between him and the actual top leader; the difference in rank was more than one level.

There were many things that Li Xianying wouldn't know if he hadn't asked his grandfather.

In fact, there were some things he knew even less than his wife.

“Some things are confidential. But once you go to Yunhai, besides bringing Qingqing back, don’t stay too long, and don’t wander around. I only know part of it. Just remember, the danger in Yunhai is countless times greater than before it was exposed. There will likely be a period of retaliation. At that time, not only you, but the city bureau will be the first to be affected,” the beautiful woman said after a moment’s hesitation.

Hearing the beautiful woman’s words, Li Xianying completely lost his composure and exploded.

This was unacceptable.

Li Xianying was now completely convinced that what he had just done was absolutely right.

Yunhai was indeed a dangerous place he couldn’t stay in.

Although as a qualified cadre, he shouldn’t have such selfish thoughts, his daughter wouldn’t have any impact on Yunhai, nor could she maintain any peace. Besides danger, she would be of no use there.

"I understand, don't worry. I checked and there are the nearest flights. I'll be right back." Li Xianying hurriedly left.

Leaving behind the beautiful woman still standing in the restaurant, looking at the table full of food, she finally let out a soft sigh.

Yunhai is too dangerous.

"But, that young man Jiang Tian is simply too outstanding. Even the officials in the city have summoned him."



Chapter 530 Utterly Enraged: Do You Dare to Question Jiang Tian?


The beautiful woman clicked her tongue in astonishment.

However, she knew through her position that the city's officials were summoning Jiang Tian, ​​the man who had saved her daughter.

But these things couldn't be revealed; there were confidentiality regulations. Even relatives, even someone as powerful as Li Xianying, couldn't leak anything.

Li Xianying, who had left, was completely unaware of what was happening.

In fact, while Li Xianying was on her way to Yunhai,

the figure of 140 tons had already caused a complete uproar throughout the city bureau.

Next came the provincial department.

This was explosive news. Don't be fooled by Minister Tan's amiable demeanor towards Jiang Tian, ​​his consistently friendly attitude.

Especially when he heard about the massive scale of Jiang Tian's drug village case, he even expressed his joy at Jiang Tian's significant achievement.

But remember, this was only in front of Jiang Tian.

The truth was...

Minister Tan completely exploded, disregarding the fact that the other leaders were resting, whether they were working overtime or dealing with urgent matters.

He immediately convened an emergency meeting.

Minister Tan could say he had overestimated the severity of the case in Yunhai, but he never imagined that there was no limit to how egregious things could get.

It was unimaginably egregious.

One hundred and forty tons, a full 140,000 kilograms, nearly 240,000 kilograms of heroin, had been sold throughout China and overseas in just ten years.

What had they done in ten years? Why was such a large-scale operation completely undetected?

He was furious.

After offering a kind word of comfort to Jiang Tian, ​​the amiable expression on his face vanished instantly.

The crucial point was that at this moment, Han Xian appeared.

That's right. Han Xian, though he'd just arrived at the Municipal Bureau and hadn't received any news yet, was deeply anxious. After much deliberation, he seemed to suddenly realize the enormity of his mistake and rushed back to explain to Minister Tan.

And while he was begging for forgiveness and promising it would never happen again, he coincidentally ran right into Minister Tan's line of fire.

"Minister Tan, I want to apologize." Faced with the destruction of his political career, Han Xian clearly made the right choice: to bow his head and admit his mistake.

What a joke.

It's safe to say that as long as Han Xian is within the government system, anywhere in the country, regardless of his current position as a bureau chief or not, if Minister Tan and Chief Gao so desire, they can suspend him from all his posts on the spot, without even needing anyone else's consent.

They possess absolute authority and power.

When he arrived, he was full of vigor. When he took over his post, Han Xian believed it was the beginning of his glorious political career.

He hadn't even had a day to officially assume his post.

Good heavens.

It's over.

He's offended the two top giants in one fell swoop.

Being held in such a grudge by these two giants, Han Xian grew increasingly fearful and terrified, even to the point of being unable to sleep when he got back.

Especially when Han Xian called his older brother, his brother instantly flew into a rage, berating him for being stupid.

His brother's words were particularly striking: "Compared to Jiang Tian, ​​you're nothing. Don't even mention that Grandpa is gone. Even if he were still alive, he wouldn't have had much of a relationship with Minister Tan or Chief Gao. How dare you belittle Comrade Jiang Tian in front of Minister Tan? Do you know how much Jiang Tian has contributed to Yunhai, and how important he is to them?"

"Foolish, stupid! This is a major international case. If something goes wrong, who are you to criticize in front of so many people? Do you think everyone else is unaware, and only you know? You deserve to die! How could I have such a stupid brother like you?"

Indeed, after hearing the whole story, Han Xian's older brother was completely dumbfounded. In fact, during this time, Han Qi's uncle had repeatedly reviewed Jiang Tian's important position in Yunhai.

He finally came to a conclusion: it was unshakeable.

There's a saying that goes, "The earth can keep turning even without anyone."

But in Yunhai, it's no exaggeration to say that Yunhai remains the same regardless of who's gone, except for Jiang Tian. Without Jiang Tian, ​​Yunhai is a completely different city, a separate Yunhai.

This is truly a testament to his influence, capable of intimidating countless criminal organizations.

Who else but Jiang Tian could do that?

During this time, Han Xian's older brother had actually investigated the Yugou.com situation and understood the terrifying contributions Jiang Tian had made. But, let alone the fact that this mistake was something he couldn't even comment on, it wasn't something someone of his level could be involved in.

Yet Han Xian foolishly went up and made sarcastic remarks. The fact that he wasn't immediately suspended was probably because Minister Tan showed leniency because of a descendant of an old acquaintance.

"Remember, Yunhai's existence or non-existence makes no difference. Even if there were thousands upon thousands of you, staying or leaving would have no impact on Yunhai. However, without Jiang Tian, ​​Yunhai will completely cease to function. Do you understand?" Han Xian's uncle spoke through gritted teeth, barely containing his inner rage.

Han Xian, pale-faced, had rushed over after hanging up the phone, only to find himself face-to-face with an enraged Minister Tan.

"Minister, I was just about to see you to offer a profound self-criticism," Han Xian said, wiping cold sweat from his brow. For the sake of his future political career, Han Xian had truly abandoned all dignity.

Especially after his elder brother's analysis, he understood the gravity of the situation and was filled with trepidation.

"You should offer a self-criticism. Not only should you offer a self-criticism, but for the safety and property of millions of people in Yunhai, you should even submit your resignation," Minister Tan said expressionlessly.

Normally, Minister Tan wouldn't act like this, as the saying goes, "It's not right to overstep one's bounds."

But at this moment, the shocking sight of 140 tons immediately terrified Minister Tan.

He had witnessed countless heroin cases, and in all his decades of experience—in fact, in the decades since the founding of the People's Republic of China—he had never seen a case involving 140 tons, or 140,000 kilograms.

The most terrifying thing was...

What truly enraged and horrified Minister Tan wasn't the 140 tons of heroin, but rather that 90 tons of it were sold to the Southeast region through Shi Hailiang.

Only 50 tons were sold through the Wenye Group.

Then the question arises:

Over ten years, this small amount is clearly insufficient to support such a terrifying national market.

And more than half of this terrifying market flowed into the inland areas through Yunhai.

If the amount involved in Shimiao Village was only this much, where did the remaining half, with such a massive market, come from?

It's chilling to think about.

How many heinous criminal groups are hidden in Yunhai?

Two rounds of investigations yielded nothing.

This precisely illustrates the terrifying nature of Yunhai. And can Han Xian, as the frontline police chief, handle such a situation effectively?

Clearly.

Minister Tan doesn't believe Han Xian is capable of handling such a regional case.

So, besides being angry about the 140 tons, Minister Tan's greater rage stemmed from an even more important factor: Han Xian dared to pick even these kinds of peaches.

This was utterly tantamount to putting the safety of countless people's property in grave danger.

Buzz!!!

At that moment, Han Xian exploded. His face turned deathly pale, large beads of sweat poured down like rain, and his eyes widened, filled with dense bloodshot veins.



Chapter 531: Utterly Devastated – Do it if you can, get out if you can't.


If Han Xian had the ability, then this peach would be his, that's his skill. But he didn't have the ability, yet he forcibly tried to eat it. In Minister Tan's view, that's the most unforgivable thing.

Because this is playing with the lives and property of millions of Yunhai citizens.

"Minister, let me explain..."

"I want to hear your explanation now." Minister Tan interrupted Han Xian directly.

He didn't want to see Han Xian right now.

Because the more he saw him, the angrier he became.

Immediately afterwards, Minister Tan began to gather people and start a meeting.

The main focus was on why the two rounds of sweeps hadn't uncovered anything in Yunhai. Judging solely from the results, an uninformed person would think it was a very safe city.

But those who understand know how terrifying the undercurrents are beneath the seemingly calm surface of Yunhai.

As the saying goes.

Minister Tan understood, and Jiang Tian certainly understood as well.

In fact, Jiang Tian understood Yunhai better than Minister Tan, having traveled back from twenty years in the future and understanding the terrifying nature of the entire city.

Many might think that after Jiang Tian wiped out so many criminal groups, Yunhai was safe.

That's a joke.

Those criminal groups are like groundhogs. You throw a hammer down, and they hide in their dark burrows, only to pop up the moment you raise the hammer.

In the future, ten or twenty years from now, Jiang Tian personally witnessed the terrifying actions his country took to completely pacify Yunhai, and the horrific damage inflicted.

To quell these criminal groups, they practically plowed the land three feet deep, turning it upside down several times over. The entire city was placed under lockdown, and everyone was subjected to scrutiny.

This action instantly brought Yunhai's development to a standstill. While the rest of the country was experiencing economic growth, Yunhai seemed to be forgotten, relegated to the dustbin of history.

It became a relic of the past.

Perhaps many years later, someone will remember Yunhai today, a city that was once incredibly prosperous, at its peak only slightly less bustling than Shanghai's Bund.

But due to its exaggerated pace of development, Yunhai's most prosperous period was even more outrageous than Bund. While the idea of ​​gold everywhere and business opportunities being plentiful might be an exaggeration, the influx of capital companies and countless businesses, seeing the massive support from the nation, all flocked in.

Everyone thought it would become the third man-made super-tier city.

However, because of this pause button, not only did the city's development stagnate while the rest of the country was developing, but the withdrawal of numerous companies also led to a terrifying fiscal deficit in Yunhai.

From top to bottom, everyone seemed to have returned to square one overnight, as if it were all a dream, as if Yunhai had never experienced such wealth.

And it is precisely because of this...

In later generations, a small portion of the Yunhai faction still harbored ambitions and believed Yunhai had a chance; others, however, completely gave up.

They stopped thinking about anything, did nothing, and simply lay low.

Why was this?

"It's because the criminal organizations in Yunhai are too terrifying."

"It seems that on the surface, Yunhai only has scattered small fry. A sweep would result in the arrest of a large number of these small fry, but the efforts to uncover the real criminal organizations are still too limited."

Jiang Tian shook his head regretfully. In truth, he hadn't been optimistic about the two cleanups, mainly because the degree of control was difficult to manage.

In Jiang Tian's view, cleaning up Yunhai would require long-term governance.

After all, the profit from smuggling heroin was too high, leading too many people to take the risk. The existence of this market meant countless people were risking their lives.

Death didn't matter.

This required a batch to emerge, and then Jiang Tian to arrest and kill them one by one.

Then, as time passed, this business would gradually disappear.

This is a case of slowly boiling a frog; a raging fire would ultimately only result in mutual destruction.

The past life was a stark example, while Jiang Tian's approach was more like targeted therapy.

Targeted therapy avoids indiscriminate killing.

Even if it causes a terrorist impact, it will remain within controllable limits.

When he missed Han Xian, Jiang Tian glanced at him, shook his head slightly, and clicked his tongue. Jiang Tian could only say that this guy's luck was truly bad.

Normally, there shouldn't be a problem.

But now is an emergency.

One hundred and forty tons, ten years. Even if it's ten years, even if not all of these one hundred and forty tons are sold domestically, it would still be better if they were all sold domestically. At least, it would mean that the criminal forces in Yunhai would come to an end and suffer a complete and devastating blow.

But now it seems...

Most of them haven't surfaced yet.

Therefore, when Shen Han received the call at the provincial department, she was still stunned.

To be honest...

The provincial department also had some tasks today, important tasks. As mentioned before, they were going to launch the final roundup of a certain major terrorist case.

We're almost there.

Today, we need to arrest a key member.

Even Bai Qiu, the head of the brigade, who held Jiang Tian in high regard and felt the case was serious, was worried about unforeseen circumstances. For such terrifying cases, Bai Qiu even felt his experience wasn't as extensive as Jiang Tian's, as Jiang Tian had far more experience handling these kinds of cases.

However, when he tried to arrange a meeting with Jiang Tian to discuss the case, he missed the opportunity. Or rather, Jiang Tian had an even more important case to deal with.

Up until now, Bai Qiu was still thrilled by the capture of the drug lord from the Windmill area.

But then, even more explosive news arrived.

"One hundred and forty tons?" Bai Qiu exclaimed.

"Hiss, I didn't expect that drug village to be so terrifying." Shen Han had personally witnessed the operation to close the net in Shimiao Village.

But he never imagined it would be this terrifying.

"One hundred and forty tons, Minister Tan, are you serious?" Shen Han swallowed hard.

"Stop talking nonsense! Why didn't you notice 140 tons right under your noses? What are you all good for? If you can do it, do it; if you can't, get out! Let capable people do it!" Minister Tan roared angrily.

It exploded.

Minister Tan was truly furious.

To be honest, who wouldn't explode upon hearing that number?

Even Shen Han was shocked.

It was a real explosion.

"140 tons?"

Shen Han's face turned pale.

Such a horrific case, occurring in Yunhai.



Chapter 532: The reporter is coming for an exclusive interview, and the whole thing has exploded.


The only good news was that Jiang Tian solved the case, meaning he belonged to Yunhai.

Such a terrifying case happening right under their noses.

But what could Shen Han do?

For years, Yunhai had been experiencing rapid economic development, and its geographical location, making it difficult to crack down on, provided fertile ground for the growth of criminal groups.

These groups reaped huge profits.

As a result, their power expanded rapidly and terrifyingly. The fact that they hadn't been corrupted was already a testament to their exceptional strength.

Taking action against these criminal groups...

Even when they occasionally uncovered major cases, the leads quickly dried up.

Ultimately, the majority of the heroin trade in Yunhai was conducted by undocumented individuals.

The cases made almost no progress.

Over the years, it became increasingly difficult to target them.

However, at this point, Shen Han clearly had no reason to object.

"Bai Qiu, you keep an eye on the case. I'm going to a meeting at the Municipal Bureau; it's very important."

Shen Han took a deep breath, then rubbed her eyes. Bai Qiu, standing in front of Shen Han, also had very noticeable dark circles under her eyes.

Shen Han's darker skin made it less noticeable, but Bai Qiu, with her fair skin, looked like a panda.

It has to be said that recently, both the Provincial Department and the Municipal Bureau have been working overtime, pushing everyone to their physical limits.

Of course, there's good news: the cases being solved are getting bigger and bigger.

"I understand," Bai Qiu immediately said, her expression sharpening.

To be honest, Bai Qiu was also harboring a lot of resentment.

That is, as the head unit of the General Team, or rather, as the leading unit of the Yunhai Branch, they were watching helplessly as the lower branches solved one major case after another because of Jiang Tian, ​​each one more terrifying than the last.

Meanwhile, they sometimes only got a scrap of food.

Sometimes, not even getting a sip of soup could fill her with pent-up resentment.

The brigade she, Bai Qiu, led was being utterly crushed by its subordinate detachments. If they didn't make any progress, how could the brigade maintain its authority over the detachments?

If there were no opportunity, so be it, but now that one was before her, how could Bai Qiu possibly let it slip away?

Just thinking about it...

One hundred and forty tons.

This terrifying number sent a chill down Bai Qiu's spine.

It felt like an eternity. She suddenly felt that the distance to the detachment was once again infinitely far.

"Bai Qiu, don't think so much. If this case is solved, it might not be much less significant than the Shimiao Village case. At least we'll be able to turn things around and hold our heads high in front of Minister Tan," Shen Han said, seemingly knowing what Bai Qiu was thinking, immediately offering words of comfort.

Upon hearing this, Bai Qiu immediately perked up: "Don't worry, Director, we'll be able to completely close the net within the next two days at the latest."

Shen Han nodded slightly, then headed to the Municipal Public Security Bureau with a heavy heart.

It was already past 5 a.m., and Jiang Tian clearly had no intention of continuing to participate in the upcoming meeting.

The main reasons for this meeting were twofold. First, regarding the 140 tons of heroin, almost all of which was sold overseas, who was responsible for the drugs smuggled out of the country?

Second, what were they all doing? Why did such a terrifying drug-producing village remain completely undetected for over a decade by neither the local police station, the municipal bureau, nor even the provincial department?

240,000 jin (120 tons)—and no one noticed?

Minister Tan grew increasingly furious.

Jiang Tian could already imagine Minister Tan berating everyone at the meeting.

Another issue concerned Hong Shiwu.

Hong Shiwu's importance was self-evident. 140 tons was an outrageous quantity, but Hong Shiwu's importance was also undeniable. In terms of international influence, the 140 tons of heroin from Shimiao Village was nowhere near Hong Shiwu's level; at most, it was just one of many transactions under his control.

The difference in importance was obvious.

Regarding the interrogation of Hong Shiwu, after consulting Jiang Tian, ​​it was ultimately handed over to a more professional team of experts, including Shang Mingyan.

Jiang Tian had no objections to this.

After all, experts are experts, and experts with considerable authority.

Especially in interrogation, these were formidable psychological experts, although Jiang Tian could handle it much faster if he were involved.

However, Jiang Tian clearly couldn't do everything alone. Even if he himself couldn't handle it, he had to leave some room for others.

"Xiao Jiang, you are now a pillar of the nation. What, you say a meeting? No, no, Comrade Xiao Jiang, this meeting is mainly for others, not you. Listen to me, health is the foundation of everything. You went abroad overnight on an important mission, traveling back and forth constantly. The most important thing now is to go back and rest. Otherwise, if you ruin your health, how can you face the millions of people in Yunhai?" Minister Tan spoke earnestly to Jiang Tian.

Without further ado, he sent Jiang Tian away.

The attitude towards Jiang Tian was completely different from how they treated Han Xian, but neither Jiang Tian nor Han Xian seemed surprised.

They even felt it was perfectly natural.

Anyone who has traveled abroad knows how terrible jet lag can be for a person's rest—it's practically torture.

And it's torture to the point of death.

Moreover, Jiang Tian had been flying back and forth continuously, carrying out such a grueling mission; just thinking about it makes one realize how arduous and exhausting it must have been.

Jiang Tian couldn't really explain it away.

Although he didn't actually feel that tired—two hours of sleep on the plane was enough to make him feel refreshed—

seeing Minister Tan's worried expression, Jiang Tian couldn't help but feel a pang of emotion.

"Alright, go back to sleep. This is none of your business," Chief Gao said, directly urging him to leave.

And so, while the others rushed to the Municipal Bureau, about to face Minister Tan's wrath, Jiang Tian leisurely left the Municipal Bureau.

Jiang Tian was already aware of what would happen next.

Han Xian's appearance did make Jiang Tian want to laugh.

Clearly, Han Xian should have been in Jiang Peng's position now.

But Jiang Peng had now become the political and legal leader of Yunhai.

In other words, although he still worked under the leadership of the municipal bureau, his job nature had changed, and he was no longer part of the civil service.

He didn't need to attend or even participate in case meetings like this.

Han Xian had just arrived in Yunhai when he immediately faced all of Minister Tan's wrath.

"Son."

Upon returning home, Jiang Tian was surprised to find Jiang Peng sitting on the sofa, puffing on a cigarette.

"I know what you did. You're excellent, and you have 140 tons of heroin. You've really made us proud." Although Jiang Peng tried to remain calm, his trembling voice betrayed him.

"Dad, you know?" Jiang Tian was astonished.

Wasn't this just the result of the interrogation?

How come it feels like the whole world knows?

"You know? The whole of Yunhai knows. By dawn, the whole world will probably be in an uproar. And, I have good news for you. A reporter from CCTV is coming to do a thorough interview with you today."



Chapter 533 Jiang's Father, the King of Mixed Learning, a Winner in Life


Jiang Tian looked at his father in surprise. He had to admit, his father's demeanor had changed after his promotion.

In a daze, Jiang Tian seemed to recall his past life. At this time, Jiang Peng and he, along with his father, had been dismissed from their posts. It was right after their mother's funeral.

His father had sat on the sofa like this then.

But back then, Jiang Peng's temples were gray, his face weathered, and he seemed to have aged decades overnight.

He had thought that after losing power, at least he could live a normal life, but at that moment he realized...

It was all just wishful thinking.

And then there was the image of Jiang Peng, heartbroken and despondent after his mother's death, ultimately dying in despair.

Jiang Tian dared not forget it. If the memory weren't so vivid, he would have thought it was all just a nightmare.

But now...

It's changed.

Everything has changed.

Jiang Tian looked at his father, so calm and composed, dressed in a Zhongshan suit. It was past 5 a.m., yet his father was already there waiting for him.

Jiang Tian couldn't help but feel a warmth in his heart.

"Dad, getting promoted really makes a difference, even your demeanor is different," Jiang Tian joked.

It has to be said, power truly can change a man; power and status can completely transform a person.

Just like...

Jiang Peng.

How could Jiang Tian have imagined that his ordinary father would possess such an aura?

After all, others might not know, but Minister Tan and other high-ranking officials, as well as Jiang Tian himself, could see that Jiang Peng was a master of the art of maneuvering.

In short, a seasoned swindler.

Otherwise, he wouldn't have been in the position of bureau chief, where others, whether they were instrumental in reforms and then ousted by criminal forces,

or perhaps they were caught colluding with criminals and were subsequently placed under investigation by their superiors in a fit of anger.

None of them fared well.

Jiang Peng, in this position, had perfected the art of mediocre success, exhibiting extreme mediocrity.

But, no one expected that Jiang Peng would ultimately be the victor.

A leader in the political and legal system.

Born of humble origins, mediocre throughout his career, promoted by luck, with no notable achievements along the way, yet ultimately becoming a leader in Yunhai's political and legal system.

Furthermore, if Yunhai becomes the fifth municipality directly under the central government, his position, while remaining unchanged, would automatically be elevated by a rank.

Not only that, but he would also be promoted again upon retirement.

What rank would he be then?

Retire with flying colors?

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue, but he understood that Jiang Peng was unsuitable for the position of bureau chief in Yunhai. However, removing him was impossible. Even disregarding Jiang Peng's contributions, removing him was out of the question—it was absurd.

Besides, the progress in Yunhai was terrifyingly advanced.

So the only option is a promotion.

Moreover, with Jiang Tian around, it can't be done half-heartedly; it has to be a department with real power.

Jiang Peng's promotion can be said to be a perfect storm of favorable circumstances.

Even if Jiang Tian were completely useless, given Jiang Peng's current position, he would still be entitled to act with impunity throughout Yunhai.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian smiled. This scene still felt somewhat dreamlike, a little unreal, mainly because the tragic end of his past life was still too vivid in his memory.

“You're still smiling, kid? One hundred and forty tons! That's one hundred and forty tons of heroin! What does that mean? The sky is falling, you know?”

Seeing Jiang Tian's faint smile, Jiang Peng stood up in disbelief and shouted excitedly at Jiang Tian.

That's right.

He admitted that his composure just now was all a facade.

“One hundred and forty tons, that's a lot. I didn't expect it either. But in just over ten years, a dozen tons a year, that's only one or two tons a month. Looking at it that way, it's quite reasonable. In fact, compared to the scale of the drug village, this amount is actually a bit small.” Jiang Tian nodded.

“Pfft!!”

“Small?”

Jiang Peng almost spat out his drink. Was this even human language?

"Aren't you surprised? Do you know what Yunhai is like? No, the entire Yunnan province is in turmoil! Just now, Huai Province called me directly. Not only Huai Province, but even Secretary Zhou Zhao and others called to ask what happened. How could 140 tons suddenly explode? What has Yunhai become? A drug den?"

Jiang Peng was furious when he saw Jiang Tian's expression.

This brat!

Doesn't he know that while he's reaping all the credit, his father is suffering?

Although he's resigned, Jiang Peng served as the director of the Yunhai Bureau for several years. How did you manage Yunhai?

Why is Yunhai like this? How could you just stand by and watch it become a drug den? What kind of director are you?

Of course, out of respect for their son Jiang Tian, ​​the leaders didn't say these things too explicitly, but the implication was the same.

Some even suspected him of colluding with criminal groups.

This completely baffled Jiang Peng.

Thank God.

Even as the bureau chief, what could Jiang Peng do? Often, by the time they launched an operation, the perpetrators had already fled.

Not only that, their personnel were frequently subjected to bribery and even death threats.

Even more terrifying, their families were threatened.

They were constantly in danger.

For a period, they would solve some minor case, and if that were all, the criminal groups would probably turn a blind eye, sacrificing a few of their henchmen as a small reward.

Of course, if it ended there, it wouldn't be a problem.

However, if you wanted to delve deeper, the criminal groups would start using ruthless methods, threatening and attacking them and their families at all costs.

This is why there had been no progress for so many years; without a definite chance of success, they dared not put themselves, or even their families, in danger.

Over time, this developed into a tacit understanding between the two sides.

It wasn't that they didn't want to act, but rather that their strength was insufficient, and moreover, their opponent was too powerful.

They couldn't withstand a counterattack.

Unless, of course, a coordinated effort from above.

At this time, Gao Xiaolong also came to Yunhai for this reason.

A series of events followed. Yunhai's problems didn't arise overnight; they stemmed from its geographical location and the fact that in prioritizing economic development over public security, regulatory oversight was neglected. By the time the problem was fully realized, it was shocking to discover that the criminal groups had become like monstrous beasts lurking in the abyss.

They had reached the stage of infiltration.

To target them now, one had to dig three feet into the ground, and even then, the slightest digging would result in them leaping up and biting.

Jiang Peng was truly wronged. After all, he had inherited this mess when he arrived. What could he do? He could only rely on his cunning and resourcefulness.

Therefore, Jiang Tian smiled without saying a word.



Chapter 534 The unimaginable horror and shock completely blew things up.


"What are you laughing at?" Jiang Peng felt a sense of foreboding.

"The waters of Yunhai are much deeper than I imagined. I've always felt that Yunhai harbors a terrifying force, but I haven't found any clues yet. However, I can sense that when it's exposed, it will shock the entire nation." Jiang Tian shook his head.

Thinking about today's conclusion, Jiang Tian's heart sank slightly. He was right; ninety tons were sold overseas, and it was impossible for such a small amount to be sold domestically.

In other words, there exists a drug-related force even more terrifying than the drug village.

Perhaps it's currently hiding in the shadows, observing everything.

Maybe it will suddenly jump out and bite you at any moment.

"Is it even more terrifying than that Fishbuy.com?" Jiang Peng's heart skipped a beat.

"Its global influence might not be as great, but domestically, the impact of Giant Drugstore is definitely not small, and its nature might be much more heinous than Fishbuy.com," Jiang Tian explained.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Peng stood up, his heart pounding.

After being promoted to the political and legal system, Jiang Peng's understanding of the Municipal Public Security Bureau's situation had dwindled, and he was no longer able to obtain firsthand intelligence.

Unexpectedly, his promotion had only lasted a few days.

"Xiao Jiang, the lives and property of millions of people in Yunhai rest on your shoulders," Jiang Peng said, somewhat apprehensively.

"Let's take it one step at a time. This is just speculation between Minister Tan and me. There's no news yet, but at this stage, some deep-sea monsters can't hide anymore, even if they want to. Soon, they'll reveal themselves," Jiang Tian sighed softly.

It can only be said that ensuring Yunhai's complete safety is a long and arduous task.

Sitting on the sofa, looking at Jiang Tian's confident and composed demeanor, even Jiang Peng had to admit that he always felt a strange sense of security in front of his son, as if Jiang Tian could handle any situation, no matter how big.

"However, you should still be careful lately," Jiang Tian casually reminded them.

Meanwhile,

Inside the room, their conversation, unsurprisingly, reached the ears of the two eavesdropping girls.

Especially when they heard that the lives and property of millions of people were on their shoulders, Jiang Xiaoyu clenched her fists, her head practically steaming with pride.

"My brother, my brother, this is my brother!"

Jiang Xiaoyu's heart pounded wildly. This was her most prized older brother!

"I know, I know," Jiang Ning said, looking at the gleeful Jiang Xiaoyu with a bead of black lines on his forehead.

Unlike the carefree Jiang Xiaoyu who had grown up in such circumstances, the harsh environment had indeed helped her mature more quickly.

Compared to the exhilarating sense of responsibility, the crisis that truly lurks behind it is the most terrifying.

A giant crocodile surfacing from the deep sea?

The sacrifice of her best friend Guo Nana's father.

And the kidnapping she herself had experienced.

If she, as a cousin, had been kidnapped, then what about her cousin's family?

How many dangers and life-or-death crises had they faced?

Thinking of this, Jiang Ning took several deep breaths.

"What are you two standing there for? Like doorposts? Get up and go wash up."

At this moment, Jiang Peng called out to Jiang Ning and Jiang Xiaoyu at the door.

As Jiang Peng spoke, his eyes were bloodshot. Jiang Ning, at least out of the corner of her eye, saw the deep worry on her second uncle's face and the bloodshot eyes from not sleeping all night.

Yes, he hadn't slept all night.

When Jiang Ning got up that night, she saw her second uncle sitting on the sofa, constantly smoking.

Perhaps it was due to anxiety, or perhaps the immense pressure of the police system, that few people didn't smoke, especially in this era. Perhaps this situation wouldn't improve for another twenty years or so.

Around 5 a.m., Second Uncle was still sitting on the sofa in the same clothes. What had happened was obvious: Second Uncle was waiting for Jiang Tian.

Although Second Uncle didn't say anything, and showed no outward signs after returning home—seemingly watching TV normally, without revealing his emotions—

this only further shook Jiang Ning.

Clearly, upon reflection, the reason Second Uncle hadn't slept all night, and why he had waited all night for his cousin, was obviously because Jiang Tian's mission was too dangerous.

For some reason, a sentence popped into Jiang Ning's mind, seemingly fitting her cousin perfectly:

Who says only those standing in the light are heroes?

But Jiang Ning knew there was nothing she could do. All she could do was study, study hard.

Besides that, she couldn't help in any other way.

As time passed,

while Jiang Tian returned home and enjoyed the warmth of family,

the entire municipal bureau was suffering terribly.

Especially the current bureau chief, Han Xian.

This had already angered Minister Tan, and then these other incidents occurred. In his mind, Han Xian was now synonymous with a good-for-nothing.

Because of Jiang Tian's presence, he couldn't freely insult Jiang Peng, but couldn't Han Xian at least utter a few words?

Besides, Jiang Peng was mediocre, yet he was forced into this position to take the blame; he was passively chosen. But how dare someone as mediocre as Han Xian come here to reap the rewards?

Who gave you the audacity?

At six o'clock in the morning, the sky was already bright.

Because the Municipal Public Security Bureau was located in the city center, next to the courthouse, it was currently empty except for a few people holding documents, waiting at the entrance for the courthouse staff to start work.

One of them walked up to the entrance and asked the security guard, "Brother, when does your courthouse open?"

"Submit documents at nine, officially start at nine-thirty," the security guard explained.

"Then why does the one next door open at six?" the young man at the courthouse entrance pointed to the Municipal Public Security Bureau next door.

"I don't know, maybe it's an important case. Alright, come back at nine." The security guard immediately started to shoo him away.

But the young man didn't move.

Because, at that moment, rows of black vehicles with armed police markings and special agents were stationed on the street in front of the municipal police station, almost one every ten steps. Soon, a special agent stood in front of the young man.

Vehicles of all kinds entered the municipal police station.

To be honest,

Jiang Tian had far underestimated the concept and influence of 140 tons.

The influence of 140 tons is terrifyingly unimaginable.

That's to say.

If Shimiao Village had discovered this terrifying quantity beforehand, the higher-ups would have mobilized land, sea, and air forces simultaneously.

One hundred and forty tons, one hundred and forty thousand kilograms.

While this represents sales over the past ten years, it doesn't represent the total.

What a terrifying quantity!

In other words, one hundred and forty tons is just the current figure, the confirmed figure, and there's still an uncertain, even more terrifying figure.

They wouldn't believe there were only these figures on the surface without other partners.

Meanwhile, the entire municipal bureau was in a frenzy of activity because far too many people had arrived.

That's why, although FishBuy.com had garnered some attention from the top, what they knew wasn't the whole story, and many people lacked understanding of the internet and the dark web, and didn't grasp its immense influence.

Many things paled in comparison to the shock caused by one hundred and forty tons.

As a result, the entire Yunnan province was in an uproar after receiving the news.

Every single one of them caused a terrifying sensation, and this sensation continues to spread.

The news eventually reached the ears of someone in Beijing. The old man took out his phone, thought for a moment, and dialed Li Xianying: "Son, do you know about Yunhai...?"



Chapter 535 The Storm Gradually Sweeps In, A Major Meeting Begins


It was a complete explosion.

The city bureau's vehicles were endless.

From the moment Jiang Tian left, provincial and municipal leaders, upon receiving the news, rushed to the city bureau to understand the situation.

At the same time, they also had to face the dreaded questioning of Minister Tan.

What?

Why do you ask why they have to face Minister Tan's questioning?

What a joke.

After this incident was exposed, no one could escape it. Even Minister Tan was furious.

This was just the initial figure; the preliminary investigation already revealed 140 tons. Further investigation would reveal that this was definitely not the final amount; the final figure could even reach 200 tons.

Of course, if it really stopped there, it meant that this case, regarding the heroin trade in Yunhai and the rest of the country, had not been properly resolved.

All heroin in the world comes from Yunnan Province, and heroin in Yunnan Province comes from Yunhai.

It can be said that Yunhai's rise has always been accompanied by doubt and various forms of questioning.

Minister Tan, among others, received numerous complaints from provinces and cities across the mainland regarding the trafficking of heroin. Investigations at each level revealed that a large portion of these cases ended up in Yunhai. Crucially, once in Yunhai, they vanished without a trace.

This happened once or twice, but the repeated occurrences drove anti-drug officials from many provinces and cities in the mainland to the brink of collapse. Some even stormed into Yunhai, demanding to know exactly what was going on.

However, Yunhai itself felt aggrieved.

As the saying goes, the waters in Yunhai were too murky.

Even Yunhai's own branches couldn't understand the situation, let alone those in other regions.

During this time, some branches from other provinces and cities, including leaders from the provincial headquarters, proposed joint enforcement operations with Yunhai to crack down on cases.

But often, as soon as an operation was launched, the heroin traffickers seemed to have received advance notice and had already fled, some even turning back to steal from them.

The most outrageous thing was that a large number of comrades from inland provinces and cities even suffered casualties. In short, whoever came was doomed.

Over time, Yunhai's reputation suffered seriously internally. Whenever the name Yunhai appeared, the leaders of the detachment and the headquarters would almost always slam their fists on the table in righteous indignation, followed by a torrent of angry rants.

Not only the detachment, but sometimes even the headquarters joined in the criticism.

Because of the detachment's situation, the headquarters, which oversees the entire province, was only second in rank. Although it was right under their noses, it was somewhat understandable that they couldn't pay much attention. But how did the Yunhai detachment handle it?

But today.

It's made a name for itself.

That's right.

Minister Tan waved his hand.

He immediately convened an impromptu national headquarters meeting.

The detachment was out of the question.

Because there are too many detachments across the country, but the headquarters is more than capable.

Today, Minister Tan wants to tighten the reins on the entire national workforce.

Eight o'clock.

Minister Tan held a unique video conference directly at the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau.

"Comrades, 140 tons! One hundred and forty tons of heroin seized and convicted in a single operation! That's a terrifying number! Who could have imagined it? Even the Yunhai branch, you think you're happy, that you've achieved something great? I'm happy for you, and I, as the minister, am happy for you too. But why did such a large case occur? That's the question I'm thinking about, and I want to ask the Yunhai team."

Minister Tan pounded on the table. Inside the conference room, a huge screen displayed numerous conference rooms from provincial and municipal departments across the country, some with banners erected behind them proclaiming "National Anti-Drug Conference."

In short, the scene was enormous.

If Jiang Tian knew, he probably wouldn't know whether this was premeditated or a spur-of-the-moment decision, or when such efficiency and speed could be achieved.

But regardless of whether anyone was absent, Minister Tan was furious.

Especially since the Yunhai team included provincial and municipal leaders, including Zhou Zhao and Huai Sheng, all sitting below listening.

Especially Huai Sheng, whose brow was deeply furrowed.

His face was almost entirely lined with wrinkles, his expression filled with difficulty.

As a leader who had only recently arrived in Yun Province, Huai Sheng felt melancholy.

Before arriving, Huai Sheng thought he had been promoted; in fact, his superiors had thoughtfully spared him a direct supervisor, allowing him to appear capable.

But only after actually taking action did he realize how incredibly difficult it was.

Every step was fraught with difficulty.

Economic reforms were manageable, but issues involving public security and the deluge of whistleblower reports were fraught with difficulties, even lacking the capacity for investigation.

Of course, Huai Sheng initially thought this was simply due to his newcomer status, hence the slow progress, and that resolving problems step by step was acceptable.

His superiors were still thinking of him; without a direct superior, he seemed to have a lot of power, but in reality, it was far too difficult.

And now…

Huai Sheng realized he was wrong, terribly wrong.

The situation in Yunhai was beyond his imagination.

It was terrifying.

Each time a major case surfaced, Huai Sheng couldn't help but feel relieved, thinking that the criminal elements in Yunhai had finally been eradicated and society was finally stable. But then, a few days later, another case would emerge, and each time Huai Sheng thought it was the last.

Until now.

When the 140 tons of drugs appeared before him, Huai Sheng finally understood.

Yunhai.

It was riddled with problems, inside and out.

It was a terrifying abyss.

He also understood why his predecessor, along with two other leaders of Yunhai Province, had either been relegated to retirement positions (a demotion in name only) or placed under investigation.

It was all about emotion.

The situation in Yunhai was already this terrifying. And this was the provincial capital! So, what was happening outside Yunhai, and what was the problem throughout the entire province?

Looking at the red-highlighted number 140 tons at the top, Huai Sheng felt dizzy.

140 tons in one go.

The impact was immense.

"Of course, the deserved rewards will still be given. We don't offset merits and demerits here. Your merits are yours, and your mistakes must be borne by yourselves, along with their consequences. I've given you the merits, and we won't pursue the past anymore. But if Yunhai repeats the past mistakes, then all the blame will be settled."

Minister Tan slammed his hand on the table, reprimanding everyone from Yunhai.

This matter involved not only the anti-drug squad, but also aspects like criminal investigation.

Furthermore,

a summary of Yunhai's recent problems was needed.

At this moment, all the conference rooms on the screen were somewhat quiet. The rustling sound of documents being turned stopped as Minister Tan's voice rang out.



Chapter 536 Shocks All the National Teams, Jiang Tian Makes a Name for Himself


This situation arose after the discovery of the 140-ton substance.

Shen Han was currently seated on the platform. As the head of the provincial department, he was expected to speak in this situation.

As for Han Xian...

Well, normally, as the bureau chief, he should also be on the platform, as the main force in solving the case and the first-hand leader, he would need to give a report.

But unfortunately, Han Xian had only just received the news and didn't even know much about the case.

In fact, Han Xian only had a vague understanding of the Shimiao Village case; he had heard some things but knew very little. Being a spokesperson was definitely out of the question.

Finally, the representative from the municipal bureau was Song Liguo.

In other words, Song Liguo, as the branch captain, was absolutely unqualified, but now, it has to be said, "when one person gains power, even their relatives and friends benefit," and Song Liguo was a prime example.

This year, Song Liguo and Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng, are the same age. Both of them were even demobilized soldiers who started from scratch in Yunhai.

But as the saying goes, different people have different fates. Song Liguo's abilities were excellent, even far surpassing Jiang Peng's.

However, Song Liguo stagnated for years, while Jiang Peng's career skyrocketed, rising almost instantly upon reaching his promotion deadline.

Sometimes it was due to problems with competitors; other times, it was because someone offered him credit, which Jiang Peng had no choice but to accept.

In short, perhaps because of Jiang Peng's mediocrity, he gained the trust and confidence of some problematic leaders, who consistently promoted him. Meanwhile, Song Liguo, due to his superior abilities, was naturally suppressed by these problematic leaders.

To this day,

Song Liguo has already resigned himself to his fate. At forty-seven, almost fifty, Song Liguo is now just an ordinary branch captain.

Even though this branch captain rank is quite high—to be precise, according to official ranks, he's already a deputy division-level cadre, considered the ceiling for ordinary people—it's still not on the same level as the incredibly lucky Jiang Peng.

Jiang Peng is now even at the level of a full-fledged department director.

Upon reaching the position of bureau chief, Jiang Peng was already doomed. His mediocre abilities and average, unambitious mindset seemed to have sealed his fate.

But who could have imagined that good fortune would smile upon him, and Jiang Peng would take off?

His son had carried him along.

Sometimes, even Song Liguo couldn't help but feel envious and resentful of Jiang Peng. He did everything, remaining stagnant and achieving nothing, while Jiang Peng's rise was meteoric.

And it continues to this day.

Actually, the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau is considered quite well-equipped.

For example, the various captains under the branch are all at the level of section chief. Each brigade has at least fifty people, and sometimes up to a hundred.

But even though it seems like a lot, it's just one brigade captain.

In local government, this level of official rank would even qualify him as the head of a township.

In an ordinary branch bureau, a section chief rank would be enough to qualify him as a deputy bureau chief.

This explains the significance of Jiang Tian's achievement of becoming a full-fledged section chief at the age of twenty. Who has ever seen a twenty-two-year-old township leader who hasn't even finished his normal internship?

As for the branch captains above the battalion commander, they are basically all at the deputy division level.

Of course, occasionally a division chief will appear, since positions in provincial capitals are generally higher than in prefecture-level cities.

The deputy bureau chief is at the division chief level, and normally the bureau chief is also at the division chief level.

The division chief and deputy bureau chief are often at the division chief level; in reality, it's just a matter of their police ranks corresponding to their civil service levels. Essentially, the bureau chief is just one more position than the deputy bureau chief, and their police ranks are roughly within the range of a deputy provincial department.

There are similar cases, such as Jiang Peng, who rose rapidly through the ranks. To make it easier for Jiang Peng to take the blame, a certain faction even gave him the position of executive deputy in Yunhai, which is equivalent to a provincial department level.

And Han Xian, after arriving in Yunhai, although he took over as bureau chief and didn't become executive deputy, he was still effectively at the deputy provincial department level.

In terms of administrative rank, before this, despite only being in the Yunhai Municipal Bureau, Jiang Peng's administrative rank was actually the same as Shen Han's.

That's why Shen Han always strives for advancement, aiming to become a standing director. In most provinces and cities, the vice-governor concurrently serves as the department head, but Shen Han is currently only a department head. Whether it's controlling those below him or handling various matters, he faces immense pressure.

For example, Jiang Peng was already a department head back then. If Shen Han, at that level, encountered a powerful municipal bureau chief who wouldn't listen to him, Shen Han would be powerless.

They could simply disobey Shen Peng's orders.

On the podium, Song Liguo glanced furtively at the leaders beside him, then nervously lowered his eyes, feeling immense pressure sitting with these bigwigs. Even as a lowly branch captain, he felt immense pressure.

However, when Minister Tan mentioned 140 tons, Song Liguo unconsciously straightened his back.

It's the same old story.

Before this, Yun Hai had already endured enough criticism and pointing fingers from his colleagues across the country.

They were practically ready to call them useless.

But now...

Previously, some rumors might have circulated, but whether they were true or not, they were all hearsay. Even when some did surface, they couldn't believe that Yunhai, always seen as a troublemaker, could actually pull off such a large and terrifying case.

But now, when 140 tons were unleashed, almost everyone in the anti-drug unit looked up at the screen. Song Liguo could even see the astonishment and disbelief in the eyes of all the officers.

They seemed unable to believe how Yunhai had solved this.

How?

"Going forward, all comrades in the national anti-drug squads must emulate Yunhai, and even more so, the Yunhai Anti-Drug Squad. However, this case also fully illustrates the severity of the problem. In the coming days, given the nationwide proliferation of heroin, it is essential to conduct even more rigorous investigations to address the increasingly serious heroin issue."

"My Party has only one attitude towards heroin: zero tolerance. Once discovered, we will investigate thoroughly, dig to the bottom, and never let any heroin offender escape." Minister Tan paused to give everyone time to process this before slamming his fist on the table and declaring.

"Yes, sir!"

Almost every officer and subordinate on the screen stood up in unison to answer.

But this wasn't the end. Minister Tan looked at Song Liguo: "Next, Song Liguo, the captain of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment, will give a general explanation of this 140-ton case, and discuss the difficulties and dangers faced by Comrade Yunhai in this case, which is worthy of emulation by comrades nationwide." Minister Tan continued, changing the subject.

Since he was already preparing to bring Jiang Tian to the forefront,

then...

Minister Tan intended to use this as a starting point.

He wanted Jiang Tian not only to become a national star, but first and foremost, to become a role model for all teams across the country.

Song Liguo, seemingly prepared beforehand, stood up and began: "That's right, this case was handled by Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​the captain of the First Detachment, who is only twenty-two years old and hasn't even graduated yet..."

Buzz! !!

All the teams across the country looked up in bewilderment. Twenty-two years old was understandable, not having graduated was understandable, the captain was understandable, leading a team handling 140 tons of cases involving thousands of people was even more understandable. But why put it all together? Could it be contained in a single sentence? Was it what they understood?

Meanwhile,

Jiang Tian, ​​who had only slept for less than two hours, yawned and looked at Fang Jie sitting opposite him on the sofa.

He couldn't help but sigh.

"I never expected that bitch to be so heartless. She dumped me and got into that rich kid's car. Damn, let's see if that rich kid gets someone to slap me in the face."



Chapter 537 A terrifying and unheard-of story.


"Wasn't Lili pregnant?"

Jiang Tian clearly remembered that at the previous gathering, Fang Jie had excitedly boasted to him that his girlfriend, Lili, was pregnant, and several months along.

How could she suddenly dump Fang Jie and run off with a rich second-generation heir?

Jiang Tian felt completely bewildered.

Even with Jiang Tian's logical thinking, he was momentarily stunned. What was going on?

Sure enough, upon hearing Jiang Tian's words, Fang Jie's face instantly darkened, and the flames of anger in his eyes almost burst forth.

"She lost it," Fang Jie said through gritted teeth.

"Lost it? You mean she lost the baby and then ran off with a rich second-generation heir?" Jiang Tian asked cautiously.

He felt there was more to this story.

In fact, given Jiang Tian's current status and position, it could be said that Jiang Tian was about to become a model, or rather, a model within the anti-drug industry, to be established.

Or rather, after so much time and so many achievements, Jiang Tian's status, position, and even his connections and background have all changed.

Compared to when he first transmigrated, Jiang Tian is completely different.

In this Cloud Sea, Jiang Tian can confidently say he has the ability to solve any problem.

However, even if he could solve it, Jiang Tian would assess the situation.

Moreover, as a qualified public servant, Jiang Tian would never do anything illegal or criminal, even with his good relationship with Fang Jie.

In fact, in his previous life, when Jiang Tian faced betrayal and isolation, a single phone call, and Fang Jie transferred all his money to Jiang Tian without hesitation.

He gave Jiang Tian all his savings.

No matter what, Jiang Tian will remember this kindness in both his lives.

“Wrong,” Fang Jie shook his head.

“There’s a twist?” Jiang Tian asked in astonishment.

“Okay, okay.”

Jiang Tian's perception of Fang Jie had even changed. After all, Jiang Tian had handled countless cases, all sorts of bizarre and convoluted ones.

Especially since Jiang Tian was now a legendary figure in criminal investigation, possessing impeccable investigative logic, it was impossible to guess just how convoluted Fang Jie's past was.

The key point was that in his previous life, Jiang Tian knew that Fang Jie had suffered a rather miserable fate because of Lili, being utterly ruined by her.

Even the child discovered wasn't his.

But after being reborn, starting with the gathering, the butterfly effect kicked in, and Fang Jie was experiencing his tragic past life prematurely.

"After the last gathering, didn't you say something was off? How did Lili get pregnant so quickly, and so suddenly? I had my suspicions. Lili probably sensed my feelings and suddenly broke up with me. I'm just not happy about it," Fang Jie said with a sigh.

"Lili broke up with me?" Jiang Tian pondered, lost in thought.

In his past life, Lili, that woman, practically emptied Fang Jie's pockets.

What? Such a dramatic story.

"You still don't agree?" Jiang Tian continued to ask.

Fang Jie nodded and sighed, "Of course I disagreed. After I got pregnant, that woman spent all my money, and I don't even know if the child is mine. Of course I'm not willing to accept it. I couldn't lose my wife and then lose my child too, so I tried to persuade her to stay, and then we argued. That's how I lost the baby."

"I see!" Jiang Tian exhaled a cloud of smoke.

He exhaled a puff of smoke, looking at Fang Jie with a hint of disappointment. There are plenty of fish in the sea.

Lili wasn't a good match.

It's just that Fang Jie himself can't get over it.

"What about the rich kid?" Jiang Tian continued to ask.

"It seems the child was the rich kid's. He abandoned the pregnant Lili, but after finding out she lost the baby, I don't know why, he suddenly changed his mind and came back, even beating me up badly."

This...

What a character!

Jiang Tian was stunned.

Now the question is...

So, if Lili is the child of a rich second-generation, then who cheated on whom between Fang Jie and the rich second-generation?

From an ordinary person's perspective, Lili is definitely a good girl; she's quite beautiful.

While her figure isn't particularly outstanding, her height and good fashion sense make her stand out from the average young woman.

No wonder Fang Jie was so infatuated with her.

"So, Lili was the rich kid's girlfriend before. After she got pregnant, the rich kid didn't want her anymore; he wanted to go back to inherit his family fortune and dumped her. Lili then planned to find you to take over and get some money from you. She thought she could use the money to pay child support and social security after the baby was born, and also blackmail the rich kid. But unexpectedly, after the miscarriage, Lili somehow got back together with the rich kid. He felt sorry for her and suddenly wanted to have her back, but you miscarried, so he beat you up?"

Jiang Tian was stunned. He believed that no one in the world understood human nature better than him.

However, faced with such a convoluted story, Jiang Tian couldn't help but exclaim inwardly, "Wow!"

After all, to have such a convoluted story, two people weren't enough; there was another, even more important person involved: the rich kid.

He abandoned his pregnant girlfriend.

Then his girlfriend got pregnant by someone else, regretted it, and wanted the baby back.

"So, why did you call me here?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Ugh, I'm so angry, but then I realized, it's not about getting revenge for me. My mom introduced me to a potential partner on a blind date, and I wanted you to check her out. I know you work in criminal investigation; you're absolutely knowledgeable in this area. With your help, you can definitely see through the blind date's true colors and prevent a repeat of what happened to Lili." Fang Jie was self-aware.

If Fang Jie came here primarily to seek Jiang Tian's help for revenge, Jiang Tian would certainly do it; at Jiang Tian's level, it would be a trivial matter with a simple order.

After all, since taking over the first team, the personnel had been replenished and expanded to suit the current situation in Yunhai. Jiang Tian now had over a hundred men under his command.

Moreover, given Jiang Tian's status, background, and connections, teaching a rich kid a lesson would be a piece of cake.

However, once this was done, it would mean the end of Jiang Tian's friendship with Fang Jie.

"When?" Jiang Tian didn't answer, secretly relieved.

He was genuinely worried someone would come asking him for favors or help. If Fang Jie came, Jiang Tian wouldn't be able to refuse.

This was even better.

However, the prerequisite was that Jiang Tian was usually very busy and didn't really have much time to play games with Fang Jie.



Chapter 538: Completely Exposed, No One Can Believe It—He Is Jiang Tian


"Tonight," Fang Jie said excitedly, then eagerly approached Jiang Tian, ​​fawningly serving him tea and water and massaging his shoulders. Jiang Tian, ​​however, took his mentor's pleasure for granted.

"Just give me a call if you have time," Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

Although he'd been busy lately, taking a little time in the evening to help was no problem.

However...

Jiang Tian's eyes narrowed. Fang Jie was, after all, his brother, even though Fang Jie knew his place.

Besides, from Jiang Tian's perspective, Fang Jie actually benefited from this situation.

It was like spending some money to get rid of the rich kid's girlfriend's child.

Only one person treated Fang Jie like a sucker, and the other even beat him up.

"Don't you want revenge?" Jiang Tian asked curiously.

Fang Jie quickly explained, "I didn't really suffer any losses. In fact, I feel like I gained something. Taking the beating is fine, but that rich kid seems quite powerful. I was afraid a conflict would cause you trouble."

"I see!" Jiang Tian nodded slightly in understanding.

In both his past and present lives, Jiang Tian had very few friends, and Fang Jie was clearly not among them. He initially wanted to have his subordinates handle it.

However, after considering it for a moment, remembering the workload at the Municipal Bureau and the need to maintain certain relationships, he called Secretary Mu's secretary, Cheng Mi.

That's right.

Meanwhile.

At the Municipal Bureau, as Secretary Mu's secretary, Cheng Mi naturally accompanied her superior, Secretary Mu, to the Municipal Bureau.

No wonder, Cheng Mi was watching with bated breath.

Sitting in the spectator stands, watching the events unfold on the platform, her heart was pounding wildly.

As the person in charge of law enforcement, Secretary Mu couldn't possibly be absent. Even Jiang Peng was actually there to attend the meeting.

After all, law enforcement primarily manages the public security, procuratorate, and courts.

Secretary Cheng received notification early in the morning, around five or six o'clock, that the Municipal Bureau was holding a major security meeting—an impromptu national conference.

Unprecedented, Secretary Cheng participated.

At first, everything was fine.

However, upon learning of 140 tons, Secretary Cheng was instantly stunned, his heart skipping a beat.

A moment later.

Secretary Cheng was still trembling with fear, but then Song Liguo stood up.

As expected, Song Liguo first affirmed that under the leadership of Minister Tan and Chief Gao, the Municipal Bureau had reached unprecedented heights.

Drug enforcement had also reached unprecedented levels.

Then Song Liguo began to address the main issue.

In front of everyone, at the national video conference of the General Team, Song Liguo, holding a hastily prepared script, delivered an important report.

"In just a few short months, Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​a police academy graduate on internship, at the age of twenty-two, ranked first nationwide in both overall drug seizure volume and actual seizure amount. The total amount of drugs seized, totaling 170 tons, also ranked first nationwide."

Boom!!!

This news was truly explosive.

Everyone was stunned.

If, upon first hearing about 140 tons, the national police departments were merely somewhat bewildered, not understanding why the situation had changed...

The national headquarters was well aware of Yunhai's past state.

Even with the recent problems, these headquarters hadn't truly changed Yunhai, nor did they believe so.

After all, Yunhai's poor performance wasn't a recent phenomenon; over the years, the national headquarters had become accustomed to cleaning up its messes.

What else could they do?

If the frontline was lagging, the second-line teams would naturally increase their efforts!

The key was that even with increased effort, the investigations would inevitably reach Yunhai, where nothing would come of it.

Give Yunhai leads, but Yunhai couldn't solve them.

Over time, whenever Yunhai was involved, and if any leads were found, these headquarters would selectively ignore them, even neglecting to issue notifications.

Because notifications were useless; they couldn't solve the problems themselves, and they wouldn't find anything. Even when they went to various regions to investigate across areas, they were completely lost when they reached Yunhai.

They hadn't uncovered anything, but several of their men would likely have been killed.

The discovery of 140 tons of drugs in the Yunhai village had initially surprised everyone present.

But the next piece of news completely stunned the entire national police force.

Good heavens, where were they all being sent?

"A trainee officer seized nearly 200 tons?"

"Good heavens, even with my 'Outstanding Officer' title, I've only been a police officer for twenty years, seizing a total of about 500 kilograms. Nearly 200 tons—what does that even mean?"

"That's what a trainee officer did in just a few months?"

It was terrifying.

It was definitely terrifying, because many things couldn't be calculated.

Minister Tan was satisfied with everyone's shock.

In reality, this total amount involved requires solid, verifiable evidence, and evidence sufficient for conviction.

Otherwise, considering Jiang Tian's handling of the Tucha case, the amount involved in that case would be impossible to calculate.

Below, Secretary Cheng's heart pounded wildly, sweat pouring down his face—not from fear, but from excitement.

You see, Jiang Tian had previously saved Secretary Cheng's life; in fact, Jiang Tian had rescued Secretary Mu and his entire family.

Secretary Cheng had been in the car that time. The situation was urgent; Jiang Tian's primary targets were Mu Feng and Secretary Mu. As for Secretary Cheng, after pulling him out of the car, Jiang Tian left him to fend for himself.

Later, the rescue team arrived and, by chance, pulled him out of the car.

Even Jiang Tian was surprised by the man's incredible luck, considering it was the Yanjiang River.

Although Jiang Tian had only offered slight assistance,

Jiang Tian had undeniably gotten him out of the car. Whether it was luck or not, trapped inside, Secretary Cheng would certainly have died.

In fact, social relationships often begin with owing favors; the more favors owed, the closer the relationship becomes.

Giving gifts to a superior is less effective than repaying a debt of gratitude; it's the pretext of gratitude that fosters a deeper relationship.

For example, the seemingly insignificant act of saving Jiang Tian's life, in the hands of someone skilled at manipulation, can be seen as a friendship akin to Jiang Tian's actual act of saving Jiang Tian.

This equates to a deep and lasting relationship.

Seeing Jiang Tian's terrifying achievements, Secretary Cheng was terrified.

Then came extreme excitement.

But this wasn't the end.

"In the past few months, Comrade Jiang Tian has successively won seven First-Class Merits, the title of Anti-Drug Vanguard, and the title of First-Class Hero, among other great honors. Furthermore, he broke historical records by receiving a Special-Class Merit Medal. He has withstood the test of the organization and the people, and has been promoted multiple times beyond the usual ranks. He is a great guardian of the people and an outstanding soldier of our party."

BOOM!!!

Song Liguo's report caused an uproar.



Chapter 539 He threw a tantrum when refused help; his terrifying influence.


Secretary Cheng's heart skipped a beat, her skin crawling with unease at Song Liguo's words.

Why?

Secretary Cheng, practically inseparable from Secretary Mu, couldn't possibly be unaware of Jiang Tian's affairs. Secretary Mu had been involved in many matters, except for family matters, which Secretary Cheng deliberately avoided knowing.

However, regarding Jiang Tian's affairs, Secretary Cheng knew almost everything Secretary Mu knew, given her position as Secretary Mu's confidante.

This was precisely why Secretary Mu was able to obtain firsthand information about Jiang Tian's past meritorious service, whether at the Municipal Public Security Bureau or the Provincial Department.

But the key point was that it was normal for Secretary Cheng and Secretary Mu to know.

After all, Jiang Tian was from Yunhai, and a police officer in the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau.

The problem was that Jiang Tian had always been involved in drug enforcement, and before this, it had been kept secret.

Of course, the secrecy surrounding this was extremely poor.

After all, at Jiang Tian's level, keeping it a secret is terrifyingly difficult, especially given the current situation in Yunhai; maintaining secrecy is practically impossible.

However, secrecy is an attitude. Talking about it openly and actually keeping it secret are two completely different things. Everyone knows, but the attitude of secrecy must still be made public. This is an attitude of protecting Jiang Tian and his relationships.

Moreover, it's a bottom-line attitude that must be upheld. Even if everyone knows the unspoken rules, the fewer people who know, the more secure it is for Jiang Tian and his family. Otherwise, no one can be sure that a few reckless fools might suddenly decide to attack Jiang Tian after finding out who killed them.

And now it's been brought up, directly discussed openly.

Secretary Cheng was panting heavily, her face almost red. Bringing it up directly, and in front of the whole country, publicly exposed throughout the entire system—what does this mean?

Although it's not yet open to the entire nation, it's almost there. It's been exposed to the national headquarters now, so it will definitely be exposed to the entire nation later.

What does this mean? It means the higher-ups have lifted the secrecy surrounding Jiang Tian. So, why lift the secrecy?

There's only one possibility: to promote him.

Abandoning this core, bottom-line logic of secrecy means Jiang Tian has a much greater role to play. It also means that with the support from above and Jiang Tian's own abilities, as long as he doesn't fall from grace, his future will be terrifyingly bright.

Secretary Cheng previously considered Shen Han as Jiang Tian's future leader, and that was definitely worth befriending.

Don't think this level is low. Out of over a billion people in China, how many are at the department-level? A full department-level official—that's only a little over two thousand nationwide!

Two thousand out of over a billion people—that's already a terrifying concept.

But now, Shen Han's level clearly shows that this is far from Jiang Tian's upper limit. At this moment, Jiang Tian's upper limit is already comparable to Minister Tan's.

The attitude from above was already quite clear. Thinking of this, Secretary Cheng's heart pounded wildly. Frankly, Secretary Cheng was in his thirties, and as Secretary Mu's secretary, what was his current position?

It seemed glamorous, but it was merely a privilege afforded by connections. Moreover, Secretary Mu, now the third-ranking official, would be stepping down in the next reshuffle.

This meant that even if Secretary Cheng were promoted and reassigned, he would lose his current background with Secretary Mu. At most, in a few years, when he was thirty-four or thirty-five, he could be transferred to a county-level leadership position, at best a division-level post.

Even then, he would need a promotion during the transfer.

Otherwise, he wouldn't even reach the division-level.

His starting point seemed high, but his future ceiling was also predetermined; he wouldn't rise very high, especially after losing his connections. Who knew what the future held?

There was no comparison with Jiang Tian.

At this moment, Secretary Cheng made up his mind to cultivate a good relationship with Jiang Tian at all costs.

This means that in the future, he will gain a friend of Minister Tan's caliber.

Even more deeply intertwined the relationship, the better.
