In China, there's no question about it. Whether it's soft or hard, if it involves drug crimes, there's absolutely no leniency, no room for compromise. This is the absolute bottom line, a line that cannot be crossed.

Abroad, even in some places, even in countries with extremely lenient laws and corrupt politics, only soft drugs are permitted, while hard drugs are strictly prohibited.

The main issue is hard drugs; they are absolutely prohibited in every country in the world.

This is an absolute red line, a line that kills anyone who crosses it. Of course, due to political differences, the situation varies across more than a hundred countries worldwide, including varying levels of importance placed on drugs and corruption.

But without a doubt, drugs are a crime that deserves even more attention than murder.

And Minister Tan's words, in that instant, after a moment of silence, were followed by an explosive roar.

"Unbelievable, absolutely unbelievable."

"Hiss, this case is terrifyingly outrageous."

"I initially thought it was just a simple case, not too serious a transnational one, but I never expected it to be completely different from anything I've seen before."

"Holy crap, 3.8 billion US dollars, that's outrageous!"

"Are you crazy? I've lived this long and never seen anything like it."

"Have you even started playing this game yet? You've lived for sixty years and I've never even heard of a case like this."

Shocked.

A deafening roar and tremor filled the air.

The scene was filled with the constant vibration and rumble of the equipment.

Everyone present, except for the nearly full conference room, was watching intently, their ears perked up, watching the large screen displaying the provincial headquarters from all thirteen cities.

Especially when they heard the news, some headquarters members sat bolt upright, as if from a nightmare. They had been reeling from the shock of Jiang Tian's news, and now they were even more stunned, jumping to their feet.

Everyone was utterly shocked.

Some headquarters members even thought they had misheard, or that there was a malfunction in the equipment. But why?

If the equipment was broken, how could they hear so clearly?

These headquarters members looked bewildered.

Mainly because, for them, such a case was something they had never even imagined, let alone heard of, anything of such magnitude and horror.

“Silence!”

The next moment.

Minister Tan slammed his hand on the table.

A murmur of commotion gradually subsided. Minister Tan scanned the room: "That's right, everyone heard correctly. Within this dark web, there are seventy-six major drug lords who have long collaborated with the mastermind behind it, scattered across the globe. Each one of them could be considered a nationally shocking case, a drug lord rarely seen in years. And these seventy-six drug lords are merely regular partners of the mastermind behind Yugou.com; their crimes are heinous and appalling."

If it weren't for Jiang Tian...

Minister Tan could hardly imagine how terrifying the scale of Yugou.com would be in ten or twenty years.

"Minister, I have a question."

Just then, a branch bureau chief suddenly stepped forward from below.

"Go on," Minister Tan said, his eyes narrowed, not angry.

"I'm very curious now. Why, for so many years, with such a terrifying, large-scale case, has no one in the world discovered it? Three billion US dollars in assets—that's been operating for at least several years, over a decade. Surely neither the US intelligence community nor the intelligence agencies of various countries around the world should have been completely unaware," the branch director inquired.

Indeed.

Why is it that the whole world seems blind to such a huge case?

Everyone listening in the room had their eyes gleaming.

"This is a bit outrageous."

At the back of the conference room, Li Xianying frowned slightly. Of course, he wasn't lying, but he seriously doubted the authenticity of this confession.

What was the person taking the confession's purpose?

This is utterly absurd, ridiculously outrageous.

Anyway, Li Xianying had never seen such an outrageous case in his entire life.

Good heavens.

The case involved hundreds of tons of drugs, and this major drug lord had taken down over seventy of them in one operation, especially since one of them was based in Beijing.

If this were true, Li Xianying felt he could die of embarrassment.

Such a large case required investigation from other regions, and he had been completely unaware of the clues and information found by others.

Not to mention the police system, even for Li Xianying, as someone in charge of administrative affairs, while his political future might not be completely ruined, it would definitely affect the perception of his superiors. How could he have done this? How could he have led the work? Why was he completely unaware of such a major event?

Besides, logically speaking, it was absolutely impossible for him to have escaped detection.

So, the question arises.

Since it's so unusual, the authenticity of this confession, this statement, this information, is probably false.

"What's going on with Old Tan?" Li Xianying frowned deeply.

He had a feeling something was wrong, despite trying to reassure himself.

But given Lao Tan's extensive experience, if it were false, why would he reveal it in such a public setting? Wouldn't that be a slap in his own face? If it were truly false, his credibility as a leader would be jeopardized.

His political future would be severely impacted.

Thinking this through, Li Xianying's heart, which had just settled, instantly tightened again.

But Li Xianying wasn't given time to react.

At this moment, Minister Tan took deep breaths, his face extremely grim. He said, “Good question, very crucial question. Why didn’t anyone discover such a huge case? It’s because the entire transaction process and procedure took place on the dark web. Both buyers and sellers conducted transactions directly on the dark web. In other words, buyers didn’t know sellers, and sellers didn’t know who buyers were. That’s why no one noticed. Hong Shiwu even established extremely trusting cooperative relationships through a secret dark web platform, ensuring stable internal relations. In other words, if these seventy-odd drug lords hadn’t been arrested, outsiders would never have known or discovered this in their entire lives.”

“Fake? No. This case, in the history of the anti-drug industry, can be said to be the largest case in the world. There has never been one before, and it will be difficult for anyone to surpass this scale in the future.”



Chapter 551 Leader, this... seems to be true?


The Dark Web.

This issue wasn't ignored, but the internet industry was still in its infancy, and many hadn't even fully understood it. Even the United States, with its extensive research, was helpless against the dark web, only able to deploy a large number of agents to monitor transactions within it.

They looked for leaks of criminal information, then used sting operations to lure criminals out and arrest them on the spot.

As for locating targets on the dark web, that was a pipe dream.

Especially since the routes on the dark web are too complex.

For example, with websites like Fishbuy, if they hadn't been exposed, even the United States would probably never have been able to find them in their lifetime.

Of course.

This is mainly because countries haven't yet taken cybercrime, especially dark web crime, seriously.

In future times, every country in the world will be using countless agents on the dark web to search for information and then use sting operations to make arrests step by step.

Indeed, decades later, the only way to uncover the truth is through the criminals themselves revealing their information, or through sting operations. There's still no way to crack their addresses online.

Of course, this is already enough. At least, with strict regulations, it's difficult to escape the surveillance of these sting operations. Other countries may not be able to control it, but at least your own country can control dark web crime.

But now it's very clear.

This case, to some extent, can completely overturn the world's perception and make everyone around the world aware of the harmful nature of dark web crime.

Moreover.

There's a saying.

When you find one cockroach in your house, there are actually cockroaches everywhere in the corners you can't see.

“Anyone have any questions?” Minister Tan asked, leaning on the table, his face grim.

The interrogation information was very detailed. Because the suspect was very cooperative, the interrogation process was almost seamless, a simple question-and-answer session, and all the key questions were answered.

Even the storage accounts were revealed without reservation.

Most importantly, the 3.8 billion US dollars had been verified through investigation, and Su Su, present, had also confirmed it.

This ensured the information was accurate.

Clearly, Shang Mingyan understood perfectly well; she too had initially found it hard to believe that such a horrific case existed, something she simply couldn't even imagine.

Now, it had actually happened.

However, people's joys and sorrows are not shared.

Sitting in the back row, Li Xianying felt like she was sitting on pins and needles, her scalp tingling, and cold sweat was already pouring down her face without her realizing it.

Especially when she heard Minister Tan's words, Li Xianying could no longer hold back, and she abruptly stood up.

As Li Xianying stood up, although there was much discussion and shock among those present, very few people were actually standing. Although Li Xianying was at the back, she was still easily seen from the stage.

When Minister Tan on the podium saw Li Xianying, his expression suddenly froze, thinking that Li Xianying had come to interrogate him.

However, he then calmly asked Li Xianying, "Do you have a question?"

Considering Li Xianying's position and his status in Beijing, Minister Tan's expression turned serious as he thought about the major drug lord in the capital.

Li Xianying didn't hesitate, saying, "Can all this information be confirmed? Have these testimonies been verified? I find it hard to believe that such a large-scale criminal organization exists in the world, especially since no country in the world seems to have discovered it."

Minister Tan nodded, his expression unchanged, "No one can be certain that it hasn't been discovered. Perhaps some countries have already discovered it but are investigating secretly. As for accuracy, the 3.8 billion US dollars have been confirmed. The accounts have been taken into custody, and the assets are being transferred domestically. Other information is corroborated by other witnesses. Therefore, Mr. Li, these are matters that our police system should be concerned about, not something you should be worrying about."

Minister Tan's words, towards the end, were quite serious, especially for someone of his position. While it didn't reach the point of open conflict, it left no room for compromise or retreat.

Already in a bad mood, Li Xianying's anger flared up upon hearing this.

Moreover, and most importantly, the idea that a terrifying drug lord involved in hundreds of tons of drugs in Beijing was possible was simply a pipe dream.

In such a place, even a facade of prosperity couldn't conceal the darkness of crime.

In other words, where there is light, there is also darkness.

But Li Xianying absolutely refused to believe that such a terrifying drug lord could have emerged in Beijing under his leadership.

This was tantamount to a blatant denial of his work.

His future career would undoubtedly be affected, but Li Xianying remained calm because the news was too shocking.

Because it was simply impossible.

But what he didn't know was that sometimes what seems impossible is actually the truth.

"You just said there's a major drug lord in Beijing. As a local official in Beijing, although I don't come from the public security, procuratorate, or judicial system, and I'm not involved in criminal investigations, my city has always been among the best in the country in terms of public security. To say that a terrifying drug lord has appeared in Beijing is utter nonsense. Few places in the country can compare to Beijing's security. Therefore, I think the veracity of your statement still needs to be verified," Li Xianying stood up and loudly retorted.

The room fell silent after Li Xianying's voice.

Li Xianying breathed a deep sigh of relief.

Thank goodness, he was smart.

When did Minister Tan become so unwise?

Could it be for August 1st?

Li Xianying suddenly realized.

"Probably so. To support that Jiang Tian, ​​they've gone to any lengths, pouring countless resources into him, even daring to interfere with the August 1st Army. As for this transnational criminal organization, they probably have a part in it, but that's definitely an exaggeration. For the sake of the August 1st Army, hah, Minister Tan, how could you become so unwise?" Li Xianying sighed and coldly analyzed to her secretary beside her.

But is that really the truth?

The next moment...

Li Xianying had initially thought that Minister Tan would be somewhat flustered after being exposed.

But now…

“Comrade Li Xianying, I can tell you clearly that I will take full responsibility for any problems with this report,” Minister Tan said, leaning on the table, his breathing rapid.

He couldn't help but be breathless. In all his years, he'd never felt so excited, especially seeing everyone's shock. But Li Xianying's unprofessional guidance of experts, which he saw as a complete joke, instantly dampened his initial excitement.

Minister Tan understood Li Xianying's repeated questioning; after all, the seriousness of the case was unbelievable even to him, let alone to a layman.

However, when it was all true…

Then the situation in Beijing was very serious.

“Furthermore, I will report this case to my superiors immediately,” Minister Tan's expression gradually turned cold.

His eyes began to flash with hostility.

I've had some dealings with them before, but Li Xianying has always served in Beijing and has no experience working in local areas.

In other words, she has too little experience.

Furthermore, she knows too little about their police system.

And judging from Minister Tan's experience...

It's fake, it's a joke.

Three billion US dollars.

This is the seized illicit funds.

And for a platform to grow in just ten years, most importantly, most of this was accumulated in recent years, because a platform must have started from scratch.

In other words, judging from the returns on the three billion US dollars in deposits, the amount of drugs they trafficked is impossible to estimate.

The confession matches perfectly; this case has completely exploded.

As for the leads in Beijing, there are some, but not many.

Therefore...

Minister Tan's gaze was grave; no matter what, he had to report to his superiors.

However, what was foreseeable was that it would be extremely difficult.

Because of the other party's specific identity, even Hong Shiwu, the platform's owner, didn't know who they were; they had only maintained a fixed partnership.

The expansion of these partnerships was beyond his control.

"Boss, it doesn't seem...like it's fake," the secretary's voice whispered beside Li Xianying.

This startled Li Xianying, who was drenched in cold sweat and felt icy cold all over.



Chapter 552 It's completely out of control; it's on the verge of causing a major upheaval.


With Minister Tan's voice, the meeting ended in an eerie and shocking atmosphere.

The sky had fallen.

Yes, without a doubt, everyone felt a chill run down their spines, because the sky truly had fallen.

Of course, whether this news was good or bad news is a matter for debate. The good news is that this case might change the course of events, something many could sense, but couldn't quite grasp.

They could only vaguely sense its terrifying impact on the world.

On the other hand, the bad news was equally explosive: if the drug lord in Beijing truly existed, then the news was terrifying.

Beijing had hidden such a powerful drug lord.

Then, in some unseen corner, was there also a problem in Beijing?

And there was something else that left people feeling uneasy.

That is, what had initially been cases in other provinces and cities in mainland China had now become a chain of cases, eventually leading to Yunhai.

This was the first time we had seen a drug case start with Yunhai and trace back to others.

After the nerve-wracking dismissal meeting,

Li Xianying was still in shock.

The more she thought about it, the colder her heart became.

As the person primarily responsible, Li Xianying, in his political capacity, also bears a heavy responsibility, though not the main one, but rather a responsibility that...

gives the impression of incompetence.

Even within the system, or in a place like Beijing, it's not about doing more and making more mistakes; the advantage is that you can achieve merit, and any contribution, no matter how small, will be known and valued by many leaders.

The disadvantage is also obvious: even a small problem will be scrutinized under a magnifying glass.

What might be insignificant elsewhere could be a major issue in Beijing—this is a fundamental problem.

After the meeting, as Minister Tan passed Li Xianying, he paused slightly and said, "I don't care what conflict you have with Xiao Jiang, or what you're really thinking, but this information is absolutely correct, and a large-scale police investigation will be underway soon. I hope you will fully cooperate, both for your sake and for ours."

Here he went.

Minister Tan paused slightly. Logically, he actually wanted to hand this case over to Jiang Tian.

However, firstly, he worried that Li Xianying might disapprove of Jiang Tian and cause problems.

Secondly, Jiang Tian had been busy handling so many cases.

No matter how great his achievements, Jiang Tian had already apprehended the mastermind, so these concerns were less important.

Now it was time to give Jiang Tian some rest.

"How could such a large drug lord exist in Beijing?" Li Xianying still found it hard to believe.

She even had some doubts about the entire case.

"It's normal to have doubts, but the $3.8 billion has been verified, and the other evidence is sufficient. So you shouldn't harbor any wishful thinking. If it weren't for Comrade Jiang discovering this case, who knows how terrifying the problems would have been when this criminal group was uncovered," Minister Tan said, still shaken.

After all, such a huge problem had occurred right under his nose in Beijing.

Minister Tan himself was dumbfounded.

Good heavens.

He went out to investigate a case, worked for half a day, and then found his house had been burgled?

But right now, Yun Hai couldn't spare the time either.

In the end, Minister Tan could only consider sending the special task force back to investigate the case. Although it was a drug enforcement case, it still required criminal investigation.

"Is it really that terrifying?" Li Xianying's face grew increasingly grim.

"Beyond your imagination."

3.8 billion US dollars, 2003, and the platform only took a small portion.

That proves that the actual transaction amount in this case is at least tens of billions, even hundreds of billions of US dollars—a drug case involving hundreds of billions of US dollars in 2003.

It can only be said, it's terrifying.

And such merit.

Did Li Xianying think he could influence the awarding of the August 1st Medal with his own power or influence?

Now he can't even protect himself.

This August 1st Medal.

If it weren't awarded to Jiang Tian, ​​it would be absolutely impossible.

If he wasn't even given this, who else would be qualified?

Watching Minister Tan leave, Li Xianying snapped back to reality.

"Boss, what do we do now?" the secretary asked, sweat beading on his forehead.

"I don't know."

Li Xianying shook his head. What did he know? He knew absolutely nothing about the details of the case.

Investigating it would likely be incredibly difficult.

But without a doubt, once it came out, it would be a bombshell.

It's insane.

It's truly insane.

After the meeting, it caused a complete uproar.

Jiang Tian's name instantly spread throughout the entire province.

Leaving aside Jiang Tian's other achievements, this one case alone caused an uproar throughout Yunnan Province.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau was bombarded with calls from higher-ups demanding answers.

Minister Tan also immediately called to report that this case was too big; even he couldn't handle it anymore. This was a disaster.



Chapter 553 Who dares to overturn Yunhai's small territory?


Outside, there was a commotion and commotion, but Jiang Tian had no idea what had happened.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was examining the evidence at the scene.

What would be a chaotic mess to an ordinary person was, to Jiang Tian, ​​a series of scenes seemed to recreate the events of that time.

He was also constantly pondering who had committed the act.

“Little Jiang, have you ever heard of Yunhai Yiba Dao?”

Mu Feng asked after a moment of contemplation.

“Yunhai Yiba Dao?” Jiang Tian’s brow twitched.

He then almost laughed. To be honest, given Jiang Tian’s current status and his experience returning from twenty years in the future, he always found those nicknames associated with people in the underworld somewhat amusing.

In this era, it might not have mattered, but more than twenty years later, “being in the underworld” had almost become a derogatory term, and “Big Brother” had almost become a pejorative term as well.

However, in this era, the destructive power of reputation was still terrifying.

"What's the relationship between Huayu Group and this Yunhai Yibadao?" Jiang Tian asked.

He was also racking his brains, trying to recall the name from his future life. Perhaps his memories were too distant, or perhaps he hadn't paid attention to this area; he'd never heard of the name Yunhai Yibadao before.

As for Huayu Group, Jiang Tian vaguely remembered it; it seemed to have gone bankrupt sometime in the future, but he'd heard the name before.

"This Huayu Group is the construction group founded by Yunhai Yibadao. It spans the entire city of Yunhai. Rumor has it that Yunhai Yibadao has connections in both the legitimate and criminal worlds, is extremely ruthless, and is a big shot in Yunhai," Mu Feng said cautiously.

Jiang Tian couldn't help but sigh when he saw his father-in-law's demeanor.

Oh no, he should say his prospective father-in-law.

This cautious manner suggested that his prospective father-in-law was an honest man.

It's important to understand that this prospective father-in-law is the son of the third-highest-ranking official in Yunnan Province. Normally, if he were truly arrogant and ruthless, he could easily manipulate both the legal and illegal worlds in Yunhai.

However, Mu Feng has always been honest and timid, afraid of doing anything that might offend Secretary Mu, so he wouldn't dare do anything illegal or criminal.

He couldn't utilize his background and status at all.

This created a stark contrast with those around him.

Especially those upright second-generation elites, while they might occasionally borrow resources, they would never use them, and Secretary Mu generally wouldn't allow them.

This is why the Huayu Group dared to act; perhaps even the most powerful figure in Yunhai never dreamed, never imagined, the power behind Mu Feng.

Or perhaps they hadn't investigated enough, or hadn't taken it seriously, because Mu Feng had been excessively low-key before.

Jiang Tian sighed slightly. He had to admit, Mu Feng's abilities were decent, but his personality was wrong. The company's success was entirely due to good luck, coupled with a bit of Secretary Mu's influence.

Overall, it's still too mediocre. They're destined for a comfortable life, but achieving significant success is impossible.

Otherwise, it would only harm themselves and others.

Jiang Tian gave this brief assessment in his mind but didn't pay attention further, instead focusing on the details of the Huayu Group.

"Yunhai Yibadao of Huayu Group, it's quite a coincidence," Mu Feng explained. "Back then, Yunhai Yibadao was just a local thug who luckily found a godfather who helped him rise to prominence. This godfather, having no children, passed the Huayu Group on to him. And this guy really lived up to expectations. The originally insignificant Huayu Group, under his leadership, became a well-known enterprise in Yunhai in just three or four years. Even with the recent collapse of Wenye Group, Huayu Group seized the opportunity to expand many times over."

"How do you know so much about Huayu Group?" Jiang Tian asked, a hint of curiosity in his eyes.

Indeed.

Huayu Group's rise to prominence has only occurred in the last two or three years. How could Mu Feng know so much about it?

"You forgot that I started in the construction industry too. Naturally, I know a bit about Yunhai's leading enterprises," Mu Feng said with a wry smile.

Jiang Tian understood. In fact, this Huayu Group needed to be closely monitored.

Previously, Jiang Tian's main focus wasn't on these matters, but now it's clear he can't ignore them. These forces serve no purpose other than hindering economic development and creating social chaos.

Although he's the head of the anti-drug squad, this is only temporary.

Once the time is up, Jiang Tian's future career path will be incredibly smooth. Even if he does nothing now, he's guaranteed to reach Shen Han's level, and that's far from the top.

"Any leads?" Qin Shiyu asked, her eyes sparkling.

To be honest, Qin Shiyu was extremely curious about Jiang Tian's crime-solving skills.

This was the first time she'd witnessed the entire process of solving a case firsthand.

Especially how he handles things like this.

"You can just resume construction. I'll have someone keep watch here," Jiang Tian said after a moment's thought.

"What if they come again?"

Yu Yao, the head of Shanghui Group, asked worriedly.

"Don't worry, it never will," Jiang Tian said, his eyes gleaming.

This situation is actually happening in many places now, and it's far more than just Yunhai. If you don't investigate carefully, it's not a big deal, but with a serious investigation...

Don't even mention Jiang Tian; anyone else, if they investigated seriously, would quickly find out the details.

Just then, Miss Qiu arrived in a police car with two officers from the Criminal Investigation Division.

"Don't worry, Captain Jiang, we'll solve this case," Miss Qiu assured him, her deadpan expression unchanged.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

"There's one more thing. Hong Shiwu confessed; the case has exploded," Miss Qiu suddenly reminded him.

"Hmm?" Jiang Tian's expression paused.

"The Municipal Bureau is in complete chaos right now, and it even involves Beijing. You'd better go take a look. The case in Beijing is quite complicated. I've studied it, but I don't have any investigative leads." Miss Qiu couldn't help but sigh.

"I'll go when I have the chance," Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

"Aren't you going to handle this case?"

Qin Shiyu watched Jiang Tian pat his behind and leave, asking with slight unease.

"Do you know the 'Queen of Crime Investigation'?" Jiang Tian countered.

"You mean Shang Mingyan?" Qin Shiyu inquired.

Professor Shang is quite famous, especially in this era, having solved many major cases.

“She’s Professor Shang’s last disciple. If the Huayu Group is involved in drug crimes, I’ll handle it personally. But right now they’ve only hired some thugs; that’s enough for them,” Jiang Tian explained.

What a joke.

What kind of status does Jiang Tian have now? Does he really think he can handle any case?

You want a recipient of the Special Merit Award, seven First-Class Merits, a First-Class Hero, a Drug Enforcement Pioneer, and so many other honors, to deal with a few thugs? Even if you tracked them down all the way to Yunhai Yibadao, what could he do? Lock him up for a few days. Jiang Tian doesn’t have that kind of time.

With what Jiang Tian has done recently, his status and position have unknowingly risen higher and higher.

Ordinary cases will naturally be handled by others.

“Okay.” Qin Shiyu said with slight regret, wanting to see it for herself.

“That guy just now looked a bit young. Will it work?” Yu Yao was a little worried.

Mu Feng was silent for a moment: "If he can't do it, no one in the entire Yun Province can solve it. Don't worry, start construction; there won't be any problems."

Jiang Tian, ​​sitting in the car, showed no qualms about Mu Feng's presence. Even though he was his future father-in-law, could he really stop him from making friends?

"Are you and Mu Feng very familiar?" Qin Shiyu asked with slight interest.

"He's paid for it," Jiang Tian replied calmly.

This, however, made Qin Shiyu's face show suspicion. She thought her information was wrong; he didn't seem to be faking it. Why wasn't she feeling guilty?

"To thank you, I'm treating you to dinner. Also, I wanted to ask about the investment," Qin Shiyu said, her eyes sparkling.

Jiang Tian had just analyzed the internet with her, but not in great detail, and Jiang Tian naturally nodded.

After the two left, not to mention that Miss Qiu was helping with the investigation...

At this very moment, in the restroom of Yunhai No. 1 High School...

Chen Xu, the heir to the Huayu Group, offered a cigarette to one of his underlings, then lit one for himself. After exhaling a few puffs, he opened his eyes and said, "Good job yesterday. In Yunhai, this little corner of the world, I don't believe they can rebel."

"Don't worry, boss, we'll send more people over today," the underling said gleefully.

"Hmm, do a good job. Anyone who dares to refuse us is courting death," Chen Xu sneered.

As the heir to the Huayu Group, although he was only eighteen, still of school age, he possessed a ruthless and decisive approach, having been involved in handling company affairs since early on.

He had even begun to gradually take over some of the company's businesses at the age of eighteen.

The investment in the amusement park was one such example, one that Chen Xu had used with almost unparalleled success; no one in the underworld or legitimate society dared to disrespect him.

All because of his father.

"By the way, that Guo Na, she's shameless. Find a way to get her tonight."



Chapter 554: Soon to enter the capital to meet the old man?


Time flies.

What name is trending today? Without a doubt, it's Jiang Tian. His name has completely exploded throughout Yunnan Province.

Of course, that's not the most crucial point. The most crucial point is the attitude expressed by the higher-ups, which is the most terrifying. Ignoring the confidentiality red line for Jiang Tian's anti-drug work means that in the eyes of those above, Jiang Tian has transcended the limitations of his profession.

In other words, he will move from behind the scenes to the forefront.

If it were ordinary, it wouldn't be so bad. But Jiang Tian, ​​with seven first-class merits and one special-class merit—his historic achievements—some of the details of his missions cannot be made public, but even a small part is enough to cause a national sensation.

Of course, is excessive exposure a good thing? This can only be described as having both advantages and disadvantages.

The advantage is that Jiang Tian will now be a calling card, an authority figure.

Countless anti-drug officers across the country can rise from ordinary personnel to authority figures, but only Jiang Tian has become a calling card.

That afternoon, the bright sunset illuminated the road as a Rolls-Royce slowly entered a mountain villa district through a magnificent gate.

Yunhai's development, to some extent, is undeniably blessed. Its superior infrastructure has attracted many wealthy individuals from Southeast China to purchase properties here.

Especially desirable properties were initially extremely popular.

Among them, the recently launched Zishan Villas were in high demand and short supply.

Of course, this was due to the booming development period. Later, when Yunhai's economy declined, these villas instantly became incredibly cheap.

In other words, property prices are actually determined by the city's development.

At least to a certain extent, the Zishan Villa District is the most exclusive villa district in Yunhai.

It's in high demand and short supply.

When the Rolls-Royce stopped at a particularly bustling villa in the heart of the Zishan Villa District, three people got out.

Jiang Tian was wearing a black leather jacket and jeans, very much in line with the times.

Qin Shiyu and Zhao Cainan, who got out of the car with Jiang Tian, ​​both twitched at the sight of the villa.

Good heavens.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​if they didn't know him well, they would definitely think he was a corrupt official.

"It was an investment I made before. I made a little money," Jiang Tian coughed.

Damn it, he was even used as a pretext for attacks because of this villa.

But there were advantages too. Now that this scandal had surfaced, Jiang Tian felt at peace with himself.

The villa before them was incredibly luxurious. At a glance, it was a large villa with an interior area of ​​at least five or six hundred square meters. There was a swimming pool in the yard, and facing the pool were huge floor-to-ceiling windows.

And what was most desirable was that this was Zishan Mountain, and the villa was located halfway up the mountain. From here, one could even overlook most of the sea of ​​clouds.

Such scenery was truly a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.

Even for Jiang Tian, ​​this was his first time visiting this villa, and his heart pounded with excitement upon seeing it.

In addition, the phantoms above the parking spaces also shimmer with beautiful colors.

Jiang Tian stood there, looking down at the sea of ​​clouds below, feeling the bustling activity of construction on the skyscrapers.

But Jiang Tian knew from his past life's memories that without his intervention, almost all the construction sites would have been abandoned within a year.

Investors and developers absconded, and 70-80% of the businesses withdrew from Yunhai over the next few years, plunging the city into utter ruin.

Jiang Tian felt heavy-hearted.

However, that was his past life. Now, he was here; he would change things and conquer.

"Such a beautiful land, if it can't become a future super-first-tier city, the central city of the entire Southeast, wouldn't that be a pity?" Jiang Tian's chest brimmed with ambition.

It might not be impossible.

Yunhai absolutely had that opportunity.

Fortune favors the bold. Although it brought enormous crime, if it could be contained and controlled, the resulting development opportunities would be unparalleled by any other city.

After all, while the Southeast region isn't well-developed, no one can deny the wealth of its elite.

"With such terrifying investment talent, one can imagine how successful you would be in business. Yet, you chose to become a guardian angel in the shadows, constantly facing danger and teetering on the edge of life and death," Zhao Cainan remarked, lost in thought.

Indeed, Jiang Tian and Qin Shiyu had spent the entire day discussing the internet, and Qin Shiyu was utterly astonished.

She even impulsively wanted Jiang Tian to join her investment company, only to realize with embarrassment that he was already a drug-fighting hero—obviously impossible.

But without a doubt, Jiang Tian had deeply impressed Qin Shiyu.

As for why Zhao Cainan was here:

First, it was for an interview—a very formal and fully public interview.

Second, it was...

"You need to go to Beijing. An old man wants to see you."



Chapter 555 How did I become famous without any support?


Jiang Tian was actually quite looking forward to the investment information he'd receive as a reward for his eighth First-Class Merit Citation.

He knew that the more First-Class Merits he accumulated, the more generous the rewards became, from the Grand Merit Citation to the Zishan Villa. This particular Zishan Villa, the king of the villas, had already been fully furnished by the developer.

The renovations alone cost close to ten million yuan, making the house itself worth tens of millions.

Of course, its current value didn't guarantee its future value. Jiang Tian knew that while Yunhai's current property prices rivaled those of first-tier cities, after the economic downturn, within two years, they would be practically worthless.

For example, the Zishan Villa—half price, no takers? Then a 30% discount.

But all of this was predicated on a collapsed urban economy. What if the economy rebounded, especially if Yunhai curbed crime?

Social problems were properly resolved, and the higher-ups saw hope for a solution, and continued to fully support development?

So, in the future, Yunhai could not only become like Hong Kong, but it's even possible that it could surpass Hong Kong.

It could become a special economic zone.

And all of this hinges on becoming a municipality directly under the central government.

This doesn't seem like a pipe dream.

By then, twenty years from now, when it has developed to that level, a villa like this would cost hundreds of millions?

Jiang Tian's heart raced slightly. He couldn't help it; he was terrified of poverty in his past life. Although he was dedicated to public service in this life, it didn't stop him from enjoying himself a little while maintaining fairness.

Besides, he hadn't embezzled anything; it was all done honestly, even if it was a system reward.

Jiang Tian looked at Zhao Cainan, a thought flickering in his mind. Perhaps he could go to Beijing and ask the old man what he thought?

"Hiss!!!"

"Is the old man looking for Officer Jiang? Which old man?" Qin Shiyu's heart pounded.

She had some guesses, but still gasped in disbelief.

Zhao Cainan's lips moved slightly, then she slowly uttered a name, leaving Qin Shiyu's head buzzing.

Even the usually decisive and shrewd Qin Shiyu looked at Jiang Tian with a completely bewildered expression.

"It's good news. The higher-ups have been wanting to see you for a while now, and it just so happens that we've run into each other. The old man told me to tell you to prepare in advance. It's best to go as soon as you're done; he can't wait." Zhao Cainan was still a little shaken.

After all, this young man was only in his early twenties, even younger than her niece.

But now, wow.

His achievements, if listed out, would be terrifying, especially since he's already a second-level police inspector.

You know, what's the most difficult part of working within the system?

Regardless of your status or who you are, you have to climb the ranks from the bottom.

Even under normal promotion rules, which typically involve promotions every two to three years, reaching Jiang Tian's position would take at least ten to twenty years under ideal circumstances.

How could he be so young?

Moreover, Jiang Tian's position wasn't a dead-end job. On the contrary, his achievements were enough to ensure he wouldn't dare to rest on his laurels, allowing him to rise rapidly and easily reach the rank of a department-level official before the age of thirty.

"Furthermore, the old man values ​​you highly. I feel he might have something to entrust to you. Think carefully about how to handle it, and don't boast. If the old man believes you and you can't deliver, you'll suffer the consequences," Zhao Cainan said quietly.

It has to be said that Jiang Tian's words during the last interview were still fresh in Zhao Cainan's memory, unforgettable, and even now, recalling them still stirs her blood.

Just a few words brought tears to her eyes.

Especially his words about what would happen if he died on the first day, the second day...

What a shocking and moving speech!

These simple words, spoken in just a few minutes, caused a terrifying uproar throughout the news. Even those at the top were deeply moved, and some even began consciously increasing the salaries and benefits for those in the drug enforcement industry.

This had a significant impact on the upper echelons, let alone those at the bottom.

Because the drug enforcement industry is extremely sensitive to corruption, it's something the public knows very little about; in other words, it's a mysterious profession for ordinary people.

It's a completely different concept from police stations or officers. In other words, even if you encounter them, you wouldn't know what they do if they were in civilian clothes.

Therefore, the public generally has a deep lack of understanding of this profession.

Jiang Tian's simple description of this profession in the news, which might be commonplace or even somewhat awkward in later generations, was a terrifying, top-down attack in this era—a complete and utter annihilation.

The lower classes were outraged. For a period of time, even walking down the street, Jiang Tian could hear people discussing the drug enforcement profession.

Countless people were frantically trying to find out who was the person with their face blurred on TV, but unfortunately, it remained a regret.

"After the last interview, our TV station was even surrounded by a frenzied crowd for a while. Everyone wanted to know who you were. We had to keep it a secret, and these people understood the need for drug control. But everyone was still incredibly interested in you. Many people never gave up. In fact, as time went by, so many people wanted to know you. You're already famous, you know?" Zhao Caini suddenly said.

Jiang Tian knew about this.

He wasn't even surprised. After all, although Jiang Tian often carried out missions, he was quite familiar with the surrounding area.

For example, Jiang Xiaoyu and his friends had discussed this with Jiang Tian when they met him, and some of his acquaintances had also expressed similar concerns.

Especially as time went on, this wave not only didn't subside but grew uncontrollably. Of course, Jiang Tian could understand this.

After all, everyone is curious about drug control, and in this peaceful era, Jiang Tian is practically the first anti-drug police officer to appear on television.

In other words, it's the first time he's been shown to the public.

"If he shows his face this time, everyone's going to go crazy."

Zhao Cainan couldn't help but sigh. There was already a lot of discussion across the country. In fact, Zhao Cainan knew that the TV station's backstage messages were increasing by tens of thousands every day, many of them inquiries about Jiang Tian, ​​along with letters and other messages. In short, Jiang Tian had become famous.

This was actually due to the limited resources available at the time; otherwise...

In later years, if this kind of news had the impact, it would have dominated the entire internet.

"Before any support, I'm already famous?"



Chapter 556 No amount of eloquent words can compare to a hero's family's simple words of well-being.


"Do you like it? If you do, I'll save you a room anytime."

Jiang Tian couldn't help but smile as he watched Qin Shiyu looking around and clicking her tongue in amazement.

That's right.

For Jiang Tian, ​​now was far from the time to enjoy life.

Besides, although the Zishan Villa was located in the city center, it was too far from the Municipal Bureau and other municipal facilities.

In other words, it was very inconvenient for their lives.

Jiang's father was a little better off, as he was already a high-ranking official, but Jiang's mother was far from it. Add to that his sister's schooling and security concerns, and the Zishan Villa wasn't actually a good option.

The Zishan Villa looked intimidating with its gate and guards, but in reality, it was just so-so. It was enough to scare ordinary people, but against malicious criminals or professional criminals, it would be easily defeated.

In comparison, the previous cadre's apartment had a much better level of security. Moreover, the security department had also assigned personnel to guard it, ensuring excellent security.

Compared to the Zishan villa area, while this place was luxurious, it wasn't suitable for Jiang Tian's current situation. Even on occasional days off, Jiang Tian would go home to spend time with his family. This Zishan villa, a reward from the system for so long, was a first for him.

The house was just sitting empty anyway.

Qin Shiyu, on the other hand, was beaming with delight: "I was just planning to look into buying a place in Zishan as a place to stay."

Now she could save money.

"Hmm, will you come to Yunhai often in the future?" Jiang Tian knew that Qin Shiyu's Dongling Capital was based in the major city of Donghai.

Aside from the amusement park, there weren't many other investment projects in Yunhai.

Normally, Qin Shiyu shouldn't come to Yunhai very often.

"I haven't come often in the past, but I should come more often in the future. I plan to set up a branch office in Yunhai and invest some money here. Mainly, I trust you," Qin Shiyu said.

She didn't explain why.

Jiang Tian paused slightly. His words, though simple, carried immense weight, representing Qin Shiyu's trust in him.

The entire Yunhai was in chaos, even before the chaos began. How many normal people would have the courage to invest in Yunhai?

But Qin Shiyu did.

The weight of her words, "I believe in you," was immense. Qin Shiyu believed Jiang Tian could resolve Yunhai's chaos, even lead it to new heights.

Indeed, with its unique geographical advantages, if Yunhai became the center of Southeast China, leveraging the resources of all the Southeast countries to develop and control crime, its future prospects would be far greater than any other city.

"I won't let you down," Jiang Tian said earnestly.

Looking into Jiang Tian's earnest eyes, Qin Shiyu stared blankly at him, her delicate yet mature face blank.

A feeling she had never experienced before surged within her. Yes, her heart seemed to be racing; this was the first time Qin Shiyu had felt this way.

She seems to be ill.

Why does she want to see Jiang Tian so much?

This queen of the investment industry, decisive and aloof in the eyes of outsiders, a queen solely focused on investments, was now deeply captivated.

Mainly, it was Jiang Tian's appearance that was incredibly alluring.

Especially since Jiang Tian was already extremely handsome, his ruthless demeanor, and the perfect physique enhanced by the system made his features even more striking.

With this added allure, Jiang Tian's charisma combined with his looks was fatally attractive to any woman. Qin Shiyu already harbored a special, hidden attraction to her savior.

This attraction was amplified countless times in Qin Shiyu's eyes.

It directly caused a blush, unnoticed by herself, to appear on this queen's already subtly captivated face, her temperature rapidly rising.

"Who am I? Where am I?"

Watching the two gazing at each other with such affection, Zhao Cainan felt utterly superfluous, completely superfluous.

“If you’re going to do anything, can’t you at least consider that I’m still here?” Zhao Cainan spoke up.

She couldn’t stay silent any longer, because she realized that if she didn’t, these two might do whatever they wanted. They were completely oblivious to everyone else; it was simply… simply…

Jiang Tian’s gaze naturally returned to his original position, his expression normal as he surveyed the villa’s interior.

After all, this was his first time here.

His own villa, his first time here.

It felt rather deserted overall.

Qin Shiyu occasionally stayed here, which at least brought a touch of warmth.

Qin Shiyu herself quickly composed herself, her strange expression vanishing instantly. Once she regained her composure, she nodded slightly with a cold expression, saying nothing.

“I shouldn’t be in the room; I should be outside,” Zhao Cainan said, looking at the two of them, feeling increasingly superfluous.

"Actually, this is a brief interview. The last one was a bit too brief, so this time we'll be more detailed. Mr. Jiang Tian, ​​many people probably don't know much about you anti-drug police. The main purpose of this interview is to give everyone a full understanding of the Yunhai anti-drug police," Zhao Cainan said.

In other words, anti-drug police are still somewhat mysterious in everyone's eyes.

Therefore, the primary purpose of this interview is to understand the daily life of anti-drug police: "If possible, we'd like to have a more comprehensive understanding of how your families view you, and what dangers you might face."

Speaking of which, Zhao Cainan looked at Jiang Tian.

As a famous host, she has hosted numerous projects.

And she has a huge market and popularity.

Zhao Cainan is arguably the ace host of the station, and it's been a long time since she's interviewed anyone, or rather, many people aren't qualified; that kind of thing isn't worthy of her interviewing.

Until last time.

Zhao Cainan was called by her niece, Zhao Qiuni. After a moment's consideration, she decided against it, not wanting to get involved with Zhao Qiuni's three generations of friends. However, after Zhao Qiuni briefly explained Jiang Tian's profession, his work, and the sacrifices he had made, she was instantly moved.

While the previous interview appeared to be at Zhao Qiuni's invitation, it was actually Jiang Tian's experiences, profession, and past that deeply touched Zhao Cainan.

Since the last interview, Zhao Cainan had been in a daze for a long time, her mood extremely low, mainly because what she said was too heart-wrenching.

Especially that sentence, "Who says only those standing in the light are heroes?"

True heroes are generally like Jiang Tian, ​​those who risk their lives countless times in unknown corners to capture vicious criminals.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, was moved. Since he wanted a shocking headline, he suddenly thought of Guo Na.

As they say, no amount of eloquent words can compare to the impact of a single sentence from a hero's family member.



Chapter 557 People fight for their pride, but it's best to swallow that pride.


No. 1 High School.

It has always been a key high school in Yunhai, and it was only established a few years ago. Its academic ranking has consistently been high.

The students here are either academically excellent,

or very wealthy.

In short, there's a polarization. The school can generate revenue by grouping wealthy but academically weak students together.

On the other hand, high-achieving students are strictly managed.

Overall, the number of good students far exceeds the number of poor students. After all, while Yunhai has kept up with the times and made many people rich, the wealthy are ultimately only a small minority.

Most families don't have that much money to waste here.

Even if a poor student comes here, it won't change their fundamental situation.

“You brought me to No. 1 High School. Is there anything worth interviewing here? Or is it because it's your alma mater, and we can get to know you better?” Zhao Cainan asked excitedly, her eyes shining brightly.

That's right.

In Zhao Cainan's mind, Jiang Tian was practically a legend.

"I think it must be the excellent learning environment and educational atmosphere of this school that fostered your growth and led to your current achievements," Zhao Cainan asked immediately, clearly having misunderstood something.

Of course, it was obvious that the higher-ups were going to support Jiang Tian and even promote him, making him a calling card. After learning about Jiang Tian herself, Zhao Cainan had also developed a strong interest in this young man who was not yet twenty-two years old, most people his age hadn't even graduated from university yet.

Especially his growth story.

Before receiving the Special Merit Citation, Jiang Tian hadn't actually received the corresponding benefits due to confidentiality regulations.

Because the most basic benefit of the Special Merit Citation was that his achievements would be displayed for all departments within the Party to observe and learn from.

For example, Comrade Jiang Tian... etc...

If the confidentiality regulations were lifted, it would, to some extent, mean that Jiang Tian would also receive this treatment. Zhao Cainan didn't know much about Jiang Tian, ​​and was now eager to interview him further.

"What kind of high school is No. 1 High School that could produce someone as outstanding as you? Not only am I curious, but everyone is probably curious too. Can you briefly tell me about No. 1 High School and its impact on you?" Zhao Cainan inquired further.

Jiang Tian was silent for a moment before quietly saying, "I'm not from No. 1 High School."

Zhao Cainan fell silent.

Fortunately, this wasn't a live broadcast.

Otherwise, Zhao Cainan felt she would be utterly humiliated by this embarrassing moment.

"Why didn't you say that just now?" Zhao Cainan gritted her teeth and rolled her eyes wildly. This little rascal—no, how could she insult a hero who saved countless lives like that?

But what could she do? She just couldn't help it.

Zhao Cainan gritted her teeth, wanting nothing more than to punch that fair, handsome face. Despite having faced countless life-threatening situations in harsh environments, Zhao Cainan couldn't understand why this bastard's skin was so good.

"You didn't ask me just now!" Jiang Tian said matter-of-factly.

Zhao Cainan's frantic expression paused slightly, then a surge of excitement rushed to her head. She had just said so much, and this little rascal had deliberately watched her wallow in self-pity, and now he was even acting self-righteously, claiming he hadn't asked?

Zhao Cainan's brow twitched.

"However, I need to find someone to interview with me, someone from this school," Jiang Tian said.

He wanted to understand drug enforcement.

He wanted to understand the families of those involved in drug enforcement.

And their daily lives.

Zhao Cainan froze. Could there be some extraordinary person on this campus?

Because the Rolls-Royce was too conspicuous, he didn't drive Qin Shiyu's Rolls-Royce; instead, he drove Jiang Tian's own Phaeton.

This car was at least less eye-catching, and in this era, many people were completely unaware of the future, and the saying "beware of cars with letters" didn't circulate. So many people only felt that this Volkswagen was different, that it was bigger, better-looking, and more imposing than other cars, but in reality, they all seemed the same.

In short, it exuded a low-key atmosphere.

Jiang Tian quite liked the car, but the same went for him; being low-key was just to avoid trouble, not because he couldn't enjoy life.

The car entered the campus of No. 1 Middle School without any hindrance.

Meanwhile,

The setting sun shone its rays into a corner of the wooded area within the campus. However, because of the woods, while the outside was bright and sunny, the area was shrouded in darkness.

No. 1 Middle School was a prestigious school with a very large enrollment.

It could accommodate nearly seven or eight thousand students across three grades, covering an area of ​​approximately three hundred acres.

The school was also quite luxuriously constructed.

About two acres of woodland were located next to the training ground. This woodland was preserved because the school had considered providing a cool place for students during summer training sessions.

But at this moment, a crime was being plotted in this woodland.

Chen Xu squatted on a stone platform.

His face was filled with barely suppressed excitement and exhilaration.

As the school's top dog, the undisputed leader, no one, inside or outside the school, dared to disrespect him. Even the teachers didn't dare to interfere.

It was all because of Chen Xu—he was rich and powerful.

And those two things were enough.

Add to that his ruthlessness; he had even broken the legs of those who had offended him before, yet they escaped unscathed, making him utterly untouchable.

He had numerous henchmen among the local thugs. It could be said that most of the time, he roamed freely within the school and even the surrounding area.

But that didn't mean he could only act arrogantly in that area. Having taken over his family's business empire ahead of time, he wielded immense power, which fueled his arrogance.

"That bitch, she doesn't know what's good for her! Brother Xu gave her face, and she dared to refuse again and again! Damn it, we absolutely can't let that bitch get away with this!" A henchman below the stone platform cursed along.

Chen Xu was indeed instantly filled with righteous indignation, and his expression immediately darkened.

"Boss confessed his feelings a few days ago, that was just to give her face. That bitch is shameless. If Boss doesn't teach her a lesson, everyone will think they can disrespect you."

"Yeah, it's all over school now. That bitch, just because her dad's a policeman, she clearly doesn't respect you, Boss."

At this age, and being young, their minds aren't mature. Teenagers act impulsively and dare to do anything.

Chen Xu has gathered a large group of such people, who are completely obedient to him.

Therefore, although Chen Xu has met and taken in many thugs from his father's company who were once part of his gang, he actually prefers this group of teenagers around his own age. They act recklessly, don't have the arrogant airs of the older generation, and are the easiest to control.

"A police officer's father?" Chen Xu sneered. "Even a grandfather wouldn't help. In front of me, even if you brought the Heavenly King himself, I'd still take that bitch down."

This is the essence of "a Buddha fights for a stick of incense, a man fights for a breath of air," especially in the underworld. Otherwise, no one would believe him anymore, and his underlings would become disloyal.

He had to earn back that breath of air. How? Tonight.

A lewd smile and anticipation gradually appeared on Chen Xu's face.

Thinking of Guo Na's face, Chen Xu's greed grew even stronger: "Have someone ask Shen Yan why he hasn't brought her yet. Why is he taking so long? Does he still want her?"

The more he thought about it, the more impatient Chen Xu became.



Chapter 558 My name is Chen Xu, arrogant and domineering, who else can compare?


"Young Master Chen gave her face, but she didn't appreciate it."

"She should have brought it already."

"Once she brought it, it's a done deal, she has no choice."

"She became Young Master Chen's concubine, and she doesn't even know how to cherish it."

In this era, it's undeniable that the popularity of gangsters, underworld bosses, and school bullies has led many to inexplicably idolize these figures.

This is especially true for young people.

They idolize Young Master Chen.

Because Chen Xu, at their age, has accomplished things they couldn't at their age—for example, owning his own company and driving luxury cars.

Even before him, similar incidents had occurred, but faced with Chen Xu's power and influence in both the legal and illegal worlds, there was practically no recourse. The parents of the insulted girls could only swallow their anger and transfer their daughters to other schools.

Even those who caused trouble quickly and quietly disappeared under Chen Xu's coercion and enticement.

There were also some reckless individuals who dared to provoke Chen Xu, only to be beaten half to death by him.

Each incident shattered the worldview of these young men.

"Young Master Chen, they're here."

Just then, one of the henchmen's eyes lit up as he looked into the distance.

A woman was leading a young girl towards the jungle.

And even from a distance, they recognized the girl immediately.

It's undeniable that Guo Na was quite beautiful, definitely among the most pretty in the school. Coupled with her mature demeanor and excellent social skills, Guo Na was incredibly attractive to the young men.

At this moment, Guo Na's brows furrowed more and more as she walked: "Meng Qian, where's Teacher Zhao?"

As she spoke, Guo Na stared intently at her classmate, Meng Qian.

Meng Qian was Guo Na's classmate.

Although their relationship wasn't great, Guo Na was walking home with Jiang Ning after school.

Recently, Guo Na's experiences had been incredibly stressful.

After her father's sacrifice, Guo Na had been dealing with the funeral arrangements for the past few days. Not only that, but even during the funeral arrangements, relatives from her own clan came to try and take advantage of her family's wealth, which chilled Guo Na to the bone.

Fortunately, the city police were very protective of Guo Na. As the only son of the Guo brigade leader, they helped her with the funeral arrangements and compensation. Although Guo Na was a minor and gave those relatives and friends a chance, under the city police's surveillance and considering that Guo Na was about to come of age, they didn't give those who wanted to take advantage of her family's wealth any opportunity.

Even so, Guo Na was extremely troubled lately.

On top of that, dealing with her father's funeral was exhausting. Just when she finally managed to resolve these issues, because the college entrance exam was approaching, Guo Na rushed back to school, only to be confessed to by those delinquent students.

As for who this Chen Xu is, she doesn't know him at all, and has had very little contact with him.

Because these good students were mostly focused on their studies, their sudden, domineering demand to be her girlfriend completely stunned Guo Na. She almost thought the person was mentally unstable.

Following this, a few thugs started mocking her, jumping out and calling her ungrateful.

After telling the teacher, the teacher, after understanding the situation, simply told her to ignore it and avoid them in the future.

At that moment, Guo Na felt somewhat helpless.

The helplessness of losing her biological father.

If her father were still alive, things wouldn't have turned out this way.

Guo Na thought to herself. After telling Jiang Ning what happened, Jiang Ning patted his chest and promised to tell her second uncle, who would definitely put pressure on these people and prevent them from acting recklessly.

Then, just as school was letting out—because it was Friday, there was no evening self-study—this girl named Meng Qian showed up.

She said Teacher Zhao needed her help.

Inside the schoolyard, Guo Na didn't think much of it. She told Jiang Ning to wait, and followed Meng Qian. But the further they walked, the more secluded it became, eventually reaching a grove of trees in a corner of the schoolyard.

"Teacher Zhao is cleaning over there, it seems she wanted your help," Meng Qian hurriedly explained.

Guo Na breathed a sigh of relief and didn't think much of it.

It was dismissal time, Friday. Teachers and others were leaving, and most students had already left the school gates. The spacious schoolyard was now somewhat quiet.

And in the grove of trees in the corner of the schoolyard, the most secluded spot, it was eerily quiet, utterly silent.

"You're lying..."

Stepping into the grove, Guo Na suddenly stopped, sensing something was wrong.

Perhaps inheriting Guo Da's talent, Guo Na keenly sensed something was amiss.

Why would Teacher Zhao be here on Friday?

What cleaning is there?

Most importantly, Teacher Zhao, who would ask a student for help even to get a water cup, was so lazy—why would she be here cleaning after all the other students had left?

"She realized what was happening, but it was too late."

Just then.

Chen Xu, who had been waiting for a long time, emerged from the dark woods with his henchmen.

Two of them blocked Guo Na's retreat.

It wasn't that Guo Na was careless.

Mainly, this was a school; no one would expect someone to be so ruthless in a school.

"What are you doing?" Guo Na looked at the people who appeared before her with tense eyes. She had a keen sense that something bad was about to happen.

She was probably in trouble.

After all, she was just a seventeen-year-old girl.

Faced with this situation, she was filled with tension, confusion, and helplessness.

Chen Xu sneered arrogantly, "You made me lose face so badly today, what do you think I want to do?"

"If you feel embarrassed, then I can apologize," Guo Na said, forcing herself to remain calm.

"You fucking don't even know who I am? I'm Chen Xu! Not just at No. 1 Middle School, but in the entire Yunhai, how many people dare to mess with me? You dare to be so disrespectful in front of so many people? I'm going to mess with you today, what are you going to do about it?" Chen Xu swung his arms, puffing out his chest arrogantly.

"My dad's a policeman," Guo Na said, her eyes wide with anger.

In broad daylight, she couldn't understand why someone could be so arrogant.

"A policeman, hahaha, I'm so scared, so scared! Hahaha, so what if he's a policeman? I can make one phone call and your dad'll be suspended tomorrow!" Chen Xu became even more arrogant.

He even took out his phone and made a call right in front of Guo Na, asking for her father's specific information.

In fact, the word "policeman" did make Chen Xu back down a bit.

"What did you say? He's already sacrificed his life?"



Chapter 559 If Nana gets into trouble, he'll be left with nothing.


The high school campus is always filled with memories of the past, even the flowers and plants are imbued with them. Jiang Tian looked around, filled with emotion.

Once upon a time...

Although Jiang Tian didn't attend the top high school, but rather another key high school, the Third High School, he had indeed been a top student in junior high, otherwise he wouldn't have been able to get into such a prestigious school.

However, after entering high school, he finally understood that saying:

Effort can't compare to talent. No matter how hard he tried, in high school, it seemed difficult to learn anything. He never had that much logic, and he could never keep up with his classmates.

His grades plummeted from above average to becoming a disgrace to the class.

They plummeted. The less he understood, the less he could learn, and learning became a source of pain, at least for him.

Until his senior year, after three years of suffering, Jiang Tian seemed to have an epiphany. Perhaps his memory finally improved a little, and his grades steadily improved, ultimately leading to his astonishing admission to the police academy.

Learning requires talent.

Jiang Tian recalled his past. Unloved and neglected by his family, his high school years were a miserable experience, looked down upon by everyone. But getting into police academy was a way of vindicating himself.

Jiang Tian felt a pang of regret.

"Coming to the high school campus, I can feel the youthful atmosphere and feeling, as if I've returned to the past. And today, our protagonist, Mr. Jiang Tian, ​​is searching for our protagonist."

Zhao Cainan sighed. The live broadcast truck had already set off on their way here.

At this moment, it wasn't a live broadcast, but a scene from the interview, which would be edited in later.

After all, if a live broadcast went wrong, it would cause huge problems instantly.

Furthermore,

Actually, the identity of Guo Na, whom Jiang Tian is searching for, still needs to be blurred out, with her face pixelated, and her identity needs to be kept somewhat secret.

Of course, it doesn't need to be kept strictly secret.

Because in cases like Guo Na's, death is the end, and Captain Guo has already sacrificed himself. Even if there are drug lords seeking revenge, they wouldn't deliberately cause trouble after Captain Guo's death.

This is a consensus.

If someone is dead, and you still seek revenge against their family, then you are challenging the bottom line—an absolute bottom line, an absolutely intolerable bottom line—and will face the most ferocious retaliation.

It might even lead to a widespread terrorist attack.

Slightly blurring one's identity can at least somewhat conceal it. Those with the ability to find the real person wouldn't do this, and those without the ability can't find out who it is.

Therefore, Jiang Tian didn't hide it too much.

After all, no matter what, he had already exposed himself, completely and without reservation. Everything else was irrelevant. After all, if you were to say who the main protagonist in the fight against drugs was, it would definitely be Jiang Tian.

Having fought battles across the country, it could be said that Jiang Tian appeared successful, but his enemies had unknowingly spread everywhere.

Blocking someone's path to wealth is like killing their parents.

Based on this statement, Jiang Tian not only blocked the drug lords' path to wealth in Yunhai, but also blocked their nationwide influence through Yunhai.

The firepower was, in fact, entirely focused on Jiang Tian.

In other words, Jiang Tian could face a terrorist attack at any moment.

Or even a fatal attack.

However, before the incident was exposed, Minister Tan specifically discussed it with Jiang Tian, ​​asking for his opinion. Jiang Tian's opinion was clear: he didn't care.

No matter how many came, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid at all.

In fact, Jiang Tian hoped the more came, the better, so he wouldn't have to dig them out one by one.

Since the last incident, Jiang Tian hadn't had time because he was constantly on missions. His mother, however, had taken Jiang Ning to buy a new phone.

After revealing his identity and entering the school gate, Jiang Tian finally called Jiang Ning.

"What a coincidence."

Jiang Tian's phone rang. He glanced at it and saw it was Jiang Ning.

"Second Brother, where are you?"

Jiang Ning's tone was a little anxious. Actually, Jiang Ning was ranked according to his family lineage, with his eldest cousin being the first, and Jiang Tian the second, so Jiang Ning simply called him Second Brother.

He was used to calling him "Second Brother."

Jiang Ning, usually rather sullen and withdrawn, sounded somewhat impatient on his first phone call, which surprised Jiang Tian slightly.

"I'm at your school. What's up?" Jiang Tian asked seriously.

Actually, after Jiang Ning was attacked and Jiang Tian wiped out his entire gang, Jiang Tian, ​​for safety reasons, moved him to his home. Besides, since the school was very close to home, only two blocks away, and he was attending school, no one was specifically assigned to protect him.

"Second Brother, I just called Second Uncle. He told me to come find you." Jiang Ning clearly had some psychological trauma from Jiang Tian. After explaining what had happened, he said he had already spoken to Second Uncle and that Second Uncle had sent him.

This indicated that Jiang Ning was somewhat sensitive.

"Just now, when Nana got out of school, someone called out that Teacher Zhao was looking for her, but I just saw Teacher Zhao, and she said there was no such thing. I've looked all over the school for Nana but can't find her," Jiang Ning said in a panic.

"Calm down," Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened. "Think carefully. Did anything unusual happen today? Or is there something else going on with Nana? Or was the classmate who asked Nana to leave acting strangely?"

Jiang Tian spoke quickly as he walked towards the teaching building.

Guo Na was the only survivor of Captain Guo's mission.

For Jiang Tian, ​​Captain Guo had actually sacrificed himself while on a mission with him, escorting those people.

In the end, Jiang Tian received 80% of the credit, but Captain Guo was the one who died.

And Captain Guo's sacrifice was undoubtedly great.

To save a high-value criminal suspect, he shielded her from a fatal bullet with his own body, which ironically led to the discovery of crucial clues in the case.

And it led to a series of subsequent events.

Therefore, Jiang Tian felt a deep sense of gratitude and guilt towards Captain Guo.

He even felt somewhat sorry for Captain Guo, because from the moment he joined the brigade, Captain Guo had taken good care of him.

And as Captain Guo's only child, even his only son...

Jiang Tian felt a pang of heartache for Guo Na. Although she wasn't much older, Jiang Tian had experienced far more in both his past and present lives, and he saw Guo Na as his own daughter.

If even Guo Na, the only child of the Guo brigade's family, couldn't be protected, then...

Jiang Tian's face grew increasingly cold.

"I remember now, this morning, there was someone named Chen Xu..." Jiang Ning recalled something unusual.

"Most importantly, I just saw Meng Qian, and Meng Qian said she'd never seen Nana before. It was clearly her who called her away! Second brother, something must have happened to Nana! That Chen Xu is incredibly audacious; he's capable of anything! Please save Nana!"

Jiang Ning's voice was urgent.

Jiang Tian had already arrived at the foot of the teaching building.

From afar, he saw Jiang Ning standing anxiously downstairs, her face showing confusion and helplessness. After all, she was only seventeen; to be able to keenly sense that something was wrong in this situation was already quite remarkable.

After Jiang Tian arrived, he comforted him, saying, "Don't worry, if anyone causes trouble for Nana, your second brother will dig up their ancestral graves."



Chapter 560 Then, let's teach this era a painful lesson.


"Heh, clueless, doesn't know what's good for you."

Not far from Jiang Ning, Meng Qian stood under a tree, watching the anxious Jiang Ning with a mocking smile.

It could be said that the longer she interacted with him and the deeper her understanding, the more she realized the terrifying power Chen Xu wielded. Yes, terrifying.

At least for Meng Qian, Chen Xu seemed omnipotent.

Big shots in society bowed and scraped before Chen Xu. Meng Qian had witnessed this firsthand, even seen it firsthand. She had even personally witnessed the incident where the parent of a girl whom Chen Xu had forced into a relationship with caused trouble, only to be forcefully suppressed by Chen Xu.

From top to bottom, he had effectively subdued the other party, forcing them to slink away from Yunhai and relocate elsewhere.

Breaking the leg of a classmate who had provoked him had no consequences.

He even frequently dealt with elites in society, handling company matters and so on.

There were so many things to uncover. Only with a deeper understanding did the terrifying extent of Chen Xu's influence in both the legitimate and underworld circles become apparent; he seemed omnipotent.

He could do anything.

He dared to do anything.

There was nothing Chen Xu wouldn't do, and nothing he couldn't do.

As for Guo Na, if Guo Na had firmly rejected Chen Shao's confession today, it would have been one thing. But perhaps Guo Na was in a bad mood, or perhaps she simply ignored Chen Xu.

For Chen Xu, who had won over countless girls and whose confessions were almost always a sure thing, this was a huge loss of face.

If he didn't regain his dignity, Chen Xu might as well give up his career altogether.

But in Meng Qian's eyes, this was Chen Xu being ungrateful. So many people wanted to be Chen Shao's girlfriend, and yet he dared to show him such disrespect.

Chen Shao taking a liking to you is an honor.

Who do you think you are, daring to be so shameless?

In this era, such things happen far too often.

It could be said that this is a characteristic of this era, or rather, the gangster lifestyle, especially in Yunhai, has reached its peak.

“Meng Qian, where exactly did you take Guo Na?” Jiang Ning approached Meng Qian, her anger boiling, and shouted.

That was her best friend, her most trusted friend!

“Heh, what does where your friend went have to do with me? Coming to me is ridiculous,” Meng Qian sneered.

“It was clearly you who called Guo Na away! Teacher Zhao never told you!” Jiang Ning’s hands trembled.

Meng Qian stared at Jiang Ning for a long time, then suddenly laughed and said, “Even if I did call her away, what proof do you have? Who saw it? Let me tell you, Young Master Chen just invited Guo Na to chat. I advise you not to meddle, otherwise, if one day you or your family members break a leg, don’t blame me for not warning you. You’d better pretend nothing happened.”

Of course, Meng Qian was just trying to reduce some trouble for Young Master Chen.

"You, you shameless bastard! I'm telling you, release Guo Na right now!" Jiang Ning said urgently.

"Kneel down and beg me, maybe I'll put in a good word for Young Master Chen," Meng Qian sneered sarcastically.

Previously, Meng Qian looked down on Jiang Ning in class because Jiang Ning dressed in shabby clothes but had poor grades, making him the teacher's favorite.

In the past, with the teacher's protection, it wasn't easy to cause trouble, but now...

Having found an opportunity, a wicked thought instantly rose in Meng Qian's mind.

She would send this already lowly person back to where she belonged.

"Say that again?"

Just then, a faint voice suddenly came from behind Meng Qian.

Meng Qian was startled, but then, as if remembering that she had been working for Chen Shao, she instantly calmed down. Having a powerful backer made her fear subside, followed by anger.

She turned around sharply and saw a cold, handsome young man.

It has to be said that due to recent modifications, various enhancements, and the golden attribute of being a superhuman, Jiang Tian had experienced reverse aging.

He was originally twenty-two years old, but now he looked like eighteen or nineteen.

The person who appeared behind Meng Qian, speaking in a low voice, was Jiang Tian.

“Can’t you even walk straight? Who the hell are you? What’s it to you? Mind your own business, get lost!” Meng Qian cursed.

Jiang Tian’s gaze grew increasingly dark. He glanced silently at the camera behind him. After all, they were still recording, and he didn’t know if it would be edited later. For the sake of public image, Jiang Tian suppressed his anger in front of everyone.

But it didn’t matter. Once inside, he could take his time.

Jiang Tian wasn’t in a hurry at all.

And he wasn’t angry at all.

He smiled, but the smile was chilling as he said, “I’ll give you one last chance. Where is Guo Na? Otherwise, the consequences will be something you can never bear.”

“I don’t know. Get lost. I have things to do. I’m leaving.”

Meng Qian wasn’t stupid.

After just finishing her rant, she saw the camera following Jiang Tian not far away and immediately realized something was wrong, so she hurriedly tried to retreat.

If Jiang Tian were here alone, Meng Qian would probably have slapped him across the face.

As someone who followed Chen Xu, Meng Qian acted like a big sister.

In reality, in Jiang Tian's eyes, this kind of delinquent was a bit ridiculous, and even more so, despicable.

It's not that being young absolves one of legal responsibility.

How could Jiang Tian tolerate such bullying of Guo Da's only daughter?

As Meng Qian turned to run, her wrist was forcibly grabbed.

"Indecent assault! Let me go! Indecent assault! Someone is indecently assaulting me here!" Meng Qian shouted loudly.

It must be said that this was a school building, and quite a few students passed through after school. Although it was past peak hours, many students were still gradually walking in.

Hearing Meng Qian's voice, they all turned their heads.

Snap!!!
