A criminal group can survive if it didn't have its own base of operations; it would have been swallowed up long ago, and wouldn't be alive now.

“So, the breakthrough is him.”

Jiang Tian held the photo in his hand, his eyes flashing with thought.

Jiang Tian was furious about Guo Na's actions.

But precisely because of his fury, Jiang Tian wouldn't act impulsively. Chen Xu alone couldn't satisfy his appetite.

Would he really bother mobilizing thousands of police officers across Yunhai with a single phone call to blockade a scumbag like Chen Xu?

These were social resources; would he really use them to pay for his rage?

Jiang Tian wouldn't do such a thing.

“Chen Xu?”

Song Liguo's eyes lit up.

“That's right. This Chen Xu, though a beast, has a very, very bad relationship with his father,” Jiang Tian said slowly.

In short, this is the story of a rebellious rich kid.

It must be said that Chen Xu is quite stupid. Although he has a high IQ, he has serious mental problems.

Using him, taking down Chen Qiang wouldn't be difficult.

Of course, for Jiang Tian, ​​the methods are terrifying.

Jiang Tian could storm their doorstep, one man with a gun, and wipe out the Huayu Group, but would that be effective?

Who knows how many crimes Huayu Group and Chen Qiang have committed?

Moreover, for Jiang Tian, ​​this behavior is utterly foolish.

If he really did this, even if Huayu Group is guilty, Jiang Tian himself would likely fall into the abyss and never be valued again.

Jiang Tian's previous brilliance stemmed from his actions being always justified. Even when he destroyed his opponents, even when he was arrogant and domineering, earning him the nickname "Killer Star," Jiang Tian always had convincing evidence to back it up.

This evidence proved that the other party's crimes were heinous and countless; at the very least, the number of people killed was enough to warrant the death penalty.

It's simple. Whether consumed personally or sold to others, fifty grams is enough for a death sentence. Anyone who does this far exceeds that number, often several times that amount. Therefore, while Jiang Tian's actions are ruthless, they are still within the framework of the law; he's not truly lawless. Otherwise, no matter how capable Jiang Tian is, the higher-ups wouldn't take him so seriously.

"And it's not just him, Chen Qiang also has a daughter." Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

He hadn't expected it to be so simple until he investigated.

"This Chen Qiang has a daughter. Coincidentally, this daughter has long since severed ties with Chen Qiang, and even got into the Yunhai Police Academy. She treats her father like an enemy, determined to bring him to justice."

As he spoke, Jiang Tian felt a slight admiration. Putting himself in her shoes, Jiang Tian fell silent. Frankly, he could never be so selfless.

Chen Qiang's daughter was undoubtedly a staunch believer in justice.

Otherwise, she wouldn't be living the life of a pampered young lady. According to Jiang Tian's investigation, even in high school, she was already working part-time, earning her own money and never taking a penny from Chen Qiang.

She even rented her own apartment, while Chen Qiang could only silently and secretly try to help his daughter.

In short...

After reading through the details, Jiang Tian was filled with mixed emotions, unsure of what to think.

However, this made no secret of Jiang Tian's desire to meet the girl.

Especially after learning that she was a junior in college, his junior, Jiang Tian's interest was piqued.

Therefore.

Song Liguo glanced at the police academy not far away. Their police car was parked on a quiet side of the road near the academy entrance.

Of course, since this was a police academy, the presence of police was perfectly normal and didn't attract much attention.

At that moment...

Lu Chun, with a little girl behind her, walked out of the school.

"Chen Liting, my name is Jiang Tian, ​​it's nice to meet you."

Jiang Tian was about to introduce himself when he saw the little girl explode with excitement.

"Officer Jiang, you're Officer Jiang! Ah, I've met you... Waaaaah, I've met you, Officer Jiang..."



Chapter 573 Good heavens, this is getting more and more terrifying, my reputation is skyrocketing.


"Jiang Tian, ​​really? Is it really you?"

"My God, I finally see someone alive!"

"You're my idol!"

The moment Chen Liting saw Jiang Tian, ​​her eyes shone with a crystalline light, as if she were seeing the sun of her dreams, or perhaps her lifelong spiritual faith.

Such enthusiasm even slightly surprised Jiang Tian.

Actually, Jiang Tian's goal in eliminating Chen Qiang had two targets: Chen Xu and Chen Liting. These two, a son and a daughter, were Chen Qiang's greatest weaknesses.

For Jiang Tian, ​​especially now that he's gradually stepping into the limelight, there's no need, and he won't, recklessly charge forward like before when carrying out missions.

While that approach is quick and powerful, leaving everyone caught off guard, the resulting aftermath and social chaos are also immense.

The damage caused by Yunhai recently is a testament to that.

Especially the Wenye Group, although recently taken over by the Tianxing Group, has caused visible social chaos and unemployment, resulting in enormous economic losses.

Of course, the Huayu Group doesn't have the same influence as the Wenye Group, and its elimination wouldn't cause as much chaos. Jiang Tian is simply using a different approach, a subtle and gradual dismantling from within.

After Chen Qiang's elimination, if there's an opportunity to cultivate someone capable of smoothly taking over the power, that would be crucial.

That's the current situation in Yunhai, the current economic environment.

Even without the Huayu Group, other forces will rise. What Jiang Tian needs now is to establish his own power base.

At this time, Jiang Tian also needs to cultivate his own forces and henchmen.

Using the Huayu Group, he can gradually control Yunhai's underworld.

Since complete eradication isn't possible, he'll use another method: a process of moving from chaos to order.

"It doesn't matter if it's a black cat or a white cat, as long as it catches mice, it's a good cat." Jiang Tian will use any method that solves the problem.

Besides, this method of Jiang Tian's isn't new. In fact, many methods and means used to deal with these criminal gangs in China are similar.

This is a product of the times.

In the future, this kind of existence will gradually disappear, but in the process of economic development, the dark side will definitely continue to exist and gradually intensify.

Even if Jiang Tian tries to eradicate them, it will still be the same.

Since that's the case, why not control them directly at the source?

However, Jiang Tian didn't expect that his plan would proceed so smoothly as soon as this idea came to him.

Too smoothly.

Chen Xu, obviously, that guy is a beast. Jiang Tian will definitely use him as an example to show everyone that anyone who dares to lay a hand on or bully the children of martyrs will only face one consequence: death.

As for Chen Liting, Jiang Tian originally thought there would be some twists and turns, but he didn't expect it to be so simple and easy.

Moreover, the existence of Huayu Group is equivalent to Jiang Tian having another intelligence force completely under his control.

"How do you know me?" Jiang Tian asked, slightly curious.

Chen Liting exclaimed excitedly, "Jiang Shen, you're the pride of our school! You have no idea you're practically a legend here. These past few days, our school has been constantly publicizing your achievements, and the teachers are all telling us to emulate you!"

"Especially since, before even graduating, during your senior year internship, you've already achieved a Special Merit Award—something unseen in decades—not to mention multiple First-Class Merits, First-Class Heroic Model, and Anti-Drug Vanguard Medals. The whole school knows you, Senior, this invincible legend! I'm your idol; we've seen your photos countless times!"

Chen Liting spoke, still somewhat incredulous that she was seeing Jiang Tian in person.

After all, she had only seen him in photos before.

Now, Jiang Tian couldn't help but click his tongue in amazement.

Because it was clear that stepping onto the stage had had an effect; once the winds shifted above, the reaction below was extremely swift.

Especially, the reaction was incredibly fast.

When the higher authorities shifted their focus, deciding to cultivate Jiang Tian as the face of Yunhai, a pillar of strength to stabilize the increasingly chaotic social situation in Yunhai,

the police academy naturally followed suit immediately.

Especially when the decision was made to cultivate Jiang Tian, ​​detailed promotional materials were prepared, including many missions that Jiang Tian could publicly display.

Although many overseas missions in Myanmar couldn't be publicized—for example, his solo infiltration of Myanmar to eliminate the massive drug lord and criminal organization in Tucha—

this was, strictly speaking, against regulations.

and wouldn't hold up internationally.

However,

the other missions were ridiculously numerous. Jiang Tian had accumulated countless first-class, second-class, and third-class merits during this period.

Especially the seven first-class merits—each case a major terrorist case of the new century, all solved single-handedly during his senior year internship—in a short period.

He was truly a model worker in the police force.

The police academy's promotion of Jiang Tian was comprehensive, truly idolizing him.

His school exploits, even those he himself might not have known, were mythologized.

For example, he was once an ordinary person, but after witnessing a certain incident, he suddenly gained a profound understanding of criminals and resolutely dedicated himself to fighting crime.

In short, some of these events weren't Jiang Tian's dark history; they were part of his journey.

This resulted in many fans of Jiang Tian within the police academy.

After all, Jiang Tian was too close to them.

A senior student.

Everyone who enrolls in the police academy enters full of passion, but for various reasons, some realize their ambitions, while others gradually sink into mediocrity, their passion slowly fading away.

But without a doubt, within the police academy, especially the experiences of Jiang Tian, ​​a senior student, instantly made him an object of admiration for everyone.

After all, for a student like Jiang Tian, ​​achieving even one-tenth of his experience would be something everyone dreams of.

"Jiang Shen, that's a nice title. I never thought I'd be called Jiang Shen one day." Jiang Tian grinned, a self-deprecating smile on his face.

Back in school, Jiang Tian was mediocre.

But from Chen Liting's perspective, his embarrassing school history—his mediocre history—was slightly altered, instantly becoming a memory of his past.

Without Jiang Tian having to say it himself, many things he didn't even know had been mythologized, leaving Jiang Tian both amused and exasperated.

"Liting, I've come to see you because there's something I'd like to ask you about."



Chapter 574 Righteousness over Family Ties, Filial Piety and Parental Love


Jiang Tian hesitated for a moment before speaking. Mainly, time was a bit rushed, and he wasn't entirely sure about the relationship between Chen Liting and Chen Xu.

"Jiang Shen, please feel free to ask," Chen Liting said excitedly.

"Get in the car, we can talk there." Jiang Tian shifted his seat, still feeling cramped, and turned to Song Liguo, saying, "Comrade Liguo, please sit in the front. I need to talk to Liting about the situation. Comrade Qiuwan, you continue driving to the target location."

Song Qiuwan was alright.

The main problem was Song Liguo's face darkened.

But at this moment, he couldn't say a word, silently opening the car door and getting out of the back seat.

Damn it.

He, a deputy director of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau, was being ordered around like a foot soldier by Jiang Tian. It was outrageous, practically a rebellion against heaven.

Song Liguo was almost bursting with anger, but thinking of Jiang Tian and what he had to do, he fell silent.

It has to be said.

Jiang Tian only briefly explained the plan, yet Song Liguo was already deeply shaken and shocked by Jiang Tian's audacity.

If this plan succeeded, it would be equivalent to the police having directly planted a powerful safeguard within the dark forces of Yunhai.

It would grant them terrifying control over criminal organizations and other powerful entities.

Oh well.

Well, it's Jiang Tian's doing.

I'll just have to bear it.

Song Liguo took a deep breath and finally obediently sat down in the passenger seat.

Chen Liting, who had been invited into the car, didn't think much of it. She simply nodded slightly to Song Qiuwan, who was driving, and Song Liguo, who was in the passenger seat, before turning back to look at Jiang Tian with adoration.

"Let me introduce you. This is Officer Song, and this is Song Liguo in the passenger seat. He's the deputy director of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau. If you graduate, you might have a chance to work under him," Jiang Tian introduced with a smile.

However, Jiang Tian immediately added in his mind that the possibility was very small, almost nonexistent.

Sure enough, Chen Liting looked completely bewildered.

Good heavens.

When she got in the car, seeing Jiang Tian's smooth commands and how harmonious everything seemed, she had assumed that both of them were Jiang Tian's subordinates.

As a result, the deputy director was treated like a lackey by Jiang Tian, ​​who bossed him around, and the deputy director didn't dare to utter a single word.

Sure enough.

Is this Jiang Tian?

Is this her idol?

"Director Song, hello, hello," Chen Liting said somewhat reservedly.

This finally gave Song Liguo a tiny bit of a sense of presence, making him feel a little better, and his tense expression finally relaxed slightly.

After Jiang Tian finished his introductions, he finally got to the point with Chen Liting: "Chen Liting, I have a very serious question for you. When family and justice stand on opposite sides, will you choose family or justice?"

Although time was short, in reality, including Jiang Tian, ​​99% of people would definitely side with their family.

Because, only with a small family can there be a larger community.

However, based on Jiang Tian's investigation and understanding, he discovered that Chen Liting was completely different. This young girl truly stood on the side of absolute justice.

Jiang Tian didn't know what had happened in the past that gave her such an extreme sense of justice, but Chen Qiang was involved in organized crime, yet Chen Liting was able to enter the police academy without hesitation and wholeheartedly take justice as her mission.

There were only two possibilities in this situation. Either she was an undercover agent, or she was already a powerful group with a scale exceeding tens or even hundreds of billions of yuan.

They had reaped enormous profits in Yunhai.

Sending his daughter to the police academy as an undercover agent was clearly something Chen Qiang wouldn't do.

Furthermore, after Jiang Tian's personal observation, he found that this possibility could be completely ruled out. If she could even deceive Jiang Tian's gaze, then Jiang Tian had to admit that this woman had surpassed him in psychological logic.

But clearly, it wasn't Jiang Tian's arrogance; such a person simply didn't exist in this world.

That clearly represents another possibility: Chen Liting is truly on the side of justice, single-mindedly dedicated to sending her own father to prison.

Therefore, Jiang Tian is very curious that this brother and sister, along with Chen Qiang, this family of three, have completely embarked on three different paths.

In a way, this is also a miracle.

“Jiang Shen, you shouldn’t ask such questions. I know, you’re asking about my father, Chen Qiang, right? Jiang Shen, are you going to do something to my father? My God, I knew this day would come sooner or later! Jiang Shen, tell me quickly, what do you need me to do? I will absolutely do it without hesitation!”

Almost instantly, Chen Liting suddenly realized something, and then excitedly and gleefully spoke to Jiang Tian.

These words almost made Jiang Tian’s CPU spin.

Jiang Tian was just thinking about how to confront Chen Liting, and in the blink of an eye, Chen Liting not only guessed it, but also excitedly wanted to kill her own father.

"No, what deep-seated hatred could there be?

It can't be some terrifying yandere, can it?"

Jiang Tian involuntarily thought of Su Tong. Yes, that girl was also a terrifying yandere. She seemed normal at first, but as he got to know her, Jiang Tian sensed her terrifying nature.

So now Jiang Tian was trying his best to avoid contact with Su Tong.

However, Su Tong kept using the excuse of Tianxing Group and Wenye Group to ask him out. Recently, Jiang Tian had refused several times because he didn't have time, but this couldn't continue indefinitely.

After all, Su Tong's father had entrusted her with the entire acquisition of Wenye Group and even bought her a luxury villa in the Zishan villa district.

"It's like this, you guessed it, but you might have some misunderstandings. This time it's mainly about your brother Chen Xu. How much do you know about him?" Jiang Tian turned and asked.

That's right.

The key is that Jiang Tian still wanted to proceed gradually, to see the relationship between Chen Liting and Chen Xu.

The next moment, Chen Liting's expression changed slightly and she said, "Jiang Shen, Chen Xu is a beast. He is not my brother. Over the years, I have seen Chen Xu do too many heinous things, but because there is no evidence, there is nothing I can do. Jiang Shen, if you have a way, you must bring that beast to justice."



Chapter 575 The time for my motherland to test me has come.


Jiang Tian's eyes lit up; such a good thing had happened!

To be honest, when Jiang Tian decided to execute the plan, he had conducted a simple investigation into Chen Liting. However, some people are very well-hidden, and thoroughly investigating them is not an easy task.

But now, after interacting with Chen Liting, Jiang Tian was completely at ease. Chen Liting's demeanor had earned his complete trust.

At least, in Jiang Tian's view, Chen Liting was absolutely selfless, completely devoid of any other emotions, wholeheartedly devoted to the public good.

Even if he were in her shoes, Jiang Tian himself could never do that, and indeed, no normal person could. He couldn't make such a sacrifice.

Sometimes, Jiang Tian's actions are based on self-interest. Although he couldn't do it himself, Jiang Tian absolutely admired this kind of person.

"That's good then. I have some unfortunate news for you. It concerns your nominal half-brother, Chen Xu. According to our investigation, he has committed numerous crimes over the years. Although young, he has harmed many people. Especially today, he dared to commit the heinous act of kidnapping a martyr's child to attempt to humiliate her. Fortunately, I discovered it quickly, and a tragedy has not yet occurred," Jiang Tian said calmly.

This is an absolutely unforgivable red line.

No matter how talented Chen Xu is, once he crosses this line, he will face a terrible end. This serves as a stark warning to everyone.

It tells everyone that anyone who seeks revenge or humiliates the children of fallen martyrs will face consequences that no one can bear.

Buzz!!!

As expected.

Upon hearing Jiang Tian's words, Chen Liting's mind exploded.

She knew a little about Chen Xu, but due to their strained relationship, the siblings had actually had little contact.

Chen Liting only occasionally knew a little about the things Chen Xu had done; in reality, she didn't know much, after all, their paths were different.

She had only noticed the smell of heroin on Chen Xu before—a foul, pungent odor, something extremely irritating and repulsive to anyone unfamiliar with it.

Having been to the police academy, Chen Liting naturally recognized that smell.

Of course, this was mainly because she had taken specialized courses on such substances at the academy.

Suspicion aside, Chen Liting lacked concrete evidence.

She had initially thought Chen Xu would at most use heroin occasionally, but she never dreamed he would dare to do something so heinous.

“Guo Na’s father was my former superior. A few days ago, after I uncovered a major case and arrested a group of key suspects, he was attacked by criminals during the transport. To protect the important criminals and preserve crucial clues, he ultimately sacrificed his life, leaving behind this only child. She already had no mother, and now she’s lost her father, all alone. It’s quite pitiful. But I never imagined that someone would dare to extend their evil hand to this young girl. This crosses the line for all police officers. After all, no one wants to see their loved ones being humiliated after their own sacrifice.”

“Especially according to the clues we’ve received, Chen Xu has done this more than once. He has forced female classmates into sexual relations on multiple occasions. When the parents protested, Chen Xu suppressed them with various cruel methods. One family even mysteriously disappeared; it’s highly likely they were secretly murdered by Chen Xu.”

Jiang Tian’s tone was relatively calm. During his time handling cases and interrogating criminals, he had witnessed almost every kind of criminal activity.

Don't even mention this; I've seen things countless times more brutal.

The most terrifying thing is that one of them was an undercover anti-drug officer.

The events that unfolded were horrifying, chilling to the bone. Even Jiang Tian, ​​after hearing about them, almost had his mental fortitude crumble.

"Heinous" doesn't even begin to describe it.

Criminals have no humanity.

Indeed.

Chen Liting's eyes were red.

"This beast, Jiang Shen, I... can I do something?" Chen Liting said through gritted teeth.

She practically wanted to tear that beastly half-brother to pieces.

Jiang Tian watched with satisfaction. Very good, full of passion. This kind of passion was most prevalent among the students at the police academy.

And Chen Liting was especially important to him.

It's no exaggeration to say that in Chen Liting's eyes, Jiang Tian was a mythical figure, a spiritual idol.

Looking at what Jiang Tian had done, coupled with the exaggerated propaganda from the school teachers, many of these events were already outrageous. The teachers embellished them slightly, and wow, they became tales of lone heroes, full of twists and turns—far more heroic and exciting than any American blockbuster.

Some teachers, in their efforts to mythologize Jiang Tian's unwavering will and excellent character, downplayed the doubts raised by some leaders, but Jiang Tian ignored all of them.

He remained steadfast in his beliefs and character.

This made Chen Liting dream of becoming a hero like Jiang Tian.

But now, what is this? Before she could even act, she died before she could even begin.

Her half-brother had committed a heinous crime?

"Now, the people are testing you. I wonder if you can withstand this test?" Jiang Tian asked with a serious expression.

Chen Liting straightened her back instantly: "Without hesitation, Officer Jiang. Give me any task you may have. Our teacher told us about something you once said, and we still remember it vividly. It's my motto now: 'The time has come for the motherland and the people to test me.'"

Jiang Tian's face flushed slightly.

But seeing Chen Liting's passionate expression, Jiang Tian felt a little embarrassed.

Indeed, those words, spoken now, were somewhat impactful, but why did he feel a little ashamed?

Overall, however, this encounter had given Jiang Tian a thorough understanding of Chen Liting.

Soon.

The car drove on the road, and unknowingly, the two armed police vehicles, one after the other, began heading towards their target location.



Chapter 576 Even if it's you, Jiang Tian, ​​you still have to give me some face.


It must be said.

Chen Xu's chosen crime location was indeed excellent.

Whether for disposing of a body or committing the crime itself, it was an extremely prime location.

It bordered the Yanjiang River.

The Yanjiang River was turbulent and its current was swift, flowing directly into Myanmar.

In fact, the Yanjiang River actually traverses the entire Yunhai region and is extremely wide, serving as a major flood control basin.

Its widest point within Yunhai reaches several hundred meters.

Even its shortest width is several tens of meters. The river is extremely turbulent, with a very fast current, especially during the current summer season when there is a lot of rain. The Yanjiang River is exceptionally turbulent.

Chen Xu's hideout was a detached villa in a relatively remote area of ​​the countryside.

Of course, this villa was built in a secluded area on the outskirts of the village, rarely accessible to most people.

For others, or normally speaking, this place would be very difficult to find, but for Jiang Tian, ​​it was surprisingly easy.

As the saying goes, never underestimate the power of a nation.

Meanwhile,

Inside this villa, Chen Xu felt uneasy.

Even though he had already knelt down, knelt before Guo Na, begging for forgiveness.

It must be said that Chen Xu, though psychologically twisted and somewhat abstract, was absolutely adaptable. When he was arrogant, he was truly arrogant; when he softened, he was just as quick.

Although this might not have been willingly done, but rather a result of gritted teeth, perhaps it was precisely because he was intelligent that he knew what all this meant.

He knew that if he didn't admit his mistake now, he would lose everything.

This wasn't because he had changed; it was simply because he was afraid.

"Guo Na, I was just joking," Chen Xu said obsequiously, but cold sweat poured down his face.

Upon seeing Chen Xu's humble demeanor, Guo Na frowned slightly.

Before Guo Na could say anything...

Chen Xu had already slammed his head against the ground.

His bullying of the weak and fear of the strong was on full display.

"Forgive me, please forgive me, I won't get up if you don't forgive me," Chen Xu pleaded, his voice trembling.

In the end, Guo Na was just a young girl with little experience.

Seeing this scene, Guo Na was completely bewildered and at a loss.

She had no idea how to react and was about to nod when

*Thump thump thump!!!*

Just then, a series of hurried footsteps sounded.

Mobilizing the entire city's police force to find one person wasn't actually that difficult.

However, for the sake of social stability, this method was unacceptable. Therefore, it had never been done often; in fact, even the police chief didn't have the authority to do so.

Firstly, the city leaders, considering social stability, would absolutely not allow it. Secondly, as the capital city, even if the city agreed, the province would have to consider the potential impact.

But don't forget, this is a period of severe crackdown.

And don't forget, who's behind all this? It's Jiang Tian!

And Jiang Tian didn't do all this solely for Guo Na. Of course, Guo Na played a significant role, but it was also an opportunity to deal with the Huayu Group.

Regardless, with so many groups and companies in Yunhai, even if they really wanted to take action against someone, they couldn't just suddenly act without explanation. Otherwise, those companies would likely descend into chaos.

Governing a large country is like cooking a small fish; this is especially true in Yunhai.

"They're here."

Chen Xu's heart skipped a beat; he suddenly realized.

Old Guai might not have lied to him just now.

However, even now, Chen Xu was still hesitant.

Because he had no idea how significant this operation was, and he didn't have a clear concept of it. But in his mind, in Yunhai, those who could challenge or deal with their Huayu Group were not nonexistent, but extremely rare.

He couldn't believe he'd stumbled upon them so conveniently.

But the large number of footsteps outside in such a short time...

inevitably made Chen Xu panic.

Godfather.

Yes, there's still Godfather.

Chen Xu hurriedly took out his phone and dialed Old Guai's number.

As for Guo Na...

Chen Xu was now a little panicked.

He was even more unsure how to deal with Guo Na.

"Have you forgiven me?" Chen Xu asked hastily.

Speaking to Guo Na, his voice was filled with anxiety, even to the point that his eyes were bloodshot.

"Say it quickly, you forgive me, say it quickly!" Chen Xu's voice was hoarse, almost on the verge of collapse.

Seeing Chen Xu's frantic state, Guo Na hesitated for a moment, then nodded slightly in fear.

Clearly, Chen Xu's manic behavior had frightened Guo Na from the start, but thankfully, after the previous attack, Guo Na's resilience had inevitably increased.

Otherwise, Guo Na didn't know if she could still stand upright.

"I forgive you, I forgive you, godfather, the classmate who joked with me has forgiven me, am I alright now?" Chen Xu didn't trust anyone else, but he trusted his godfather, Lao Guai.

And at this moment...

On the other end of the phone, after hearing Chen Xu's words, Lao Guai's tense expression relaxed slightly.

After all...

No matter what, even if you're Jiang Tian, ​​you still need evidence to solve a case.

Now, as long as everyone sticks to the same story and gets rid of that girl, and as long as she insists it was just a joke, with a little maneuvering, even if it's Jiang Tian, ​​you won't have any grounds to retaliate.

"Good job, Xiao Xu. Once this crisis is over, you must be more restrained in the future. Don't do this kind of thing again," Lao Guai said hesitantly.

The main thing is to educate him. It seems we've been too lenient. In the future, we can control him a little, but this wolfish nature can't be eliminated.

Actually, Lao Guai and Chen Qiang's philosophies are completely different.

In Lao Guai's view, a wolfish mindset is very important. What did Chen Qiang rely on to get ahead? Kindness? Reason? It's all nonsense. Did he get there by killing with a machete?

Similarly, Lao Guai also adopted a completely permissive approach in nurturing Chen Xu.

However, it seems he was a bit too indulgent now. The environment in Yunhai isn't quite right, so he needs to rein it in a bit.

Otherwise, there's nothing else to it.

After all, Lao Guai considers himself to still have some self-respect.

"They seem to have appeared outside, Godfather. What do we do now?" Chen Xu asked, his forehead covered in cold sweat.

Old Guai thought for a moment and said, "Don't panic. Just insist it was a joke with a classmate, and that you've already made up. Leave the rest to me."

In Yunhai, Old Guai believed that even Jiang Tian should give him some face.

"Okay," Chen Xu breathed a sigh of relief.

After hanging up the phone, Chen Xu's tense expression relaxed again.

And just then...

BOOM!!!

A huge sound rang out.

The next moment.

The tightly closed door was kicked open violently.

Two teams of masked armed police rushed in, and in the middle of the path, a figure walked in from outside, bathed in the evening glow.



Chapter 577 Murder! Murder!


Silence.

Deathly silence.

In the shadow of the setting sun, Chen Xu couldn't see the other person's face. He could only see that after entering—no, after kicking open the door and walking in—the person stood still. Even though the sunset's glow obscured their back, the oppressive aura emanating from them still sent chills down Chen Xu's spine.

A wave of oppression washed over him from the depths of his being, making Chen Xu's breathing rapid.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

A moment later, the figure slowly entered, the steps unhurried, but with each step, the oppressive feeling grew increasingly terrifying.

Chen Xu's breathing became increasingly rapid, and unknowingly, large beads of sweat dripped from his chin to the ground.

But unfortunately, the other person seemed not to even look at him. Instead, they walked past him and gradually reached Guo Na.

Until this moment.

Chen Xu finally got a clear look at the other person's face, which had been shrouded in shadow. Handsome features, an expressionless face, and the other person hadn't even glanced at him during that time.

Instead, the other person went to Guo Na.

He carefully examined Guo Na, seemingly checking for injuries. After seeing no obvious wounds, the person visibly relaxed.

The tense expression on his face gradually softened into a slight smile.

Seeing the other person smile, Chen Xu couldn't help but unconsciously smile as well.

"Are you alright?" Jiang Tian asked gently.

"Waaah, Brother Jiang, thank you."

At that moment, seeing Jiang Tian, ​​seeing the armed police and special police rushing into the room—almost all of them fully armed, each wearing a mask, but their murderous gazes and gun barrels fixed on Chen Xu—it seemed that if he made the slightest movement, they would shoot without hesitation.

Don't doubt it, or rather, Jiang Tian has the right to do so.

Jiang Tian is currently in the anti-drug force, but he's also a member of the security force.

Within this role, carrying a gun is just the most basic requirement; many privileges haven't been revealed. For example, right now, Jiang Tian only needs to give the order, and Chen Xu will be killed here, executed.

It's just unnecessary. Killing Chen Xu like this would be like having a dry, lifeless head, offering absolutely no benefit to Jiang Tian's high-profile operation today.

Besides, even if he killed him, what difference would it make? Would it change the current situation in Yunhai?

Jiang Tian has now moved beyond the low-level, mindless rush to accumulate merit and strength; he's entered the strategic planning stage.

Guo Na also understands better, both Chen Xu's sudden change in attitude after receiving a phone call, and Jiang Tian's presence here now.

Even a fool like Guo Na understands.

Such a grand display.

It's important to know that countless people go missing every day across the country, but disappearances are not reported until three days after the initial disappearance.

And now, in such a short time—less than two hours since Chen Xu abducted him—Jiang Tian has already arrived with a large force.

It's likely that Jiang Ning noticed his disappearance and summoned Jiang Tian.

Twice.

Twice in a row.

Last time, she was implicated by Jiang Ning, or rather, indirectly implicated by Jiang Tian.

But now.

Saved by Jiang Tian again, twice in a row—the so-called "girlish heart is always full of poetry."

At this moment, seeing Jiang Tian, ​​Guo Na felt an overwhelming sense of security, as if all danger would vanish with him by her side.

At this moment, Guo Na threw herself into Jiang Tian's arms without hesitation.

For Jiang Tian, ​​at this moment, there were no other thoughts, only the extreme rage hidden deep within his heart.

You must know, if Captain Guo hadn't been shielding a high-ranking criminal, there would have been no risk of death, and Captain Guo's sacrifice was worthwhile.

Through Shi Haichao and his team, they traveled overseas and, following the clues, arrested Hong Shiwu.

Furthermore, they completely dismantled the criminal platform on the dark web, capable of causing global upheaval, and apprehended all its leaders.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian obtained all the information, including the dark web access to Yugou.com and its source code. He also copied all the backend information – all of which was extremely valuable data.

This data was equivalent to containing information on over seventy criminal organizations worldwide, essentially compiling information on the world's largest drug trafficking ring, covering nearly half of the global drug cartel.

Of course, this dark web transaction information was limited.

While it might not be enough to locate and arrest the perpetrators, it could be crucial clues.

Sometimes, the biggest fear in solving cases is having no leads. Once you have a lead, everything becomes much simpler. All of this could become leverage in negotiations, potentially propelling Jiang Tian to global fame as a drug enforcement celebrity.

Even a global legend in drug enforcement.

Like Sherlock Holmes in the world of criminal investigation.

This case brought Jiang Tian tremendous improvement, a breakthrough, and all of this was thanks to Captain Guo's contribution.

Previously, Jiang Tian hadn't had time to visit or learn about Guo Na due to the case, but during his conversations on the way, he learned that some of Guo Na's relatives even took a cut of Guo's family's compensation.

Now, she's facing this unexpected tragedy.

It's fair to say that without Guo, Jiang Tian wouldn't be on the verge of becoming a prominent figure in Yunhai. After being groomed as such, Jiang Tian knows that the next step is for him to be promoted to a national or even world-class legendary status.

In fact, YuGou.com is a key factor in Jiang Tian's promotion.

Jiang Tian himself doesn't know what his future holds, or what path he will take.

But Jiang Tian does know one thing.

Guo's only child, who died in his mission, didn't even get to see Guo Na one last time before dying.

Now, this only child is being treated like this.

He feels Guo Na's frail body trembling and weeping.

Jiang Tian raised his hand and gently patted Guo Na's back to comfort her.

Just as Jiang Tian was suppressing his anger...

A voice rang out inconveniently beside Jiang Tian: "You must be that famous anti-drug policeman named Jiang Tian, ​​right? Let me introduce myself, my name is Chen Xu, the general manager of Huayu Group. Look at this mess! I was just joking with a classmate, and it's caused such a huge misunderstanding. Give me some face, everyone disperse, hehe..."

Anyway, Guo Na wasn't harmed.

Chen Xu, on the other hand, felt no burden.

After all, Huayu Group wasn't going to be trifled with after their outburst.

But the next moment...

BOOM!!!

Pfft!!!

Accompanied by a huge, muffled sound, Chen Xu, who had just finished speaking, felt a piercing pain in his abdomen. This excruciating pain caused him to instantly lose consciousness, but then, a deafening boom...

His entire body slammed into the wall behind him. The back of his head hit the wall, and the excruciating pain jolted Chen Xu back to reality. After a brief moment of confusion, came the excruciating agony.

Thud!!!



Chapter 578 Rebellion Against the Heavenly Gang: The Underworld Actually Threatens Me at the Crime Scene?


Chen Xu fell from the wall to the ground.

His entire body was convulsing with excruciating pain.

How dare he?

How dare he?

Chen Xu's pupils were bloodshot, his eyes wide and bloodshot, staring intently at Jiang Tian, ​​as if trying to imprint Jiang Tian's image into his very being.

You see, Chen Xu's life had been marked by a sensitive nature since childhood, and then, after his father Chen Qiang became a gangster, he was constantly indulged and spoiled.

Aside from the occasional beating from Chen Qiang, when had he ever endured such a brutal beating?

There was absolutely no mercy; the attack was clearly aimed at killing him.

With that kind of force, he truly felt he was going to die.

Chen Xu's mental state completely collapsed. He vowed that as long as he got out, he would take revenge on this person—no, he would take revenge on Jiang Tian's entire family.

He would make them live in agony for the rest of their lives.

Hatred burned fiercely within him, but clearly, he didn't realize his chance was gone.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Jiang Tian's footsteps landed on the ground, Chen Xu's face pale as he tried to beg for mercy.

The next moment.

"Stop."

A voice rang out.

Jiang Tian stopped, accompanied by a series of hurried footsteps.

He saw a man, probably in his forties, with an extremely ordinary appearance, guarded by two SWAT officers. He was the kind of person everyone would instinctively overlook in a crowd.

Too ordinary.

However, Jiang Tian couldn't help but notice.

"Officer, he said he's a high-ranking official at Huayu Group, and the owner of this house. He's here on important business."

A SWAT officer explained from behind.

Jiang Tian glanced at him but remained silent. After all, they were just ordinary SWAT officers; one couldn't expect too much from them.

Of course, with so many people mobilized, especially targeting the Huayu Group, Jiang Tian couldn't possibly be so reckless as to bring everyone in; that would be foolish. Many personnel remained outside.

"Let me introduce myself. My surname is Tang, and my given name is Tang Guai. Of course, everyone likes to call me Lao Guai. Officer Jiang, you can just call me Lao Guai." Lao Guai glanced at Chen Xu on the ground.

At this moment, Chen Xu was still convulsing and trembling, his body almost curled up like a giant shrimp on the ground.

This sight made Lao Guai's eyes almost red.

This was someone he had doted on since childhood, not his son, but infinitely closer than a son.

And now he had been beaten like this.

However, Lao Guai understood that the situation was beyond his control, so he suppressed his anger. Over the years, handling countless matters had honed his composure to a high degree, so he forced a smile at Jiang Tian and said, "Officer Jiang, I don't know what crime my godson has committed to deserve this beating. If you can't provide a reasonable explanation, our Huayu Group has a dedicated legal department and will file an appeal."

Lao Guai immediately seized the initiative.

In Lao Guai's view, although there was still a significant difference between the mainland and Hong Kong, and legal personnel didn't have the same influence in Hong Kong...

Over there, a truly exceptional lawyer can turn a guilty plea into an acquittal, as long as you can find loopholes in the law. Of course, things have improved a lot in recent years, but legal professionals still wield enormous influence.

In mainland China, this power is much less; more power remains in the hands of judges.

But right now...

If a legal team were to exert pressure, coupled with the pressure from the Huayu Group...

The police officers facing this situation would have been completely at a loss.

Or rather, they would have begun to lose their composure.

But upon hearing this, Jiang Tian smiled and slowly said, "Kidnapping, is that enough?"

"Kidnapping? How is that kidnapping? My godson was just joking with his classmate. The girl wasn't harmed at all. Officer Jiang, I know you're focused on your cases, but you can't falsely accuse someone. Otherwise, if word gets out, people will think you used to force confessions in those cases."

Old Guai's words were practically a threat.

Jiang Tian frowned subtly and glanced at the special agents guarding the door.

Old Guai could come.

However, Jiang Tian hadn't expected the special agents to bring in Old Guai and two other people who appeared to be with him.

That's right, behind these two special agents were two lawyers.

Jiang Tian didn't know what was happening outside.

But he understood now.

This matter was likely to encounter some unexpected twists and turns.

But that didn't really matter.

For Jiang Tian, ​​no one could influence what case he wanted to handle, especially not threats. This old man dared to threaten him in return.

Jiang Tian laughed heartily and asked, "Do you know who you're threatening?"

The old man frowned slightly, but inwardly he was overjoyed. He glanced discreetly at the two lawyers behind him. Indeed, at the door, he had relied on their silver tongues and various legal expertise to force his way in, much to the skepticism of the special agents.

Not only that, they even brought secretly recording equipment.

They had recorded everything that happened when they entered.

After all...

No matter what...

To assault someone before the case was clear... if word got out, well...

Originally, the old man only had a 50-60% chance of success, but now he was 80%.

“Officer Jiang, you’re still young. You don’t know how dangerous rumors can be. If word gets out today, I’m sure you wouldn’t want that, would you? After all, your future is bright. If word gets out that you wantonly beat innocent people, it’s hard to imagine the public opinion, and your bright future will likely be fraught with difficulties, your path might be ruined. My godson was just joking, and he’s learned his lesson. Officer Jiang, please be merciful!”

Old Guai said confidently.

But the next moment…

Click!!!

Old Guai stared blankly as he was handcuffed.

“What the hell are you talking about? A gangster comes out and threatens me? Has the world turned upside down?”



Chapter 579 In this world, no one can judge me.


"What are you doing? I'm followed by a famous lawyer, are you crazy?"

Money makes the world go round.

You have to understand, Lao Guai isn't a simple person; he's a top lawyer directly recruited from Beijing's elite circles. These lawyers have connections and networks.

Of course, no matter how things appear on the surface, there are still various issues at play. These lawyers have powerful resources; you could say that as long as you offer enough money, they can solve many problems for you.

"What are you doing? What are you saying I'm doing?" Jiang Tian's face darkened.

Damn it, these people are really giving them a hard time.

Jiang Tian has seen all kinds of situations, but he's never seen anyone so confident. Or perhaps Yunhai's long-term comfortable life has made them lose sight of their own limitations.

Of course, Jiang Tian understands this to some extent. After all, they've developed habits over the years, and they've even toned it down considerably, but they haven't yet grasped the current situation.

"I have top lawyers from Beijing hired at exorbitant prices behind me. Are you crazy? Aren't you afraid of being exposed?" Old Guai's eyes widened.

But Jiang Tian didn't say a word. He just glanced at the two lawyers behind him, said nothing, and sniffed Old Guai twice.

That's right.

"What are you doing?" Old Guai asked.

"You recently came into contact with heroin, involved in drug crimes, didn't you?" Jiang Tian inquired.

Old Guai's heart skipped a beat.

Because what Jiang Tian just said was indeed true.

Just two days ago, Old Guai had transported a batch of heroin.

There was no other way.

The bigger the storm, the more valuable the fish—that's what Brother Qiang said, and Old Guai completely agreed.

Even though Huayu Group was already very wealthy, with a money-making speed that even a printing press couldn't match, who would complain about not having enough money?

Old Guai and Chen Qiang were no exception.

Thanks to Jiang Tian's recent crackdown on drug crimes, the price of heroin in Yunhai has skyrocketed. Now, the market is so saturated that there's practically no supply, with prices doubling and then doubling again.

The profits were already terrifyingly high, but now the price has doubled repeatedly. One doubling might be tolerable, but a second doubling?

Who could resist? Absolutely not.

Thus, they secretly made a fortune. After all, it wasn't just them; other criminal groups were also committing crimes recklessly during this period.

They were making a killing.

While some could resist, not everyone could. With large-scale shipments going unchecked, a herd mentality naturally emerged.

This was also due to a weak foundation. Previously, Yunhai's crackdown and investigation of drug trafficking were not strict enough, and there was a lack of comprehensive control measures. Even with widespread searches and crackdowns, it was difficult to achieve effective results.

"Are you kidding me? You're going to arrest me based on this?" Old Guai was dumbfounded.

Yes, he had done it.

But how could he admit it?

Besides...

Are you a ghost?

Or do you have a dog's nose?

Two days have passed, and you can still smell it—no, this person is bluffing.

Old Guai comforted himself.

But he didn't know that Jiang Tian already had enough with that, and besides, Jiang Tian never intended to let Old Guai go from the start.

"Jiang Tian, ​​I advise you not to make a mistake. All your actions will be exposed." Old Guai was furious, looking at the handcuffs.

Damn it, he failed to rescue his godson and was directly arrested by Jiang Tian, ​​this unpredictable person.

"Officer, we are from the Dongcheng Law Firm in Beijing. This is my lawyer's ID. We have witnessed all your actions. We have the right to expose your overstepping of authority and disrespect for human rights."

At this moment, the two lawyers behind Old Guai seemed to be seeing this for the first time.

They hadn't reacted at first, but now they exchanged a glance and stepped forward.

Mainly, they were somewhat intimidated by this scene.

They had rarely seen such a terrifying situation and criminal case.

But there was no other way; having been paid to solve someone's problem, he had to grit his teeth and walk out.

"Officer Jiang, I advise you not to make a mistake. As our client said, we have the right to record and preserve all your actions, and if necessary, expose them. We might even appeal against you in Beijing. We hope you won't let a moment of anger drag the entire Yunhai City Public Security Bureau into this mess, causing them to bear heavy penalties together," another lawyer stepped forward and spoke up.

Jiang Tian's gaze remained unchanged.

Old Guai smiled; he was afraid. In his eyes, Jiang Tian was indeed afraid.

Chen Xu, lying on the ground, also let out a deep sigh of relief.

Then, Chen Xu's face filled with ferocity. Today's humiliation would be repaid in the future.

He would get revenge. Once he got out, he would make Jiang Tian suffer terribly.

"Then, I will now place you in criminal detention on suspicion of drug trafficking," Jiang Tian said nonchalantly, shrugging.

Jiang Tian knew he would face these lawyers sooner or later.

The killings were too swift, so the case was closed before these lawyers even had a chance to appear, and once it reached the prosecutor's office, it had nothing to do with Jiang Tian anymore.

"Do you have any evidence? And I must solemnly warn you, without evidence, you have no right to criminally detain my client," one of the lawyers quickly jumped in.

There was no other way.

Old Guai had given too much.

Huayu Group was far too wealthy.

Besides, their Dongcheng Law Firm wasn't afraid of these things.

When it came to connections, their Red Circle firm wasn't afraid of anyone.

Old Guai's lips curled into a wide, uncontrollable smile.

His eyes held a hint of sarcasm.

No matter what, this was a society governed by laws and regulations. Even if Jiang Tian was domineering, could he really act against the law?

He might have been able to do so before, but now, it was all in the lawyers' hands.

Especially now, with Chen Xu lying on the ground, it's a pity that Jiang Tian didn't get a chance to make a move. Otherwise, it would confirm the disrespect for human rights and the illegal assault on a suspect. Of course, it would be enough to make Jiang Tian regret it now.

“Now, release me and Ah Xu, and we can be friends again, pretending nothing happened.” Old Guai decided to back down half a step to prevent Jiang Tiangou from taking a desperate gamble.

He held the advantage, so Old Guai only allowed himself to retreat half a step; no more.

“What, you’re really not afraid? Without evidence, you have no right to arrest people,” Old Guai pressed further.

That was true.

Even if it were an arrest, it could only be called a summons for investigation. The key was that without any evidence, the person involved had the right to refuse to cooperate with the investigation.

But the next moment…

A voice suddenly rang out from inside the room.



Chapter 580 Crazy Explosion, Goodbye Billion-Dollar Crime, It Exploded


"I testify that Tang Guai conducted a drug deal the day before yesterday. I personally witnessed him conspiring with Chen Qiang."

Whoosh!!!

Accompanied by the sound.

Instantly.

All eyes converged on one direction, and the direction of the shadow was Chen Liting.

Yes, at this crucial moment, Chen Liting stepped forward.

Or rather.

Chen Liting had been silently observing since she entered.

Especially seeing the terrified look on Guo Na's face, and even recalling the rumors about this beastly brother, she found it hard to imagine that without Jiang Tian's intervention, this only descendant of an anti-drug hero, the child of a martyr, her father having just sacrificed his life, her only daughter, would have suffered such a terrible fate.

That kick, Jiang Tian's kick, Chen Liting felt a great sense of satisfaction.

She even felt it wasn't enough; such a beast deserved to be torn to pieces.

She just hadn't expected so many things to happen afterward.

And at this crucial moment...

Just when everyone thought Jiang Tian was out of options, or rather, had no way out under pressure from the two top-tier law firms,

Chen Liting stepped forward.

Others stepping forward had no effect.

But when Chen Liting appeared,

“Officer Jiang, giving false testimony carries legal consequences.”

“That’s right, I hope you don’t make another mistake.”

“Officer Jiang, giving false testimony is going too far. Or perhaps you don’t know the power of our top-tier law firms in Beijing?”

The two lawyers were slightly taken aback for a moment, then reacted, a cold smile appearing on their faces. Having handled so many cases, they had seen it all.

At this point, giving testimony out of nowhere was completely useless.

Unless…

There’s someone else involved.

Or rather, this person giving testimony must at least have a respectable identity.

Before the two lawyers could finish speaking, Old Guai's shocked voice rang out: "Liting, you..."

"Mr. Tang, you know her?" The two lawyers' hearts skipped a beat.

Unfortunately, Old Guai was only filled with shock.

In reality, Old Guai treated Chen Xu very well, and similarly, he treated Chen Liting well.

He knew that although Chen Liting had been admitted to the police academy, she was full of justice. Although Old Guai was on the opposite side, he didn't have many thoughts about this niece, and even thought it might be a good thing.

After all, a family can't be completely wiped out; there always has to be someone completely innocent.

It was also to prevent future accidents. Chen Xu had already gone astray, but Chen Liting was a good alternative.

Old Guai just didn't expect that the person who would deliver the final blow to him would be Chen Liting.

"Who are you?"

"Mr. Tang, who is this woman?"

Chen Liting's appearance also interrupted the two lawyers' flow of conversation.

This caused them to feel flustered for the first time since entering the room.

Or rather, it was beyond their expectations.

What happened next was completely unexpected and would far exceed their predictions.

"My dad is Chen Qiang, the chairman of Huayu Group. The night before last, I overheard his conspiracy with him. They've sold nearly a billion yuan worth of heroin recently, making over 900 million yuan in profit."

There's practically no cost. To put it another way, in the current climate where the bigger the storm, the more expensive the drugs, heroin has virtually no cost.

Because the profit is terrifyingly high.

From sourcing the goods to even manufacturing them themselves, there's almost no cost. And the price, which was already only one-fifth to one-tenth of the cost price, is now almost one percent of the cost to profit.

In other words,

a cost of one yuan has yielded a profit of nearly one hundred yuan during this period.

Who wouldn't be envious?

If regulations were more robust and enforcement stricter, this kind of boom could be stopped.

But unfortunately, the foundation is too weak.

This area has always been a major drug hotspot, almost never experiencing a surge in activity. Therefore, effective control has been virtually nonexistent. Even with widespread, overt searches, many small shipments of drugs have been uncovered.

However, some clandestine channels have their own hidden routes and transaction methods, coupled with established customers, making effective arrests difficult unless they are dismantled from within. This requires undercover agents, but due to a weak foundation, undercover agents are scarce, and methods like baiting and setting traps are virtually nonexistent.

The inability to effectively prohibit drugs means that those who initially resisted temptation, seeing those around them making a fortune while walking on the edge of a precipice, could no longer resist.

The greater the power, the more they earn.

In this short period, less than ten days or half a month, Chen Qiang personally amassed nearly a billion yuan.

That's how terrifying it is.

"Liting, what nonsense are you talking about?" Old Guai stared wide-eyed, unable to believe that Chen Liting had betrayed him at this crucial moment.

The most crucial question is, why is Chen Liting here?

“Uncle Tang, I overheard your conspiracy with my father the night before last. I don’t know if this can be used as evidence, but I can testify that Uncle Tang and my father did indeed conspire to trade drugs, with profits exceeding one billion.”

Chen Liting reiterated this point.

She then emphasized, “This one billion profit was earned in less than half a month.”

BOOM!!!

It exploded.

Initially, those present were incredulous.

Followed by utter madness.

One billion profit.

What terrifying profits!

Most terrifyingly, this was achieved in just half a month.

Actually, thinking about it carefully, it makes sense. Perhaps Huayu Group hadn’t always been involved in this, but Huayu Group had its own natural advantage in distribution channels: its various entertainment venues, including nightclubs and bars.

These were practically natural sales venues, allowing for direct, one-stop sales, possibly even eliminating middlemen.

From this perspective, if they aggressively stocked up, within ten days or so, during this period when the entire Yunhai region was trembling with fear and white powder was in short supply, a daily profit of 100 million yuan would be entirely within a reasonable range.

However, the way it's phrased sounds different.

One hundred million a day.

What terrifying profits!

Even among domestic listed companies, are there any that can achieve this level of profit?

Yes, but extremely rare.

And the key point is...

No one expected that Jiang Tian's latest move, this casual action, would uncover yet another horrific and heinous drug crime.

This is...

the taste of a first-class merit award?



Chapter 581 Piercing Nails, I'm a Professional


Of course, Chen Liting wasn't just making things up. The other night, when the two were discussing this matter over making money, they didn't even consider keeping it from Chen Liting.

Or rather, Chen Liting had never been interested in these kinds of things.

It wasn't that similar things hadn't happened before, but Chen Liting basically had no desire to even listen, let alone participate. They understood that Chen Liting didn't want to be complicit with them.

They shared the same father, yet the brother and sister were on opposite extremes: one extremely righteous, the other having embarked on a path of evil.

Sometimes Chen Qiang even wondered if God was deliberately punishing him for these two.

Of course, being his daughter, she occasionally overheard some of his schemes.

The other day, she happened to overhear.

A profit of one hundred million a day was truly terrifying.

Their voices unconsciously became louder as they spoke.

So, in the end, Chen Liting overheard.

She exploded.

The scene erupted in chaos. No one could believe it. Everyone knew who Chen Qiang was, especially since the daughter of the chairman of Huayu Group was personally testifying.

The two lawyers turned green.

They could accuse Jiang Tian of perjury before, but now they were contradicting themselves.

Was this perjury?

This was utter nonsense!

“Liting, are you crazy? That’s your own father!” Old Guai was dumbfounded.

This scene was completely unexpected.

But Chen Liting sneered, “I don’t have a father who’s a social scumbag. He’s a menace to society, involved in organized crime in Yunhai, and countless people have been persecuted. Now I’m saying no to him.”

Perhaps Chen Liting didn’t need such a privileged background.

Or rather, she didn’t enjoy her family’s wealth much. After starting university, she became self-sufficient. Even in high school, she hardly needed her family’s money, supporting herself while earning a living.

Of course, if it weren't for making money, he might have been able to get into a better school.

"Tang Guai, do you have anything else to say? Of course, everything you say now will become evidence in court, and you have the right to remain silent." Jiang Tian remained calm.

In this situation, Jiang Tian hadn't even shown any other expression.

Or rather, even without Chen Liting today, Jiang Tian was determined to arrest her.

Jiang Tian could arrest someone, even without evidence now, but with his abilities, he could easily find a mountain of irrefutable evidence within hours. If you don't want people to know, don't do it in the first place.

But Tang Guai was Chen Qiang's trusted henchman; could he possibly be clean?

"Holy crap, ten billion in ten days!"

"That's it, no doubt about it, another first-class merit."

"No, the sheer scale of Huayu Group is terrifying in itself, hiss!!!"

Meanwhile, standing in the crowd like a nobody, Song Liguo, who had been sweating bullets from being harassed by the two lawyers, now stared wide-eyed, his eyes bloodshot and his breathing rapid.

Damn it.

Song Liguo never expected this!

Today, Jiang Tian suddenly mobilized the entire police force of Yunhai, directly targeting the children of martyrs and the daughter of his former leader who had been bullied.

All for Guo Na alone.

What does this mean?

In fact, Song Liguo wasn't without concern beforehand.

Because it's clear that this incident requires an explanation. Jiang Tian has more privileges than others, but the city and province require an explanation.

Just because Guo Na was bullied, or rather, because one person was kidnapped.

Initially just a disappearance.

He suddenly mobilized such a large number of resources. In a sense, Jiang Tian is essentially using thousands of police officers to find one person.

But at the same time.

Other people going missing or being kidnapped, at most a large team would be mobilized, twenty or thirty people at most.

This easily gives others ammunition.

Without a satisfactory result or achievement, it's easy for others to hold them accountable.

Leaving aside others, let's take Han Xian, the current director of Yunhai Bureau.

Although he's been semi-hidden since the last incident, not daring to cause trouble, if he's in the right and has found a weakness in Jiang Tian, ​​Han Xian will definitely seize on it relentlessly.

Don't be fooled by the silence everyone seems to have after Jiang Tian's phone call; they're all waiting to see him make a fool of himself.

It's like when Chen Qiang called his backer, and that backer's sarcastic remarks weren't without this reason.

"Take them all away." Jiang Tian waved his hand, directly ordering Chen Xu and Lao Guai to be taken to the police car without further ado.

As for him, he naturally had other things to do.

First, today's events definitely needed to be kept secret.

After all, no matter what, Jiang Tian planned to install Chen Liting as the successor to Huayu Group.

Huayu Group couldn't fall.

Or rather, even if Huayu Group fell, the market would still be there, and the demand would remain.

Even without the Huayu Group, various other entities have emerged to fill the market gap left by its disappearance.

So, since a complete sweep is impossible, and since others will continue to control the market, it's better to let the Huayu Group maintain control.

In fact,

through the Huayu Group, Jiang Tian could still have immediate and clear information about the movements of the entire Yunhai underground criminal organization, preventing this current situation.

There's absolutely no information about the Yunhai underworld.

"Alright, it's alright. This kind of thing will never happen again." Jiang Tian patted Guo Na in his arms.

His heart held no other evil thoughts, only pity.

After all, this young girl had endured too much.

Especially.

This time, Guo Na had even helped him a great deal. Without her, Jiang Tian wouldn't have targeted the Huayu Group so quickly.

One hundred million a day!

What era is this? One hundred million in 2003—not to mention surpassing a billion in later generations, the current population of one hundred million is definitely smaller than the future population of one billion.

Without massive economic stimulus or a booming real estate market, the wealth of the rich in this era is still incredibly substantial.

On the other hand, in this era, nightclubs and bars are synonymous with great fame.

Especially the Southeast Road Bar of the Huayu Group.

It's now a renowned nightlife venue throughout Yunhai, and even resounding in several Southeast Asian countries.

By controlling the Huayu Group,

Jiang Tian can then use the Huayu Group to continuously plant undercover agents, eventually taking control of the entire Yunhai underworld. At that point, dealing with criminal groups will be a piece of cake.

Any criminal activity will be met with swift and decisive action from Jiang Tian.

One strike, one elimination.



Chapter 582 A tit-for-tat confrontation, news that ignites the entire nation.


Today's events served as a lesson for Jiang Tian: simply cracking down on organized crime is useless. The social environment here provides fertile ground for dark forces.

This isn't just true here; it's the same in mainland China.

Of course, the situation in Yunhai is currently even more serious.

In fact, many ruthless thugs from Myanmar have come to the area with organized crime. They're willing to do anything; as long as they're paid, murder and other crimes have no psychological burden on them. After committing the crime, they quickly flee the country.

There are too many similar examples.

They've even gone so far as to attack those being escorted. You can imagine how arrogant and terrifying they are.

After Chen Xu and Tang Guai were put into the police car,

Jiang Tian comforted Guo Na briefly, then placed her next to Song Qiuwan so she could stay by her side.

After all, the matter here wasn't over yet.

"Were all these people involved?" Jiang Tian asked, pointing to the seven or eight young men with dyed blond hair at the scene.

Just moments ago they were incredibly arrogant, or rather, as arrogant as could be at school.

But now, they were all obediently facing the wall.

They squatted on the ground, hands covering their heads, trembling.

"Hmm," Guo Na nodded.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​her eyes shone brightly.

At this moment, in Guo Na's eyes, Jiang Tian was like a god.

Unlike what Chen Liting had heard from others and in legends, in Guo Na's presence, Jiang Tian always seemed to appear riding on auspicious clouds.

Whenever she was in danger, Jiang Tian would appear in time to save her.

"Take them all away," Jiang Tian waved his hand.

Seven or eight young men with blond hair were escorted into the car.

Of course, these people would all be prosecuted as accomplices in kidnapping, and even though they were minors, they could not escape legal punishment.

And because of the heinous nature of the crime, the minimum sentence would be three years.

If the nature of the crime is egregious, or if other crimes are uncovered after interrogation, then the minimum sentence is ten years.

That means if they are minors, the sentence isn't just ten years; if they were adults, it would be at least execution.

"Boss, what should we do with these people?" Zhao Wei asked Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian thought for a moment and said, "Take these young men back for separate interrogation, especially focusing on getting them to confess to past crimes related to Chen Xu. According to the investigation, this isn't their first offense. They'll be easy to interrogate. First, solidify Chen Xu's charges, then submit the case to the prosecutor's office."

"I understand, boss," Zhao Wei nodded seriously.

These people's actions against Guo Na angered not only Jiang Tian.

Even those present at the scene were filled with rage upon seeing Guo Na.

Although, as law enforcement officers, they couldn't let their emotions influence them, they didn't mind teaching these unruly bastards a lesson within reasonable limits.

Especially Jiang Tian's men, many of whom were from the anti-drug unit, and all of them were longtime acquaintances of Captain Guo.

This made them even more empathetic.

As the special police officers inside the house gradually left,

the two lawyers' faces flushed red and then turned pale.

There's nothing else to it. As lawyers in the "red circle," when have they ever been ignored like this? No matter where they go, even if it were the city police chief, these "red circle" lawyers would treat them with utmost respect.

Not to mention, the "red circle" wields terrifying power.

This one person alone is someone no one else would dare offend.

But now, Jiang Tian has actually ignored them.

Regardless, even if a crime has been committed, since the "red circle" has intervened, they should at least show them some face, right? But you're not giving them any face at all?

You're even completely ignoring them.

"Very well, very well, Officer Jiang, is it? Young hero, we've certainly witnessed your power today. But as the saying goes, the mountains and rivers still flow, and we'll meet again." One of the lawyers approached Jiang Tian with a grim face and said darkly.

They've lost face big time today.

But regardless, having taken his money, they have to find a way to help him.

The case has reached this point.

The two lawyers were terrified by Lao Guai's crimes.

But Jiang Tian showed them absolutely no respect.

If word got out, how could they ever work in the industry again?

"Are you threatening me?" Jiang Tian asked expressionlessly.

You, a lawyer, dare to threaten me?

Jiang Tian practically laughed.

When you're out there, you flaunt your connections to others, showing off what kind of place you're in – Yunhai.

Here, right now, Yunhai doesn't care about connections, and Jiang Tian doesn't care about connections either.

In fact, in the next few days, Jiang Tian will be traveling to Beijing to meet with the city's bigwigs, awaiting their well wishes.

Of course, more than anything, Jiang Tian probably still cares about securing the August 1st award.

After all, unlike others, August 1st needs to be presented to the entire nation; it's like a business card. Jiang Tian's greatest asset is his youth, but also his greatest weakness.

Youth means instability.

Plus, being unmarried adds to his instability, which raises some concerns for the higher-ups. Therefore, they definitely need to meet with Jiang Tian before the August 1st award ceremony to determine if he can handle it.

After all, if he goes off the rails immediately after the award, who knows what embarrassing things he might do? In that case, it won't be Jiang Tian who loses face, but the country that bestowed the honor.

Putting all that aside, there are still Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan on Yunhai's side.

Jiang Tian was even less inclined to get entangled with these people.

"Bring on your skills."

"Young man, don't be so arrogant. My name is Sun Yu, and I'm a lawyer at Dongcheng Law Firm. You can handle your cases, but since we've taken this case, we won't just leave it alone. We'll be keeping a close eye on you. Or, if you're so capable, you can come find me. I think I'll be in Yunhai for the foreseeable future."

One of the lawyers, who had been speaking the whole time, took a business card from his pocket and placed it on the table next to him with a cold smile.

"Sorry, I don't have the habit of getting to know random people."

Jiang Tian always treated others with the utmost respect, and these two lawyers had been picking a fight ever since they came in.

They acted as if they were superior to him.

Jiang Tian simply couldn't understand these people's logic. Just because you're from Dongcheng Law Firm, does that mean we have to give you face?

"Fine, you're very good. We'll meet again soon. I hope you'll still be saying these things then." Sun Yu sneered and turned to leave.

Originally, he didn't want to get too involved, after all, criminal cases could easily land him in jail, but now Sun Yu had made up his mind.

He wanted to see what Jiang Tian's confidence was all about.

And the next day arrived quickly.

A piece of news exploded across the country instantly.



Chapter 583 The Weaknesses of Human Nature, Taking Things Out of Context


The Red Circle firm is undeniably capable, and these two lawyers aren't ordinary lawyers; they're even partners.

They're considered top-tier in the legal world. Ultimately, it's because money talks. Huayu Group lacks many things, but not money.

This law firm, if you have money, lots of money, they'll solve any problem for you, and if they can't solve it, they'll find a way.

Huayu Group has always invested heavily in its legal department, maintaining a large staff and having its own, given its deep pockets.

Realizing this matter was difficult to resolve, they immediately contacted the Red Circle, a top-tier firm with formidable background and influence. If it could be resolved, money wasn't an issue.

Prior to this, Chen Qiang had already anticipated unforeseen circumstances.

Therefore, initially, driven by the promise of huge profits, they spent a fortune—20 million a year—to apply for legal aid from the Dongcheng Law Firm.

After all, Huayu Group has nothing but money.

A bombshell piece of news suddenly exploded the very next day, seemingly going viral overnight.

The most prominent story was:

The second-generation captain of the Yunhai anti-drug squad, surnamed Jiang, disrespected human rights.

He assaulted a criminal suspect.

In this era, this headline was inherently controversial, concerning interrogations and other aspects. Criminal suspects didn't have an easy time, even though they all deserved their punishment.

However, respect for human rights has always been a focal point of society.

Even twenty years later, such incidents still occur. Aside from highly developed first-tier cities, such behavior remains rampant in some remote areas.

At this moment.

For example, Xiao Zhang was watching TV at home, his family eating lunch.

The television station suddenly interrupted with a news report.

It was that a hidden camera had been secretly filming the scene, and the two lawyers who had been following along had been secretly filming.

Therefore, the scene was shown completely and without reservation.

In the news report, the host raised a very serious question: "Should the human rights of criminal suspects be valued and respected?"

This very first sentence immediately attracted a lot of attention.

Following this was a video of the scene with blurred faces.

"Recently, regarding the case of the second-generation anti-drug police officer surnamed Jiang in Yunhai, who physically harmed a minor while he was playing, this is a very serious social issue. A young person under the age of 18 may have broken the law while playing, but whether physical harm is permissible is a very serious social problem."

It exploded.

It was extremely explosive.

Because Chen Xu's convulsing on the ground was very, very pitiful.

This also attracted people's sympathy.

It's well known that people always sympathize with the weak.

Similarly, given this situation, and the fact that the law generally provides preferential treatment to minors, this led to social attention and controversy.

However, aside from a small minority, the vast majority of people sympathized with the underdog, and Chen Xu was the underdog in this situation.

Although short videos weren't wildly popular at the time, people still paid an alarming level of attention to trending news stories.

Anything that made it onto television,

would inevitably have a huge impact, especially on such controversial issues.

Unfortunately,

most people were simply following the crowd.

This was especially true online, where the discussion exploded.

Only a small minority remained clear-headed, because the news report made it sound easy that the suspect had broken the law while playing around, but it didn't specify exactly what he had done.

These clear-headed individuals insisted that he had absolutely violated the bottom line of the law.

Moreover, crimes committed by young people, especially children, have historically received lenient treatment.

This has led to many people exploiting legal loopholes, especially organized crime groups. To evade the law, they recruit large numbers of young people, typically fifteen or sixteen, or even fourteen or fifteen, as thugs.

These young people are immature and prone to acting impulsively.

In an emergency, they might act rashly, even resorting to violence without fear.

The key is that they enjoy legal protection, making them reckless. Their immaturity means they act without considering the consequences, attacking without hesitation.

They are driven, and crucially, they enjoy preferential treatment.

This has resulted in a widespread phenomenon, attracting attention from some.

However, people have a herd mentality and sympathize with the weak. Behind the scenes, some are intentionally covering up the perpetrator's actions, and reports are vague, leading to Chen Xu being portrayed as the victim.

And since Chen Xu is the victim, Jiang Tian is naturally the villain.

"It's outrageous! How could such a thing happen? This police officer surnamed Jiang is even a second-generation rich kid!"

"He relies on connections and even goes so far as to do something like this. Does he have no humanity?"

"That's right, he's utterly inhuman. This person is using his privileges to relentlessly harass others."

"Yes, it's so pitiful. Doesn't this person have even a shred of humanity?"

The Zhang family watched with furious glares.

Especially Xiao Zhang, who seemed unable to contain his anger.

He immediately put down his chopsticks and rushed online to find related posts. Unexpectedly, the posts about this news had completely exploded.

After all, the numerous controversial issues involved caused the online buzz to spread like wildfire.

It simply exploded.

Xiao Zhang thought for a moment, and quickly edited a long post, detailing his views: "First of all, everyone has human rights, even criminal suspects should have their human rights respected. This is especially true for a sixteen or seventeen-year-old boy who was beaten like this. This clearly shows how bad the situation in Yunhai is. Real criminal suspects are rarely apprehended, yet they've committed such acts of abuse. We have to consider whether the lack of strict supervision is ultimately good or bad for them.
