I believe it's more harmful than beneficial. Excessive power and arbitrary actions will lead to unimaginable consequences in the long run."

Then, Xiao Zhang elaborated on his views from this perspective, offering deeper analyses of society and the underlying problems.

He also discussed the future, wondering if things would completely spiral out of control.

This might just be the beginning. Will things get worse? Will it happen to you? You can remain silent until it happens to you or your child.

What should you do then?

And then it all exploded.



Chapter 584 Chief Gao was shocked; another first-class merit?


Especially the criticism directed at this so-called Jiang family head was intense.

This news was explosive.

Especially at this critical juncture, Jiang Tian was about to be exposed as a scandalous figure.

And now this has happened.

It has a terrifying and significant impact on the entire situation, and the entire overall plan. If not handled properly, it could be devastating to the entire plan.

It could be said that it had to happen now of all times.

"Heh, they don't know what's good for them."

"Does he really think Dongcheng Law Firm is just sitting around doing nothing?"

Sun Yu sneered. Jiang Tian wasn't giving him any face.

If only both sides had compromised and offered each other a way out, this situation wouldn't have happened. After all, their law firm also cares about its reputation. Otherwise, if the news got out, people would think that Dongcheng's background and influence had weakened.

"Yeah, a direct confrontation. I really don't know if he's just too arrogant, or if he really thinks he has the power to challenge our Dongcheng Law Firm." Another lawyer shook his head and sneered.

That's right.

The entire news event was orchestrated by them, through their law firm.

With this kind of news being exposed,

it's safe to say that Jiang Tian's future is completely ruined.

Such a huge stain has been reported.

"But, with things getting this big, won't something unexpected happen?" Another lawyer's face immediately showed some worry.

After all, the intensity of the incident had somewhat exceeded their control.

It had completely spiraled out of control.

And it was a national issue.

Of course, on the other hand, it was also because the Dongcheng Law Firm wasn't to be underestimated; they had hired a very influential local television station.

Their original intention was to create a moderate impact, completely destroying Jiang Tian.

They were keeping their work a secret.

The key was that everything was under their control, but then, something went wrong, and it exploded instantly, completely.

"Won't something bad happen?" The other lawyer was somewhat cautious, even a little uneasy.

"In the end, it's just a small drug enforcement case, what's the big deal?" Sun Yu shook his head, his face full of sarcasm.

Not only were they fine now, but even if something did happen, they wouldn't panic at all.

Jiang Tian, ​​after all, was just a captain.

A captain was nothing compared to them.

"So don't worry, I'll handle anything," Sun Yu added.

The other lawyer seemed to be affected, his tense emotions gradually relaxing.

Actually, they didn't know.

The impact of this incident, once it broke online, was too late even to suppress the news; it instantly became a social hot topic.

After all, it was extremely controversial.

Although most people sided with Chen Xu, condemning Jiang Tian in various ways.

But this wasn't the end.

Even on the afternoon the incident broke, while Jiang Tian was still at the Municipal Public Security Bureau,

instantly,

a large number of personnel flooded into the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

All demanding severe punishment for Jiang Tian.

Inside the entire municipal bureau, it was so quiet you could hear a pin drop.

Especially from Minister Tan's temporary office at the Municipal Bureau, from this angle, one could even see a large crowd gathered at the entrance.

It's out of control.

Completely out of control.

Even with an immediate lockdown, it was, after all, a major local news program.

Although it wasn't a broadcasting station, the news broadcast instantly caused a terrifying explosion of attention.

"Wait, you just said it involves the Huayu Group, which amassed a billion yuan in ten days?"

Before Minister Tan could speak, Gao Xiaolong, standing to the side, couldn't sit still any longer.

He stared at Jiang Tian, ​​dumbfounded, feeling a chill run down his spine.

He knew the rewards for the drug village hadn't been issued yet.

After all, August 1st usually has one or two batches every year.

The process requires multiple layers of screening to check for any past irregularities or illegal activities.

This has led to a proliferation of August 1st awards ceremonies.

The resulting impact is excessive.

Even the awards ceremony itself is broadcast live, serving as a significant form of praise and encouragement.

It's fair to say that every year on August 1st, someone is watching.

Moreover, many people are paying attention, and some even explain the main reasons for winning the August 1st Army Day award, aiming to set an example for others.

Many people also make comments and evaluations every year.

Do they even qualify?

Regardless, the annual awarding of these prizes always sparks huge controversy.

Some believe they are more than qualified, while others think certain individuals are completely unqualified.

Of course, the level of attention is always explosive every year.

And this year's list will be announced in a few days.

Jiang Tian has now successfully made the list. Firstly, it's based on his contributions; secondly, it's to see if he's reliable. Otherwise, he might be rewarded and praised, and then his reputation would collapse shortly after.

Then, it might not just be him who loses face.

August 1st is already extremely important.

Now, Jiang Tian's inclusion in the August 1st list is practically a done deal. At this point, unless he commits some unforgivable crime, his fate is sealed.

The announcement of the list is scheduled for a few days later, and the awards ceremony is still more than a month away.

Jiang Tian's inclusion in August 1st was already a certainty.

However, the crackdown on dark web forces in Windmill Country is still ongoing.

After all, there are over seventy powerful and terrifying drug lords from various countries around the world.

Once exposed, it will undoubtedly cause a global sensation.

Jiang Tian had just cracked and arrested the person in charge of this case, and the August 1st Incident had just concluded.

Watching closely...

"One hundred million a day, almost a billion in ten days!"

Gao Xiaolong's heart raced.

This terrifying rate of wealth accumulation... assassins...

Even Gao Xiaolong found it hard to believe.

Similarly, it explained why so many people were willing to take the risk and turn to crime.

This terrifying speed of wealth accumulation was something even well-known large domestic enterprises couldn't achieve.

"It's insane."

"The bigger the storm, the more expensive the fish."

Gao Xiaolong was shaken.

This case was terrifyingly serious.

Moreover, the scent of a first-class merit award was very strong.

If this was indeed the case and the information was accurate, then Jiang Tian's first-class merit award was practically guaranteed. Thinking of this, Gao Xiaolong's head buzzed, and he almost fainted.

How many first-class merit awards has Jiang Tian received? Did he buy them wholesale?

"Not many, not many, maybe eight more, not that many," Jiang Tian said modestly.

Hiss!!!



Chapter 585 Guo, a first-class meritorious soldier of the sacrificed brigade, was punished to the fullest extent of the law.


Just as Gao Xiaolong was lost in thought, he saw a large, dense crowd suddenly appear outside the door. The news reports clearly identified them as a narcotics officer surnamed Jiang from Yunhai, though they hadn't explicitly stated he was from the municipal bureau.

But did it matter?

As long as it was Yunhai, not the provincial level, even if it was from a branch bureau, it was still under their jurisdiction.

This instantly sparked a huge online discussion, especially among the enthusiastic local residents of Yunhai, who spontaneously gathered outside the municipal bureau.

They wanted a solution and to take down this bad apple.

So, while Gao Xiaolong was still reflecting on the situation, he could see the scene outside from his office.

He was still contemplating the terrifying impact of the entire case and the Huayu Group.

He was completely taken aback by the sheer number of people at the door.

On the other hand…

Besides lamenting the Huayu Group's rampant corruption, Gao Xiaolong also pondered the overall anti-drug plan for Yunhai.

He decided that changes were absolutely necessary, especially in the control of heroin, which needed to be further strengthened.

Following this, he learned about everything that had happened yesterday.

He was shocked.

Gao Xiaolong was extremely shocked, and even more so, extremely angry.

Lawless.

That's how it is.

Strict supervision is necessary in many areas because it can easily lead to many tragedies.

That's exactly what happened now.

A tragedy almost occurred, and Gao Xiaolong found it hard to imagine what would have happened or what the consequences would have been if Jiang Tian hadn't reacted in time. But judging from past history,

99% of the time, that little girl would have met a tragic end.

Beast.

"He deserves to be torn to pieces," Gao Xiaolong roared.

Jiang Tian wholeheartedly agreed. If it weren't for the lack of such a punishment in the law, he would definitely suggest that such scum deserve to be torn to pieces.

"You did a very good job, extremely well. I still remember that little girl. A lively and pretty young girl, almost met with such a terrible fate, especially after our comrade's sacrifice. If her family doesn't receive a satisfactory resolution, who will be willing to make sacrifices in the future?"

Gao Xiaolong's expression also turned serious.

You see, secrets never stay hidden forever. If things really escalate and spread, and in the end, Guo, the captain, has just sacrificed his life, leaving behind his only child who suffers a terrible fate.

Wouldn't everyone be living in fear then?

Moreover, since they dared to lay a hand on this little girl, they definitely deserve the most severe punishment.

"Treat this to the fullest extent of the law, and publicize it nationwide. This is to tell everyone that some people are not to be trifled with. No matter how powerful they are, when they encounter certain people, there is only one consequence: death."

Gao Xiaolong roared in fury.

Treat this to the fullest extent of the law.

What does "treat this to the fullest extent of the law" mean?

It's safe to say that just the sheer number of crimes Chen Xu has committed, if uncovered, any one or two would be enough to warrant execution. In other words, Chen Xu's future is sealed: a lifetime ban from political power.

Future?

There is no future.

These words, spoken by Gao Xiaolong, definitively sealed the deal, and there will be no change.

"Oh, right, there's one more thing," Jiang Tian suddenly said.

"Something else?" Gao Xiaolong was slightly taken aback, nodding to signal Jiang Tian to continue.

"This Huayu Group is very wealthy, so they brought in a powerful and influential figure in the Red Circle."

Jiang Tian recounted the events from beginning to end, explaining the cause and effect almost perfectly, without any misrepresentation.

He was simply stating the facts, but even this fact caused Gao Xiaolong's expression to change drastically.

"They dare."

Before Gao Xiaolong could speak, a voice suddenly rang out from behind Jiang Tian.

Immediately afterward, Minister Tan emerged from behind Jiang Tian. It turned out he had already arrived but remained silent, listening to their conversation.

The events leading up to this had already transformed Minister Tan's initial surprise into rage.

You see, the Windmill Kingdom case only erupted after the Poison Village incident.

And within this...

one person deserved a significant share of the credit—yes, that person was Captain Guo.

Good heavens.

Because of Captain Guo's sacrifice—Captain Guo's contributions were already substantial, worthy of high praise—and his death, a sacrifice of a significantly higher rank, further solidified this recognition.

This was also a common practice.

Furthermore, Captain Guo was not treated unfairly by his superiors.

"This time, a collective award of merit was presented, especially to Captain Guo. For his role in this incident, he was selected as a First-Class Hero. He's already received his First-Class Hero medal. And yet, someone dares to bully the child of a First-Class Hero? It's utterly audacious!"

Minister Tan was practically bursting with rage.

Then, Jiang Tian abruptly changed the subject, mentioning the two lawyers circled in red. Minister Tan completely lost his temper and stepped out from behind Jiang Tian.

Or rather, Jiang Tian had already noticed Minister Tan's approach, but after recognizing who it was, he had no more reservations.

After all,

the top two police officers were present.

If there were any problems that they couldn't handle, then Jiang Tian would have no choice but to go to Beijing.

That's why Jiang Tian actually had the contact information of a big shot in Beijing.

This person.

It was Zhao Cainan who gave it to Jiang Tian, ​​and after Jiang Tian made a call, his assistant answered, but the person who actually answered the phone quickly and praised Jiang Tian highly.

He also praised Jiang Tian extensively.

He also emphasized and affirmed Jiang Tian's past achievements, etc.

They also offered their perspectives on Jiang Tian's future.

Of course, the most important thing was that they arranged for Jiang Tian to travel to the capital in a few days to meet him in person. It wasn't just this powerful figure who seemed to be Zhao Cainan's elder; another powerful figure also wanted to meet Jiang Tian.

If things really couldn't be resolved, Jiang Tian's trip to the capital was no joke.

Of course, if they did that, who would suffer? It would be these two top leaders. If they can't even protect their own trusted subordinates, who would believe they can get things done?

Therefore,

Jiang Tian would absolutely never do that unless it was the last resort.

The impact would be far too severe.

Minister Tan's fury stemmed from this as well.

Clearly, Minister Tan was aware of the power of the Red Circle.

"The Dongcheng Law Firm, I heard they're trying to ruin me. Just because I didn't give them face, they made sure I couldn't even work as a policeman. I've been so scared these past two days I haven't been able to eat or sleep. It's terrifying," Jiang Tian said with an exaggerated smile.

But when he mentioned Dongcheng, a dazzling cold light flashed in Jiang Tian's eyes.

It sent a chill down one's spine.

In this world, everything ultimately comes down to reason.



Chapter 586 Down with the Jiang family, the black sheep? Are you crazy?


Jiang Tian would find it unacceptable to witness such behavior on others, but to be bullied like this himself—his fiery temper couldn't possibly tolerate it.

"Such prolonged leniency has emboldened them to become increasingly audacious. What is Dongcheng trying to do? Protect criminals? Are they trying to oppose justice? For money, they'll do anything. Threatening and intimidating key investigators is utterly depraved. You can arrest them for obstructing official business. With us here, no one dares to do anything to you," Minister Tan sneered.

Minister Tan was well aware of the power of the red circle.

However...

For someone in Minister Tan's position, what kind of power couldn't be measured? He didn't really care much.

Because no matter how powerful someone was, they had to be obedient in front of Minister Tan.

Because none of those people had a higher background or connections than Minister Tan's position.

Of course, this shouldn't be underestimated.

After all, those people, like spiders weaving a web, had a considerable impact.

"Of course, Xiao Jiang, you don't need to worry. Dongcheng Law Firm might have some influence, but given the current situation in Yunhai, who dares to interfere? Those people won't dare do anything, especially with us here. They certainly won't do anything."

Minister Tan reassured Jiang Tian easily, showing his confidence in him.

After all, his presence here wasn't a secret; many people knew, and those people were smart enough to know what they could and couldn't do.

However...

"Recently, our supervision of them has indeed been a bit lax, emboldening them. If they show up again, don't waste time with words, just arrest them and teach them a lesson. Don't worry about trouble; I'll cover for you no matter what." Minister Tan's face still burned with anger.

Although he was certain they wouldn't dare do anything,

the threat to Jiang Tian was real.

Minister Tan decided that once he returned, he would definitely convene a meeting with all the partners from the law firms in Beijing and give them a warning.

They needed to teach those two a heavy lesson. Threatening Jiang Tian was tantamount to courting death.

Didn't they know that Jiang Tian had become an extremely crucial person?

"Is that so?" Jiang Tian's expression was somewhat strange.

"What's wrong? What do you think? Or perhaps you have some misunderstanding about us? Do you think those people would dare to cause any trouble in Yunhai? Don't worry, unless they're insane." Minister Tan shook his head.

Anyone who wasn't insane knew that messing with Jiang Tian was equivalent to crossing a very serious line.

Even with the strongest backing, those people would be wiped out.

What? You say it's that serious?

What a joke. A quick search of their case evidence would be enough to make them regret it. As the saying goes, no one in this world has a completely clean record. Those who rise to power have something to hide.

And for those people...

It would be incredibly easy to cause them trouble.

"So, Xiao Jiang, you have to trust us. Chief Gao and I are both backing you up in Yunhai. Your little Dongcheng law firm doesn't need to pretend anymore." Minister Tan saw right through Jiang Tian.

Minister Tan even smiled.

Because Jiang Tian's feigned surprise was quite convincing.

This little rascal.

He's deliberately making a scene here.

The Red Circle, while possessing some influence, is in a situation in Yunhai where no one dares to get involved. It's safe to say that the waters of Yunhai are already unfathomably deep.

Anyone who accidentally falls in will face certain death.

Anyone who dares to touch Jiang Tian is tantamount to disrupting a major strategic direction.

It's practically making an enemy of everyone.

Those who can touch Jiang Tian, ​​besides criminals, are some small forces that don't understand his importance.

Those who do know Jiang Tian's importance simply don't dare to do anything.

The Red Circle clearly knows Jiang Tian's importance, but clearly, those two lawyers don't.

"Little Jiang, stop pretending. We know you too well. It's pointless to keep up the act. You couldn't beat hundreds of armed men, so why would you be afraid of a small law firm in Dongcheng?" Minister Tan chuckled.

That's right.

It's just this kind of insignificant force.

At least, compared to what Jiang Tian had done before, Minister Tan and Chief Gao were very skeptical. Could Jiang Tian even take this seriously?

"No, Minister Tan, look out the window." Jiang Tian pointed to the floor-to-ceiling window.

A scene unfolded before their eyes.

"Fairness, impartiality, and transparency!!!"

Six large characters were displayed before everyone.

The font was extremely large.

This was at the very front; behind it, there were smaller prints, but this was only relative. In reality, in the city bureau's office, anyone with normal eyesight could easily see them.

"Severely punish Officer Jiang and give the people justice!"

"Down with the black sheep!"

"We can't let this cancer exist."

"A black sheep, a cancer!"

"Down with the black sheep!"

The handwriting was a bit blurry, but the roaring shouts rang out.

That's it.

Actually, people have an inherent tendency to stand on a moral high ground and point fingers, especially when the weak are being bullied.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian presented himself to everyone as a strongman, and a strongman who bullies the weak, which naturally made many people side with Chen Xu.

At first, Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan didn't quite understand what was happening below, and the handwriting was blurry and hard to read.

Until Jiang Tian went to the window and opened it.

A whooshing sound suddenly came in from outside the soundproofed window.

"Down with the Jiang family, those black sheep!"

"Down with the black sheep!"

"Get the black sheep out of the ranks!"

The voices roared out in unison.

This is unbelievable.

But...

Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan were completely dumbfounded, their heads practically exploding with a buzzing sound.

No.

We just meant...

"How come I didn't know there was another black sheep surnamed Jiang in our municipal bureau?" Minister Tan asked subconsciously.



Chapter 587 Wrath, You're Courting Death


Wait a minute.

No.

Minister Tan had barely finished speaking when he abruptly stood up.

The surname Jiang.

There are several people with the surname Jiang in the entire Municipal Public Security Bureau. While Jiang isn't a particularly prominent surname like Li or Wang, it's not uncommon.

In short, Minister Tan didn't initially think of Jiang Tian. This seemed absurd.

Who is Jiang Tian? If Jiang Tian is a black sheep, then what normal people are left in this world?

But then Minister Tan realized something.

Because in the entire Municipal Public Security Bureau,

as far as Minister Tan could remember, there was only one person: Jiang Tian.

"Down with the anti-drug officer surnamed Jiang!"

"Down with this black sheep!"

"Get this black sheep out of our ranks!"

The shouts echoed continuously from outside the window.

This gave Minister Tan a sudden shock. At first, he thought there really was some black sheep who had incited public outrage.

But then, a voice from below sounded, announcing:

"The one surnamed Jiang."

While there might be a few people with the surname Jiang within the entire municipal bureau, when the circle narrowed down to the anti-drug team, Minister Tan was certain. He had reviewed the anti-drug squad's roster more than once, and in the entire Yunhai anti-drug squad, there was only one Jiang Tian.

On the other hand.

Besides, the sudden outburst of voice from the other party truly enraged Minister Tan. Initially, his anger stemmed from the belief that someone had committed some heinous act, but then, as the target narrowed down to Jiang Tian, ​​Minister Tan's face instantly darkened.

Because it was obvious.

As Jiang Tian gained momentum, his actions became increasingly egregious.

As he offends more and more people, it's inevitable that some will use all sorts of methods to deal with him. However, for these people, for this group of people...

Minister Tan absolutely cannot tolerate this.

Especially at this stage, just when Jiang Tian was about to become a prominent figure, this kind of thing happened. It was practically a major strategic blunder.

"These people are here to see you?" Gao Xiaolong looked at Jiang Tian with a completely astonished expression.

Actually, Jiang Tian was scratching his head at this moment.

Regardless, even if Jiang Tian were a god, he couldn't have foreseen this from nowhere. The main reason for this sudden outbreak was its sheer speed.

The speed was too fast.

If it weren't for dedicated monitoring from the beginning, and the initial emergence of the issue not being prevented, then it wouldn't have been a problem.

However, once certain problems arose,

or rather, once there was even a slight oversight,

or a lack of timely attention, but since no one initially anticipated that someone would generate public opinion from this angle, it wasn't controlled in time. By the time a reaction was made, the public outcry had already completely erupted.

This also led to

the fact that from its emergence to its explosion, only a morning had passed, yet it had already exceeded everyone's expectations.

Or rather,

the two Dongcheng lawyers who orchestrated this, perhaps they initially spearheaded it, but the subsequent developments were something they simply couldn't comprehend.

The water in the sea of ​​clouds was beyond their comprehension.

It could be said that Jiang Tian had offended many people, who were lurking in the shadows, waiting for an opportunity to retaliate. And now, that opportunity had presented itself.

Good heavens, Jiang Tian's flaws had finally surfaced, and the masterminds behind the scenes had completely exploded.

They instantly spread public opinion.

Someone was fanning the flames.

There were also controversial social issues.

This led to the outbreak of this incident, exceeding everyone's expectations and imagination.

At this moment, the three people in the office—Gao Xiaolong, Minister Tan, and Jiang Tian—were all exchanging bewildered glances. Jiang Tian himself was completely dumbfounded.

Me, the black sheep?

Jiang Tian almost couldn't help but sneer.

Or rather, regardless of who was behind it all, from this perspective, Jiang Tian could only laugh.

Because Jiang Tian had nothing to worry about.

In fact, Jiang Tian vaguely guessed something.

"Probably. In the entire anti-drug squad, if I'm not mistaken, I'm the only one with the surname Jiang. There's no one else. And some people are saying he's the captain or something, so in the entire Yunnan province, it's probably only me." Jiang Tian shrugged.

At first, he didn't understand, but then, Jiang Tian quickly scanned the situation in his mind.

It was obvious that someone was stirring up trouble online.

And so many people had suddenly gathered.

Without a doubt.

It was definitely targeting him; his past actions had been taken out of context and exposed.

Besides that, Jiang Tian really didn't know anything else.

Thinking about what could cause such a stir, and considering the threats from Sun Yu's two lawyers yesterday, Jiang Tian finally understood.

At this point, even a fool could figure it out.

Boom!!!

Before Jiang Tian could say anything, a deafening roar suddenly exploded.

Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table, his face contorted with rage: "Who did this?"

Yes.

Who did it?

Who was behind this organized and premeditated act?

Most importantly, the impact of this incident is now beginning to affect Jiang Tian.

And now, at this crucial juncture.

This incident is extremely important, because it's not just about building a reputation for Yunhai, but even about establishing Jiang Tian as an international authority in criminal investigation.

After all,

From any perspective, or in terms of the achievements Jiang Tian has gained, it's more than enough. And to put it bluntly, in the future, leveraging the FishBuy website and following the trail to target those seventy-odd drug lords worldwide, Jiang Tian could be the one in charge.

This would further solidify Jiang Tian's authority as an expert.

And as Jiang Tian becomes an authority on a global scale, the nation will naturally become an authority as well.

This grand strategy has multifaceted implications for international standing and the future direction of the nation.

And now, at this moment...

Someone actually dared to step forward and target Jiang Tian in public opinion?

How could Minister Tan not be furious?

You see, Jiang Tian took off as planned; their entire police system benefited from his success.

He would instantly become a global authority on drug control.

The result.

At this time, you dare to cause trouble.

Moreover.

And someone even dares to insult Jiang Tian?

Meanwhile.

"Chief, Chief, something terrible has happened!"



Chapter 588 An opportunity arises, is it a chance to fish in troubled waters?


In the bureau chief's office, Han Xian, his heart heavy and face pale, stared listlessly at the ceiling. The reason was simple: because of the previous incident, in the eyes of his two top and second-in-commands, Han Xian had become synonymous with a clown.

This was nothing short of a bolt from the blue for Han Xian, who had dedicated his life to politics, and now he was following in his nephew Han Qi's footsteps.

He was filled with melancholy.

Just then, someone suddenly pushed open the door and rushed in.

She's Han Xian's secretary.

Of course, to be precise, Han Xian's current position is only that of an assistant; a secretary doesn't yet have that qualification or ability.

This assistant is one of Han Xian's closest confidants, whom he brought from Beijing.

She's also a trusted confidant who has always followed Han Xian, loyally handling countless matters for him.

But now...

The assistant's face lit up with surprise.

"What is it?" Han Xian asked, frowning.

The assistant's demeanor displeased Han Xian. This assistant had never acted so frivolously before; otherwise, he wouldn't have been grooming him as a close confidant.

"Director, good news! Good news!"

The assistant's face was full of joy.

Because Song Liguo had become the deputy director, and given his considerable influence within the city bureau, his promotion was a natural progression. No one would deliberately sabotage him. Furthermore, many admired Song Liguo, and compared to Han Xian, more people preferred to be close to him. Therefore, most of the time, they reported to Song Liguo, and gradually, Han Xian's position became more like a display piece.

Especially...

Not long ago, Han Xian was treated like a clown, criticized and talked to by the top two leaders in turn.

This almost instantly turned Han Xian into a clown.

Furthermore, there are no secrets within the system. Soon, these rumors spread from who-knows-where, and some versions, due to alterations and the addition of personal touches, became far more outrageous than the truth.

But regardless of the version, Han Xian appeared even more like a clown than the original, and crucially, the story was presented convincingly and with high credibility.

This made even more people unwilling to get close to him.

Moreover, there were rumors of a conflict between Han Xian and Jiang Tian.

This led many people to prefer going directly to Song Liguo rather than approaching Han Xian. Even those who initially came to report to Han Xian almost stopped after this incident.

Now, to put it bluntly, Han Xian is almost a lone commander.

But all of this is ultimately due to Jiang Tian.

Everything else is secondary, because many people say there's a massive, even terrifying, conflict between Han Xian and Jiang Tian.

Both have significant internal conflicts. Within the Municipal Public Security Bureau, Jiang Tian has long since become an authority figure.

Now, this authority has been challenged.

This is equivalent to Han Xian arriving with resentment towards Jiang Tian, ​​which has provoked widespread discontent.

This is something Han Xian didn't understand from the beginning.

By the time Han Xian realized it, it was completely too late.

In other words, ultimately, the root cause of everything lies with Jiang Tian.

Having figured everything out, Han Xian couldn't help but feel hatred towards Jiang Tian. Whether it was his nephew Han Qi, who was already in jail, or himself, their political careers were completely ruined by Jiang Tian alone.

"Good news, good news, Director, please listen to me."

The assistant quickly recounted what had happened outside.

After hearing the details,

Han Xian's eyes lit up.

"What did you say? You mean Jiang Tian used underhanded methods, got caught, and even made it into the news, causing a media frenzy online?"

Han Xian's breathing quickened, and he abruptly stood up.

The cause of all this was Jiang Tian. In Han Xian's view, as long as Jiang Tian was in trouble,

it would all be over.

Jiang Tian would be finished.

"Minister Tan and Chief Gao will eventually understand. A young man with no experience is unreliable. How could a young man be dependable? Heh." Han Xian sneered.

That's right.

Jiang Tian is too young.

After hearing his assistant's account of the events, and then searching online, Han Xian had only one thought:

Jiang Tian is finished.

Completely finished.

This incident has occurred and attracted widespread public attention.

Regardless of who it is or what they did, it takes two to tango. Does Jiang Tian himself bear no responsibility?

Disrespect for human rights.

That's enough.

And the most important problem is...

His future prospects for promotion—no, forget about promotion.

Working within the system, encountering something like this, even a low-level team leader, let alone a provincial leader, what will the outcome be?

Only one outcome: either being dismissed or retiring.

It's always been like this.

Everything exposed is like this.

And a lowly team leader, even if he wanted to find someone to take his place, the crucial point is that the news even mentioned his surname.

If Jiang Tian manages to escape this ordeal, then Han Xian will be walking backwards.

Because Han Xian has never seen anyone remain unscathed amidst this kind of online public opinion.

Especially.

Han Xian looked up and saw a dense crowd outside.

At this moment, Han Xian felt his opportunity had arrived.

"Chief, now is the best opportunity. The news has only exposed the surname Jiang, but no one knows Jiang Tian yet. If you, Chief, accidentally let slip his name, Jiang Tian will absolutely have no chance left."

Dead.

Dead for sure.

At least his political career is definitely over.

Han Xian's eyes lit up, a thought forming in his mind.

Sure enough.

After understanding the situation, Han Xian's cold smile deepened.

Because after understanding the situation, Han Xian's initial 50-60% certainty had now increased to 70-80%.

Even 70-80% was a conservative estimate; now it was practically a sure thing.

Because no matter what, Jiang Tian hitting someone is wrong and disrespectful of human rights.

Unless you are preventing a crime, otherwise you are disrespecting human rights.

It was obvious that the situation at the scene couldn't have prevented the crime; in other words, Jiang Tian was utterly doomed, without a doubt.

A pleasant surprise, a huge surprise.

With Jiang Tian dead, Han Xian was proven right, absolutely right.

Therefore,

Jiang Tian was the one who was wrong.

What he said before was absolutely right, no mistake whatsoever. All of you high-ranking officials have wronged me.

I have absolutely no problems.

In the future...

Won't I still be given an important position?

The more Han Xian thought about it, the more excited he became. He pushed open the door and went out.

Facing the excited crowd outside... he took a deep breath and began...



Chapter 589 With this back-and-forth singing, have I become a cancer?


First, at this point, Han Xian understood that he absolutely had to reveal Jiang Tian's name in a plausible but difficult way.

Second, he needed to ensure Jiang Tian's identity was exposed. This secrecy might allow Jiang Tian to escape punishment if his superiors intervened.

But once Jiang Tian's name was completely revealed,

all of that would be impossible.

Everything would be certain.

"Here he comes! This person is wearing white; he must be a high-ranking official."

"Sir, is there a narcotics officer surnamed Jiang in your city bureau who, during law enforcement, improperly used violence to assault a suspect—let's call him a suspect for now—because you didn't confirm the extent of the crime at the scene."

"That's right, we heard. It was just a small conflict, a little joke between classmates, but you've blown it out of proportion. It was just a joke between underage classmates. Are you trying to meet your performance targets, so you're frantically hiring people to boost them?"

"That's right, that must be it. Not only that, but you people... crime in Yunhai is already lawless. There are so many criminals, and you can't even arrest them, yet you're making a big deal out of a student. How can you be so shameless? Please give us, the public, an explanation."

"That's right, give us an explanation."

"Strictly punish that Officer Jiang."

"We absolutely will not allow such a black sheep to continue to be the real culprits harming us."

Chaos ensued.

When Han Xian appeared, being a bureau chief and wearing a genuine white police uniform, he stood out among the ordinary officers, attracting attention and prompting a frenzy of swarms.

Everyone knew that Han Xian's presence indicated he was the highest-ranking official present, further enraging the already indignant crowd.

The escalating tensions resulted in nearly a hundred people being present.

It's impossible that such a rapid commotion could have occurred without some powerful force secretly manipulating the situation.

Of course, Han Xian couldn't care less about those things now. He had only one thought: to eliminate Jiang Tian and prove himself right.

Then the leaders would surely compensate him, preventing him from being labeled as jealous and envious. They might even praise his foresight.

Han Xian, at this moment, was struggling to find the right words when he saw the excited crowd shouting and shoving as they surged forward. Suppressing his own excitement, Han Xian solemnly and sternly commanded, "Maintain order! Everyone, what are you standing there for? Maintain order now! What do you want? To rebel?"

Han Xian's stern shout instantly silenced the crowd.

Seeing this, Han Xian's heart skipped a beat. What if these people were suddenly frightened?

Han Xian was still pondering how to get rid of Jiang Tian and how to make amends when his assistant, seemingly sensing his predicament, quickly jumped in to play the good cop: "Of course, everyone, don't be afraid. A country has its laws, and a family has its rules. As public servants, our job is to serve the people. We will fully meet everyone's demands. Please ask questions and discuss things calmly. And please trust us; the Yunhai City Police Bureau will absolutely not protect any bad apples. Any corrupt elements will be strictly purged from the police force."

As the assistant's soothing words rang out, the previously silent, stunned, and awkward atmosphere gradually returned to its usual lively state.

One of the men suddenly broke free of his restraints and ran up to Han Xian: "Sir, may I ask if the anti-drug officer surnamed Jiang is from your city bureau? You just said you would never cover up anything."

At this moment, two officers rushed forward to stop him, but Han Xian quickly stopped the other two who had the courage, reprimanding them: "You can't ignore the people's demands. You two step back. I will answer any questions you have."

At this moment, Han Xian's excitement grew, and he was already planning to outwardly appear reluctant, but inwardly reveal all the information about Jiang Tian as if it were unavoidable.

Only in this way would Han Xian leave a perfect impression on his superiors.

"Young man, I can tell you, our city bureau does indeed have a young anti-drug captain, and his surname is Jiang, but he has made many contributions and is definitely not the black sheep you claim he is."

Han Xian spoke righteously.

Boom!!!

The previously uncertain crowd erupted instantly.

It was confirmed.

The young man who rushed forward angrily demanded, "Does he have a high-ranking official as his father? Is he a second-generation rich kid?"

Han Xian feigned reluctance, but inwardly he was overjoyed.

After all, these kinds of things rely on a leader, and this person was clearly one.

Excellent.

Such a leader is excellent.

Han Xian was ecstatic. He swore he hadn't arranged this person's actions, but they had perfectly aligned with his desires. Otherwise, he wouldn't know how to reveal Jiang Tian's eagerness to expose him.

Even though he was almost bursting with elation, Han Xian remained resolute on the surface, saying, "He does indeed have a high-ranking official as his father, but I can assure you all, in my name as Director Yunhai, that this Officer Jiang is absolutely not the black sheep you're portraying him as."

Han Xian admitted it while simultaneously denying that Jiang Tian was a black sheep.

On the surface, they seemed to be protecting Jiang Tian, ​​but in reality, their intentions were despicable; they had confirmed all the uncertain information for everyone.

The atmosphere at the scene exploded again, almost spiraling out of control.

"Kill the black sheep."

"It's him, that's right, it's him."

"That's Director Yunhai. He can definitely handle this. Let him handle it."

"That's right. We absolutely cannot allow such a black sheep to exist."

A loud explosion erupted at the scene, followed by a series of explosive revelations.

Han Xian was overjoyed.

However, he couldn't reveal the name, because if he did, it would seem too deliberate.

But not revealing the name,

would give the other side an opportunity.

This put Han Xian in a dilemma.



Chapter 590 Minister Tan is furious. This is outrageous! This is utterly insane!


The assistant, sensing Han Xian's predicament, and being a trusted confidant whose fate was intertwined with his own, naturally knew how to ease the tension: "Our Officer Jiang Tian has rid the people of evil, solving numerous major cases. Let him be deliberate; after all, he was outwardly protecting Jiang Tian.

Now it exploded even more.

"Kill this black sheep!"

Everyone was in a frenzy.

This person was from their unit.

It's gotten out of hand.

It's gone mad.

After all, what's often lacking is a leader, even though some people rush in impulsively.

However, a small group of people, rarely clear-headed and still maintaining their rationality, were startled when they saw the situation escalate to the point of storming the city police station. They quickly hid in inconspicuous spots to watch.

At the entrance of the city police station, there weren't many people present at this moment, only eight or nine officers maintaining order. After all, combat personnel were out on missions in various parts of Yunhai at this time, either investigating or making arrests. Although Jiang Tian had taken the lion's share, it wasn't unreasonable for them to take a small share.

The eight police officers, under the leadership of someone with ulterior motives, were quickly pushed aside by the crowd of onlookers.

Then they rushed into the city police station in a frenzy.

They were shouting slogans.

"Down with Jiang Tian!"

"Down with the black sheep!"

"Jiang Tian is the biggest scourge of Yunhai!"

"Down with the cancer Jiang Tian!"

Slightly different, but the meaning was essentially the same.

Everyone roared as they rushed in.

Their blood was boiling.

After all, in this situation, even a normal person would be infected, let alone those who had been tricked into coming here. Most of them weren't particularly bright; in other words, they were quite stupid.

They hadn't even seen through the slightest leak.

They hadn't even realized that this was a conspiracy. Otherwise, why would they have been able to storm into the Municipal Public Security Bureau so easily, even taking such drastic actions?

Unfortunately, after rushing in, they were somewhat lost.

They didn't know which way to go.

"Insolence!"

And at the same time.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong, who had just descended the office stairs and reached the lobby entrance on the first floor, were suddenly confronted by the imposing entrance, just a hundred meters away. The sight made Minister Tan's head spin, and he nearly choked.

Good heavens!

He was right here in Yunhai, and yet someone managed to force their way in.

Causing such chaos.

This is outrageous!

This is defying the heavens!

This is blatant defiance!

But look at what they're trying to do, and what they've actually done.

They actually want to bring down Jiang Tian, ​​this black sheep?

Do these people even realize what Jiang Tian represents to Yunhai? It's not that Jiang Tian can't live without Yunhai, but that Yunhai truly can't live without Jiang Tian.

Minister Tan can even guarantee that if Jiang Tian leaves Yunhai, within a week, the entire Yunhai heroin market will continue to flood the market. The existence of this market will also create a corresponding number of criminals and industry professionals.

In other words, because of the scarcity of supply, the market might be even more booming than before.

And the increased market vacancies will inevitably create an equal number of criminals.

In other words,

Without Jiang Tian, ​​the vacant market will soon be taken over by emerging criminal forces. In other words, all of Jiang Tian's previous efforts will be in vain.

It's equivalent to everything he did being for nothing.

"They've rebelled! They've all rebelled!"

Minister Tan was furious, his eyes wide and bloodshot.

Even without Gao Xiaolong, Minister Tan had already drawn his sidearm in a fit of rage.

Did they really think he hadn't heard anything?

They had been standing at the door, quietly gathering information, and after gathering information,

especially about the person who had barged in initially,

Minister Tan's experience allowed him to immediately see that something was definitely wrong.

This was definitely organized and premeditated.

It was a conspiracy entirely aimed at Jiang Tian.

However, just as Minister Tan was about to ask Jiang Tian about the situation,

How could that be possible? They had been in the system for so many years; they could see through any tactic at a glance.

Because they could see it coming, they were already furious enough.

Then, these people stormed into the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Outrageous.

Utterly outrageous.

What kind of place is the Municipal Public Security Bureau? Is it a place where others can run wild? It represents the authority of the organization.

At this moment.

Han Xian's interference at this crucial moment.

Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan's anger was actually directed partly at these foolish ordinary people who had been maliciously deceived.

Not only Minister Tan, but Minister Tan could already imagine that the higher-ups were waiting to support Jiang Tian, ​​to pave the way for his international success.

That's right.

With Jiang Tian's existence, they could even conveniently transform Yunhai into a globally renowned holy land.

If Jiang Tian hadn't been exposed, there would still be a significant opportunity and time for reconciliation later on. But now...

It's too late to regret it now.

But...

This is no longer just simple jealousy; it's downright disregard for the bigger picture.

If Han Xian continues as bureau chief, what will happen in the future?

Who can imagine?

Furious.

Minister Tan took a step forward, and in the next instant, he had already drawn his pistol.

At that moment, the leader, seemingly sensing something, instantly met Minister Tan's furious gaze.



Chapter 591 This is suicidal, a shocking outrage


Bang!!!

A terrifying gunshot suddenly rang out. As Minister Tan fired, a large horde of armed police arrived outside.

What a joke.

If you were just protesting, that would be one thing, but you dared to come here, even storming the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

This is completely illegal and criminal.

And that's not all.

What truly enraged Minister Tan was...

What happened outside, and what Han Xian said, was all just a charade by a cunning fox.

They even laughed at you.

A clown.

This is just a clown.

What is Jiang Tian's importance?

And what you're doing now, and what you'll ultimately do, is going in the opposite direction to the higher-ups, creating trouble for them.

As Minister Tan fired his gun,

the crowd was instantly brought under control.

In truth, a large portion of the people present were brought in by the leader through various means. Some of these people weren't there voluntarily; others were paid to come.

Why risk their lives for work? Letting them join in the fun is fine, but when faced with real problems, they'll all back down.

That's exactly what's happening now.

The previously noisy crowd fell silent instantly.

The leader, seeing the armed police rushing towards him, silently tried to retreat and disappear.

Unfortunately, Minister Tan had already spotted him: "Grab him! Arrest and subdue that person!"

Minister Tan pointed to the leader retreating into the crowd.

The young man's heart skipped a beat. Two armed police officers were already in control, and with Minister Tan's shot, the situation had been completely pulled back from the brink of chaos.

What a joke.

How many people were there?

And what kind of mental fortitude did they have?

Jiang Tian could take down hundreds of drug dealers, all fully armed thugs, let alone these people.

One shot, and everyone was terrified.

"What are you all standing there for? Are you trying to silence everyone? What right do you have to arrest me?"

"Let me go!"

Li Tian, ​​precisely controlled by the armed police, immediately retreated in fright, while simultaneously trying to urge everyone to continue the chaos.

But unfortunately.

Minister Tan was standing upstairs, having seen everything clearly, including everything Han Xian had said and done.

He had heard it all.

This person was definitely the leader.

Of course, Minister Tan hadn't yet figured out the situation—why things had suddenly escalated so much, why Jiang Tian had inexplicably become a black sheep—but none of that mattered.

The most important thing was…

“Who told you to do this?” Minister Tan walked up directly, his face expressionless.

“Me? What? Who told me to do this? What do you want? Don't we even have this much right? That Jiang Tian, ​​he disrespects human rights, even wantonly beating minors. Simply put, he's killing innocent people and claiming credit for it! If you're so capable, go arrest those vicious criminals! Are you only targeting ordinary people like us?” Li Tian struggled frantically to retreat.

Minister Tan sneered.

He could say with certainty:

It was absolutely impossible for this to happen without someone organizing it.

And it couldn't possibly be some simple force.

“Jiang Tian, ​​why is he a black sheep?” Minister Tan's face was icy.

Li Tian, ​​his face contorted with rage, spoke out as if defending others, shouting, "That Jiang Tian..."

Then, Li Tian recounted the information that had surfaced online and in the news.

"News?"

Minister Tan's brow twitched.

It's in the news.

Minister Tan's eyes instantly flashed red—a murderous intent.

If it were just circulating on a small scale, it wouldn't be so difficult to rectify.

But it's in the news, and more importantly, so many controversial issues and problems.

It's caused a huge stir and controversy online.

The more Minister Tan listened, the more his brow twitched.

He's gone mad.

He's rebelled.

"This is outrageous!"

"Which TV station's news?" Minister Tan asked grimly.

"Someone actually dares to do this!"

"Yucheng TV Station," Li Tian replied without hesitation.

That's right.

It's Yucheng TV Station.

Minister Tan frowned at the mention of Yucheng TV Station. It wasn't that he didn't recognize it; rather, he was quite familiar with it.

In short, Yucheng TV Station is a public-private partnership. In other words, while the station itself is publicly run, many of its management personnel are privately managed.

Combined with its relatively high degree of autonomy, the station's content is very entertaining, resulting in high viewership.

Furthermore, Yucheng TV Station is located not far from Beijing, even adjacent to it. Although it's a small city, it has perfectly taken over many of Beijing's affairs and problems.

Because it's self-operated, its annual profits are enormous.

This has created a very serious problem.

The statement from Yucheng TV means that this issue and matter are beyond repair, even if they wanted to.

Jiang Tian has been completely exposed.

His reputation online is ruined.

How will things be handled afterwards?

How will the plan be implemented?

Although there's still a chance to salvage the situation,

this also means that reversing the damage will be extremely difficult.

"Damn it!" Minister Tan cursed angrily.

His eyes were already bloodshot. Do you know how much effort the higher-ups put into promoting Jiang Tian? Do you know what Jiang Tian's future development plans are?

It's fair to say that Yunhai can function without anyone, even Zhou Zhao's absence wouldn't affect anything, except for Jiang Tian.

Yunhai cannot function without Jiang Tian. Most importantly, Jiang Tian is now involved in many international affairs; a single move could have far-reaching consequences.

With this incident, all plans will instantly be shelved.

The grand strategy has been jeopardized.

This is tantamount to...

Treason.

Minister Tan's face was ashen.

"You're incredibly audacious! You're courting death!" Minister Tan said through gritted teeth, enunciating each word clearly.



Chapter 592 No one can judge me, no one


"Who gave you permission to do this?" Minister Tan asked grimly.

Even if it was a television station, it had only been around for a short time. How could nearly a hundred people have come to storm the Municipal Public Security Bureau so quickly?

It was utterly audacious.

Clearly, those who benefited from this were doing it to get rid of Jiang Tian.

Who were these beneficiaries?

It was obvious: those who feared Jiang Tian, ​​dreaded Jiang Tian, ​​and wanted him dead.

Or perhaps, they didn't dare or had the ability to kill Jiang Tian, ​​so they resorted to other methods.

Did Jiang Tian actually care about any of this? Minister Tan could say that; he clearly understood how capable Jiang Tian was.

He also understood that even leaving the police force wouldn't mean much to Jiang Tian.

"No, no one told us to do this. We organized it spontaneously online," Li Tian said, shrinking back.

At this moment, seeing Minister Tan and noticing that the scene was under control, Li Tian dared not act recklessly.

Although he chose to answer honestly,

but...

did Minister Tan believe him?

No one present was a fool.

“Arrest them, interrogate them rigorously, find out who organized this, who gave them permission to do this, arrest all the most vocal individuals.” Minister Tan acted swiftly.

While not all were arrested, the key groups were all arrested together and interrogated separately.

Without the ringleader, things would be much easier.

The remaining people were even less of a threat.

“Minister, won’t this cause even more trouble? After all, once it’s exposed, it’ll easily cause a huge public outcry.”

At this moment, Han Xian ran out and stood before Minister Tan, his face seemingly full of concern for the greater good.

Minister Tan, seeing Han Xian’s expression, laughed in exasperation.

Others might not know.

But could he really not know?

Or rather...

Minister Tan saw through Han Xian's thoughts but didn't reveal them, yet Han Xian still dared to speak out at this moment.

"Moreover, is doing this just for Jiang Tian alone too costly? Wouldn't a more conciliatory approach have been better?" Han Xian couldn't just watch things be resolved so easily.

His eyes were almost red with anger. Putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, if he were in Han Xian's shoes, he was certain that Minister Tan and even Gao Xiaolong would remain silent, immediately subduing him and then giving the public an explanation.

The usual solution was this: regardless of whether there was a problem, if a major disturbance occurred, first detain the person, then investigate slowly.

This also quelled public anger.

But why was Jiang Tian being supported by the top two police officers? Why was he being protected despite such widespread public resentment? What would the rest of the country think of Yunhai if word got out?

But Han Xian clearly didn't know.

No matter how much public resentment there was, if even one or two of Jiang Tian's first-class merits were leaked, it would be enough to silence everyone.

"So, what do you intend to do?" Minister Tan suppressed his anger, his voice filled with a low, cold laugh.

Although Han Xian sensed something was wrong, at this moment, he felt this was his only chance in his life. If he lost this opportunity, he didn't know if he would ever have another.

For this once-in-a-lifetime chance, Han Xian would naturally seize it with everything he had.

"Right now, we should first detain Comrade Jiang to give the public an explanation. At the very least, we should suspend him from his duties for investigation to quell public anger and allow for a smooth transition. After the incident has subsided, it wouldn't be unreasonable to reinstate Comrade Jiang to his original position," Han Xian suggested with a smile.

At the same time, he glanced at Jiang Tian out of the corner of his eye, a cold smile creeping across his face.

He knew it.

Excessive rigidity leads to breakage; a tree that stands out in the forest is bound to be felled by the wind. Jiang Tian's rise was too smooth, too high. Could such achievements at such a young age bring any good?

How could it?

Without a solid foundation and having offended so many people, it was practically a death sentence.

Now, it's exactly as he expected.

There are far too many people eager to kick him while he's down, wanting to see him fall from grace.

Moreover, that's what they say, but once he's suspended from his post for investigation, what does that mean? How could he possibly be reinstated? Suspension is easy, but reinstatement is difficult. In that case, even Han Xian would be the first to prevent Jiang Tian from being reinstated.

If they drag this out for a few years, Jiang Tian will be able to control them at will. And if they want to come back, they'll be dreaming.

It's just a nice idea, though.

Reality, however, was far more harsh.

"Do you know what Jiang Tian means to Yunhai?" Minister Tan sneered, clearly seeing through Han Xian's intentions.

He hadn't originally planned to argue, but now, an unnamed fire was rising and boiling within him.

"What do you mean?"

"You want to suspend Xiao Jiang? You...are you worthy?"

Minister Tan sneered, almost enunciating each word.

Han Xian's face changed slightly, twitching a little.

Say this in public, it truly showed no respect whatsoever.

"Minister, this is just a delaying tactic," Han Xian stammered, trying to explain.

His mind raced frantically, searching for a solution.

Beads of sweat dripped from his forehead.

Even with this explanation,

Minister Tan only sneered repeatedly.

A joke.

While it wouldn't matter if Jiang Tian's identity were exposed, since it was bound to happen sooner or later, it absolutely couldn't happen now.

And it wasn't something Han Xian could control.

*Slap!*

A resounding slap ripped through the air.

It was especially loud in the otherwise silent environment.

Han Xian covered his face, staring at Minister Tan in disbelief.

That's right, Minister Tan's slap had completely stunned him.

Why?

Han Xian's mind went blank.

Why?

Why?

Han Xian's eyes widened.

"Do you think we don't know what you're thinking? Let me tell you, you're not worthy to treat us like fools. Especially, how Jiang Tian decides is not your business. Most importantly, this matter is not something you can participate in. Who do you think you are?"

You want to come here and reap the benefits.

We can turn a blind eye.

Even if you don't care about anything, don't do anything, and just act as a mascot, at least it won't matter if you're there.

As long as you cooperate with Jiang Tian, ​​that's enough.

You don't need to have extraordinary abilities; at least, knowing how to cooperate and knowing who the powerful are is enough.

But you absolutely cannot come here not only to offer no help but to cause trouble.

Even Minister Tan cannot make a decision or take action regarding Jiang Tian.

Because that's the bigwig above.

As for why...

Yunhai is facing a transformation, and Jiang Tian is key to that transformation.

"Now, return to your office and await further instructions," Minister Tan said expressionlessly.



Chapter 593: Lock down all doors and arrest everyone; come find me if anything happens.


These words were incredibly significant for Han Xian, who was eager to make a name for himself, especially in such a public setting. It was practically a death sentence for him, given that the top two police officers had effectively handed him a death warrant.

At the very least.

There are no secrets within the system. In the future, someone who has been so harshly criticized by the top officer will be virtually untouchable within the entire police force, even if they escape punishment.

“Minister Tan, why? Just because he’s Jiang Tian, ​​does that mean he can get special treatment?”

At this moment, Han Xian clearly understood.

What did Minister Tan’s attitude mean to him today? He would have virtually no place in the police system. Even if he wasn’t suspended in Yunhai, who would want to associate with him?

Trying to reap the benefits?

Now Han Xian realized that this was no easy task; it was like picking a cactus, leaving him with prickly hands and bleeding.

“Special treatment?”

Minister Tan gave Han Xian a strange look, or perhaps Han Xian was genuinely unaware or feigning ignorance.

"Then do you know how many First-Class Merits Comrade Jiang has?" Minister Tan asked coldly.

Han Xian froze.

He'd been in the Municipal Bureau for quite some time now, and while he might not know much else, this was the question he'd heard most about lately, especially news about Jiang Tian and details of some less-than-classified missions.

He had to admit it.

Even Han Xian acknowledged Jiang Tian's contributions to Yunhai. Without Jiang Tian, ​​the entire situation in Yunhai would have been completely nonexistent.

In fact, rumors had circulated in Beijing about Yunhai's situation: whenever someone was preparing for something big, they would all inevitably die in some mysterious way.

Finally, a mediocre, incompetent guy like Jiang Peng was chosen to barely maintain order. In a way, Jiang Peng's contributions to the increasingly out-of-control situation in Yunhai were also considerable.

The situation in Yunhai, which was gradually spiraling out of control, was brought back under control. Although this was a process of compromise, within the system, compromise is actually a crucial aspect of politics.

“Seven…” Han Xian said, forcing a smile.

“Then do you know why such a huge mess occurred this time?” Minister Tan pressed further.

Han Xian’s expression instantly changed.

“You know what Xiao Jiang did, and you dare to make this decision now?” Minister Tan pressed further.

Han Xian’s face grew even paler.

That’s right, he had only heard what the crowd was saying; he truly knew nothing else.

“Don’t know?” Minister Tan sneered.

“Then let me tell you, the reason for this is that the child of the heroic captain of the anti-drug squad who sacrificed his life was kidnapped by that so-called classmate who wanted to humiliate her, even trying to use heroin to humiliate her. Now do you know what happened?” Minister Tan said coldly, expressionless.

That’s right.

Minister Tan was furious the moment Jiang Tian finished speaking.

"You're asking for it."

"That's right."

"You're asking for it."

Besides, could a simple man like Chen Xu handle such a huge matter? If he couldn't, the entire Huayu Group would be dragged down with him.

"Children of martyrs?"

Han Xian was stunned.

After all, regardless of whether Jiang Tian was at fault, the public outrage had to be quelled eventually.

But now...

The situation seemed completely different from what he had imagined.

"Han Xian, although you're new to Yunhai and don't understand the situation, your performance is extremely disappointing. Your narrow-mindedness is obvious. Do you think we're all fools who can't see through you? Do you think you can fool us? If so, you're wrong. Without understanding the situation, you caused a huge negative impact, even encouraging public anger to some extent. As the director of Yunhai Bureau, I can formally tell you that your performance is incompetent. Now, shut your mouth."

Gao Xiaolong's face was also completely expressionless.

Only his tone and emotional control were slightly better than Minister Tan's.

In such matters, it's always a matter of one playing the good cop and the other the bad cop. The fact that both the top two police officers spoke out simultaneously cast a huge shadow of criticism on Han Xian.

Jiang Tian stood at the back, remaining silent.

But watching the scene unfold before him, he truly looked like a clown. No wonder people say the situation in Yunhai is incredibly complex.

Like a vast ocean.

Other places are manageable, but in Yunhai, the higher the sky, the deeper the water.

The situation in Yunhai was already complicated enough.

Moreover, after Jiang Tian's actions in Yunhai, the eyes of Beijing instantly focused here, even the top two police officers were present.

What appeared to be a huge mess was actually quite minor thanks to Minister Tan's intervention.

However, while the immediate problem was solved, the most important one remained unresolved.

Especially since this incident was just an extension of something else.

And especially, the leader, Li Tian, ​​was now pale, large beads of sweat constantly streaming down his face.

"Minister?"

Li Tian's eyes widened as he stared at the two men before him. They were dressed in plainclothes, not even in police uniforms, just plain clothes.

At first, he thought they were just two ordinary people, or perhaps leaders from the municipal bureau. He never expected them to be ministers.

Especially since behind these two was a young man.

He was wearing the uniform of a second-level police inspector.

Standing beside the two men,

his expression was extremely calm.

Li Tian instantly realized these people were no ordinary individuals.

Because of the way these two men had just berated Director Yunhai outside.

He had sensed something was amiss then.

And now…

Upon hearing the word "minister," Li Tian's face turned pale.

"You're a minister?"

He asked.

Even a regular municipal bureau leader wouldn't dare act like this, because if it got out, it would be a serious matter.

Especially for local leaders.

They were even more concerned about this kind of thing.

Moreover, chaos of this scale would require reporting up the chain of command at least several levels. According to Li Tian's thinking, as time went on, the impact would grow, and the municipal police bureau would absolutely be unable to withstand the pressure, and even the city government of Yunhai would be unable to withstand any pressure.

How could things be like this, with no preamble?

Armed police were arriving in droves.

As for what they were doing there?

Of course, it was to continuously control the crowd.

A full hundred people—it was like trapping a dog inside a closed door; once the door was closed, even if they wanted to escape, they couldn't.

Furthermore, a gathering of this scale could not have been organized by one or two people; all these people would be interrogated.

In other words, regardless of whether they were innocent or not, they were all arrested at once.

Especially Li Tian, ​​who was the ringleader and the most vocal, was also arrested.



Chapter 594 A Police Baton Teaches You a Lesson, Aba Aba, the Kid's Got It


"You're a minister, does that give you the right to disregard human rights?"

Li Tian forced himself to calm down.

He looked at the three people in front of him with forced composure. Yes, in the interrogation room, Minister Tan, Chief Gao, and Jiang Tian were the three.

They looked at him indifferently, or rather, at this point, in a way, it was better to guide than to suppress.

Since suppressing the issue now would only fuel more conspiracy theories, it was better to amplify it from now on, letting the news spread further.

That's what they were doing now.

After doing some research online, Minister Tan had already decided to let things slide for the time being.

Or rather, to lay the net.

Let's see how many people are behind this.

As the higher-ups could say, cleaning things up little by little was becoming increasingly troublesome.

Since that's the case.

Then it's better to start now, put some bait in, and wait for all the fish to swim in.

Then catch them all in one fell swoop.

Since the chaos is prolonged, it's better to grit your teeth and endure the pain now than suffer continuously.

"That's right, let me introduce myself. I'm the Police Minister, my surname is Tan, you can call me Minister Tan." Minister Tan nodded slightly.

At this point, whether Li Tian confesses or how much he knows is irrelevant; Minister Tan has already begun casting his net.

Especially since he's started tracking online.

Among them, there might be innocent people, but because of the high profits, a large number of online trolls have been rampant.

These people, used to causing trouble, initially thought the higher-ups weren't taking the matter seriously when they saw the news wasn't censored.

So these trolls became increasingly bold, fueling the flames and appearing frantically online.

But regardless of whether they have ulterior motives,

at this point, it's impossible to distinguish, and there's no need to analyze.

Some people will always pay a heavy price for offending those they can't afford to offend.

“My surname is Gao,” Chief Gao nodded slightly.

As for Li Tian, ​​his pupils contracted rapidly. Chief Gao, though not explicitly named, was someone Li Tian had seen on the news more than once.

Because he had seen him before,

he knew what Chief Gao did.

Li Tian's head was buzzing.

What exactly happened at the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau? Why were these two big shots here?

Why hadn't anyone told him?

Why hadn't the person behind this told him beforehand?

That's right.

According to their plan, if Yunhai handled it itself, regardless of the local procedures, by the time the solutions were finalized and after a series of meetings, the situation would have already spiraled out of control.

Once they stormed into the city bureau, the city leaders would surely compromise with them.

But Li Tian never dreamed this would happen.

Good heavens.

These two big shots were here, completely disregarding normal procedures.

"Who are you?"

Li Tian turned his gaze and noticed Jiang Tian.

After all, he was standing side by side with two big shots in the interrogation room.

Although he remained silent, his imposing aura was impossible to ignore.

While Jiang Tian was young,

he certainly looked very, very young, but to stand side-by-side with two powerful figures, he was clearly no ordinary person.

Moreover, there were two professional interrogators present, yet they stood behind the three men.

“I am the black sheep you speak of, Jiang Tian,”

only then did Jiang Tian speak slightly.

His voice was calm.

It was as if he hadn't just been the one facing the chaos, or as if he hadn't been the one being berated.

In reality, anyone else, even a city leader, would be filled with anxiety, unsure of how to proceed.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian had no worries whatsoever.

Even if he were to be removed from his post, with Jiang Tian's current abilities, frankly, he wasn't worried about his future.

At the very least, Jiang Tian could simply lie low and become a billionaire.

With a little investment, becoming a billionaire in the future would be a breeze. A billionaire is a worldly millionaire, tens of thousands of times better than this shady, dangerous profession that puts even your family at risk.

There's practically no comparison.

Of course, Jiang Tian knew this was impossible.

"What, you're all yelling about taking down this black sheep, but you can't even recognize me?" Jiang Tian tilted his head and sneered.

Stupid.

Jiang Tian could say that someone like Li Tian, ​​regardless of whether he knew the inside story, was either stupid or evil.

In short, he absolutely deserved to die.

"You're Jiang Tian?"

"The Captain Jiang from the internet?"

Li Tian stared intently at Jiang Tian.

"You're about to storm the city police station to take me down, and you still don't recognize me? You're really useless, even when being used as a pawn." Jiang Tian shook his head.

"What did you say? I don't understand what you mean. What do you mean by using someone as a tool? Officer Jiang, solving a case requires evidence, investigation, and meticulous tracing before you can obtain irrefutable evidence to convict someone. It's not like you, relying on your own imagination, conjuring up someone else's crime in your mind, and then deceiving yourself," Li Tian sneered.

Regardless, the top two officers in the police force were here.

He didn't believe Jiang Tian would dare do anything to him.

His attitude instantly hardened.

After the initial panic, Li Tian thought of his backers. The people behind him certainly wouldn't just stand by and watch him stay here.

Besides, at most, it would be a matter of following the crowd. The law doesn't punish everyone; they couldn't possibly sentence all the hundred-plus people he brought.

Even if he did get sentenced, a few years in prison, and perhaps his boss could compensate him with a large sum of money.

Li Tian valued money above all else.

He wasn't afraid of spending a few years in jail.

Since that's the case, it's only natural to take this opportunity, in front of the two big shots, to mock and ridicule Jiang Tian.

Perhaps the big shots behind him will be pleased and give him a few more points, making them appreciate him even more.

Li Tian, ​​originally a thug, to be precise, is a scoundrel, and a scoundrel who fears neither heaven nor earth.

He's like a dead pig that doesn't fear boiling water.

It's just a pity.

"What, you want to hit me? Come on, hit me! My reputation is right here. Let's see if you dare. If you don't make a move, I'll look down on you."

Jiang Tian took a step forward.

Li Tian even leaned forward, his face full of sneers.

After all, with two big shots present, he still had to save face.

You, Jiang Tian, ​​actually dare to make a move in front of these big shots?

Boom!!!

This thought had barely formed when

a loud sound rang out.

Li Tian's mind exploded instantly, his eyes glazing over like a baby's.

Drool dripped unconsciously from his mouth.

And he made a faint sound, which, upon closer listening, was: "Aba, aba!!!"



Chapter 595 The Pinnacle of Psychology, Unstoppable


Jiang Tian expressionlessly lowered his baton slightly.

What a joke.

Did they really think Jiang Tian wouldn't act just because the two bosses were here?

That's a joke.

Jiang Tian's actions, especially what happened today,

have crossed a line.

Li Tian's head was buzzing as he stared at Jiang Tian, ​​who was expressionlessly lowering his baton.

Then he stared blankly at the two bosses behind him.

The two bosses remained expressionless, showing no sign of wavering.

Using vigilante justice!

This is using vigilante justice!

This is outrageous.

This is defying the heavens.

Li Tian was completely dumbfounded. They came to Jiang Tian about this matter, only to be arrested and beaten by him using vigilante justice?

The blow with the baton left Li Tian's head spinning, his face blank, drooling, but to be honest, he was actually unharmed.

Jiang Tian had long since mastered the control over his strength.

"You hit me?"

Li Tian asked, his face still blank.

Jiang Tian looked at Li Tian, ​​then smiled slightly and said, "I hit you. What can you do about it? Since you know I'm resorting to vigilante justice, you should understand what will happen to you here in my hands. I dare to hit people even with lawyers around. Now, in this small, dark room, why do you think I wouldn't dare to hit you? Besides, you asked me to hit you. I've never heard of such a request in my life."

As he spoke,

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue slightly.

Then his gaze gradually fixed on the other person, and his smile faded: "Now can you tell me who did it?"

Li Tian shook his head: "Who did it? I just had a bit of a problem with you online, so I organized a group."

"You admit you organized them?" Jiang Tian countered.

"That's right, it was me." Li Tian admitted directly.

What a joke.

Even if he goes to jail, how many years can he stay?

A few years later, his boss will take notice, and he'll be a hero again, maybe even able to defy fate and reach the pinnacle of life.

"Do you know what laws you've broken?" Jiang Tian retorted.

"It's just rioting, so what if I get a few years?" Li Tian said nonchalantly.

Unfortunately.

Just then.

Jiang Tian turned to the two interrogation experts and asked, "Given his crime, its social impact, and his malicious non-cooperation, which resulted in serious and egregious consequences, what will be his final sentence?"

"Death penalty."

At that moment, the interrogation expert said decisively.

Pfft!!!

Li Tian almost spat out his drink.

His eyes widened, then he sneered, "Who are you trying to scare? Death penalty? At most, I've only been charged with rioting. I've never heard of rioting being a death penalty."

He didn't believe it.

Li Tian simply didn't believe it.

But.

The interrogation experts weren't lying to Li Tian; this crime really was a death sentence.

So, the interrogation expert, without any anger, smiled and said, "What you understand about 'gathering a crowd to cause trouble' is at most the kind where there's no serious impact, and the consequences aren't severe. If you read on, you'll know that if the impact is extremely serious, causing severe social harm, then the maximum penalty under this law is the death penalty."

This wasn't a lie.

Based on the impact Li Tian caused, although it didn't reach that level, the overall impact could be considered accomplice treatment, easily resulting in a death sentence.

If the municipal bureau recommends a death sentence, and the consequences are indeed extremely serious, impacting the municipal bureau and causing severe consequences,

the death penalty is highly probable.

The prosecution generally won't reject it, and at the court level, it's basically just a formality, and they certainly won't reject it.

So, overall...

Even though Li Tian's eyes were wide and his face was full of disbelief, when Jiang Tian took out his phone and found the legal interpretation clauses, cold sweat began to pour from Li Tian's forehead.

"If you confess now, we can give you a lighter sentence, maybe even life imprisonment or a fixed term. With good behavior, you could be out in ten years or so. You're not very old, probably only in your twenties, and you'll be in your thirties when you get out, right in your prime. Think about it yourself, but don't think that not confessing means the end of this."

Jiang Tian tapped the table: "If you don't confess, it's not just the death penalty. Even if everyone else confesses, your gritted teeth here won't do any good!"

Loyalty?

What is that?
