"Who is this young man, Liting? What brings you here?"

"Oh, it's my niece! Hahaha, I was so blind, I didn't recognize you. But niece, since you're already here, why kick down the door?"

That's why, no matter what they were thinking, at this moment, even if they were a little irritable inside, after kicking down the door, some people were about to burst their tempers.

However,

they still had to maintain a certain level of decorum. This was Huayu Group, and who was in charge? Naturally, it was Chen Qiang!

Even if they were extremely unhappy inside, they absolutely couldn't show it here before they overthrew Chen Qiang.

All eyes were focused on Chen Liting.

Although Jiang Tian acted assertively, they subconsciously assumed he was Chen Liting's bodyguard. After all, Jiang Tian was undeniably burly and had the air of someone skilled in martial arts; the description of him as a bodyguard didn't seem inaccurate.

Therefore, they assumed Jiang Tian kicking down the door was Chen Liting's order.

"Liting, it's one thing to cause trouble at home, but how can you continue to do this at the company? How did Old Chen raise you? You cause chaos at home, and now you're behaving like this at the company. These are all your father's old brothers; how dare you kick down the door? What do you want?"

Just as Chen Liting was about to say something,

Zeng Wei, sitting in the main seat, frowned slightly. While everyone was still exchanging greetings, he began to reprimand Chen Liting in an angry and reprimanding voice.

Finally, Zeng Wei even slammed his hand on the table and stood up.

The moment Zeng Wei spoke, all sound in the conference room vanished. Everyone's eyes turned to Zeng Wei, and as he slammed his hand on the table and stood up, five or six people followed suit.

"That's right, Liting, you were incredibly inconsiderate today. We usually let your young age fool us, but this is the company, an important internal meeting, and you dared to bring people in and kick the door down! Your father's here, and I'm going to ask him how he disciplines you. Can't he even raise you properly? Even if you're being unreasonable, there's a limit."

"Liting, come here and apologize to all your uncles and elders."

The last sentence was spoken by Zeng Wei, his face grim, filled with a threatening and intimidating pressure, as he stared intently at Chen Liting.

Despite Chen Liting's usual boisterousness and even a touch of daring, and her stubbornness, her heart pounded uncontrollably in this situation.

After all...

Speaking of which, these people were all those who followed Chen Qiang in building his empire back then!

Chen Liting, in the end, still lacked experience.

“I don’t want to.” Although a little scared, Chen Liting still stepped forward and bravely shook her head in refusal.

“Well, Liting, I’ll give you one last chance. If you continue to be disrespectful, don’t blame your uncle for disciplining you on behalf of your father.” Zeng Wei cracked his neck.

At this moment.

Jiang Tian’s gaze swept around.

Finally, his gaze returned to Zeng Wei.

One glance.

Jiang Tian could analyze what kind of person Zeng Wei was.

Ambitious.

That’s right.

This person is too deceptive. His face is like a smiling tiger, and even his appearance makes him seem like a kind old man.

When he speaks, he often seems weak and submissive.

However, upon entering, in that instant, Jiang Tian realized that Zeng Wei might actually be the biggest tiger in the entire conference room.

Ambitious.

Absolutely unwilling to be subservient to anyone.

Moreover, among these people, Zeng Wei's red aura was the most dazzling; in other words, his level of sin was even more terrifying than Chen Qiang's.

How many sins had he committed?

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

However, he didn't act immediately.

Reaching out, as Chen Liting shook her head, her face pale, Jiang Tian grabbed her hand.

Chen Liting, who had been extremely frightened and nervous, felt as if she had been suddenly enveloped in light in the darkness. The warmth of Jiang Tian's palm clearly spread throughout her body.

"Don't be afraid, I'm here," Jiang Tian patted Chen Liting's shoulder with his other hand, then whispered in her ear.



Chapter 608 You still dare to call me brother? All units, take action!


Only then, amidst Zeng Wei's squinting, somewhat bewildered gaze, did she slightly turn around, her eyes meeting Zeng Wei's.

"Who is this brat? Who let him near here? This is the company's confidential conference room. Where are the security guards outside? Drag this brat out and deal with him!" Zeng Wei waved his hand and loudly ordered.

A show of force.

Chen Liting was, after all, Chen Qiang's daughter, and Zeng Wei couldn't do anything too outrageous at this time. But the young man beside Chen Liting...

Zeng Wei's eyes lit up.

Because he realized that the relationship between the two seemed more than simple.

So, if he dealt with this young man in front of Chen Liting, even breaking both his legs...

His niece would be heartbroken.

A cold smile curled at the corner of Zeng Wei's lips.

The next moment, Zeng Wei's sneer froze. This brat, meeting his scrutinizing and murderous gaze, casually strode in and walked to the main seat in the conference room—the empty seat belonging to Chairman Chen Qiang.

He stopped next to Zeng Wei, also in the empty seat beside him. Zeng Wei initially thought the brat was going to speak to him or beg for mercy, but before he could say anything, the brat pulled out a chair.

*Buzz!*

Zeng Wei's eyes widened.

The brat sat down.

Did he know what he was doing?

He'd gone mad.

*Whoosh!*

Zeng Wei's head exploded, and everyone in the conference room—over thirty people—had their eyes practically bulging out of their sockets.

That's why these old buddies knew Chen Qiang best.

While playful banter and jokes are fine in everyday life, in formal settings, or rather, when it comes to getting things done, Chen Qiang is absolutely the most disciplined and ethical, and also the most ruthless.

This seat, don't underestimate its simple appearance.

In reality,

it's a symbol of power, the chairman's emblem.

Not to mention a complete stranger, even his old buddies wouldn't dare touch this seat. Touching it, in their line of work, signifies that they have designs on the leader's position, even an attempt to overthrow him.

And given Chen Qiang's ruthlessness in dealing with such matters, nine times out of ten, it's a matter of sealing the container in cement and sinking it into the sea.

Oh no, in Yunhai, there's no sea; most of the time, it can only sink into the Yanjiang River, or be buried directly in the ground.

"Are you crazy?"

"Young man, are you out of your mind? Stand up!"

"Who told you to sit there? Security, quick, where's security? Call security!"

"Hiss, who is this young man? Does he have a death wish?"

If someone really thought Chen Qiang respected the rules and wouldn't do anything, they were being too naive.

This young man might actually get killed.

Of course, these things weren't important.

The most important thing was that their old buddies were here.

If Chen Qiang appeared at this moment and happened to see that his seat had been taken by a strange young man, he might have some bad thoughts, wondering if they were plotting something.

They pushed a young man forward and then tested him.

After all, they were all seasoned veterans of the underworld, old foxes at heart.

Who would judge a book by its cover?

Even Zeng Wei was clutching his chest at that moment.

Pointing at Jiang Tian, ​​Zeng Wei's face was filled with disbelief.

"You're courting death! You're practically courting death!" Zeng Wei said, pointing at Jiang Tian incredulously.

"Couldn't die?"

Jiang Tian almost laughed out loud.

It has to be said, the chairman's chair was incredibly comfortable. Jiang Tian felt surprisingly good sitting in it; at least, it was countless times better than the chair he used in the city bureau office.

"Kid, if you're looking to die, don't do it here. Otherwise, you'll regret it. Think of your family, if not yourself," Zeng Wei said grimly.

He was also worried that Chen Qiang, upon arriving, might witness this scene and have some unpleasant associations.

Although he didn't want to admit it,

"Conquering an empire is easy, maintaining it is difficult"—this saying is a universal truth. Sharing hardship is possible, but sharing wealth is incredibly difficult.

The brothers hadn't come this far easily.

Mutual suspicion was inevitable. Zeng Wei knew Chen Qiang was wary of them, so he always feigned weakness, but Zeng Wei wasn't resigned to it.

He also had the qualifications to own this multi-billion dollar conglomerate.

Back when he was starting out in the business, Chen Qiang was nowhere to be found. In fact, Chen Qiang's rise to power was largely due to Zeng Wei's connections. If it weren't for him, Chen Qiang would be nowhere to be found.

Therefore, over the years, Zeng Wei has never been satisfied with what he has. Although he has lived a wealthy life and has more money than he can spend, human ambition is also endless. He wants more of what he has gained.

It was only a matter of time before things escalated to a point of irreconcilable conflict.

But definitely not now. Zeng Wei would absolutely not cause trouble now. Yunhai was too chaotic, the situation too complex. For an old fox like Zeng Wei, even if he were given control of Huayu Group now, he wouldn't be too willing.

Because if they weren't careful, Wenye Group would be their cautionary tale. As chairman, could he possibly escape?

“Looking for death? No, old man, I think you're the one looking for death,” Jiang Tian said with a slight smile, cracking his neck.

Buzz!!!

After Jiang Tian's calm voice rang out,

the scene instantly fell silent. Everyone stared in disbelief at the scene before them.

As the most senior person here, even surpassing Chen Qiang in seniority, and a master of using his age to his advantage, Zeng Wei was a veteran in the underworld, someone no one dared to disrespect.

When, how long, had it been since they had heard such insults and abuse?

For a moment, Zeng Wei and everyone present even thought they were hallucinating.

Courageous.

This young man.

Too courageous.

“Are you out of your mind?” At this moment, someone in the conference room suddenly stood up and asked incredulously.

That’s right.

This is simply reckless!

Zeng Wei was also awakened by this voice.

After a moment of daze, Zeng Wei’s eyes instantly reddened. No matter how good his self-control was, all those years of recuperation couldn’t withstand Jiang Tian, ​​a young man, calling him an old man and accusing him of courting death.

For so many years, he hadn't suffered such humiliation.

"You...you...Security, security, break this man's legs, no, smash all his teeth!" Zeng Wei roared, his face flushed with rage, his hands gripping the table.

At that moment,

two security guards entered.

They were about to approach Jiang Tian.

But to their utter surprise, Jiang Tian was incredibly close to Zeng Wei, practically within reach, their seats less than a meter apart.

So...

*Smack!*

*Pfft!*

*Buzz!*

At the same time, Jiang Tian's slightly cold laugh echoed in the deathly quiet conference room:

"You think you're the only one with brothers?"

"All units, move out!"



Chapter 609 A Single Wreck, So Simple


Stunned.

Everyone present was stunned, mainly shocked by the series of unexpected events.

Who was Zeng Wei?

He was undoubtedly the leader among them all.

Or rather, although Chen Qiang was the apparent leader, Zeng Wei actually wielded considerable influence within the company, and was arguably the most senior.

Back when Chen Qiang was just starting out, Zeng Wei was already a local bigwig. In fact, Chen Qiang's rise to prominence was entirely due to Zeng Wei's patronage and promotion. Without Zeng Wei, Chen Qiang might still be a henchman, perhaps even murdered in the street at some point.

Although he later stepped back from the limelight due to Chen Qiang's overwhelming power, Zeng Wei's seniority still made him a true leader. Furthermore, with no one capable of succeeding Chen Qiang, Zeng Wei's ambitions began to grow.

That's human ambition—always wanting more, yet never truly satisfied.

Although Chen Qiang played a major role in the company's development to this point, and it's possible that without him, Huayu Group would still just be a criminal gang.

But now that things have come to this point, it's far from possible to satisfy their ambitions.

And Zeng Wei, with such a long tenure, how many years has he been in this position?

How many years has it been?

Zeng Wei himself couldn't remember, no, in fact, he had never experienced such a resounding, straight slap in his entire life.

His face hurt.

It hurt a lot.

Then, an explosive rage began to boil over.

"He's asking for death! This guy's asking for death!"

"He dares to hit me! He actually dares to hit me! Are you all just sitting around doing nothing? Kill this guy for me!"

Although he had been recuperating for many years and had cultivated his composure for just as long, no one could tolerate this scene.

If he didn't teach this guy a cruel lesson, wouldn't it show everyone that Zeng Wei was someone everyone could bully?

He didn't care who this brat was, who hired him, or what his purpose was.

He had only one thought: to kill this brat.

*Whoosh!*

As soon as Zeng Wei finished speaking, some of the men reacted instantly, quickly standing up and reaching for their weapons.

Jiang Tian had already spoken into the walkie-talkie.

His voice was calm, but his gaze held a cold smile as he looked at them.

Of course, what chilled them to the bone wasn't Jiang Tian's cold smile, but the content of his voice—the content of which made their eyes widen.

"Brothers, sorry, you guys have brothers, but I seem to have a bit more than you," Jiang Tian said quietly.

The police officers who were originally lying in ambush on the perimeter, after Chen Qiang's cooperation, had now formed groups of three to five and were lying in ambush throughout the entire Huayu Group building, just waiting for Jiang Tian's order to unleash their full force.

As for the consequences of attacking the Huayu Group...

Even...

After attacking the Huayu Group, would anyone cause trouble for Jiang Tian? And what impact would the ensuing chaos at the Huayu Group have on society? Would any superiors later find fault with Jiang Tian in the procedural process?

Jiang Tian had never truly worried about these issues.

He hadn't given them a second thought.

After all,

Such a large-scale operation, normally speaking, wouldn't be something a mere Jiang Tian, ​​or even a city bureau chief, could decide—taking action against personnel of a multi-billion dollar group.

Even Zhou Zhao in the city might not have the authority to make such a decision; he would have to consult higher-ups, and might even ultimately have to consult Elder Mu.

After all, regarding illegal activities and matters of law and order, Elder Mu wielded considerable influence throughout Yunnan Province, and was the primary direct leader, without exception.

However, Jiang Tian's status was too special, even unique in Yunnan Province and perhaps the entire country.

It wasn't about Jiang Tian's achievements, but rather the importance placed on him by higher authorities, and the recent actions he had taken, which instilled immense confidence in the top-down, internal and external reforms of Yunhai.

Under these circumstances, Jiang Tian's autonomy in his actions was virtually limitless.

Not to mention Jiang Tian's relationship with Elder Mu, and even the backing of the top two police officers in Yunhai, providing him with comprehensive support and assistance.

So much so that even if he were to take action against this multi-billion dollar corporation,

Jiang Tian wouldn't even need to report to anyone.

But as the saying goes, the main meeting was meant to motivate everyone, to strengthen their resolve, to offer a dose of inspirational platitudes.

The truly important aspect was what was demonstrated in the smaller meetings, where Jiang Tian, ​​in this case, was having his top two officers brief him beforehand.

If it were truly a matter of reporting up the chain of command, the lessons learned from past mistakes—why had Yunhai's previous operations never succeeded—was that by the time the reports were submitted, the criminals had already obtained firsthand information through various connections.

The fact that these people knew everything about Jiang Tian, ​​even his many achievements, was telling. However, their understanding was only superficial; they couldn't grasp the essential details.

That's the core issue.

This means that Yunhai isn't beyond saving; there's still hope.

Most importantly, if they truly wanted to blockade it, these criminal groups wouldn't know. At least, what comforted Jiang Tian was that there weren't any internal spies within the city police department. Or perhaps there were some initially, but they were all uncovered and taken away during subsequent operations and thorough investigations.

Of course, Jiang Tian also intervened and investigated a number of people close to him; he couldn't allow any problems to arise.

Jiang Tian also felt regret about these things.

Because he empathized deeply and clearly understood the terrifying psychological pressure those around him endured every day.

On one hand, they received a monthly salary of a few hundred yuan.

And they faced terrifying dangers while carrying out missions, with the possibility of death at any moment. On the other hand, the criminal gangs used tens of thousands, hundreds of thousands, even millions of yuan in bribes.

The terrifying torment of psychological and physical pressure.

It wasn't easy.

Of course, Jiang Tian only cleaned up the area around him to facilitate his mission. The situation in other departments was outside his jurisdiction. Even if he could see something amiss, he would only keep it in mind and wouldn't do anything about it.

"What do you mean?" Zeng Wei, still furious, hadn't understood Jiang Tian's words.

But he had just asked.

*Whoosh!*

Outside the door.

A terrifying sound of footsteps suddenly rang out.

Following closely behind, more than a dozen plainclothes officers, pistols drawn, rushed in.

Even the two security guards who had been dawdling just moments before instantly pointed their guns at them.



Chapter 610 A Grand Plan: I, Jiang Tian, ​​will bring peace to this sea of ​​clouds.


When Zeng Wei finally snapped out of his daze,

inside the room, thirty or forty fully armed special agents, some even wearing masks, were already pointing assault rifles at each of them. More than a dozen plainclothes officers stood beside Jiang Tian, ​​pistols pointed at them, their faces filled with excitement.

Yes.

How could they not be excited?

Following behind Jiang Tian, ​​Zhao Wei's heart was pounding like a deer's. His face was flushed, purely from excitement.

Because Zhao Wei knew his own capabilities very well.

Without Jiang Tian, ​​he would just be drifting through life in Yunhai, and perhaps one day, bad luck would strike and he wouldn't even know how he died.

Not only were his abilities not great, but Zhao Wei even felt he wasn't very smart.

But the only truly wise thing he had ever done in his life was to stand firmly by Jiang Tian's side. Although his abilities might no longer be of much help to Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian would always bring him along on every mission, especially important ones.

So, in a short time, although it didn't seem as freakish as Jiang Tian's leap—no, a leap of four or five levels—

it still smoothly promoted Zhao Wei from a trainee police officer to a second-level police superintendent, completing a second-level jump. The key is Zhao Wei's age!

He's about the same age as Jiang Tian.

Twenty-two years old. Most importantly, there are Zhao Wei's achievements.

Now, Zhao Wei can even be said to have multiple collective merits, and even individual merits. Although he only did errands, he still earned two second-class merits and three third-class merits.

What, you look down on these two second-class merits and three third-class merits? Do you know what that means? In the same-level promotion selection, having one third-class merit gives you an overwhelming advantage; ordinary people don't even have the qualifications to compete.

As for the second-class merits...

Their importance is self-evident.

Within the system, a second-class merit also represents a promising future.

Now, Zhao Wei can be said to be riding high.

Standing behind Jiang Tian, ​​Zhao Wei raised an eyebrow at Zeng Wei, his face full of smug arrogance and mockery.

"Old man, it's an honor for you that Brother Jiang slapped you just now. You know, many drug lords bigger than you don't even deserve a slap from Brother Jiang. This slap isn't a disgrace; if word gets out, you old man, you'll consider it an honor. After all, very few people get slapped by Brother Jiang, but each one is an absolute big shot. You're nothing in the middle." Zhao Wei said with a slight sneer, holding up his gun.

These words...

The atmosphere in the room seemed to gradually return to normal, and the eyes of everyone who had been stunned and terrified, their minds blank, regained their focus.

Then, following Zhao Wei's words, all eyes turned to Jiang Tian.

Although initially they thought Zhao Wei was bragging,

it was only a matter of time before someone realized something.

Brother Jiang?

Here in the sea of ​​clouds.

How many people could summon such a terrifying number of armed forces on the scene with just a phone call and an order, and carry out the operation without any prior warning?

And those with the surname Jiang are even fewer.

The answer seemed obvious.

“You’re Jiang Tian?” Zeng Wei stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian.

“Looks like you’re not so stupid after all,” Zhao Wei sneered, then pulled out handcuffs and walked towards Zeng Wei.

This caused Zeng Wei’s face to flush red and then pale, finally turning ashen, with large beads of cold sweat streaming down his face.

“Wait! Why are you arresting me? What evidence do you have to arrest me?” Zeng Wei still struggled desperately.

Or rather, everyone is stubborn until they see the coffin, even Chen Xu, caught in the act, could lie through his teeth.

But.

The police aren’t stupid.

The law isn’t stupid either.

Jiang Tian certainly isn’t stupid.

"Would I have come without evidence?"

"Besides, if I didn't have evidence of your crimes, would I have mobilized so many people?"

"Most importantly, if I say you're guilty, then you are guilty."

"I, Jiang Tian, ​​have personally arrested at least eight hundred, if not a thousand, criminals, and to this day, not a single one of them is innocent."

"And everyone, like you, won't admit their innocence until they're faced with death, all shouting their innocence when they go in."

Jiang Tian's voice drifted out lightly and unhurriedly.

*Snap!*

Zeng Wei only felt a cold touch on his hands; before he could react, they were handcuffed.

What? You say you resisted? Don't be ridiculous. Look at all these armed forces here.

Moreover, at this point, even if Zeng Wei were stupid, he understood one thing: Jiang Tian couldn't have accomplished what he did tonight without an inside man. So, who was the inside man?

Zeng Wei, that old codger, wasn't stupid. Considering the current situation and Chen Qiang's continued absence, a terrifying thought suddenly occurred to him.

"You arrested Chen Qiang, and he's already implicated us?" Zeng Wei exclaimed.

Yes.

That was the only possibility. Otherwise, Zeng Wei couldn't understand how Chen Qiang could pull off a truck self-destruction. After all, Chen Qiang was the one who spearheaded many things.

Even the recent rampant illegal dumping.

It was like a money-printing machine, and Chen Qiang was ostensibly leading that too.

Although Zeng Wei had secretly led his men to force him to do it.

But if Chen Qiang confessed, he would be the first to be implicated. Therefore, Zeng Wei truly couldn't understand why Chen Qiang would self-destruct.

"Zeng Wei, is it? You've lived this long; surely there are still many things you can't understand?" Jiang Tian shook his head slightly.

It's like the unreleased "Infernal Affairs" movie; you can infiltrate the police and other sectors, planting agents and spies.

But similarly...

Can't the police infiltrate your underworld?

Don't be ridiculous.

Jiang Tian already has a rough plan in mind.

Prevention is worse than cure.

Since the situation in Yunhai, the crime problem, seems difficult to completely eradicate—given its geographical location—it appears unlikely to be a complete solution.

In that case,

Why not directly use controllable forces to control Yunhai's underworld, creating a spiderweb-like network that leaves no secrets within it?

Then, take control of all criminal gangs.

Finally, reduce, or even eliminate, all crime?

"No, I'm innocent! I didn't do anything!"

"Don't arrest me!"

"Let me go, Chen Qiang! Chen Qiang, you beast! You betrayed us!"

At this moment, these underworld bosses went berserk. Some frantically cried for help, some begged Jiang Tian for mercy, and others hurled insults at Chen Qiang.

Jiang Tian watched coldly from the sidelines, while Zhao Wei watched from behind with a face full of admiration.

As for Chen Liting, her eyes were filled with starlight as she looked at Jiang Tian.



Chapter 611 With so many criminal groups, why did they choose us?


They were devastated.

They were simply enjoying life, and then, inexplicably, they were arrested here.

The most crucial thing was...

Jiang Tian.

It was Jiang Tian.

Although Jiang Tian was about to step into the limelight, his name was still only known within a limited circle, not a household name.

Under these circumstances, ordinary people might not know Jiang Tian's exploits, but they all knew that Yunhai had produced a killer, a monster.

The old days were gone forever. Previously, the police were easy prey for them, but now, since Jiang Tian's sudden emergence, everything had changed. It was completely different from before.

As a result, one powerful force after another fell to Jiang Tian's hands. Not long ago, they even heard that the city police had arrested over a thousand people in one go, wiping out an entire village under Jiang Tian's leadership.

An entire village, a large village of several thousand people, was almost completely wiped out.

A complete sweep.

It's said that even the Municipal Public Security Bureau is overflowing, and the branch bureaus and detention centers in various places are practically bursting at the seams, mainly due to the sheer number of people.

In Yunhai, most detention centers have been completely full in a short period of time.

So much so that sometimes, trials are conducted directly through a simplified process, followed by sentencing. Those facing death row are categorized first, then tried quickly, and then executed in batches. Even executions are now scheduled.

When they arrived, some even joked that even executions in Yunhai are now scheduled. Whether this is a good thing or a bad thing for death row inmates is unclear. Waiting in line for several days might extend their lives, but the psychological torment during those days is probably far more terrifying than immediate execution; the agony of waiting to die is horrible.

From now on, we must be careful and not do anything too outrageous, especially regarding our crimes. We must curb our activities recently and avoid attracting the attention of that ruthless Jiang Tian.

But the outcome was completely unexpected.

Zeng Wei was handcuffed, and a pistol was even pulled from his waist.

As for why Zeng Wei didn't draw his gun earlier, or rather, why he had the time to draw it and why he didn't fight to the death, it was because Zeng Wei understood one thing.

If the person in front of him were someone else, Zeng Wei would have drawn his gun given the slightest opportunity. But this person was Jiang Tian. A person's reputation precedes them; that's the meaning of the saying. He might be able to draw his gun on others, but this was Jiang Tian. To draw a gun in front of Jiang Tian—think of all the rumors surrounding him.

In the end, Zeng Wei didn't have the courage to draw his gun, nor did he do it, nor did he have the guts to do so.

"In the entire Yunhai, there are so many criminal gangs, so many people who seek riches through danger. Many people are willing to take risks. Why target me? Why target us?!"

Zeng Wei stood there, unwilling to move an inch, his emotions nearing a breakdown.

That's right.

Just as Zeng Wei said.

There are far too many criminal groups operating in Yunhai; what has been uncovered so far is merely the tip of the iceberg.

In other words, Yunhai has become rotten to the core without anyone realizing it.

If Yunhai still had any chance of change, why would Jiang Tian, ​​before his transmigration, have tried several times to investigate the situation in Beijing before immediately sending in the military without hesitation?

The most terrifying thing was that the most absurd thing that happened after the military's intervention was that, as the investigation progressed, Beijing, which initially only wanted to control the situation, discovered something terrible—a complete explosion. After understanding only the tip of the iceberg, they decisively and completely cut off Yunhai's entire development and placed the entire city under strict control.

Because it was rotten to the core, from top to bottom. After so many years of development, Yunhai's crime rate had far exceeded anyone's imagination.

Under these circumstances,

the higher-ups also chose to act decisively at the first opportunity.

They completely abandoned Yunhai.

Currently, Jiang Tian has taken comprehensive control over Yunhai and even launched a devastating crackdown on the entire criminal group in Yunhai.

However.

The final problem remains obvious.

As Zeng Wei said, even without the criminal groups Jiang Tian swiftly dismantled in Yunhai, what remains—or rather, what Jiang Tian painstakingly eliminated—is merely the tip of the iceberg.

Here.

This situation is good for Jiang Tian because, for capable people, this is a paradise for accumulating merit. In peacetime, obtaining a first-class merit is incredibly difficult; in the mainland, it's extremely rare, almost nonexistent.

But in Yunhai, Jiang Tian only needs to make a slight move to earn a first-class merit.

It's practically like he's churning it out.

He was grinding like crazy.

It was like a max-level player wanting to rack up merits; they needed to defeat a boss. But in the mainland, finding a boss was incredibly difficult, entirely dependent on luck.

However, in the Cloud Sea, they were everywhere. Max-level players could just wander around, casually defeating a couple of bosses, and that would be a first-class merit boss.

The merits were incredibly satisfying.

Therefore, Jiang Tian wasn't just enjoying the situation; he had essentially made the Cloud Sea his private domain.

His private domain for accumulating merits.

Here, Jiang Tian would accumulate terrifying resources, laying an invincible foundation for his path to the heavens—a foundation strong enough to support him on that path.

"Heh, Zeng Wei, is it? I already saw your file before you came. Everyone else was shouting that they were innocent, demanding I produce evidence. Only you said something like that, which proves you're a smart man." Jiang Tian waved his hand.

This signaled the two special agents holding Zeng Wei to stop.

In Jiang Tian's presence, or rather, here, Jiang Tian was the boss.

Jiang Tian had unlimited autonomy; he could do whatever he wanted.

These special agents were merely cooperating with Jiang Tian, ​​or rather, with anti-drug operations, following orders.

They weren't required to speak.

"I only want to know one thing right now: there are too many criminal organizations in Yunhai. Why are they targeting me, targeting Huayu Group?" Zeng Wei's eyes were bloodshot.

His face still bore the bloody handprint of Jiang Tian's slap.

Zeng Wei is right. There are too many criminal gangs in Yunhai. Although Huayu Group has assets approaching 10 billion yuan, it's really nothing compared to the overall size of Yunhai.

That's why Yunhai is almost a first-tier city, with its development ranking among the top in the country. However, the government has very little money; ultimately, it's the lower-level criminal gangs who are siphoning it off.

These are the criminal gangs operating extremely profitable businesses.



Chapter 612 One Yuan Transfer, Billions of Assets Sold Off


While Huayu Group might be powerful enough among these criminal groups, it wasn't particularly outstanding. In fact, its recent wealth was simply due to its own factories operating covertly, giving it a natural advantage. They could guarantee they wouldn't alert the police. So why had the police suddenly targeted them?

Zeng Wei was utterly baffled.

"Speaking of which, I should thank your Huayu Group's General Manager Chen's outstanding son, Chen Xu. He's a genius, or rather, your Huayu Group is incredibly audacious. Today, I'll expose you. There was a former leader in the Municipal Public Security Bureau with whom I had a very good relationship and shared hardships. He sacrificed his life in a major case recently, but he also made a significant contribution. He left behind an only child, who was then targeted by that genius Chen Xu, who wanted to humiliate him, even using drugs and other shameless methods. Your Huayu Group deserves to die." Jiang Tian's voice gradually hardened and became a roar as he spoke.

Furious.

This matter truly enraged Jiang Tian.

And did he think it was over? No, far from enough. For Jiang Tian, ​​this was far from enough; no, this was only the beginning.

One Huayu Group wasn't enough.

Moreover...

Actually, Jiang Tian was speaking out now for another reason.

That was to tell everyone to spread the news completely.

To provoke the families of the police.

If you're capable of handling this, or even if you think you've escaped unscathed and feel smug, you're sorely mistaken.

The grievances have their source, and the debts have their debtors.

The fate of the Huayu Group will be the complete annihilation of its top management.

It's to tell you all.

Some people are not to be trifled with. Anyone who dares to reach out, anyone who dares to touch them, will be uprooted by Jiang Tian.

"Chen Xu?"

Zeng Wei's eyes practically spit fire, his gaze wide.

But no, the people at the scene fell silent, carefully reviewing the whole incident, and suddenly realized how innocent they were.

It was clearly Chen Xu who started this mess, and the worst part is, Chen Qiang's father actually tricked them, calling them to come over and then cooperating with the police to arrest all of them.

Where's their sense of justice?

Chen Qiang.

"Chen Qiang, you'll die a horrible death!"

"You bastard Chen Qiang, you beast!"

"The grievance has its source, the debt has its debtor. It was Chen Qiang who provoked you. Why are we being dragged into their father-son affair?!"

"That's right, that's right, Brother Jiang, we're innocent. If you want to find someone, find them, father and son. It has nothing to do with us. We didn't even know about this."

They were furious.

They were all furious.

Damn it, just think how aggrieved this is!

Look at the whole thing, it was Chen Qiang and his son who provoked it from beginning to end.

"Do you think Chen Qiang and his son are enough after doing such a thing? Far from enough. All of you combined barely qualify. After all, you beasts are just worthless trash, worthless lives. You really don't think one person can compare to our people, do you? Even combined, you're barely enough," Jiang Tian sneered mercilessly.

*Pfft!*

As soon as Jiang Tian finished speaking, Zeng Wei instantly spat out a mouthful of blood.

His entire expression was extremely listless.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​Zeng Wei hesitated, wanting to say something, but then realized that at this moment, it was all about winning or losing.

At this moment, Zeng Wei was like a fallen savannah king, an old lion utterly stripped of all its power. Yes, in his youth, how could he care who the opponent was, whether they were a worthy adversary? He'd be full of passion, drawing his gun without hesitation. Why was he now like this, lacking even the courage to draw his weapon?

In the end, he was old.

He no longer had the courage of his youth.

"Officer Jiang, is it? By now, Chen Qiang must have confessed everything to you. No matter how you intend to judge us, we accept it. After all, we've committed too many sins in this life." After saying this, Zeng Wei seemed to age a thousand times over in an instant.

As the saying goes, a dying man's words are kind. Zeng Wei, seeing all this, suddenly realized that all the so-called wealth and glory, all the enjoyment, are things you can't take with you when you die.

Now.

At the last moment, they suddenly realized that all the money they had made, even at the cost of resorting to any means necessary, was ultimately useless.

"Wait, since you've all confessed, I'd like to ask for your help. After all, you might get a lighter sentence, and you might even make it out of prison in your lifetime."

The group being escorted out of the conference room stopped in their tracks.

These words weren't even spoken by Jiang Tian.

Moreover,

the voice even came from outside the room.

The group, who had completely lost their fighting spirit, couldn't help but look up upon hearing this. For a moment, they thought they were hallucinating.

Chen Qiang.

After a moment of confusion, the thirty-odd men exploded.

This bastard! If he had cooperated with the police in arresting them, that would have been one thing. After all, he confessed under interrogation, and they could understand. At worst, they would have cursed him for being a spineless coward and misjudging someone.

But...

What made them furious was...

The person who provoked Jiang Tian, ​​dragging the entire Huayu Group and them down with them, was none other than Chen Qiang's son, Chen Xu.

This father and son, one causing trouble, the other providing the confession, completely betrayed them, one after the other. While they were counting their money at home, they had already been sold out.

“Chen Qiang, die!”

“You beast, how dare you show your face here!”

“Kill this beast!”

“Damn it, you still have the nerve to wear a suit? You don’t deserve it!”

“This beast doesn’t deserve it at all.”

“Why is the police treating him differently? This beast has committed the most crimes, why haven’t you put him in shackles and a prison uniform?”

“A beast in a suit.”

Insults, frenzied insults.

More than thirty of Chen Qiang’s former brothers, except for a few, almost all exploded, hurling the most vile insults at Chen Qiang.

Chen Qiang wasn't annoyed. Seeing everyone's powerless rage, Zeng Wei, who was in the lead, still maintained some rationality. Although he was angry, he didn't vent his anger in vain, seemingly waiting for Chen Qiang to continue.

"I've prepared over thirty share transfer agreements here. I hope you can cooperate with me. After all, if you don't cooperate, your shares will be confiscated anyway. It's better to cooperate and get it all done at once, avoiding another round of transfers. My daughter is prepared to use 30,000 yuan, regardless of the number of shares, 1,000 yuan per person, to acquire all the shares of Huayu Group you hold." Chen Qiang's words were shocking.

"This 1,000 yuan per person is my living expenses for you old brothers while you're in detention."

Chen Qiang still had some conscience.

But the thirty-odd old brothers present, including Zeng Wei, who was known for his composure, were speechless at this moment. How could there be such a shameless person in the world?

Everyone's eyes widened, their eyeballs almost bulging out.



Chapter 613 Everything is settled, the beginning of controlling the sea of ​​clouds.


It exploded. Completely exploded.

Everyone present, to varying degrees, held shares worth at least several hundred million, with some, like Zeng Wei, even owning shares worth over two billion.

This was no exaggeration; after all, they had all followed the startup group, and now the group's total assets were worth tens of billions.

These tens of billions weren't listed due to the company's nature, but if they had, the share price could have tripled, starting at three hundred billion, given the Huayu Group's proven profitability and its highly lucrative businesses.

“You're dreaming.”

“Are you crazy?”

“What do you mean? What are you trying to do?”

“Take it all by force?”

These people stared wide-eyed.

At this point, Jiang Tian didn't even need to say anything.

Standing to the side, Chen Qiang sighed slightly. "Damn it," he thought, "I've spent half my life scheming to get my shares back, calculating and plotting, but in the end, I never expected to get them back so easily and dramatically."

As for the alleged violation...

What a joke.

What violation?

It was all part of the anti-drug operation, part of the organization's mission.

Both Jiang Tian and Chen Qiang were just cooperating; the real beneficiary was Chen Qiang's daughter, Chen Liting.

In other words, even if the company doesn't go public, Chen Liting easily controls assets worth tens of billions. If it does go public, her assets could reach 30 to 50 billion. Even if Huayu Group doesn't have its shady businesses, if its existing businesses expand further, following Yunhai's development, the future value and potential of Huayu Group will be immeasurable.

And all of this...

Although Chen Liting controlled the company, to some extent, she was Jiang Tian's devoted fan, making her essentially no different from someone controlled by Jiang Tian.

Even if Chen Liting had ulterior motives, it wouldn't be possible to hide it from Jiang Tian. He could easily discover it, and taking action would be effortless. Going against the state apparatus was simply impossible, and Jiang Tian, ​​in the eyes of these people, represented the state apparatus.

Chen Liting was a smart woman, and Jiang Tian liked dealing with smart people.

"I agree."

Although everyone wanted nothing more than to kill Chen Qiang—if looks could kill, Chen Qiang would have died countless times—to everyone's surprise, Zeng Wei, after his initial rage, seemed to lose all his energy in a short time.

He also nodded in agreement, took the share transfer agreement from Chen Qiang, and signed his name.

They were all smart people; there was no need to be too obvious.

Although Chen Qiang's actions seemed excessive, even a fool knew that everything he did was ultimately controlled by Jiang Tian. In other words, it was Jiang Tian's will, and thus represented the will of the state apparatus.

A bitter smile crossed Zeng Wei's aged face in a daze.

Yes!

He had fought his entire life, risking his life his whole life.

After writing his name, Zeng Wei seemed to have completely lost all will to resist. In other words, he had given up, or perhaps he had come to terms with it. To everyone's astonishment, Zeng Wei looked at Jiang Tian, ​​a bitter smile on his face, and spoke words full of hidden meaning.

This also sent a jolt through everyone present, and they all conceded.

“We fight our whole lives, risking our lives, and in the end, we can't withstand a single word from you, even a single glance. The wealth we've fought our whole lives to build can be wiped out by a single look from you.”

What's the use of being successful?

In front of Jiang Tian, ​​in front of those who hold power, what are they?

At this moment, Zeng Wei understood.

No matter how successful you are, it's useless. If someone wants to take you down, it's just a matter of a word. They don't even have the right to struggle.

Zeng Wei once thought he was successful enough.

After all, Zeng Wei thought about it carefully. In Yunhai, having reached his level, even nationwide, he was probably among the most powerful. But what was he compared to Jiang Tian? Jiang Tian could easily take him down, and Zeng Wei could do it with ease. The entire Huayu Group could be turned upside down with a single thought from Jiang Tian.

That was the difference.

“No matter what you think, if you had walked the straight path, would you be where you are today? Even if I had immense power, as long as you were upright and had nothing to hide, I wouldn't touch you. So, if you have the opportunity in the future, walk an honorable path,” Jiang Tian said calmly.

However, he said this on the surface; no one knew what Jiang Tian was really thinking.

Or rather…

Jiang Tian was just saying it on the surface. Everyone present was a shrewd old fox; they all understood the true situation.

Clean or not, would the final outcome change?

After Chen Xu provoked them, could they, even if they were completely clean, escape this calamity?

Perhaps.

But whether they can still possess this wealth and status, no one knows; it all depends on Jiang Tian's whim.

What is this? This is power.

Zhao Wei and Li Zijin watched from behind Jiang Tian, ​​their blood boiling.

These people, each one a prominent figure in Yunhai, are now nothing!

Before Jiang Tian, ​​they are utterly insignificant.

With a single word, they have no power to resist.

This is power.

Even Zhao Wei, from the capital, felt this terrifying power at this moment.

Perhaps, this is the truth.

With Zeng Wei leading the way, the others understood that in this situation, there was no chance of resistance.

Since that's the case, it's better to try to lessen their punishment.

Even if they don't cooperate, for Jiang Tian, ​​it might just be a slightly more complicated procedure, but the outcome will absolutely not change.

And that's how it is now.

Since that's the case, why not perform better?

The only thing they couldn't accept was why Chen Qiang ended up getting everything.

One by one, more than thirty people signed their names, then were hooded by special agents. Only when the hoods were on did many of them realize, in a daze, that their lives were over.

Their wealth and glory had vanished completely.

But the price they paid for it all was too late.

"Why did you leave us here?"

"Officer Jiang, what's this?"

"We don't know why Officer Jiang wants to see us?"



Chapter 614 An Old Friend: The Beginning of All This Misfortune


Inside the conference room, the crowd gradually dispersed. Even Chen Qiang left under the surveillance of special agents. Of course, he didn't completely leave; he only went with Chen Liting to prepare for taking over and understanding the entire Huayu Group.

Going to Chen Qiang's office was also a way for Jiang Tian to learn from his experience and the situation of the Huayu Group.

Jiang Tian's idea was that the entire Huayu Group couldn't be in chaos, at least not outwardly.

At the same time, all criminal issues, including those related to crime and illicit activities, should be temporarily disclosed and suspended. Relevant personnel should cooperate with the police to control them and await subsequent trials. In short, no one should escape.

Given the scale of the Huayu Group, one can imagine the extent of the criminal activity, including all those involved, their networks, and those who bribed officials.

Of course, these follow-up matters would be handled by specialized personnel for investigation and arrests. Anti-corruption issues were already beyond Jiang Tian's authority.

These matters were essentially no longer Jiang Tian's concern.

Jiang Tian's main task was to ensure a smooth takeover for Huayu Group. However, the handling of the criminal issues needed to be cautious; he couldn't alert the criminals or let other criminal groups realize there was more to the story. In short, on the surface, everything remained the same, but secretly, the operation was gradually cleaned up.

As for the meeting room, four people remained. These four were all relatively old and considered Chen Qiang's old comrades. However, unlike the others, these four possessed a strong business acumen.

They were all nominally in charge of these businesses and industries, and initially lacked understanding of the gray market. They had committed some crimes; while not entirely clean, they were certainly not guilty of countless offenses. Even if they were arrested, their crimes wouldn't be particularly serious, and at most, they would only serve a few years in prison.

“I’m keeping you here. Your intelligence should be enough to understand what I mean. You’re different from those old buddies who’ve been involved in Chen Qiang’s crimes since childhood. You are all top students, and even joined the group later in life. If my investigation is correct, two of you graduated with degrees in Business Management from Yunnan University, and the other two are PhDs from Beijing. You are all highly educated individuals who haven’t been heavily involved in the crimes; at most, you just withheld information. That’s forgivable.”

Jiang Tian smiled slightly, looking at the four men before him, and invited them to sit down.

The four men exchanged glances.

“Therefore, I hope you can continue to cooperate with Li Ting, allowing her to quickly take control of Huayu Group and ensuring a smooth transition without any disruptions,” Jiang Tian said quietly.

Huayu Group actually owns an entertainment company.

The manager is one of these four men, and there are even several newly emerging stars in the entertainment industry.

In terms of future prospects, there are certainly opportunities. In fact, Yunhai boasts an abundance of scenic wonders and natural beauty. If the opportunity arises, Jiang Tian even envisions building a film and television base there.

Of course, these are somewhat unrealistic dreams, but Jiang Tian feels that they can be realized through the Huayu Group.

After all, Jiang Tian's base of influence in Yunhai is already formidable. Barring unforeseen circumstances, his future appointments will likely remain in Yunhai.

Besides, Huayu Group's other businesses include entertainment clubs and bars, including the Southeast Road Bar, the most vibrant bar in the entire Southeast region.

Many wealthy individuals and criminals in the Southeast region frequent the Southeast Road Bar.

Furthermore, if Yunhai becomes the economic center of Southeast China, like a pearl of the East China Sea, attracting countless wealthy individuals, these entertainment and business clubs will become primary intelligence gathering centers.

In other words,

Those underworld bosses might be discussing illegal activities while getting a foot massage, and their conversations could end up on the police's desks.

Imagine how much crime will decrease in Yunhai.

As the cost of crime increases,

the crime rate will naturally decrease until, ultimately,

Yunhai might become the safest place.

Of course, this is just Jiang Tian's vision.

After all.

“Officer Jiang, you may not know this, but while Huayu Group's entertainment clubs are very popular, they only occupy a portion of Yunhai. Completely controlling Yunhai is simply impossible. Not to mention anything else, just look at Yunhai's other entertainment group, Yunya Group; their bar and entertainment businesses have already completely surpassed our Huayu Group.” This was the fourth person among the four after understanding Jiang Tian's thoughts.

These four people didn't harbor any resentment. As they'd said before, they were just managing and developing the company. Although they knew it was illegal, they rarely participated, completely turning a blind eye and pretending not to know. They were simply doing their own thing. To put it bluntly, although they joined Yunhai Group halfway through, while there was some affection, profit was the primary motivation.

For them, as long as their income didn't decrease, working for someone else didn't matter. In fact, working for Officer Jiang wouldn't mean constantly living in fear of being killed.

Moreover, with Jiang Tian's background...

It was hard to imagine how far the company could develop in the future.

At that time, with higher profits, their commissions and bonuses would naturally be substantial.

“Yunya Group, I'll investigate that later. But as I said before, the four of you control the most important industry of Huayu Group. For now, it's about a smooth transition, allowing Liting to successfully take over the entire Huayu Group. During this period, Chen Qiang will fully cooperate, and I hope you will also fully cooperate with them.” Jiang Tian stood up.

“Officer Jiang, rest assured, we'll go through fire and water.”

“That's right, we all have the organization in our hearts, but we haven't had the opportunity before. We hope that if there's a chance, Officer Jiang can introduce us so we can join the organization.”

“That's right, that's right, we're devoted to the organization, but we just haven't had the opportunity before.”

These four were also intelligent.

Being intelligent and educated, their words were pleasant to hear. Jiang Tian smiled and nodded.

Just as he finished speaking, Jiang Tian's phone rang.

After taking out his phone and glancing at it, Jiang Tian was slightly surprised.

There was no one else there.

The caller to Jiang Tian was Han Zi.

Jiang Tian looked at the caller ID on his phone, his brow furrowing slightly.

This name was very special.

How special?

It was arguably the deepest and most important name in Jiang Tian's heart; more seriously, it was the beginning of all his misfortunes.



Chapter 615 A ​​National Goddess of Golden Flower Level, the Girl Deepest Within


The tragedy of Jiang Tian's past life actually began with Wang Yifei, or rather, it was the deepest secret hidden within Jiang Tian's heart: a girl had always resided deep within his mind.

Or perhaps, everyone goes through adolescence and youth, and during this stage, it's inevitable that young girls harbor romantic feelings and poetic thoughts; the same is true for teenage boys.

When Jiang Tian first entered high school, an extremely beautiful girl appeared at his school. This girl attracted a lot of attention from the moment she appeared, and all the boys in his class blushed and dared not utter a sound when they saw her.

In his past life, Jiang Tian was similarly silent.

Everyone has their own perspective and way of seeing. Later, after that girl transferred schools, Jiang Tian's unrequited love finally ended.

But while it seemed to have ended on the surface, it lingered in his mind for a long time. Then, Jiang Tian was pleasantly surprised to find that he saw the girl's shadow in Wang Yifei.

Regardless of whether others noticed anything alike, or even felt the two bore no resemblance whatsoever, Jiang Tian saw a single resemblance in Wang Yifei. This slight similarity completely terrified him, setting him on a path of no return.

The phone call... listening to the voice on the other end...

"Long time no see, it's Han Zi, do you remember me?"

Jiang Tian's gaze was filled with astonishment.

Actually, Jiang Tian hadn't entirely ignored the possibility of a chance, but then he overheard a classmate familiar with Han Zi saying that she had transferred to an arts and sports school, seemingly preparing to become a star.

In short, it was many years ago.

Strictly speaking, it was the beginning of Jiang Tian's misfortune.

After all, without this girl's appearance, how could Jiang Tian have become so obsessed, ultimately being so easily manipulated by Wang Yifei?

Of course, blaming her wasn't entirely fair, because Jiang Tian awkwardly realized that everything back then seemed to have been wishful thinking on his part.

“I’ve kept your phone number.” Jiang Tian was silent for a moment, then his expression calmed down.

Now, very few things could stir Jiang Tian’s emotions, but this girl had indeed shaken him, though only for a very short time.

Jiang Tian was no longer the young boy he once was, nor the boy who had just entered high school and was instantly captivated by a single glance.

Now, Jiang Tian, ​​while not a seasoned veteran, had seen all kinds of girls.

Past memories were, after all, the past.

“You kept it, so why haven’t you called me during this time?” Han Zi asked, her tone somewhat surprised.

She genuinely seemed surprised.

In truth, Jiang Tian was also somewhat confused, because at this point in his previous life, his experiences were tragic; he had completely fallen into Wang Yifei’s trap and was living a hellish life.

His family had long since fallen on hard times; his parents had suffered one misfortune after another. Jiang Tian had somewhat forgotten whether he had received that phone call back then; perhaps he had, but he didn't remember.

However, Jiang Tian definitely had no recollection of answering that call in his previous life.

"Han Zi, is something wrong?" Jiang Tian asked.

Five or six years had actually passed in the blink of an eye since then. Han Zi had transferred to a specialized arts and sports high school, and she and Jiang Tian had long lost contact—for several years without any communication.

Therefore, Jiang Tian didn't really experience any particular emotional fluctuation.

For Han Zi, it was five or six years, but for Jiang Tian, ​​it was almost twenty years in both his past and present lives. Time was enough to fade everything, especially since, after his rebirth, were any of the women around Jiang Tian simple?

Whether it was Gao Yangyang, Qin Shiyu, or Mu Ya, there were far too many.

These women were all top-tier; even the least attractive were still stunning beauties, not to mention Su Tong, who was infatuated with Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian had truly lost interest in rekindling old flames.

"Can't I contact you for no reason? I heard you became a policeman and even arrested one of our old teachers?" Han Zi inquired.

Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

"Jiang Tian, ​​don't misunderstand. I was just asking. The main thing is, I contacted some old classmates and found out about your situation."

Han Zi quickly explained, seemingly afraid Jiang Tian would misunderstand.

Of course, Jiang Tian knew this wasn't a secret anymore. Back at the class reunion at the karaoke bar, Jiang Tian had pulled off a stunt and taken down a bunch of gangsters.

It caused a huge uproar among the classmates.

"Thanks for your concern, but I have work to do. If you have time, let's meet and catch up." Jiang Tian glanced down at the four people across the conference room, who were staring at him obediently, not daring to utter a word.

He still had some things to say.

This was a matter of utmost importance, concerning Jiang Tian's grand future plans, and no mishaps could be tolerated.

"No, I need your help with something," Han Zi said hurriedly.

This caused a hint of displeasure to appear on Jiang Tian's face. Help, pulling strings—he especially loathed people who hadn't been in contact for a long time suddenly calling to ask for favors.

The key was that there were truly the most such people in the world.

Especially some of his former high school and university classmates, seeing Jiang Tian's current rise to prominence, were contacting him in a manner bordering on harassment. Jiang Tian had only one approach to those people: block them all.

"A small favor is appreciated, but a large one breeds resentment," as the saying goes. After Jiang Tian blocked them, they continued to badmouth him behind his back. Of course, there were many such people, and as long as Jiang Tian didn't witness it, he generally ignored them.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to hang up, Han Zi hurriedly said, "Jiang Tian, ​​don't misunderstand. I'm not asking you for favors. It's just that I feel very unsafe lately. My assistant seems to be using heroin. I'm really panicked, but I'm afraid to call the police rashly, and even more afraid that this assistant will endanger me. I happened to hear that you're involved in this kind of work, and you're also a narcotics officer, so I wanted to talk to you about it."

So that's how it is. Jiang Tian's face gradually relaxed.

After all, although past memories are long blurred, they are always the most perfect and flawless memories deep in his heart.

In Jiang Tian's mind, Han Zi's smile was the most perfect, unforgettable, and almost, this beautiful image was completely shattered.

"You should have said so earlier. But if that's the case, why didn't you fire this assistant?" Jiang Tian asked curiously.

He knew that Han Zi had embarked on the path of becoming a star, but Jiang Tian didn't really know much about it.

However, in his previous life, when Jiang Tian saw her, this girl had already become a rising star of the new era.

She was even called a national goddess.



Chapter 616 Jiang Sir's debt of gratitude is never fully repaid; salute to the officer.


Back then, the difference between her and Jiang Tian was like heaven and earth. So, in her past life, even until Jiang Tian's death, she never saw him again.

The gap between them was simply too vast.

One was like a swan in the sky, while she wasn't even a toad, and yet a female toad was relentlessly clinging to her.

How could mere words describe the unbearable pain?

However, past and present lives are reversed.

“It’s not that simple, Officer Jiang. You don’t know, we celebrities are all signed contracts of servitude. Our assistants are all sent by the company. Big stars are alright, but we small stars don’t even have a voice. Our voices are less powerful than an assistant’s.” Han Zi’s voice was filled with bitterness.

She hadn’t been so full of youthful arrogance when she entered the industry. Only after joining did she suddenly realize this.

All her previous thoughts had become so naive.

Ridiculous.

They seemed utterly insignificant.

She thought being a celebrity was easy, but signing with a company revealed it was practically a contract of servitude.

She had even witnessed some of her seniors betray their profession for fame, which terrified her, but it was too late for regrets.

"I see. Can you come to the Municipal Public Security Bureau? It's getting late. Come to the Municipal Bureau tomorrow morning, and we can discuss this in detail," Jiang Tian said.

Other matters were hard to discuss, but when it came to drug-related crimes, Jiang Tian perked up. After all, it was his job.

Moreover, many seemingly minor and ordinary crimes often conceal terrifying major cases when investigated further.

"Can you come now? My assistant seems to have taken drugs again. I feel she's not acting normally; she's stayed here and hasn't left. I'm a little scared," Han Zi said.

Jiang Tian frowned slightly, already wondering if there was some kind of scheme involved.

This wasn't Jiang Tian overthinking, or rather, being suspicious. The main issue was that at his level, countless criminal groups were constantly watching him, and one wrong move could mean facing their terrifying killing intent.

Just as Jiang Tian was thinking this,

a frantic banging on the door suddenly came from the other end of the phone.

Furthermore, there were shouts of "Open the door!" after the banging.

"Give me your address now, I'll come over," Jiang Tian said.

"Okay, I'll send it to you right now."

"By the way, you're in Yunhai, right?" Jiang Tian knew that celebrities were always traveling all over the country, so he asked.

If she wasn't in Yunhai, Jiang Tian would have to seek assistance from the local police.

"Yes, I'm in Yunhai, at Zishan Apartment," Han Zi quickly replied.

"Okay, make sure you're safe, I'm coming right away." Jiang Tian didn't hang up.

“Officer Jiang…”

Seeing Jiang Tian turn to leave, the four men at the scene grew anxious.

Jiang Tian glanced back, thought for a moment, and said, “Here’s what we’ll do. For now, you continue operating as usual. Zhao Wei, you take the economic comrades, and each of you will have one follow them.”

Jiang Tian didn’t even try to hide anything from them.

He openly told them they were being monitored.

As long as they weren’t fools and didn’t do anything stupid, these men could even protect their safety.

"Boss, where are you going? I'll see you off." Zhao Wei followed closely behind Jiang Tian, ​​not wanting to fall behind.

After all, he could never repay Boss Jiang's kindness.

"I have some personal matters to attend to. You stay here and control the situation. Also, prepare a report, recording everything that happened tonight in detail, and hand it over to Minister Tan."

Logically, he should report to Han Xian, but that's ridiculous.

Han Xian is nothing!

Now that everything is out in the open, everyone knows Han Xian is targeting him. For Jiang Tian to foolishly report to him would be utterly foolish.

Besides, who knows what Han Xian's future holds? No one knows how the higher-ups will handle him, or even if he'll still be the bureau chief.

"Yes, sir!" Zhao Wei nodded quickly.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly and then left.

It was already 8 PM.

Jiang Tian put his phone on speakerphone in the car and drove his Phaeton. A roar of the accelerator filled the air as the V8 engine unleashed its power, propelling him through the sea of ​​clouds.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian's license plate wasn't ordinary; it was registered with the traffic police—in other words, a special official vehicle license plate.

He sped along the highway.

Unexpectedly, Zishan Apartments, which appeared to be in Zishan, just like Jiang Tian's Zishan Villa, and indeed was, was located in Zishan, but Zishan was vast.

It covered a wide area.

The Zishan Apartments were more than ten miles away from the Zishan Villa, even as the crow flies; they weren't adjacent.

Moreover...

When Jiang Tian arrived at Zishan Apartments, he looked up and glanced around, unable to help but frown slightly.

The environment here was too chaotic.

Jiang Tian only gave it a brief look.

Although Zishan Apartments was also in Zishan, it was a large apartment complex.

Perhaps during its early development, Yunhai built a number of affordable apartments to accommodate the increasing number of migrant workers.

Zishan Apartments was one of them.

The Zishan Apartment complex was quite large, consisting of five buildings. Most importantly, it was chaotic, with the ground almost entirely filled with parked electric bikes, bicycles, and other vehicles.

Cars were also crammed into the apartment entrances.

The entire complex looked poorly designed, but of course, given its affordable nature, it was tolerable.

“I’ve arrived.”

Jiang Tian reached the apartment entrance and squeezed through the gaps between the electric bikes into the elevator.

At that moment, the banging on the door on the phone gradually subsided.

Before Han Zi could even breathe a sigh of relief and say anything, a furious voice suddenly came from the phone.

“You’re in there! I heard you in there! Open the door! Open the door now, you bitch! You don’t know what’s good for you!”

It was the sound of a man’s rage and banging on the door.

This almost completely shattered Jiang Tian's worldview. How could a minor celebrity's assistant treat a star like this?

Who's the boss here?

And what's going on?

"He's not gone, he's still here. Be careful when you come over," Han Zi said, her voice relatively calm.

Or rather, Jiang Tian felt this wasn't the first time this had happened.

At that moment, once inside the elevator, the signal was lost.



Chapter 617 I'm dumbfounded. How am I supposed to put this knife away?


What intrigued Jiang Tian even more now was what kind of entertainment company was so powerful that it dared to treat a celebrity like this, especially a mere assistant, with such lawlessness?

Most importantly, this happened after he had used heroin.

This truly piqued Jiang Tian's interest. After all, for him, the August 1st Film Festival was practically in his grasp. He was expected to travel to Beijing in the next few days, meet with the bigwigs, and then it would be a matter of going through the motions before he could acquire the festival.

However, during this time, he needed to build momentum.

The initial plan was to first create momentum for Jiang Tian, ​​to bring him from the shadows to the public eye, to make him a trendsetter.

But plans don't always go as expected. Before he could even begin the online campaign, a large number of malicious individuals had already begun smearing Jiang Tian online, even contacting news channels.

Yucheng TV and other media outlets were also publishing negative reports about him. Coupled with some malicious instigation, Jiang Tian was now almost a universally condemned online pariah.

If Jiang Tian were to continue his online propaganda efforts at this juncture, taking a positive stance, it might inadvertently cause a huge uproar.

Moreover, all sorts of conspiracy theories would emerge.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian's status as a second-generation rich kid—albeit a minor one—would be enough for many conspiracy theorists to launch a vicious smear campaign against him.

That would be counterproductive, and could even cause the situation to spiral out of control.

Therefore, a revised plan is to simply let Jiang Tian emerge through the August 1st incident. As for the current online public opinion, there's no need to control it.

Let them smear him online as they please.

But will it have any impact? Those who fear Jiang Tian will remain fearful, those who hate him will remain hateful, and those who support him will remain supportive, regardless of the online attacks.

Even the top executives of Huayu Group haven't cared about or paid attention to those rumors.

They understand Jiang Tian's actions far better than those foolishly swayed by the narrative.

Do nothing then. No matter how harsh the criticism, the moment Jiang Tian stands on the podium on August 1st, solemnly accepting the medal before everyone on the evening news,

all the rumors will crumble on their own.

In the meantime, this whole affair is more like a spice to Jiang Tian.

It's a welcome respite from the recent stress and numerous cases, a chance for him to relax.

As for the entertainment company's background, connections, etc.

What a joke.

These things are nothing but paper tigers to Jiang Tian, ​​who now possesses invincible power. The Huayu Group, a multi-billion dollar conglomerate, is something Jiang Tian can easily destroy. Even without evidence, he can wipe them out based on the information he has. A small entertainment company is nothing compared to that.

Even a multi-billion dollar entertainment company, if faced with Jiang Tian, ​​could be wiped out if you actually used heroin—not just a lowly assistant, but the entire boss, even the chairman.

This was an absolute red line.

Anyone who dared to touch heroin, whether buying or selling it, was crossing an absolute red line.

Soon.

Before the elevator doors even opened,

Jiang Tian heard a loud banging on the door.

When the elevator doors opened,

the loud noise became even clearer before Jiang Tian.

At that moment,

he saw a dilapidated apartment door, a rusty security door.

The inner door was tightly shut.

However, this rusty security door didn't look very sturdy either; it appeared to be on the verge of collapse after being frantically pounded on.

It looked as if it would be kicked flat at any moment. The neighbors on this floor, the two houses on either side, had their doors open, peeking out secretly.

At first glance, the apartment whose door had been banged on was the middle one, followed by the one on the left, right by the elevator. Jiang Tian got off the elevator and was just a step away from their door.

Most importantly, this family consisted of a mother and daughter, one looking to be in their mid-twenties, the other looking like they were under ten.

As the shadowy figure approached, it turned and saw Jiang Tian.

"Shh!!!" The older woman quickly hissed at Jiang Tian, ​​trying to quiet him down.

Jiang Tian, ​​seeing that Han Zi seemed unharmed, wasn't in a hurry.

"Don't go over there now, it's dangerous. That person is going crazy again," the woman said, her lips a vibrant red.

"Isn't this the first time?" Jiang Tian asked.

"While not every day, it happens every two or three days, and at the same time every day. Young man, it looks very dangerous over there." The woman noticed Jiang Tian's youth; he looked like an ordinary college student, young and with a very sunny disposition.

This instantly garnered the woman considerable goodwill.

“This person should be considered a disturbance, right? Hasn’t anyone stepped forward?” Jiang Tian’s eyes flickered, sensing something was amiss.

It’s been a long time.

So, does that mean it’s really as Han Zi said?

Jiang Tian fell into deep thought.

“Of course no one stepped forward. The young man living in the east wing initially said a few words, and then this guy went crazy, pulled out a knife, and chased after us. After that, no one dared to mess with him.” The woman made no attempt to hide anything.

“No one called the police?” Jiang Tian was shocked.

If the police were called, this should be easily resolved, right?

“Of course they called the police, but after the police questioned her, the young girl whose door was banged on denied it. Although I said a few words of fairness, she didn’t say anything when her door was banged on, so what could we say?” The woman spoke with a face full of anger.

After all, this happened every now and then, and always in the middle of the night.

Does that mean everyone else won't sleep if he doesn't?

"Denied?" Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened.

However, considering Han Zi's identity and the entertainment company's situation, Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile, a sudden surge of interest rising within him.

After all...

For Jiang Tian, ​​these kinds of cases were just a spice of life.

"I still don't know your name?" the woman asked, having established a rapport.

Only then did the woman reveal her entire face from the shadows. Jiang Tian couldn't help but click his tongue; what a beautiful and charming woman.

Actually, this charm wasn't about being breathtakingly beautiful at first glance, but rather that this woman, around twenty-five or twenty-six years old, possessed an exceptionally pure and innocent appearance.

At first glance, she seemed quite pretty, but after a few more looks, she left a lasting impression—a pure and unforgettable charm.

"My name is Jiang Tian," Jiang Tian said with a smile.

“Jiang Tian? There are only three households in this building. I've never seen you before, young man. If you're from another floor looking for this person banging on the door, I advise you not to be impulsive. He's a lunatic, and you might be in danger.” The woman spoke for a moment, then suddenly remembered something, assuming Jiang Tian had come from another floor looking for the person causing the disturbance.

Jiang Tian shook his head and smiled without saying a word.

“My name is Yang Jing, this is my daughter, Yang Keke. Young man, whether you're a new resident or not, don't mess with him. He's a lunatic; he can really kill when he's in a rage. By the way, would you like to come in and sit for a while?”

Yang Jing assumed Jiang Tian was a neighbor from a different floor and that they would see each other often, so she introduced herself very enthusiastically, even inviting him in late at night.

Mainly because Jiang Tian's appearance was too alluring.

At least, at first glance, Yang Jing saw that this young man exuded a scholarly air, clearly a college student, with a somewhat youthful face and sparkling eyes.

Yang Jing recalled the online rumors about the foolishness in the eyes of college students. So, the gleam in this young man's eyes must be the foolishness of those college students.

Thinking this,

Yang Jing became even more completely unguarded.

However, Yang Jing's lack of vigilance meant that Jiang Tian, ​​after understanding the situation, had no intention of continuing the conversation.

"Sister Yang, do you know the resident in the middle? Why do they deny having their door kicked down?" Jiang Tian asked.

Continuing to probe, while using his Judgment Eye, Jiang Tian focused on the assistant who had kicked down the door.

He looked young, around twenty-seven or twenty-eight years old.

He was quite burly, and it seemed like his suit jacket concealed a lot of muscle.

If this assistant had feelings for Han Zi, why did he wait until the middle of the night, after he had attracted attention? Why not at other times? And didn't he have other options? Why did he have to kick down the door?

Jiang Tian felt there were other issues involved.

Sure enough, after a quick glance, Jiang Tian instantly formed a guess.

However, Jiang Tian wasn't entirely certain yet.

"Hey, I really don't know anything about this. This girl in the middle household looks quite pretty, but I wonder what she does," Yang Jing said, genuinely unaware of the situation.

Meanwhile, she lost interest in watching the door-kicking and was already preparing to go home; after all, watching a spectacle is human nature.

However, watching a spectacle sometimes comes at a price, and sometimes that price can be the heaviest.

Even while speaking, Yang Jing kept her eyes fixed on the assistant kicking down the door in the middle apartment, afraid that the assistant might use them to vent his anger.

If she sensed anything amiss, Yang Jing was able to lock the security door immediately.

“Hey, little brother Jiang, where are you going?” Although their conversation was brief, Yang Jing had developed a considerable liking for Jiang Tian.

In just a few words, Yang Jing could tell that this young man was knowledgeable and spoke with tact.

Moreover, he understood the situation immediately, so Yang Jing thought to herself, “This is what cultured people are like.”

Look at him, he immediately assessed the situation, completely different from the other family who went straight for the fight.

But the next moment…

Just as Yang Jing was lost in thought, staring at the assistant who had kicked down the door, Jiang Tian's back suddenly appeared before her. Jiang Tian, ​​who had just been talking to her, was now receding into the distance.

At this moment, Yang Jing could only pray that Jiang Tian wasn't there to cause trouble.

He was just passing by; nothing serious.

As long as he didn't disturb this crazy lunatic.

But the next moment...

She saw Jiang Tian standing at the door of the middle apartment.

He was face-to-face with the mentally ill man, the two staring at each other. As for Jiang Tian's hand, without her noticing, he had already grabbed the man's wrist.

Yang Jing's head buzzed.

"Little brother Jiang." Yang Jing saw this, her head still spinning.

Because she had personally witnessed another family simply go out and exchange a few words with him, offering some reasoning.

But the next moment...

This lunatic pulled out a knife and chased after the man, slashing him. Luckily, the man reacted quickly; otherwise, he would have been killed.

How could this person be insane?!

He even dared to attack directly.

Wouldn't this madman be completely insane?

Sure enough, just as expected.
