The assistant seemed completely taken aback that someone would dare to grab his wrist at this moment. His head exploded with shock.

From disbelief to utter dumbfoundedness, and then his eyes turning bloodshot and exploding in an instant, it took a full three seconds.

Because Jiang Tian said one sentence.

Or rather, the transformation from dumbfounded disbelief to bloodshot eyes and complete explosion was due to Jiang Tian's words.

"I'll give you two choices. First, kneel down and put your hands behind your head. Second, lie down."

When had this assistant ever heard such words?

The company's CEO was his brother-in-law.

The company's shareholder was his sister.

He was, without exaggeration, practically a princeling within the entire entertainment company, or rather, the entertainment conglomerate.

His brother-in-law was usually quite strict and rarely interacted with him, but his sister doted on him.

Because his sister knew.

He had a slight mental illness.

Therefore, no one in the entire company dared to mess with him.

Only this woman before him, or rather, the woman inside the security door.

He had done it time and again.

With this relationship, why would he become a mere assistant?

There was a reason. Wasn't it because he had initially taken a liking to this woman? Han Zi had clearly chosen him as her assistant.

Why did she ignore his pursuit?

Even consistently reject him.

Normally, this assistant dared not say anything.

But after taking drugs, his hidden mental illness exploded instantly, an uncontrollable outburst, or rather, a deliberate act of complete indulgence after getting high. He appeared insane, but most of it was an act.

Jiang Tian understood all too well.

Jiang Tian was far too knowledgeable about psychology.

“I’ll kill you!”

The assistant exploded.

Reaching out, he instantly pulled out a machete.

Regardless of where the machete came from, it was surprisingly well concealed.

Although he didn't look like he was carrying a machete at all, things quickly took another unexpected turn. He thought the man would run, but when the machete came down,

the man was still standing there.

How could that machete possibly fall?



Chapter 618 Kill these two scoundrels? A misunderstanding?


The assistant hesitated.

The key issue was that Jiang Tian was standing there, seemingly without any intention of dodging. Shouldn't he be running, frantically trying to escape? Why was he still standing there?

Although high on drugs, the assistant still had some sense and knew what he could and couldn't do. Everything else was minor, but if he actually killed someone with a single blow at this moment,

he would definitely be in deep trouble.

"What, too scared to act?" Jiang Tian tilted his head.

Actually, this person should be thankful, because according to Jiang Tian's Judgment Eye, although his actions seemed unforgivable, they weren't actually a very serious crime.

At least it was certain that this person hadn't been involved in drug trafficking.

Of course, this wasn't always a panacea. For example, the serious crime of allowing others to use drugs together, in Jiang Tian's observation, had a relatively low culpability, but if taken seriously, the actual sentence wouldn't be light.

“Kid, I’m in a good mood right now, I’ll spare your life. Why aren’t you running? You really think I won’t kill you?” the assistant gritted his teeth.

“You won’t,” Jiang Tian said decisively.

“What? I’m going to…” the assistant exploded instantly.

But…

“Go ahead and do it,” Jiang Tian shook his head, his face full of disdain.

In reality, it was only because his sin value was relatively low that Jiang Tian could easily kill the assistant in this situation.

However, Jiang Tian wasn’t the kind of person who would indiscriminately kill innocent people, nor would he kill people casually.

But now, forced into this situation by Jiang Tian, ​​the assistant felt his blood boiling, and coupled with the drug he had taken, he completely lost control.

His last shred of reason vanished.

Perhaps in a conscious state, he would never have done this, but at this moment, he had clearly lost all control.

Just as he was about to strike, cornered against the wall, he swung his knife down.

But Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged.

Only Yang Jing's eyes widened, her pupils contracting rapidly: "No, stop!"

Yang Jing reached out.

Although it was only a preliminary exchange, Yang Jing still harbored a considerable amount of goodwill towards Jiang Tian.

Of course, it wasn't about romantic feelings; it was purely admiration. Besides, Jiang Tian gave her the impression of being very sunny, and he seemed like a naive college student with a sly look in his eyes.

The machete was getting closer and closer.

At this critical moment, what seemed extremely dangerous was, for Jiang Tian,

a crisis he had experienced countless times. This kind of danger was far from being a threat to him; at most, it was just a game.

Boom!!!

The next moment, in Yang Jing's dilated gaze,

she didn't even see how Jiang Tian moved, but as the machete was still falling, the thug who had kicked the door was instantly sent flying several meters backward.

Oh no, she seemed to have just seen something—one of Jiang Tian's feet moved.

But it was moving too fast; she didn't even get a clear look.

A blurry shadow flashed by, and then the thug was sent flying.

A cracking sound followed—the sound of bones breaking.

The assistant slammed hard against the wall.

A broken bone.

It was unclear where the bone was broken, but somewhere was definitely fractured.

Then, with a thud, the assistant fell from the wall to the ground.

Ding Teng's face flushed red. At that moment, the excruciating pain made him almost numb; he didn't even know where the pain was coming from, only that it hurt terribly and his head was spinning. But simultaneously, inevitably, Ding Teng's rationality returned.

His intelligence returned.

He raised his head, his eyes blank and somewhat bewildered, staring at Jiang Tian's leather shoes.

And just then.

With a cracking sound.

Before Jiang Tian could do anything, the security door was opened.

It turned out that although Han Zi was inside, she had been watching the situation outside through the peephole. She had seen Jiang Tian appear.

However, what happened next happened in the blink of an eye, so fast that Han Zi had no time to react. After Ding Teng made his move, he was immediately kicked away by Jiang Tian.

When she opened the door,

she saw Ding Teng lying on the ground.

So, Han Zi ran behind Jiang Tian and checked him over. Seeing that he was unharmed, she breathed a sigh of relief.

This whole scene was clearly and completely displayed before Ding Teng, inevitably leading to a misunderstanding.

A lover.

That's right.

It must be this woman's lover.

Whoosh!!!

His newly regained rationality was once again overwhelmed by the flames of anger.

"I'm going to kill you two, you adulterous couple!" Ding Teng struggled to his feet, like a runaway bull, wanting to charge at the two men with his knife and kill them.

But before he could even stand up,

the next moment, he felt a terrifying force on his back.

Thump!!!

Ding Teng fell to the ground again, and not only that, a cracking sound rang out once more.

In fact, many times, especially now, for Jiang Tian, ​​it was already enough to do as he pleased. Regardless of whether Ding Teng had any other crimes, assaulting a police officer was enough.

And with a murder weapon.

A few years in prison would be sufficient.

Everything else depended on Jiang Tian's mood, so the speed and intensity of his actions were almost entirely based on his whim.

Of course, Jiang Tian wasn't indiscriminately killing. This kick, though he held back a little, landed heavily on Ding Teng's back.

His ribs were definitely broken.

Combined with his previous injuries, it was impossible for Ding Teng to get up.

"Hiss!!!"

The excruciating pain caused Ding Teng to gasp wildly, his entire body trembling.

His anger subsided repeatedly, and reason returned once more.

"Fine, fine, fine, Han Zi, you're fine, you're fine, you're finished, I'm telling you, you're finished, you two are both finished!" Ding Teng roared through gritted teeth.

He looked as if he wanted to kill them both with his eyes. After speaking, he even took out his phone, ready to call for backup.

"No!" Han Zi's face immediately filled with tension.

Jiang Tian turned to look at Han Zi, puzzled.

Han Zi also looked confused.

However, seeing Jiang Tian's confused expression, Han Zi quickly said, "His brother-in-law is the chairman of Yunya Group. Rumor has it that his sister controls half of Yunhai's underworld."

Half of the underworld again?

Jiang Tian heard this phrase, this familiar term. If he remembered correctly, Chen Qiang was once a powerful figure in Yunhai, rumored to have controlled half of Yunhai's underworld.

Very well.

So, the entire underworld of Yunhai is ruled by your Yunya Group and your Huayu Group?

But what the reality is, those who know, know.

The real power lies behind the scenes; how could everyone possibly know?



Chapter 619 Getting a first-class merit just for lying down? That's too much!


It's like looking across the Atlantic at the seemingly wealthy—how much money do they actually have? The terrifying amount hidden behind the scenes is a stark contrast.

It's like comparing a small fry to a big fish.

In fact, the same applies here, in the dark world of Yunhai. The true rulers never appear in the open.

Besides, Han Zi is just a minor celebrity.

So, anyone who claims to control half the industry, even a minor celebrity, is likely just bragging.

Of course, the Yunya Group still piqued Jiang Tian's interest considerably.

After all, Jiang Tian had recently been investigating the Yunya Group.

No other reason than that the Yunya Group and the Huayu Group have direct conflicts, business clashes, and even frequent armed conflicts.

The businesses involved are similar, making the investigation simple; following the Huayu Group's model is sufficient.

Of course, the power structures of the two groups are quite different.

"Your sister is in the mafia?" Jiang Tian grinned.

Good luck.

Jiang Tian had originally come on vacation to relieve mental fatigue, but this unexpected surprise was quite a surprise. He had initially thought Ding Teng was just a weakling.

Jiang Tian had even planned to teach him a lesson, interrogate him briefly, and scare him a little.

But now, Jiang Tian had a completely different idea.

Because this could very well be another first-class merit award!

“You’re scared? Hahaha, too late now.” Ding Teng held up his phone, his face full of arrogance.

Jiang Tian scratched his head. First-class merit awards were too many; they were terrifying!

“Sis, I’ve been beaten, I’m almost dead! Come quick! I’m at Zishan Apartments, that bitch’s place!” Ding Teng cried into the phone.

The pain was genuine; he wasn’t faking it. He didn’t know how many bones were broken.

Not only were his bones broken, but other parts of his body seemed severely injured as well. His lower limbs, the most vital parts, were completely numb.

But he clearly remembered that at first, he felt excruciating pain in his vital areas; the most terrifying thing was the lack of sensation.

"What?" Ding Teng's eldest sister's eyes widened.

Someone dared to hit her own brother in Yunhai?

Insane.

Have they gone too far?

Besides, her brother was usually so timid.

"Fine, fine, fine! Daring to hit my brother, Ding Ya Guan's brother! They've gone too far!"

Ding Ya Guan slammed her hand on the table, her face contorted with rage.

Everyone in Yunhai knew how much she doted on her brother.

She practically fulfilled every request he made.

Even his every interest.

"Little brother, protect yourself first. I'll send an ambulance right away. As for who hit you, don't ask now. Leave everything to your sister." After saying this, Ding Ya Guan began her full-speed attack.

Jiang Tian and Han Zi watched the entire scene unfold.

Of course, for Jiang Tian, ​​this was nothing.

However, for Han Zi, at this moment, her face had already turned deathly pale.

After all, Han Zi's understanding of this matter came only from a close friend.

Speaking of which...

Back when she first entered high school, Han Zi only kept in touch with one of her close friends from Jiang Tian's class. This friend, knowing she was having difficulties, suggested she ask Jiang Tian for help.

Coincidentally...

Although Jiang Tian was initially quite low-key, Han Zi had actually retained a significant impression of him over the years.

After all, they had been deskmates.

Although Jiang Tian was practically invisible back then, their contact only lasted a few months before ending quickly.

Han Zi transferred schools and left that high school.

However, Han Zi still vividly remembered Jiang Tian. Her impression of him was that he was quite hardworking, but unfortunately, he didn't seem to have much natural talent.

She even felt sorry for him and tutored him a little.

But he seemed quite slow-witted and couldn't seem to learn anything.

Unexpectedly, years later, through her close friend from back then, news of what happened at Jiang Tian's last gathering reached Han Zi's ears.

From what Han Zi knew, Jiang Tian had risen through the ranks of the police force.

He seemed to have a powerful background.

Even ordinary organized crime groups wouldn't dare mess with him; a single phone call could mobilize a large police force to arrest a low-level gang leader.

It was incredibly powerful.

But the outcome was unexpected.

Initially, Han Zi only wanted to contact Jiang Tian to have a good talk with Ding Teng, to get him to behave, or, if Ding Teng really was using that drug, to have Jiang Tian intimidate him.

However, ultimately, Han Zi's own judgment prevailed.

It wasn't because Han Zi lacked legal awareness—after all, she was a graduate of a reputable arts and sports university—but mainly because she knew Ding Teng's background, the influence of his older sister in Yunhai, and the immense power of his brother-in-law.

It could be said that in both the legal and illegal worlds, with countless connections, how many people dared to disrespect them and ended up in dire straits?

Han Zi knew far too much about these things.

Therefore, in Han Zi's view, if she had leverage over Ding Teng, and with Jiang Tian helping him intimidate him, even if Ding Teng had powerful backers, such a small matter shouldn't cause them any trouble.

She herself would be free from trouble.

At that point, everyone would be satisfied, and there wouldn't be any further issues.

However, she never expected that things would develop to this point in the blink of an eye.

Because, Ding Teng now looked extremely miserable.

He seemed about to breathe his last, his mouth full of blood, looking incredibly gruesome, struggling and rolling on the ground.

Han Zi's eyelids twitched uncontrollably.

"You're doomed! Even a god couldn't save you this time! Han Zi, you bitch! You're so shameless! I really thought you were some innocent sweetheart, but it turns out you already had a lover! You slut! If I'd known you were this kind of person, I would have fucked you already!"

Ding Teng was furious and spewing profanities.

The next moment, Jiang Tian kicked him right in the mouth.

*Pfft!*

A muffled thud.

Ding Teng let out a series of pitiful screams.

Jiang Tian tilted his head, his mood having been quite good just moments before—after all, he'd even managed to earn a first-class merit while lying down—but now…



Chapter 620 There are such awesome people in Yunhai? I, Ding Yaguan, will not allow it!


"Kid, watch your mouth, or I might hit you hard," Jiang Tian said, squatting down and smiling at Ding Teng.

"You..."

Ding Teng wanted to curse, but his voice trailed off the moment he looked up.

He saw a dark object in front of him. Ding Teng's eyes were initially dazed; he couldn't make out what it was. But when he looked closer, he realized it was a gun barrel.

Ding Teng's dazed eyes instantly cleared, his gaze becoming completely lucid.

In the blink of an eye, everything happened. Yang Jing, disregarding her own safety, seeing Jiang Tian in danger, went from running to jogging, and then stopped.

She watched helplessly as the young man held a black pistol, pointing it at the thug's head.

No.

Wait, in this situation, who's the thug?

Oh no, or rather, ever since Jiang Tian made his move, Yang Jing felt he was basically sick, because he couldn't even distinguish who the thugs were anymore. Why did the thug who kicked down the door suddenly become like this in front of Jiang Tian?

Why did the situation suddenly reverse?

“Come on, be good, listen to me, don't move, otherwise, my pistol could accidentally go off at any moment, and you'd die here for nothing,” Jiang Tian said with a smile.

“Big brother, what's the matter?” Ding Teng immediately backed down.

Quickly.

Very quickly.

He was so arrogant, yet he only dared to carry a knife. He was actually different from a real gangster; he was just using someone else's power to act recklessly.

However, he understood one thing even more: in the eyes of these people with guns, no matter who the other party was, they were not people he could afford to mess with.

Did he cause trouble?

"Why didn't you just do this sooner? Why did you have to get beaten up before you behaved?" Jiang Tian said with a slight smile.

But that smile, in Ding Teng's eyes, instantly froze him, as if he had seen a demon. After all, who had ever seen someone holding a gun, pointing it at them, and yet still smiling like that?

This kind of person was ruthless, definitely someone he couldn't afford to mess with.

"Don't shoot, be careful, bro, be careful it doesn't go off accidentally," Ding Teng quickly accepted reality after admitting defeat.

And this scene...

This scene, seen by Han Zi, also made her eyes widen.

After all, Ding Teng was like a chick in Jiang Tian's hands, but similarly, Han Zi, in front of Ding Teng, was like an insect that could be crushed by the chick at any moment.

That's why this scene shocked Han Zi, even making her feel like her worldview was being shaken, a strange feeling suddenly welling up in her heart.

Yang Jing might still feel that Jiang Tian was an even more insane thug than Ding Teng, but Han Zi knew Jiang Tian was a policeman. So, Han Zi's current concern was whether Jiang Tian's use of a gun under these circumstances was a violation of regulations.

However, seeing Ding Teng lying at Jiang Tian's feet like a dead dog brought Han Zi a strange sense of satisfaction.

That's right.

For Han Zi, the entire Yunya Group was a terrifying conglomerate. Most importantly, Ding Teng's sister wielded immense power throughout Yunhai. This wasn't just rumor; numerous accounts confirmed that Ding Teng's sister held considerable influence in Yunhai's underworld.

Besides this, there was a deeper issue.

Ding Teng's brother-in-law was the chairman of the Yunya Group, which was the parent company of Yunya Entertainment, and Han Zi belonged to Yunya Entertainment.

After joining the company, Ding Teng was practically a figure of immense power to Han Zi; crushing him would be incredibly easy.

Fortunately, she discovered that although he was interested in her, he was rather timid. Furthermore, as time went on, Ding Teng seemed to realize that continuing like this offered no real chance.

So, after taking that substance, Ding Teng seized the opportunity to confess his feelings to Han Zi. After Han Zi rejected him, Ding Teng began to gradually descend into madness.

Later, it gradually evolved into this situation. The key point is that Ding Teng only went berserk after taking the drug, and then immediately reverted to his previous cowardly and timid self.

In short, it's been dragging on until now. Han Zi had called the police before, but Ding Teng's connections were very strong.

Originally, Han Zi thought that scaring Jiang Tian would be enough, but she never expected him to be so ruthless.

The most terrifying part was...

While Han Zi was still excited and shocked, thinking about the consequences, at that moment, Ding Teng's phone seemed to be on speakerphone, and a very magnetic and rough female voice came through.

"Little brother, give the phone to the other end. I want to see who in Yunhai is so powerful that they can even carry a gun?"

Ding Teng turned pale with fright.

In that instant...

Ding Teng looked up, fearfully and tentatively glancing at Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow. Han Zi had just revealed the caller's identity, and he guessed the person on the phone was probably the big sister of the Yunya Group.

"My sister, it's on the phone, big brother, do you want to answer?" Ding Teng's hand trembled.

He was scared, scared of being beaten, and most importantly, terrified by the pistol in Jiang Tian's hand.

On the other end of the line, Ding Ya Guan's brow furrowed deeply with displeasure.

Mainly because of his younger brother's behavior. Of course, that wasn't important; he knew his brother was like that. The key was that Ding Ya Guan was now frowning. Who was this person who dared to attack his younger brother and even had a gun?

Was there such a powerful figure in Yunhai?

Was she a ruthless outlaw, or just a show-off?

While Ding Ya Guan was pondering this,

sitting in the car, Ding Ya Guan fell into deep thought, but at that moment, a voice came from the phone, interrupting him.

"There are many awesome people in Yunhai, and coincidentally, I'm one of them. I'm awesome, do you want to try and see just how awesome I am?"

Hiss!!!

Ding Yaguan's eyes widened, and his breathing became rapid.

Both his eyes turned red.

With a flick of his long, wavy hair, he stood extremely tall, easily over 1.8 meters, his physique incredibly imposing, and his exposed skin was adorned with terrifying graphic tattoos.

Enraged.

Ding Yaguan was enraged.

“Kid, do you know who you’re talking to? Do you know that in this Yunhai, no one has ever dared to speak those words to me, Ding Yaguan? I’ll give you one chance: kneel down and apologize to my underlings, and I’ll pretend this never happened. Otherwise, you can ask around and see what I, Ding Yaguan, can do.” Ding Yaguan’s face was fierce.

In the underworld, sometimes reputation is everything.

Without reputation, you have nothing.



Chapter 621 Hey, boss, where's the first-class merit? Don't rush.


Ding Yaguan understood all too well that the important thing wasn't her underlings getting beaten up, but the word getting out. If she, Ding Yaguan, backed down today, more and more people would look down on her in the future. Many people already harbored a deep-seated resentment towards her simply because she was a woman.

Over the years, Ding Yaguan had endured all kinds of hardship and experienced all sorts of things. She understood even more that she absolutely couldn't back down at this moment. Because of this, Ding Yaguan was actually more ruthless than the average person, and her methods were even more vicious than those of the average underworld boss.

This also spread Ding Yaguan's long-standing notoriety.

Of course, what difference would it make if word got out? Jiang Tian hadn't known this before.

Yunhai was vast.

The power in Yunhai was terrifying, wave after wave of influence.

Mainly because there were too many criminal groups in Yunhai. It was like a crime paradise, with internal crime, external crime, and joint crimes between the two sides. Terrifying crimes occurred every day, every hour.

Various criminal cases and crime rates appear peaceful on the surface, but in reality, comparing the data, Yunhai has long been far ahead of the rest of the country.

"How about this, I'll give you a chance to kneel down, and I'll spare your life," Jiang Tian said slowly.

*Whoosh!*

Jiang Tian only felt a fragrant breeze; it was Han Zi beside him. His legs went weak, and he collapsed onto her.

Of course, Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes, slightly shocked. He hadn't expected Han Zi, who appeared unassuming, to possess such formidable strength.

Of course, Han Zi didn't do this intentionally.

Because Jiang Tian's words directly enraged Han Zi.

Ding Ya Guan.

This name speaks volumes. As one of the few female gang leaders, her fearsome reputation ranks among the top even among many underworld bosses.

And because of her ruthlessness, people in the underworld called Ding Ya Guan the "Deadly Rose."

Having been with the company for so long, and knowing it was under Ding Yaguan's protection, she had gone from being clueless to understanding the terrifying power Ding Yaguan wielded in Yunhai.

"Are you crazy?" Han Zi's eyes widened, her pupils contracting as she stared in disbelief at Jiang Tian's handsome profile so close to hers.

"Fine, fine, fine, you're very good, hahaha, it's been a long time since anyone dared to treat me like this. There's actually someone as powerful as you in Yunhai, very good. I'll be there soon. I hope you don't run away; we'll have a face-to-face discussion." Ding Yaguan used reverse psychology.

Mainly, Ding Yaguan feared Jiang Tian was a desperate criminal who might kill her underlings and then flee.

That would be the most troublesome scenario, but as long as Jiang Tian remained there.

Then, Ding Yaguan had a thousand ways to make Jiang Tian die a agonizing death.

And she would subject him to all kinds of torture.

She would also spread the news.

To tell everyone what the consequences would be for anyone who provoked Ding Yaguan.

"Anyone who runs away is a coward, I'll be waiting for you here," Jiang Tian said cheerfully.

After all, a first-class merit was practically handed to him; who wouldn't be happy? Jiang Tian was delighted too. Although he already had many first-class merits, the next one would be a Rolls-Royce!

Jiang Tian had recently grown a bit tired of driving his Phaeton, so switching to a luxury car would be quite enjoyable.

Moreover, Qin Shiyu was currently staying at Jiang Tian's Zishan Villa, and Jiang Tian occasionally drove her car.

Qin Shiyu also told Jiang Tian that if she wasn't in Yunhai, the car would just sit there anyway, so she gave it to him to drive freely.

She had many cars.

This gave Jiang Tian the feeling of being kept, and he resolutely refused to drive it.

But he had to admit, Lao's actions were truly top-notch!

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue inwardly, casually hung up the phone, and tossed it to Ding Teng, who was still lying on the ground.

"Thank you, brother, thank you, brother, thank you for sparing my life," Ding Teng hurriedly thanked him, trembling.

"You should leave quickly. His sister is a big boss in the underworld, and I heard she has hundreds of men under her command. She has many lives on her hands but is still at large. We can't afford to mess with someone like that," Han Zi hurriedly told Jiang Tian.

Now Han Zi was already regretting dragging Jiang Tian into this terrifying quagmire.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to say something, the phone rang suddenly.

“You’d better leave quickly, her sister really will kill you,” Han Zi was still pulling Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian could feel that Han Zi’s hand was ice-cold; she seemed quite frightened.

“I’ll take this call first,” Jiang Tian waved his hand.

“What time is it? Don’t answer the phone! Go back! Her sister won’t dare to touch you. You’re really in danger here,” Han Zi said anxiously.

That’s right. If Jiang Tian was a policeman, he should be safe at the police station. After all, no matter how powerful someone is in the underworld, can they really arrest someone at the police station?

“What will you do if I leave?” Jiang Tian asked Han Zi calmly, almost jokingly.

“I’m fine. I didn’t do anything. She won’t do anything to me,” Han Zi said, her voice growing softer.

Han Zi herself didn't know the consequences, but they were likely to be dire. Jiang Tian was the one Han Zi had called in, and he had beaten Ding Teng so badly and even verbally abused the gang leader.

If Ding Ya Guan could swallow this insult, he wouldn't be Ding Ya Guan.

He'd be finished in the underworld.

"Are you sure?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Don't worry about me for now. I'll scout ahead for you." Han Zi pushed Jiang Tian away.

But Jiang Tian's feet seemed rooted to the ground; he wouldn't budge. Han Zi's face grew increasingly anxious.

Of course, this made Jiang Tian slowly breathe a sigh of relief.

Actually, for Jiang Tian, ​​although Ding Teng was indeed within his jurisdiction, if Han Zi really just wanted to use him, Jiang Tian could only say that his past fond memories had been completely shattered.

As for now...

Although Han Zi might have her own ulterior motives, she was indeed a rather simple girl, not the scheming, manipulative person he imagined.

Otherwise, there really wouldn't be any future contact.

"Let me take this call," Jiang Tian said, taking out his phone.

Han Zi stood to the side wiping her sweat; it was hard to tell if it was from exhaustion or from being frightened.

Of course, what shocked Han Zi the most was that Jiang Tian truly showed no reaction, nor any sign of fright.

Instead, he's making this call so calmly and unhurriedly.

Why should he?!

Does Jiang Tian really think Ding Yaguan would hesitate to kill him because of his status?

Don't dream about it. These people have blood on their hands!

Jiang Tian took out his phone a moment later, smiled when he saw the caller ID, and said, "Boss, don't rush. I just got another first-class merit award. Oh, I haven't made a move yet, but it's probably almost mine."



Chapter 622 One Sea of ​​Clouds a Day: Is There Hope? The Boss is Stunned


Meanwhile, inside an office within the city walls.

An elderly man held a mobile phone. Across from him stood another elderly man; the two had just been discussing something, both wearing broad smiles.

The smiles on their faces were filled with joy.

Clearly, both were satisfied with their conversation.

"That Xiao Jiang is truly a good comrade. He's fearless, tireless, and I heard that during a tense mission, he went without sleep for days and nights. Luckily, he's young and can handle it; otherwise, old guys like us wouldn't have lasted a day and a night."

"Yes, Xiao Jiang is good in every way, but there's one problem. I heard he has quite a few ladies' nights out."

"Haha, well, he's capable, after all. I've investigated this matter, and it doesn't seem to have much to do with Xiao Jiang. It's mainly because he's too outstanding; young girls can't resist him. We can't blame Xiao Jiang, can we?"

"You're just protecting him. But it really isn't a big problem."

As the saying goes, women—this is a minor issue compared to all other issues. In fact, throughout history, those with ability and strength always acquire more resources.

Of course, Jiang Tian is outstanding, and these young girls can't resist throwing themselves at him; we can't blame Xiao Jiang, can we?

That wouldn't be fair.

Besides, Xiao Jiang isn't the kind of scumbag who starts relationships and then abandons them; from that perspective, there's nothing wrong with him.

"In that case, I'll ask him soon when Xiao Jiang is coming," one of the square-faced old men said.

The other old man nodded slightly, clearly indicating his agreement.

And then, after the call connected...

The old man who made the call was initially quite pleased to hear Jiang Tian's voice on the other end, but soon, his eyebrows furrowed slightly.

A big shot?

That's how he addresses him.

"You brat," the old man said, not angry, a helpless smile on his face.

However, just as the old man was about to say something, the business content on the phone made him involuntarily straighten up, and the smile on his face gradually faded.

"Are you serious?" the big shot's eyes widened.

"Hahaha, of course, how could I dare lie to you? I didn't expect to just stroll around and stumble upon this first-class merit award." Jiang Tian didn't hide anything, his face full of ease.

In fact, he seemed to show no significant emotional fluctuation.

This made the old man's lips twitch wildly, his eyes widening.

"Good heavens, this is exactly what you wanted, kid."

The old man exclaimed in shock.

"I told you, whoever said Yunhai was hell, that anyone who went there would die, even sent several capable men there. But after arriving in Yunhai, some died, some were injured, and some came back saying they had no other options."

"There's simply nowhere to start over there."

"Those criminal groups are everywhere, like the quills on a hedgehog. There's nowhere to strike. If you do, it's like a cataclysmic event; you'll be killed first."

At first, the old man thought it was a joke. In this land, there were still people who didn't follow the rules; they were practically courting death."

But then, after this situation occurred repeatedly, the old man was dumbfounded.

"Yunhai, you're really that powerful?"

"Since you're really that powerful, then there's no other way."

"The only option is to abandon this place for now and undergo a systematic, radical overhaul."

"But this also presents a difficult choice. Once we begin the abandonment and radical overhaul, it means the entire city is utterly finished."

"Yunhai's development to this point hasn't been easy. Resources have been supplied in every aspect, and its future strategic direction is to become the economic center of Southeast China, etc."

Don't underestimate those countries in the Southeast. They may look dilapidated, with only a few worth considering.

The people in those small countries are all incredibly poor, but what can they do? The poverty of the common people has nothing to do with the wealth of the rich; overall, they are still relatively prosperous.

Once Yunhai becomes the center of the Southeast, its economic hub, its future development prospects will far surpass those of any other city in China.

This is the most important strategic direction.

Because of this dilemma, things have been delayed without them realizing it. During this time, Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng, was coincidentally brought up to stabilize Yunhai.

However, these powerful figures didn't expect that this opportunistically chosen individual, while lacking real ability and only good at smoothing things over, was ultimately unremarkable.

But they couldn't resist their son's ambitions, and the son, being incredibly capable, went on a killing spree.

It's gotten really intense.

Actually, many people in Jiang Tian and Yunhai didn't know that after Jiang Tian faced Tu Cha in Yunhai, earned his first first-class merit, and after his first operation with Gao Xiaolong, the higher-ups had already started paying close attention.

Unsurprisingly, things went smoothly, and everyone thought it was over. But unexpectedly, for this little monster, it was just the beginning. The subsequent missions were increasingly impressive, with these bigwigs saying "Wow, one every day!"

"One every day! There are still people this powerful!"

"One every day! Yunhai is saved!"

After all, if problems could be solved this way, it truly would be a solution to all problems with minimal cost.

However, no one expected that after Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan returned to Beijing, Yunhai suddenly ran into trouble. Although it seemed like just a power struggle, with Jiang Tian being an innocent victim, a deeper investigation revealed traces of a criminal organization behind it all.

There was nothing they could do but curse Jiang Tian's incompetent father, who couldn't even protect his own son—a complete waste of space.

Fortunately, later on, Gao Xiaolong was stationed in Yunhai to maintain order. As the incident and the case escalated, even Minister Tan was conveniently dispatched there.

This was to absolutely ensure no unforeseen events would occur.

After all, in their eyes, Jiang Tian was more important than anything else at this stage.

"Yeah, I just came out for a stroll, but I didn't expect him to come back again." Jiang Tian clicked his tongue in surprise. Of course, it was genuine; Jiang Tian swore he had absolutely no intention of showing off in front of the big shots.

But the big shots didn't think that way!

"Good lad, you've tried to show off on us."

The two bigwigs exchanged a glance, each able to read the other's mind.

A hint of helplessness crossed their faces.

But the most crucial point was...

"Everyone says Yunhai is hell, but you, in Yunhai, that bullshit hell is like a tiger entering a sheepfold, a buffet restaurant. First-class merits are like ordering dishes, you're ordering three dishes a day, aren't you?"



Chapter 623 Promise, This Time They Really Want to Walk Sideways


The bigwigs laughed heartily, joking.

In reality, these bigwigs, aren't they all smart? How could they not see through it? It wasn't showing off, it was just fooling around. Did they think they were fools?

It's just that people always give absolute preferential treatment to outstanding individuals.

If a piece of trash dared to show off like that, they'd be slapped away. Of course, trash wouldn't dare show off in front of them, especially not someone as talented as Jiang Tian.

What's wrong with showing off a little? He's done the work; can't he at least boast a little?

"Alright, alright, Xiao Jiang, don't worry about having too much merit or not having enough seniority. These aren't problems. The rules are only for ordinary people. Seniority is a thing of the past. The capable rise, the mediocre fall. Xiao Jiang, you can focus on your work. If there's anything, go to your Minister Tan. If Minister Tan can't handle it, we old guys will back you up."

In one sentence.

Jiang Tian felt reassured.

"But when are you coming?"

When the voice came, both big shots looked expectant.

After all,

this kid.

this little guy.

They really wanted to see him.

Although they had read about Jiang Tian's actions and even his missions, their blood was boiling, and they had read it more than once. They had even read Jiang Tian's biography.

They knew all the details, even more than Jiang Tian himself. They knew exactly what he had done. They absolutely didn't believe Jiang Tian was innocent; the information didn't lie. But to say Jiang Tian had done anything wrong was even more impossible.

Young Jiang was still young, neither of them married. What was wrong with having a couple of close female friends? Besides, some of them were only somewhat ambiguous and hadn't confirmed their relationship. Putting all that aside, even if Jiang Tian were really married, was it too much to have three or five close female friends?

This is not an exaggeration at all. There are far too many cases like this. As the saying goes, a woman might be icing on the cake with evidence, but she can never, and should never, be the primary evidence for conviction.

It's simply impossible, no matter where you are.

Young people driven by passion are completely understandable!

However, while they understand, they haven't witnessed it firsthand. They're incredibly curious about how Jiang Tian did it. According to the information, this guy can single-handedly take on thousands of people, kill hundreds, and escape unscathed!

He's practically a monster.

And in solving cases, he's meticulous and thorough.

This is unbelievable.

The more they learn, the more they feel they're going mad—the world has gone mad.

His fighting prowess is one thing, but his deductive reasoning is another. They consulted Shang Mingyan, the legendary female detective in the world of crime investigation. According to Shang Mingyan, Jiang Tian's deductive reasoning skills are already superior to hers.

This is simply a fantasy.

"I'll definitely go in the next couple of days," Jiang Tian quickly assured him.

Mainly, this matter couldn't be postponed, and the plan couldn't be delayed either.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian had already postponed his trip for a long time due to an important case; further postponement would seem excessive.

No matter what it is, he absolutely had to make time to go.

"Okay, we'll be waiting for you in Beijing. Don't break your promise, otherwise, Qiu Ni and Shi Yu, those two girls, are probably in Yunhai. I've had them kidnap you and bring you back. We even postponed August 1st for you." The big boss finished speaking meaningfully and hung up the phone.

But Jiang Tian held his phone, speechless for a long time.

Because in that moment of daze...

The main issue wasn't the postponement of August 1st, but rather the two people the big boss had mentioned meaningfully.

Actually, Jiang Tian knew Zhao Qiu Ni had a terrifying background, so he wasn't surprised that she was mentioned.

The key was Shi Yu, whom the big boss specifically mentioned—Qin Shi Yu.

This woman, the future financial legend.

Before transmigrating, Jiang Tian had even read rumors online that this financial legend had even attacked Wall Street with terrifying funds, and most importantly, she actually won.

Of course, according to the current timeline, this would still be four or five years in the future, around 2008.

Jiang Tian had always sensed that Qin Shiyu's background was extraordinary, and now, it was indeed true.

Most importantly...

Jiang Tian stroked his chin, momentarily unable to grasp the meaning behind the big boss's meaningful remark. He had a guess, but it felt too absurd, like a dream, something he dared not contemplate, absolutely dared not imagine.

Because the more he thought about it, the more excited Jiang Tian became.

While Jiang Tian was lost in thought...

Outside the Zishan Apartments...

Two passing traffic officers abruptly stopped their motorcycles.

Looking at the convoy of cars congesting towards the Zishan Apartments, they couldn't help but gasp.

Nothing more.

Just looking at the license plates was enough to make their scalps tingle.

All of them were Mercedes-Benz W14s, a total of eight W14s escorting the cars.

In this era, a W14 was the symbol of an absolute top-tier boss!

Eight W14s—what did that mean?

The car in the middle was even more breathtaking—a brand-new Rolls-Royce, adorned with a prominent gold Spirit of Ecstasy emblem on the front.

What was most intimidating was the license plate: a striking five-digit number from Yunnan (云A).

Terrifying.

They could only describe it as terrifying.

“Hey, where are you going?”

“Master, this convoy ran a red light.”

“Are you crazy? Can’t you see the license plate? This isn’t something we can get involved in.”

“No, a violation is a violation, how dangerous!”

“Come back. I’m telling you, this kind of thing requires approval from higher-ups.”

The old man speaking looked helplessly at his apprentice, whose eyes, at first glance, resembled those of a college student, making him seem foolish.

Good heavens, doesn’t he know what kind of people they can’t investigate?

What kind of car is that?!

It’s obviously someone important.

Who knows what kind of dangerous vehicles are behind them.

Besides, going up there now would be dangerous given their superior numbers. Even if they give me a traffic violation, I should remember their license plate, register it when I get back, and then report it to my supervisor. That's the safest and most correct course of action.

Still so young!

Sure enough, in the next moment...

Seven cars pulled up in front of Zishan Apartments. The apartment's security guards, seeing this, didn't even dare to come out and ask what was wrong. They immediately opened the gates, letting the cars in.

"Master, what do we do now? Are we just going to stand by and watch?"

"What do you mean, what do we do? We'll see. Think twice before you act in the future, don't be reckless. But let's follow along and see." The old traffic cop sighed inwardly.

In truth, he had been through the same thing.

Looking at his young apprentice, he felt like he was seeing his past self. When did he become so timid?

Perhaps, it was because of too many cases. After all, the criminal forces in Yunhai were too rampant.

"Read my eyes and do what I say, understand?" the old traffic cop instructed his apprentice.

Preparing to personally demonstrate and make a lasting impression on his apprentice, he was, undeniably, a qualified mentor.

"Yes, Master is the best. We're going to crack down on these despicable lawbreakers again," Xie Sisi said sweetly, her big eyes sparkling.



Chapter 624 Let's give the newly graduated beauty a little shock.


Xie Sisi had just graduated this year and joined the Yunhai Traffic Police Detachment; she was a complete newcomer, and this was her first day on duty.

Her mentor was a seasoned veteran, over forty years old, a true elder statesman, with an intimate understanding of Yunhai's situation and internal workings.

Because of this deep understanding, as a veteran, he knew exactly what a license plate with five eights represented in Yunhai—the terrifying power it wielded.

Such people were normally difficult for them to mess with.

However, looking at his apprentice, at the clear look in her eyes, Wu Zhongting instantly felt as if he were seeing his younger self. His long-cooled passion reignited.

Besides, he was now of a considerable rank; even powerful figures would at least give him face. More importantly, he didn't want to see this young girl, fresh out of school, develop too much disillusionment with society on her very first day.

It was just a traffic violation; it shouldn't be a big deal, right?

Wu Zhongting thought to himself, then turned to look at the pretty, seemingly innocent young girl. Her eyes revealed a clear, naive foolishness—the kind of blank, dazed foolishness one has when first graduating.

In fact, this kind of look is quite common among recent graduates entering the workforce.

Jiang Tian was also puzzled. Despite having experienced so much, despite being ruthless killers, his eyes still appeared the same to others.

His entire demeanor was incredibly sunny and bright. When he was thinking, his eyes were clearly full of wisdom, yet he gave off a somewhat dazed feeling, seemingly devoid of any threat.

Xie Sisi was the same.

To a recent graduate, Jiang Tian was a fake; Xie Sisi was the real deal.

In her mind, if you break the rules, if you run a red light and I see you, then it's my duty to penalize you, shouldn't you be fined or even given a safety lecture?

Xie Sisi's thinking wasn't wrong. As a master, and indeed a qualified one, she didn't want to discourage her little apprentice, so she gritted her teeth and went along with it.

Then the two of them rode their motorcycles, one after the other, following the luxury cars to the entrance of Zishan Apartments. The security guards at the gate, in this kind of apartment complex, weren't very useful; they just quickly opened the gate for anyone they saw.

Ultimately, they were just trying to make a living.

After entering the complex,

"Well, apprentice, how about we call it quits for today? It seems we can't afford to mess with them."

"Gulp."

As he spoke,

Wu Zhongting swallowed hard.

Xie Sisi, as the apprentice, wasn't stupid. She was just inexperienced after graduation, not truly naive. Seeing the scene before her, she was dumbfounded.

Her sense of justice unconsciously began to fade at this moment.

But her inner morality kept condemning her.

"Disciple, let it go today, spare him this time." Wu Zhongting's eyelids twitched, and he couldn't help but swallow frantically.

Mainly because...

The scene before me was terrifying.

What came into view were eight brand-new Mercedes-Benz W14s—four in front and four in the back—from which emerged more than thirty burly men in suits. They all looked like real men.

The key was their intimidating aura.

Especially the Rolls-Royce in the middle; at that moment, a man in black jogged up, and after the door opened, a woman stepped out.

She glanced at them, but then paid them no heed, not even pausing to look for a second.

Indeed, for a gangster of Ding Yaguan's caliber, they were no longer ordinary gangsters.

In fact, most so-called gangsters operate in a gray area, somewhere between black and white, especially the gray area.

And some, with their ruthless hearts, go all the way down a dark path.

After all, the deeper the color, the greater the profit.

These people are almost insanely ruthless in their pursuit of wealth, committing any illegal and criminal act, and after initially accumulating wealth...

They would no longer pursue wealth through sheer opportunism, but instead focused on enhancing their social influence and strengthening their background and power to rapidly expand their influence.

Ding Yaguan, though a woman, was undoubtedly one of the most outstanding. The criminal underworld in Yunhai was vast, encompassing domestic, international, and even combined forces.

This included foreign organized crime groups also seeking to profit from the situation—the list goes on and on.

This environment naturally bred rampant crime, and the previously unchecked free development allowed these criminal groups to grow and expand like a virus.

Even so, among all these criminal organizations, Ding Yaguan was the only female gang leader to have reached this level of power.

What she relied on was that she was more ruthless, vicious, and intelligent than any other underworld boss. She almost always eliminated threats completely, leaving no room for future trouble, to intimidate others.

Over the years, Ding Yaguan had come this far.

She had seen far too much.

She had experienced far too much.

She had been through almost everything.

But after all these years, Ding Yaguan couldn't recall anyone daring to provoke her like this.

And so arrogantly.

It was practically suicide.

"Boss, how do we deal with that kid?" At this moment, a well-dressed woman in a suit and tie jogged out of the passenger seat of Ding Yaguan's car. Her hair was parted in the middle, gleaming like a traitor's, and she was carrying a briefcase.

That's right.

This was Ding Yaguan's butler, her representative outside the gang, and her trusted confidante who collected money, solved problems, and even did shady jobs for her.

Although many looked down on him, believing he had sided with a woman,

he felt no shame. Instead, he thought those people were just sour grapes. He was content to live a peaceful and comfortable life, with his older sister handling everything while he stayed in the background.

He even got to serve his older sister when things were calm.

He was very satisfied with his life.

Seeing his older sister's brother being beaten, Cui Hao decided to uphold his domineering leadership and resolutely implement the ruthless measures.

"Chop him up and feed him to the dogs," Ding Yaguan said expressionlessly.

"Okay, don't worry, big sister."

Eager to make a good impression, Cui Hao led his men towards the apartment.

But just then...

The sound of mopping suddenly came from inside the apartment.



Chapter 625 The Big Sister Cried in Pain, You Had a Steel Plate in Your Crotch


This attracted the attention of Cui Hao and Ding Yaguan, mainly because, besides the sound of mopping, there was also the sound of clapping.

Not only them, but everyone's gaze turned towards it.

Accompanied by a series of crackling sounds, Wu Zhongting and Xie Sisi, master and apprentice not far away, couldn't help but look over.

The next moment.

They suddenly saw a figure emerge from the apartment elevator.

His steps were unhurried.

The clapping sound came from this person, and most importantly, there was a figure lying at his feet.

Seeing this scene.

Everyone's pupils contracted slightly.

Even Ding Yaguan was full of surprise, and then, after seeing the dead face at the person's feet, Ding Yaguan's mind went blank.

It was her younger brother.

Her, Ding Yaguan's, younger brother.

Ding Yaguan gritted her teeth, the grinding sound echoing, her face so cold it seemed to lower the surrounding temperature considerably.

Having lost her parents at a young age, Ding Yaguan had relied on her younger brother for support. This experience made her exceptionally mature, instilling in her the understanding that only by being more ruthless than others could she prevent them from bullying her, leading her down a path of no return.

And similarly,

having grown up together with her brother, Ding Yaguan doted on him and rarely allowed him to participate in anything.

But now, her own brother lay before her like a dead dog.

"Very well."

Ding Yaguan laughed in anger.

Beside Ding Yaguan, Cui Hao instantly shuddered.

He even took a step back in terror.

The men in black beside Ding Yaguan also moved away, their faces pale.

The main reason is that a person's reputation precedes them. Ding Yaguan's name wasn't just boasted; it was earned through sheer brutality.

Her methods of killing, and the ruthlessness they displayed, were simply chilling.

Just hearing the rumors, let alone witnessing them, was enough to send chills down one's spine.

And this was how it always seemed whenever the "Big Sister" used such cruel methods.

"You're very good."

Ding Yaguan stepped forward, looking at the young man before her, who appeared to be about the same age as her younger brother.

Ding Yaguan narrowed her eyes.

This person showed no fear whatsoever, despite her formidable presence.

This made Ding Yaguan unsure of what to do.

But regardless of who the other party was, what resources or confidence they had to dare to do such a thing here, in public. If she, Ding Yaguan, couldn't handle this situation properly, wouldn't she think everyone was someone to mess with?

How could she lead her team then?

"Of course I'm good, but your little brother seems to be in a sorry state," Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

After all, anger?

Fear?

Is it ridiculous?

It all comes down to this:

There's a huge difference between a ragtag army and a regular army. Jiang Tian, ​​when facing fully armed criminals—hundreds or even thousands—overseas, managed to break through enemy lines unscathed.

So why would Jiang Tian be afraid of these petty thugs in China, who might only have a few small sticks?

And most importantly, this kind of thing is unavailable anywhere else.

This kind of good fortune is practically like a first-class merit award being delivered to your door without any danger.

Good heavens.

How could Jiang Tian not be happy?

“Do you know what you’re doing, kid? Although you seem to have some influence, do you know the magnitude of the trouble you’ve caused? Your family, your friends—not only will you die, but your actions might also implicate them. Can you still laugh?” Ding Yaguan felt as if he had been insulted.

He had actually been humiliated.

“I’m not only going to humiliate you, I’m going to arrest you.”

Jiang Tian’s smile gradually faded.

Then, unhurriedly, he took out his police ID from his pocket, opened it, and flashed it in front of Ding Yaguan.

After casually putting it back in his pocket, he lowered his head and fumbled for his handcuffs.

“Officer, arrest?”

Ding Yaguan admitted he hadn’t seen it clearly, but he had seen the badge.

So, after a moment of stunned surprise, they quickly snapped back to reality.

“Arrest! You said arrest! Hahahaha, I'm dying of laughter! Arrest me! He said arrest me!”

Ding Yaguan pointed at Jiang Tian.

The black-clad henchmen behind Ding Yaguan seemed to have heard the biggest joke in the world; some even burst into laughter, as if they'd heard the most outrageous joke imaginable.

“Probably just a recent graduate from some small branch office.”

“A newborn calf isn't afraid of a tiger.”

“Hahahaha, I'm dying of laughter.”

“This guy is still too young. He probably heard about the boss's name somewhere and came here to investigate, trying to lure her out, but, hahahaha, I can't take it anymore, I'm going to die laughing.”

Overall, Jiang Tian seemed too young, even so young that these people felt they'd heard the biggest joke in the world.

If several fully armed police officers had come today,

they might have taken it more seriously, but like this…

Of course, ultimately, it was because Yunhai had brought them so many laughs that they had long since lost any respect for the police.

Furthermore, the recent popularity of the "Young and Dangerous" movies had spawned a growing number of "emo" youths, who, being young and immature, caused serious social chaos.

Of course.

This also influenced them, making them increasingly cynical about the police.

They increasingly felt there was no threat, especially compared to Jiang Tian, ​​who looked too young, almost like a recent college graduate.

This made them laugh.

A newly graduated intern police officer suddenly showed up, saying he wanted to arrest their "big sister"—what a joke!

Ding Yaguan also smiled broadly.

She had thought the other party had some kind of backing to do this.

But it turned out to be just this?

Just this?

If that's the case, Ding Yaguan, still smiling, stretched her neck.

And you know what? With a height of nearly 1.8 meters and a very robust physique, Ding Yaguan exudes an imposing presence, clearly a woman who works out regularly.

His face was initially all smiles, but those smiles grew increasingly cold, and then, in the next moment...

Ding Yaguan suddenly kicked out with all her might.

She kicked Jiang Tian directly in the stomach.

Ding Yaguan could almost see this fresh-faced police officer writhing in agony on the ground.

But the next moment...

When she kicked him...

Hiss!!!

A steel plate?

"You...you have a steel plate in your crotch?"



Chapter 626 Is there still justice? Is there still the law?


This is utterly unethical! Who would dare put a steel plate in someone's groin?

It hurts, it hurts like hell. You know, Ding Yaguan, despite being a woman, is definitely more ferocious than any man, incredibly strong, and regularly works out.

Otherwise, she wouldn't have built up her fortune back then, wouldn't be where she is today.

As everyone knows, strength is reciprocal. Ding Yaguan's kick was incredibly powerful, so the reaction was even more intense.

And what does that mean for Jiang Tian now?

Jiang Tian, ​​without exaggeration, looks normal on the outside, but he's no longer an ordinary human being.

A superhuman.

Simply put, he's evolved from the ground up. His seemingly ordinary body, after Jiang Tian mustered all his strength, reacted to this kick...

...even cracked.

...He's broken his bone.

That's right.

Ding Yaguan's bone is broken.

Ding Ya Guan, wanting to demonstrate her ferocity and ruthlessness, wanted to set a bad example and prevent anyone from thinking she was easy to bully. So, she appeared cheerful, but her attack was incredibly vicious.

Jiang Tian was certain that an ordinary person would have their groin area completely destroyed by this kick.

It was incredibly cruel.

However, for Jiang Tian, ​​it was nothing out of the ordinary.

"Hiss!!!"

Ding Ya Guan's face paled from the excruciating pain, large beads of sweat rolling down her back. Her eyes widened as she stared at the person before her.

She even clenched her small fists.

"A steel plate?" Ding Ya Guan gritted her teeth, her eyes wide.

What kind of person would put a steel plate in their groin?

In all her life, Ding Ya Guan had seen a lot, but she had never seen anyone like this. Was this a psychopath? No, this seemingly sunny young man had actually put a steel plate in his groin.

"You're shameless!" Ding Yaguan gritted his teeth.

Shameless?

"You attacked me from behind and then called me shameless?" Jiang Tian was instantly enraged.

Good heavens, you tried to ambush me but failed, and now you're calling someone else shameless?

"I've seen plenty of scoundrels like you. Sure enough, just a recent graduate. Very well, you've thoroughly enraged me."

Ding Yaguan trembled, and with Cui Hao's help, he managed to stand up, though he was still somewhat unsteady.

But Cui Hao felt sorry for him.

Jiang Tian, ​​his face contorted with rage, roared, "Fine! You dared to ambush Big Sister? Kid, I'll make you wish you were dead!"

"Kill him!"

"Damn it, what kind of thing is this? How dare he ambush Big Sister?"

"Yes, that's right, kill him!"

"Brothers, come up here! Big Sister has been attacked! Avenge Big Sister!"

These burly men in black didn't care what had just happened. In their eyes, Big Sister had been ambushed. This bastard, this seemingly sunny young man, was so heartless.

........

"Hey, disciple, where are you going?"

On the other side, Wu Zhongting was startled when his disciple suddenly went forward.

His face turned pale.

"Master, these people are bullying that young man." Xie Sisi couldn't stand it.

But now...

Wu Zhongting was terrified.

Especially after hearing her apprentice's words, she shuddered violently, gasping, "Are you crazy? Do you know who those people are?"

This also stunned Xie Sisi, who looked at her master with curiosity, "Who are they?"

She was too far away to know about other things, but she had witnessed these people bullying Jiang Tian. Especially the woman who got out of the Rolls-Royce; after kicking Jiang Tian, ​​she pretended to be injured and squatted down, while her thugs surrounded her.

Seeing the sunny young man about to be attacked, Xie Sisi felt she absolutely couldn't stand idly by.

However, although Xie Sisi was naive, she wasn't stupid; she was extremely angry: "Master, no matter who they are, they can't bully people like that! These people attacked first, and now they're hitting that big boy! How can we just sit idly by?"

But Wu Zhongting shook his head frantically.

"Master, what happened?" Xie Sisi asked.

Wu Zhongting's face was pale. "Do you know who the other person is?"

"Master, you know?" Xie Sisi was even more confused, because her master clearly didn't know either!

"The team just sent me a message. This person is the core leader of the Yunya Group in Yunhai. Rumor has it that he has terrifying power under his command, and he controls the Yunya Group. Do you know how terrifyingly large the Yunya Group is? And, most importantly, I heard that this person's influence is deeply entrenched, and almost no one dares to provoke him."

Wu Zhongting said, his face full of shock.

He knew this was someone he couldn't afford to offend; even his entourage was beyond his reach.

Even though he knew that the young man might be in a terrible situation, getting involved at this point would only result in them getting beaten up.

Of course.

In fact, the root cause of all this is that such things are common everywhere, and there are many underworld bosses around at this time.

The criminal gangs in Yunhai, in particular, are extremely rampant, with deeply entrenched influence.

It's fair to say that among the large number of people Jiang Tian has dealt with, countless others have fallen from grace due to their involvement. In short, this is because Jiang Tian has a powerful background; otherwise, it would be very difficult to resolve the situation.

Even with his formidable methods, Jiang Tian still has to abide by certain rules.

This is also because Jiang Tian has powerful backers. Even if Minister Tan couldn't withstand the pressure, there were higher-ups, and if all else failed, there would always be someone who could hold him off.

As long as Jiang Tian is fighting crime, he hardly needs to worry about the consequences.

However, this is only a personal privilege for Jiang Tian. Although he fought for it, for others, this terrifying power is simply beyond their reach. A single word from them could cost them their jobs, and losing their jobs might be the least of their worries. Could ordinary people like them withstand the retaliation?

Ultimately, the problem lies in the sheer number of social issues at present, and Yunhai still has many unresolved issues.

However, at least now the right direction has emerged, and thanks to Jiang Tian's existence, a solution exists.

This is the most important thing.

"You may not know what the Yunya Group represents, but this woman, named Ding Ya, nicknamed 'Deadly Rose,' wields terrifying power in the underworld. She's someone we can't afford to mess with. Besides, we only manage transportation; we can't get involved in this kind of thing." Wu Zhongting sighed, his face filled with helplessness.

That's right.

In fact, there's a lot of helplessness in these situations, but there's nothing he can do. He's past that impulsive, hot-blooded age; he's too entangled with too many things.

He can no longer act as he pleases.



Chapter 627 A Shocking Event: A Kick Causes a Truck Crash


"Are we just supposed to stand by and watch them break the law?" Xie Sisi asked angrily.

Her sense of morality and justice simply couldn't accept this.

Even though they were just traffic controllers, they couldn't just turn a blind eye to something like this.

"It's alright, I've already reported this to the city bureau. They should have someone handle it. There's nothing more we can do," Wu Zhongting sighed. This was all he could do.

Xie Sisi wanted to say something more, but Wu Zhongting then revealed the terrifying influence of the Yunya Group and the terrifying nature of Ding Yaguan.

After her initial outrage, Xie Sisi fell into a long silence.

Because Xie Sisi realized how terrifying, how utterly terrifying.

This woman was terrifying.

She possessed a deeply entrenched network of connections in Yunhai, terrifying wealth, and extremely important industrial influence. Most importantly, she was rumored to be ruthless and merciless.

The number of terrifying incidents rumored in society was countless.

Of course, at this point, it's best to keep a low profile. Being too arrogant at this level can easily attract attention, and being used as a prime example to eliminate would be a great injustice.

Therefore, Ding Yaguan has been living in a luxurious villa estate in Myanmar for a long time and rarely returns to Yunhai.

This time, he came specifically to handle some business matters.

Who knew he would run into his brother's problem?

Xie Sisi, after learning how terrifying this woman was, was also bewildered.

She trembled and stared coldly at the 'frail young man' surrounded by people in the distance, facing imminent doom, and was plunged into deep doubt.

What exactly is justice?

Xie Sisi was confused.

But just as Xie Sisi was confused,

seeing the scene before her, even hating her own weakness for not standing up for justice at this moment,

she was also anxiously waiting for the Municipal Bureau's reaction.

But Xie Sisi wasn't stupid. Her master had just made it clear that this person had connections that reached the highest levels. Even if they had received the news, would this woman receive the punishment she deserved?

While Xie Sisi was filled with self-doubt, Wu Zhongting could only pray in his heart that the young man would have good luck and only get a beating.

He could only blame his bad luck for provoking such a powerful figure.

While they were lost in their own thoughts...

At this moment, Jiang Tian, ​​surrounded by the burly men in black, showed no signs of tension.

Of course, to others, he appeared completely terrified.

Cui Hao wore a cold smile.

"Kid, blame yourself for offending the wrong person in this life. Don't be so foolish in your next life. Some people are people you can't afford to mess with in this lifetime. Otherwise, the price you'll pay will be your life, or even worse, a fate worse than death."

Cui Hao's eyes flashed coldly. Those who did shady work for Ding Yaguan, the kind of people who were good at backroom deals, were unlikely to be kind.

Moreover, their methods of tormenting people, in some ways, were so brilliantly inflicted on the mind that they rivaled even the Ten Great Tortures.

Cui Hao shook his head inwardly, but unfortunately, it was too late to be terrified.

Just as Cui Hao raised his hand to order Jiang Tian to be taken away,

Jiang Tian's light, airy voice made Cui Hao's eyes widen, his face filled with disbelief.

"I don't believe it."

Hmm.

It was Jiang Tian's light, airy voice; Cui Hao almost thought he was hallucinating.

Stupid, huh?

This kid's stupid, huh?

"Take him away, take him away." Cui Hao said coldly.

At this moment, the injured Deadly Rose, Ms. Ding Yaguan, had already arrived in front of her younger brother. Seeing him lying on the ground like a dead dog, his mouth full of blood and his teeth scattered everywhere, Ding Yaguan's fists clenched so tightly they made a chilling cracking sound.

Cui Hao wasted no time.

"Kid, come on over here."

A burly man placed his hand on Jiang Tian's shoulder.

Another man, with a cold smile, placed his hand on Jiang Tian's other shoulder as well. Instantly, the two burly men had 'pinned down' Jiang Tian without lifting a finger.

This scene...

Han Zi, watching from the apartment building below, instantly turned pale.

It's terrible.

Han Zi knew she was done for.

"Jiang Tian," Han Zi cried, her face pale.

Then she hurriedly tried to rush down.

A person's reputation precedes them. As a company employee, Han Zi naturally knew Ding Yaguan's name better than others.

But the more she knew, the more terrifying it became.

Recent years had been a little better, but during the chaotic period, the bloodshed on Ding Yaguan's hands was countless.

He truly embodied the saying, "One general's success is built on the bones of ten thousand."

Han Zi was filled with regret and didn't know what to say.

“Girl, you'll be in big trouble if you go down there.”

At this point, Yang Jing, being older, remained relatively calm.

But even with her composure, she had just heard how terrifying the other side's power was, and Han Zi had also mentioned Ding Yaguan's formidable strength.

Yang Jing sighed inwardly.

It's over.

She knew it was over.

There was no turning back.

“I'll go down and plead with them.” Han Zi wanted to slap herself.

She had only intended to scare them initially; she had never imagined things would escalate to this point.

“It's no use. If, as you say, that young man is doomed, you can't outrun them either. So, I think you should call the police; perhaps there's still a chance.”

Although Yang Jing knew it wouldn't be very effective, it was at least one way.

It was their only chance.

Han Zi's face was pale; she couldn't even bear to look down.

As for Jiang Tian possessing a handgun, she hadn't even considered it. A joke! It might scare people, but to fire a gun in front of so many people...

What would the consequences be?

How much trouble would that cause?

It would probably cause a catastrophe.

Han Zi didn't dare to think about it. Her subconscious thought was that Jiang Tian would be taken away, and then those people would torture him.

The more she thought about it, the more terrified Han Zi became.

Her whole body went cold.

But in the next moment...

A dark shadow flashed past, flying out from below.

Han Zi thought she was seeing things.

But in the next moment...

Looking again, Han Zi stared blankly.

Their heads practically exploded.

Because.

While they were frantically trying to make a move.

Jiang Tian acted, at that very moment.

He grabbed the wrist of one of the people next to him, and with a sudden, forceful pull, sent the man flying through the air in a daze.

The second person fared even worse.

Jiang Tian grabbed this man's wrist and swung him around with a slight twist. In the instant the man was airborne, Jiang Tian unleashed a powerful kick.

This kick was earth-shattering.

This kick.

was like a truck hurtling at 100 miles per hour.

Thud!!!



Chapter 628 Every move is deadly, unleashing a bloodbath


A terrifying black shadow soared into the sky.

That's right.

Accompanying the first black-clad man who had placed his hand on Jiang Tian's shoulder, attempting to control him, being kicked away at lightning speed by Jiang Tian,

the second man was also grabbed by the wrist by Jiang Tian and flung into the air. In that brief moment of airtime, Jiang Tian's kick was truly like a cannonball.

This kick was slower.

This allowed more people to see how Jiang Tian kicked the man in the chest.

And it was visible to the naked eye.

Especially Cui Hao, who was closest, saw that the kick almost went straight into the man's stomach.

The internal organs were shattered.

The black-clad man opened his mouth and spat out a mist of blood. After a moment of airtime, like a cannonball, he crashed into the two black-clad men blocking his way, then continued his momentum, flying backwards for at least twenty or thirty meters.

This kick...

It was as destructive as a 100-mile-per-hour impact from a heavily loaded truck.

When the man in black coughed up blood in mid-air, he had already completely lost his breath and vital signs. In other words, he showed no signs of life in the air; he was physiologically dead.

During the latter half of the flight, his vital signs had ceased; he barely even struggled.

Thump!!!

The man in black descended from the sky. When Wu Zhongting and his apprentice, twenty or thirty meters away, looked over...

They saw, to their horror, a corpse.

Yes, a corpse.

Lying on the ground, eyes wide open in death, head tilted to the side, eyes devoid of any light, seemingly staring straight at them.

Buzz!!!

After a brief silence...

Exploded.

Exploded.

Especially for Xie Sisi, a recent graduate with little experience, the impact of this scene was simply overwhelming.

She had never even killed a chicken in her life, and now she witnessed it being kicked to death.

Silence.

Deathly silence.

"Quick, call an ambulance! Call an ambulance for me!"

Just then...

Coincidentally, the first man in black, kicked out by Jiang Tian, ​​wasn't dead, but was struggling on the ground, only five or six meters away from Xie Sisi and the others. He lay there, his face contorted in pain, pleading for help from Xie Sisi.

"I...I'll call right away," Xie Sisi frantically grabbed her phone.

But when she was about to dial...

The sound went silent.

When Xie Sisi refocused her gaze, the first man in black who had been thrown out, struggling and pleading for help, was now limp on the ground, his eyes lifeless and devoid of color.

Dead.

This one too.

Dead.

The two men in black, kicked out by Jiang Tian, ​​died instantly.

This scene, unsurprisingly, was witnessed by those observing from a distance.

Everyone stared at Jiang Tian, ​​frozen in place.

Dead.

In broad daylight, he had personally killed two people?

Kicking one to death—that was murder.

Absolutely audacious.

Incredibly audacious. Even a powerful figure like Ding Yaguan, who wanted to kill Jiang Tian, ​​would only dare to take him away, dispose of him in a secluded place, or teach him a lesson first and then have him secretly kidnapped.

After all, times have changed.

Each year is harder than the last, and these criminal groups have a clear sense of that. Back then, they could organize hundreds of people to fight knife-to-knife in the streets; now, who would dare to do that?

Yunhai is far too sensitive now.

Even Ding Yaguan wouldn't dare do that. This guy, just by touching shoulders, had two of his men kicked to death.

Crazy.

"You killed two of my brothers?" Cui Hao's eyes widened.

Of course, what he found even more unbelievable was Jiang Tian's actions just now. Crazy? Is this even human?

Ding Yaguan, who had been squatting on the ground watching over her younger brother, happened to turn her head and witness Jiang Tian kicking the second man away.

After standing up,

Cui Hao, pale-faced, rushed to Ding Yaguan and said, "Big sister, they're dead! Both brothers are dead! He kicked them to death!"

Ding Yaguan's pupils contracted.

"Master, someone killed him!"

"I...I saw it."

At this moment,

Xie Sisi and her apprentice, who had been watching this scene from a distance, gradually came to their senses. Their minds had been blank, but now they were slowly regaining their senses.

But after regaining their senses, they were even more horrified.

What kind of monster is this?

Kicking one to death.

Even so...

But no matter what, murder was undeniable.

Now they had witnessed such a ruthless person, and what drove them crazy was...

Damn it.

Just now, in Xie Sisi's eyes, Jiang Tian was a sunny, cheerful young man, seemingly without any threat or danger, even giving off a quiet and gentle feeling.

This left her completely bewildered.

"What...what do we do now?" Xie Sisi's legs trembled slightly.

She trembled uncontrollably. She had thought herself brave, but now it seemed utterly useless; she couldn't withstand a single kick.

Not to mention her, these two burly men looked incredibly strong, easily weighing at least 170 or 180 pounds.

If they were killed by a single kick, then someone like her, even with clothes and shoes, probably wouldn't weigh more than 100 pounds. She wouldn't even last a casual slap. Cause of death? Died in the chaos of battle?

At this moment, Xie Sisi felt a sense of relief.

"These people are all heartless and ruthless. We should try to stay away as much as possible," Wu Zhongting said, his heart pounding, large beads of sweat dripping to the ground.

Xie Sisi nodded frantically, even agreeing vehemently.

And at this moment, the situation seemed to be under control.

Ding Yaguan arrived not far from Jiang Tian.

His expression kept changing.

He kept scrutinizing Jiang Tian.

His face was filled with disbelief.

Ding Yaguan had been in the martial world for a long time and had seen all kinds of people, but was someone this monstrous even human?

Even the fluidity and simplicity of Jiang Tian's movements gave Ding Yaguan the feeling that he could easily do the same.

But that was a complete illusion. Good heavens, Jiang Tian effortlessly lifted a seemingly light 170-180 jin (approximately 85-90 kg) man into the air and kicked him to death.

Was this even human?

"No wonder you dared to come looking for me alone. Turns out you have some skill. That makes sense, otherwise you probably wouldn't have come alone." Ding Yaguan's expression showed a slight increase in respect for Jiang Tian.

But even just that, it was already a display of exceptional skill.

"However, you think you can catch me like this? You're thinking too simply." Ding Yaguan sneered.



Chapter 629 He's gone mad with killing, my leader from Zishan?


Just moments ago, Ding Yaguan had taken a picture of Jiang Tian and sent it to the people behind her.

After all, things were different now. Before, she could do whatever she wanted.

But now...

She had to give him some face.

Besides, the situation in Yunhai was murky now, and Ding Yaguan couldn't act as recklessly as before.

But now, she had to give an explanation today.

The method was good.

It was just a pity.

Ding Yaguan sighed inwardly. Recently, several bureau chiefs in Yunhai had been too out of touch with reality, which was why Ding Yaguan hadn't been able to infiltrate the municipal bureau.

However, Ding Yaguan also had a large network of connections in other places.

Some of these connections were even implicated and in danger, currently under investigation and double-regulation, but similarly, many others had slipped through the net.

At this very moment...

The person calling Ding Yaguan was from a branch bureau.

"Hey, Director Liu, what's the situation? Have you found out yet?" Ding Yaguan looked at Jiang Tian, ​​then held up his phone expressionlessly.

This guy was ruthless, his fighting ability terrifying.
