But there was one thing he seemed to have misunderstood.

Because he hadn't done anything. This guy, a police officer, had only had two of his men put their arms around his shoulders when he arrived.

He was kicked to death.

No matter what this psychopath's motives were, it was all over now.

A life for a life, that's a matter of course.

Even if I was going to kill you, even if I was arresting and controlling you, even if you were a police officer, even if you could fight back, you couldn't kill.

Because if you kill, you have to pay with your life, even for this profession. Ding Yaguan firmly believed this.

The only pity was that he couldn't use underhanded methods anymore.

Ding Yaguan's lips curled into a cold, mocking smile.

"Kid, do you know what power and status are? This is power and status. So what if you can fight? Even if you're a cop, murder is punishable by death, it's a fundamental principle. Even the emperor is subject to the law, that's an age-old truth. Do you think you can outmaneuver me?"

"Fight back? If you're strong, then Ding Yaguan will use your official methods to deal with you. If you don't fight back, then I'll take you away and do whatever I want with you."

Jiang Tian smiled at this.

Yes, he almost laughed, a bitter laugh.

He wasn't in a hurry.

After all, the man had already revealed his background, even if it was just a little, Jiang Tian was still happy to.

Then, Ding Yaguan recounted what had just happened, such as his younger brother being inexplicably beaten half to death, severely injured and unconscious, and how after Jiang Tian arrived, the man became even more ruthless, killing two of his men just for asking questions. Murder must be punished with death.

Upon hearing this, Director Liu's face instantly lit up with a greasy sheen.

"Alright, alright, Boss Ding, give the phone to that person. Although he's in the police system, it's probably a classified department; I haven't been able to trace it. But as long as he's in the police system, no matter where he is, he has to listen to me." Director Liu readily agreed.

This matter was handled very simply.

In the blink of an eye, even the most naive person would understand that Ding Yaguan had definitely taken things out of context, but it didn't matter. Ding Yaguan had brought him so much wealth; in that case, it was just a favor.

The murder was real, the crime was real.

Therefore, there was no possibility of framing.

"Didn't find anything?" Ding Yaguan frowned slightly.

"Mr. Ding, please don't misunderstand. Recently, the situation in Yunhai has been tense, especially with all classified units. Their access has been greatly increased. Even if I wanted to access information, it wouldn't be possible for those outside our city bureau. Besides, some classified units are simply beyond my purview."

Director Liu quickly explained with a forced smile.

Ding Yaguan understood somewhat. The recent tense situation in Yunhai, and many other events, were obvious to everyone. Ding Yaguan was somewhat aware of them, although being abroad, his knowledge was limited.

But he knew how difficult the situation in Yunhai was.

"Kid, you're from the police system, right? The person on the other end of the phone is the director of our Zishan branch. Regardless of your rank, just wait for orders." Ding Yaguan handed the phone to Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian calmly took the phone. In an instant, the voice on the other end became authoritative: "Comrade, this is Liu Changshun, the director of the Zishan branch. I don't care what department or unit you're from. I order you to cease all your current actions and remain where you are. Do not make any moves or show any inclination."

Without further ado, Liu Changshun issued the order.

This left Jiang Tian stunned.

Where did this come from?

The Zishan branch?

Okay.

Even if it's a branch, Jiang Tian mentally considered the position, rank, and corresponding grade.

But...

Jiang Tian's own rank was already a second-level police inspector. What did that mean? This branch director was at most a second-level police inspector.

Besides.

The administrative rank of a branch bureau chief, in essence, is only at the deputy division level.

Jiang Tian, ​​although currently only a captain, in other words, his position is somewhere between a section chief and a division chief, just a little bit lower.

Even within a single unit, the leader is uncertain; let alone a branch bureau—how dare you interfere with my city bureau?

“Liu Changshun, is it? Very well, I’ll remember that name. Now, let me introduce myself. I am Jiang Tian, ​​captain of the Narcotics Control Detachment of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau. Now, I expect you to stay put and not make any moves, awaiting your arrest. Well, daring to collude with criminals, utterly lawless!”

Jiang Tian sneered, then hung up the phone.

And on the other end of the line, all sound completely disappeared, because Liu Changshun was about to explode.

Completely exploded.

While there's no publicity elsewhere, internally the name Jiang Tian has already begun to circulate. The only thing yet to be publicized is Jiang Tian's photo, or rather, what he looks like; that hasn't been leaked.

Even so, many people already know the name Jiang Tian.

Liu Changshun just attended a meeting today, a meeting with the leaders of the Municipal Public Security Bureau, chaired by Zhou Zhao, the Party Secretary of Yunhai.

At this meeting, Minister Tan was present and emphasized the outstanding contributions made by Comrade Jiang Tian of the Municipal Public Security Bureau's Narcotics Control Detachment.

From now on, the entire Yunhai District, all its branches, must unconditionally support Comrade Jiang Tian's actions.



Chapter 630 A Joint Trial, I, Han Xian, Am Not Convinced


Cold, chilling.

Liu Changshun stood frozen, his initial nonchalant expression turning completely stiff after the dial tone came through the phone.

His expression remained the same as before.

But...

His expression was now utterly frozen.

Zishan Branch.

To be precise, it should be Zishan District. Administratively, it was under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

As mentioned earlier, the police system itself has its own directly subordinate agencies, but it also falls under the jurisdiction of local leaders, much like other leadership structures.

It has its own direct superior, and on the other hand, it is under the local government.

But as mentioned before...

While local leaders may have jurisdiction over local matters, internal issues are entirely handled and managed by the police themselves.

In other words...

Upon hearing the name Jiang Tian from the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau, Liu Changshun was dumbfounded.

Running away.

That's right, running away.

Liu Changshun was drenched in cold sweat. Others might not know, but he knew perfectly well.

What did Jiang Tian's words represent? Moreover, Minister Tan was currently stationed in Yunhai, rumored to be Jiang Tian's backer. In other words, in Yunhai, Jiang Tian was practically untouchable. At least with Minister Tan there, even local leaders could only watch helplessly. "If you have the guts, go talk to Minister Tan directly."

In short,

Jiang Tian's words, to some extent, represented an imperial edict.

And his phone call was even more outrageous.

What did this mean?

"Damn it, you son of a bitch, Ding Yaguan, you fucking ruined me!"

Liu Changshun reacted instantly, his face filled with fury.

He was insane, absolutely furious. Why the hell did you have to provoke Jiang Tian and call me? Don't you know who Jiang Tian is? Don't you know what Jiang Tian does?

"Are you out of your mind?"

"The key is, you're out of your mind and you still offended me?"

The more he thought about it,

Liu Changshun's anger flared.

But after all these years, Liu Changshun suddenly realized the problem with himself.

"I can't stay here, I can't stay in Yunhai! What? Jiang Tian told him to stay put and not make any moves? What a joke!"

"If you won't let me leave, I won't leave!"

He quickly left the country before everyone could react.

While Liu Changshun was frantically packing his things, as for whether he would be alright, he didn't dare to even think about it. He had just said something to Jiang Tian.

And he knew that bastard Jiang Tian better than anyone else, after all, they were part of the same system, and with the recent lifting of the confidentiality regulations,

the entire Yunhai police system instantly saw who had recently received the first-class merit and special-class merit awards—it was Jiang Tian.

Moreover, the scale of the drug dealers arrested and the amount of drugs seized were at least hundreds of tons.

Although he appeared to hold a high position, it was only an appearance. In reality, Jiang Tian was already a captain, and the difference between them was very small, not that significant. In other words, in terms of police rank, he was only one minor level higher than Jiang Tian.

For Jiang Tian, ​​this level wasn't really high at all.

This was someone he absolutely couldn't afford to offend; if he didn't run now, he'd be a fool.

Just as Liu Changshun was packing his things, Jiang Tian hung up the phone, tossed it to Ding Yaguan, who was standing there dumbfounded, and then took out his own phone and dialed a number.

And at this moment.

The Municipal Bureau.

The person sitting opposite Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong was someone Jiang Tian would immediately recognize if he were here: Han Xian, whose face was still ashen and bloodless.

This Director Han sat across from the two high-ranking officials like a schoolchild.

Especially noticing Minister Tan, who had just hung up his phone and didn't even acknowledge Han Xian, he turned to Gao Xiaolong and said, "The bigwigs in Beijing have called, saying that Jiang Tian seems to be up to something again. They want us to keep an eye on him. He agreed to go to Beijing in the next two days, so we need to hurry."

Gao Xiaolong's mouth twitched, his expression almost contorted.

That sentence was utterly devastating.

Damn it.

This Jiang Tian, ​​he's simply...

Gao Xiaolong was at a loss for words.

Good heavens, do you know how many high-ranking officials across the country are eager to go there, only to be completely ignored?

And you, on the other hand...

You even keep delaying when the higher-ups want to see you.

The worst part is, the higher-ups aren't angry; they've even told them to keep an eye on things.

"Keep urging him on."

Good heavens.

Gao Xiaolong said awkwardly, "What could this brat possibly have? It's all about the Huayu Group. Wasn't it already dealt with? The aftermath has been handed over to someone special."

The progress at the Huayu Group is going very smoothly.

After all, with Chen Qiang's daughter, Chen Liting, taking over, whether openly or covertly, after Chen Xu's arrival, only Chen Liting has the legal and compliant status to inherit the company, and no one can voice any objections.

Furthermore, with the Municipal Bureau's support, the overall progress has been very smooth. Chen Liting has essentially taken control of about 80-90% of the Huayu Group.

Internal resistance is being eliminated through the cooperation between Chen Liting and the Municipal Bureau.

It shouldn't be long before it's perfectly resolved.

"Who knows?" Minister Tan shook his head.

Of course, he didn't care about the others, but Minister Tan couldn't figure out Jiang Tian either.

They only briefly touched on the topic, not delving deeper, especially since there were outsiders present. While speaking, their gazes fell on Han Xian, whose face was filled with shock.

That's right.

He had heard Minister Tan mention the "elder," and he had also heard that the elder was going to meet Jiang Tian. It was precisely because he had heard that his fists clenched, his eyes filled with jealousy, resentment, and an inexplicable envy.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong, observing Han Xian's expression, couldn't help but shake their heads and sigh inwardly.

In reality, Han Xian's presence here was due to their past connection with that old man.

But, as the saying goes, death is the end. After the old man's passing, they couldn't possibly stop him from using his own resources to get here, within their means.

Besides, this young man, according to rumors, had previously performed quite well.

Who knew Han Xian would turn out like this after he arrived?

He doesn't even know who he relies on for a living anymore.

"He's taking someone else's bowl and then smashing their pot! Han Xian, I never thought you'd be so shameless."

Gao Xiaolong sighed. He'd met Han Xian when he was a kid.

Generally speaking, they were all people in the same circle; their network of relationships, though seemingly vast, was actually quite small.

But nobody expected this kid to be so shameless.



Chapter 631 Go back to where you came from.


Having been openly mocked by Gao Xiaolong, Han Xian was already filled with envy, jealousy, and hatred towards Jiang Tian. In his heart, he was certain he could do countless times better than Jiang Tian.

He had only been out of the picture before coming to Yunhai.

If these two powerful figures supported him, allowing him to act without restraint, he could dominate Yunhai and, under his leadership, gradually bring order to the city.

That's right.

In his view, Jiang Tian's success was solely due to the support of these two powerful figures.

Otherwise, why? Just because he was better at fighting, stronger, and luckier.

He was certain he could do better.

This was the real reason Han Xian had been targeting Jiang Tian. Before coming, he had investigated and knew that with Jiang Tian here, he had no chance of advancement.

With Jiang Tian around, he could only hope for a small share; achieving anything substantial was impossible.

Ultimately, it all came down to personal gain.

But he had never considered why these two powerful figures valued Jiang Tian so highly, even to the point that high-ranking officials from Beijing were personally receiving him.

He also hadn't considered why Yunhai had fallen into such chaos before Jiang Tian's sudden emergence, with the police system practically existing in name only.

What a terrifying phenomenon.

Without Jiang Tian, ​​all of this would likely continue indefinitely.

But Han Xian didn't think about any of this. All he thought about was that Jiang Tian had stolen his life. This place was practically paradise. Han Xian understood that with absolute power, there was no shortage of major cases here; in fact, any case, any criminal gang, could be a nationally sensational case.

He could easily rack up first-class merit awards.

"I refuse to accept this," Han Xian said, clenching his fist.

Refuse to accept this?

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong instantly turned cold, even looking at Han Xian with sneers. Even on the verge of death, he remained unrepentant.

"What are you dissatisfied with? What have you done for Yunhai that you're dissatisfied with?" Gao Xiaolong asked calmly, without getting angry.

What have you done?

That's right.

If you're dissatisfied, then what contributions have you made to Yunhai?

But that's the awkward part.

Han Xian believed, firmly believed, that given time, he could create a legend in Yunhai. Jiang Tian was just a lucky joke.

The era ultimately belonged to him.

But the key was, he had only just arrived in Yunhai.

"Speak up, in the thirteen cities of Yunnan Province, in the entire city of Yunhai, nearly ten million people are constantly suffering from the ravages and oppression of organized crime. What are you dissatisfied with? What have you done for them that makes you dissatisfied? What contributions have you made? What merits have you received that allow you to say you're dissatisfied with Jiang Tian?"

His thinking was flawed.

Minister Tan wasn't angry.

It wasn't worth it.

He had no intention of letting Han Xian stay in Yunhai any longer.

They've already been working internally, and the reason they haven't immediately suspended Han Xian is to save face for him, and also to give their old friend some respect.

But this is already their biggest concession.

Compared to Jiang Tian, ​​you're not even worthy!

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong sighed inwardly. The main reason was that Jiang Tian's achievements were too enviable, inspiring jealousy and resentment—the saying "the tallest tree catches the most wind" applies here.

People like Han Xian, who had just arrived, believed they could achieve the same results, thinking Jiang Tian was just lucky, and harbored resentment, so they targeted him at every turn.

Targeting Jiang Tian was targeting Yun Hai.

Even their own sons would be slapped aside, let alone the child of their old friend.

"I admit Jiang Tian is indeed excellent in some aspects, but look at how many of his missions were just due to luck, and we don't even know if he lied. If it were me, I would do even better, with less impact, and I could find better solutions to many cases," Han Xian said loudly.

He knew this was his only chance.

Once these two bigwigs left, everything would be set in stone.

Han Xian's words, however, made Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong smile.

Yes.

Actually, even setting aside Han Xian, they initially thought the same thing.

But if your understanding of Yunhai is based solely on what you read on paper, then you're sorely mistaken.

Before Jiang Peng, why did so many directors of Yunhai, parachuted in one after another, die unexpectedly?

“Han Xian, that's enough!” Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table.

Back then, because of their distrust of Jiang Tian, ​​or rather, simply letting Jiang Tian go back to rest, the entire anti-drug team lost nearly a hundred outstanding comrades in a foreign land.

The lesson of the past is so recent, and the fresh blood has only been replenished for a short time.

“You still haven't figured yourself out. Who do you think you are? Why should we trust you? What are you? Do you think you have a lot of influence? That your face is greater than the organization’s?” Minister Tan interrupted Han Xian, his face icy cold.

But before he could finish speaking,

Minister Tan's phone suddenly rang.

Minister Tan looked at Han Xian, whose face was a mask of shock, a mixture of frustration and resentment. He wanted to say something to him, but ultimately remained silent.

He could only conclude that Han Xian's thinking had gone awry.

"Then I'm sorry, I can only ask you to leave Yunhai."

"Go," Minister Tan said directly.

Buzz!!!

Han Xian's mind went blank. He stared at Minister Tan in disbelief.

"Minister, I'll leave? Where will I go?" Han Xian's voice grew increasingly agitated.

His emotions were beginning to spiral out of control.

Minister Tan remained expressionless. "Yunhai can't handle someone like you. Go back to where you came from. Prepare your things; the transfer order will be issued soon."

From that moment on,

Han Xian's political future was over.

Once he returned,

Han Xian would have no chance of recovery.

Zhe Rang Hanxian's eyes widened, his face turning pale. He even thought he had misheard: "Just because I questioned Jiang Tian, ​​you want me to leave?"

He's the bureau chief!

He's the bureau chief of Yunhai!

Why?

Why?

If he were transferred away after only a few days, without having accomplished anything, regardless of the resources his family had invested, it would be an absolute stain on his record and political future.

Minister Tan didn't even acknowledge Han Xian. He glanced at the caller ID and saw it was Jiang Tian calling. Minister Tan, who had initially intended to go outside to answer the phone, decided not to leave his seat.

After glancing at Han Xian, he didn't try to hide anything and directly answered Jiang Tian's call. In a mere fraction of a second, Minister Tan's expression shifted from icy to a warm, smiling greeting: "Little Jiang, what are you doing? An important figure in Beijing requests your presence. When are you available?"

Han Xian sensed caution in that tone.

Buzz!!!

It crashed.



Chapter 632 Oh no, I've been infected too! This kid is terrifying!


*Pfft!*

Invited?

A big shot in Beijing invited you?

When do you have time?

Can you go tomorrow?

Han Xian realized that he recognized all the words, but why did they sound so unfamiliar when put together? Combined, they formed a completely foreign concept to him. He knew that even when his grandfather was alive, he was roughly at the same level as Minister Tan, only promoted upon retirement.

But overall, they were on similar levels.

And the truly top-tier figures were completely beyond the reach of a newbie like him; they weren't even in the same social circles.

The reason his nephew was so desperately pursuing Zhao Qiu Ni was because of the connections her family had.

Or rather, their circle was a level higher than his. In other words, each level represented a different circle, one that his family couldn't possibly enter.

But now...

What was Minister Tan saying?

Why couldn't he understand?

He completely couldn't understand what it meant.

Of course, just as Han Xian was filled with shock, bewilderment, and inexplicable jealousy and frustration,

Minister Tan's face showed surprise.

Yes, he had just been asking questions, but now his face was filled with shock.

"What did you say? There's just been a major breakthrough in the investigation of another criminal gang!" Minister Tan exclaimed.

His heart skipped a beat.

Han Xian, watching from the side, felt his heart pounding, his eyes wide.

Putting everything else aside, in terms of cracking down on these criminal gangs, if Jiang Tian said he was second, probably no one in the world would be first.

Ultimately, it was because the speed was too fast.

The Huayu Group's operation had just ended, and was still in progress, and Jiang Tian had already uncovered another criminal gang.

At this moment, Minister Tan didn't know whether to be happy, shocked, or just amused.

Because...

The entire detention center in Yunhai, and even many detention centers throughout Yunnan Province, is now full. Even standard six-person rooms are overflowing, and in some places, they're severely overcrowded, with over ten people crammed into a single six-person room.

There's no other way; there's simply no room. They've even booked hotels and are working overtime to build new buildings specifically for the purpose of accommodating these criminals.

The courts are also working overtime on approvals. It's not just the municipal bureau that's busy; the public security, procuratorate, and courts work in tandem. In other words, the municipal bureau makes arrests, gathers evidence, submits it to the procuratorate, the procuratorate reviews it, and finally, the procuratorate submits it to the court, which then conducts the final trial based on the evidence.

This is the entire chain of operations. With so many arrests, the municipal bureau, procuratorate, and courts are completely overwhelmed.

They're working non-stop and still can't keep up.

On the other hand, Minister Tan was even more worried: "Xiao Jiang, if you do this, won't you be able to make it tomorrow? This won't do! You've already dragged this out for too long. The boss in Beijing has already given me a task. If you don't go tomorrow, I won't be able to explain it!"

*Buzz!*

Hearing this, and even seeing the earnest pleading on Minister Tan's face, Han Xian was nearly driven mad. His fists clenched tightly, his teeth clenched.

A beast!

This is simply a beast!

That Jiang Tian is truly a beast.

He's utterly shameless.

Why?

Why are these bigwigs treating Jiang Tian so differently? Is there no justice? Is there no law?

Han Xian went mad, completely losing his temper.

Unfortunately, no one present paid him any attention.

In the eyes of Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong, Han Xian had practically been given up on.

In other words, after their conversation, they had completely abandoned Han Xian. At most, he would be transferred away in a few days. As for the record in his file after he left, and the devastating blow it would cause to his future, that was none of their concern.

The only thing they could do was write in the performance review report when he was transferred that he lacked the ability to handle the job.

Otherwise, they could have simply written an evaluation of him as jealous and envious of talent, with the two big shots signing it off. Good grief, Han Xian would probably be forced into early retirement.

This time, they're just giving their old friend some face.

What a pity!

At such an advanced age, he's lived like a pig. Here in Yunhai, not only does he not recognize who the big boss is, but he even targets the big boss.

Is this a joke?

The last time they questioned the big boss, the consequences were devastating. How many people were sacrificed in the Municipal Bureau is still fresh in their minds. They can't make the same mistake twice.

Besides, the last problem arose after Gao Xiaolong returned to Beijing, leaving no one to oversee Yunhai. Now they've learned their lesson; the two big shots are here to ensure Jiang Tian has no worries.

Otherwise, given the chaos and economic losses Jiang Tian caused, the local leaders would probably be talking to him every day, rendering him useless.

"It's alright, we should be able to handle it most of the time today. Arrest the ringleader and his gang, and the lower-ups can handle the rest," Jiang Tian said.

Jiang Tian liked to do things this way. He could keep everything to himself, but he'd always share some of the spoils. Otherwise, why would so many colleagues in the city bureau be so dedicated, risking their lives for their cause?

Protecting the country is one thing, but you can't stop people from seeking credit.

Jiang Tian would always be lenient in this regard.

"Oh, this credit isn't small, and you're just giving it away?" Minister Tan asked, surprised.

"I've always done it this way. Besides, this isn't much of a merit, is it? It's already a first-class merit, not a first-class merit. Giving the comrades a little work to do should earn at least a second or third-class collective merit," Jiang Tian said casually.

What? You're saying the merit is already decided before the person has even been arrested?

If it were anyone else, Minister Tan would definitely have yelled at him and kicked him out.

But Jiang Tian said it?

Oh, never mind then.

Minister Tan hung up the phone, feeling utterly dejected. Every time he called Jiang Tian, ​​he had this strange feeling that even if the sky were to fall, Jiang Tian could hold it up. He didn't know where this feeling came from.

But Jiang Tian's confidence deeply infected Minister Tan.

It even gave him a surge of adrenaline, an urge to charge into battle.

"This kid, his infectious energy is terrifying."

Minister Tan was shaken, secretly clicking his tongue in amazement. Then, when his gaze swept across the screen and he saw Han Xian on the other end, Minister Tan's expression darkened.

"Why are you still here?" Minister Tan's tone instantly turned displeased.



Chapter 633 Cross-border? A desperate comeback?


Without comparison, there's no harm done. Han Xian, in theory, has far more age and experience than Jiang Tian; he's almost forty.

Yet, he's developed this character.

Beer belly, bald head, and most importantly, a narrow-minded heart. Minister Tan found him increasingly displeasing.

"You've lived such a long life, a complete waste! Even if you were given the position of Director Yunhai, would you even dare to sit there? Don't you ever look in the mirror and see what kind of person you are? No ability, no looks, your only advantage is your age, which has turned you into this jealous and envious person. These next few days, go and see if there's anything you haven't yet assigned, and get it done. Remember, if you don't have the ability, don't even think about that position."

Minister Tan stood up, completely losing interest in exchanging any more words with Han Xian.

You should know, Minister Tan is only able to control himself now because he's cultivated his mind and body over the years. If he were a few years younger, he'd probably want to kick him a couple of times.

Compared to Jiang Tian?

You're not worthy!

The key point is, even after being told, he's incorrigible.

Han Xian's face turned bright red from the scolding, but looking left and right, he wondered if this was his office.

This made Han Xian almost grind his teeth to dust.

Most importantly, just then, Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong's voices came again from the doorway.

Han Xian vaguely heard Gao Xiaolong's voice: "Let's go, let's hurry up and make arrangements. Overall, the operation will result in a second-class merit for both the individual and the group. Let's arrange it ahead of time. Ideally, we should apply for a first-class merit before August 1st, so we can deal with the recent public opinion."

His voice was full of satisfaction.

Gao Xiaolong hadn't expected Jiang Tian to be so capable.

Everyone had high hopes for you, but you were the one who proved them wrong.

That feeling, Gao Xiaolong could only say, was exhilarating!

"Yeah, you're right. Damn it, seeing all that online hype lately, and those malicious trolls stirring things up behind the scenes, while we can't let the people behind it get away with it, we can't let these trolls get away with it either. Do they really think they can take Jiang Tian down like those celebrities?" Minister Tan said, his face filled with rage.

Speaking of the recent escalation of the public opinion, it's getting increasingly serious.

Especially...

Recently, the public opinion has been filled with all sorts of fabrications, especially conspiracy theories—things like being a second-generation rich kid, paving the way, even rumors that Jiang Tian is the illegitimate son of some bigwig in Beijing. This is clearly paving the way, using all of Yunhai's resources to pave the way.

There aren't that many criminals in the world. To put it bluntly, it's just paving the way for this second-generation rich kid.

That's right.

Under the guidance of the hype, and with the deliberate manipulation of the trolls and those behind the scenes, the public opinion has gone to this absurd level. Most importantly, countless people believe it.

Many people online now know the name Jiang Tian. That's right, Jiang Tian's name has been exposed; only his photo hasn't been released yet.

This has already created a massive backlash against Jiang Tian.

The comments are appalling.

Minister Tan recently took notice and is practically itching to eliminate those who are muddying the waters and the masterminds behind it all.

"Not yet, it's not the right time."

"Don't worry, none of them will escape."

Gao Xiaolong's expression also turned slightly cold.

Damn it.

Those people are really audacious, daring to say anything, daring to spread any rumor, even fabricating the story that Jiang Tian is the illegitimate son of a powerful figure.

The most outrageous thing is, there's also talk of forging merits.

Framed loyal officials, killed innocent people and claimed credit, etc.

Do they think they're all fools?

The key is, there are actually quite a few people who believe these outrageous claims.

As the sounds gradually faded away.

Han Xian slumped into his chair.

At this moment, his assistant pushed open the door and, seeing Han Xian's appearance, also looked dejected.

"What's with that gloomy look? Do you think I'm finished, that there's no future with me?" Han Xian was now like an enraged lion.

A bomb that had already exploded, but was forcibly held back.

But as soon as the assistant entered, before he could even speak, the enraged lion unleashed his fury.

"No, Chief, it's not that. It's just that I have a lead here that could be of great help to you, perhaps even a game-changer, allowing you to demonstrate your abilities to the higher-ups."

The assistant rushed forward, his face filled with panic.

Han Xian instantly regained his composure, then excitedly asked, "What is it? What lead?"

"Chief Bai is currently investigating and apprehending a transnational criminal syndicate. Their crimes are extremely large-scale; the planning alone took a long time, and they're about to close the net. Chief Bai has requested our city bureau's assistance in the joint operation, which may involve international pursuit."

The assistant hurriedly explained.

*Whoosh!*

Han Xian jumped to his feet.

His eyes gleamed, staring intently at his assistant, his breathing rapid.

They'd turned the tables.

They'd really turned the tables?

Han Xian was brimming with excitement because Boss Bai, also known as Bai Qiu, from the provincial anti-drug squad, had indeed been executing and planning an exceptionally important transnational case.

This transnational case was undoubtedly extremely important, even terrifyingly so, no longer confined to just one or two provinces.

"How so?" Han Xian asked impatiently.

"This is a transnational case, extremely large in scale, perhaps even comparable to that so-called Fishbuy.com case. I've heard it involves a wide range of people. Boss Bai has already coordinated with multiple locations for a joint manhunt, and some of the key figures will even be operating across borders," the assistant quickly replied.

"Which country?"

"Sakura."

The assistant slowly replied.

Sakura?

Han Xian's eyes flashed with a terrifying light.

This was it—a chance to turn the tide. If he could perform exceptionally well in this mission, even Minister Tan wouldn't transfer him, right?

At the very least, a good performance would earn him a first-class merit.

"When, where, how to coordinate the operation?" Han Xian asked impatiently.

While Han Xian was hurriedly making preparations,

Actually,

Jiang Tian on the other end had already hung up the phone.

And after sending a message to Minister Tan, he immediately dialed Song Liguo's number.

Meanwhile, Song Liguo had already indicated that they were on their way; Minister Tan had just given them the order to set off with their men.

"Good, good, I trust Minister Tan with everything he does."

Mainly, Jiang Tian hadn't expected to catch such a big fish, so he hadn't brought enough manpower immediately. But thankfully, it's not too late now.

"Watch out!!!"



Chapter 634 The moment you raised your gun, you were already dead.


Just as Jiang Tian hung up the phone, Xie Sisi, from her vantage point, could see the scene unfold. She could even see the burly woman suddenly pull something out of her clothes as Jiang Tian turned to hang up.

At first, Xie Sisi didn't see clearly, but the moment she did, her mind went blank.

Her eyes widened in disbelief. What was it? A gun! A handgun!

The woman was holding a handgun, suddenly pointing it at the back of Jiang Tian's head.

"Watch out!"

At that moment, Xie Sisi didn't think twice. In that instant, following her instincts, before her mind could even process it, her subconscious sense of justice compelled her to shout.

In that instant, Xie Sisi even saw Jiang Tian look up, his gaze fixed on her.

Danger!

Xie Sisi screamed internally, but she could only watch helplessly as Jiang Tian...

"Idiot! Don't look at me! Look behind you! You're in danger!"

"Stop!!!"

Finally, Xie Sisi shouted a second time.

Her body moved forward without thinking, disregarding the danger.

As she moved, a slight smile appeared on Jiang Tian's face.

Unexpectedly, Jiang Tian was somewhat surprised. He truly hadn't expected to encounter a traffic police officer at this moment, especially a young girl. This young girl, in fact, was willing to step forward at this critical time.

Jiang Tian's observation skills were incredibly sharp. He had almost complete control over the entire surrounding situation, including noticing Xie Sisi and the others when they first appeared, which he had already observed out of the corner of his eye.

This was also the reason why Jiang Tian had been able to face so many life-or-death situations outside and still remain safe and sound. Besides his own abilities and reflexes, he also possessed an almost instantaneous and thorough understanding of his surroundings.

He could also react in an instant.

At this moment, Jiang Tian smiled. He hadn't expected this young girl to be so righteous. Why would a mere traffic cop risk her life at a time like this?

Besides justice.

Jiang Tian couldn't understand it. She was just a traffic officer; speaking up and stepping forward at this moment was practically gambling with her own life.

The key point was that if it weren't Jiang Tian, ​​but someone else, this young girl's fate would likely be dire.

But not now.

Still smiling.

Still smiling, you idiot! Look behind you!

Xie Sisi was almost going crazy, screaming internally, but she could only watch helplessly.

Because she sensed the woman's murderous intent. She raised her hand, raised her gun—it was too fast.

Bang!!!

Almost instantly.

A gunshot rang out.

Buzz!!!

Xie Sisi felt a chill run through her body.

She stared into the distance.

She had thought Jiang Tian was dead, completely done for, his head blown off, but unexpectedly, the next moment, he was gone.

Xie Sisi's gaze flickered with confusion as she lost sight of Jiang Tian for a moment, then, as she regained her senses,

she suddenly saw him.

Jiang Tian, ​​like a ghost, had already bent down, his other hand gripping the wrist of the deranged woman. The woman's hand, holding the gun, was now raised high in the air.

It was too fast.

Xie Sisi's eyes widened, her face filled with disbelief.

Is he insane?

Is this even human?

Has some unknown evolution begun in this world, somewhere she doesn't know about?

Is this something a human could do?

Is this speed even possible for a human?

Before Xie Sisi could react,

that woman, being a member of the underworld, was incredibly fast, and her reactions were even faster. In this sudden turn of events, Ding Yaguan was utterly astonished.

In fact, the moment he heard Jiang Tian's name and recognized him, Ding Yaguan instantly made up his mind to kill him.

Kill Jiang Tian, ​​kill him here, and then leave the country immediately.

Many criminal gangs in Yunhai share a common understanding: the root cause of all this trouble, the reason criminal groups are struggling so much, lies entirely with Jiang Tian. If Jiang Tian dies, it won't be long, not even a few years.

In this fertile ground most prone to darkness, criminal groups that have disappeared will reappear and return in even greater numbers.

Driven by profit, countless people will join in, vying for territory.

The entire situation in Yunhai will be infinitely more chaotic than before.

But now.

Ding Yaguan swore that he acted without any authorization, almost without anyone's expectation. Even his trusted confidant, Cui Hao, was stunned, seemingly not expecting it.

That's right, killing someone in public is already explosive enough; if the boss were to shoot someone here as well.

Good heavens, that wouldn't be as simple as just causing a major incident.

But now.

Cui Hao looked at the scene before him.

He couldn't help but swallow, then twisted his neck.

Sure enough, after the gunshots rang out, many residents on various floors of the Zishan Apartment complex had already drawn back their curtains and craned their necks to look down. When they saw what was happening, their faces lit up with excitement; after all, everyone has a natural talent for watching a spectacle.

Now it had attracted attention, and more and more people were turning their gazes down.

Wow!

Those who saw it exclaimed in amazement. They rarely saw such a thrilling scene, even in movies, especially those on nearby floors witnessing a live battle.

Their expressions were incredibly excited. Those with cameras were already taking them out to film, while those without were using their phones to roughly capture the scene before them.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​and Ding Yaguan opposite him...

Jiang Tian, ​​however, didn't seem to care.

After all, the exposure was already underway. Besides, everything comes at a price; there's always some sacrifice involved. Jiang Tian's name is now practically infamous online, almost on par with that second-generation rich kid, Li.

And with the added fuel to the fire, it's even worse than before.

Killing innocent people and claiming credit for them.

This has almost certainly aroused public outrage.

But if you ask what case it was about—murdering innocent people and then claiming credit for it—those people online wouldn't know. However, the mere mention of Jiang Tian's name is automatically associated with that.

It's that ridiculous.

Anyway, it's about to be exposed, so Jiang Tian doesn't care. Besides, before his photos surface, no video could possibly appear online.

As for Ding Yaguan, nobody knows.

"I didn't expect to run into you. What, the recent public opinion hasn't overwhelmed you? You actually have time to support a minor celebrity?"

Ding Yaguan lowered his gaze, and the instant he spoke, his other hand directly poked Jiang Tian's eye.

The speed was incredible, the movements even faster, almost swift, precise, and ruthless.

It gave no chance to react.

In fact, if it were anyone else like Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian could swear that very few people in the world could react to Ding Yaguan's continuous barrage of attacks.

But the person he encountered was Jiang Tian.

Well, sorry.

*Snap!*



Chapter 635 I bet your gun isn't loaded.


Jiang Tian didn't hesitate for a moment; in that instant, he grabbed Ding Ya Guan's outstretched fingers.

"So fast." Ding Ya Guan's pupils contracted.

*Snap!!!*

The next moment.

A sharp pain shot straight to the top of her head.

"Hiss!!!"

Ding Ya Guan gasped sharply. True to her reputation as a woman who had survived this ordeal, even in this situation, she only gasped, her forehead covered in cold sweat, but she managed not to cry out.

She just stared intently at Jiang Tian.

"Release Big Sister."

Cui Hao's eyes instantly turned red.

"All of you, get him! He's alone. Kill him!" Cui Hao shouted the order.

What a joke! Although the situation was completely out of control, although it seemed like things couldn't be resolved peacefully, and it seemed like everything was about to be exposed.

But it didn't matter.

At the very least, Ding Ya Guan couldn't be allowed to get caught.

Many things, though seemingly insignificant until weighed, can become incredibly powerful once placed on a scale.

Ding Yaguan has connections, not only with the branch bureau chief but also with higher-ups and leverage over her. But as the saying goes, if she's not caught, all is well; but if she is, she's finished.

Everyone will want her dead.

This kind of thing actually happens frequently. Even the criminal suspects or gang leaders arrested by Jiang Tian are under strict surveillance, with armed forces from Beijing even deployed for their guard. Yet, even so, murder plots still occur from time to time, some successful, some not.

Overall, even the anti-corruption bureau is incredibly busy.

Moreover, this isn't from Yunhai; it's from Beijing. The situation here is no longer under control. After Jiang Tian revealed just the tip of the iceberg, the entire Yunhai region has completely erupted in chaos. Simply put, once the curtain was lifted, everything was laid bare.

A large number of reinforcements rushed from Beijing, partly to maintain order and partly to oversee the overall situation.

Jiang Tian, ​​while seemingly doing the most work, was actually the one lifting the lid. The impact of arresting someone was immense; the people behind them needed to be investigated, their connections needed to be examined, and their criminal records needed to be reviewed.

In short, no bad person could be let go, and no innocent person could be wrongly accused.

If Ding Yaguan hadn't been arrested, that would have been fine. But once arrested, those behind Ding Yaguan would be the first to want him dead. Besides, Ding Yaguan couldn't withstand an investigation, and neither could the entire Yunya Group.

"Kill him, kill him." Even with two fingers crushed by Jiang Tian, ​​Ding Yaguan calmly directed his men to kill Jiang Tian.

At this moment, the circumstances didn't matter.

The most important thing was to kill Jiang Tian, ​​escape Yunhai, and then nothing would happen.

Even if the heavens were to be struck, once he was abroad, overseas, could they still find him anywhere in the world?

Moreover, Ding Yaguan had already realized that this was his only way out.

"Understood."

"Quick, kill him."

"So many people are watching."

"What are you afraid of? There are so many of us. Let's kill them quickly and run!"

"That's right, something's happened. Big Sister's in front, what's there to be afraid of?"

These thirty-odd burly men in black weren't first-time criminals, so they had no qualms about it. Besides, there was no turning back now.

Since Big Sister had already spoken,

*Whoosh!*

Some of them drew sharp machetes, their blades gleaming coldly, and charged towards Jiang Tian.

And Jiang Tian was still surrounded.

In this situation,

it could be said that he was almost certainly going to die.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, swiftly snatched Ding Yaguan's pistol.

Bang! Bang! Bang!

The deafening gunfire erupted instantly in the Zishan Apartment complex.

Zishan Apartment was, in general, a budget-friendly option, mainly because apartment buildings were relatively inexpensive, making it suitable for out-of-towners. However, the overall facilities and environment were relatively basic.

Simply put, the soundproofing was poor, as evidenced by the continuous gunfire.

Many people were completely stunned.

Then they opened the curtains to check the situation, and when they saw the source of the explosion, they were even more stunned.

Xie Sisi was running.

She only ran two or three steps.

Her pace went from fast to slow, and finally stopped, almost as if she had come to a complete halt.

Eyes widened.

They stared at the scene unfolding before them.

They couldn't even see how Jiang Tian had acted. The tall, burly Ding Yaguan had not only had his gun seized by Jiang Tian, ​​but Jiang Tian had also grabbed Ding Yaguan by the neck, ignoring his struggles and thrashing about as if he were clamped in iron pincers.

Most terrifyingly, after grabbing Ding Yaguan's neck, Jiang Tian held up a pistol in his other hand, the muzzle now smoking from overheating.

Right in front of Jiang Tian, ​​five figures—or rather, five corpses—lay on the ground.

Seven people.

Including the two who had just died, seven people were dead.

Seven lives!

For those witnessing murder for the first time, this was a terrifying shock, a shattering of their worldview, leaving them feeling as if their very being was being torn apart.

Cui Hao stared at the corpses strewn across the ground, his legs trembling uncontrollably.

But...

The next moment, Cui Hao kicked a burly man in black beside him, roaring, "What are you afraid of? What are you trembling for? There were only six bullets left in the gun, and we're out of bullets now. Go and kill him!"

"That's right, out of bullets."

"Charge!"

"Kill him!"

Just as the others were cheering him on...

Cui Hao pulled a pistol from his pocket, sneering at Jiang Tian, ​​"Out of bullets, huh? Now it's our turn. Kid, you're lucky today. We'll let you die right here. Of course, your family will have to come looking for you. Just wait, kid, your family will be coming down to find you soon."

Bang!!!

The next moment, a gunshot rang out.

Jiang Tian, ​​expressionless, raised his hand and fired a shot.



Chapter 636 The Price of Greed is Life


Cui Hao knew Jiang Tian's gun was empty. There were six bullets in total. Besides the one shot fired by the older sister at the beginning, which felled five people on the spot—in other words, five people were shot—all six bullets were gone.

No bullets left.

I'm not just betting your gun is empty.

I'm even pretty sure your gun doesn't have a single bullet left.

Cui Hao held the pistol in his hand. Like the mantis stalking the cicada, unaware of the oriole behind, Cui Hao smiled: "Kid, didn't expect that, did you? You thought only the older sister had a pistol? Wrong, terribly wrong. Didn't expect that, did you? I have another gun. Your abilities are good, but unfortunately, I've seen many geniuses, but these geniuses are either corrupt or already dead. Do you know why? Don't worry, now you know."

There are many geniuses.

But as I said before, for criminals, we can make many mistakes, you can perform many times, you can arrest many of us, but there are many people behind us.

Therefore.

Once you make a mistake, then I'm sorry, the price is your life. We have countless reckless thugs, but your life is no different from theirs—just one bullet.

"Cough cough cough, not bad, indeed very strong. Of all the men I've ever met, I admit, you are the most terrifying and horrifying non-human I've ever seen. But unfortunately, you're going to die here this time. No wonder you dared to come to me; it was you. It's all your fault for being too careless. If you had gathered a lot of people beforehand, I might really be finished this time. But you dared to come alone, so I'm sorry, your life is left here."

Ding Yaguan's face was flushed red, and her voice was gradually becoming cough-like. Mainly because Jiang Tian was gripping her quite tightly, at one point Ding Yaguan even felt that Jiang Tian was going to strangle her.

But she still forced herself to speak.

At this moment, Jiang Tian should have been insane.

Or perhaps begging for mercy in terror.

Ding Yaguan had only two reactions in mind, because she had seen this situation countless times. Without exception, everyone either became completely insane and lost their minds,

or knelt on the ground and surrendered completely.

Faced with death, they presented two extremes, so Ding Yaguan wanted to see how the legendary Jiang Tian would react to death.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian spoke.

"Oh, you mean I'm the strongest man you've ever met? Does that mean there's actually a woman as strong as me?" Jiang Tian asked, seemingly with a hint of surprise.

Huh?

Are you crazy?

"You're going to die, do you know that?"

"Why were you even able to ask that?"

Ding Yaguan coughed violently, nearly choking on Jiang Tian's words.

"Kill, kill..." Ding Yaguan stammered, her face flushed red.

In reality, Jiang Tian was deliberately controlling his strength. If he wanted, he could have snapped Ding Yaguan's neck in a fraction of a second.

He was only able to speak because Jiang Tian wanted to know what Ding Yaguan would say, to see if she could reveal some information, something she thought she had won.

But unfortunately...

So Jiang Tian lost interest in continuing the conversation.

"Looks like you were lying to me," Jiang Tian said with slight regret.

The higher you climb, the colder it gets.

She really thought having a woman as powerful as him would be a good opportunity for some serious sparring. After all, being invincible is lonely, and having no opponent is even lonelier.

"Die."

Cui Hao held a pistol in his hand.

He fired at Jiang Tian, ​​not upwards, of course.

Mainly to avoid accidentally hitting his older sister; he only aimed at Jiang Tian.

But as he tightened his grip...

Bang!!!

A sharp pain shot through Cui Hao's wrist.

Then, with a splatter, blood gushed out, even landing on Cui Hao's face.

This made Cui Hao's face look even more ferocious; the blood-covered face only added to his crazed nature.

And the pistol in Cui Hao's hand fell to the ground with a clatter.

His expression froze, his blood-stained face completely frozen, his eyes wide as he stared at the person opposite him.

He was staring intently at Jiang Tian.

“You have bullets?”

“Why, why can you fire bullets?”

“Didn’t you just fire six bullets?”

Cui Hao had gone mad.

Where did this bullet come from? After all, the fervor that the surrounding men in black had just mustered had cooled with that single gunshot.

Holding his machete, he seemed to have lost much of his strength.

His gaze was filled with doubt and fear.

After all, no one wanted to rush towards a pistol that could shoot them at any moment; otherwise, what was the difference between that and being shot for cutting in line?

“Who said this is your older sister’s gun? Your older sister’s gun is on the ground. Look, I threw that gun on the ground a long time ago. This gun is mine,” Jiang Tian explained.

Cui Hao's heart pounded, then he roared menacingly, "Charge! Kill him! Kill him! I'll give you a million, plus a company bonus! Even if you don't kill him, the first person to charge in and chop him down will get a hundred thousand!"

Since ancient times, even before a battle, money is always given out, especially for suicide squads. They're always paid before setting off.

It has to be said, it truly incites greed.

These burly men in black, who had initially hesitated, went completely berserk upon hearing about the money.

What does a million mean? At this time, a million could buy a house in some areas of Yunhai.

But you have to understand, this is a million for a house in Yunhai. In other, less developed cities, a million at this point in time is an terrifying fortune.

The remaining men exchanged glances; greed ultimately triumphed over reason.

"It's a pity. No matter how much money you have, can it compare to life? What good is money if you're dead?" Jiang Tian shook his head with slight regret.

Looking up, he saw a burly man in black approaching. The man grabbed Jiang Tian's wrist, the one holding the gun, with both hands. He was clearly experienced and possessed considerable strength; it seemed he had some training.

Jiang Tian was slightly surprised. Unfortunately, when the man in black grabbed Jiang Tian's gun-wielding arm, he immediately tried to use a hand-to-hand combat technique to throw Jiang Tian to the ground.

However, when he turned and exerted force, he discovered that Jiang Tian didn't budge an inch.

The burly man in black was stunned.

Thump!!!

Then, the burly man's heart skipped a beat.

You see, in the instant of combat, many people can actually sense the strength and skill of their opponent. This burly man in black was no exception; his expression changed drastically, and he wanted to run away as fast as he could.

But the next moment...

Boom!!!

A very, very muffled, and incredibly, teeth-grinding sound, accompanied by the cracking of bones.

"Ah!!!"



Chapter 637 One per second, more people, or more bullets?


The burly man in black screamed almost instinctively, writhing in excruciating pain in his side—yes, his kidney, the area below his ribs—a piercing and terrifying agony.

Almost instantly.

Visibly, the burly man in black was kicked in a bizarre arc, like a fully drawn bow, stopping in sight for a fleeting moment, like a cannonball, the man in black transforming into a streak of black light from mid-air.

Then, bang, bang! Two more burly men in black were struck in quick succession on the road. Those struck felt as if they had been hit by a truck traveling at 100 miles per hour; one man was instantly bleeding profusely.

As for those who could fly freely through the air—those with some skill, at least trained fighters—had already been kicked by Jiang Tian, ​​letting out a scream in mid-air before completely losing all signs of life.

"What a joke! That kick, in that spot, even an ordinary person's strength would cripple them, let alone Jiang Tian's. A slight exertion on his side waist—that's an instant kill.

A true instant kill, not just unconsciousness, but death on the spot.

The two burly men in black who were hit lay on the ground, seemingly lifeless. One of them was sent flying several meters before his head slammed into the wall.

This horrific scene terrified the others.

They started running, then slowed down, then walked slowly, finally stopping in their tracks. They were only about ten meters away from Jiang Tian—not far, actually very close.

From here, Xie Sisi could even see the details on Jiang Tian's face, even the subtle expressions on his lips.

She could also see how ruthless and terrifying Jiang Tian's attack was.

But was that the end?

No.

It was all visible to the naked eye.

Jiang Tian turned and kicked a nearby burly man in black, sending him flying.

The man bled from all seven orifices mid-air, looking as if he'd been kicked to pieces.

The corpse landed in front of Xie Sisi.

Xie Sisi stared, completely stunned, at the still-warm body beneath her feet. The man's unseeing eyes and bleeding orifices made her legs tremble.

The crucial point was that in that instant...

Jiang Tian released Ding Yaguan, grabbed the hair of the man in black beside him, and yanked him down, followed by a knee strike.

The man in black collapsed limply to the ground.

The next moment, Cui Hao seemed to react, hurriedly crouching down to pick up his pistol.

Bang!!!

Cui Hao's arms flared limply, blood dripping from his other hand.

It's the same story.

As for the lowly soldiers, Jiang Tian could act freely without needing to restrain himself.

However, sorry, if you were an important person, Jiang Tian would generally spare your life. For example, he spared Cui Hao's life now, though Cui Hao lost both arms.

The other men in black stared wide-eyed at the sight.

"Don't kill me!"

"No, don't do it, boss, don't kill me!"

"Don't kill me, I surrender."

"Damn it, I'll kill you."

Seven or eight of the thirty-odd men in black died instantly.

This caused a huge shock among the other men in black. Some, in extreme fear, immediately collapsed to the ground in surrender, their hands instinctively covering the back of their heads in a perfectly executed posture.

This was quite normal; those who had risen through the ranks in the underworld often encountered similar situations before they became powerful, and were frequently arrested in the past, so this process was very standardized and familiar to them.

Some turned to run.

Others, their ferocity completely ignited, saw Cui Hao's hands crippled and desperately wanted to prove themselves.

After all, if they could kill Jiang Tian at this moment, what would the consequences be? A promotion and a raise, directly reaching the core of the group, possessing the power to defy fate, and transcending social classes.

But unfortunately for them.

Five or six people like this rushed forward.

But Jiang Tian's movements were swift.

Bang bang bang!!

Jiang Tian's pistol showed no mercy.

These men, seeing that Jiang Tian hadn't killed Cui Hao when he fired, were emboldened by his own malice. But the next moment, their minds went blank, and they lost all consciousness.

At this point...

More than thirty men in black got out of the car, but those still breathing were...

That's fourteen or fifteen people.

The key point is that seven or eight of these fourteen or fifteen people were crouching on the ground.

Seven or eight of them were already trying to escape.

Jiang Tian calmly changed his magazine, didn't even look, and started firing at the seven or eight fleeing people one by one.

One, two, three...

Jiang Tian fired at the target until the sixth man, then leisurely reloaded. The last fleeing man in black immediately dropped to the ground, crying and surrendering. Only then did a slight smile appear on Jiang Tian's face.

He seemed to be finally in a better mood, appearing somewhat happy.

Then, Jiang Tian's unhurried gaze fell upon the five or six men who had rushed forward to pick up the pistol. Five of them were already shot in the head and lying on the ground. The last one had survived because Jiang Tian's pistol was out of bullets.

So he had miraculously survived.

Frozen in place, trembling, he watched Jiang Tian approach, a grimace spreading across his face.

Green.

A look of utter terror.

Jiang Tian glanced at him but remained silent.

The man in black clutched his chest.

His entire body was trembling, and his face was deathly pale.

When Jiang Tian placed his hand on the man's shoulder, the man in black turned pale, then suddenly convulsed and collapsed to the ground, lifeless.

He died of fright.

Or did he die suddenly?

Jiang Tian scratched his head.

But it didn't matter to Jiang Tian anymore.

Jiang Tian glanced at the corpses scattered on the ground. His recent massacre was mainly due to his being alone and outnumbered.

If these people were to all flee, Jiang Tian alone wouldn't be able to handle it all. Since these people were a scourge on society, Jiang Tian didn't mind cleaning them up.

Originally, there were thirty-four or thirty-five people at the scene.

But now, those killed by Jiang Tian, ​​those he just shot at, and those who were scared to death by him—a total of twenty people—lay on the ground in pools of blood, each with their eyes wide open in death.

Of those still alive, only about ten remain.

"Why bother?"



Chapter 638 A sheer veil adorns the entire venue; who else is there?


Blood flowed like a river. In reality, looking at the blood-soaked ground and the remaining people, crouching or lying on the ground with their heads in their hands, the scene was utterly silent.

If...

The only thing that made Jiang Tian frown was that, looking up at the apartment building, the gunshots had attracted many people who were peeking through the windows or gathering to watch.

Of course...

Although it was a minor inconvenience and caused some minor negative impact, in reality, when the city police came and investigated door-to-door, they could delete all the videos, and it wouldn't be a big problem. Even if some escaped, it wouldn't matter.

After all, this was about fighting crime; even if some videos leaked out, it wouldn't matter; it would be a further deterrent to criminals.

His gaze sharpened, accompanied by a series of crisp footsteps.

Ding Yaguan's pupils contracted continuously, large beads of sweat rolled down his face, and as Jiang Tian approached, Ding Yaguan even limped backward.

Jiang Tian's appearance just now truly terrified Ding Yaguan.

She could swear that in her entire life, Ding Yaguan had never witnessed a scene more terrifying, or more despairing.

What kind of psychopath was this?

Even if there had been occasional murders or gang fights before, there had never been anything like this—a complete madness, utterly insane.

"You're from the Municipal Public Security Bureau!

Why are you so much more terrifying than these criminal gangs when you kill?"

"Don't come any closer."

Ding Yaguan's voice was hoarse.

Just now, that scene made Ding Yaguan swear that he would never forget it, even if he died.

Unfortunately, Ding Yaguan's voice was hoarse as he tried to drive Jiang Tian away.

But as he approached, the footsteps continued relentlessly.

"Don't come any closer! Don't come any closer!"

Ding Yaguan sat on the ground, retreating in terror.

Why? Ding Yaguan couldn't believe there were people like this. Was this a butcher?

The most terrifying thing was that whether it was firearms,

or close-quarters combat and being surrounded, it was almost utterly hopeless, leaving no room for resistance.

Dare to approach Jiang Tian? He could kick you to death, or rather, he could kill several people in a second, and every move was swift and decisive, killing in a fraction of a second.

And firing a gun was even easier; a single shot could kill several people in a second.

"Just look at you, killing like this! Don't your eyes get dry?"

Corpses littered the ground.

More than twenty corpses. Of the survivors, only about ten remain. Ding Yaguan and Cui Hao were spared by Jiang Tian's men for evidence review and investigation.

Not a single one of the others was injured.

They were either completely lifeless or perfectly unharmed.

And that was the most terrifying part, because apart from Jiang Tian deliberately showing mercy, there weren't any wounded at all!

"Now you know fear, you're terrified, but it's too late." Jiang Tian shook his head. If only he had known this would happen.

Jiang Tian had never shown mercy to these people.

As Jiang Tian's voice rang out, the deathly silence was shattered.

Especially for those who witnessed this scene firsthand, it was nothing short of a terrifying psychological trauma.

Xie Sisi, who was only a few steps away from Jiang Tian, ​​felt her legs were incredibly heavy. She tried desperately to take a step, but unfortunately, even with all her strength, she couldn't budge an inch.

Her head was buzzing, and she stared in terror at Jiang Tian not far away.

Her lips were cracked and dry.

She's gone mad.

Completely mad.

A serial killer?

No?

Until Jiang Tian's voice rang out.

Xie Sisi snapped back to reality in a daze. Was it too late?

As Xie Sisi's mind slowly raced through Jiang Tian's words, the tall, imposing woman opposite him stared at her with a stiff expression.

"If I were you, I wouldn't have any other thoughts right now. I would await the judgment of the law. This time, I won't show mercy. I spared you only because you still have value in life. But if you continue to stubbornly resist and assault the police, then perhaps only death awaits you." Jiang Tian's voice was calm.

It was as if nothing had just happened.

Just now, Jiang Tian had acted as if he had stepped on some ants, so casually, exactly like his mood before he acted.

And that was the most terrifying thing.

You must know that Jiang Tian had just killed more than twenty people. That was real; every move he made was a life, one or several lives, with absolutely no room for mercy.

The stench of blood at the scene was so strong that it made Xie Sisi's stomach churn; the food she had just eaten was already churning inside her.

"Ugh!!!"

The next moment, Xie Sisi couldn't hold it in any longer and vomited violently.

The vomiting was devastating.

Most importantly, several burly men in black lay dead on the ground, their eyes wide open in disbelief.

Xie Sisi stared, completely dumbfounded.

The commotion also made Jiang Tian turn his head slightly. He recognized the young girl; she was very distinctive.

She was also wearing a police uniform. Although she was a traffic cop, when she saw him being bullied, even though the perpetrators were not to be trifled with, she rushed to his aid immediately, completely disregarding her own safety.

Such things were not uncommon in Yunhai. There were too many hidden underworld bosses in the city, and in Yunhai itself.

All sorts of crimes.

There are also various underworld bosses, almost none of whom are easy to mess with. They're known for their violence and brawling, which happens almost daily, with gangland shootouts occurring frequently.

Any criminal organization will inevitably experience friction. To expand their influence, they must engage in constant fighting.

Whoever resolves the conflict wins and expands their wealth.

In reality, it's like the Warring States period, with its seven warring states constantly battling each other. Today you win, tomorrow I win, and if I keep winning, then sorry, I'll be able to take over all your territory.

And for underworld bosses, territory is most directly reflected in increased wealth.

The larger the territory, the greater the criminal power.

Recently, the entire Yunhai underworld has gradually matured—yes, matured. After the initial chaotic and frenzied fighting, they've all become more sophisticated.

None of them can truly defeat the others, and, in order to make money together, their conflicts have significantly decreased.



Chapter 639 I have never seen such a great person in my life.


In their words, it's all about making money; whoever has the ability makes the money—it's that simple.

During this period, especially with those from overseas, or those collaborating with neighboring Myanmar, and even some with local warlords, such instances were countless.

These are the root causes of the chaos.

After all, the border between the two sides is extremely long, leading to rampant smuggling and collusion.

Often, serious crimes don't even require these underworld bosses to lift a finger. A little money is all it takes to hire desperate criminals from the neighboring region to handle the situation, then they're happily sent away from Yunhai, and the cases are never solved.

Although Yunhai may seem to have a low crime rate, the actual number of unsolved cases is far higher than in other areas.

Even the clues and cases uncovered by Jiang Tian's investigation, which pointed to Myanmar, ultimately remained unsolved.

Besides Myanmar, due to the previous frenzied development, there were other places where criminal gangs were being established, such as the one Bai Qiu had uncovered.

In his previous life, Bai Qiu had even suffered horrific torture and sacrificed himself in this very crime case.

And, judging by the timeline, that time shouldn't be far off.

Jiang Tian thought to himself, not looking at Ding Yaguan. Even if Ding Yaguan had a gun now, what was there to fear?

He believed Ding Yaguan was a smart man.

At this point, if he fully cooperated with the city police in solving various cases and confessed to all his crimes, he might still be able to survive.

Of course, it was only a possibility. In practice, in these kinds of cases, once the investigation was complete, there was a 99% chance it would be the death penalty. However, if there was significant meritorious service, a suspended death sentence might be possible.

Then, with good behavior, the sentence could be reduced to seventeen or eighteen years, and he could be released.

And return to society.

"Are you alright?"

Just as Xie Sisi was vomiting uncontrollably, a gentle voice rang out. Xie Sisi, slightly dazed, looked up and met Jiang Tian's familiar yet terrifying face.

They were inches apart, their eyes barely twenty centimeters apart, they could even hear each other's breathing.

Buzz!!!

Xie Sisi's head exploded.

Her mind went completely blank, exploding.

Her eyes widened.

Xie Sisi stared at that terrifying face—a demon, a devil.

An executioner.

Countless words surged wildly through Xie Sisi's mind.

"Ah!!!"

They barely locked eyes for two seconds before Xie Sisi screamed in terror, "Don't kill me! Don't kill me! Waaah! It's not my fault! I wasn't an accomplice! I was just passing by! Don't kill me, brother! I just graduated!"

She had acted impulsively in the heat of the moment, but as her emotions cooled and terror overwhelmed her, Xie Sisi completely exposed herself.

Of course, Jiang Tian could understand.

The screams were deafening, especially for Jiang Tian, ​​whose senses were incredibly acute, even superhumanly sensitive to sound waves. Although his tolerance was high, the sheer volume and proximity of the screams made his eardrums vibrate.

Jiang Tian raised a hand to clean his ears, his lips twitching, but a smile remained on his face: "Don't worry, I won't do anything to you. You have to believe me, I'm on your side. I'm a decent person."

A decent person?

Xie Sisi almost spat out her drink. A decent person? Look at all these corpses! How could he lie like that? A decent person?

And there were people standing on the sidelines?

Weren't they accomplices?

Accomplices of the thug who killed over twenty people?

Xie Sisi's face turned green with fear. She had just graduated!

Her beautiful life, her beautiful life dedicated to maintaining world peace, had only just begun, and now it was going to end?

Just as Xie Sisi was frozen in shock, Jiang Tian's voice rang out again.

"Don't be afraid, don't worry. You've done a good job. Although you didn't help much, your courage to stand up has already defeated 99% of the people. Congratulations, you've done a good job." Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

A person who stands up at this crucial moment is undoubtedly filled with a sense of justice and is willing to disregard their own safety. What, you say the other person is cowardly?

Sorry, a gentleman judges by actions, not intentions. If you judge by intentions, there are no gentlemen in the world.

So what if she's cowardly? She deserves it because the other person stood up.

Even if it was just a reminder, Jiang Tian could apply for personal-level merit for her.

At this moment, Xie Sisi was terrified.

That's right.

She seemed to have been trying to remind Jiang Tian, ​​and she did, even helping him.

Legally speaking, hadn't she unintentionally become an accomplice of thugs?

Buzz!!!

Thinking of this, Xie Sisi's face turned green, filled with despair.

The most terrifying thing was...

Just then...

Woo-hoo-hoo!!!

A chaotic, fluctuating cacophony of police sirens blared, forming a continuous chorus, creating a terrifying and intimidating atmosphere.

Moreover, it was very clear that the sirens were from far more than just three or five police cars; the number was definitely much greater.

"Brother, it has nothing to do with me, it really has nothing to do with me," Xie Sisi cried, forgetting her fear.

A thug responsible for over twenty deaths.

Yet he claimed she was an accomplice.

The crucial point was that she had actually helped him at the beginning.

Legally speaking, this seemed to constitute suspicion of accomplice, meaning that although it had nothing to do with her, unintentionally, she seemed to have become an accomplice.

Even if it was all a misunderstanding, negligence is still a crime. Her job, her future, her life that had just begun—everything seemed to be coming to a complete end.

The more Xie Sisi thought about it, the more she felt this way.

There was no hope in this life.

It's over. She's completely finished.

Her beautiful life hadn't even begun, and it was already about to end completely.

"Hmm, it has nothing to do with you. Are you sure?" Jiang Tian asked, his face full of surprise.

This was the first time he'd seen someone who didn't even want credit.

This is a personal achievement!

Moreover, normally, the starting point should be a third-class merit award. If Jiang Tian said a few good words, a second-class merit award wouldn't be out of the question.

And what does a second-class merit award mean for someone who has just graduated and joined the police system?

It at least means that promotions in the first two or three rounds are absolutely guaranteed. Before the age of thirty, reaching the level of section chief to deputy director is a sure thing.

Without this achievement, one might still be stuck at the section chief level at thirty.

The difference between these two is enormous.

This is the first time Jiang Tian has heard such a request.

“Brother, it really doesn’t matter. We have absolutely no relationship. I haven’t helped you. I beg you, brother.” Xie Sisi was almost crying.

“Well, alright.” Jiang Tian scratched his head, then felt a surge of emotion.

Look at her, even ignoring the merit, what an outstanding young woman!

Xie Sisi had a pure and innocent smile on her face, and even Jiang Tian seemed much more pleasing to the eye.



Chapter 640 Are they all asleep?


The intense sirens of police cars snapped the scene back to reality. Even for the men in black, the sound of sirens was like a savior – a rescuer had arrived.

Indeed.

Even if they were arrested, so what? At least they could survive, and with luck, they might get out.

But this monster killed indiscriminately; it was a brutal massacre.

How could such a heartless person exist in the world? And this person, you call him a policeman?

Are you insane?

The men in black were completely devastated.

As for the two junior traffic police officers, upon hearing the sirens, they breathed a deep sigh of relief and completely relaxed. At that moment, both Wu Zhongting and Xie Sisi seemed to have completely released all the tension after extreme stress; their bodies were sore and weak. Xie Sisi even realized then that she had been sweating profusely.

So much so that Xie Sisi felt completely soaked, drenched in cold sweat.

“Aren’t you going to run?” Xie Sisi asked.

At this moment, Jiang Tian was still thinking about how to reward Xie Sisi. Although she didn’t need it, Jiang Tian believed that such courageous behavior deserved encouragement and praise.

Faced with evil, even in dangerous situations, she still stepped forward, disregarding her own safety. Such a person must be rewarded.

However, while Jiang Tian was still pondering this, hearing Xie Sisi’s voice brought him back to his senses. His eyes widened in confusion as he asked, “Run?”

“Run what?”

Jiang Tian asked, bewildered.

No.

Now, the entire scene is under control.

The First-Class Merit Award is right there. Once everything is verified, the First-Class Merit Award will be easily within his grasp.

The key point is that, for Jiang Tian, ​​the First-Class Merit Citation isn't actually that important anymore, but it's still the highest honor.

This kind of honor...

Even though Jiang Tian has received many, each one symbolizes a completely different meaning and reward, and is equally important.

This is the foundation for Jiang Tian's future promotions.

Especially since Jiang Tian also has a stamp collecting reward.

Now Jiang Tian already has seven First-Class Merits, not counting other honors, and a Special-Class Merit Citation, etc. Just in terms of material rewards, the system's rewards are generous—a Rolls-Royce. Although Qin Shiyu's Rolls-Royce is always kept at his Purple Mountain Villa, and Qin Shiyu has said that she can drive it whenever she wants, she doesn't use it often.

But living off a woman is shameful; what belongs to others is never as good as what you have yourself.

This First-Class Merit Citation also symbolizes a big Rolls-Royce.

Such a generous reward, and you want me to run away?

I don't want this reward?

"What, you want to embezzle from me?"

Of course, Jiang Tian was just joking in his mind.

"Why should I run?" Jiang Tian scratched his head.

"The police cars are here, aren't you afraid?" Xie Sisi asked, bewildered.

In such a terrifying scene, regardless of who the other party is, Jiang Tian's status as a thug is undeniable. He'll at the very least be taken away and shot.

Shouldn't they be running for their lives right now?

How can they be so calm? Are they really not afraid of dying?

"Why would I run when the police are here?" Jiang Tian laughed.

Just as Jiang Tian spoke,

the sirens grew louder and louder, and the police cars entered Zishan Apartments.

The armed police, special forces, and other special forces were all mobilized.

After all, Jiang Tian had called for backup and mentioned needing assistance with a relatively important case. Song Liguo had mentally calculated that if others said it was "relatively serious," it was probably just moderately serious, not a major problem.

But when Jiang Tian mentioned needing assistance with a "relatively serious case," it meant it was likely a significant case, based on past experience.

The severity Jiang Tian described was completely different from what others had said.

From afar,

Song Liguo saw a figure lying on the ground inside the police car.

Good heavens.

"Dozens of people, I suppose," Song Liguo sat bolt upright, clicking his tongue in amazement.

"Yeah, it looks like they've all been brought under control," Wei Xun remarked from the side.

More than thirty people.

The key is, it seems Jiang Tian alone has managed to control them.
