Moreover, while ordinary people wouldn't be a problem—even a little training could intimidate them—desperate criminals are a completely different story.

Desperate criminals or criminal gangs—intimidation is utterly useless. Trying to scare them with sheer force is laughable. If they want to get you, even if you fight back, they can be infinitely more ruthless and reckless.

These are desperados.

And they have families.

Therefore, when dealing with desperate criminals or criminals, a single person is practically ineffective as a deterrent. An overwhelming advantage in numbers and weaponry is necessary to render these criminals hopeless and force them to submit.

But now, Jiang Tian alone has managed to subdue over thirty men, all of whom appear quite burly, and bring them to the ground.

This is incredible.

"Only Jiang Tian could do that. Besides our Captain Jiang, no one else could pull this off. Judging by the scale, it's definitely a sizable criminal organization," Song Liguo couldn't help but remark, as the car drew closer.

Song Liguo was even more impressed by the scene being brought under control.

Damn it, who else?

Who else could make a criminal organization kneel down like this?

These are over thirty people!

The only thing that puzzled Song Liguo was why they were lying there haphazardly, some far apart and some close together.

“Xiao Jiang is lacking in this area. Look at those guys over there, so close to the escape route and cover. If they really run, there's no way to control them. They need to be kept in one place and effectively guarded.” Song Liguo understood.

After all, in his view, while Jiang Tian's entry into the team was extraordinary, experience takes time to accumulate. Song Liguo didn't believe anyone could be born with innate knowledge; that was simply impossible.

So, he's still too young; he still has a lot to learn and grow.

As Song Liguo thought this, he got out of the car and saw someone lying face down on the ground.

But from a distance, he might have misjudged; the person's posture seemed a bit off.

Song Liguo took a closer look.

Good heavens.



Chapter 641 Is this a judgment of fate? No, this is a first-class merit.


The man was sprawled on the ground, dressed in black, and clearly very burly.

However, with the passage of time, his face, having been dead for some time, had long since lost all color.

It was deathly pale.

His eyes, too, had completely lost their light, turning a dull gray-white, utterly lifeless.

“Dead?”

Song Liguo's expression instantly turned grave. Wei Xun, standing beside him, also narrowed his eyes, then quickly turned and ordered, “Everyone, get out of the vehicles and immediately take control of the scene as instructed.”

With a whoosh!

A group of over a hundred armed men quickly disembarked and sealed off the entire Zishan Villa.

Yes, the entire Zishan Villa.

Mainly because the situation seemed to be spiraling out of control.

Song Liguo had initially thought that once the scene was under control, these men lying on the ground wouldn't dare resist, but he was completely wrong. This was not the case at all!

"Damn it, everyone lying on the ground looks like they're dead."

This...

If the death toll is really too high, something must have happened at the scene, or rather, Jiang Tian has lost his temper.

And to be honest, Song Liguo has actually witnessed Jiang Tian lose his temper before.

Precisely because he's seen Jiang Tian lose his temper before, Song Liguo is genuinely afraid that if it were leaked, it would cause a truly terrifying uproar.

A killing machine, when Jiang Tian truly goes into action, he becomes a heartless killing machine, utterly devoid of mercy or compassion. Every strike he takes results in death. Those who survive a single blow from Jiang Tian are only valuable to him; otherwise, he would never have seriously or lightly wounded victims, only the living and the dead.

"Hiss!!!"

"Holy crap!!!"

Wei Xun and Song Liguo, under scrutinizing gazes, approached step by step, only to discover that the corpses on the ground were all lying haphazardly, their eyes wide open in death. Many others, though without obvious wounds or gunshot wounds, were...

However, large amounts of blood flowed from the nostrils and other openings.

This method...

Internal injuries.

And among their knowledge, there was only one person capable of causing such horrific damage and creating such a terrifying scene in such a short time: Jiang Tian.

"Chief Song, the death toll has been determined. There are twenty-two dead so far, and two others with minor injuries."

"As for the others, they're all unharmed."

"These must be the ones who were more obedient earlier."

The busy Wei Xun, having finished his investigation, rushed to Song Liguo's side. To be honest, Song Liguo was now a deputy chief.

But actually, Song Liguo also held a part-time position: the captain of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment. So, those in the narcotics control detachment still habitually addressed him as "Chief Song."

Moreover, Song Liguo spent most of his time working at the detachment.

His deputy chiefship was more of a side job.

Of course, things have changed recently. Because of Han Xian's problem, the entire political landscape of the Municipal Bureau has shifted, and Song Liguo has become a highly sought-after figure with real power.

He's been quite busy lately.

Now, he really does exude the aura of a leader.

Xie Sisi saw this scene, then looked at Jiang Tian.

Xie Sisi was inwardly impressed.

What kind of mental fortitude is this? Xie Sisi could swear that she had been observing Jiang Tian from beginning to end, but Jiang Tian, ​​from beginning to end, had absolutely no change in expression.

Has he resigned himself to his fate?

Or is he also a victim seeking revenge?

Xie Sisi had already imagined a thousand different stories in her mind, such as these people being Jiang Tian's childhood enemies who massacred his entire family. He was the only one who escaped the massacre, then escaped and had a miraculous encounter, risking his life in the outside world until he became invincible.

Then he returned as a king to seek revenge.

Now that his great revenge has been achieved, he has lost all hope of survival.

In that short time, Jiang Tian was unaware that Xie Sisi, standing opposite him in a daze, had already completely conjured up a whole story of love and hate in her mind.

If Jiang Tian knew, he would probably slap her.

Sigh!!

Xie Sisi suddenly sighed.

Seeing Jiang Tian looking at her with confusion, Xie Sisi sighed even more deeply. She had imagined a story, and Jiang Tian had instantly transformed into a pitiful child.

But there was nothing she could do now. Wrongdoings always have consequences.

"The price of impulsiveness is high. Why bother?" Xie Sisi sighed and stood up.

She couldn't bear to see Jiang Tian being taken away.

Because Song Liguo was accompanied by seven or eight police officers. Song Liguo, in particular, was wearing a white shirt, walking with a powerful stride, his expression unusually solemn and serious. As he walked, his eyes swept across the surroundings like lightning.

Those eyes were filled with a terrifying, intimidating, and oppressive force.

Xie Sisi glanced at him briefly, then dared not look any longer. She couldn't bear to imagine what fate awaited Jiang Tian once he fell into this man's hands.

This child's future was destined to be incredibly tragic.

The more she thought about it, the more Xie Sisi sighed.

Finally, Song Liguo approached, now only inches from Xie Sisi. However, Song Liguo didn't look at her. After all, he was just a lowly traffic policeman, and in this situation, he needed to quickly ascertain the situation.

As for the men behind Song Liguo, they were all menacing and menacing; it was immediately obvious.

These men were from Jiang Tian's team.

They were Jiang Tian's henchmen who had worked many jobs for him. These men had at least a few lives on their hands; after all, they had followed Jiang Tian all over the country and seen all sorts of things.

The murderous aura and terrifying intimidation emanating from them were completely undisguised.

Finally.

For Xie Sisi, each day felt like an eternity.

Xie Sisi's heart clenched. Putting herself in others' shoes, she realized that if she were in their shoes, facing such a desperate fate, she would surely be terrified.

But unfortunately, wrongdoing must be punished.

Xie Sisi thought to herself.

Just as Xie Sisi's mind was racing with wild thoughts, she didn't even dare to look at the police officers behind Song Liguo.

They were all too frightening.

Their eyes were too sharp.

Xie Sisi didn't dare to look at them even once.

But the next moment.

These extremely menacing men suddenly bypassed Song Liguo and stood in front of Jiang Tian.

"It's over, it's over." Xie Sisi even wanted to close her eyes, unable to bear it any longer.

After all, Jiang Tian gave her a good feeling, and she had just imagined quite a few melodramatic scenarios.

But the next moment.

"Captain!"



Chapter 642 Oh, you're actually the captain?


*Snap!*

Attention!

Then a salute. The five or six officers following Song Liguo were actually members of Jiang Tian's squad; in other words, they were Jiang Tian's subordinates.

So, upon seeing Jiang Tian, ​​they immediately came over and saluted him.

"Captain."

Jiang Tian merely gestured slightly, leaving Zhao Wei and Lü Chun full of curiosity.

Damn it.

Because of this, Zhao Wei knew almost everything about Huayu Group.

He never expected that the matter there wasn't finished. Looking at the corpses scattered on the ground, Zhao Wei felt utterly horrified.

Even with Song Liguo present, he didn't actually speak; Jiang Tian was the one issuing the orders.

Although Song Liguo was the deputy director,

in handling cases, especially those like this, Jiang Tian's words carried the most weight. Moreover, Song Liguo had experience; in situations where the situation wasn't clear, it was best not to give orders.

As the people dispersed, at this moment...

Xie Sisi, standing beside Jiang Tian, ​​felt as if she had seen a ghost. The moment those people spoke, Xie Sisi's mind went blank.

She didn't understand what her boss meant.

Then, turning her head, Xie Sisi was stunned to see those people saluting Jiang Tian.

Xie Sisi's head was buzzing.

Because, mainly because Jiang Tian looked about her age, Xie Sisi was certain that this Jiang Tian was definitely not old, in fact, he was most likely her age.

At times, Xie Sisi felt that Jiang Tian might not even be as old as her.

So...

Being called "boss" was one thing, but being called "captain," Captain Jiang?

Good heavens.

Xie Sisi was instantly dumbfounded.

Captain?

You mean this guy is a policeman?

Oh no, he's actually a captain?!

Are you crazy? Is this world too insane?

Jiang Tian was also completely confused. He even felt that this young girl might be a little slow on the uptake, otherwise, why would she be so easily startled?

Because Xie Sisi had just imagined Jiang Tian's tragic backstory—perhaps he suffered a horrific massacre as a child, and these same people were responsible. Jiang Tian had narrowly escaped, and now he'd returned to exact revenge.

Xie Sisi felt a pang of regret.

She had initially felt a little affection for Jiang Tian, ​​but now, it was all over.

Jiang Tian was probably completely finished; once caught, he was gone for good.

But then, things took an unexpected turn.

She had assumed the special agents would arrest Jiang Tian immediately, but instead, they saluted him first and then went off to carry out their mission.

Seriously?

Are these police officers?

"Still your captain?"

"Are you crazy? How can you be the captain? Why are you the captain?"

"The police are one thing."

Xie Sisi asked, her face filled with terror.

Because Xie Sisi realized that everything she had imagined was completely fake, all fabricated.

The reality was, this guy was actually the captain?!

The police station captain?

That's what I'm saying.

Why do you look younger than me, but I'm just a newbie being mentored, while you're already a captain?

"Captain, what's wrong, young lady? Are you alright?" Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

"Ah, it's nothing, it's nothing, cough cough, it's just that I didn't expect you to be so young. I didn't expect you to be the captain, haha. What's your name? I haven't asked yet. It's just that you look so young."

Xie Sisi swallowed hard, quickly scratching the back of her head and chuckling awkwardly.

Perhaps she misjudged him; Jiang Tian was actually quite old.

Otherwise, why would he be the captain?

It's just that he looks young.

"Yes, it's mainly because there are very few people as great as you. Brave and great. I was originally going to apply for a personal merit award for you, but I didn't expect you to be so great. It's a bit of a pity. You know, these people are the leaders of criminal groups. Their capture would be a huge achievement." Jiang Tian said with a hint of regret.

Xie Sisi, standing opposite Jiang Tian, ​​instantly froze.

Her eyes widened as she stared at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

Wait a minute.

What did this person just say? Personal merit, apply for it for her?

Buzz!!!

Xie Sisi's heart raced, but then she heard Jiang Tian's admiring voice. Indeed, Jiang Tian himself was pursuing fame and fortune, yet this young girl had already let go of such things.

In this respect, Jiang Tian truly admired her, so he felt he absolutely had to grant her request.

"No."

"Officer Jiang, after thinking it over, I believe I need this small merit. It may be insignificant to you, but it's very important to me. I believe that with merit, I'll have a bigger world to explore and be able to better serve the people in the future." Xie Sisi corrected him with a serious expression.

"Oh?" Jiang Tian nodded, unsurprised.

He knew it! The girl must have misunderstood something. Now the misunderstanding's cleared up. This girl is really eager for credit!

"But, can I possibly have any credit? You know, I didn't do anything!" Xie Sisi swallowed hard.

You know, Jiang Tian was just going on a killing spree!

Jiang Tian went on a killing spree.

But...

All she did was bravely warn everyone, Xie Sisi, and that was it. The thirty-odd men in black initially thought Jiang Tian was a thug, but they never expected he was just carrying out a mission.

Good heavens.



Chapter 643 The case is serious; a superstar is involved.


Xie Sisi's heart pounded. She was completely confused; she couldn't figure out which department Jiang Tian belonged to.

Even after Jiang Tian nodded in understanding and then turned and left without a word, Xie Sisi was filled with anxiety.

She was even filled with trepidation.

Mainly because...

On one hand, Xie Sisi was thinking about the credit she'd received; on the other hand, who exactly was Jiang Tian?

Distracted, Xie Sisi looked up and saw a policeman in front of her whose face wasn't fierce, but rather rather comical. It was this man who had just called Jiang Tian "Boss."

Xie Sisi had many questions she wanted answered.

"Comrade," Xie Sisi called out to Zhao Wei.

Zhao Wei stopped, a smile spreading across his face. "Comrade, what's the matter?"

"Um, I wanted to ask, was that officer your captain?" Xie Sisi pointed at Jiang Tian.

Because Zhao Wei looked to be about the same age as her, Xie Sisi was still confused.

Zhao Wei, however, seemed to instantly understand, a smile spreading across his face. "That's right!"

"What department are you from?" Xie Sisi swallowed hard.

"The First Brigade of the Municipal Narcotics Control Detachment, Captain."

A playful smile appeared on Zhao Wei's face.

Mainly because Zhao Wei completely understood Jiang Tian's age, he deliberately added "First Brigade Captain" at the end.

He emphasized the "Captain" part.

This made Xie Sisi's eyes widen, her breathing becoming more rapid.

That's right.

Good heavens! Xie Sisi had just been wondering why, even though they were both captains, Jiang Tian's performance seemed worlds apart from her master's, a mere squad leader.

With just a word and a gesture, he mobilized dozens, even hundreds, of special forces officers, and in a short time...

Why was her mentor, who seemed like a captain, so hesitant to speak up, especially in situations like this?

Now she finally understood.

What a load of rubbish, a mere squad leader!

He was clearly a battalion commander!

A battalion commander?

Good heavens!

What does battalion commander mean? It means that within a branch bureau, the battalion commander is the highest-ranking officer in that department.

However, in the city bureau, especially in the provincial capital, where rank is often three levels higher, it's common for positions to be held by higher-ranking officers with lower-level responsibilities.

In some situations, a second-level police inspector like Jiang Tian could actually be considered a deputy director or director of a branch bureau in some prefecture-level cities.

After Zhao Wei left,

Xie Sisi felt a deep sense of loss.

Because as time passed, the scene had begun to be cleaned up.

As for the survivors, or rather, the ringleader, they had all been taken away.

As for the cars and other items on site, they were naturally managed by designated personnel.

Finally, everything was handled uniformly.

“Master, I saw a god! The captain, the captain looks about my age!” Xie Sisi exclaimed, her heart pounding.

People say that the provincial capital is a place where hidden talents abound, but Xie Sisi had no idea before.

As a result, after only a few days at work, she suddenly witnessed what true fierce competition was all about.

Ding Yaguan is powerful, incredibly powerful. For someone like Xie Sisi, he's completely outmatched.

But in front of Jiang Tian, ​​sorry, he's destined to be instantly defeated.

Or rather, destined to prostrate himself on the ground awaiting judgment.

At this moment,

Xie Sisi seemed to understand what power truly meant.

As for her mentor, this squad leader,

he was utterly powerless in front of him. With so many police officers present, no one paid them any attention; everyone was diligently doing their own job.

The outside world also reported it to the police.

There will be someone specifically assigned to communicate with them later.

As for the Zishan Apartments, Song Liguo immediately called in people to search each apartment.

Of course, even with a search, Jiang Tian knew it was impossible to completely prevent it. Even if some people deleted the videos, they would likely leave backups, and ultimately, the videos would still be leaked.

One can imagine the horrific scene that would unfold if this information were leaked.

Of course.

Jiang Tian is no longer bothered by it.

Because at his current level, at Jiang Tian's stage, everything he does and handles cases is based on evidence.

Moreover.

Actually, it's not all bad. The internet is full of accusations that Jiang Tian killed innocent people and claimed credit for them. Fine, let's show you what that really means.

How does Jiang Tian handle cases?

Those fabricated achievements and murderous crimes that you see are child's play compared to him.

What are his daily case-handling methods like?

How do he treat criminals?

He single-handedly takes on thirty or forty people.

And in the end, he directly killed more than twenty.

Inside the police car directing traffic, Song Liguo looked at Jiang Tian sitting across from him, his face blank with disbelief. "We've already had people investigate house-to-house, but the situation at Zishan Apartment is far too complicated. There are too many residents, all sorts of people. I reckon the video will definitely leak out. Will that affect you?"

Of course, Song Liguo also meant whether Jiang Tian had violated any rules.

If there were no violations, it would be easier to handle. But if there were people who had already surrendered, and Jiang Tian was still killing them, the news leaking out would likely create terrifying public pressure.

"Don't worry, those I killed were definitely people who were already committing the crime, preparing to commit it, or trying to escape after the crime," Jiang Tian replied.

In other words, there was no possibility of any violations.

Jiang Tian knew exactly what he was doing.

Song Liguo's expression softened. Good, otherwise, he wouldn't know how to report to his superiors.

"So, have you thoroughly investigated the crimes at Yunya Group?" Song Liguo inquired.

This is the most important thing. The person has been caught, but the follow-up investigation is a crucial issue.

Jiang Tian, ​​on the other hand, wasn't surprised at all.

With the ringleader arrested, what trouble could the others possibly cause? It's impossible.

"Uncle Song, we've known each other for quite some time now. Do you think I wouldn't have acted without thoroughly investigating?"

Jiang Tian had already inquired with Chen Liting about the criminal activities of the Yunya Group. It's important to know that the Huayu Group and the Yunya Group are in the same industry, and are also direct competitors.

To investigate the Yunya Group's criminal activities, the Huayu Group has a wealth of dirt on them, because they even frequently plant spies between each other.

"So you mean..." Song Liguo's heart skipped a beat.

"Several celebrities from the Yunya Group are suspected of drug use." Jiang Tian stated definitively.



Chapter 644 Taking it One Step at a Time, Digging Deeper


Wow!!!

This is no small matter.

First of all, arresting people isn't something you can just do by Jiang Tian declaring you a criminal organization, or by arresting you after labeling you a criminal organization. That kind of award is absurd.

The prerequisite for a first-class merit award is that you must have an extremely serious criminal record, have dealt a devastating blow to them, and have a very positive impact on subsequent society, as well as the significant benefits the operation brought to society as a whole.

This award is multifaceted.

For example, Jiang Tian's award of a special merit was mainly because Tu Cha's actions were outrageous. He brought a large number of people into the country, even creating a serious crisis for Gao Xiaolong. Most outrageous of all, Tu Cha even tried to turn the tables and attack the police—that was utterly reckless.

Therefore, after Jiang Tian physically eliminated Tucha, he even rescued a very important comrade, and this had a significant impact and deterrent effect on the entire country and Myanmar.

This is the deterrent power it has on a region, which is the key reason for awarding Jiang Tian the Special Merit Citation. Firstly, without Jiang Tian, ​​according to calculations, Gao Xiaolong might have been killed there.

The subsequent consequences would have been even more terrifying and severe.

Furthermore, the criminal group completely lost control, and no one took them seriously.

Jiang Tian's devastating operation in Tucha was nothing short of a cataclysmic upheaval.

This is why, from all aspects, the impact was so enormous, and the contribution so significant, that the Special Merit Citation was reinstated for Jiang Tian.

And now...

Although Ding Yaguan was arrested, the operation against him was very sudden. The overall criminal activities of the Yunya Group have not yet been definitively defined, although in the eyes of the police and among many others, the Yunya Group is involved in serious illegal and criminal activities.

While the police were conducting their emergency operation,

the Yunya Group was still brightly lit.

Especially since the chairman of the Yunya Group, although seemingly in control, had a very peculiar structure.

Because the person in control behind the scenes was Ding Yaguan.

The final mastermind was apprehended in one fell swoop. It could be said that subsequent operations, with the Yunya Group leaderless, were far too easy to carry out.

Perhaps, it wasn't even done that way.

Who made the arrests from top to bottom? Look at how a normal police operation usually ends up.

Good heavens.

It's all about setting up a plan, then investigating layer by layer, gathering information layer by layer. By the time the investigation is almost complete, well, the mastermind has already escaped.

Too many similar incidents have happened in the past, so the Municipal Bureau, especially the police, could only be astonished by Jiang Tian's absurd approach to solving the case.

But getting them to take action was absolutely impossible.

Because without investigation, how can we know who the real culprit is? The key point is that by the time the investigation is complete, the culprit will probably have already gotten wind of it and fled.

“Although the Municipal Public Security Bureau has conducted numerous investigations, it's difficult to guarantee that no one within the bureau has been corrupted. These criminal groups are all intricately connected. If we investigate from the bottom up, by the time we find the mastermind, it will be too late.”

The Yunya Group was brightly lit.

Everyone looked extremely busy.

Or rather…

Yunhai, on the surface, appeared prosperous, but at its core, it was already rotten to the core.

What was needed now was a painful period of adjustment to thoroughly cleanse the wound and eradicate the problem from its roots.

Gao Xiaolong puffed on his cigarette, then looked thoughtful.

In fact, the police system is under immense pressure, especially in peacetime. The police system controls the stability of society and the management of all criminal and illegal activities.

The pressure is immense, especially for leaders within the police system. Many of them come from high-pressure departments like criminal investigation, which has led to a significant smoking addiction among many leaders.

"Xiao Jiang, what exactly happened with Ding Yaguan?" Minister Tan asked with a serious expression.

That's right.

Just recently, Jiang Tian finished organizing and arranging the scene.

Then he left Song Liguo to handle the aftermath at the scene. Jiang Tian, ​​however, went directly to the Municipal Public Security Bureau with his men, ignoring Han Xian. Jiang Tian now roughly knew the situation.

Han Xian's time at the Municipal Public Security Bureau was running out; it was practically on its last legs. To turn things around, unless Jiang Tian personally intervened, Han Xian's entire political career, like his nephew's, was completely ruined.

Plus, Jiang Tian knew from the first glance that Han Xian had a grudge against him. Of course, this whole family was acting like they were crazy, targeting Jiang Tian.

Just like before, Jiang Tian was inexplicably locked up in a private room.

In short, he was placed under investigation, leading to extremely serious consequences.

And this Han Xian still hasn't learned his lesson.

Of course, it's also because Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan are both overseeing the Municipal Public Security Bureau, and given the magnitude of the matter, Jiang Tian felt it necessary to report to these two bigwigs.

Mainly because the information Jiang Tian obtained was quite serious.

Especially regarding criminal activity.

"That's right, I can say with certainty that I also possess certain evidence. This evidence was obtained through Huayu Group, and its authenticity is 100%. There have been rumors that Yunya Group, especially Ding Yaguan, has been controlling celebrities through heroin."

Jiang Tian pondered.

This is no small matter. Good heavens, public figures—one wrong move and it could be a catastrophe. Jiang Tian was afraid that if things went wrong, it could trigger a terrifying public outcry.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian is currently already in the eye of the storm.

Therefore, the subsequent investigation was somewhat tricky for Jiang Tian.

This was because Yunya Group actually had conflicts with Huayu Group in the entertainment industry, but in the entertainment sector, Yunya Group was actually ahead.

The entire Yunya Group boasts several A-list celebrities and numerous second-tier stars, all with terrifyingly large fan bases.

If things go wrong...

If it were ordinary people, it would be easy.

Just arrest them, conduct a urine test, and the truth would be revealed effortlessly.

But these celebrities... goodness, those who know what's good for them are a little better, but those who refuse to cooperate, or even try to manipulate public opinion, are in serious trouble.

"They've gone mad."

"These bastards are capable of anything, no matter how heinous."

"They should be shot."



Chapter 645 You hid it well, but I have God's perspective.


Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table, cursing. His main concern was that the entire criminal activity of the Yunya Group was a ticking time bomb; once detonated, it would cause a devastating earthquake.

First, he needed to consider this.

If Jiang Tian hadn't discovered this beforehand, or taken action against the Huayu Group earlier, would these celebrities, already addicted to heroin, have publicly promoted it? In other words, their words and actions would have a direct and terrifying impact on their fans.

The most profound impact of this would be leading fans down a path of no return. Many people wouldn't even know the terrifying dangers of heroin.

A little exposure would mean a lifetime of ruin.

"Besides," Jiang Tian said, "according to the information I currently have, the Yunya Group has been involved in countless crimes over the years, including dozens of murder cases, all with records of time and location. I've had them verified, and the mastermind behind it all is Ding Yaguan."

"Inhuman," Minister Tan's expression changed slightly.

That's what's recorded.

In other words, the Yunya Group's competitors, namely the Huayu Group, knew about dozens of murders. So, what about the undisclosed cases? Ten times, dozens of times more? How terrifying is the true number?

But then again, while it seems outrageous, upon closer examination, it's not so outrageous at all.

Because of the history of these criminal groups in Yunhai, especially Ding Yaguan, a woman who built her empire from scratch, she must have been countless times more ruthless than the other leaders; otherwise, how could she have risen to such a position?

Dozens.

In fact, in Jiang Tian's view, "Huh, the Yunya Group has grown so large, yet after so many years, it only has a few dozen murders? That seems rather small."

But then...

The actual investigation revealed that in these types of cases, the number generally starts at ten times.

The true number is a secret; besides Ding Yaguan, probably no one else knows exactly how many people she has killed.

"A decisive blow! We must strike! We must wipe these criminals out completely!"

Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table, his voice blaring. There was no room for discussion.

The Yunya Group was too wicked.

It had to be dealt with.

Jiang Tian shared this opinion.

"Of course, there's another issue now. According to my investigation, the chairman of the Yunya Group seems quite clean, seemingly well protected by Ding Yaguan," Jiang Tian pondered.

As the apparent controller of the Yunya Group, Jiang Tian had investigated its numerous crimes repeatedly, and the result...

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue; he simply couldn't believe it.

"A gigolo?" Gao Xiaolong inquired.

This was indeed suspicious.

But it wasn't entirely impossible. What if Ding Yaguan genuinely liked this gigolo and wanted him to be clean, so he kept him out of the criminal activities, taking all the blame himself?

It wasn't impossible.

"This guess is quite possible. After all, almost all the dirty work was done by Ding Yaguan alone. Putting a clean slate on the surface provides a safety net should anything go wrong," Jiang Tian pondered.

This possibility isn't nonexistent; it's entirely plausible.

Moreover, based on Jiang Tian's understanding,

the possibility seems quite high. After all, Ding Yaguan's domineering nature is well-known.

"In that case, I'll get started," Jiang Tian said, still somewhat unsure.

That's right.

Even when taking action, some preparations need to be made in advance.

"Don't worry, none of the news about these celebrities from Yunya Group will leak out today. Even if it does, it won't appear online."

Minister Tan spoke with great certainty.

If the higher-ups were determined to block the news and had made prior preparations,

these celebrities would have absolutely no power to resist.

Otherwise, without any control, they could truly rebel.

Meanwhile,

The chairman of Yunya Group appeared to Jiang Tian to be a very refined and cultured man.

Upon meeting Jiang Tian, ​​he smiled broadly and said, "Thank you, officer. Thank you so much, officer. Without you, I really don't know what I would have done."

The chairman of Yunya Group met with Jiang Tian and his group in the conference room.

Jiang Tian had already completely sealed off the entire Yunya Group.

All criminal records and documents related to Yunya Group were sealed off to facilitate a more detailed investigation later.

"What do you mean?" Jiang Tian asked, slightly surprised.

He seemed surprised that the chairman of Yunya Group would be so grateful.

"Officer Jiang, you have no idea how terrible Ding Yaguan was to me. You have no idea how perverted she is, what she did to me." The chairman, perhaps overwhelmed with grievance, began to cry.

He looked utterly aggrieved, which made Jiang Tian's heart sink.

Especially when the chairman mentioned Ding Yaguan's perverted actions, Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched, and he thought to himself, "Such a thing is possible?"

"Xiao Wei, go pour Officer Jiang and the others a glass of water, with my best tea."

The chairman of Yunya Group instructed his secretary.

Xiao Wei glanced at Jiang Tian and the others curiously, then quickly went to do her work.

Jiang Tian watched Xiao Wei's retreating figure, his eyes slightly narrowed.

From the moment he entered, Jiang Tian had clearly sensed it.

Things were progressing too smoothly, everything was going too smoothly, seemingly without any obstacles. But, as the saying goes, there's always something fishy going on.

Or...

Is this person truly innocent?

Is he genuinely willing to cooperate wholeheartedly? The key is, good heavens, is that Ding Yaguan really that twisted, as this person says?

But despite his assertiveness, Jiang Tian felt that Ding Yaguan also seemed like that kind of person!

Jiang Tian had a thorough understanding of human nature and other people's personalities; he could discern a person from a psychological perspective.

And this chairman...

Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes.

"Officer Jiang, don't you like this tea? Xiao Wei, can you get me a fresh cup? It's alright. If you don't like it, I have plenty of other good teas here. Officer Jiang, all the information I know about Ding Yaguan's crimes is here, not a single piece missing. I collected all this information before because I wanted to report him and get him out of this mess, but I never had the chance. Thank you so much, Officer Jiang."



Chapter 646 Acting Skills? I'll Pretend I Didn't See It


"It would be best if you could cooperate with the police."

Jiang Tian nodded, and Zhao Wei, standing beside him, immediately took the documents and placed them in front of him.

Jiang Tian glanced at them and found them indeed quite good.

No, even more than he knew, much more than he expected.

"Officer Jiang, I'm very sorry. I've been collecting evidence and information all along, and I've tried to prevent crimes whenever possible. I never expected that Ding Yaguan would be caught so easily. This has really helped me."

The chairman of Yunya Group.

At this moment, he expressed his gratitude to Jiang Tian. The expressions of the people in the room were somewhat strange, because according to the chairman of Yunya Group, he seemed completely innocent, and most importantly, what he did didn't seem wrong.

He seemed to be a victim as well.

An innocent person persecuted by Ding Yaguan.

But...

"Boss," Li Zijin looked at Jiang Tian.

Her face was filled with deep emotion.

"This Ding Yaguan is utterly wicked! He even used heroin to control the behavior of celebrities—it's absolutely insane! The most terrifying thing is that he even uses the influence of celebrities to select people for sale."

Li Zijin was horrified.

Speaking of which...

Li Zijin and Zhao Wei had always followed Jiang Tian. When they went on missions, Jiang Tian would take them along, mainly because they were convenient to use. Besides, from the beginning, these two had been with Jiang Tian, ​​and whatever they did, they almost never made a mistake.

Therefore, as Jiang Tian rose in rank, it was a case of "one man's success brings his entire family to prosperity."

And because Li Zijin's position was already relatively high, she was now even a second-level police superintendent.

She had been promoted exceptionally.

These two are a prime example. Following the right person is paramount. While they may not have individual merits like first-class merit, they have the collective first-class merit. Even if they didn't have individual merits, they still have second- and third-class merits.

Although they got their positions through luck, by following Jiang Tian, ​​regardless of how they obtained them, this merit guarantees a smooth political path.

"Yes, their actions are utterly heinous," Jiang Tian nodded slightly. Beside him, forensic personnel were photographing the evidence.

And it wasn't just this.

After reviewing the evidence, Jiang Tian became increasingly horrified. Gambling, prostitution, and drugs—these three crimes are the most direct.

These are also the most favored by criminal groups. Although illegal, they are the three paths that drive many desperate individuals to extremes.

These are the classic three.

Yunya Group, ostensibly an entertainment company, operates entirely in entertainment-related industries.

However,

the internal workings of Yunya Group are absolutely appalling.

They are rife with crime; it's utterly corrupt.

Moreover, there's a complete criminal process running from the bottom up. For example, minor celebrities seeking fame are controlled through various criminal means.

Jiang Tian thought of Han Zi.

To be honest, Han Zi was just lucky.

Although the assistant kept harassing her, Han Zi escaped unscathed because the assistant was Ding Yaguan's brother. Otherwise, following the established process, she would have already been caught in the middle.

The more he thought about it, the more terrified he became.

This has blown up.

Besides the entertainment industry, they also run nightclubs, etc.

These industries are actually complementary, and Yunya Group is quite clever, perfectly linking the nightclubs with its own celebrities.

For example, they often have their top stars make guest appearances at their various nightclubs, sometimes even performing.

This attracts a large crowd.

Ultimately, they complemented each other, and the nightclub's reputation grew exponentially.

This move was a game-changer, a single, decisive blow that forced the Huayu Group into a series of defeats. The Huayu Group, whose main business was nightclubs, was ultimately forced to support entertainment companies and popular celebrities due to the Yunya Group's relentless pressure.

Especially after Jiang Tian learned that the Yunya Group had recently taken over a group of trainees from Nanhan who had returned from abroad, each having trained for over two years.

With a little hype, they instantly gained massive popularity, which then flowed back into the Yunya Group's nightclub business.

In short, this move made the Yunya Group incredibly wealthy.

"Boss, this... this is... how do we handle this?" Li Zijin was sweating profusely.

Good heavens.

This is absolutely incredible.

Just a glance was enough to send chills down Li Zijin's spine. She had no idea how to handle the situation. And this kind of thing, if things went wrong, wouldn't just involve one or two celebrities, but most of the entertainment company. Very few people were truly innocent anymore; if they were arrested, there were rarely any innocent victims.

If this escalated, and a mass crackdown ensued...

Once things got out of hand, Li Zijin felt a chill run down her spine. If it aroused public outrage, the terrifying consequences... Ugh, Li Zijin's face turned green with fear.

She couldn't handle it, she simply couldn't!

"It's nothing, just a small matter."

Jiang Tian waved his hand.

That's right.

Jiang Tian had already prepared himself, so saying it was truly a small matter gave Li Zijin a strange sense of confidence.

"I believe that Chairman Wen will cooperate with us, right?" Jiang Tian said with a smile, his expression completely unchanged.

This startled Mr. Wen, the chairman of Yunya Group, who was seemingly observing Jiang Tian.

Putting everything else aside, at this moment, Mr. Wen was certain that anyone else would report to their superiors immediately after reviewing the report.

But this police officer...

Looks so young.

Good heavens.

"Are the police out of their minds? Letting such a young person handle this? And more importantly, after witnessing such a major case and its terrifying impact, how can they remain so calm?"

"Are you crazy?"

"Of course, of course, Officer Jiang, I will fully cooperate with any requests or actions of your police force. That's something I'm happy to do."



Chapter 647 The Chicken Feet Are Showing


Mr. Wen appeared utterly righteous.

And his attitude was impeccable.

Li Zijin, Zhao Wei, and Jiang Tian's old subordinates, all present, smiled, looking at Mr. Wen with a mixture of admiration and sympathy, seemingly feeling sorry for his plight.

"These are the names of all those involved in the case. Our Yunya Group can state that we absolutely support the police and will assist you 100% in solving the case," Mr. Wen said, standing up.

That's right.

The chairman of Yunya Group was surnamed Wen.

He looked to be in his thirties, and importantly, he exuded a very favorable aura, seemingly posing no threat whatsoever.

Especially in his speech and actions, he was impeccable.

"On behalf of the police, I'm very grateful for your efforts, Mr. Wen."

Jiang Tian's eyes narrowed slightly.

"I wonder, Officer Jiang, how you plan to handle these celebrities involved in the case? I can guarantee that they only used the items themselves; there was no sale or other activity." Mr. Wen patted his chest to assure him.

Jiang Tian nodded.

"So, to avoid causing you trouble, I actually hope you can give them a lenient sentence, and they will definitely plead guilty and accept punishment." Mr. Wen further stated.

Hmm?

Jiang Tian's face almost broke into a smile.

A lenient sentence?

They still want to be celebrities? Have they lost their minds?

If it were any other crime, that would be one thing, but you're even using heroin! As a public figure, you can make mistakes, but you absolutely cannot cross the line. And clearly, this is a matter of the bottom line, and the consequences will be enormous.

Besides, your guarantee? You're all in cahoots! What kind of guarantee is that?!

"The main thing is, I and they are all willing to accept your education and criticism, and we absolutely accept your sending people to assist them in banning heroin, etc.," Mr. Wen explained.

At least, in Mr. Wen's view, it was reasonable.

But Jiang Tian was truly stunned.

He's gone mad.

This Mr. Wen really seems to have lost his mind. He must have been provoked by something. If he wasn't provoked, how could he say such incoherent things, taking his name so seriously?

"Mr. Wen!" Jiang Tian said with a face full of emotion.

He even drew out the syllable when saying his name.

"Officer Jiang, you need to understand, this is no small matter. If it's not handled properly, the entire public opinion will explode. The consequences, Officer Jiang, are absolutely beyond your capacity to bear. If something unexpected happens, all the blame will fall on you. You need to think about whether you can handle that kind of pressure."

Mr. Wen seemed very confident.

In fact, according to Mr. Wen, in other words, the police had essentially wasted their time, because all those involved were innocent, and the blame fell entirely on Ding Yaguan and his group.

If it wasn't premeditated, Jiang Tian absolutely didn't believe it. Besides, it was impossible for them to be completely clean.

There would always be something involved.

So…

Now, Jiang Tian stroked his chin,

strickenly scrutinizing Mr. Wen before him.

Mr. Wen felt uneasy under Jiang Tian's gaze and hurriedly said, "Officer Jiang, you're so young, holding such an important position, your future is absolutely bright. But once you take action, you need to think carefully about the consequences, and whether you can bear them."

He was using both soft and hard tactics.

Mr. Wen seemed to have already handled the situation with ease.

His face was full of confidence as he spoke.

This confidence made Jiang Tian laugh; he didn't even know where this guy got so much confidence.

Good heavens.

He thinks they're fools.

So, Jiang Tian turned his gaze back to Mr. Wen, carefully examining his criminal record.

At this moment, Mr. Wen seemed to be reminiscing about the past, saying with a sense of loss, "Officer Jiang, back then I..."

Mr. Wen muttered to himself, recounting how he unexpectedly met Ding Yaguan, and then, when Ding Yaguan was in his most difficult time, Mr. Wen stepped in to help at that crucial moment, and from then on, their relationship became good.

The result was completely unexpected.

Later, Ding Yaguan's true nature was revealed. After getting involved with the group, she displayed her twisted side, subjecting him to all sorts of torture.

In short, she portrayed herself as extremely tragic.

And believe it or not.

Jiang Tian glanced at her and, wow, quite a few people in the room were listening intently, their faces filled with shock.

Mainly because Mr. Wen was so deceptive; it seemed impossible for him to be lying, especially since he spoke so persuasively, making everyone in the room curse Ding Yaguan as heartless and insane.

And Ding Yaguan's blatant act of cuckolding him, etc., even evoked sympathy from the men in the room.

Interesting.

Very interesting.

Jiang Tian became interested.

He had a feeling something was off about this guy.

And after Jiang Tian's gaze swept over, his Eye of Judgment instantly activated.

The instant Jiang Tian activated his Eye of Judgment...

Hiss!!

The dazzling red light, the blood-red glow, almost left Jiang Tian speechless.

The intensity of the crimson color sent chills down his spine.

Very well.

Jiang Tian had reached a level of psychological expertise few could match, but psychology only analyzes behavior and logic. To be precise, with a little training in micro-expression control, even Jiang Tian could still discern a tiny bit.

However, it was still largely impossible to see through him.

It seemed abnormal, and in reality, while it could be helpful in crucial moments, against truly ruthless criminals who could control their emotions and overcome all subconscious micro-expressions, Jiang Tian could only shrug helplessly.

This Mr. Wen was like that.

Actually, it's quite simple; if Jiang Tian can't see through it, nothing is simple.

Think about it, damn it, did they really think they were treating Jiang Tian like a fool?

Treating others like this is one thing, but the real problem is that in front of Jiang Tian, ​​even a slight slip-up wouldn't be a big deal, yet he still managed to say something like that.

"Damn it, so after all this time, the real mastermind behind the crime is here," Jiang Tian clicked his tongue.

However, just as Jiang Tian was about to say something...

Mr. Wen's secretary, Xiao Wei, walked in from outside.

She was carrying a tray with freshly brewed tea.

Jiang Tian turned around at the sound.

"Damn."

After one look, Jiang Tian's eyes widened.

Nothing else.

This woman.

This seemingly ordinary secretary.

Her sin points were actually...



Chapter 648 A family with 100,000 sin points, neatly arranged.


The mere presence of Mr. Wen, with his sheer amount of sin, already surprised Jiang Tian.

It was, to say the more, the more sinful.

Jiang Tian glanced at it and grasped the general picture.

The mastermind? Jiang Tian immediately identified Mr. Wen. This Mr. Wen was the true mastermind, and this was something completely untraceable and undocumented.

Or rather, even with Jiang Tian's legendary criminal investigation skills, his absolute mastery of psychology meant he could only sense something was amiss with Mr. Wen.

Yes, something was amiss.

However, Jiang Tian's understanding of the actual situation wasn't particularly deep, and he wasn't entirely certain. He could only sense something was wrong and then observe through his Judgment Eye.

Damn it.

After using his Judgment Eye, Jiang Tian already knew perfectly well what was going on.

This sin value, while not as terrifying as Tucha's, is at least three times that of Ding Yaguan. In other words, if Ding Yaguan's sin value is 10,000, then Mr. Wen's sin value is 30,000.

Wow.

He's struck it rich.

Jiang Tian's brow twitched.

Leaving aside other matters, this sin value alone is definitely an unexpected windfall for Jiang Tian.

As for whether the mastermind behind the scenes will escape punishment, normally, if Mr. Wen hadn't been handling this case personally, it might have been a different story.

But since Jiang Tian is handling it here today, things are a bit awkward.

"This tea is something I've treasured for a long time, and I haven't been willing to part with it. Xiao Wei's tea-brewing skills are always excellent, Officer Jiang, please do me the honor of trying some," Mr. Wen said with a smile.

And seemingly, there wasn't a trace of murderous intent about him. Even a seasoned detective wouldn't be able to discern any criminal intent in Mr. Wen's current state. But the truth always defies expectations.

However, after eliminating all the impossibilities, the only remaining truth, no matter how unwilling you are to admit it, is absolutely the final truth.

"You didn't poison him, did you?" Jiang Tian nodded with a smile, but his words were followed by an instant silence.

Especially the officers accompanying Jiang Tian, ​​they instantly tensed, their hands instinctively reaching for their weapons.

You see, those accompanying Jiang Tian, ​​especially those on missions, and even within the narcotics unit, normally have the right to carry firearms, even off-duty. In other words, they possess gun licenses.

Of course.

Gun licenses are for law enforcement and frontline personnel. Those in administrative units or those who don't frequently work on the front lines are not authorized to carry weapons.

All the men Jiang Tian brought were armed, the kind who could draw their guns instantly at the slightest provocation.

"No, no, how could that be? Officer Jiang, please don't joke like that, it's terrifying."

Mr. Wen appeared startled, his face turning pale. After a sudden jolt, he quickly smiled and spoke to Jiang Tian.

He seemed genuinely timid, as if he had just been frightened.

Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged as he stared at Mr. Wen for a long time, until cold sweat beaded on Mr. Wen's forehead. Jiang Tian's expression then relaxed slightly, and he smiled again: "Look how scared you are! And you're the CEO of Yunya Group? I was just joking, and you're already scared? How will you lead such a large group in the future?"

Jiang Tian spoke in a seemingly joking tone.

With Jiang Tian's words, the atmosphere instantly relaxed.

Mr. Wen, however, wore a bitter smile. "Officer Jiang," he said, "to be honest, the Yunya Group was originally orchestrated by that woman, Ding Yaguan. Moreover, she handled all the underworld and legitimate business dealings. Now that she's been arrested, I have no chance of controlling the Yunya Group. Therefore, I'm preparing to resign and have already contacted the shareholders to transfer all my shares. Then, I'll leave and go far away."

He seemed to be someone truly pursuing a dream.

It also seemed that Ding Yaguan had genuinely left a terrible psychological scar on this timid man.

If the investigation were genuine, it might reveal that Ding Yaguan did indeed have an affair with his butler.

In other words, Mr. Wen had been cuckolded.

From any perspective, he appeared to be an ordinary person in a weak position, completely suppressed and controlled by Ding Yaguan.

He was only lucky enough to be favored by Ding Yaguan, which led him to his current situation.

But only Jiang Tian knew:

It was all a lie.

To be honest, this situation was practically foolproof.

If Jiang Tian hadn't noticed that the other man had more than twice the sin value of Ding Yaguan, even he might have been skeptical. Of course, even without the Eye of Judgment, Jiang Tian would definitely have been able to arrest him eventually, but it certainly wouldn't have been as smooth as it was now.

"Is that so? He's quite a pitiful person!"

Jiang Tian seemed very sympathetic, his gaze fixed on Mr. Wen, until Xiao Wei came in and brought water to Jiang Tian's side.

Actually, at this moment, Jiang Tian was using the Eye of Judgment, constantly monitoring Mr. Wen's sin value, and hadn't paid attention to his surroundings.

After taking the tea, Jiang Tian was deep in thought, mainly considering all the information he currently had, as well as some recent criminal activities seen in the Eye of Judgment.

According to the Eye of Judgment, in the past day, he had been directing Ding Yaguan to sell heroin through celebrity channels.

Mainly...

Actually, there's a real untapped market in this area. Jiang Tian thought about it carefully. Celebrities almost always carry large amounts of luggage, and if a celebrity were involved in transportation—or even more alarmingly, human transport—

the chances of escape would be enormous.

Jiang Tian considered the matter: who the celebrity was, when they left, and how they were transported. He had a general idea of ​​what was going on, and he felt he had completely resolved the issue.

Jiang Tian, ​​lost in thought, quickly poured himself some tea and turned to thank Xiao Wei.

However, Jiang Tian had his Judgment Eye activated at that moment, so he saw her immediately.

"Holy crap!!!"

Jiang Tian instantly cursed.

His face was filled with bewilderment and disbelief.

Mainly because, if Ding Yaguan was worth 10,000,

then if Mr. Wen was worth 30,000,

and this person...



Chapter 649 Not only cherry blossoms, but also a black dragon.


This secretary, named Xiao Wei, seemed ordinary; Jiang Tian hadn't even paid her much attention, and hadn't even considered her from the start.

At this moment, in Jiang Tian's eyes, her sin value had reached at least 50,000.

What did this mean? It was close to, or even surpassed, Tu Cha's sin value.

In other words,

the Yunya Group alone could bring Jiang Tian an upgrade to a golden attribute.

Moreover, besides upgrading attributes, these sin values ​​had many other uses.

They were practically omnipotent.

However, Jiang Tian racked his brains but couldn't figure out, much less understand, the network of relationships within the Yunya Group.

Meanwhile, Zhao Wei was also watching Jiang Tian's expression. In fact, he had already made preparations beforehand. Jiang Tian throwing down his cup was the signal; as soon as Jiang Tian threw down the cup, they would immediately launch an operation to thoroughly purge the entire Yunya Group.

But...

Jiang Tian, ​​who had been about to smash the glass, abruptly stopped in his tracks.

His cold expression vanished in an instant,

becoming warm and gentle.

Xiao Wei, who was closest to him, immediately felt the temperature drop. In a flash, it felt like the dead of winter, though the sensation was so sudden it was almost an illusion.

"Xiao Wei, thank you so much," Jiang Tian said, as if making small talk.

"It's only right, it's all right. To be honest, if Yunhai didn't have heroes like you, we really don't know how we'd survive," Xiao Wei said with deep emotion.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly, glancing at Mr. Wen, whose expression remained unchanged. Jiang Tian was pondering the relationship between the two or three parties.

After all, some cases are very complex.

This one is like that. He initially thought Ding Yaguan was the only mastermind, a gangster boss, but unexpectedly, there was also a seemingly innocent chairman who had suffered a lot of abuse—it turned out this man was the real mastermind.

Just when he thought Mr. Wen was the mastermind, well, now the situation has taken an even more dramatic turn.

Among all the cases Jiang Tian has handled, very few can compare to Tu Cha, some he's never even seen before.

And the result is something Jiang Tian never expected.

Wow.

The seemingly most inconspicuous secretary beside Mr. Wen was actually the real mastermind! Was this some kind of game of the mantis stalking the cicada, unaware of the oriole behind?

But this was a bit too much!

“Miss Xiao Wei, your accent sounds a bit off. Aren’t you from Yunhai?” Jiang Tian changed the subject.

Hearing Jiang Tian’s words, Mr. Wen’s expression changed slightly, but he quickly regained his composure. Xiao Wei, however, possessed a level of disguise and skill that far surpassed Mr. Wen’s.

Facing Jiang Tian’s questioning and scrutinizing gaze, she feigned perfectly measured surprise and said, “I didn’t expect Officer Jiang to notice. Yes, I’m from out of town.”

“Not just out of town, are you?” Jiang Tian said meaningfully.

Hiss!!

Upon hearing this, the other officers looked puzzled, unable to understand why Jiang Tian would say such things. Most importantly, why wasn't he taking action now, instead focusing on questioning a mere secretary?

No.

Li Zijin pulled back Zhao Wei, who was about to speak, and secretly gave him a look. While the others might be simply making empty threats, they had known Jiang Tian for a long time. If he were truly so simple and ordinary, why would he waste time at this moment?

Jiang Tian's words seemed to have completely shattered Mr. Wen's earlier composure.

He hurriedly stood up, walked in front of Jiang Tian, ​​blocking his view of Xiao Wei, and hastily said with a forced smile, "Officer Jiang, you have such keen eyes! Nothing escapes your notice! Yes, Xiao Wei is indeed from out of town. A young girl, unfamiliar with the place, it's quite pitiful. I took her in as my secretary."

"Oh, where are you from?" Jiang Tian asked, his expression unchanged.

Mr. Wen was about to speak when Xiao Wei blinked and stepped out from behind him, saying, "Officer Jiang, I'm just a secretary. I haven't done anything wrong, have I? You're making me very scared!"

"Perhaps, Officer Jiang has taken a liking to you, who knows? Officer Jiang, you wouldn't really have taken a liking to my secretary, would you?" Mr. Wen quickly interjected, trying to smooth things over.

"No, no, no, I wouldn't dare!" Jiang Tian said with a smirk.

He didn't bother wasting time on any kind of planning, setup, or investigation. These were things Jiang Tian never followed.

Because when Jiang Tian made an arrest, he didn't even need to gather evidence beforehand. Instead, he arrested first, then convicted and collected evidence.

Normally, arresting first and then collecting evidence is the fastest way, but it's hard to guarantee that the wrong person wasn't arrested. So, procedurally speaking, it's wrong.

Of course, this doesn't include Jiang Tian.

A normal investigation, including searches, setup, etc., could take a year or more to complete.

Just like Bai Qiu, the CEO, wow, this whole case has been under investigation for over half a year now, all the while they've been laying the groundwork.

"Officer Jiang, what do you mean by that?" Mr. Wen asked tentatively.

"What I mean is, it's terrifying!"

"Your secretary."

Jiang Tian clicked his tongue and shook his head.

With a crime value comparable to Tu Cha's, Jiang Tian now had a strong curiosity about this secretary named Xiao Wei. He thought Mr. Wen was already a pleasant surprise, but he never expected the real surprise to come later, from Xiao Wei herself.

Although Xiao Wei looked very pretty, and had very distinctive features, etc.

But for Jiang Tian, ​​he'd seen plenty of beautiful women. Even if Xiao Wei was pretty, sorry, in Jiang Tian's eyes, she was just a figurehead.

"Terrible? Am I that scary, Officer Jiang? If you say that, I'm really going to shut up!" Xiao Wei was still putting on a facade, appearing completely normal.

But Jiang Tian's keen observation had already noticed fine beads of sweat forming on Xiao Wei's forehead.

They were still very small.

But Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile. Yes, that was enough.

More than enough.

"Miss Xiao Wei, do you know who I am?" Jiang Tian was no longer paying attention to Mr. Wen.

Clearly, the mastermind behind everything was this secretary of Xiao Wei.

"Officer Jiang, the affairs of Yunya Group have absolutely nothing to do with Miss Xiao Wei, especially regarding all the crimes. I can guarantee with my own life that Miss Xiao Wei is innocent." Mr. Wen's emotions were becoming somewhat agitated.

Before Jiang Tian noticed Xiao Wei, he had been able to remain calm.

But now.

"Miss Xiao Wei, you must be... from Sakura, right?" Jiang Tian changed the subject.

"Ah, Sakura?" Mr. Wen breathed a sigh of relief.

"I didn't expect Officer Jiang to have noticed." Xiao Wei's face relaxed.

"Yes, not only Sakura, but it also seems to be from the Black Dragon Society."



Chapter 650 My words are the evidence


If it weren't for Jiang Tian's discerning eye, who would have imagined that Ding Yaguan and this Mr. Wen, who appeared to be on the surface, were actually just scapegoats?

If it were a normal arrest, Ding Yaguan would probably be finished. Judging from their actions, it would be difficult to implicate Mr. Wen.

But...

even implicating Mr. Wen was already difficult enough. This Xiao Wei behind him was just an ordinary, unremarkable secretary to Mr. Wen; who could possibly notice her?

Even Jiang Tian was blinded by the red light emanating from Xiao Wei.

The key point is...

Jiang Tian's gaze was on Xiao Wei.

That's right.

This Xiao Wei is from Japan, and not only Japan, but also the Black Dragon Society.

To be honest, without Jiang Tian's discerning eye, it would have been very difficult to tell that Xiao Wei was Japanese, because she spoke without an accent.

Jiang Tian was just making things up.

Because Xiaowei genuinely had no accent, and her disguise was flawless in every aspect, but unfortunately, she encountered Jiang Tian's discerning eye.

As soon as Jiang Tian uttered those words, "You're from Japan," the atmosphere became somewhat strange.

Xiaowei's smile began to falter slightly, but it was subtle and fleeting, quickly disappearing. Her smile remained radiant.

But soon.

That's right.

Just as Xiaowei was still putting on a show, still pretending, the moment the Black Dragon Jiang Tian had mentioned appeared.

The entire room fell silent.

The next moment.

Even without Jiang Tian making any arrangements.

A rustling sound instantly erupted.

Zhao Wei and Li Zijin, regardless of how much the explanation went on, becoming increasingly confused, understood the most important sentence.

This Xiaowei was from Japan, and from the Black Dragon Society.

Good, good, good.

In Japan, the yakuza are ridiculously legal in certain aspects, leading to their rampant expansion.

Of course, the yakuza are a unique characteristic of Japan.

And because of this legalization, incredibly, these yakuza members in Japan actually enjoy the benefits of the law, even adhering to its provisions.

Of course, that's typical of Japan—they know how to play the game.

The legalization of this has multifaceted effects, impacting social and economic issues, among others.

Of course, none of that concerns Jiang Tian.

Now, Jiang Tian stares expressionlessly at Xiao Wei. He has already seen it all.

Although Xiao Wei continues to try to feign innocence, it's all in vain.

Even her smile becomes increasingly forced.

As for Mr. Wen beside Xiaowei, his reaction had become quite intense. If Mr. Wen had remained calm throughout, even when he seemed to have been somewhat exposed, he would have appeared quite composed.

However, the moment Xiaowei's identity was revealed, Mr. Wen's mind seemed to explode.

Even at this moment, Mr. Wen suddenly stood up, his voice clearly out of control, shouting, "Come at me if you have a problem! What kind of person are you to bully Xiao Wei? What Japanese? What Black Dragon Society? We've never even heard of them! This is a fabricated charge, a complete joke! I want to see your superiors! I want to file a complaint! Do you handle cases based entirely on guesswork?"

"See? I told you not to be so hasty, and you're already getting agitated."

Jiang Tian gestured for Mr. Wen to calm down.

But Mr. Wen was clearly on the verge of losing his temper. Seeing him about to lose control, Zhao Wei immediately rushed forward and subdued him.

Although Zhao Wei was completely bewildered, having no idea that Xiao Wei, just Mr. Wen's secretary, was suddenly involved with Japan and the Black Dragon Society,

none of that mattered. Since Jiang Tian had said it, it must be true.

"Let me go!" Mr. Wen's emotions escalated.

He easily broke free.

He was about to do something...

Just then, Xiao Wei, who had been so cautious and even feigning composure, stood up: "Enough."

The emotionally charged Mr. Wen instantly calmed down.

Xiao Wei, however, looked at Jiang Tian with a serious expression.

"What, can't keep up the act anymore, Secretary Xiao Wei?" Jiang Tian's face held a hint of amusement.

"How did you know?"

Xiao Wei took a deep breath, trying to calm herself down.

But calm down my foot! If Jiang Tian had only mentioned Japan, it might be a coincidence, or his accent might genuinely be off. But mentioning the Black Dragon Society...

This meant one thing.

Jiang Tian must have found some evidence. Continuing to pretend was just a waste of time.

But Xiao Wei racked her brains trying to think of possible leaks, but after thinking for a long time, she couldn't find anything. Even if Ding Ya Guan was caught,

all the information Ding Ya Guan knew came from Mr. Wen. In other words, Ding Ya Guan didn't know who she was.

And he didn't know what she did.

So...

How did Jiang Tian find out?

“Your disguise was indeed quite good. If it were anyone else, they might not have been able to detect it at all. But, I forgot to tell you, my surname is Jiang, and my name is Jiang Tian. Whether you've heard of this name before or not, I believe this name will tell you the answer.”

By now, the name Jiang Tian, ​​those two characters, were an authority.

Jiang Tian wasn't just showing off when he said this.

Many of the cases he solved defied common sense.

“But even if I'm from the Black Dragon Society, so what? Officer Jiang, I have to admit, you truly live up to your reputation. I've heard your name more than once. But the Black Dragon Society isn't illegal in our island nation; it's a legal organization. And if you don't have evidence of my crimes in your country, you have no right to arrest me.” Xiao Wei shook her head.

“Oh.”

Jiang Tian stood up.

Xiao Wei, however, remained unfazed, her gaze calmly fixed on Jiang Tian.

Even the contempt and sarcasm deep in their eyes were palpable.

Xiaowei was very confident. Everything was orchestrated by Mr. Wen. Even if Mr. Wen was arrested, he was her loyal follower. She was certain that even if Mr. Wen was tortured to death by any means, even if all the current technology and ruthless methods were used, he would never betray her.

There was no evidence.

Then she would be safe.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian's voice suddenly rang out: "My words are the evidence."

Xiaowei's pupils contracted and her eyes widened.



Chapter 651 The evidence is conclusive; it's time to blow things up completely.


"I'm not satisfied! I want to see your leader! This is a violation of humanitarian principles! I'm going to sue you!"

Xiao Wei was clearly panicked.

She was even more furious.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​she was completely bewildered. She had thought of many things, but she hadn't expected Jiang Tian to be so unethical.

Jiang Tian chuckled.

"This is nothing."

"Furthermore, do you know the consequences of doing this? Since you know I'm from the Black Dragon Society, we will absolutely not let this go. You should know that arresting me will bring you the wrath of the Black Dragon Society. Can you bear that price? And let me tell you, no one in this world can withstand the wrath of the Black Dragon Society."

Xiao Wei's tone was clearly flustered.

Actually, Jiang Tian could completely understand.

It's easy to dodge a spear in the open, but hard to guard against an arrow in the dark. The Black Dragon Society had too many people under its command, and they weren't even breaking the law in their own territory; in fact, they were a legal entity, able to expand and fill their ranks with impunity.

The Black Dragon Society's presence is felt across the globe, its influence stemming from its headquarters and its vast global network of agents.

Whether you're wealthy or a political figure, unless you're under constant, 24-hour protection, you're always vulnerable to being eliminated.

You might be able to defend against one assassination attempt, but what about ten? A hundred? Even if you're lucky and well-protected each time, the constant fear of being assassinated is more agonizing than execution.

And yet, they possess this power, ensuring you live in constant anxiety.

Globally speaking,

Offending the Black Dragon Society is never a good choice for anyone.

This is arguably Xiao Wei's greatest asset.

But unfortunately...

Jiang Tian handcuffed Xiao Wei.

"Sorry, I never look at backgrounds or identities when I arrest people. Those who break the law and commit crimes must be punished," Jiang Tian said calmly.

In other words, he was responsible for making the arrests, but what happened afterward, how to handle the situation, that was a matter for his superiors to consider.

"You're quite interesting."

Xiao Wei looked at the handcuffs on her wrists, two dimples appearing on her round face as she smiled.

How could she still smile?

Jiang Tian looked at Xiao Wei, his gaze scrutinizing her, using a judgmental eye to examine her from head to toe.

"To be honest, I've met many people, and I've heard your name, but I've never had the chance to see you in person. I also thought much of what I heard was fabricated. But seeing is believing, Officer Jiang, you are indeed very interesting. We'll meet again soon." Xiao Wei showed no sign of panic.

"Of all the criminal suspects I've arrested, I must say, you are the most blatantly arrogant."

Jiang Tian sneered.

Nothing more.

Goodbye.

"We'll definitely meet again."

Because once inside the Municipal Public Security Bureau, Jiang Tian still needed to interrogate this so-called "Xiao Wei."

"By the way, Xiao Wei is just my alias. My real name is Mitsui Miko. Well then, Officer Jiang, see you next time."

Xiao Wei, oh no, Mitsui Miko was being led away by Li Zijin.

As they reached the door, she even smiled at Jiang Tian.

This made Jiang Tian feel uneasy.

But seeing that both Mitsui Miko and Mr. Wen had been taken away, Jiang Tian shook his head, dismissing the thought from his mind.

"Something's wrong with this Mitsui Miko. Could she really be planning to use some means against you? Should we be on guard?"

Song Qiuwan witnessed the whole thing, but overall, although Song Qiuwan had always followed Jiang Tian, ​​and they were even in a superior-subordinate relationship in their respective departments...

Once...

Song Qiuwan had always treated Jiang Tian like a younger brother. She even thought he hadn't grown up. It could only be said that the impression he left on her from childhood was too deep.

That little brat who followed her around, that little brother who always seemed immature and needed her protection and advice, and who didn't even have any opinions of his own.

Because of this stereotype, when working with Meili Ruan last time, she completely disregarded Jiang Tian's words, causing a huge rift in their relationship.

Thinking about this, Song Qiuwan couldn't deny her regret.

She had initially thought of him as a protective, immature younger brother, but unexpectedly, in the blink of an eye, he was now her direct superior in two different departments.

It just goes to show...

Life is so unpredictable.

Of course, Song Qiuwan has actually come to terms with it now. Aside from initially struggling to accept the huge difference, she's now used to it and has let it go.

Song Qiuwan's emotional shifts have never escaped Jiang Tian's control.

For Jiang Tian now, his frequently used subordinates are constantly observed and monitored, whether for mental health or emotional issues.

“Something’s not right, but it doesn’t matter anymore. He’s in the city police station now, he has no say in the matter. And we don’t need to worry about the Black Dragon Society. If we’re afraid of retaliation, we shouldn’t arrest anyone. We can just stay in the city police station and watch these criminal groups commit crimes with impunity,” Jiang Tian shook his head and sneered.

“Alright, but we still need to be careful. This Mitsui Miko probably has more tricks up his sleeve,” Song Qiuwan also keenly sensed something was wrong.

However, at this point, nothing can be changed.

We can only take it one step at a time.

Jiang Tian nodded without saying a word.

At this moment, after the two were taken away, the large number of men Jiang Tian brought had already begun to take control of the entire Yunya Group, especially verifying the information they had gathered from Mr. Wen and the Huayu Group.

But it must be said…

The Yunya Group has been developing in Yunhai for many years, and its influence is deeply entrenched, but it involves more than two hundred criminals.

These two hundred-plus people were considered the core personnel of the Yunya Group.

The fact that they were able to take control so quickly was largely thanks to Mr. Wen's cooperation; he delivered such a generous gift right from the start.

Therefore, at this moment, only Song Qiuwan and Jiang Tian were in the office.

This was the only time Jiang Tian and Song Qiuwan were alone together in a long time, and Song Qiuwan felt the atmosphere was a little awkward.

She wanted to say something.

But Song Qiuwan opened her mouth, completely at a loss for words.

"I..." Song Qiuwan looked at Jiang Tian, ​​finally mustering the courage to speak.

Thump thump!!!

A series of hurried footsteps sounded.

Then, in the next moment, Lü Chun rushed into the office, not even knocking, and hurriedly said, "Boss, except for a few who were running around doing promotions, most of the signed artists have been apprehended and taken away for testing. However, the evidence is all correct; these people are all involved in heroin use. Just the final testing is needed."

"How many people?" Jiang Tian asked hastily.

"About thirty signed stars, including one A-list celebrity who was also involved."

Hiss!!!

This is completely different from others. With more than 30 celebrities involved, if this case is exposed, it's likely to cause a huge uproar. The amount of attention it generates will probably explode across the entire country.



Chapter 652 Everything is ready, just waiting for the right opportunity.


The entire arrest process wasn't actually that difficult.

The main considerations were the subsequent impact and other related issues.

Of course.

For Jiang Tian, ​​this case could be considered preliminarily complete.

Although Jiang Tian felt something was off about Mitsui Miko; after all, even the execution of Tu Cha hadn't felt this strange.

Jiang Tian didn't dwell on it. Whatever tricks Mitsui Miko might pull next, well, Jiang Tian was prepared to accept them all.

Downstairs.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong sat in their car, watching the officers outside who had finished their arrest and were leaving.

They were completely numb.

Yes, utterly numb.

This case.

Based on their initial understanding, it was a very large and serious case.

In terms of its subsequent impact, it would definitely have an extremely negative influence on the entire social environment.

It's important to understand that with so many people involved, especially celebrities, the impact of disgraced artists on society is absolutely terrifying.

And if this bomb were to explode one day,

it would be an extremely terrifying bombshell for the entire society.

Moreover, the influence of celebrities on their fan base is also enormous and terrifying.

"By the way, what's the situation with Bai Qiu?" Minister Tan asked, frowning slightly.

"I just checked with Shen Han. The operation by the headquarters has begun, and the overall operation is progressing smoothly. They're preparing for the final roundup," Gao Xiaolong said after a moment's thought.

Bai Qiu's case is equally important.

However, these two bigwigs didn't see anything wrong with it. Their judgment had become too shrewd, completely spoiled by Jiang Tian.

Look at what Jiang Tian has been doing lately.

Look at how fast Jiang Tian has handled cases.

In fact, in their view, the headquarters' case was being handled far too slowly.

“No wonder Yunhai has made so little progress. At this rate, it would be strange to see any progress at all,” Minister Tan shook his head.

“The case is, after all, a bit complicated,” Gao Xiaolong shook his head. After all, there are only a handful of people like Jiang Tian.

You could say that throughout history, it's probably just Jiang Tian himself.

Click!!!

Just as they were speaking,

Jiang Tian pushed open the door and walked in.

The moment Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong saw Jiang Tian, ​​their faces lit up with smiles.

Good heavens.

Who wouldn't like a subordinate like that!

What, you're saying it might have too much of a negative impact on society and Yunhai's economy, or that the methods are too radical?

What a joke.

The two bigwigs have already figured it out.

Yunhai needs a radical overhaul.

"The Yunhai case, the criminal groups—each one more terrifying than the last. Yunhai has practically become a paradise for criminals."

"Xiao Jiang, you did the right thing today. I truly didn't expect so many celebrities to be involved."

After listening to Jiang Tian's overall account of the case, Minister Tan had gained a deeper understanding of it.

Especially after hearing about Mitsui Miko.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong were stunned.

“The Black Dragon Society?”

“From Japan?”

“How did this get involved with Japan?”

Minister Tan frowned deeply.

“Is something wrong?” Jiang Tian quickly asked.

“Well, it’s nothing much, but the Black Dragon Society issue needs to be handled carefully.” Minister Tan pondered for a moment.

Gao Xiaolong didn’t say anything.

The involvement of the Black Dragon Society did indeed complicate the case further.

However, the intelligence and information currently available were sufficient.
