In other words,

Yunya Group was involved with the Black Dragon Society, and the connection was quite deep.

There were many internal criminal issues that needed further investigation, but according to the current investigation, Yunya Group, especially Mr. Wen and Ding Yaguan, coerced or used various means to force artists to engage in criminal activities.

This included, but was definitely not limited to, fan-favorite activities; it even involved transportation and other such activities.

Of course.

Actually, it's also because this era is a bit too rampant, unlike later generations where, twenty years later, all sorts of high-tech methods are readily available.

Right now, it hasn't reached that point yet.

Moreover, the laxity in supervision and other aspects has further exacerbated the problem.

Of course.

Actually, Jiang Tian is relatively relaxed. For him, the current goal is to resolve the issue in Yunhai.

At least, the millions of people in Yunhai, and even in Yunnan Province, are innocent.

Although the country has a large population, a megacity with tens of millions of inhabitants, even if it develops at a normal pace, will definitely become a first-tier city in the future.

"Put this case aside for now. Handle the handling of this case and other matters at hand with Shang Mingyan for now. Professor Shang has extensive experience in handling complex cases. No matter what happens, go to Beijing tomorrow," Minister Tan instructed.

"Should we put it down now?" Jiang Tian wanted to investigate Mitsui Miko's situation first after returning.

"That's right. This case can't be solved in a short time. I've already recorded your contribution, and I've applied for a first-class merit for you. Don't worry, the information we have now is enough for a first-class merit."

In other words, further investigation into this case wouldn't warrant a special-class merit, or rather, it wouldn't even meet the requirements for one.

"Alright." Jiang Tian nodded.

There have indeed been many things going on lately, and the cases are all very complex. Although many cases were uncovered by the anti-drug unit, there are still many that require criminal investigation, and even other comprehensive investigations.

Of course, in the current case, the anti-drug squad has actually exceeded its authority to handle it alone. Jiang Tian could handle it, but in a sense, using him to handle this kind of closing case is a waste of his talents.

"I'll hand over the case to Professor Shang later, and I'll take you to Beijing tomorrow," Minister Tan said.

Jiang Tian nodded, then reviewed the situation at Yunya Group to ensure everything was alright before returning to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Shang Mingyan had already arrived.

And to be honest,

Jiang Tian felt that Shang Mingyan's skin was noticeably fairer than when he first arrived.

It was almost like he was aging in reverse.

Wearing a suit, he used to walk with such confidence, but now, Jiang Tian felt his aura had weakened.

But for some reason, Jiang Tian felt that Shang Mingyan always seemed to roll his eyes at him?



Chapter 653 We've had too many leftovers, let's eat something fresh.


Shang Mingyan has indeed been in the news a lot lately.

Before this, although Shang Mingyan traveled all over the country, some serious cases required him to work overtime and stay up all night, and occasionally he even encountered serial crimes.

But these were occasional occurrences.

In reality, although Shang Mingyan is an expert and a long-standing authority in criminal investigation, the number of truly notable cases he has solved since joining the police force is only about a dozen or twenty.

Of course, these cases are all of a nationally significant scale.

And while it seems like a lot, in reality, after more than a decade or two of police service, Shang Mingyan only solves one or two per year.

This is because such shocking cases are actually rare.

Once every now and then, it's already quite a lot.

The main issue is that these are some of the most notable cases of this nature since the founding of the People's Republic of China, cases that are considered among the most egregious in terms of their severity. While many may remain unsolved cases, overall, they haven't caused any particularly severe consequences.

"The Black Dragon Society? I've solved so many cases, and I've never even come across one like this. You, of all people, brought it up yourself, and then just walked away without asking any questions?"

"And it involves so many celebrities?"

How complicated is the case?

Good heavens, Jiang Tian, ​​you ran away with your first-class merit award, and then you hand over such a complex and troublesome case to me?

How can you be so shameless?

Just asking you, how can you be so shameless?

Shang Mingyan's mentality exploded.

Of course, it wasn't that Shang Mingyan was complaining about the work. The main issue was that Jiang Tian noticed Shang Mingyan had turned pale. This wasn't an illusion; Shang Mingyan hadn't seen the sun for over half a month since arriving in Yunhai.

Is this reasonable?

Since arriving in Yunhai, they've been working day and night, but the key issue is that they haven't uncovered many cases themselves in criminal investigation.

It's all been Jiang Tian's team, uncovering major cases every day, and one criminal organization after another has been brought down by Jiang Tian.

But they still need to take over these cases.

These are relatively simple, but there are also some troublesome transnational cases, like the Yugou.com case. The boss and mastermind have been caught, and Jiang Tian has claimed the credit.

But this case can't be left unresolved, so the experienced Shang Mingyan needs to take it over.

She came to Yunhai and did nothing but follow Jiang Tian around, taking on these cases.

Once or twice is tolerable.

But the problem is, the cases are getting more and more complicated.

Jiang Tian finishes his work, smugly takes his credit, and runs off, leaving Shang Mingyan to painstakingly clean up the mess?

"How can you still be emotional when handling a case?"

Jiang Tian entered his office and watched Shang Mingyan constantly rolling his eyes. The key point was that Shang Mingyan possessed an extremely refined air; even his eye-rolling exuded a captivating charm.

Most importantly, when had the usually decisive and efficient Shang Mingyan ever shown such emotion?

Of course, Jiang Tian hadn't thought too deeply about it. The main reason Shang Mingyan showed this emotion was because she used to be too domineering. In other words, she could see through the filth in the hearts of ordinary men, no matter how outstanding they were.

Therefore, she never had any goodwill or attitude towards her.

But Jiang Tian was different. He was the first man who could make Shang Mingyan feel inferior, even completely unfathomable. This instilled a sense of vulnerability in her, causing her usual domineering nature to unconsciously reveal its true colors in front of Jiang Tian.

"What do you think?" Shang Mingyan asked, her hands resting on the table.

Since his promotion, Jiang Tian had been assigned a private office of several dozen square meters at the Municipal Bureau. Before, he always used the larger office area.

Of course, he didn't need it then.

Jiang Tian himself felt he didn't need an office.

But after his promotion—a second-level police inspector and head of the anti-drug squad, and a high-ranking position in the provincial capital—it was natural that, according to regulations, he should be assigned an office.

Of course, the municipal police station building is quite large.

It must be said that Yunhai's economy and funding are quite good, so the office building has ample office space, even with the anti-drug squad continuously expanding its manpower, and even the headquarters in Beijing sending personnel to work there.

Of course, this deployment is purely for assistance, nothing more.

Mainly because the cases in Yunhai are far too complex and numerous.

In terms of manpower, over three hundred people have been transferred and added, but of course, frontline personnel like Jiang Tian are only a small portion of these three hundred.

The main manpower is allocated to other areas, primarily technical matters and network surveillance, etc.

"Next time, next time there's a new case, I'll definitely notify you immediately."

Jiang Tian rubbed his temples.

He has to go to Beijing early tomorrow morning. Jiang Tian has been working a lot of overtime lately, not so much physically, but primarily mentally exhausted.

Even Jiang Tian felt mentally exhausted, let alone Shang Mingyan. No matter how talented he was, he was still just an ordinary person.

Shang Mingyan's workload, especially mentally, was actually not much less than Jiang Tian's.

Thinking about it, others might be okay, they could easily get some credit without much ability, but for an authority like Shang Mingyan in criminal investigation, constantly being relegated to the sidelines and getting the leftovers would eventually cause resentment.

"Next time?" Shang Mingyan shook his head.

"At least three times." Shang Mingyan added seriously, holding up one finger.

"Okay, three times it is. From now on, when you're in Yunhai, I'll contact you immediately if I discover any cases." Jiang Tian nodded.

Anyway, Yunhai has everything, but there are countless major cases.

Don't be fooled by how many cases Jiang Tian has cleared up now; the real big fish are yet to come.

Especially.

There are still some cases that are too important, even involving higher-ups.

Take Ding Yaguan, for example, or Yunya Group, which has reached a scale of several billion yuan. Is it possible that they have no connection?

Normally, the investigation would proceed step by step, starting small and working its way up, until the mastermind is finally apprehended.

But Jiang Tian was different. He went straight for the top dog, leaving no chance for the others. During the subsequent trials, a significant portion of them will inevitably fall.

“You said it, pinky swear.” Shang Mingyan’s eyes seemed to blaze with fury.

Jiang Tian was utterly astonished, never expecting this side of Shang Mingyan.

But seeing Shang Mingyan’s expression shift from light to dark, Jiang Tian quickly reached out his hand. Shang Mingyan’s face instantly broke into a smile: “That’s more like it. Don’t worry, I’ll definitely handle everything regarding the Black Dragon Society cleanly, ensuring your first-class merit goes smoothly.”



Chapter 654 Murder Case? The Murderer Dares to Provoke Us?


"Alright, I'll be going now. I'll leave Mitsui Miko of the Black Dragon Society to you."

Jiang Tian stood up. He came to the Municipal Bureau to hand over the reins to Shang Mingyan. Besides Mitsui Miko and the Yunya Group, Jiang Tian had other cases to hand over, and he also needed to understand some other cases.

After all, there were too many cases lately.

Incidentally, he also needed to tell Shang Mingyan that the Huayu Group had been developed into a bottom line.

In other words, the Huayu Group was now essentially under Jiang Tian's control. Of course, this wasn't some kind of collusion; it was purely about developing an undercover agent and a bottom line. Jiang Tian wouldn't have any financial ties with them.

On the other hand, the Yunya Group's arrest was so successful, and it could be said that the Huayu Group, and even Chen Liting, had contributed a lot.

"Wait." Shang Mingyan reached out and stopped Jiang Tian.

"Is there anything else?" Jiang Tian asked.

There's nothing else to say. Since Shang Mingyan arrived, it has to be admitted that although there was a misunderstanding at the beginning, Jiang Tian's workload has decreased exponentially after Shang Mingyan got involved.

This also gave Jiang Tian time to investigate cases outside. Otherwise, just look at those criminal gangs like the Wenye Group, involved in complex crimes across all aspects, and with intricate connections to foreign forces. Dealing with them would require an unimaginable amount of time and energy.

This one case alone could practically tie Jiang Tian down.

"It's like this, I happen to have a case here, could you take a look at it for me?" Shang Mingyan stated his ultimate purpose.

"Is it troublesome?" Jiang Tian asked.

"It's quite troublesome," Shang Mingyan thought for a moment and answered honestly.

"Then forget it, I have important business in Beijing tomorrow," Jiang Tian said helplessly.

Goodness.

The August 1st Awards are about to be presented, and the bigwigs haven't even met him yet. This trip to Beijing is a chance to chat with them tomorrow.

Then he'll stay there for one day before the awards ceremony.

The time is tight.

Of course, this is also because Jiang Tian just happened to be there. The August 1st Awards are sometimes only presented once every few years, and Jiang Tian just happened to be there, contributing so much.

The key is that he ultimately sealed the deal with the Yugou.com scandal, wiping out the entire network. The influence and the contribution were immense. So, although it didn't meet the rules, this contribution became the crucial reason why Jiang Tian was able to be an exception and become a candidate at the last minute, and successfully selected.

There's no time to delay.

"Tell me in detail, what's the problem?" Jiang Tian didn't leave immediately.

Mainly because Jiang Tian guessed what was going on as soon as Shang Mingyan spoke.

The case must be extremely complicated.

Otherwise, Shang Mingyan wouldn't have contacted him.

Besides, in ordinary cases, you wouldn't find Shang Mingyan.

"A heinous murder has occurred at Yunnan University. The difficulty lies in the fact that after our thorough investigation, we've found nothing. All suspects have been ruled out. We've also investigated mobile criminal activity, but without success. The case is now completely at a standstill. This incident has caused considerable public outcry and significantly damaged Yunnan University's reputation," Shang Mingyan said.

Seeing Shang Mingyan mention Yunnan University, Jiang Tian instantly thought of someone.

That was Lian Xin, Jiang Xiaoyu's best friend.

Speaking of which, Jiang Tian hadn't seen that girl in a long time. Actually, regardless of his past or present life, Jiang Tian had always held a good impression of Lian Xin.

Of course, don't misunderstand, it was purely goodwill. In his past life, that girl had indeed been tirelessly working on Jiang Xiaoyu's case.

Wait.

"When did this happen?" Jiang Tian asked hastily.

It was only then that Jiang Tian realized he hadn't paid attention to those around him for a long time. This was mainly due to the complexity of the case, and also, he had to admit, that he always felt guilty towards Jiang Xiaoyu because of what happened in his past life.

"It happened yesterday. The murderer was extremely arrogant, even leaving a provocative message at the scene," Shang Mingyan sighed.

"Let's go take a look."

Jiang Tian frowned, a flicker of interest rising within him.

If it were a case with a wide-ranging connection, Jiang Tian wouldn't have time to get involved.

But for murder cases like this, the connections are usually simple. Besides, once the perpetrator appears, Jiang Tian can even skip the deductions.

"I'll take you there," Shang Mingyan volunteered.

Jiang Tian nodded. Recently, he'd noticed he'd caused Shang Mingyan a lot of trouble in many cases, and crucially, Shang Mingyan had been constantly solving his problems.

So, Jiang Tian would definitely help with this small favor. Of course, the most important thing was that Jiang Tian would absolutely not allow any danger to Jiang Xiaoyu or Lian Xin at Yun University.

That's right, the same applied to both Jiang Xiaoyu and Lian Xin.

Shang Mingyan was on a business trip, so he was driving an official car. With Jiang Tian accompanying him, he didn't bring anyone else, just the two of them.

The car window was open, and because it was already autumn, the wind blowing in from outside carried a chill.

"There are fewer cars on the road today," Shang Mingyan said, looking out the window.

"Do you often come over at night?" Jiang Tian asked.

"It's all because of you. I'm always busy handling those big cases you're handling. I only have time to bring Xiao Qiu over at night," Shang Mingyan rolled his eyes.

Jiang Tian nodded thoughtfully.

Yun University wasn't located in the city center of Yunhai; it wasn't a short distance.

While it wasn't exactly the suburbs, it was on the outer edge of the cloud sea.

At first, there were quite a few vehicles, but as they drove on, the number of cars around them gradually decreased.

Shang Mingyan would occasionally turn his head slightly to observe Jiang Tian out of the corner of his eye, noticing that Jiang Tian was always looking out the car window, his expression calm and silent.

His serious, enigmatic demeanor drew frequent glances from Shang Mingyan. After a couple of looks, Shang Mingyan blushed slightly and muttered under his breath, "Showing off."

But in reality, Jiang Tian was looking out the window, his brows deeply furrowed.

He paid no attention to Shang Mingyan's mutterings.

The night was dark, the streets dimly lit, and there wasn't a single pedestrian or car in sight.

Then, out of the corner of his eye, Jiang Tian caught a glimpse of a dark shadow flash across the road.

A sharp screech of brakes followed.

"What happened?" Jiang Tian asked.

"It looks like I just bumped into something?" Shang Mingyan asked uncertainly.



Chapter 655: They were all blown away and they still say they're fine?


As they spoke, Shang Mingyan opened the car door and got out. As expected of Shang Mingyan, he was always decisive and efficient. His everyday attire consisted of sportswear and sneakers, and he possessed a rather aloof demeanor.

Jiang Tian had vaguely seen something, though he hadn't paid much attention, but his reflexes were sharp.

"It's a cat."

Shang Mingyan looked under the car.

"It looks like a tabby cat, probably a stray." Shang Mingyan breathed a sigh of relief.

Jiang Tian glanced around. It turned out that the streetlights had all gone out when they got out of the car, leaving only dim streetlights.

"Never mind, let's go check the crime scene first." Jiang Tian checked the time.

The surrounding space had darkened. Both Shang Mingyan and Jiang Tian were experienced in all sorts of situations, and especially since it was late at night, the presence of two people made them feel less apprehensive.

Shang Mingyan stood up, about to open the car door, when Jiang Tian suddenly reached out and grabbed her hand.

Shang Mingyan could feel the warmth of his hand. Although she was in her thirties or forties, she had handled many corpses in her life, but this was the first time she had encountered such a scene.

Her face flushed instantly.

At that moment, Jiang Tian's voice slowly rang out: "Tell me first, what's the situation with this case?"

"The case?" Mentioning her expertise, Shang Mingyan was instantly intrigued.

"That's right, I haven't looked into it yet. Tell me the details of the case first," Jiang Tian inquired.

Looking at Jiang Tian's serious expression, Shang Mingyan couldn't help but start thinking. He hadn't asked her anything at the city police station, nor in the car.

She had originally planned to explain in detail at the crime scene, but why now, in the dark?

And she glanced around and saw no one nearby.

Why was this happening?

“A young girl was murdered in her dormitory. At first, we thought it was suicide because the door and windows were locked from the inside. But later, we found signs of assault on her body. Not only that, the killer left a message on her body.” Shang Mingyan's mind was a little foggy.

Because she couldn't understand Jiang Tian, ​​she subconsciously overthought things, trying to figure out what he wanted.

According to behavioral logic, in this kind of environment, in a dark and secluded place, a lone man and woman together are most likely to create an unusual atmosphere.

And at this time, it's easy to arouse certain impulses in men.

Shang Mingyan's mind unconsciously started overthinking.

“What message?” Jiang Tian inquired further.

“I'm back.”

After Shang Mingyan finished speaking, she noticed that Jiang Tian had fallen completely silent, seemingly lost in thought. This made Shang Mingyan look at Jiang Tian with suspicion.

She had been wondering whether she should refuse or… if Jiang Tian made any further moves.

After all, Jiang Tian seemed quite young, and people his age are often impulsive.

But it turned out that Jiang Tian didn't seem to have any such thoughts at all.

Was I really overthinking it?

Shang Mingyan couldn't help but doubt her own charm. Of course, it wasn't that she liked Jiang Tian; age didn't matter to her. The main reason was that Jiang Tian was the first person who had piqued her curiosity and made her want to know more about him.

"Based on experience, it should be a simple locked-room murder case," Shang Mingyan added.

The main problem was the lack of leads.

You see, a criminal investigation without evidence, relying solely on a few scattered clues, is incredibly difficult. Expanding the search area could stretch into years, consuming enormous energy.

And even then, there's no guarantee of solving it.

The main reason is that the methods and levels of crime-solving in this era are far too outdated.

The search is practically a house-to-house investigation.

"Have you offended anyone in Yunhai?" Jiang Tian asked.

"What do you mean?" Shang Mingyan asked, puzzled.

In the next instant, Jiang Tian grabbed Shang Mingyan's wrist and swiftly lurched her to the roadside.

At the same moment Jiang Tian acted,

the official car Shang Mingyan was about to open its door erupted in a terrifying and violent roar.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian had shielded Shang Mingyan in front of him.

Throughout the entire ordeal, Shang Mingyan was completely stunned. Even with all her experience, she was utterly overwhelmed by this scene.

If it hadn't been for Jiang Tian, ​​she would already be inside the car.

The crucial point was that the official car had exploded.

It had exploded.

And the power of the explosion was terrifying.

Even though she was in Jiang Tian's arms, completely protected by him, the terrifying impact still sent both of them flying. The terrifying flames made Shang Mingyan feel the surrounding air was scalding hot.

The initial shock and lingering fear were fleeting. Then, using the firelight, Shang Mingyan looked up, his eyes shining as he gazed at Jiang Tian's calm face.

Even in this situation, he remained so calm.

At that moment, Shang Mingyan suddenly felt an unusual sensation, a sensation he had never experienced before.

No.

Wait…

"Jiang Tian, ​​how are you? Are you hurt? I'll call an ambulance."

Upon landing, Shang Mingyan crashed onto the soft grass. Jiang Tian braced himself, flipped over, and stood up abruptly.

Lying on the ground, Shang Mingyan clearly saw that Jiang Tian's clothes were completely torn to shreds.

His skin was burned black.

The car had exploded; Shang Mingyan was shocked. Jiang Tian had saved him. This was the first time Shang Mingyan had experienced such an unusual feeling, but seeing the blackened back of Jiang Tian, ​​Shang Mingyan's mind went blank.

He was injured.

Moreover, her injuries were very serious.

Shang Mingyan had witnessed the explosion firsthand; the distance was too close. If Jiang Tian hadn't pulled her back, she would be dead.

Similarly, she was standing right next to the car. If Jiang Tian hadn't protected her, she would have been killed instantly at that close range.

And during this short period, Jiang Tian not only saved her twice, but he himself was also seriously injured.

Panic ensued.

Shang Mingyan had experienced a lot, and none of it had caused him much emotional fluctuation. After all, he had seen all sorts of crime scenes. However, Jiang Tian's injuries made Shang Mingyan somewhat panicked.

"I'm fine," Jiang Tian shook his head.

"What do you mean 'fine'? You're seriously injured. You need to go to the hospital now." Shang Mingyan's eyes flashed as he looked at the back of Jiang Tian's clothes, which had been blown to shreds, revealing his blackened skin.

He was blown away.

And he says he's fine.

Shang Mingyan was almost laughing in anger.



Chapter 656 Dad, we've all made something of ourselves!


Shang Mingyan leaned close to Jiang Tian, ​​supporting him, which left Jiang Tian looking utterly helpless.

In reality, a normal person might have been instantly killed by the explosion, or at least seriously injured. But Jiang Tian was different. He looked frightening, but in fact, Jiang Tian had a very high tolerance for injury. His appearance was only superficial.

Actually, after a quick clean, his skin would be fine. Even if he had minor injuries, he wouldn't need to go to the hospital.

But Shang Mingyan didn't see it that way!

Most importantly, Shang Mingyan's earlier thought was quite accurate. At Jiang Tian's age, and given his good constitution, being so close to Shang Mingyan was bound to cause some damage.

He missed Gao Yangyang.

Hmm.

Jiang Tian fell into deep thought.

"Going to the hospital isn't the priority. The key is, have you offended anyone recently?"

Compared to his injuries, Jiang Tian's most important concern now was his curiosity about the person who planted the bomb in the car.

Or rather, his fury.

If he hadn't been there today, if he had been with Shang Mingyan, he would definitely be dead.

Most importantly...

Jiang Tian looked at the stray cats on the street, and his subconscious told him that this was definitely not a coincidence. Bomb, stray cat, explosion.

When all of this came together, it was a premeditated explosion.

The key question is, how long has Shang Mingyan been in Yunhai? He hasn't stayed in Yunhai for very long. Who exactly did he offend?

"Are you worried about me?" A strange light flashed in Shang Mingyan's bright eyes.

It has to be said that Jiang Tian's charm at this moment, at least in front of Shang Mingyan, was completely different, at its peak. Even the way he looked at Jiang Tian was different.

It's the same old story: the hero saves the damsel in distress. Although it seems like a cliché, it's precisely because it's cliché that it becomes a classic. Clichés are effective because they work every time.

"Besides the Municipal Bureau, has this car been to any other places in the past few days?" Jiang Tian asked.

He knew Shang Mingyan's position required him to frequently visit crime scenes.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau could be ruled out first because the car was parked there; if such a high-explosive charge could be planted there, the officers would have simply run over themselves.

Of course, it wasn't impossible that things were under their noses.

However, the Municipal Public Security Bureau has close surveillance; if the perpetrator acted there,

that would be suicide.

Conversely, if he were the perpetrator, the best place would be—the crime scene, of course. Considering Shang Mingyan's statement about frequently visiting Yunnan University at night recently...

That means...

He's at Yunnan University.

In fact, the perpetrator...

The perpetrator is at Yunnan University.

Jiang Tian had already reached a conclusion, a cold smile playing on his lips.

This person is incredibly audacious, openly provoking Shang Mingyan, and even threatening to kill him. Did this person know Shang Mingyan before, or is this just an isolated incident?

Such blatant provocation, and the fact that they targeted Shang Mingyan directly...

Jiang Tian's mind raced with deductions. While the details and information were limited, Jiang Tian's reasoning ability was legendary, comparable to Sherlock Holmes.

With the limited details available,

Jiang Tian had already roughly simulated the situation in his mind.

"Xiao Jiang, let's go to the hospital. The explosion blew everyone away." Shang Mingyan, usually so composed, was now somewhat flustered.

"I'm really fine. These are just burns. If you wipe them off, there's really no real injury."

Jiang Tian quickly shook his head. Damn it, they were heading to Beijing tomorrow.

And this was related to subsequent strategic matters.

If someone got hospitalized today, Beijing would be on the verge of exploding, and Yunhai would be in even greater turmoil.

The key point was that if Jiang Tian's deduction was correct, causing such a huge upheaval wouldn't be beneficial; on the contrary, it would be a pure waste of resources.

"Impossible! That explosion was with high explosives. You were holding me and you were blasted seven or eight meters away. No one could withstand that kind of impact." Shang Mingyan simply didn't believe it.

"Then do you think I'm a normal person?" Jiang Tian retorted.

This momentarily stunned Shang Mingyan. Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​he recalled what Jiang Tian had done in the past.

This... it seemed that what Jiang Tian said wasn't impossible.

But how could this be?!

How could a human be completely unharmed after such an impact, even with their clothes torn apart? Was this a god?

Jiang Tian didn't continue speaking. He took out his phone and dialed Zhao Wei's number.

At this time, Zhao Wei was still busy at the city police station. After receiving Jiang Tian's call, he immediately sent people over.

Jiang Tian didn't wait any longer.

After all, there wasn't any useful information from the car, so Jiang Tian hailed a taxi and took Shang Mingyan directly to the scene of the crime.

On the way, Shang Mingyan kept a close eye on Jiang Tian.

It was late at night.

Lights out.

As for students, aside from the diligent ones studying in the library, most had already returned to their dorms and gone to rest.

"Speaking of which, a law professor from Yunnan University wants me to come here for graduate studies," Jiang Tian said, looking at Yunnan University.

Mainly, he'd been too busy lately. The professor had called Jiang Tian, ​​but he hadn't had time to come.

Also, his internship at the police academy wasn't over yet.

But it would be soon.

Yes, Jiang Tian was still technically an intern on an off-campus internship, which was absurd, but normally, it would be over soon.

So now Jiang Tian needed to plan ahead. A bachelor's degree in criminal investigation alone wouldn't be an advantage for future promotions.

And clearly, if he wanted to pave a wider path in the future, a higher degree was also crucial.

Of course, that wasn't particularly important to Jiang Tian right now. His priority was to focus on his legal studies.

“Law? You can come to me. I’m a professor of law at Beijing University,” Shang Mingyan said.

“Professor? Aren’t you from the police academy?” Jiang Tian asked in surprise.

A professor at Beijing University?

Jiang Tian looked at Shang Mingyan. Good heavens, his abnormality relied on cheat codes, but Shang Mingyan's was a genuine, terrifying level of skill based on real ability!

No wonder everyone calls her Professor.

"A part-time position, huh? I'm proficient in both criminal investigation and law." Shang Mingyan seemed to finally find a little bit of superiority over Jiang Tian.

"It's a bit too far." But Jiang Tian was somewhat tempted.

Damn it, although Yunhai University is also a Double First-Class University, it can't compare to Beijing. The key is, if I go to Beijing, I can't come back to Yunhai to handle cases!

"That's simple. Anyway, I'll be staying in Yunhai for the time being. With your qualifications, I'll transfer your student status there." Shang Mingyan rolled her eyes.

Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat.

Dad, I've finally made something of myself.

In his previous life, Jiang Tian wouldn't have dared to dream of this.



Chapter 657 A Tragic Past, A Breakthrough


Of course, is this operation against the rules?

That's nonsense. A normal person would definitely be breaking the rules doing this, but Jiang Tian is absolutely exceptional right now. Seven First-Class Merits, the only recipient of a Special-Class Merit in recent decades, numerous honorary medals, and the soon-to-be August 1st Medal.

Going to your Beijing University is giving you face.

You'd even have to beg.

"Okay," Jiang Tian was tempted.

Mainly, Jiang Tian wasn't very familiar with the professors at Yunnan University. Since that was the case, it would be better to let Shang Mingyan handle it directly.

Besides, Jiang Tian had always worked well with Shang Mingyan. After all, no matter what mess, Shang Mingyan could always handle it beautifully.

If he really let Shang Mingyan leave, Jiang Tian would really be reluctant.

"Is there anything I need to do?" Jiang Tian asked uncertainly.

"It's alright. Going to Beijing University is giving them face. We'll transfer your files there first, and we'll brush up on your law studies soon, then you can go straight to graduate school."

"Have you ever studied law before?" Shang Mingyan asked.

"I know it inside and out," Jiang Tian nodded.

It might have been difficult before, but for Jiang Tian, ​​with his current intelligence and the legendary gold-level criminal investigation skill, what is that? Does criminal investigators not understand law?

That's ridiculous!

Now Jiang Tian has enough knowledge in everything, he just lacks a formal academic degree.

Therefore, as long as it involves assessments, Jiang Tian will have no problem.

"That's even simpler then. Just wait for the news," Shang Mingyan confidently assured him.

As they spoke, Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan had already entered through the school gate.

It must be said, the scenery at Yunnan University is quite beautiful.

Spanning several thousand acres, and with its many mountains and rivers, the campus is nestled against the mountains and beside the water, with excellent greenery and tranquil groves along the roads.

Jiang Tian remained silent, his mind preoccupied with the case the entire way.

Regardless, Jiang Tian felt that Shang Mingyan had helped him immensely, both in solving the case and in getting to school today.

Otherwise, without Shang Mingyan's help, even though Jiang Tian was qualified, he probably wouldn't have had the freedom he needed at Peking University.

In other words, he could never repay Shang Mingyan's kindness.

But as they walked, Jiang Tian's ears twitched; he heard a strange sound.

Shang Mingyan, following behind Jiang Tian, ​​didn't speak for a moment. Crucially, the two of them strolling through the quiet campus created an inexplicable atmosphere.

It made Shang Mingyan's heart race.

Shang Mingyan was stunned, completely bewildered.

She had experienced so much in her life, but she had never felt this way before—her heart racing.

Her face seemed to be turning slightly red.

But after walking a few steps, the already strange atmosphere finally reached Shang Mingyan's ears, and the peculiar sound finally did.

*Whoosh!*

Shang Mingyan's face instantly turned red.

In fact, Shang Mingyan had seen many things, even handled various criminal cases, and had even witnessed people die suddenly while working.

But he had never felt this way before.

Normally, Shang Mingyan wouldn't have felt anything. After all, she was highly intelligent and mature, and more importantly, she was an authority in psychology, able to see through anyone's thoughts or sordid ideas at a glance.

The key was that the people she was with today were different.

Her relationship with Jiang Tian had already become somewhat unusual after what had just happened.

And then this huge thing suddenly happened.

Even Shang Mingyan, whose brain usually worked at lightning speed, felt her mind go blank.

And the further they walked, the clearer the voice became.

Jiang Tian, ​​unable to think straight about the case, awkwardly changed the subject, saying, "Students these days have no shame, no morals. Doing this kind of thing in broad daylight."

"Have you done it at school?" Shang Mingyan blurted out instinctively.

When she realized what she'd said, Shang Mingyan couldn't take it anymore. She didn't know how she'd asked that question, why she'd said such a thing.

Especially now, Jiang Tian's expression was priceless upon hearing those words.

His mind raced, trying to decipher their meaning.

Was it something he intended?

Or was there another motive?

Or was he testing his character?

Jiang Tian's mind was racing.

Even some clues he had just considered regarding the case suddenly vanished.

Sure enough, women only slowed him down.

Jiang Tian inwardly cursed himself, but looking at Shang Mingyan's expression, he understood somewhat.

Although he was somewhat slow in this regard—after all, criminal investigation and logic are based on intelligence, completely different from emotional intelligence—he was bound to be a little slow in these situations.

But even the slowest person could see what Shang Mingyan meant at this moment, unless they were an idiot.

Jiang Tian couldn't help but sigh inwardly. Why did everyone love playing the hero and rescuing a damsel in distress? Even without the means, they'd create them. This tactic was something even a highly intelligent elite like Shang Mingyan couldn't resist, especially considering she was a criminal investigation authority!

She'd seen it all, yet she couldn't withstand this kind of approach.

When Jiang Tian looked down, their hands were unconsciously clasped together.

Jiang Tian sighed inwardly. While he had to admit he was quite pleased that even a woman like Shang Mingyan could fall for him, after some hesitation, he ultimately acquiesced to the relationship.

Shang Mingyan, however, was already a master of psychology. She clearly saw the fleeting smugness on Jiang Tian's face and couldn't help but roll her eyes.

"Actually, all these years, I've never thought about finding a boyfriend or anything like that. In my eyes, all men are the same, there's no difference, and I'm not interested in them at all. Besides the fact that psychology allows me to see through everyone's thoughts and I can't muster any interest, the main reason is that my parents died thirty years ago, when I was very young, in a series of murders on a rainy night. I only want to avenge them." Shang Mingyan seemed to have regained his composure.

His beautiful eyes were also frighteningly intelligent.

Jiang Tian, ​​however, was surprised to hear this for the first time and exclaimed, "Thirty years have passed and the case still hasn't been solved?"

“No, absolutely no clues. The physical evidence from back then was destroyed due to technical limitations, and there were no witnesses. The killer was extremely ruthless; their killings always had one characteristic: they even dismembered the bodies.”

Shang Mingyan spoke calmly, but Jiang Tian felt his hand tighten.

One could imagine the immense psychological trauma this incident inflicted on young Shang Mingyan.

“The victims in that case were mostly under thirty, couples over twenty. My parents were among them; I was lucky to survive.”

“Is there any indication of who the killer is?” Jiang Tian’s eyes flickered.



Chapter 658 An Unexpected Fall in Love, a Deadly Poison


This was Jiang Tian's first glimpse into Shang Mingyan's past. In fact, Jiang Tian knew quite a bit about Shang Mingyan from his previous life, mainly because within the police system, he would inevitably come into contact with this woman, but not in real life.

Rather, he knew her from news reports, from internal newspapers.

In his previous life, Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan were from two different phases, on two different levels. In his past life, Jiang Tian could only work in an administrative unit, enduring exclusion, drifting through life in obscurity, watching Shang Mingyan chair meetings in the lowest-level unit.

This was the intersection between Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan in his previous life. But at that time, what he knew about Shang Mingyan was the series of major cases she solved. Every time a major case was solved, Shang Mingyan would appear in the newspapers, such as hosting press conferences.

She truly was an authority figure.

After his rebirth, Jiang Tian had frequent contact with Shang Mingyan, but his primary focus was on understanding Shang Mingyan's abilities and, more importantly, his personality.

Shang Mingyan had initially seemed like a workaholic, a machine devoid of emotion when solving cases.

However, now, Shang Mingyan gave Jiang Tian a completely different impression. He discovered that even this authoritative figure had his weaknesses.

"Thirty years have passed. Although I reopened the investigation before, almost all the clues from thirty years ago have disappeared. Tongtian District is completely different now. With millions of people in Tongtian District, where are we supposed to find a murderer? Unless, the murderer strikes again."

"But thirty years of silence... the murderer might even be gone."

The era was too long.

The time.

It was also too long.

So long that Shang Mingyan had even given up.

She spoke of it simply because confiding in Jiang Tian felt like a huge weight had been lifted from her heart for decades, a truly liberating feeling.

It was as if having Jiang Tian as a confidant allowed her to finally breathe a sigh of relief.

"Not necessarily. I'm going to the capital tomorrow. I'll probably stay there for a few days. If I have the chance, I'll see if I can find any clues," Jiang Tian said thoughtfully.

Others might not be able to find anything, but that doesn't mean Jiang Tian can't.

Judging from the description of the case, there's almost no chance left, unless the murderer reappears thirty years later and commits another crime. Even then, finding them isn't guaranteed; there's only a sliver of hope for solving the case.

High-IQ crime.

It has always been the most difficult aspect of solving cases.

Even from Jiang Tian's perspective, this case is somewhat tricky; for an ordinary person, it's even more impossible.

"Forget it, it's impossible. I'm telling you this because I know you can understand me. Although I'm an authority in criminal investigation in the eyes of outsiders, I know that your expertise in criminal investigation is definitely no less than mine. We both understand that this kind of case is impossible to solve. Thirty years have passed, and things have changed drastically. Chasing after it would just be a waste of time and resources." Shang Mingyan shook his head and refused.

Because she knew it was impossible. This wasn't the only similar case; many similar cases had occurred over the decades.

But almost all of them became unsolved mysteries, unless the killer could strike again.

Or, some unsolved cases left behind physical evidence like DNA, which, decades later, might be solved through a combination of chance and technological sifting.

Otherwise, actively searching would not only waste countless resources but also ultimately amount to nothing more than a waste of resources.

“How will we know if we don’t try? Don’t worry, I know what I’m doing,” Jiang Tian said earnestly, looking at Shang Mingyan.

After understanding her, at this moment, Jiang Tian realized that he seemed to be the first to truly understand Shang Mingyan.

This was also Jiang Tian’s promise to Shang Mingyan.

Similarly, at this moment, seeing Jiang Tian’s earnest expression, Shang Mingyan, for the first time, was captivated.

Her eyes became somewhat lost.

An unprecedented feeling arose in her heart.

Since losing her parents, she had even refused all help due to her precocious nature, living independently for so many years on her parents' inheritance.

This was the first time, and the very first person, that Shang Mingyan had let her guard down completely.

"Although it's impossible, thank you anyway."

The two figures overlapped. Feeling that broad chest, Shang Mingyan relaxed for the first time in her life. She knew Jiang Tian also knew it was impossible; with his criminal investigation experience, he certainly understood the problem with this case—it was unsolvable.

But Jiang Tian still said those words. Shang Mingyan smiled and silently muttered to herself, "Fool."

"Let's go. Let me see who's going to attack you first, and then see if it's the same person involved in the Yun Da case." Jiang Tian was leaving tomorrow, his eyes deeply furrowed.

Because...

Her sister and Lian Xin were both studying at Yun Da!

If this case wasn't solved today, in other words, even if Shang Mingyan was careful, there would still be danger. Lian Xin and Jiang Xiaoyu might face the murderer directly.

Even leaving Yunhai wouldn't put Jiang Tian's mind at ease.

Today was an extremely special day for both Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan. For Shang Mingyan, Jiang Tian's lack of rejection meant agreement.

Besides, Shang Mingyan's thinking was truly different from ordinary people. He was very comfortable to be around; if they wanted to be together, then they would be together. As for other things—age, relationship—did they matter?

Absolutely not.

Therefore, Shang Mingyan had shed all her psychological burdens, and her close contact with Jiang Tian became increasingly natural.

As they walked, they seemed like a couple.

They strolled through the campus.

For Jiang Tian, ​​this was a way of making up for a regret. After all, back in school, Shang Mingyan was solely focused on criminal investigation, while Jiang Tian had been dragged along by him.

The novelty of this experience was different.

And now, hearing that embarrassing sound again, they were both used to it.

It was a very comfortable feeling, but every story has an end, and this journey also has an end. After arriving at the girls' dormitory, Shang Mingyan left Jiang Tian's side with some reluctance and regret.

After all, one should be mindful of their image when visiting a girls' dormitory.

The dormitory supervisor at Yunnan University was quite dedicated; she seemed to have just returned from fetching water, carrying a kettle in her hand. Upon seeing Shang Mingyan, she recognized him immediately: "Officer Shang, you've arrived."

"Just checking again today to see if there are any oversights," Shang Mingyan nodded indifferently.



Chapter 659 Okay, okay, I've become a hooligan?


Completely different from her behavior with Jiang Tian, ​​this seemed to be Shang Mingyan's truest personality.

But Jiang Tian knew that this aloof exterior was merely Shang Mingyan's way of protecting herself.

In fact, it was only today that Jiang Tian truly discovered how deeply Shang Mingyan hid her true nature, because her heart was absolutely vulnerable.

It was just that her aloof exterior concealed this vulnerability, and no one could penetrate Shang Mingyan's professional knowledge to see her inner fragility.

As luck would have it, today's unexpected turn of events allowed Jiang Tian to see Shang Mingyan's most authentic side. This also meant that Jiang Tian had gained Shang Mingyan's complete approval. If there were a rating system, before today, Shang Mingyan's approval rating was probably only around 60 or 70 points—a mix of curiosity, admiration, and goodwill.

But after today, it was likely to have surpassed 100.

In other words, she had completely entrusted herself to Jiang Tian.

Although Jiang Tian had experienced a great deal, the fact that a woman as remarkable as Shang Mingyan could tear down her defenses and fall completely in love made even him feel incredibly lucky.

"Officer Shang, you've come several times already, is there anything else wrong?" the dormitory supervisor asked curiously.

"Don't ask what you shouldn't ask. Of course, don't overthink it either. It involves solving a case, and the criminals are ruthless. Telling you might put you in danger too, but this is to protect you," Shang Mingyan said calmly.

The dormitory supervisor turned pale with fright and hurriedly nodded, "Yes, Officer Shang is right."

This dormitory supervisor was very perceptive. When the case first occurred, the police arrived and simply cordoned off the scene. After a preliminary investigation, no one dared to move. It was only after a long wait that Officer Shang arrived with dozens of officers.

Moreover, Shang Mingyan was wearing a white police uniform at that time—he was definitely a high-ranking official. How could the dormitory supervisor dare to offend him?

But the dorm supervisor's gaze shifted, and upon seeing Jiang Tian, ​​she felt no qualms.

Young.

His eyes held a dazed look.

He was clearly a recent graduate, probably still in his internship.

For such a major case, this Officer Shang seemed a bit unreliable.

Moreover.

"It's so late, it might be inconvenient for this comrade to go in, Officer Shang. Perhaps we could have him wait downstairs?" the dorm supervisor said, pointing at Jiang Tian.

Huh?

Jiang Tian, ​​who hadn't said anything initially, glanced at the dorm supervisor.

"What are you looking at? These are all female students. You're so young, you've probably only been out of school for a few days. Do you think you can help with such a big case? Besides causing trouble for Officer Shang, who knows what you might see inside? Letting you in would be irresponsible to the female students," the dorm supervisor scoffed.

While speaking, she completely failed to notice that Shang Mingyan's gaze had become increasingly dangerous.

Putting aside the recent breakthrough in Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan's relationship, just consider this: Jiang Tian's current position and achievements—do you, a mere dormitory supervisor, have any say in this?

Most importantly, he's involved in a police investigation.

And you, a mere dormitory supervisor, are giving me free rein?

“Actually, I was just thinking about the female students at the school. Young man, you should understand. It's already too late, and the area is already cordoned off. If you want to come, you can come during the day,” the dormitory supervisor said, changing the subject.

At this moment,

Shang Mingyan's cold and beautiful face darkened.

Her eyes flashed with a dangerous light.

“Do you know you're obstructing the police investigation?” Jiang Tian said calmly.

“You, the police investigation?”

The dormitory supervisor couldn't help but sneer, almost bursting out laughing. “Young man, you're just an intern at best, trying to label people. Let me tell you, I'm not someone to be trifled with.”

In her eyes, Shang Mingyan was just someone with a lowly driver. Besides, to be a dormitory supervisor here, she had to pull strings with a director at the school.

After saying that, the dormitory supervisor sneered and muttered, "What are you trying to prove, you lowly intern? This place is full of top-tier university students; you're not worthy of being compared to us."

Now Jiang Tian finally understood what it meant to make things difficult for others within the limits of his authority.

Just then...

Not far from the door, in the corridor, stood a girl in a nightgown, holding a basin of water, seemingly having just finished washing up. She was of average appearance, with freckles on her face, and seemed to understand what was happening.

Her face instantly filled with offended rage. Suddenly, she pointed at Jiang Tian and yelled, "Coming to our girls' dormitory so late at night, you must have bad intentions! Why don't you come during the day? Coming so late means you're up to no good, trying to take advantage of us! I'm going to complain to your supervisor!"

That's why universities aren't so strictly managed, and there aren't strict rules about lights out.

After all, the atmosphere at Yunnan University, and even in terms of management, is relatively lenient.

Even during the day, some boys would come to the girls' dormitory to find their girlfriends.

It was already quite late.

Like in high school, lights out and everyone was asleep long ago, but university is quite different.

Of course, none of that matters.

What matters is...

Jiang Tian felt offended.

"Taking advantage?"

Jiang Tian looked at the freckled, ordinary-looking guy in front of him—the kind of guy who probably wouldn't even get a second glance in a crowd—and thought, "This guy's self-esteem is a bit too high."

No, no, no.

Jiang Tian quickly shook his head.

At this moment, Jiang Tian couldn't help but ponder whether there was a problem with the atmosphere at Yun University.

Different perspectives, different positions, different considerations.

If it were before, Jiang Tian would have been furious.

But now, at Jiang Tian's current rank—a second-level police inspector and captain of the anti-drug squad, at twenty-two years old—in other words, if Jiang Tian wanted to advance, he would have to slowly grind for ten or twenty years, and even without any merit, he could smoothly enter the department.

Arguing with a dorm supervisor and a woman this stupid? Sorry, Jiang Tian really couldn't bring himself to do it right now.

That's because different positions lead to different perspectives.

"Heh, I'm going to complain to your superiors. From the moment you walked in, you've been staring at me. This is our girls' private space at night. You've seen the crime scene hundreds of times. Coming here late at night is one thing, but for a scoundrel like you to show up like this, what else could it be but malicious intent?" The freckled woman held her head high like a proud swan.

For some reason, Jiang Tian saw a resemblance to Wang Yifei in her.

Wait, why is this woman so narcissistic?

And this dormitory supervisor.

Jiang Tian frowned, too lazy to argue. Seeing Shang Mingyan about to explode, Jiang Tian raised his hand to stop him.

Shang Mingyan looked at Jiang Tian, ​​confused.

"Let's put this case on hold for a few days. You can stay at the Municipal Bureau for the next few days. I happen to have a bed in my office. We'll talk again when I get back from Beijing."

Jiang Tian also planned to have Lian Xin and Jiang Xiaoyu take a few days off.

After arguing with them until the wee hours, and with a trip to the capital the next morning, Jiang Tian had no time to waste arguing and getting humiliated by these people.

What would he gain from actually getting humiliated?

"No, you can't leave! You filthy scoundrel, you took advantage of me and now you want to leave?"



Chapter 660 Advanced Advancement, School Leaders Are Rushing to the Battlefield


Jiang Tian paused. Shang Mingyan, standing beside him, looked at him, waiting for his reaction. Jiang Tian, ​​observing Shang Mingyan's expression, understood.

Women!

That's how they are.

Once they've decided on someone, their entire demeanor changes drastically. Even the usually calm and highly intelligent Shang Mingyan was now acting according to Jiang Tian's wishes.

Or rather, waiting for Jiang Tian's reaction, seeing how he intended to handle the situation.

Frankly, this only applies to people at Jiang Tian's level.

Before, Jiang Tian might have slapped her a couple of times, bringing her back to reality, but that was some time ago.

Now, in terms of status, power, and everything else, they're on completely different levels.

Otherwise, when have you ever seen someone from the Ministry of Personnel get angry with a young girl? Even if they're in the right, it wouldn't look good if word got out.

Because this no longer requires Jiang Tian to handle it.

As they spoke, the freckled woman blocked Jiang Tian's path, loudly accusing him of taking advantage of her.

Then, the two dormitory rooms facing the entrance couldn't resist opening their doors, and girls peeked out, curiously watching the scene.

Of course, while they were watching the spectacle, they weren't going to actually get involved. Most people were normal; watching was one thing, but getting involved was another.

Thus, Jiang Tian was blocked, and more and more people gathered to watch.

"Taking advantage of you?" Jiang Tian tilted his head, his face full of disbelief.

"You could say it's because you're pretty, but I didn't see anything, so how could I take advantage of you?"

Most importantly, he should take a look in the mirror and see what he looked like.

Besides, although it was night, the freckled woman who came out to wash up wasn't dressed lightly; it was casual clothing, and even in her pajamas, nothing was showing.

"Taking advantage of you? Where does that come from?" Jiang Tian wanted to hear her explanation.

"You're spying on me." The girl, phone in hand, blocked Jiang Tian's path.

That's right.

She had just sensed that Jiang Tian definitely had ill intentions.

His eyes had taken the opportunity to glance at her several times.

What else could this be but spying?

Even if he hadn't physically done anything, he had mentally acted on it.

That's still illegal.

If Jiang Tian hadn't been a police officer, she would have called the police by now.

Of course, although she didn't call the police immediately, she still took out her phone and dialed the school administration.

Her first words were that she had been sexually assaulted.

Jiang Tian fell silent.

What kind of trend is this?

When did this kind of thing happen?

Oh no.

Jiang Tian turned his head and glanced at the two open dormitory doors he could see directly. Seven or eight girls' heads were peeking out. Thankfully, after Jiang Tian's glance, there was no further spying.

Jiang Tian unconsciously wiped away cold sweat. It turned out, thankfully, this wasn't a widespread issue, just an isolated case.

Of course, there are all sorts of birds in a big forest, and that's not entirely wrong. However, given how absurd this had become, Shang Mingyan, who had been with Jiang Tian the entire time, was practically laughing with rage.

Meanwhile, while the freckled-faced woman was on the phone, Jiang Tian was silently contemplating how to handle the situation.

Not far from the dormitory, a large group of people was gathering.

These people all shared one characteristic: they were all dressed formally in suits, especially the leader. If Jiang Tian were there, he would definitely recognize him.

Lian Ying from Yunnan University.

He was the person who had been extremely grateful to Jiang Tian, ​​saving his career.

That's right, the drug case at Yunnan University was terrifyingly far-reaching. Such a problem within the university was unbelievable. If the impact were to expand, it was entirely possible that everyone from top to bottom would be wiped out.

Moreover, the consequences were so severe that Lian Ying once thought he was finished; being placed under investigation was the least of his worries.

His life was completely over.

But thankfully, thanks to Jiang Tian's heroic investigation, the facts of the case gradually became clear, and those involved were apprehended one after another.

As for those who were kept in the dark, they certainly couldn't escape unscathed; at the very least, there was a lack of effective oversight.

Of course, with this level of punishment, there's at least a chance for them to recover in the future.

Jiang Tian, ​​you could say, saved Yunda University.

Otherwise, every single one of these leaders would likely have been implicated.

"Fortunately, Yunhai has Comrade Jiang Tian. Otherwise, the consequences would have been unimaginable. It's not just a problem with Yunda University, but with every aspect of Yunhai. Without Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​the consequences would have been truly unimaginable."

One of the leaders was still sighing as he walked.

Back then, Jiang Tian was just beginning to show his talent; it seems like only yesterday.

Although they were at Yunnan University, not part of the police force, the events surrounding Jiang Tian had already caused such a stir among ordinary people, let alone the university leaders, who were naturally well aware of everything.

Of course, while the specifics of Jiang Tian's accomplishments were unclear, his achievements were crystal clear. A first-class merit was practically a given, practically a unique honor in peacetime.

Unprecedented and unparalleled, no one had ever achieved this before, and it's unlikely anyone will in the future. One man truly changed a city, and even the entire landscape of society.

"Although this murder case is a terrifying and major case for us, with public opinion and widespread shock both inside and outside the school, for Comrade Jiang, it's perhaps just a minor case. We are all grateful to Comrade Jiang for being here," Lian Ying remarked.

Speaking of which,

he also learned about it from the security guards. Of course, the security guards knew Jiang Tian all too well; they had witnessed firsthand how he slaughtered everyone in the gunfight that erupted on campus last time.

They were extremely familiar with Jiang Tian; if it weren't for him, these security guards and gatekeepers wouldn't know what was going on.

So when Jiang Tian appeared, the security guard saw him and immediately notified the school leadership, who then relayed the information up the chain of command until it reached Lian Ying.

It's worth mentioning that

Since the last incident, security on campus has been raised significantly. While it's not quite at the level of complete control, it's not as free as before.

Especially online, security guards and gatekeepers are checking everywhere for contraband.



Chapter 661 Xiao Jiang is here, which means Bao Qingtian is here.


"That kind of thing happens once, and you might get lucky and get away with it. But if it happens a second time, forget about what the higher-ups will do to them; they themselves won't be able to continue working."

"That's right. I heard from my friend in the police system that Professor Shang, the leading criminal investigator who came to our school, is even more terrifying than Comrade Jiang in terms of investigative and crime-solving abilities. I've heard that in Comrade Jiang's hands, there's no case he can't solve, no cold case he can't handle. No matter the case, how big the impact, how many connections, Comrade Jiang can solve it in a day at most." At this moment, a school leader quickly spoke up.

This was the vice-principal, and as soon as he spoke, everyone else's faces showed immense eagerness.

After all,

Jiang Tian had already proven how terrifying his abilities were to them. They had all witnessed firsthand how Jiang Tian apprehended criminals; his moves were deadly.

Of course, most importantly,

Jiang Tian's confidentiality regulations have recently been lifted. At this stage, continuing to keep it secret would only smack of trying to cover something up.

Others might fear retaliation.

But now public opinion has taken hold, and Jiang Tian has essentially been exposed on certain sensitive websites.

Except for some classified cases, everything about Jiang Tian's life and his work at the Municipal Public Security Bureau has been revealed, then selectively quoted and manipulated by those with ulterior motives.

After this happened, the higher-ups understood that someone wanted to target Jiang Tian from this angle. Keeping it secret wouldn't be good for Jiang Tian or for their authority. So, taking advantage of the August 1st announcement, Jiang Tian was officially brought into the spotlight.

From now on, Jiang Tian is no longer a hero in the shadows, but stands before everyone.

Although this might slightly increase the danger, can Jiang Tian's personal safety be guaranteed now?

Even the security detail assigned to Jiang Tian when he goes out has been withdrawn.

Because it's pointless; Jiang Tian can handle them alone. If a fight breaks out, it's unclear who will protect whom.

It's come to this point. Those who could act have already acted, and those who couldn't will find it difficult to act in the future. Those who should act, regardless of whether it's exposed or not, will act sooner or later given the extent of Jiang Tian's exposure. Since there's no difference between keeping it secret and not keeping it secret, there's naturally no need to try to cover it up and let the rumors spread online.

Just completely and utterly disclose what Jiang Tian has done.

And, the scale of the seized case is as high as hundreds of tons. That's right, the amount of heroin seized and interrogated by Jiang Tian is already over a hundred tons.

Of that, about fifty tons were already in hand.

This is just the amount already in hand, the amount that has been traded and the amount that has been interrogated and confirmed. Jiang Tian alone created a miracle of over one hundred and fifty tons.

Of course, now that the confidentiality regulations have been lifted, especially for people in other departments, anyone with connections can easily hear about the terrifying aspects of Jiang Tian with a little inquiry.

Most importantly, and most authoritatively, even though Shang Mingyan possesses considerable authority in criminal investigations throughout Beijing, the country, and even the world, he's merely following in Jiang Tian's footsteps.

Then, Shang Mingyan's very presence indirectly confirms the veracity of these events.

Initially, they were elated that the case would be solved quickly when Shang Mingyan arrived, but they never expected the perpetrator's methods to be so horrific.

They left absolutely no trace.

This put immense pressure on the leaders. Even with their efforts to explain, defuse public opinion, and suppress information, the truth couldn't be hidden forever. Every day that dragged on, they endured unimaginable, terrifying pressure.

Crucially, over the days, the leads dwindled.

The difficulty of the investigation became extremely high.

Most importantly, they were currently at a loss.

This was the most terrifying aspect, because they might be stuck here, and the case could not be advanced at all.

The pressure on these leaders was mounting, from above and below, and now, Jiang Tian himself had arrived.

Putting aside everything else, although Jiang Tian is young, his abilities are terrifying!

His achievements are something no one else could boast about. Those within their own ranks know their own situation; the rumors circulating online are pure jokes to them.

Good heavens, they've even come up with such absurd nonsense as killing innocent people and claiming credit for them.

To try and kill innocent people and claim credit for them, and then create so many first-class merits, is simply pure fantasy.

"With Comrade Jiang Tian involved, the problem should be easily solved," a leader said, wiping his sweat and letting out a sigh of relief.

Lian Ying also nodded slightly.

It wasn't just the pressure of the unsolved case; the most crucial issue was the constant possibility of a second incident.

As long as the murderer remained at large,

what if they struck again? Yunnan University had already experienced the previous incident, and the higher-ups had already voiced their opinions. This latest incident would place immense pressure on them.

And if it happened again...

Good heavens.

They themselves were almost too ashamed to remain at Yunnan University.

Regardless of whether it was their fault, so many major incidents had occurred during their tenure, and the consequences...

ultimately, it would all be their responsibility.

"In short, Comrade Jiang's reputation is well-deserved. Let's go to the girls' dormitory and wait to see if there are any new developments in Comrade Jiang's investigation."

Lian Ying walked at the front, inwardly sighing deeply.

They hadn't relaxed their vigilance regarding the case, constantly monitoring the progress, but unfortunately, they still hadn't found any leads.

Of course, normally, they wouldn't be informed of the progress of a murder case like this.

Outsiders have no way of knowing the progress.

However, someone like Lian Ying, a top leader at Yunnan University, is already at an extremely high level in terms of rank and other aspects.

The rank of a president of a Double First-Class University is self-evident.

Shang Mingyan wouldn't hide anything at a time like this.

Moreover, even Lian Ying knew the gravity of the situation; only three or five key leaders knew the progress of the case.

They knew their own business best, and if this case wasn't solved soon, the consequences would be unimaginable.

This time, they might really be finished.

If Jiang Tian could save them again, they would be willing to kneel down and thank him.

"Hmm, what's the situation up ahead?"

"Looks like Comrade Jiang just arrived."

"Hmm, how can you talk like that? We arrived too quickly."

"Yes, yes, go and ask what's going on."



Chapter 662 Self-created Code: Mental Indecency


From afar, although the lights were off, the entrance was still brightly lit. Jiang Tian was currently blocked, with the dormitory supervisor watching him from behind and the freckled woman in front.

Shang Mingyan watched Jiang Tian the entire time, seemingly waiting for his decision.

Perhaps, the change a woman can cause once she falls in love is terrifying, and Shang Mingyan was a prime example. If someone familiar with Shang Mingyan witnessed this scene, their eyes would probably pop out of their sockets.

Four or five school leaders, mainly Lian Ying accompanied by two vice principals and two important group leaders, came together.

Why? Most importantly, they came to see Jiang Tian.

They were overjoyed to see him.

Especially Lian Ying, walking at the front, observing the people around Jiang Tian and their apparent conversation, assumed Jiang Tian was investigating a case.

So Lian Ying even unconsciously slowed her pace and smiled, saying, "Comrade Jiang definitely has his own unique skills in solving cases. He's probably just trying to understand the details now. I just don't know if he can help us solve this one."

"Principal, I think you're overthinking it. The cases Comrade Jiang solves are all huge; he often takes down entire criminal organizations. Such big cases are nothing compared to those he's cracked with a wave of his hand. Even the best-hidden murder case can't be as difficult as those criminal organizations that have been entrenched in Yunhai for decades," the vice-principal beside Lian Ying said with a smile, trying to reassure her.

Sure enough, upon hearing his words, Lian Ying's anxious expression instantly disappeared, and she unconsciously relaxed.

Because, that's very true!

What kind of cases has Jiang Tian solved before?

So many complex cases, such complicated problems, Jiang Tian has been able to solve them with ease, let alone a case like this.

Of course, they weren't complete laymen; they knew a thing or two about some things.

However, as Lian Ying, leading the way, approached, her voice unknowingly reached his ears. Crucially, the voice she heard seemed rather offensive.

"Heh, a wolf in sheep's clothing. What kind of school is this? Everyone here is a prodigy. And you, coming in at the middle of the night, what else could it be but ill intentions? What's more, you just stalked me! Now you think you can leave so easily? I've already reported it to the school leaders. You just wait." The freckled-faced woman spoke loudly, seemingly very self-satisfied.

But...

Jiang Tian frowned slightly.

To be honest, at this level, he wouldn't easily get angry. Mainly, he pondered the meaning behind the freckled-faced woman's words, which made Jiang Tian hesitate.

This woman is seriously ill.

But this is going too far with her unreasonable demands.

Jiang Tian's investigative mind was incredibly sharp, and he immediately sensed something was amiss.

However,

Jiang Tian didn't sense anything, but Lian Ying, who was approaching, suddenly exploded.

It was a top-down explosion.

A real explosion.

He thought he'd just overheard something incredible.

"That woman, what did she say? I must have misheard, yes, I must be hallucinating," Lian Ying said, walking ahead, thinking he'd misheard.

Otherwise, what gave that woman the audacity to say such things to Comrade Jiang? Wasn't she courting death?

Just then, an even clearer voice rang out. Not only did he hear it, but the leaders behind him also heard it.

These five people were arguably the highest-ranking members of Yun University.

Although they weren't young, they weren't deaf at all.

"Even if you're a policeman, I'll call the police. I refuse to believe I can't bring you to justice. Legally speaking, heh, mental molestation is also a crime. You see, I'm a voyeur. I'll not only send you to jail, I'll skin you alive," the freckled woman said smugly.

That's right.

Anyone who saw Jiang Tian's face would think the same thing.

First, he's so young.

He looked like a recent graduate starting his job.

Secondly, Jiang Tian didn't seem threatening at all. A little intimidation would surely make him back down; this tactic always worked for her.

"Do you have anything else to say?"

The freckled-faced woman believed she had Jiang Tian under her thumb.

She even wanted to see fear and dread in him, but unfortunately, Jiang Tian's expression was excessively calm.

This made the freckled-faced woman frown.

She wanted to threaten him further.

After all, the freckled-faced woman believed she had the upper hand. Legally speaking, it was he who came to the girls' dormitory.

And then she could insist that he had looked at her, that he had groped her.

To some extent,

For an average young college student, that might actually be intimidating.

But for Jiang Tian...

Good grief.

You're really talking nonsense.

"What do you think I am? You're a law student?

Scaring a naive young boy is one thing, but to use this on him?!"

Jiang Tian smiled, not angry. Mainly, he wasn't angry at all with someone like this.

Besides,

he didn't need to be angry.

Because, just as the freckled-faced woman finished speaking,

a voice suddenly rang out from afar, calm but filled with barely suppressed anger.

"What a 'stalking'! I didn't know the law had this aspect. When did our college create a new civil code?"

Lian Ying was truly surprised.

Completely surprised!

She thought she had misheard, but good heavens, she hadn't misheard. Someone at their school had actually created a civil code and even come up with a 'stalking' concept.

The key is, you can say that to others, but

you come to Jiang Tian and flaunt it!

"Who?"

The freckled-faced girl frowned, her face showing displeasure and anger, seemingly thinking it was some busybody.

But as she turned her head...

The dormitory supervisor, who had just been working with her to pressure Jiang Tian, ​​suddenly spoke up: "Principal, Principal, you're here! Please come and judge this! In the middle of the night, this man wants to enter the girls' dormitory. He claims to be a policeman, but we don't know if it's true or not. And why does he have to come to the girls' dormitory so late at night? Why doesn't he come during the day? Doesn't that mean he has dirty thoughts? Sure enough, he just groped a student with his eyes."



Chapter 663 The Fall of the First Generation in its Early Stages


Wow.

Jiang Tian chuckled, "That ugly face, how could I molest her?" ”

!! buzz

Jiang Tian's words instantly made the freckled-faced woman so furious she nearly lost her senses.

After all, the more a person lacks something, the easier it is to break down defenses somewhere.

That's how it is.

The freckled-faced woman snapped at Jiang Tian, sneering coldly and cursing, "Principal, look! This thug not only came to take advantage of me and harass me, he doesn't even show any sign of admitting fault. Principal, you should stand up for the students." ”

She ran up to Lian Ying.

His expression changed instantly, from being so aloof to Jiang Tian just moments ago to finally returning to normal.

But at this moment, Lian Ying's face was burning with rage.

Alright, alright.

Damn.

After finally helping solve the case, Comrade Jiang Tian came here to help solve the case, and now you actually blocked him halfway. What do you want to do?

Don't you know that even Shang Mingyan has become a tricky and clueless case in this world, besides Jiang Tian, no one else can pull it off?

If this case cannot be solved, a second or third offense will occur.

At that point, one of Yun University's people could be taken out by the killer at any moment.

Most importantly, by then, they would absolutely have no future left.

He was even unable to hold this position any longer.

So, this freckled-faced woman was practically trying to kill them.

The four school leaders following behind Lian Ying were instantly in shock.

Among them was a vice principal who seemed to have a fiery temper. More importantly, faced with this scene, he couldn't control himself. The key was that he was very muscular and well-built.

Of course, the most crucial thing was that his temper was the most hot-tempered among them.

So instinctively, in his rage, he completely lost his reason and slapped him directly.

He slapped the face of the completely unguarded freckled-faced woman.

!!

That slap was earth-shattering.

His freckled face was instantly deformed by the blow.

This time the strike was extremely ruthless.

It was clear there was no mercy. In fact, when Jiang Tian directly slapped the freckled-faced woman to the ground, he vaguely saw that she was bleeding a mouthful of blood, seemingly containing two teeth.

In other words, all the front teeth were knocked out.

Wow, you can imagine how terrifying that slap must be.

Meanwhile, the freckled-faced woman's mind was buzzing and exploding.

For a moment, even his mind went blank, he didn't feel any pain. Still, when he regained consciousness, a terrifying pain shot straight to his head.

On the mouth and on the face.

"Principal Hina, why are you hitting me?" The freckled-faced woman was completely stunned.

The reason she kept doing this for so long without any trouble was because the freckled-faced woman knew the status of her status, and she knew exactly what kind of people she could afford to offend and which she couldn't afford to offend.

Usually, more than ninety percent of the people come from simple families and young boys. Someone like that is practically under her thumb.

But for those kinds of people, it's not simple at all.

Freckle-faced women never dared to offend anyone proactively.

That's also why nothing has happened until now.

But now.

"You haven't answered my question yet, so answer my question first." Principal Hina squatted down and said expressionlessly.

"What is the problem?" The freckled-faced woman forgot the pain and stared blankly.

"Since when did the Civil Code mention 'witnessing treachery'? Wielding a broadsword in front of Lord Guan's door, even looking at treachery, making things up nonsense, and most importantly, you're actually obstructing official duties?"

"Do you know what Comrade Xiao Jiang is doing here? Do you think you deserve to be seen by others?"

Principal Hina spoke without mercy, mainly because Principal Hina's little temper was truly unbearable.

The key is.

Didn't Comrade Xiao Jiang say he planned to come to Yunnan University for Double Rest Law in the future? But now?

Principal Chu looked at Jiang Tian and quickly explained, "Comrade Xiao Jiang, don't misunderstand. People like us in our school are definitely exceptions, not common practice. Please don't overthink it—we will definitely treat them as a typical example." Principal Chu quickly spoke up to assure her.

"So, Comrade Xiao Jiang, give our Yunnan University a chance. I believe we can definitely give you a perfect outcome."
