Principal Hina kept explaining.

Jiang Tian wasn't angry at first; in fact, in this era, this kind of situation was truly an isolated case.

"Alright, Principal Hina, I'll go back and wait for your decision." Jiang Tian smiled faintly, truly not angry; it was not worth dealing with people like this.

He really had something to do tomorrow.

He had come here tonight to take a quick look. If there were any clues, Jiang Tian felt that if he could handle it quickly tonight, it wouldn't delay tomorrow.

But after coming here, all sorts of messy things happened again. Well, after returning, I'd ask someone I knew from Yun University to take a few days off, and wait for Jiang Tian to come back before dealing with it.

But Jiang Tian's attitude made them mistakenly think he was angry in front of these leaders.

Wow.

Now, even Shang Mingyan had no clue; the only hope lay with Jiang Tian. Not to mention anything else, if they couldn't solve the case for a day, they would endure terrifying pressure every day.

"Comrade Xiao Jiang, don't go, wait." Lian Ying hurriedly grabbed Jiang Tian.

!! buzz

The freckled-faced woman, who had been slapped directly to the ground, widened her eyes, feeling as if her mind was exploding.

What does that mean?

Why?

You have to know, the freckled-faced woman is now in her graduation internship state. It's just that she can still live on campus at this stage, saving a lot on accommodation fees, so she stayed until after her diploma was issued.

In other words, like Jiang Tian, he was still in the internship phase.

Of course, she wasn't a freshman. Every year, the leaders gave speeches, and it was these few leaders over and over, so she naturally knew them all. They were all the school's principals, vice principals, and other leaders.

And now she saw something.

What are these leaders doing?

That little cop—isn't he just a little cop?

Why you?

The freckled-faced woman felt a bit flustered inside.

"Which department are you in? What year are you in? What's your name?" Lian Ying didn't give Jiang Tian a chance to speak at all. He turned and directly found the freckled-faced woman, his face dark as he asked.

"Me."

The freckled-faced woman looked a bit panicked.

"Let me ask you one last time: what's your advisor's name and which department he's from?" Lian Ying asked coldly.

At first, the freckled-faced woman was a bit flustered, but soon she felt she was right. As long as she bit her lip, she would never be able to do anything to her. At worst, she would make a big scene—who would be afraid of whom—so she stood up and loudly said her name.

"Alright, from now on, our school has never had a student like you. Making mistakes in law enforcement and even creating your own code to convict others. Your status is too great. Our Yun University can't afford you, and I suppose you don't even care about our diplomas." Lian Ying said calmly.

!! buzz

And Jiang Tian smiled.

This must be the early fall of the First Generation.



Chapter 664 Hello everyone, I'm an intern who has been working for two and a half years.


Then, Jiang Tian watched as the freckled-faced girl's haughty swan-like expression gradually froze, finally settling into a blank, incredulous look, as if she were dreaming. Jiang Tian said nothing; this was reaping what she sowed.

Besides, this was also why Jiang Tian hadn't made a move.

At this moment.

It's one thing for students to be ignorant, but for a dormitory supervisor to interfere so much.

Even if Jiang Tian wasn't someone important, a regular intern was here to solve a case.

And you think you can stop him?

That's right, the freckled-faced woman was Xu Yun, her real name was Xu Yun, a law student.

Now she was even paler.

Why?

Xu Yun was utterly baffled.

On the way here, something unexpected happened. Professor Shang's car was planted with a time bomb, and it exploded on the way. Since Professor Shang has been visiting Yunnan University quite often lately, I suspect the bomb was planted while the car was parked here."

Jiang Tian's tone was calm and unhurried, but his words instantly stunned the leaders present.

Cold sweat poured down his face.

Good heavens.

Shang Mingyan's car was secretly planted with a bomb?

Even he was starting to feel overwhelmed.

Unable to contain her emotions, Lian Ying looked at Shang Mingyan.

"That's good, that's good." Lian Ying breathed a sigh of relief; at least no one was injured.

Otherwise, what if something had happened to the two of them in the bus? What if the investigation revealed that the bomb was planted at their university?

Lian Ying's face turned green at the thought.

Before Lian Ying and the other leaders could breathe a sigh of relief, Shang Mingyan's voice rang out, "That means Comrade Jiang was blown about ten meters away."

Thump! !!

The leaders' hearts, which had just settled, instantly jumped into their throats.

From heaven to hell.

That's the gist of it.

Moreover, Shang Mingyan pointed to Jiang Tian's back. Sure enough, they hadn't noticed it in the dim light, but now they could see that Jiang Tian's clothes were tattered.

Especially after Lian Ying took a few steps, sure enough, Jiang Tian's back was completely blackened.

Blown more than ten meters?

This...

The officials in Beijing are urging him to leave tomorrow, and Comrade Jiang insisted on coming to check on him. Nobody expected something like this to happen," Shang Mingyan explained from the side.

Lian Ying looked at the injuries on Jiang Tian's back; it wasn't fake.

Because the marks from the explosion were definitely not fake. If the power wasn't strong, how could his clothes be torn to shreds like that?

Besides, it couldn't be anyone else.

But...

Jiang Tian.

His past achievements all say that Jiang Tian's very existence is a legend.

Most importantly...

Shang Mingyan said it himself, so it couldn't possibly be fake.

"Ten meters or so" might be an exaggeration for others, but when Shang Mingyan said it, it was absolutely true. She believed in logic more than anyone else and didn't speak lightly; one was one, and two was two.

But this only fueled Lian Ying's anger.

Look at Comrade Jiang Tian!

Because of what happened at their school, these two fathers almost died on the road, and now that he's come all this way, you bastards are bullying him!

You bastards, look in the mirror!

Lian Ying glanced at Xu Yun out of the corner of her eye. This woman's face was full of mocking sneers, though she seemed to hold back, considering the presence of many powerful figures, but the sarcasm was palpable.

She even seemed to disregard Shang Mingyan's words.

But...

You think you're worthy?

Originally, she hadn't intended to go all the way, but now, since the other party had acted this way again and again, Lian Ying could only sigh and accept it.

"Isn't he an intern?" Xu Yun pointed at Jiang Tian.



Chapter 665 The consequences are getting more and more terrifying; even Ying is bombed.


That's right, even now, this freckled-faced woman still treats Jiang Tian as an intern.

Jiang Tian's age, in fact, seems younger than hers.

At this age, he'd probably just graduated this year, and his demeanor doesn't suggest he's been out of school for long; he looks like an average college student.

Sure enough.

After saying that,

Xu Yun immediately burst out laughing.

Level Two Police Inspector.

That's right.

Ordinary people probably don't know what a police rank is, how many levels there are.

Or how much prestige it has, how much influence it carries.

What are the scope of management and responsibilities?

Ordinary people might never understand in their entire lives, but that definitely doesn't include Xu Yun, who studied law.

Level Two Police Inspector, what does that mean?

In an ordinary prefecture-level city, even becoming a deputy bureau chief, or even with connections leading to early promotion, might only get you a second- or first-level police inspector.

After all, becoming a police inspector isn't easy.

Sure enough, Xu Yun exploded.

Actually, Xu Yun's actions today had a reason, but Jiang Tian didn't care. He didn't plan to have any future dealings with her, and it wasn't worth arguing with someone like her.

Including what she was trying to do.

In fact, upon closer examination, anyone who acts outside the expected path definitely has their own purpose. And what does a woman about to graduate want to manipulate a trainee police officer for?

It's to use the moral high ground to blackmail others.

To blackmail them in various ways to achieve her own goals.

So why would a woman about to graduate cause trouble at this time? What is her purpose?

Jiang Tian could roughly guess.

Xu Yun was indeed in a similar situation; she was about to leave, but who would want to leave if they could stay on campus?

Unfortunately, given her abilities, it was impossible for her to pass the entrance exam again. By chance, after hearing that other schools had schemes to secure admission through threats, Xu Yun naturally harbored wicked thoughts.

Her mind was already preoccupied with what to do.

Just when she had no clue what to do, a murder occurred. This time, Xu Yun realized it was her last chance.

Just then, Jiang Tian appeared.

She keenly sensed this was an opportunity, a chance to use threats to get herself sexually assaulted at this critical moment.

Yes.

That's how it happened.

Although she didn't know what to do next, driven by instinct—the instinct to stay on campus—she did it anyway.

The consequences?

She looked like a helpless intern, someone she'd seen plenty of—a naive young man she could easily manipulate.

But as it turned out, it was wrong, terribly wrong.

Second-Class Inspector?

What does that even mean?

It takes a year to go from intern to full-fledged Third-Class Superintendent before being officially commissioned.

And after becoming a Third-Class Superintendent, there's a minimum three-year promotion standard.

However, this doesn't mean you're 100% guaranteed a promotion in three years. Of the ten people in this batch, only two might get promoted after three years. What about the remaining eight? They'll have to wait, wait for the next three years.

The key is, another batch of people will be promoted after that.

It's a one-person-one-position system; three years is just the beginning.

In reality, reaching the rank of First Superintendent is already a 90% finish line.

And a true Police Inspector, that level is already considered mid-to-high-level. At least within the police system, almost none are under forty years old, and all have made outstanding contributions.

Over forty, and with a proven track record of merit.

This completely surpasses the average person's career ceiling.

The crucial point is, Jiang Tian looks only in his early twenties; Xu Yun even feels he's younger than her.

How is this possible?

Unfortunately.

No matter how shocked Xu Yun is, no one is watching her now, or rather, her fate is already sealed.

And now.

All eyes were on Jiang Tian, ​​who hadn't uttered a word since the beginning.

This impressed those secretly watching the spectacle; his composure in the face of adversity was astounding. Truly remarkable for someone so young, he had already achieved such a feat.

Especially the students secretly watching, looking at Jiang Tian and then at themselves.

Fine.

They were called the pride of heaven, but compared to Jiang Tian, ​​they didn't even seem qualified to stand before him.

"You said you're going to the capital to see an official tomorrow?"

Lian Ying keenly heard the information in Shang Mingyan's words and accurately grasped the key point in Jiang Tian's statement.

The bomb had exploded.

It had happened.

This made Lian Ying even more anxious, especially after hearing that Jiang Tian was leaving Yunhai for the capital and didn't know when he would return.

The crucial point was that Lian Ying's mind was filled with the thought that the vicious murderer might be on the Yun University campus right now.

In other words...

At any moment, the murderer could commit a horrific crime.

The key is, no one knows what heinous things the murderer might do. If a bomb can destroy a famous building, what about planting a bomb in a classroom?

After all, the heinous things that have already happened, anything is possible.

No.

He can't leave.

Jiang Tian can't leave.

Lian Ying was terrified.

If something like that happened, Lian Ying didn't even want to think about the consequences for herself. Of course, her own responsibility was one thing, but the crucial question was how many innocent people would suffer.

"Comrade Jiang, our Yun University solemnly implores you to work overtime tonight and save Yun University."

Lian Ying grabbed Jiang Tian's hand and wouldn't let go.

At this moment, Lian Ying felt like killing the dormitory supervisor and Xu Yun.

But some things were clear to everyone.

At this point, it's crucial to prioritize. These two individuals could be dealt with strictly at any time.

They could even be arrested for obstructing official business.

However, right now, solving the case is the most important thing.

“This…” Jiang Tian looked at the girls’ dormitory and said, “Forget it. What if someone jumps out and says I took advantage of them? I, Jiang Tian, ​​have my pride too. Let’s come back during the day when no one’s in the dorm.”

Jiang Tian wasn’t one to fawn over those who wouldn’t listen.

The next murderer’s target might just be this woman.

“No, we can’t leave. Let’s see who dares.”



Chapter 666 After all, it's all for the benefit of everyone.


Of course, from a behavioral psychology perspective, this killer is extremely vicious, far beyond the ordinary.

Jiang Tian had a premonition.

Based on the information Shang Mingyan provided, this killer is highly likely to commit something serious later. The first thing he did was leave a provocative message: "I'm back."

This either indicates a grudge against someone, or there might be a similar case in the past; this isn't the killer's first offense.

But clearly, this wasn't random. The killer planted a bomb in Shang Mingyan's car. While it's still uncertain whether the bomb belongs to this killer, based on the current situation, the probability is high.

If the killer is deliberately so arrogant, then it's clearly driven by a strong purpose. Criminologically speaking, this isn't a crime of passion or a random act; premeditated crimes are generally the most terrifying, with the most horrific consequences.

Jiang Tian originally wanted to work overtime.

"Comrade Jiang, although I know your business tomorrow is very important, and logically we shouldn't say much, while Beijing is important, this place is equally important. So many students here, so many students, are in constant danger of losing their lives. Right now, besides you, I really don't know how to solve this case."

Lian Ying, seemingly afraid of causing a misunderstanding, quickly added, "Of course, I'm not saying Professor Shang is incompetent, I'm just saying that if we can solve this case faster and better, and catch the murderer, we can all feel at ease. As for their situation, Comrade Jiang, don't worry, there will be no place for them at Yun University anymore. In fact, I even propose arresting them for obstructing official business; this is a proposal from our university."

Lian Ying emphasized repeatedly.

The consequences were unmanageable, unbearable.

If Jiang Tian leaves, solving the case will be a distant prospect.

And, most importantly...

What will happen next is completely unknown.

What if the killer planned even more horrific and terrifying crimes afterward? The entire leadership of Yun University, from top to bottom, would be utterly finished.

Of course, they weren't afraid of being finished, but what about the innocent students involved? To put it bluntly, if a bomb were planted in a classroom and exploded during a lecture, Yun University would be destroyed.

What? You say that's impossible? Good heavens, even Shang Mingyan's car was secretly planted with a bomb.

Just as Jiang Tian was about to say something, the phone rang. Jiang Tian glanced at it and saw it was Zhao Wei. Without replying to Lian Ying, he calmly took out his phone and answered the call.

"Boss, we towed the car back. The forensic department just did a preliminary examination. The bomb was probably hidden under the driver's seat base. Judging from the explosion marks, it was TNT, detonated by a timed detonator," Zhao Wei said.

"Is it possible for the killer to plant a bomb inside the city police station?" Jiang Tian asked.

In reality, there aren't that many coincidences in the world. Jiang Tian had already sensed something was wrong while he was in the car.

Even with the bomb outside the car, Jiang Tian could sense it; he even had a premonition of danger. Moreover, the smell of TNT inside the driver's seat was immediately noticeable the moment he got in.

"Impossible. The city police station has surveillance cameras, and there are dedicated personnel guarding the parking spaces. There's no way anyone could have opened the car door," Zhao Wei said without hesitation.

"First, check the surveillance footage of the car at the city police station recently, see if any suspicious individuals have been in contact with it," Jiang Tian instructed preemptively.

That would be easier to rule out.

Besides, bombs are usually planted on a timed 24-hour period.

This car's recent driving route has been between the school and the city police station. If the city police can rule it out, then we can basically confirm that it's linked to the perpetrator here and handle the case together.

"Boss, are you alright?" Zhao Wei asked worriedly.

"Don't worry, I'm fine. You can continue the investigation," Jiang Tian said, hanging up the phone.

His gaze then turned to Lian Ying, saying, "Principal Lian, since you've spoken, I won't give you any special treatment. Ultimately, we're all serving the people, and I understand your feelings. I truly cannot ignore the students' lives."

Jiang Tian was now increasingly certain that the murderer was planning something much bigger.

Upon hearing Jiang Tian's words, Lian Ying almost wanted to kneel before this living ancestor.

"Good, good, good."

Lian Ying smiled broadly, "Comrade Jiang, thank you, thank you so much! You've saved Yun University twice already!"

"Let me make this clear first, I can't guarantee a solution. And my time is limited to tonight. Regardless of any leads, I'll be leaving Yunhai tomorrow."

"Comrade Jiang, no matter how modest you are, I don't know about others, but if you, Comrade Jiang, can't solve this, our school will have no choice but to temporarily suspend classes." Lian Ying sighed.

If Jiang Tian couldn't solve it, and Shang Mingyan had no leads, Lian Ying didn't know who else in the country could.

There probably weren't any.

"Stop watching, lights out. Don't go watching the commotion, go back to sleep. And remember, keep everything that happened today a secret. Don't spread it outside, or you'll face the highest level of punishment. The school is our home; we all need to maintain it and not destroy it." At this moment, Principal Chu walked inside, addressing the figures peeking from the two open dormitory doors with an annoyed tone.

"It's nothing, let's go."

Jiang Tian turned and left, from beginning to end.

He didn't even glance at the dormitory supervisor or Xu Yun.

No, or rather, he'd been judging them from the start.

Generally speaking, none of them were heinous criminals, but they did have some red aura about them, though these were mostly moral issues, nothing more.

Jiang Tian was even less inclined to pay them any attention.

What kind of level is this?

Have you ever seen a bureau-level official go to a village and start a tantrum with the women?

The dormitory supervisor and Xu Yun are worlds apart from Jiang Tian; they're practically from different worlds. Engaging with them would be beneath him.

However,

Although Jiang Tian didn't speak, their fate was definitely not going to be good.

Lian Ying would handle things perfectly.

"From the moment it happened, our school took it very seriously, immediately sealing off the scene. The scene was well-preserved. Aside from the body being taken away by your city police for an autopsy, everything in the dormitory is exactly as it was." Lian Ying was afraid Jiang Tian would escape, so she stayed close to him.



Chapter 667 My Eyes Are a Ruler


"Hmm." Jiang Tian nodded without saying a word.

His composure, like a mountain collapsing before him, calmed even the somewhat flustered Lian Ying, who felt a surge of calm upon seeing his expression.

"Putting that aside for now, based on the intelligence and information I've gathered, this killer is planning something big. Planting a bomb in Professor Shang's car and then killing someone on campus—a simple murder wouldn't involve planting a bomb to target Professor Shang. Therefore, this killer has an important objective yet to be achieved. This is probably just an appetizer. That's why I'm here today."

Jiang Tian sighed. He had initially thought it was an isolated murder, but now it seemed more complex.

"The deceased was twenty-one years old, a junior in college. The time of death was approximately two days ago at 3 PM, during class time."

Shang Mingyan relayed the details and information to Jiang Tian.

After Jiang Tian and the others left,

only the dormitory supervisor and Xu Yun remained at the scene.

At this point, both of them were speechless.

They had initially assumed Jiang Tian was an intern. Internships, it's safe to say, are among the weakest in society.

Their status is even lower than when they were in school.

Because this is the lowest level in the workplace, everyone in the department can boss you around.

Your seniority is also the lowest.

You know the least.

So, those who know about interns know what's what.

Xu Yun understood this all too well.

He had initially thought that Jiang Tian was like himself, someone he could easily manipulate. He figured that if he made the first move, even Shang Mingyan wouldn't say anything.

After all, they should be afraid of public opinion. If he could maintain a foothold, he could use it to threaten the school.

What? You say this has nothing to do with the school?

What a joke! He could use this as a pretext to shut down the school and release the details of the case online.

He thought he could easily manipulate them, but now…

"A second-level police inspector? What's that?" The dormitory supervisor was still completely bewildered.

Just moments ago, she had been so self-righteous, but after Lian Ying arrived and slapped Xu Yun across the face, the previously composed supervisor was instantly terrified.

Initially, she had only intended to use moral blackmail, to exert some pressure, but she hadn't expected that the person she was casually harassing would be a second-level police inspector.

A second-level police inspector and an intern?

What's the difference between the two?

The dormitory supervisor was in a state of confusion; after all, this was a very prestigious profession.

Ordinary people wouldn't even have the chance to get into it.

"A second-level police inspector, huh? That's the level of a police chief in a prefecture-level city. What level do you think that is? Someone you can't afford to offend." Xu Yun stood up, her face dark with anger as she looked at the dormitory supervisor.

She felt she had been betrayed by this dormitory supervisor.

Her life had been ruined.

If it weren't for this dormitory supervisor, she wouldn't have ended up like this.

It was the dormitory supervisor's words that misled her.

The more Xu Yun thought about it, the more bleak her future felt, and the more desolate her life seemed.

After so many years of hard work, her student status was about to be lost.

How could this be salvaged?

They didn't know, they had absolutely no idea.

Just then, Jiang Tian's voice rang out from the hallway: "By the way, get a few guys over here. There are two people obstructing official business at Yun University. Arrest them and lock them up for a while."

Besides, Jiang Tian was known for his good temper.

That just meant he disdained arguing, but it didn't mean he was the type to hold a grudge—that was a joke.

If it meant making things difficult for them, Jiang Tian was happy to oblige.

"Okay."

Zhao Wei hung up the phone.

The dorm supervisor and Xu Yun, upon hearing this, felt like their world had collapsed.

Especially hearing Jiang Tian's calm words, as if he had done something insignificant, but for them, it was like the sky had fallen.

They were dizzy.

Completely dizzy.

Their lives were plunged into darkness.

The key issue isn't the dormitory supervisor; the important thing is that Xu Yun is finished. She was almost graduating, and now she's been arrested.

What about work? She won't even have a diploma. In other words, over ten years of hard work studying is completely over. Entering society, she'll be nothing more than a high school graduate.

And her internship company, after hearing the news of her arrest, will probably fire her immediately.

In other words, her life is completely over.

As the sound faded into the distance, Xu Yun's face turned ashen.

Of course, this was just a minor incident for Jiang Tian. The world is never short of people who use their meager power to make things as difficult as possible for others.

Different social classes naturally have different perspectives.

Soon, Jiang Tian arrived at the scene of the crime, but staring at the dormitory room number for two seconds.

"What's wrong?" Lian Ying asked.

"Is anyone living in this dormitory?" Jiang Tian pondered for a moment.

“Our undergraduate dorms at Yunnan University are all six-person rooms. The other five students have already moved to other rooms, and there’s no one in the dorm right now,” Lian Ying quickly said.

Jiang Tian’s eyes narrowed slightly.

“According to the people in the dorm, they had all gone to class when the incident occurred. When they called the deceased, she was still sleeping, so they left first. When they returned, they found the door locked from the inside. After calling the dorm supervisor to open the door, they found the deceased was already dead.”

“Furthermore, there were signs of assault on the deceased’s body. Our investigation yielded no useful clues. The windows were locked from the inside. During this time, we questioned the surrounding students and conducted a series of checks, but we found no trace of the murderer.”

As Shang Mingyan spoke, Jiang Tian went to the deceased’s bed, where she had been found.

The key point is…

The whole case seems incredibly bizarre.

In this environment, after being raped and murdered, there wasn’t a single clue.

The murderer’s abilities are terrifying.

"Are you sure it was that time of death?" Jiang Tian asked.

Shang Mingyan understood Jiang Tian's meaning: "The autopsy results confirm that the time of death was exactly that time; there's no way it could be wrong."

"Interesting," Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

It had been a long time since he'd encountered such a challenging case.

Moreover, although Jiang Tian already possessed the golden tag of legendary criminal investigator, this was the first time he had encountered such a high-IQ crime investigation case.

It was definitely different from those brutal criminal gangs.

Jiang Tian then sharply scanned the scene, especially when he reached the window. He put on gloves and opened the window.

One thing was certain: the murderer had definitely been in the room, and there would definitely be traces.

And even in the darkness, Jiang Tian could say one thing:

"My eyes are my ruler."

"That footprint appeared about three days ago. Professor Shang, please have the forensic department come and examine it." Jiang Tian pointed to a small area on the outdoor air conditioner unit and then shone a UV light on it.

Shang Mingyan looked carefully for a moment, somewhat confused: "A footprint here?"

"Look closely, there are friction marks. These marks are no more than three days old," Jiang Tian asserted.

"Huh?" Shang Mingyan felt like he was blind.

Why couldn't she find anything even under the strong ultraviolet light?

After a pause, Jiang Tian said, "After all, my eyes are my ruler."



Chapter 668 Emerging from the Surface, Demonstrating Strength


In this regard, Jiang Tian could confidently say, "My eyes are my ruler."

His extraordinary physique undoubtedly granted Jiang Tian a terrifying boost to his five senses.

Combined with the legendary criminal investigation credentials, in terms of solving cases, although this was Jiang Tian's first experience with such high-IQ crimes, frankly, he felt it wasn't difficult.

In fact, Jiang Tian's senses had already sketched out a reconstruction of the crime in his mind.

Touching the air conditioner unit outside.

Jiang Tian peered down, shining a UV light on it. Finally, after meticulous observation, he saw it on the glass, on the glass outside the window.

"Here, the murderer grabbed here."

"Really?" Shang Mingyan was even more confused.

But as he said before, "My eyes are my ruler."

"Now we can confirm one thing: the murderer entered from outside. Also, you said the victim died yesterday morning, but according to the traces at the scene, the date left was probably three days ago. There's a discrepancy. Starting from this point, we can likely find some clues."

Jiang Tian followed the traces to the back of the dormitory building, carefully searching with a UV light. Behind the building was a dense forest, the same path Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan had just taken.

It wasn't particularly secluded.

However, very few people came to the back of the dormitory building. After searching for a while, Jiang Tian finally found a complete but very faint footprint under a leaf.

"Found it," Jiang Tian said, pointing to the footprint.

It has to be said.

The level of scientific investigation and crime-solving skills in this era are completely different from those twenty years later.

In this era, solving crimes, especially high-IQ crimes, truly relies seven parts luck and three parts skill.

The accumulated case clearance rate over the years, even the unpublished figures, is already a terrifying number.

Moreover, only a very small fraction of cases are ever made known to the outside world.

In reality, the vast majority remains completely unknown.

The reason for all this is the extremely outdated methods of investigation, especially in remote and underdeveloped areas, where wrongful convictions are highly likely.

Of course, on a large scale, any of these things are possible; these are unpredictable events.

Overall...

After studying the area, Jiang Tian...

These footprints, in this era, could easily have been missed, mainly because of the distance. Furthermore, it can't be definitively said that these footprints belong to the murderer; this isn't a deserted place, and other people's footprints are entirely possible.

However, it's different for Jiang Tian. He already has a complete mental map of the footprint's shape and how long it took for the footprints to form.

Jiang Tian was able to pinpoint the time of the murderer's appearance—three days prior—by analyzing the diagrams and following clues, and then search for traces left from three days ago.

Of course, this kind of investigative skill is unique to Jiang Tian; in other words, the meticulousness of his observations and the subtle clues he can spot during searches are beyond the capabilities of ordinary people.

Twenty or three decades from now, when investigative techniques have advanced, perhaps they could find it.

But unfortunately, even if a god were to appear a decade or two later, these clues would be gone. Therefore, many cases simply remain unsolved.

Because the investigative methods simply no longer exist.

Furthermore, with the passage of time, many traces and clues left at the scene have disappeared. In this situation, the difficulty of solving the case has skyrocketed, making it virtually impossible.

Therefore, Jiang Tian's investigative methods, to some extent, are unreplicable.

Shang Mingyan, who was beside Jiang Tian, ​​was completely bewildered.

No.

What I mean is...

You see a footprint, or rather, Shang Mingyan, under the ultraviolet light, could only vaguely see a tiny trace, not even a clear footprint.

Shang Mingyan was quite certain that even if she saw the mark, she couldn't be sure if it was a footprint. Besides, people often walked around here, and there were even people doing *that* in the middle of the night. Although there weren't many people coming and going.

But there are definitely not a small number.

Who can definitively say these footprints belong to the murderer?

If this person wasn't Jiang Tian, ​​Shang Mingyan would almost have thought he'd been fooled.

The most outrageous thing is that they could even recognize footprints from three days ago, based on such faint traces?

"Are you sure?" Shang Mingyan asked, bewildered.

"Yes, look at the soil left behind. It's already solidified and deformed. And this bit of dust is clearly different from the surrounding soil. Furthermore, if you look closely, you can see footprints appearing intermittently. Also, it seems someone wore shoe covers or something under this tree, and those marks disappeared."

Jiang Tian stated confidently.

"Is that so?"

Shang Mingyan's heart skipped a beat. Sure enough, following Jiang Tian's deductions, and progressing further, Shang Mingyan's highly intelligent mind began a whirlwind of thought.

This clue is enough.

Enough.

Completely enough.

The key is that Shang Mingyan was deeply shaken, even looking at Jiang Tian with a hint of admiration.

Is this even human?

Nationwide, Shang Mingyan could say with certainty that while there might be some who surpassed him in crime-solving skills, there were definitely no more than one or two.

But even so, the crucial point was that he hadn't found anything, or rather, all the professional criminal investigators in the country had searched this place for two days and ultimately found no clues.

Jiang Tian had been here for less than ten minutes.

Good heavens, what does this mean?

Found a clue?

He'd been here for ten minutes and hadn't even found all the clues?

He was stunned.

"Anything else? Anything else you've found?" Shang Mingyan asked excitedly.

In the past, Shang Mingyan knew Jiang Tian's criminal investigation skills; he had demonstrated them once before, and they were outrageous and almost superhuman.

But that was only once.

Now?

That time wasn't nearly as visceral as this.

This time, the feeling was absolutely horrifying for Shang Mingyan; he was internally screaming, "Is this even human?"

"Let me see."

Jiang Tian rolled his eyes at Shang Mingyan's anxious expression. Good grief, all those experts have been searching for days without finding a clue, and I've only been here a short while, yet I've already brought you the murderer.

"Ahem, sorry, I got too excited." Shang Mingyan said, his face slightly flushed.

"What's wrong?"

However, Shang Mingyan looked up and saw Jiang Tian staring blankly.

Thinking that Jiang Tian had discovered something again, Shang Mingyan's heart was pounding with excitement.

"You keep making discoveries?" Shang Mingyan's voice was somewhat excited.

Good heavens.

This is absolutely amazing.



Chapter 669 A Process Beyond Everyone's Imagination


This is already a clue, as long as the investigation follows this precise timeline and the direction is correct.

It's safe to say there's absolutely no problem; at least there will be some discoveries later, and who knows, it might even lead to clues about the murderer.

This can be considered a clue.

It can also be considered a major discovery.

"Professor Shang, why do you think criminals usually like to return to the scene after committing a crime?" Jiang Tian asked, stroking his chin.

"It's a common psychological tactic of criminals. Normally, they want to know the police's understanding of the situation, the progress of the case, and their own standing," Shang Mingyan said.

"But what about this murder case?" Jiang Tian asked, considering the bombing case; clearly, it wasn't this conventional approach.

"In this case, the murderer clearly had a provocative mentality. Seeing us police officers running around like headless flies, he viewed us with mockery and contempt, or rather, it was a deliberate act of provocation, showing utter contempt for us," Shang Mingyan stated directly.

"Perhaps there's something else going on, a vengeful mentality we don't know about, though we don't know why they're retaliating," Shang Mingyan added.

Perfect.

Jiang Tian nodded slightly.

"That's right. If the bombings were committed by the same person, it's obvious they're at the school, hiding somewhere we don't know, plotting something. You mean, they're still here?" Shang Mingyan suddenly asked excitedly.

This narrowed the scope of the investigation.

Of course, this was mainly due to Shang Mingyan's initial thought.

Without any leads, the case made no progress.

Now, with leads, everything seemed too simple.

"Yes," Jiang Tian nodded.

"What?"

Their discussion didn't shy away from the school leaders, and even Lian Ying was shocked, her face turning green.

The fact that the perpetrator was still at the school meant that they could take further action at any time, or even escalate their harmful behavior, potentially harming them at any moment.

What if, in a certain classroom...

Or perhaps other harmful acts.

“Comrade Jiang, our Yunnan University is counting on you!” Lian Ying excitedly grabbed Jiang Tian’s hand.

If the murderer hadn’t been caught, the consequences would have been unimaginable; the more Lian Ying thought about it, the more frightened she became.

It was too dangerous.

“Sigh, the main problem is the extremely close connections with overseas entities. Many people willing to take the risk possess weapons of mass destruction, which can easily cause enormous social harm,” Jiang Tian sighed.

Whether it’s heroin or this case, weapons and heroin are two of the most common criminal activities.

And the harmful nature of both is almost identical.

In fact, smuggled weapons are often more dangerous than heroin. Some weapons of mass destruction, even a slight disturbance in a busy urban area, could cause an explosion.

“Jiang Tian, ​​I’ll immediately have people conduct a large-scale search of the campus,” Shang Mingyan said.

A previous check had already been conducted, but nothing was found.

This time...

Perhaps a completely different outcome is possible.

"No need." Jiang Tian raised his hand, indicating it wasn't necessary.

There was absolutely no need.

"There are about two thousand teachers and students at the school right now. Checking them shouldn't be too difficult." Lian Ying, thinking Jiang Tian was having trouble, quickly assured him the school was fine.

"Wait, Jiang Tian, ​​what do you mean?" Shang Mingyan's heart was racing.

Jiang Tian looked in one direction.

Shang Mingyan's heart pounded even faster.

"I just didn't expect the killer's audacity to be so much greater than I imagined."

Jiang Tian's gaze fell on the playground.

A small path led from the woods behind the teaching building, offering a direct view of the playground.

Speaking of which,

Although it was late,

there were still people on the playground.

After all, although such a major incident had occurred at Yunnan University, the university's capacity to withstand it was limited. Moreover, the case itself had already caused quite a stir; deliberately imposing a lockdown at this time could easily backfire.

Therefore, it was best to let things take their course.

So, although it was quite late, couples were still strolling along the streets, and some students were out for a walk after dinner or simply bumping into each other.

In short, this is what university life is like.

This was the kind of life Jiang Tian had once lacked, so his gaze held a hint of melancholy.

“What’s over there?” Shang Mingyan followed Jiang Tian’s gaze.

However, even with Shang Mingyan’s limited vision, he could only see figures strolling or exercising on the sports field.

“The murderer is over there,” Jiang Tian said with a slight smile.

Somewhat surprised.

Mainly because the distance was quite far; Jiang Tian hadn’t approached that area before, and his scanning radar’s diameter was only a kilometer from the center point.

Jiang Tian had been quite a distance from the killer earlier, but after arriving here and going downstairs, he accidentally spotted a crimson light.

As for other minor crimes and offenses, Jiang Tian would deal with the truly heinous ones, but he could immediately spot petty theft and other unethical acts.

Jiang Tian had just tested his deductive reasoning skills, and unexpectedly, he turned around and saw the killer.

Of course, he wasn't certain it was the killer.

But, at this time, and still at school,

he could basically make a preliminary determination.

That's why Jiang Tian was astonished by the killer's audacity.

"Someone who has been operating within the school for a long time and can silently plant bombs in cars is probably someone from within the school. Since the first investigation failed, another attempt wouldn't make a difference. However, I hadn't considered this possibility before."

Jiang Tian sighed.

The main issue is that, according to normal criminal investigation thinking, it's difficult to initially consider someone so vicious as to use a bomb, making it hard to believe they're from within the school.

"You're saying the killer is over there?" Shang Mingyan was completely bewildered.

He didn't even understand what had happened.

He could understand it after the explanation, but now he simply couldn't.

He was deducing things here, and suddenly you're saying the killer appeared a kilometer away?

“Comrade Jiang, we’re in a hurry, but… but there’s no need for this…” Lian Ying hurriedly grabbed Jiang Tian.

Good heavens.

With a single gesture, the murderer appeared.

This…

How do you look at it?

This seems like Jiang Tian is just playing games with them.

And in this life-or-death situation, this…

“I’ll call the city police,” Shang Mingyan said.

“No need. The murderer is alone; he can’t cause any trouble.” Jiang Tian shook his head and walked directly towards the playground.

At that moment, on the sports platform facing the path, a young couple was holding binoculars.



Chapter 670 The purest friendship during student days


This couple, placed on the sports field, didn't stand out at all. Although Yun University had recently experienced a lot, it had little impact on the students; they continued attending school and sleeping as usual.

It had no effect on their lives. At most, they might pay slightly more attention to the school's events, but as time passed, this impact was negligible.

The main reason was that too many incidents had occurred at Yun University recently. Several students were caught using heroin on campus not long ago, and the impact of such matters was enormous.

Furthermore, this murder case occurred not long after, and its nature was particularly heinous, especially since the murderer left provocative messages, which prompted Shang Mingyan to personally intervene.

Of course, given the level of technology at this time, even DNA testing was incredibly difficult, sometimes requiring travel to the capital for testing.

The technology wasn't even widely available yet.

Therefore, this couple on the sports field wasn't particularly special. If anything, they were only noticeably good-looking.

The woman was beautiful.

Very beautiful, and she seemed very quiet and refined.

The man, too, possessed the elegance and quietness of a student, sitting there like a dashing, handsome young man.

With such high looks, he was probably the only one attracting attention among the many student couples.

After all, many couples like to take a walk at this time.

Even after lights out, as long as the dormitory doors aren't locked, or if they're allowed to talk, the rules are generally quite lenient in this regard.

“What are you looking at?”

The girl sat on the small steps, her hands resting on her knees, her demeanor gentle and quiet. She looked at her boyfriend holding binoculars, a hint of curiosity on her face.

“Looking at the stars,” the boy lied blatantly.

“I clearly saw you staring straight at them.”

The girl pouted, glaring angrily at her boyfriend.

She seemed to feel deceived.

"Just kidding, I saw a couple doing something rather shady in the grove. Want to see?" The boy grinned mischievously, holding binoculars.

"You're so annoying!"

The girl's face flushed bright red, glaring angrily at the man. "Chu Mo, are you asking for it again?"

"Spare me, I was just kidding, Si Qinghan, stop!" Chu Mo's boyfriend quickly begged for mercy.

"Fine, I'll forgive you this time. But if you do it again, I'll hit you." Si Qinghan snorted, a smile unconsciously playing on her lips.

At this moment, Si Qinghan suddenly felt that life was actually quite beautiful.

Unless...

If the school didn't cause so much trouble, it would only make her appreciate the beauty of her life even more.

At least...

Although the two were developing into a couple, and their relationship was gradually stabilizing, they were getting to know each other better, and after graduation, they would support each other.

Si Qinghan contemplated their future together, feeling an increasing sense of wonder at how wonderful their future life would be.

Especially since they were both from Beijing, yet both chose to attend university here. Si Qinghan simply thought the scenery was beautiful, allowing for better travel during her university years.

As for Chu Mo, his reasoning seemed similar, but Si Qinghan wasn't entirely convinced.

Of course, none of that mattered; what mattered was fate.

But...

“I love you, Qinghan. Be my girlfriend,” Chu Mo suddenly said.

It was so sudden.

The suddenness left Si Qinghan completely stunned. Although they had been dating, they hadn't yet confessed their feelings.

“W-w-w-w, why so suddenly?” Si Qinghan was somewhat bewildered.

“Hahaha, just kidding,” Chu Mo joked.

At the same time, as he spoke, Chu Mo's gaze drifted to three people not far from the sports field.

There were two men and one woman. Except for one man who looked older, the woman was very beautiful, elegant, and aloof, but her age was hard to guess.

Only the other man looked the youngest, like a college student.

This peculiar group occasionally caught the eye of passing students.

That's right.

The trio consisted of Jiang Tian, ​​Shang Mingyan, and Lian Ying.

Jiang Tian hadn't planned to bring Lian Ying along, but Lian Ying insisted on coming. He wanted to see who was trying to harm him.

Upon arriving, Lian Ying looked around, but frankly, found nothing.

After pausing for a moment,

he overheard a young couple displaying affection nearby.

Being the principal, he deeply understood the students' joy. After all, it was a common occurrence in university, so Lian Ying wasn't angry at all; instead, a maternal smile and a sense of nostalgia appeared on his face.

"Back in my university days, I lived a similar life, and my first love and I even spent time here..."

Lian Ying's face broke into a wide, doting smile as she drifted into fond memories.

Even Shang Mingyan glanced at her slightly.

After all, student romances are always so beautiful.

Then Shang Mingyan's gaze fell on Jiang Tian, ​​and the thought involuntarily crossed his mind: what if he and Jiang Tian had met during their student days? Oh, there's an age gap, but that doesn't matter.

It's just a pity.

Shang Mingyan felt a pang of regret.

"A well-rounded development in morality, intelligence, physical fitness, and aesthetics is the foundation of a university. Yunhai University has always been committed to enhancing students' autonomy and ensuring that every student who comes to Yunhai University feels at home. This is a tangible experience," Lian Ying said, her doting smile still lingering.

But then Jiang Tian's voice suddenly rang out: "Your self-reliance is indeed impressive."

"Little Jiang, look, even you think so," Lian Ying said excitedly.

This was undoubtedly recognition of Lian Ying. It didn't matter to her about others, but for Jiang Tian—considering her legendary experiences these past few days—to be recognized by Jiang Tian was the greatest affirmation of his role as principal.

"School is like a small melting pot of society. Once you enter the workforce, you completely lose that feeling. That's why, during your final years as a student, Yunnan University is committed to making every student feel happy. Xiao Jiang, if you came to Yunnan University, you'd experience the same thing," Lian Ying said with a smile.

But just as she finished speaking, Lian Ying suddenly felt a chill.



Chapter 671 Crushing you is merely a favor to you


This made him instinctively cross his arms, glance around, and then look up to see Shang Mingyan staring at him expressionlessly, as if to say, "You old codger, are you looking for trouble?"

How rude!

Lian Ying felt a chill run down his spine from Shang Mingyan's piercing gaze.

That look was too dangerous.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian strolled leisurely to the bottom of the small steps on the playground. The steps weren't high, just a small platform with about a dozen steps.

It was only about two meters high overall. Jiang Tian's approach instantly attracted the attention of the couple sitting on the platform.

Both Chu Mo and the extremely beautiful Si Qinghan initially looked at Jiang Tian with some wariness, but when they saw his young face, which seemed harmless, they relaxed somewhat.

"Excuse me, is there something you need?" Si Qinghan's voice was very pleasant.

"You're very beautiful, young lady," Jiang Tian complimented without reservation.

Si Qinghan's gaze held a hint of disgust, assuming she'd encountered another person trying to strike up a conversation, and her guard rose again.

Her snow-white neck held her chin high, her condescending expression filled with disdainful mockery as she said, "Excuse me, this is my boyfriend. My boyfriend is a 9th-degree black belt in Taekwondo, you know. He could kill you with one punch, so you'd better leave before he gets angry."

Jiang Tian's gaze shifted to the boy, Chu Mo.

At this moment, Chu Mo also frowned as he looked at Jiang Tian.

From the moment Jiang Tian appeared, Chu Mo hadn't uttered a word.

He recognized Shang Mingyan and Lian Ying.

These two, and even more so, these two had just been walking behind this boy who looked very young, even younger than him.

It seemed as if those two were subservient to him.

This filled Chu Mo with shock and wariness.

Especially looking at Jiang Tian's harmless appearance, he seemed more like a tiger disguised as a lamb.

In this person, Chu Mo sensed a kindred spirit.

However, Chu Mo chose to remain silent, wanting to know what this person was doing here and what his identity was.

Si Qinghan, on the other hand, didn't have so many thoughts. In her eyes, Jiang Tian was just a junior student, or at least he looked very young.

"Junior, you look young, you'd better leave quickly. If my boyfriend gets angry and hits you, it'll be too late for you to get angry. Besides, my boyfriend has a terrible temper," Si Qinghan said.

That's right.

In Si Qinghan's eyes, Chu Mo was the kind of person with a somewhat eccentric temper, seemingly normal, but absolutely unapproachable on ordinary days.

Although he was always humorous in front of her, when he lost his temper in public, there was even an incident where some thugs harassed her, and Chu Mo beat four or five of them to a pulp.

That incident caused quite a stir.

Looking back at Jiang Tian, ​​Chu Mo figured he could probably break five ribs with a single kick to this fair-skinned junior.

Si Qinghan was lost in thought.

"Wow, that's impressive. I don't believe your boyfriend would dare touch me," Jiang Tian said with a smirk.

Si Qinghan's pupils contracted.

She thought to herself, "It's over! It's over!" She knew Chu Mo's personality perfectly well; she was his Achilles' heel. Daring to tease her like that, he was definitely dead.

And indeed, it was.

You can't reason with a devil who's destined to die.

Si Qinghan looked at him with regret. He seemed so young, how could he be so foolish?

This was practically courting death!

However, after waiting a while, there was still no movement beside her. Si Qinghan sensed something was wrong. Based on her understanding of Chu Mo, he should have already made his move. Why was there still no movement?

Turning her head, Si Qinghan looked at Chu Mo, her close friend who was only one step away from becoming her boyfriend.

But she found that Chu Mo was smiling.

The two were staring at each other.

She felt like an outsider.

Si Qinghan was stunned.

What was going on? Did they know each other?

What was she just now, a clown?

Thinking of this, Si Qinghan was furious. She pouted and gritted her teeth, glaring at Chu Mo, and said through gritted teeth, enunciating each word clearly, "You know each other, are you all here to tease me?"

Chu Mo shook his head: "No, first time meeting."

"Then what's this?" Si Qinghan was stunned.

"Although it's the first time meeting, it feels like we've met many times before." Chu Mo shook his head.

It's roughly like meeting a kindred spirit.

"It's like, instant rapport." Chu Mo thought of a phrase.

"No, I don't think so." Jiang Tian shook his head.

This was the first time he had encountered such a high-IQ crime.

Although the murderer had been found, the crime scene reconstruction wasn't 100% accurate.

Of course, Jiang Tian still wanted to understand what the murderer was thinking.

"You have a different perspective?" Chu Mo's gaze sharpened.

"You'd say 'cat and mouse' is instant rapport?" Jiang Tian asked calmly.

Chu Mo's pupils contracted instantly.

Then, a smile appeared on his face as he said, "Excuse me, classmate, you just look familiar. I don't understand what you mean."

Si Qinghan roughly realized that the two didn't know each other, so she instantly glared at Jiang Tian angrily and said, "Are you crazy? What kind of cat and mouse game is this? Coming here out of nowhere and saying nonsensical things."

"What I mean is, you should understand. Now that I've found you, you should understand that you can't escape. Pretending only makes me think you're on the same level as those trashy criminals. For those kinds of people, I usually don't waste words and just crush them. I'm telling you all this simply because I think you're different from those trash, but now, you've disappointed me a little."

Jiang Tian sighed. He thought he had met his match, but he felt a little like he had won unfairly. Of course, Jiang Tian wouldn't have an easier way to solve the case; he chose something more difficult.

That would be pure madness.

But that didn't stop Jiang Tian from wanting to understand the situation with this kind of high-IQ criminal.

Si Qinghan thought she had encountered a madwoman.

What the hell is this nonsense?

The key is the pretentious way they're talking, like they're some kind of big shot. What's with this talk about being on the same level as trashy criminals, or being able to crush them without a second thought?

So, they're implying that not crushing us is a favor?

"So we should thank you for not trampling us to death?" Si Qinghan's temper flared with anger.

"Strictly speaking, that should be correct," Jiang Tian nodded seriously.

This instantly infuriated Si Qinghan.

She was absolutely furious.

Especially looking at Jiang Tian's indifferent face, this guy was insane.

"Chu Mo, this lunatic! Why aren't you saying anything? Say something!"

As Si Qinghan spoke, Chu Mo beside her fell silent. Chu Mo's silence left Si Qinghan bewildered. Wait, why are you silent?



Chapter 672 Not brothers, and they've already caught them?


"I forgot to introduce myself. I'm not a student at this school. My surname is Jiang, and my given name is Tian. You can call me Jiang Tian," Jiang Tian introduced himself.

It went very smoothly, surprisingly smoothly for Jiang Tian. However, often, solving a case is only a hair's breadth away from finding the culprit, and that hair's breadth can be ten or twenty years, or even a case that will never be solved.

Like this Chu Mo.

If Jiang Tian hadn't been using his special abilities, possessing a scanning radar, it's hard to say how long it would have taken to find this person. At least in terms of outward disguise, even a psychology master like Jiang Tian, ​​passing by or conducting a large-scale screening, would find it difficult to spot anything unusual.

Unless you observe very carefully.

But if the culprit just needs to be as ordinary as everyone else, without any abnormalities, it's actually very difficult to detect. Even a psychology master isn't a god; their accuracy isn't always that high.

"You're not a student at this school, so how did you get in? Are you crazy? I'm calling security right now," Si Qinghan said, looking at Jiang Tian with suspicion.

That's right, in her eyes, Jiang Tian had gradually become a lunatic.

Otherwise, if he wasn't a lunatic, why would he be like this?

He was spouting a bunch of nonsensical words.

Jiang Tian wasn't angry and waved his hand apologetically, saying, "My bad, I only mentioned my name, not my profession. My name is Jiang Tian, ​​and I'm currently the captain of the Narcotics Control Brigade of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau. I'm here to assist in solving the indoor murder case that recently occurred on your campus."

"Huh?"

Si Qinghan was stunned.

"You?"

"Narcotics Control Brigade Captain?"

"Pfft!!!"

"Are you kidding me?"

"Junior, have you graduated yet? And you're the Narcotics Control Brigade Captain? Don't you know we're being bullied? My family may not be very powerful, but we have a few people in the police force. A captain in Yunhai City should at least be a police inspector, or even a second-level police inspector or higher, right? You, a second-level police inspector?"

Si Qinghan almost spat out her drink.

Looking at Jiang Tian's youthful face, it was utter nonsense.

A Second-Level Police Inspector—this was a position most people could only dream of achieving in their entire lives. Even with numerous merits, it would take at least until their thirties or forties to reach that level.

That's assuming numerous merits, a powerful background, and exceptional ability.

Even with consistent promotions, it would still require at least fifteen years of service.

"I know you don't believe me, but here are my credentials."

Jiang Tian pulled two booklets from his pocket, one red and one black. The red one, of course, was his other security-related departmental certification.

This certification, in essence, granted Jiang Tian significantly more authority and virtually unlimited enforcement power—a position with virtually no safety net.

In reality, however, it had almost no impact on Jiang Tian in any way.

He only occasionally cooperated.

But so far, Fang Zheng hadn't contacted him, so Jiang Tian was naturally enjoying two salaries. After all, the salary and bonuses for that department were top-tier.

Of course, these are all just supplementary measures; their real purpose is to give Jiang Tian unlimited firing rights.

And most importantly, wireless law enforcement authority.

As for the black one, that was Jiang Tian's legitimate identification.

Jiang Tian took it out and glanced at it.

Si Qinghan almost burst out laughing, because she was now absolutely certain that Jiang Tian was a counterfeit.

Most importantly, it looked fake no matter how you looked at it.

"There are too many fake IDs, I don't know which one it is?" Si Qinghan said, suppressing a laugh.

"Oh, there isn't one, this is my ID."

Jiang Tian took out the black police ID, which had a badge and Jiang Tian's portrait on it.

He placed it in front of Si Qinghan, and Si Qinghan couldn't help but laugh.

"Chu Mo, Chu Mo, look at this! I'm dying of laughter! I can't take it anymore! This fake guy is trying to tease me! Hey, I remember, counterfeiting IDs is illegal, right? Can we call the police and have him arrested?" Si Qinghan clutched her stomach, unable to stop laughing.

She even patted Chu Mo's shoulder beside her.

However, for some reason, Chu Mo's shoulders seemed a little damp.

The weather was dry, and it wasn't even that hot, so why was he so damp? Was he sweating?

Si Qinghan was still thinking about this.

Then, Chu Mo, who had been so cooperative, suddenly became completely silent, which made Si Qinghan feel a little awkward.

What? Was it too awkward for her to smile?

Was Chu Mo doing this on purpose?

Just to see her embarrassed?

She was furious.

Si Qinghan abruptly turned her head and angrily said to Chu Mo, "Chu Mo, why aren't you smiling?"

But as soon as she said that,

Si Qinghan suddenly realized, good heavens, Chu Mo's face was covered in cold sweat.

"Are you hot?" Si Qinghan asked belatedly, stunned.

“Yeah, haha, it seems a bit hot. Qinghan, didn’t you have something to do? You should go first.” Chu Mo’s smile faded as he spoke, his face turning serious.

“No, what do you mean?”

“Don’t ask, just go.” Chu Mo interrupted Si Qinghan directly.

Then, turning his gaze back to Jiang Tian, ​​Chu Mo explained, “Officer Jiang, my girlfriend has nothing to do with this. She knows nothing about it. Can we go?”

“No, Chu Mo, you…” Si Qinghan began.

“Shut up, don’t speak.” For the first time, Chu Mo snapped at Si Qinghan harshly.

“I…” Si Qinghan froze.

At this moment, even if she were stupid, she would realize something was wrong.

Something’s wrong.

A big problem.

This…

Si Qinghan's eyes widened even more: “Wait, you just said Officer Jiang? You're crazy! He's fake, an imposter! His acting is terrible!”

She thought Chu Mo had gone mad.

But only Chu Mo knew.

This wasn't fake; it was real, unmistakably so.

Such a young man.

Chu Mo looked at Jiang Tian before him. He looked even younger than Chu Mo, yet he was already the head of the anti-drug squad, and even held the rank of second-level police inspector.

This…

It seemed as fake as could be, but Chu Mo knew it was all true. Not only true, but this man's current position was entirely due to Chu Mo.

"It's real, not fake. It's Officer Jiang from the Municipal Narcotics Control Team," Chu Mo said in a deep voice.

"Really?" Si Qinghan was dumbfounded, utterly dumbfounded, then stared at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

Thinking back to her clumsy performance just now, it turned out that she herself was the clown in the whole thing?

"How is this possible? He doesn't even look as old as me. Impossible, absolutely impossible," Si Qinghan said, unable to believe it.



Chapter 673 What? You're threatening me with your girlfriend?


"Young lady, you don't know Officer Jiang, but you should know me, right?"

Just then, a voice rang out.

Si Qinghan turned around and saw Lian Ying standing not far away.

She was looking at them with a serious expression.

Just now, Shang Mingyan was coordinating manpower. The moment he sensed something was wrong, especially after Jiang Tian's contact with Chu Mo,

Shang Mingyan immediately realized something was amiss and secretly mobilized officers from the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

As for Jiang Tian's comment that using so many police officers for a single murderer was a waste,

this was a vicious criminal, after all. Who knew what he might do? Shang Mingyan still quietly mobilized a considerable number of people. Only after mobilizing all the officers did Shang Mingyan arrive with Lian Ying, and then they encountered this scene.

"Principal Lian," Si Qinghan said, her face full of astonishment.

"Alright, since you recognize me, I can tell you that this Officer Jiang in front of you is the real deal. He's a police god in Yunhai, awarded multiple first-class merits. What he says is never wrong; you don't need to question him." Lian Ying said solemnly.

Because the situation was more serious than he had imagined.

He had initially thought it was just a transient crime.

If it were a transient crime, their responsibility would be less, but damn it, now he discovered it seemed to be students from their own school. This...this responsibility was serious.

If it were just simple murder, that would be one thing, but the key point was that they even used a high-powered bomb, almost blowing up Shang Mingyan and Jiang Tian.

This responsibility was very serious.

Even Minister Tan was personally involved, and it was even possible that the capital city knew about it and was also paying attention to the case.

The more Lian Ying thought about it, the more he wanted to curse.

They were courting death themselves, why did they have to implicate so many people?

"Really?" Si Qinghan was completely stunned.

If Jiang Tian was telling the truth, then everything he said was absolutely true.

Then...

What was Chu Mo? What did he just say?

"I just said, it's like a cat catching a mouse. I'm the cat," Jiang Tian joked.

But this joke...

Si Qinghan couldn't laugh.

Chu Mo remained silent.

You're a police officer, you're the cat, so Chu Mo is the mouse?

What is a mouse? The opposite is naturally a criminal.

"Officer Jiang, are you mistaken? We're innocent. We don't remember doing anything heinous." Si Qinghan's mind worked quickly.

"Whether you did it or not, it's not up to you to decide," Jiang Tian shook his head.

"Chu Mo, quickly tell them you're innocent!" Si Qinghan hurriedly tried to speak.

But the next moment...

Si Qinghan felt a terrifying force approach, followed by a cold touch against her neck.

"Too bad, I told you to leave, but you wouldn't. If you don't want to leave, then help me one last time," Chu Mo's sinister voice rang out.

Si Qinghan's body stiffened, her expression frozen, and she trembled as she said, "Chu Mo, you...you're joking with me, aren't you?"

"What did you think?"

Chu Mo's knife tip pressed against Si Qinghan's neck, a drop of blood sliding down her neck.

This is no joke.

Chu Mo is real.

But how is this possible?

At this moment, Si Qinghan's face froze, facing Jiang Tian's playful gaze.

And behind her, on her neck, was a chilling sensation.

The slight pain in her neck told her that Chu Mo was holding a dagger to her neck, ready to take her life at any moment.

Why? Why is all this happening?

"I already told you, this person is a rat, a criminal, why didn't you believe me?" Jiang Tian shook his head regretfully.

Shang Mingyan and Lian Ying behind Jiang Tian also quickly recovered from their initial shock.

Mainly, this happened too fast.

At the same time, it came too suddenly.

Clearly.

At first, they only had a premonition, but never imagined it.

Jiang Tian solved the case so quickly—no, let's just say, he was just investigating the traces.

"We just found the footprints, the forensic team is still on their way, they haven't even arrived yet to confirm if they're the murderer's footprints, and you just arrived at the playground and, in no time, you've already identified the murderer?"

"Hahaha, I didn't expect this, I really didn't expect this! I was already overwhelmed by the arrival of Shang Mingyan, but even the legendary you have come in person! It seems my work has been quite successful!"

At this moment, Chu Mo suddenly laughed.

His laugh contained no tension whatsoever.

In fact, it carried a hint of relaxed amusement.

Of course, Jiang Tian's amusement was genuine; he had seen through this dramatic scene from the beginning.

Whether Chu Mo's amusement was feigned was another matter.

But Chu Mo's words brought the completely bewildered Si Qinghan back to her senses, and she understood her situation.

So, this man in front of her—no, he looked even younger than her, someone she should normally call a junior in school—was he?

But what did Chu Mo say?

Her demeanor was undeniably extraordinary. After the sudden and dramatic change, although the reality was difficult for her to accept at first, she eventually came to terms with the situation.

In other words, what she needed to think about was how to solve the problem, or rather, how to survive.

Jiang Tian.

Her target was clearly the man before her named Jiang Tian, ​​the person she had just mocked. The unpredictability of life was truly unbearable.

Just a moment ago, he was someone she thought was an imposter, but now, the only person who could possibly save her was him.

"You know me?" Jiang Tian wasn't too surprised; his reputation had already spread far and wide.

"Throughout Yunhai and the underworld, your name has long been known. Although I'm not involved in organized crime, your name is still widely known. Several notorious gangsters have fallen at your hands, and many other ruthless individuals have met their demise at your hands. It's hard to say about others, but you, it's true, could crush me."

Chu Mo was very open-minded, or rather, very clear-headed. Initially, he was confident, but after learning that the other person was Jiang Tian, ​​Chu Mo understood that his only chance was with the woman who was about to become his girlfriend.

It's just a pity.

He hasn't even enjoyed it yet.

After hearing Chu Mo's words, Si Qinghan

reacted after a brief moment.

Indeed.

Her chance lay with this person. Si Qinghan's eyes turned to Jiang Tian.

"Let me leave here. I won't hurt her. I know how powerful you are; I've heard you can take down hundreds of people single-handedly. I don't have the confidence to fight you. Let me go, or I'll kill her."



Chapter 674 Life and death were in his hands.


As he spoke, the dagger drew closer to Si Qinghan's neck, the tip piercing her skin, blood trickling down her neck.

Si Qinghan froze, completely stiff.

The greatest sorrow is a broken heart. At this moment, Si Qinghan finally realized that the person behind her had been blind; the person she had once taken a liking to was actually going to kill her.

To die for such a person?

A strange will to live surged within Si Qinghan.

To live, she wanted to live. To die for such a person, Si Qinghan didn't want that.

She had once been blind.

Fortunately, it was just a good relationship; it seemed they were mutually attracted but hadn't developed any further. Perhaps this was Si Qinghan's only solace.

"Save me."

Si Qinghan looked at Jiang Tian with pleading eyes. She didn't want to die.

In fact, she was far more mature than others. Suicidal thoughts weren't her style; wanting to die for someone she'd only known for a short time would be foolish.

Even if there was already a certain foundation of affection between them.

But for someone as clear-headed as Si Qinghan, she clearly understood what would happen to her family if she died.

She even involuntarily thought of a hero interviewed in the news recently. Although the hero hadn't shown his face, didn't know his name, age, or even what he looked like, his words remained vivid in Si Qinghan's memory.

"If I die.

What will happen to those who hate me? What will happen to those who love me?"

These words went viral online, instantly sobering many people up because they were absolutely true.

"If you die, those who love you will be devastated, while those who hate you will rejoice.

So why do something that causes pain to loved ones and joy to enemies?

Isn't that foolish?"

If she died, if she died for a beast like Chu Mo, how would her family cope?

The more she thought about it,

the more Si Qinghan wanted to live; she didn't want to die.

"Stop! Do you have any humanity left?" Shang Mingyan's face was icy cold.

She had just begun to experience the beauty of love, and then she turned around to see this scene.

This was blatant provocation to Shang Mingyan, especially at such a sensitive time.

This betrayal revealed it all.

Chu Mo had lost all humanity.

"Humanity? Of course I have humanity, Shang Mingyan. I didn't expect you to survive." Chu Mo sneered.

"You admit you planted a bomb in my car?" Shang Mingyan's gaze was chilling.

This was tantamount to admitting that he was the one who planted the bomb.

"That's right, it was me. I just didn't expect you to survive. That surprised me." Chu Mo also sneered.

Then, turning his gaze to Jiang Tian, ​​he said, "Don't make any rash moves. I know you, and I know your skills, so I need absolute security."

"You can't escape now," Jiang Tian said expressionlessly.

"Who knows? What if you do manage to get away?" Chu Mo chuckled.

"You're very confident. Who gave you that confidence? Why did you do this? Satisfy my curiosity, and perhaps your fate will be better." Even the most ruthless person wouldn't go to such lengths to target Shang Mingyan.

In other words.

Why?

Everything has a reason.

"Why? You want to ask this woman? My mother has been ill since I was very young. My father worked hard to earn money for her medical treatment, and this woman killed him. From that moment on, I swore I would take revenge, and I will." Chu Mo's eyes were bloodshot.

But deep within his eyes was a sheet of ice.

This person is not simple; Jiang Tian had already sensed it.

This feeling was obvious, very clear.

Jiang Tian could even sense the danger emanating from this person, a great threat. This was a person brimming with danger.

Jiang Tian didn't even dare to imagine the horrific crimes this person would commit if he hadn't been able to control them.

But as the saying goes, the unknown is the most terrifying. Once you know the truth, once your identity is exposed, it's not so dangerous anymore.

"Is that so?" Jiang Tian thought of a revenge drama.

But, Shang Mingyan indiscriminately killed innocent people?

How could that be?

Shang Mingyan's intelligence, and his abilities all along, would never allow him to do such a thing, much less create a wrongful conviction.

Then...

"Thirteen years ago?"

Shang Mingyan paused.

Her mind raced, then suddenly a flash of inspiration struck her. Her face turned icy as she said, "I remember now. Thirteen years ago, the serial robbery and murderer, in your words, became someone who supplemented their income. When they were being arrested, they used firearms to forcibly resist arrest, and in your words, we became murderers."

Given Shang Mingyan's personality, she wouldn't even bother to explain.

However, with Jiang Tian here, she seemed afraid he would misunderstand, so she recounted what had happened before, even the exact moment it was decided.

"To supplement the family income?

That's a joke."

"There are so many criminals in the world, why target my dad? Our family is so pitiful, why are you targeting us?" Chu Mo acted as if he were insane.

But Jiang Tian saw through him.

Behind this apparent madness lay a calm exterior.

That's right.

A facade. Behind this apparent madness, Jiang Tian could see that his emotions were very stable; in other words, it was all an act.

Indeed, this was a dangerous criminal!

"A murderer deserves to die. Killing your father only proves a waste of a bullet, a waste of resources. A piece of trash who only caused trouble even after death deserves to die without a burial place. Giving him a bullet was already a favor. What's the point of keeping such trash alive? Isn't your father trash? Oh no, your father is trash, and you're the same kind of trash, so you're in the same boat." Jiang Tian sneered.

And those words...

A normal person would probably explode upon hearing that.

But that's exactly what happened.

Chu Mo was different. Highly intelligent criminals were like that; they possessed extremely calm and rational personalities, and strong psychological resilience.

Jiang Tian's words would probably make anyone explode, but he wouldn't.

Not only that.

Chu Mo's face and eyes instantly filled with vigilance, and even his pretense crumbled.

"Is that so!"

Chu Mo's voice was low, revealing no anger.

Lian Ying was practically exploding in the back.

Especially after hearing Jiang Tian speak, Lian Ying frantically tried to dissuade him. Wait, wait a minute, why was Jiang Tian still provoking the murderer?

This...



Chapter 675 Prove to me, you think you can escape?


"Is this insane? What if he kills someone?"

This killer's personality is unstable.

"Officer Jiang, your reputation precedes you. Even though I don't know much about you, even just a glimpse is enough to show how terrifying you are. Not only your combat abilities, but your intelligence is also terrifyingly high."

Chu Mo smiled.

Although all his actions seemed insane, utterly deranged,

Jiang Tian understood.

He could sense that this person was many times more dangerous than any criminal he had ever encountered.

"In all these years, you're the first person who has made me feel threatened."

Chu Mo continued.

Even though it seemed like he was kidnapping Si Qinghan, Jiang Tian could tell from his body language:

It was very steady.

In fact, every limb, every joint, was positioned for maximum counterattack. Any abnormality would ensure a fatal blow within a fraction of a second.

This...

Jiang Tian had encountered countless criminals, but this was the first time he had truly seen one like this—incredibly strong.

“Your movements bear the marks of training. Though they appear ordinary, every pause in your body guarantees a draw and counterattack within a fraction of a second, and a kill within a meter within that fraction of a second. This kind of skill doesn't seem like something you can develop on your own. You've been on the battlefield, haven't you?” Jiang Tian's interest was piqued.

A strong person, Jiang Tian confirmed in his mind.

This was a powerful individual.

“I saw through you!” Chu Mo smiled.

Jiang Tian's eyes narrowed slightly.

Pressure.

No.

This isn't pressure.

Jiang Tian only felt, for the first time, a tiny, at most tiny, sense of being challenged.

But.

Very powerful.

Jiang Tian was certain that if Shang Mingyan had brought a few people, this person could have killed them all within a minute.

And that was with his bare hands.

Although he hadn't actually fought,

all his physical actions indicated that this person was extremely skilled.

At least in terms of combat power, he was an absolutely top-tier individual.

“That's right. After my parents died when I was young, I swore that I would take revenge and make sure all those who harmed them died without a burial place. That's why I came to Yunhai specifically, because the environment here allows me to act freely and without fear,” Chu Mo said.

“Then you just need to commit a few major cases here, and it will definitely attract Shang Mingyan’s attention,” Jiang Tian said directly.

“Yes, I’ve escaped death countless times overseas, strengthening myself so I can return for revenge, to take revenge on everyone. Shang Mingyan was just the first, but I never expected to fail.” Chu Mo said regretfully.

“You’ve never had any real feelings for me?” Si Qinghan suddenly asked.

“You were just a tool I used.”

Chu Mo’s voice was cold and sarcastic.

But…

Really?

Si Qinghan thought back to the past. If he was truly heinous, why had he never laid a hand on her?
