Moreover, their interactions had always remained within the bounds of propriety, which was why Si Qinghan was quite surprised and held him in such high regard. Chu Mo had always maintained a great deal of respect for her, never even shaking her hand.

Only tonight, perhaps Chu Mo sensed something, and his words and actions broke through that barrier.

But...

Does it matter?

It no longer matters.

Tonight, none of this matters anymore.

“How about this, I'll let this girl go. She's quite pitiful. Let me leave,” Chu Mo said.

“Do you think you can escape? Even if I let you go, there are already countless police officers surrounding you outside.” Jiang Tian didn't act rashly.

Ordinary people might be an exception, but now, Jiang Tian was certain.

While he could definitely kill Chu Mo himself, Si Qinghan, this innocent person, would definitely die here.

He wasn't lying; he was truly a professional.

At least in the area of ​​killing.

"I only need one promise from you: don't take any action against me. You don't need to worry about the others; if they die at my hands, it's because I'm not strong enough. Besides, that's not a big deal. I can add another: I've planted high explosives in both the male and female dormitories, all in load-bearing positions. Once detonated, everything will be reduced to ashes. As long as you don't make a move, I won't detonate the explosives. Is that enough?" Chu Mo said indifferently.

Hiss!!!

Shang Mingyan's pupils contracted.

Lian Ying almost collapsed to the ground.

"Xiao Jiang," Lian Ying's voice trembled.

As expected, Jiang Tian wasn't surprised. From his behavior, he had roughly analyzed that this was a criminal with a dangerous personality.

"You're willing to pay such a high price just to prevent me from making a move? Do you think so highly of me?" Jiang Tian chuckled.

His smile held no threat whatsoever.

But...

Si Qinghan sensed it; Chu Mo's hand was trembling.

This sent a jolt through Si Qinghan's mind, shaking her to her core, leaving her completely blank.

Jiang Tian, ​​this seemingly harmless man.

Could he really be that dangerous, more dangerous than the countless police officers lying in ambush outside?

What exactly had Jiang Tian done?

But Chu Mo's next sentence completely cleared up Si Qinghan's doubts: "Girl, the legendary figure in the Yunhai police force that you've always admired, the one whose life's motto you held so deeply in esteem, died a death that brought grief to his loved ones and joy to his enemies. Those who loved him were heartbroken, those who hated him were ecstatic. Days later, he became a skeleton, and his relatives would occasionally visit. This is the same person who was masked back then. Do you understand?"

That's right.

Si Qinghan was a fan of that man.

A fan won over by a single sentence.

Besides that one sentence, there was the narration from back then, which briefly explained Jiang Tian's general achievements at that time, without much else.

Just a simple sentence:

The recipient of six First-Class Merits.

What he did is self-evident.

Of course, this was in the news before. During this period, Jiang Tian gained another first-class merit—no, he's about to have two—no, if the bomb was planted at the school this time, if he successfully averted this disaster.

That should count as another first-class merit, right?

Jiang Tian's expression was a little doubtful.

This first-class merit came a bit too suddenly.

He already had seven First-Class Merits, and with these, wow, nine First-Class Merits?

Plus, an August 1st Honor.

And a Special-Class Merit.

Jiang Tian felt his invincible invincibility was a bit too much.

Anyone who dared to interrogate him, he would crush them with his First-Class Merits.

“In the past, no criminal has ever escaped my grasp. I can give you an hour at most. I won't make a move within this hour. After an hour, I will personally arrest you, provided you can evade the layers of police surrounding you.”

“Run. I want to see how capable you are.”

“If you can escape, then you will become the first criminal to escape from my grasp. This will be the glory of your life.”



Chapter 676 Family members, who can understand me?


Chu Mo released Si Qinghan. In that instant, Si Qinghan felt as if she had been given a new lease on life. Just moments ago, she had almost died.

Now, she was alive again.

She had been reborn.

Si Qinghan's face showed some lingering shock, but she immediately ran downstairs, without any attempt to question him.

Questioning Chu Mo at this moment would be idiotic. Would she be blaming him for not having a knife? Perhaps some women might choose to angrily question him at this point.

They might believe that he wouldn't kill them out of affection, nor would they risk their lives. But that kind of behavior is simply idiotic.

If he wasn't idiotic.

How could he do such a thing? At this moment, nothing was more important than her own life!

This caused a flicker of admiration to cross Jiang Tian's eyes. Regardless of the circumstances, Si Qinghan at least understood that Jiang Tian's deliberate provocation was to see if the other party would reveal a weakness, primarily to rescue the hostages.

After all, with people at stake, and the school bomb looming, this was a serious matter.

Jiang Tian was confident in his abilities, but he wouldn't gamble with the entire school's safety by risking an explosion, betting he could kill Chu Mo before the bomb detonated.

Even if he succeeded, reporting it would likely result in a severe punishment. Taking such a risk was playing with the lives of countless people.

Although Jiang Tian didn't actually take action, in other words, he had saved the lives of many at the school.

So,

He won.

He was numb with victory.

Unconsciously, another first-class merit.

Simply and easily.

Si Qinghan ran to Jiang Tian's side, then fearfully hid behind him. After spending so much time together, Si Qinghan realized for the first time that she truly didn't understand Chu Mo at all.

Or rather, Chu Mo had been pretending the whole time.

Terrifying.

Comparatively, to make someone as terrifying as Chu Mo so fearful, even if the person mentioned in the news was this man,

there was probably no one in the entire Yunhai safer than this heroic police officer beside her. If she wasn't safe with him, then there was nowhere safe in the entire Yunhai; she might as well just wait to die.

Jiang Tian glanced at Si Qinghan, and Si Qinghan, seeing his gaze, blushed slightly, clearly remembering what she had just said and feeling somewhat embarrassed.

However, Jiang Tian wasn't a petty person. Besides, being misunderstood was normal; Si Qinghan's reaction was that of a normal person.

Jiang Tian turned his head, a slight interest rising within him: "You really believe I won't do anything?"

"I don't believe what others say, but you're Jiang Tian. I know you're a man of your word, someone like you."

"Like I said, this is the remote key."

Chu Mo took out a remote key and tossed it to Jiang Tian. Jiang Tian caught it, glanced at it, and couldn't be sure if it was real or not.

Based on Chu Mo's logic, the possibility that it was true was extremely high.

In other words, if those two dormitory buildings were to explode...

What would the consequences be?

Two thousand teachers and students.

That loss.

Even on a global scale, it would be an absolutely terrifying case.

If this were true, for Jiang Tian, ​​who prevented this disaster, even a first-class merit might not be enough.

Because this would reach the level of a special-class merit.

Special-class merit.

Two special-class merits.

Jiang Tian fell silent.

Although not explicitly stated, Chu Mo's actions seemed to have a hint of giving him credit.

"Then, let's leave it at that."

Chu Mo smiled, turned, and jumped off the platform. He ran towards the dense forest through the hazy playground, his voice ringing out at the same time: "We will definitely meet again."

At that moment, Shang Mingyan and Lian Ying rushed to Jiang Tian's side.

"You're really not going to make a move? Maybe I should go and arrest him," Shang Mingyan said, swallowing hard.

"No, if you go, you're just asking for death. He could kill you easily." Jiang Tian sensed a slight threat.

Although it was only a tiny threat, in this world, the only time Jiang Tian could feel even a slight threat was back in Myanmar, when he had ventured into a dangerous place and was surrounded in a villa by hundreds of fully armed criminals.

And back then, Jiang Tian's combat strength was far weaker than it is now.

In other words,

this person's combat strength is very strong.

If it were a one-on-one battle in the wilderness, such a person could even take down a hundred elite, well-trained soldiers.

Although it seemed unbelievable, Jiang Tian's intuition was accurate.

"Is he that strong?" Shang Mingyan asked incredulously.

"If I were to take him down personally, it would take at least a minute, and that minute would be enough for me to deal with a hundred people," Jiang Tian replied.

One minute.

Actually, this was under the condition that Jiang Tian needed to ensure his safety. While Jiang Tian could dodge bullets, that was only based on anticipation.

A true master wouldn't give him that chance to anticipate.

Just now, Chu Mo was very vigilant, not even letting Jiang Tian get within ten meters.

During this time, Jiang Tian couldn't gamble that the other side wouldn't detonate the bomb.

If they did, then Jiang Tian would truly be deeply sinful.

Even though Jiang Tian is currently highly regarded, causing such terrifying consequences would mean nothing in the military; he'd be headed straight to court.

"That terrifying?" Shang Mingyan said in disbelief.

Si Qinghan, standing to the side, was stunned. No, I mean, was this just bragging? But Chu Mo's behavior didn't seem like bragging at all.

She had just sensed Chu Mo's fear.

Rather than seeing this as a gift to Jiang Tian, ​​it was more like, if Jiang Tian made a move, he wouldn't have any confidence in escaping.

In other words, this guy might not actually be bragging.

Taking down a hundred fully armed men in a minute—this guy might really be able to do it. Similarly, Chu Mo seems quite strong too.

But they seem incomparable to this guy.

Truly worthy of being my idol.

Si Qinghan's eyes flashed with a dazzling light as she stared intently at Jiang Tian.

Meanwhile,

outside the school.

After Shang Mingyan called for backup.

As the bureau chief, at this crucial moment in his final term, upon learning of Shang Mingyan's request for assistance, Han Xian naturally rushed to the scene immediately.

Family members, who can understand, who can understand Han Xian's feelings!



Chapter 677 Comrade Xiao Jiang is invincible; everything he does is right.


His political career seems to be sealed.

That's right.

This might be his last period as bureau chief. If he can't salvage the situation by now, leaving this place will mean his political career is completely ruined. Although he might still be within the system, seemingly alive, he's practically dead.

His future is definitely in a retirement department, and that's the best-case scenario. If someone decides to investigate him, the consequences would be unimaginable.

In conclusion, considering everything, he will do everything he can to reverse this situation at the end of his career.

Not much is needed.

Jiang Tian has earned so many merits; one first-class merit is enough.

Besides, looking at the many cases and opportunities in Yunhai, Han Xian is quite confident in his abilities. He just received news that Boss Bai is about to launch a massive operation.

And now, another dangerous murderer has appeared on Shang Mingyan's side. Ordinary murder is one thing, but the key is that a bomb was planted in Shang Mingyan's car. What does this mean?

The situation and circumstances were completely different!

So, under these circumstances, upon hearing that the murderer was in grave danger, Han Xian hurriedly arrived with his men. He wanted to safeguard his final glory at the end of his career.

And successfully salvage everything.

“Maintain strict security, especially at all intersections. Set up the strictest cordon. Not even a fly should be allowed to escape. Within this school, ensure there's a sentry every three steps and a patrol every five steps. No one should be allowed to slip through.”

Han Xian had brought hundreds of armed police and special agents to make a grand entrance.

“Chief, I've already ordered guards at all intersections around Yun University. There's absolutely no way anyone can get out of the school.”

His assistant spoke up at this moment.

Han Xian nodded slightly. Given the nature of this case, if he succeeded in apprehending the culprit, other merits were uncertain, but personal commendations should be easily attainable.

While it wouldn't completely salvage his career like a first-class merit award, at least there was a significant chance to change the higher-ups' opinion of him.

But in reality, Han Xian still didn't understand one thing.

He especially didn't understand what his superiors meant.

The reason they brought you here, or rather, replaced him, wasn't because of his lack of ability. What they needed was someone who could cooperate with Jiang Tian's operations, and even, in certain situations, act as Jiang Tian's backup.

Not someone where Jiang Tian is charging ahead while the bureau chief is dragging his feet.

They didn't even need healthy competition. What competition? You're not qualified to compete with Jiang Tian. What level are you at? What rank are you? You're just here to reap the rewards, trying to drive away the one who planted them?

Who gave you the audacity? Are you even worthy?

"Everyone, do your job well this time. I know there have been rumors about me lately, but tell them that as long as I'm in this position, I'm the bureau chief. They must obey orders unconditionally. If anyone makes a mistake, don't blame me for being ruthless," Han Xian said sternly.

He believed this was his first major battle to turn his life around, and he had to win it spectacularly.

"Yes, Director," the assistant hurriedly went to make the arrangements.

It must be said, this assistant was quite capable.

But Han Xian had no idea what was happening on campus at that very moment, much less that the brightly lit city police station was in complete chaos.

"Something terrible has happened."

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong sat stunned, facing each other in their office.

And not only them.

Upon receiving the news, Zhou Zhao and Jiang Peng, who had already assumed a leadership position in the political and legal system, along with other key city leaders, were all in their offices.

Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan barely hesitated; after all, such a major event had to be reported to their superiors immediately.

This was no small matter.

This concerned the very survival of Yun University!

Good heavens, if a bomb explodes, the whole world will explode.

Even Jiang Tian hadn't expected the murderer to be so vicious, to have reached a level of utter depravity that completely surpassed humanity.

Jiang Tian even felt that Chu Mo's motives for all this seemed solely to satisfy his own desires.

Such a person is the most terrifying and horrifying; revenge seems merely a convenient excuse.

So-called revenge is just a pretext to mask his desires.

Jiang Tian immediately called Minister Tan. Upon hearing the news, Minister Tan fell into a long silence, unable to process it.

Because anyone who heard this news would be in an uproar.

Moreover, this happened while he and Gao Xiaolong were both in charge of Yunhai. What would be the consequences if something this serious occurred?

Just thinking about it sent chills down his spine.

The implications extend beyond Yunhai or Yunnan Province. Even those from top to bottom, including Minister Tan, would have faced immense responsibility if this hadn't been stopped; they might not even be able to stay in their positions.

In other words,

Jiang Tian's discovery saved them.

There were many people in the office.

But those who spoke...

No one was there.

Even their breathing had become incredibly faint; the atmosphere was so heavy it felt almost palpable.

"Yun Da, over two thousand people... just now, by a hair's breadth, they were all gone. Everyone, tell me what happened." Minister Tan tapped the table.

We can't rush things.

The more urgent the moment, the less we can afford to rush.

Minister Tan, having weathered many storms, found this situation difficult to accept, but the reality was undeniable.

“The first priority is to immediately organize the students' evacuation from the dormitories,” Zhou Zhao abruptly declared.

The world was at stake.

The students' evacuation was paramount.

“You’re right. On the other hand, the provincial department needs to urgently send additional bomb disposal experts to investigate,” Minister Tan nodded.

The sky had fallen.

For everyone, it was a catastrophe.

Even though Yunhai had already been the site of countless major cases uncovered by Jiang Tian.

But this case was the most unique.

“By the way, what about the murderer? Has he been apprehended?” Zhou Zhao asked.

He knew the general outline of the situation.

However, the specific details were completely unknown.

Minister Tan, on the other hand, had already conducted a thorough investigation and knew quite a bit.

“He escaped.”

Minister Tan said expressionlessly.

“What, he escaped?”

“How could such a dangerous murderer escape?”

“Yes, why didn’t Jiang Tian arrest him? Such a dangerous murderer got away. Isn’t he supposed to be very good at catching criminals?”

At this moment, someone from the municipal government suddenly frowned and asked.

Minister Tan seemed to have anticipated this, saying, “The murderer said he has a bomb and is said to be extremely skilled. If he makes a move and the bomb explodes, can you take responsibility?”

“You will take responsibility?”

Huh???

“Comrade Jiang, do you even have the right to question me?”

“When has Comrade Jiang ever made a mistake? What are your intentions?”



Chapter 678 The real show has only just begun.


Is Jiang Tian responsible? In this matter, if he didn't act, he was merely doing a good deed, even arguably ensuring the safety of two thousand teachers and students by preventing any future problems and simply letting the perpetrator escape.

On this point alone,

no one can have any reason to hold Jiang Tian accountable.

If Jiang Tian had acted...

Regardless of whether the perpetrator had other means to detonate the bomb, or whether he failed to stop it before detonation, all the consequences would be attributed to Jiang Tian's willful act. In fact, even if he had acted forcefully, it would have been considered a success.

After all the details of the case were revealed, Jiang Tian also bore significant responsibility because he played with the lives of thousands.

You say you can succeed, but what if you fail? Thousands of people would pay the price for your recklessness.

From the higher-ups' perspective, this isn't a merit; it could even be seen as gambling with the lives of thousands of students for your own gain.

Therefore, many things cannot be done unilaterally.

Having experienced so much in his past life, Jiang Tian's mindset was completely different. His extensive experience had taught him that things weren't always as they seemed.

The principle of "too much rigidity leads to breakage" applies here. Even if Jiang Tian was 80% confident he could resolve the situation, ensuring the safety of the teachers and students and the elimination of the criminals, why gamble with that remaining 20% ​​chance?

Doing nothing was enough to earn a first-class merit, no, even a special-class merit.

Why bother with further effort?

One hour was Jiang Tian giving the criminals time.

This hour was also time Jiang Tian was giving the police to defuse the bomb.

With one hour to evacuate the teachers and students, Jiang Tian could act. Even at the last moment, if the teachers and students were safe, the criminals' frenzied rage would be futile.

However, this was contingent on the criminals remaining within a kilometer radius of Jiang Tian.

There also needs to be a general direction for the search, but Jiang Tian estimated that Chu Mo was still very likely to escape.

“You did the right thing. I was really afraid you would act impulsively. If you managed to act in time, that would be fine, but if you didn't, you would bear all the responsibility.”

Shang Mingyan was now completely focused on Jiang Tian. No matter what Jiang Tian chose, Shang Mingyan wouldn't stop him; he would only support him.

Because it was Jiang Tian's choice.

“Do you really think I'm a brainless brute? Thousands of lives! If we hadn't stopped them, I wouldn't be able to bear the responsibility even if I died.”

Jiang Tian chuckled. How could that be? He could easily get the credit and complete the mission perfectly without doing anything.

Why did he have to go up there? Why look for some excitement?

Isn't that a stupid thing to do?

“Um, thank you.”

Just then, Si Qinghan suddenly spoke up.

Standing beside Jiang Tian, ​​looking down at her toes, her voice tinged with a little embarrassment as she expressed her gratitude.

Because it was so obvious that the mockery just now, and how...unpleasant it was, made the situation incredibly awkward now.

However, Si Qinghan felt none of that mattered. The most important thing was that she had met her idol!

This really was her idol.

In reality...

Jiang Tian's popularity is currently explosive. On the internet, due to a lack of control, and especially since Yunhai has no shortage of wealthy business owners, these owners are frantically hiring online trolls to smear Jiang Tian, ​​even taking advantage of the situation to launch a massive anti-Jiang Tian campaign.

Jiang Tian is practically becoming a cancer on the internet.

However, this is limited to the general public. In fact, any reasonably intelligent person wouldn't believe the descriptions from these online trolls.

It's purely a charade to fool idiots.

Because many of the rumors are fabricated, almost none of them are true. The most ridiculous thing is that in this era, even the killing of innocent people to claim credit has occurred.

Other achievements are one thing, but the key issue is that so many first-class merits—if it's about killing innocent people to claim credit, then everyone is being treated like fools.

Of course, the instigators of all this, the two lawyers with red circles, are actually under surveillance right now.

They haven't acted yet simply because it's not time to close the net.

The best time to prove everything and take action will be when the medal is officially awarded and Jiang Tian is publicly honored. But now is definitely not the time.

Si Qinghan, in fact, comes from a family with considerable influence, not just in terms of financial status. She's not stupid either.

The online smears and rumors are utter nonsense in her eyes; she's never believed them.

In fact, to prove the online rumors false, she specifically consulted her family, who were deeply wary of this police hero.

Furthermore, some revelations have revealed only the tip of the iceberg of Jiang Tian's achievements.

The deeper she learns, the more shocked and admiring Si Qinghan becomes.

She never imagined that one day she would even mock this police hero.

She thought he was trying to take advantage of her when he came to arrest a criminal.

The more Si Qinghan thought about it, the more she wanted to disappear into the ground.

"This is all to be expected. Next time you choose a boyfriend, be more careful. This man is a heartless criminal. To be honest, you're quite lucky he didn't commit a crime against you."

Jiang Tian glanced at her with a judgmental eye. Mainly, he felt that Chu Mo was trying to cover something up—no, even deliberately trying to distance himself from Si Qinghan.

Jiang Tian had just been at Yunya Group. Good heavens, no one expected that the two seemingly ruthless and notorious underworld bosses weren't the real leaders. The real mastermind was just a secretary, and a lowly secretary from Japan named Mitsui Miko at that.

Thank goodness.

Jiang Tian glanced at Si Qinghan; she was indeed innocent.

"No, no, no, we're not even boyfriend and girlfriend yet. I never imagined he could be such a heartless person." Si Qinghan hurriedly shook her head in denial, her face showing some anxiety.

"We heard everything. In short, I hope you'll behave yourself. Also, go back with the city police to give a statement. If you have any news about Chu Mo in the future, please report it immediately." Jiang Tian yawned.

There probably won't be a chance to make a move today.

As for the police officers guarding the outside, after Jiang Tian understood and observed Chu Mo's true strength, he had a general idea. Unless there were thousands of people forming a tight human wall without any loopholes, there was little chance of escape.

But now, it was simply impossible. Jiang Tian and Minister Tan had learned that the city police were led by Han Xian himself, with only five or six hundred armed police officers in total.

This number seemed large, but given the vast area of ​​Yunnan University, and its location in a somewhat remote, mountainous region, breaking through the encirclement would be incredibly easy.

He could easily escape.

Putting himself in Chu Mo's shoes, Jiang Tian had already been able to do this easily since the day he was reborn.

But Chu Mo...

His strength...

If he could stop him, Jiang Tian needed to reassess his abilities.



Chapter 679 Is Jiang Tian the only one in the entire Yunhai?


“Actually, you could have secretly followed him. You already have the remote control; it's not too late to act now.”

Lian Ying, drenched in cold sweat, finally breathed a sigh of relief, but the thought of such a terrifying criminal outside, and what if he appeared again…

A wave of fear washed over Lian Ying.

Jiang Tian looked at Lian Ying. Actually, Lian Ying wasn't very old; perhaps due to early marriage, she was around forty-five this year and already the president of Yun University.

This position was at a level where advancement was possible before retirement; she was a true young adult.

Her future was limitless. The only question was whether this recent incident would affect Lian Ying's career, since those next two steps were crucial.

“President Lian, you're thinking too simply. Do you think someone with this modus operandi wouldn't have taken precautions?” Jiang Tian shook his head with a chuckle.

“You mean, the remote control is fake? Then why did you…” Lian Ying's eyes widened.

"Who said it's fake? Even if it were real, couldn't there be two, or perhaps other triggers were broken?"

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

A strong interest rose within him.

To be honest, he had taken down so many criminals, but this was the first time he had encountered a criminal with such high intelligence as Chu Mo.

Besides his high intelligence, even his methods were unfathomable.

Of course, if we're talking about a worthy opponent, Chu Mo wasn't quite there.

But at least he could give Jiang Tian a slight sense of challenge.

This kind of opponent was somewhat interesting.

"I see, Xiao Jiang, thank goodness for you. Otherwise, I really don't know what I would have done. Last time at school, those students selling heroin... if it weren't for your decisive action, I don't know what the consequences would have been." Lian Ying's face was full of gratitude.

Truly, Jiang Tian had completely saved her political career twice!

This time, it was even students.

“Given Yunhai’s geographical location and circumstances, it’s impossible to completely avoid this situation. It’s very difficult to eradicate it entirely. Smuggling has always existed; it just hadn’t been exposed before,” Jiang Tian shook his head.

Lian Ying, having reached this point, understood perfectly well. He suddenly thought of something and said to Jiang Tian, ​​“By the way, aren’t you going to Beijing tomorrow? This might not be a bad thing. You could mention this situation to your superiors.”

Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow.

Lian Ying was right.

The problems were becoming increasingly apparent.

But…

The difficulty in dealing with Yunhai’s problems lay precisely in this.

If it were completely shut down, what future would Yunhai’s economic development have? Ultimately, it would follow the same path as in his previous life, with development completely halted.

But if it was left unattended…

The chaos next door had also contributed to the problems here.

Jiang Tian frowned.

“Let’s try our best and see if we can find a solution,” Jiang Tian sighed.

And it was during their conversation…

Outside the school.

At this moment, Han Xian was holding his phone, receiving a call from Minister Tan himself.

"How could Jiang Tian let such a terrifying criminal escape?" Han Xian was trying his best to suppress his emotions.

But after hearing the details, he couldn't help but raise his most stern question.

Regardless of personal relationships.

However, faced with this issue, Han Xian understood the gravity of the situation.

"How can you stop them? They've taken a student hostage and planted a bomb in the school. If Comrade Jiang Tian makes a move, the perpetrators will detonate the bomb in the school dormitory immediately. Can you bear the responsibility for the lives of over two thousand teachers and students?" Minister Tan said coldly.

Uh.

Han Xian was utterly astonished.

His pupils contracted rapidly.

At the same time, a flame seemed to burn in his eyes.

This.

The problem Jiang Tian couldn't solve, if he could solve it perfectly.

No.

Han Xian quickly realized that if he took action, the responsibility, if anything went wrong, his so-called political career would likely be over; he'd probably be in jail.

"Then, I'll have someone clear the way immediately."

Han Xian hurriedly said.

"No, you don't need to. You can proceed normally, with the people you brought," Minister Tan said.

Han Xian was even more dumbfounded: "Minister Tan, what if something goes wrong?"

Inwardly, Han Xian was about to curse.

Such a terrifying criminal, holding the safety of so many students in his hands.

How could he dare?

If anything went wrong, it wouldn't be as simple as his political career.

"The criminal gave the bomb remote control and other equipment to Jiang Tian, ​​but Comrade Jiang couldn't be sure that this was the only way to detonate it, so he didn't act. However, the criminal made a condition: as long as Comrade Jiang doesn't act, he won't detonate the bomb. Apart from Comrade Jiang, you can proceed with normal pursuit and interception, even killing him outright," Minister Tan said.

This condition.

Han Xian's face changed drastically.

An insult.

This was simply a blatant insult.

What does this mean?

It means...

In the other party's eyes, there is only Jiang Tian.

Besides Jiang Tian.

He doesn't believe anyone else poses a danger to him.

"Outrageous! This criminal is so audacious! Minister Tan, rest assured, I will bring him to justice."

Furious, Han Xian's initial anger subsided, replaced by a surge of fervor.

His blood was boiling.

An opportunity.

This is a tremendous opportunity.

In other words.

All it takes is arresting this criminal.

That would be equivalent to proving his abilities.

And solving this problem would be enough to prove his competence in front of his superiors.

"I understand your abilities. Comrade Jiang said the criminal is somewhat dangerous, so he hopes you will act cautiously to avoid unnecessary losses." Minister Tan trusted Jiang Tian's words implicitly.

Others saying it's dangerous is hard to say.

But Jiang Tian said the criminal is very strong.

That proves one thing: the criminal's fighting ability is terrifying and frightening.

At this moment, Han Xian's heart burned with anger. This was blatant disrespect.

Han Xian's face flushed red and then turned pale, signifying his utter incompetence.

Therefore, the leader had no hope whatsoever.

Was Jiang Tian the only one capable of getting things done in Yunhai?

After hanging up the phone, Han Xian was furious and immediately ordered, "Have everyone in the police force be on high alert and not give the criminals any chance to escape."



Chapter 680 I can't help but wonder if this is my only chance in this life.


The more those above don't believe in me, the more determined I'll be to prove them wrong and show everyone that Jiang Tian isn't the only capable person in Yunhai.

Han Xian isn't actually incompetent; before this, he had done a lot in his position, always striving to do better within his responsibilities.

Of course, this is a polarized phenomenon. Some people who want to climb the ladder work incredibly hard and perform exceptionally well.

They work diligently.

And this is especially true for those with promising futures. Others just coast along, ignoring everything and letting life go on without any ambition.

Han Xian belongs to the former group, which is why he's always been active.

Especially after coming to Yunhai—what was the point of all this fuss about coming here if not to work?

The thugs look down on them, even saying that only Jiang Tian has the qualifications and ability to arrest him.

They even said that their only condition for dealing with the situation was that Jiang Tian shouldn't make a move; everyone else could act at will.

Disdain.

It's one thing for the criminals to look down on him, but the key issue is that even the leaders look down on him, even saying that arresting him within their capabilities is sufficient, and there's no need to force it.

In fact, Minister Tan knows how terrifying the destructive power of someone with formidable combat strength and strong individual combat capabilities can be in open-world warfare.

The most obvious example is Jiang Tian.

However, Jiang Tian is more like a superhuman version of an invincible being, while Chu Mo is just a weakened version.

There is still a significant gap in their skill levels.

Even so, Chu Mo is actually a terrifyingly powerful soldier. What would be the consequences if he were to achieve such a feat?

Thinking about it this way, it's quite frightening.

Consider Jiang Tian's consistent individual combat capabilities; even weakened countless times, in the open world, no matter how many men are sent, they would be like cannon fodder.

And Minister Tan's concern, in Han Xian's eyes, seems more like looking down on him.

Just as Han Xian was preparing to learn more about the situation...

Suddenly, gunshots rang out in the quiet of the night.

Actually, according to the higher-ups, it's alright if he ran away; the most important thing now is to ensure the safety of the students in the dormitories.

Especially since bomb disposal experts from the provincial and municipal bureaus rushed over at top speed.

Besides them, school personnel were also arranged for the emergency evacuation of students, at least to the safe area on the playground.

Everything else is unimportant.

In this regard, as soon as Chu Mo left, Lian Ying immediately contacted all school leaders and teachers to urgently evacuate the students.

Time was of the essence.

Even Jiang Tian couldn't guarantee that Chu Mo's ruthlessness wouldn't lead him to break his promise and bomb the school again after leaving.

Of course, even if there were no bombs in the dormitories, just a threat wouldn't matter.

This doesn't necessarily mean Jiang Tian did anything wrong, because there is a high probability that this is the case, especially given that a bomb was planted in Shang Mingyan's car, and the perpetrator simply did it on a whim.

Who can guarantee the killer didn't have fewer bombs on him?

"Found the killer?" Han Xian jumped to his feet, his face beaming with excitement.

What's so great about him? How strong is his fighting ability?

He's just carrying a head on two shoulders. One bullet and he's dead.

He refused to believe that with so many people, finding the killer and failing to take him down was impossible.

Most importantly...

"If I can't seize this opportunity, I have to wonder if this is my only chance in this life."

"Chief, we found him! We found him! From the direction of Haihe Road, the killer was preparing to go up the mountain, and we've found him."

At that moment, his assistant rushed over.

"Good, good, well done!" Han Xian's eyes lit up. "Have everyone surround and eliminate him."

"Yes, sir."

After the assistant left, Han Xian stood there, his face filled with excitement and joy.

He could almost see the first-class merit waving at him.

Of course, the only regret was not receiving the highest credit for this case.

That meant finding the murderer, planting a bomb in the dormitory building, and so on. If he could step forward at the crucial moment and claim all the credit, Han Xian felt it would at least be worthy of a Special Merit.

Even a First-Class Merit would be satisfactory; at least it would prove that after the mission's success, he could demonstrate to all the leaders that his abilities were in no way inferior to Jiang Tian's.

The more he thought about it,

the brighter the smile on Han Xian's face became.

But he didn't know that at this very moment,

not far from them, in the direction of Haihe Road, which was mentioned earlier, was an escape route that Chu Mo had researched from the beginning. In other words, a high-IQ criminal had prepared his escape route from the start.

Especially since he had prepared ammunition, weapons, and other equipment.

So when he encountered an interception, the ruthless Chu Mo acted immediately.

Strike first to gain the upper hand.

Especially after realizing he was being surrounded, he showed no mercy.

It was a single exchange.

Several people have already lost their lives.

At the time the gunshots rang out...

Jiang Tian was still in the dormitory building. Outside, in the pitch black, students, under the guidance of teachers and school leaders, had successfully left the building.

Meanwhile, at this moment, bomb disposal experts, dispatched via an urgent dispatch, arrived.

Because the situation was so serious, two bomb disposal experts were directly borrowed from the provincial department, and one from the Yunhai City Bureau. All three experts arrived at the scene.

The lead bomb disposal expert was a man around forty years old, with a square face. He gave off a very reliable impression, and indeed, after a brief conversation, Jiang Tian also felt he was very dependable.

"Thank you for your hard work. This is a very dangerous mission. I hope you can do your best to ensure your safety. If the difficulty becomes too great, your safety should be your priority," Jiang Tian said.

Although Jiang Tian is now very capable...

However, in the end, everyone has their own expertise. Jiang Tian may seem to be able to do everything, such as shooting people, investigating cases, etc., but for things like bombs, the most professional personnel are still needed.

“Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​I know your name. In short, we both serve the people. The danger I face is incomparable to that of you frontline personnel.” The square-faced bomb disposal expert from the provincial department, understanding the seriousness of the situation, immediately nodded in response.

Jiang Tian also nodded slightly.

Watching the bomb disposal expert enter the dormitory building, carrying some technological equipment to excavate and inspect the bomb, Jiang Tian frowned and asked, “How long has it been?”

“Ten minutes.”

A lot has happened in those ten minutes, Jiang Tian thought, narrowing his eyes.



Chapter 681 The Bomb Incident Escalates Further


Shang Mingyan knew what time Jiang Tian was asking about: ten minutes, the time Jiang Tian and Chu Mo had agreed upon.

During this time, Jiang Tian didn't see Jiang Xiaoyu, but he did see Lian Xin. Lian Xin was also surprised and delighted to see Jiang Tian.

"Brother Jiang."

Lian Xin happily raised her hand to greet Jiang Tian.

Mainly, the students didn't know what had happened, and besides, whether it was true or a threat, it could never be exposed.

Especially since Minister Tan had called immediately, repeatedly instructing them to keep this matter strictly confidential.

To avoid causing huge panic and public opinion, Yunda and Yunhai were already in the spotlight. If something like this happened, the subsequent chaos and public opinion would be terrifying, especially before the matter was resolved.

If the matter was resolved perfectly, this issue could be slightly publicized.

This would indirectly demonstrate Jiang Tian's contributions and the extent and magnitude of the incident, thus silencing the overwhelming public opinion online.

"Brother Jiang, what brings you here?" Lian Xin approached Jiang Tian excitedly, but after a moment, her face suddenly filled with suspicion.

Nothing much.

It seemed that every time Jiang Tian appeared at the school, nothing good came of it. The two times he'd been there, it was as if a gunfight had broken out.

Sometimes, Lian Xin even wondered if she lived in an oil-producing region.

"Nothing much, don't worry, just a small matter," Jiang Tian smiled. It was best not to let Lian Xin know about such dangerous things.

"Oh, that's good. Brother Jiang, are you looking for Xiao Yu?" Lian Xin felt relieved; after all, she trusted Brother Jiang's words.

"Just dealing with a small matter."

Jiang Tian was speaking when Shang Mingyan's voice suddenly came.

"I have something to do. You stay here for now, don't wander around." Jiang Tian squinted at Lian Xin.

It has to be said that Jiang Tian was quite surprised by Lian Xin. Without the events of his previous life, the Lian Xin he knew was terrifyingly different from the Lian Xin he remembered.

After all, in his past life, this girl was a terrifying queen in the legal world.

Moreover, it was said that she even had a formidable network of connections in international lawsuits.

When they met in his past life, she was the epitome of a powerful female CEO.

But after being reborn, Jiang Tian suddenly realized that the CEO persona was gone, and this girl had become... well, you know.

She's becoming more and more affectionate and clingy.

Where's the powerful CEO he remembered?

"Don't worry, Brother Jiang, I'll be the most obedient," Lian Xin waved her hands.

Jiang Tian smiled and nodded before leaving.

Only after Jiang Tian left did Lian Xin let out a deep breath.

The smile on her face faded slightly, and she watched Jiang Tian leave with a worried look in her eyes.

Although she acted that way, the gunshots outside were so loud; Lian Xin wasn't deaf, how could she not hear them?

"Lian Xin, who was that boy?"

Just as Lian Xin was looking somewhat reluctant to leave after Jiang Tian left, a voice suddenly came from behind her.

Lian Xin frowned, looking at the boy who appeared behind her. His hair was slicked back, and he was wearing a suit. At first glance, she might have mistaken him for a school leader, but his youthful demeanor made him seem somewhat out of place.

"Cheng Yang, what's it to you?"

Lian Xin was already worried, and this man's appearance only worsened her already displeased mood, making her face even more clouded.

Then her brows furrowed deeply.

Her previously warm and lovely expression vanished instantly, replaced by a look increasingly similar to the domineering CEO Jiang Tian remembered.

His cold, condescending rebuke was something Jiang Tian would definitely applaud if he were here, because this was exactly the woman he remembered.

"I... I was just asking what happened. As the student council president, I have the right to worry about your safety, both personally and personally. Besides, as a senior, it's normal to care for a junior."

Cheng Yang spoke with a gentle expression of concern: "Besides, that person is a stranger, doesn't seem like a good person at all. I'm just worried about you."

Ugh!!!

Lian Xin really wanted to spit at him, but then she thought, if she did, he'd probably think it was a reward, so she suppressed her anger.

A cold smile appeared on her face.

She knew this person very well.

Not many people at the school knew Lian Xin's background.

But this student council president, Cheng, definitely didn't. After accidentally learning that her father was the principal, he began pursuing her.

On the other hand, this person seemed to have some connections and was determined to enter the system. Becoming student council president was his way of securing a position within the system.

Of course, this starting point already surpassed 90% of people; it was very high.

He was so outstanding, Cheng Du believed he was completely worthy of Lian Xin. If he married her, he'd even have political capital.

His future prospects were limitless.

But Lian Xin wasn't exactly intelligent; how could she not know his thoughts?

"Then I should thank you," Lian Xin said, growing increasingly impatient.

So smug about a little achievement?

Lian Xin almost slapped him. This guy dared to question Jiang Tian's background?

Jiang Tian was also a senior, and he was already a second-level police inspector.

You're also a senior, but instead of doing an internship, you spend all your time chasing girls on campus.

What a piece of trash.

Lian Xin ignored him.

But Cheng Yang pressed his advantage: "Lian Xin, look, the sound outside, that's definitely gunfire. The Yunhai police, huh? That's all they're capable of. Wait till I become a leader, I'll definitely clean them up one by one. All the incompetent ones will be purged from the team. Those bad apples, I won't tolerate them. The school is already this chaotic, you can't even imagine what it's like outside. I'll definitely change the landscape of Yunhai."

Lian Xin was stunned.

Is this guy crazy?

Clean them up?

Look at yourself in the mirror, see what you're really made of.

Others might not know, but she knew, and had experienced it firsthand more than once.

How many times had Jiang Tian risked his life to protect the people? And this person was still slandering him behind his back?

If someone like that became a leader, how could anyone live a normal life?

"You know the kind of police you call incompetent? My best friend's brother is one of them. He works all night every day catching criminals, sometimes going several nights in a row without sleep. Wait until you can work three days straight without sleep before you say that. You're not worthy yet." Lian Xin, with her strong independent personality, spoke with sarcasm, making Cheng Yang's face turn red.

"I..."

"What 'I'? He's out there with a gun catching criminals. What, if I gave you a gun, would you dare fight him?" Lian Xin crossed her arms and looked down at him condescendingly.

She had been holding back for a long time.

This anger, which she had been holding for so long, was finally released.

She felt a sense of relief, but looking in the direction Jiang Tian's figure disappeared, her heart was heavy with worry.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian, ​​whom she was worried about, was outside the dormitory building.

"How did it go?" Jiang Tian asked, facing the bomb disposal expert.

"Found it! Three bundles of TNT were found in the load-bearing column on the first floor. Enough to collapse the entire dormitory building. Both dorm buildings are like that."

Hiss!!!

Is this true?

"But..."



Chapter 682 You have absolutely no skills, yet you want to do anything?


*Hiss!*

Shang Mingyan gasped, his pupils contracting rapidly, then abruptly turned to look at Jiang Tian.

Terrifying?

This was undoubtedly terrifying.

What would be the consequences if it exploded?

Shang Mingyan dared not even imagine. If two thousand teachers and students died here, in this dormitory building, the shockwaves across the country, even the world, would be devastating.

This was the most terrifying event in recent years, bar none.

Many probably wouldn't even dare to think about it. The most terrifying thing was that it was placed on a load-bearing pillar. If it exploded, both buildings would collapse instantly. Even if some lucky people survived, the consequences would undoubtedly be dire, with 80-90% of the population dying.

This was no joke.

The most terrifying thing was that Jiang Tian had anticipated this beforehand, or rather, before the investigation, there was absolutely no trace of it.

This could be interpreted as a desperate lie told by the perpetrator, or it could be highly likely a fabrication, as few would believe such a horrific story.

However, Jiang Tian seemed remarkably resolute in this matter, as if he had anticipated the presence of the bomb from the outset.

"How long will it take to disarm?" Jiang Tian inquired.

"At least half an hour, almost an hour," the square-faced bomb disposal expert said after a moment's thought, offering a conservative estimate.

Before anyone could speak, the expert continued, "The bomb's structure is extremely complex, especially the detonator, which has five dotted lines. Cutting any one of them incorrectly will cause an explosion."

Shang Mingyan remained silent.

Jiang Tian's expression remained unchanged. In fact, the reason he had given Chu Mo an hour, and even nodded in agreement, was precisely because of this.

He had also estimated that it would take at least an hour for the bomb disposal experts to arrive and resolve the situation. During this time, Jiang Tian couldn't act. Even if the teachers and students had already escaped, the consequences of a bomb explosion were unpredictable.

Conversely, even if the bomb didn't exist and everything was resolved,

Chu Mo wouldn't get far in ten or twenty minutes if he wanted to escape. Without the bomb, ten or twenty minutes would be enough for Jiang Tian to catch up and eliminate him.

In short,

Chu Mo might seem powerful, even invincible, to ordinary people, or even to those with professional training, but compared to Jiang Tian, ​​who was not human, he was simply no match.

One minute was the most Jiang Tian had given him.

And that was within the time it would take to draw his gun and fire at a greater distance.

Of course,

the system's attributes were also powerful, but ultimately, they didn't transcend the human realm.

In other words, Jiang Tian had reached the theoretical limit of human potential, and his genome had even undergone preliminary alterations. He was still several times stronger than the absolute limits of human capability, but a single bullet to the head could still kill him.

Within a few meters, Jiang Tian could eliminate Chu Mo instantly, but Chu Mo remained at a distance of over ten meters, armed with a weapon. Eliminating him would take at least a minute or two, and in that time, who could guarantee that the other wouldn't detonate a bomb?

So, Jiang Tian had a two-pronged approach: if the bomb was real, it would be easily dealt with.

If the bomb was fake,

that was even simpler; Jiang Tian could easily catch up before the attackers got too far.

“Are you in danger?” Jiang Tian asked.

“There’s a chance, but it’s very small. With our solid professional knowledge, we can usually disarm it successfully. Besides, the bomb can’t be moved; it has an internal level. Even the slightest vibration would detonate the detonator,” the bomb disposal expert sighed.

Jiang Tian wasn’t surprised at all by this situation; it just goes to show that high-IQ crimes are entirely plausible.

Just then…

Outside the school, gunfire erupted.

“They’ve exchanged fire,” Shang Mingyan said.

“Hmm, who’s there?” Jiang Tian frowned.

In fact, Jiang Tian had already told Minister Tan that there was no need to stop him unless absolutely necessary. He knew the dangerous nature of Chu Mo; if he took action, the consequences would be unimaginable, and the number of casualties would be incalculable.

“Director Han,” Shang Mingyan said with a strange expression, “Director Han just called me. He probably knows you're at the school, so he didn't come to the school to investigate. He just asked me about the suspect's physical characteristics and other details.”

That's right, Director Han had indeed called Shang Mingyan to inquire about the situation.

Shang Mingyan naturally couldn't hide anything. Regardless of Han Xian's relationship with Jiang Tian, ​​or what conflicts they might have, personal grudges were irrelevant in this matter.

However…

“Didn't I tell you to try not to take action?” Jiang Tian frowned.

Without him taking action, or rather, without the military present, even if many people came, they would be easy prey.

They posed no threat whatsoever.

Especially in the dead of night.

"I've said all that, but I think Director Han is trying to outmaneuver you. He's practically desperate on this matter; how could he possibly stay put?" Shang Mingyan chuckled.

That's right.

Han Xian is practically in dire straits.

Everyone knows that Han Xian's term as director is nearing its end.

The fact that he hasn't stopped is a final favor from above.

But once Han Xian leaves, it can be said that his political career is over. His future can only be described as retirement; climbing the ranks is impossible.

Unless, during this period, Han Xian finds a lifeline.

Even if Minister Tan is transferred, it won't be easy, because if Han Xian received a first-class merit, it means that Han Xian still has ability.

Why was he let go, or rather, why wasn't he hired?

That would leave ammunition for gossip.

"Foolish," Jiang Tian's face turned cold.

Jiang Tian wouldn't be happy if Han Xian fell into misfortune.

If Han Xian truly had the ability to capture him, Jiang Tian wouldn't say anything, he might even applaud and celebrate. Jiang Tian is clear about right and wrong on matters of principle.

But...

What can you do by going up there like this, besides adding to the innocent losses?

Every one who sacrificed their life was a comrade!

"But doing nothing wouldn't be right either," Shang Mingyan said cautiously.

That's true.

Doing nothing and watching them leave—Minister Tan himself couldn't bring himself to say that. At most, he could only advise Han Xian to prioritize the safety of his own personnel.

It must be said that, regardless of Han Xian's character, Minister Tan had considerable trust in his abilities, given their past experiences.

But that definitely didn't apply to Yunhai.

Or rather, being able to oversee all of Yunhai's security was like sending a somewhat naive and capable second-generation rich kid to Yunhai, whose connections were unclear, and expecting him to do the work.



Chapter 683 Shifting Responsibility, Old Han Cried: You're All Targeting Me


The current prosperity of Yunhai, or rather, the gradual disappearance of dark forces, stems from two main reasons. First, Jiang Peng, as bureau chief, was incompetent and negligent.

In Yunhai, the more you do, the more mistakes you make. The connections are intricate and intertwined; any small action will inevitably lead to a chain reaction of problems.

Chaos erupted instantly.

The second reason is that while Jiang Peng remained inactive, Jiang Tian, ​​operating from below, implemented a series of swift and decisive measures, wiping out entire groups of enemies at once.

"Chu Mo's methods are formidable. You might not grasp the full extent of this, but let me tell you, when the Tucha conspiracy occurred, I killed over two hundred armed men with terrifying marksmanship. Chu Mo's marksmanship is perhaps only slightly inferior to mine back then."

There's something else Jiang Tian didn't mention: Chu Mo's physical abilities far surpassed his own at that time. Back then, Jiang Tian only possessed the golden attribute of "unfailing accuracy," but lacked attributes like physical prowess.

“Even if I were to do it myself, it would take considerable effort in this desolate wilderness. Han Xian leading his men to chase after them would be utterly useless, like delivering vegetables to the enemy.” Jiang Tian sighed.

This…

Shang Mingyan was stunned.

Is it really that terrifying?

However…

Jiang Tian’s first appearance in a case was no secret; many people knew it was his first mission.

It was a terrifyingly important mission.

In that mission, Tu Cha led hundreds of armed men, even launching a counter-encirclement, and most importantly, Gao Xiaolong was even inside the encirclement.

If they had failed, the consequences would have been unimaginable.

But back then, Jiang Tian single-handedly took down over two hundred armed men.

And from then on, Jiang Tian’s rise was meteoric.

This Chu Mo is no less capable than Jiang Tian back then.

The key is…

The bomb disposal expert, who was about to leave, paused upon hearing Jiang Tian’s words, his lips twitching violently.

Two hundred-plus people, taking down two hundred in one night—and this guy said it so casually.

The bomb disposal expert swallowed hard, then hurried away.

"Garlic Bird, Garlic Bird," this level of frontline combat was beyond their capabilities. They were too monstrous, no, they were inhuman.

Damn it.

The bomb disposal expert even wondered if he was outdated. Had people outside become this monstrous? One person fighting hundreds, and they could talk about it so casually.

These frontline combat personnel were terrifying.

"I'll make them stop now."

Shang Mingyan took out his phone.

He immediately dialed Han Xian's number.

At first, Shang Mingyan hadn't grasped the seriousness of the situation, but now, after hearing Jiang Tian's words, he was completely numb.

His hands and feet were ice-cold.

Faced with an opponent of this strength, Jiang Tian was certain that if a large-scale encirclement and suppression operation were carried out by the military, the chances of success would be quite high.

However, these armed police special forces and the like, numbering three or four hundred, would just be easy prey.

Of course, it wasn't that they were completely powerless to resist.

Mainly, in the complex environment, facing someone with such overwhelming individual combat abilities, they were utterly defenseless.

As expected.

Just as Shang Mingyan was making the phone call.

Inside the police car at the school gate, Han Xian's face was deathly pale, his breathing rapid.

But his eyes were already red.

"So many people, why can't you catch even one person? What are you doing?"

Han Xian had become like a gambler who was about to lose everything, roaring with bloodshot eyes.

He was enraged.

He was furious.

The confrontation had lasted less than ten minutes.

So far.

Not to mention whether anyone had been caught.

Even how many of his own men had been sacrificed?

At this moment, the leader of the armed police, Old Chen, had been working in Yunhai for many years.

He had even cooperated with Jiang Tian's operations many times, and the two sides had a great deal of tacit understanding.

Of course, the most important point is that Old Chen had never felt so triumphant before.

Mainly because following Jiang Tian was truly exhilarating.

Firstly, major cases came up every few days.

Besides, taking action was incredibly easy. No matter how difficult the task, Jiang Tian could handle it. When they arrived, they mostly just cleaned up the mess.

Even when they were required to take action, there were rarely any particularly difficult tasks.

The key was the sheer amount of credit they received.

Following Jiang Tian, ​​they had already accumulated several collective merits.

Their enthusiasm for coordinated operations was extremely high.

But today was different.

This Director Han had gone mad.

"Director Han, I hope you can watch your words. Our comrades are risking their lives to apprehend the criminal, but you haven't even understood the criminal's level of danger or the difficulty of the capture. You sent us in recklessly, and now seven or eight of our armed police officers have already sacrificed their lives. You need to give us an explanation."

Old Chen's face darkened. He always treated others with utmost respect.

You, Han, have come to Yunhai without achieving anything, without any merit, yet you dare to boss us around.

Most importantly...

Old Chen was also shocked!

The killer's danger was terrifying.

"This killer is an absolutely professional and terrifying individual. Whether it's investigation or counter-investigation, they have undergone the most rigorous training. But before the operation, you only said it was an ordinary criminal, and you directed the entire operation. You need to be responsible for our fallen comrades." Old Chen was seething with anger.

We worked so hard to capture them, even at the cost of so many lives.

And all we got in return was your accusations.

If it were Jiang Tian...

They only needed to keep watch on the perimeter. The moment the criminals appeared, they'd probably be dead by Comrade Jiang.

The key is that letting them go up was the right thing to do, but you should have clearly stated the level of danger the criminals posed so they could prepare in advance.

No preparation whatsoever, and so many people have been sacrificed.

And you're standing there criticizing them?

"Comrade, what kind of attitude is this? It's our duty to protect the people and apprehend criminals. These criminals are dangerous; are we just going to stand by and watch them escape?"

Han Xian suppressed his anger.

But his mood grew increasingly heavy.

"The criminals have already broken through and completely disappeared into the mountains. Trying to capture them, given their terrifying investigative abilities, is like looking for a needle in a haystack," Old Chen said expressionlessly.

Continuing the manhunt would only increase casualties.

Bang!!!

Han Xian stood up angrily and shouted, "Are you shirking responsibility? I just wanted to see if the sky over Yunhai was black!"

Old Chen was stunned, thinking to himself, "Are you crazy?"



Chapter 684 I told you it was dangerous, but you still went. If you don't take responsibility, who will?


"Shifting blame? Let me tell you, you're shifting blame. Don't think I don't know. When Jiang Tian asked you to assist, no matter how many criminals there were—hundreds or thousands—you managed to keep every single one from escaping. I asked you to assist just to arrest one criminal, and you've sacrificed so many lives. If this isn't intentional, what is it?"

He's lost his mind.

Normally, Director Han wouldn't have said that. Anyone with reason wouldn't say such a thing. But the prerequisite is that you still have reason. Clearly, Director Han has completely lost his mind.

The main problem is the unbearable consequences: the loss of seven lives, and the criminals escaped.

This mistake can only be attributed to the criminals being too strong, which is understandable. But the fact is, Minister Tan had already warned him beforehand, yet he didn't take it seriously at all.

Protecting the lives of your own people was your top priority, and you still caused so many losses.

This further demonstrates Director Han's incompetence.

What was supposed to be a chance to turn things around has now turned into a deeper and deeper mess.

His only chance wasn't just gone; it was perhaps his only chance to turn his life around, and it was gone, even worse than before.

Thinking about it this way,

Director Han couldn't sit still.

Putting himself in his shoes, it was understandable. Old Chen had cooperated with Jiang Tian so many times, arresting so many people without a single mistake. And yet, this time, he couldn't even cooperate? Wasn't this targeting him?

But Old Chen was also wronged!

"Director Han, you're mistaken. You can say I'm not capable enough, you can insult me, but you absolutely cannot insult our professional ethics. The seven heroes who sacrificed their lives are not for you to insult. And compared to Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​when he asked us to cooperate, it was always to assist him. Most of the time, he had already apprehended the criminals, and we had to clean up the mess ourselves. For example, in the attack on Wen Yulin of the Wenye Group on the outer ring road, Comrade Jiang Tian single-handedly wiped out fifty or sixty fully armed criminals. Can you do that? We can clean up the mess for you too."

Old Chen showed no mercy.

This was giving him too much face.

Although Old Chen might be slightly lower in rank, the two weren't even in the same system. Director Han could request him to assist in operations, but he wasn't Old Chen's direct superior and had no jurisdiction over him.

Old Chen, of course, wouldn't swallow his anger. Besides, tonight's operation had been incredibly frustrating; so many brothers had sacrificed themselves, and yet Han Xian blamed them for not doing their best, as if it were intentional, making sarcastic remarks. Old Chen was furious, but thankfully he maintained his composure and didn't openly escalate the conflict.

However, these words were tantamount to directly insulting Director Han.

It was like rubbing salt in the wound, touching on a sore spot.

Besides everything else, Han Xian already had a deep-seated grudge against Jiang Tian. He could criticize anyone else, but Jiang Tian was a red line in Han Xian's heart.

It was the kind of thing that would set him off at the slightest provocation.

And then...

Han Xian, already on the verge of exploding, completely lost his composure.

It exploded.

He was furious.

Thankfully, just as Han Xian was about to lose his temper and speak irrationally, a phone call instantly brought him back to his senses.

Han Xian's anger dissipated in an instant.

Titling at Old Chen was pointless.

They were here to cooperate.

The primary responsibility still lay with Han Xian; it was an inescapable responsibility. And no matter where he went to say it, Han Xian couldn't say it was intentional. What about the seven fallen heroes?

If he said that, he'd probably explode on the spot.

So, even after calming down, Han Xian was sweating profusely. The news he'd just received had left him somewhat desperate, causing him to lose his composure and say irrational things.

In the system, speaking is a complex art; sometimes, a single sentence can affect your political career.

One wrong word can have terrifying consequences.

Moreover, there's a saying that's quite true: top-tier talent stays in the system, second-tier talent becomes business owners.

While not absolute, it certainly represents the majority.

“Shang Mingyan.”

Upon seeing the incoming call, Han Xian's expression shifted several times.

To be honest,

Shang Mingyan was undoubtedly the most dazzling flower in the police system.

Of course, to judge Shang Mingyan by her appearance would be an insult. Yes, Shang Mingyan was beautiful, but compared to her other virtues, her stunning beauty and charisma were actually her least outstanding qualities.

Similarly, Han Xian was in his thirties, and so was Shang Mingyan. In other words, how could Han Xian not be attracted?

It was just a pity that although they had met before in Beijing, Shang Mingyan had never given him any chance.

Less than three seconds after the initial spark ignited, Shang Mingyan had already decisively stated it was impossible, showing absolutely no consideration.

Then Han Xian came to Yunhai, partly because he hoped to have a chance to work with Shang Mingyan and advance his career.

But now...

Han Xian looked at the scene and fell silent.

This time, if they had succeeded, there would have been a chance to turn things around, but now he knew it was completely over. Everything was over.

Jiang Tian was leaving for Beijing tomorrow. Similarly, he wasn't just leaving; he was leaving with his future and his political career.

In other words, Jiang Tian's departure also heralded a new era for Yunhai.

Of course, Han Xian genuinely didn't know about the relationship between Jiang Tian and Shang Mingyan. If he did, he'd probably be spitting blood.

"What happened? What was the result of your confrontation? I just said the murderer was dangerous. How did you handle it?"

What? Han Xian is the bureau chief?

What a joke.

This matter has absolutely nothing to do with Shang Mingyan.

Shang Mingyan is a legitimate leader of the General Bureau.

In terms of both position and future prospects, the two were not on the same level. The key difference was that Shang Mingyan had reached his current position entirely through his own abilities—abilities that were absolutely irreplaceable and unmatched.

It was a leading position in the field of criminal investigation, a level that transcended an era.

Of course, that was before Jiang Tian appeared.

Today, Shang Mingyan realized he was a frog in a well.

Because she discovered that the true level of superiority lay with Jiang Tian—his crime-solving abilities and methods were something Shang Mingyan, even watching the entire process unfold, couldn't fully comprehend.

Good heavens! He was just starting to analyze a few clues when, in the next instant, he'd already identified the culprit. Regardless of how he did it, he knew exactly who the killer was. It was utterly incomprehensible.

"I..."



Chapter 685 A collective sense of lingering fear, a collective uproar.


Shang Mingyan's barrage of questions left Han Xian stammering, unsure how to answer.

Could it be that the murderer escaped, and more importantly, seven or eight people were killed?

"The murderer escaped." Shang Mingyan instantly understood.

"Yes."

Han Xian answered after a long silence.

"You took action, didn't you?"

Judging from Han Xian's attitude, Shang Mingyan had already guessed what was going on. Without giving Han Xian a chance to speak, he directly asked, "How many casualties were there?"

"Seven."

Han Xian answered, forcing a smile.

"Han Xian, what kind of work are you doing? Minister Tan must have told you, and I've told you repeatedly, Comrade Jiang Tian has already assessed the situation. The murderer is extremely dangerous, with very strong combat capabilities, especially high individual combat skills. How could you not have made any preparations?"

Shang Mingyan immediately rebuked him.

Jiang Tian.

It's Jiang Tian again.

Han Xian's eyes instantly turned red.

Damn it.

These people only had eyes for Jiang Tian. Did Jiang Tian's statement about danger necessarily mean danger? Okay, Han Xian admitted the murderer was indeed dangerous.

But why did they believe everything Jiang Tian said? Why couldn't they trust that he could catch the murderer?

Your distrust of him is the root of this problem.

Just as Han Xian was pondering this, his gaze shifted, and he suddenly noticed flashing lights in the distance. A convoy of police cars and special agents were speeding towards them.

Especially the convoy, which stopped directly in front of Han Xian.

When the car door opened, Minister Tan stepped out.

His face was expressionless.

That's right, the problem was too serious. After holding an emergency meeting, Minister Tan arrived as quickly as possible.

But unfortunately...

Actually, Minister Tan hadn't wasted much time at the Municipal Public Security Bureau. Those leaders had also arrived at the Municipal Public Security Bureau immediately to understand the situation. After all, who wouldn't be afraid?

"Don't they know what kind of case this is? Good heavens, this is a huge bombshell! Yunda University is wiped out, the entire police system from top to bottom has been dismantled, and Yunhai is definitely not going to escape either, they're practically the ones in charge."

"They'll all be going straight into private cells to await investigation. Whether they get out or not is another matter, let alone whether they can keep their positions."

"Thankfully, the only good news is that Jiang Tian is at the scene."

"Actually, no matter what, Jiang Tian has given many people the impression that, no matter how big the matter, as long as Jiang Tian is there, as long as Jiang Tian is involved, he gives people a strong and stable sense of security."

"Even so, Minister Tan arrived at Yunda University as soon as he finished explaining the situation, no, even while the other leaders were still at the Municipal Bureau."

"The murderer escaped?"

"Minister Tan asked expressionlessly."

"Minister Tan showed no surprise at this result."

"He wasn't angry either."

"After all, Jiang Tian had previously said that he had strong individual combat capabilities."

To be honest, this was the first criminal Jiang Tian had ever encountered who could elicit such a statement.

"How many people were killed? Comrade Chen, you tell us," Minister Tan inquired.

These people are all old foxes, and incredibly shrewd individuals.

Especially Minister Tan's meaningful gaze, or rather, it seemed he knew from the beginning that Han Xian wouldn't obediently comply and would definitely take action.

But Minister Tan didn't stop him.

Regardless, apprehending the murderer was a matter of duty.

It was a responsibility.

Letting Han Xian act, to some extent, if successful, would be a win-win situation; the disaster would be averted, the murderer apprehended.

The whole incident would be perfectly resolved.

As for whether Han Xian could retain his position, that could be handled flexibly later.

It could also be given an opportunity to prove himself.

"Seven comrades have sacrificed their lives," Old Chen said hastily, saluting.

"The main problem is the killer's investigative and counter-investigative abilities, as well as his individual combat skills. They're all too superhuman. Even when dozens of us surrounded him, he could instantly kill several of us and break through. If we continued the fight, it wouldn't just be a matter of losing ten or eight comrades."

Old Chen was straightforward; he spoke his mind. The killer was incredibly powerful, possessing almost superhuman combat abilities.

Old Chen even felt that he was stronger than some of the military's most elite soldiers.

During the daytime was a little better, but in complex combat environments, for someone like him, it was like a dragon entering the sea—they had no power to resist.

"Okay, I understand, Director Han. Clean up all the loose ends of this incident."

"Especially what happened tonight. We need to give the students an explanation and prevent rumors from spreading and causing unnecessary panic. Can you do that?"

Originally, Minister Tan thought that handing this case over to Jiang Tian was the most suitable, or at least Shang Mingyan.

But there was no other way.

Jiang Tian himself already had a mountain of unresolved cases to deal with, and he had to hand all of these matters over to Shang Mingyan. Jiang Tian also had to go to the capital tomorrow, and this matter was of utmost importance and could not be delayed.

"Yes."

Han Xian stammered.

It's over.

Han Xian had only one thought: it's over.

Sometimes, the most terrifying thing is that it's easier to just yell at him; a scolding would make things much simpler. But Minister Tan didn't even mention him.

What did this mean? It meant that Minister Tan didn't think he'd have any future opportunities here.

Or rather, he simply didn't care about him anymore.

Ignoring someone is often more terrifying than verbal abuse.

Minister Tan glanced at Han Xian, said nothing, and turned to leave.

Mainly because Jiang Tian called on the way; the bomb had been found.

The nature of the matter had completely changed.

A simple hostage situation wouldn't be a big deal.

Even lying and intimidation wouldn't be a big deal.

But if you actually planted a bomb, the nature of the problem is completely different. Two dormitory buildings—if they exploded, especially while the students were resting, the consequences were unimaginable.

Even though Jiang Tian knew the truth, he remained outwardly calm.

But a burning fear and rage simmered within him.

It also served as a wake-up call for Jiang Tian: he had been somewhat careless lately, perhaps due to overconfidence in his abilities.

Coincidentally...

When Minister Tan arrived, the bomb disposal expert, drenched in sweat, had just carried out the high explosives from the boys' dormitory.

"Danger!" Minister Tan's secretary was terrified.

Good heavens.

Even though you're wearing a bomb suit and holding a bomb, who can guarantee it won't detonate at any moment?

"Danger what? Get out of the way, the bomb's been defused." Minister Tan's face froze, cold sweat pouring down his back, his heart trembling with lingering fear.

If that bomb had exploded...

Minister Tan felt a chill run down his spine.

His gaze towards Jiang Tian changed completely.

A savior.

This is his damn savior.



Chapter 686 Look at Xiao Jiang, then look at you all


It's blown up.

This incident has completely exploded.

The explosion was far greater than Jiang Tian had anticipated; in fact, it's the kind of thing that could shatter the heavens.

What, you want to stop this incident, prevent it from escalating, or even suppress it?

Sorry.

Who would dare do that? Other problems in Yunhai can be suppressed, but this incident—who dares to intervene, who dares to speak up, who dares to make decisions? And if any mishap occurs, let's just say:

Everyone.

Anyone who dares to act, and then fails or causes problems, will face absolutely devastating consequences.

Forget about getting a private room; you'll be sent to the execution ground as quickly as possible.

No matter what your leadership position or rank.

Therefore.

After Minister Tan witnessed the bomb being defused and its components examined, reaching a definitive conclusion that the perpetrator was fully capable of detonating it,

Minister Tan took Jiang Tian back to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Of course, although Jiang Tian had other commitments the next day, the situation was extremely serious. Even knowing Jiang Tian's commitments and having worked tirelessly for several days without rest, Minister Tan had no choice but to reluctantly call him in.

Although…

Minister Tan himself felt he had gone too far.

"Comrade Jiang, you have to understand us. This case is extremely important. We all know you need rest, and you have a lot to do tomorrow, including a trip to Beijing, but…" Minister Tan hesitated, his words trailing off.

Jiang Tian raised his hand to stop Minister Tan from continuing, because there was no point in continuing.

"I understand how important this case is, but it's ultimately about serving the people, so I know what's important. It's alright, I can rest in Beijing too."

Jiang Tian's lips twitched. He glanced at the time. Good heavens, it was already past 2 AM by the time the cleanup was finished, and his flight was scheduled to depart from Yunhai Airport at 6 AM.

According to the bigwigs in Beijing, all the candidates had already arrived, some even a week in advance, and some had already started rehearsing.

After all, it would be on the news, during prime time, a live broadcast to the whole country at 7 PM.

Therefore, there couldn't be any mistakes.

Jiang Tian would arrive tomorrow, and the bigwigs probably wouldn't even have time to give him instructions. His speech that evening, and some interviews, he would have to handle things as they came.

However, according to Jiang Tian's estimation, or rather, it was certain that tomorrow would be a day of explosive media attention.
