Sometimes, luck really is a mysterious thing; some events can become incredibly popular for no apparent reason.

Similar to the Jiang Tian incident, in essence, it was just two lawyers in red circles who disliked Jiang Tian. They hired some people to find something out of Jiang Tian's actions, then extracted inconsistencies, took things out of context, and posted it online, finally hiring online trolls to generate buzz.

Normally, this would only have remained within a small circle.

It wouldn't have involved many people.

In fact, it might have simply faded away after everyone stopped paying attention. But fate is strange; what seems like a series of coincidences actually has a clear underlying pattern. No one expected that while there might not be many people who loved Jiang Tian, ​​there were far too many who hated him and wanted to see him fall.

So many that even Jiang Tian himself didn't know how many enemies he had.

Some people, after offending someone, can quickly identify and target their revenge. But Jiang Tian, ​​even if he were retaliated against, wouldn't even know which vengeful hero had done it.

In short, the actions of those two individuals, marked with red circles, instantly enraged countless criminals who hated Jiang Tian and wished him dead. They were all outraged.

Immediately, they even spent money and connections to get this news trending. Not only that, they created successive versions of the story, fabricating accusations and slanders. Eventually, they even began to question the legitimacy of Jiang Tian's achievements, raising reasonable suspicions that he was a second-generation heir who had murdered innocent people and claimed credit for them.

Normally, this kind of news would have disappeared without a trace, but with television coverage, subsequent hype, and the highly controversial nature of the story,

some believed it was justified, others questioned it.

But regardless, the news exploded in popularity.

The price of this explosive popularity was the disclosure of information about a narcotics police officer surnamed Jiang, from the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau.

Although we are not yet in the era of the internet explosion of later generations,

in this era, this kind of explosive news still caused a terrifying upheaval.

It instantly became a terrifying social hotspot.

Similar to the "my dad is so-and-so" kind of thing.

While Jiang Tian had been paying attention, he didn't actually feel anything particularly special.

Everything would be over by tomorrow.

Jiang Tian also knew that the higher-ups wouldn't allow him to suffer an injustice.

Even if one had to say where privilege was used, it wasn't Jiang Tian, ​​but rather Jiang Peng, who benefited from Jiang Tian's influence.

"Comrade Jiang is indeed a man of great principle. Let everyone see what you've done, Comrade Jiang. Everyone heard it, right? 'Serving the people,' these words sound like a catchy rhyme, but can you all honestly say that you yourselves have lived up to them?"

"What has happened during this period? I'm sure you all know perfectly well. How many people haven't upheld their bottom line? There hasn't even been an internal investigation. How many people have been implicated just after the arrest of the criminal gang?"

Minister Tan turned to look at the leaders in the conference room.

Some people lowered their heads, not daring to look at Minister Tan; others wore expressions of lingering fear; and still others watched the spectacle unfold.

The main issue was that countless people had recently been implicated by criminal groups.

Jiang Tian hadn't inquired about or participated in those matters, but he knew it was a terrifying number. Even within the civil service system, a large number of leaders had recently been replaced.

These were all unfamiliar faces from other places.

However, none of this concerned Jiang Tian. To him, meddling in other people's business was purely a thankless and pointless endeavor.



Chapter 687 The Adult's Promise Exploded


Jiang Tian was simply doing his job, ignoring all internal matters and focusing solely on apprehending the criminal gang.

As for the rest of you being implicated by the criminal gang due to your own uncleanliness, that had absolutely nothing to do with him.

The first priority was to immediately issue a nationwide arrest warrant for Chu Mo.

Second.

The purpose of Jiang Tian's visit.

Tomorrow is the day of the medal award ceremony.

If something this big happened in Yunhai today...

No.

Not just today, never.

A truly heinous and horrific event.

“How did you do your job? Why did you allow so much high-risk explosives to flow into Yunhai?”

Firearms and ammunition, after all, have limited destructive power.

But explosives are a different story.

“According to our testing, these explosives were not manufactured domestically; they are all sophisticated, professional-grade explosives. Without a doubt, they smuggled in from abroad. I bear enormous responsibility for this.”

Minister Tan immediately bowed his head and admitted his mistake.

That's the problem.

Sitting here, with this problem, Minister Tan is naturally the first to be held accountable.

However, the higher-ups also know that holding Minister Tan accountable for this matter will at most result in a reprimand. In reality, Minister Tan is truly innocent; this kind of problem has existed since ancient times.

The serious consequences had far exceeded their expectations.

After regaining their composure, the voice on the phone continued, clearly audible to all the leaders in the Yunhai office.

"From what I understand, Comrade Jiang has solved several major cases in the past few days. The major cases involving Yunya Group were just handed over, and it seems he hasn't had a break for several days. And tonight, he solved such a big case."

Those above knew how much overtime Jiang Tian had been working lately.

In fact, for frontline workers in such high-risk situations, overtime is the norm. Even clerical staff work inhumanly long hours.

This is different from later generations.

Later generations have large AI models. They only need to input recent photos of criminals, and the database will automatically match and search. As long as a criminal appears in any public place with a camera, their face can be matched instantly.

But in this era, cameras are used for observation, at most four times the magnification, and only two cameras are monitored.

In serious cases, simply staring at the cameras can be bewildering.

For example, Shang Mingyan, leading the special task force in this murder case, investigated all the cameras at Yunda University but found no useful clues.

"That's right," Minister Tan said, somewhat embarrassed.

Shang Mingyan, sitting next to Jiang Tian, ​​felt extremely uncomfortable.

After all, Jiang Tian was already yawning frequently beside her.

Physically, Jiang Tian didn't care; he didn't feel it. However, his habitual, long-term sleep schedule, coupled with several days without sleep, had left him mentally fatigued.

"Where is Comrade Jiang now?"

The superiors didn't continue asking about the next steps in the case, but went straight to Jiang Tian.

Is this case serious?

Very serious.

The problems with the case have far exceeded expectations.

But equally serious.

While the case itself was important, Jiang Tian was paramount, especially after Minister Tan explained the details of the incident. The higher-ups placed even greater emphasis on Jiang Tian.

In this era—no, in any era—what is most important? Without a doubt, it's talent, especially key talent.

At this moment, in their minds, Jiang Tian had risen to the level of strategic talent.

A multifaceted strategic talent capable of stabilizing an entire region.

“Comrade Jiang is here. Would you like to inquire about him?” Minister Tan asked hurriedly.

“Ah, thank you for your hard work, Comrade Jiang, but this case…”

The higher-ups couldn't sleep.

After learning about the incident, especially after learning that the criminals had escaped, they couldn't sleep at all.

This was no longer an ordinary criminal.

This was a criminal who had to be eliminated; moreover, this criminal had risen to a terrifying level.

And if such a person planned another crime, this time in Yunhai, who knew where he would be next?

Especially terrifying, his intelligence and abilities were terrifying.

How to solve it?

They're numb.

The higher-ups are numb.

Their individual combat capabilities are incredibly strong.

Reconnaissance and counter-reconnaissance are almost all highly professional.

High-IQ crime.

When all of this is combined...

It's fair to say that the harm caused by this one person is so immense that even those above them feel a slight sense of suffocation.

"Comrade Jiang, tell me first, what should we do about the criminals?"

The voice from above rang out.

The phone was placed to Jiang Tian's ear.

To be honest,

Jiang Tian didn't want to think about this problem right now.

Because thinking about it wouldn't help.

The harm and destructiveness this kind of person poses to society...

Jiang Tian thought about how scanners are everywhere in the future, especially in public places.

He understood.

Quite a few people have indeed done this.

Hiss!!!

Then he looked at the phone.

As for the Special Merit Citation?

Chapter 688 Those who aren't envied are mediocre; I'm not mediocre.


This...this...

Jiang Tian was somewhat taken aback. Although he had somewhat expected it, the emergence of a Special Merit Citation was actually a once-in-a-lifetime event, occurring only once every few decades.

A Special Merit Citation hadn't appeared in recent decades.

And now...

What's going on?

Hiss!!!

This is simply terrifying. Of course, if you asked who "hiss" refers to, Jiang Tian wouldn't know. However, Jiang Tian knew that at the age of twenty-two, he was about to receive two Special Merits and become a First-Class Police Inspector.

What does a First-Class Police Inspector mean?

Don't underestimate the difference between a Second-Class and First-Class Police Inspector; it's a qualitative leap.

A First-Class Police Inspector, the next step, is a true high-ranking position, at the bureau chief level.

Even in the municipal police bureau of this provincial capital, that's equivalent to a bureau chief.

In other words, a First-Class Police Inspector is essentially a bureau chief.

And don't forget this is the provincial capital!

If he's already the provincial capital's bureau chief, what's the next step? That would be the provincial department level.

In other words, Jiang Tian is already qualified, because the bureau chief selection is based on selection from the first-level police inspector.

And now, the most crucial point is that Jiang Tian hasn't been a battalion commander for very long. If he becomes a first-level police inspector, it means he'll be qualified to become a branch captain.

That's a branch captain! That's not something a battalion commander can compare to.

There's practically no comparison. The previous branch captain was Song Liguo, who worked in that position for over ten years. Even so, he only became branch captain after earning significant merit in the military.

He then served as branch captain for ten years, and is now over fifty. But even after earning merit in the military and holding a high rank, he only became branch captain in his forties!

But how old is Jiang Tian now? How old does he look?

Twenty-two!

He's only twenty-two!

Just twenty-two years old.

And now, you're telling me you're going to be a branch captain.

Of course, rank is rank, and position is position. Position represents the responsibilities and power you hold.

But rank is the prerequisite for everything—your entire position.

Only with this prerequisite can you have the opportunity, the qualification.

And what does this mean now? Jiang Tian is qualified.

Just ask.

When a position becomes vacant and promotions happen.

Just ask yourself, besides Jiang Tian, ​​who else is qualified to compete? In other words, when Jiang Tian's rank is high enough, naturally no one else can compete for the position except him. That's Jiang Tian's power.

Who dares to compete? With all those merits, do you even have the face to compete with Jiang Tian?

In other words, Jiang Tian is currently not limited by anything except seniority. While the government doesn't discriminate based on background, Jiang Tian's youth is undeniable, and more importantly, his rapid promotions are equally undeniable. Often, he's barely grasped the basics of a position before being promoted again, giving him no time to adapt.

The crucial point is that Jiang Tian has only been in office for a short time; in other words, he's already reached an absurd level of being promoted once a month.

Even with extraordinary connections, most promotions still adhere to the system of one promotion every two or three years. No one can change this, except for Jiang Tian, ​​this prodigy.

He has completely shattered this convention, a phenomenon almost unprecedented in history.

"First-Class Police Inspector, your current merits are more than enough to warrant a Special Merit Award. Logically, you've just been promoted, but I can make the decision. If you successfully apprehend Chu Mo, I guarantee you not only a Special Merit Award, but also a promotion to branch captain, and even higher, to First-Class Police Inspector."

In other prefecture-level cities, a Second-Class Police Inspector is practically equivalent to a bureau chief or deputy bureau chief.

However, in the provincial capital, generally speaking, the rank is one level higher but the position is lower than in other prefecture-level cities.

In other words, even a lateral transfer from the provincial capital is only a one-level promotion in terms of rank.

If you're promoted one level from the provincial capital, that's a direct jump of two levels, and those two levels are a huge difference below.

A high starting point has its advantages.

And the biggest advantage of Yunhai compared to other places is the endless stream of cases and criminal gangs being apprehended.

Their purpose is simply to apprehend these criminal gangs.

The more they capture, the more merit they accumulate, and naturally, the more opportunities for promotion.

In other words, it's a virtuous cycle. But for Jiang Tian, ​​while all of this benefits him, he's unwilling to sacrifice Yunhai for his own advancement. Although, in the current situation, the more criminals Yunhai has, the more benefits Jiang Tian will gain.

However, given a choice, Jiang Tian would actually prefer Yunhai to be completely devoid of criminals.

"This...this...this!!!"

"First-level police inspector, this...is this insane?"

"No, twenty-two years old, if I remember correctly, Comrade Jiang is twenty-two. Promoting to first-level police inspector at twenty-two, isn't that a bit too fast?"

"That's right, in our entire country, from ancient times to the present, it seems there has never been such a situation before."

"This is a bit too...too much. So young, and if he's directly managing a branch, well, none of the others have even had their probationary period!"

Insane.

The entire conference room erupted in chaos. After the adults spoke, everyone present went wild.

It was explosive.

Good heavens, absolutely good heavens.

This was utter chaos.

The leaders present, even the youngest among them was Jiang Tian's father, Jiang Peng.

In other words, the eldest child of any leader is definitely older than Jiang Tian.

They are younger than their own children, but seeing Jiang Tian about to stand shoulder to shoulder with them is difficult for them to accept from any perspective.

In fact, this has become a major obstacle for Jiang Tian.

Now that Jiang Tian has been promoted, future promotions will only be a matter of going with the flow. His current exceptional promotion will only make future exceptional promotions twice as difficult.

Because of seniority, even with numerous achievements, seniority, though now a derogatory term, is still something everyone considers.



Chapter 689 With the reins firmly in hand, more trump cards have been added.


Many leaders, seeing Jiang Tian, ​​older than their own sons, about to become their superiors, found the age gap difficult to accept, and now this even greater disparity had arrived.

Moreover, aside from his previous promotions, Jiang Tian's next steps seemed destined for meteoric rises.

From squad leader to branch commander.

From branch commander to bureau chief.

From bureau chief to department head.

Good heavens, at this rate of promotion, was Jiang Tian planning to become a department head before he even turned twenty-five?

That just seemed absurd.

No, it looked like he might have a chance at twenty-three.

Of course.

The past is the past, the future is the future. They knew that was impossible. The higher-ups wouldn't allow Jiang Tian to be promoted so quickly; such a rapid advancement would be excessive.

"If anyone has the ability, you go and arrest Chu Mo yourself. Whoever succeeds will be promoted and commended."

The big boss is indeed a big boss. His words, though seemingly calm and unchanged from before, are actually filled with oppressive force if you listen closely.

Clearly, the big boss on the phone had heard everything that happened in the conference room.

For those below, they scrutinize everything.

They even assess your qualifications, then obstruct you in various ways, ostensibly to "train" you and let you mature.

But for the big bosses above,

it's all a joke.

For these big bosses, qualifications and all that nonsense are unimportant. They don't care at all; what they need are people who can actually get things done.

In other words, what do the top bosses need?

Jiang Tian knows perfectly well.

It might even affect Jiang Tian's future development, easily inciting jealousy from others.

But...

Now it all seems like a joke.

That's right.

What nonsense about holding grudges.

Now that Jiang Tian is on the right track, nothing else matters.

Continuing to suppress him is pointless; it's just a waste of time for everyone else.

Why are Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan still stationed in Yunhai? What's their purpose?

To be more precise, it's to prevent Jiang Tian from being influenced by others, especially by those miscellaneous leaders.

Like, not long after Gao Xiaolong left, what happened? Leaders from this department and that department all showed up.

Of course, it's fine if they come.

But if they come and then hinder Jiang Tian, ​​that's their problem.

In other words, don't blame others for this.

"Is it hard to accept? If you're not convinced, go and try to achieve merit yourself. If you can't, and you're still envious of others, then we need to consider whether you're truly serving the people, and we need to question whether there's a serious problem with your thinking."

The voice on the phone was emotionless.

But it was this emotionless tone that was most unbearable for them.

It was what terrified them most.

The key was this leader, the leader on the phone.

To even hear him call in person was an unimaginable blessing.

Every single one of them broke out in a cold sweat.

That's how it is.

Especially for Jiang Tian, ​​with Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong here.

Especially the follow-up work.

Especially regarding explosives, and a strict screening of all primary, secondary, and tertiary schools in Yunhai.

"If anything happens, you can call me directly. No need to go through anyone else. I'll handle it for you."

The boss repeatedly emphasized on the phone.

Everyone else can be absent.

But Jiang Tian absolutely cannot be absent.

Even if it's a huge matter, you must come to Beijing.

Minister Tan glanced at the leaders of the government departments.

Strictly speaking, Jiang Tian had dual jurisdiction over both the government departments and the police department. However, in reality, the primary and secondary jurisdictions were sometimes unclear.

Some were entirely local government duties.

But others were direct superiors in official matters.

This mainly stemmed from the fact that, for the same issue, two different opinions might arise. However, whoever held the higher rank and influence between the police and government department leaders had the more effective orders—whoever's orders were obeyed. Jiang Tian's smooth sailing in Yunhai was due to Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan.

Good heavens.

The police department was practically on a pedestal. Did they really think this was a joke?

What a joke.

In his view, the promotion was truly deserved.

In fact, based on his merits, a second promotion wouldn't have been a problem.

Now, he's even been promoted too little.

Therefore,

within his authority, only Jiang Peng could control Jiang Tian; everyone else could be ignored.
