Chapter 690 This damn thirst for knowledge, you really deserve to die!


After Jiang Tian gave a detailed report on the case, he explained that normally he would have special privileges, but this time was completely different. The matter had escalated so much that Jiang Tian needed to personally give a report.

Another reason was how Jiang Tian found the murderer.

Of course, Jiang Tian couldn't claim to have a scanning radar, and more importantly, many aspects of his life were unclear.

Indeed, it didn't need to be explained too clearly.

After all, Jiang Tian's methods were often inexplicable. His solutions to many cases were astonishing, and this approach often dealt a fatal blow to criminal gangs, catching them completely off guard.

He would apprehend them all in one fell swoop.

So, Jiang Tian briefly explained that based on the clues, they deduced it was a student. Furthermore, considering the psychology of a criminal returning to the scene, they deduced the perpetrator was definitely there; if he appeared, then someone must be the criminal. The final conclusion was correct, and so on.

What? You say these deductions are completely illogical?

But sorry, Jiang Tian's words are logical, even Minister Tan was astonished.

This...

It seems like a combination of guesswork and luck, but how could that be? It's a joke! An entire expert team worked for days, without a single clue, bringing the case to a dead end. This is the most professionally competent, even the most elite, criminal investigation team in the country, and not only did they not find a single clue, but you solved it in no time?

No matter how you look at it, it's a bit hard to accept.

But that's the reality. In the end, Minister Tan could only sigh. This is the unique method of a truly brilliant person; others simply cannot learn it.

Even Shang Mingyan was completely dumbfounded.

Then Shang Mingyan could say with certainty that Jiang Tian was just bluffing. Yes, he was definitely bluffing; he must have some secret formula he wasn't revealing.

After Jiang Tian finished speaking, the expressions of everyone in the conference room were quite interesting, and Shang Mingyan kept glancing at Jiang Tian.

Others might not know, but Shang Mingyan had been following the whole thing.

What Jiang Tian did and the process seemed to make sense at first glance, but what sense did it make? In Shang Mingyan's opinion, it was pure nonsense and deception.

It all seemed reasonable at first glance, but upon closer inspection, it was utterly devoid of any substance.

So, by the time Jiang Tian left the Municipal Bureau, it was already past 3 a.m.

Looking at the time, well, Jiang Tian wasn't going to get any sleep.

There's no need to go back, because I've already booked a flight. In fact, due to the current transportation conditions and the backward economic environment, there's only one flight from Yunhai to Beijing in the morning. If it's late, I might not even be able to catch the flight and it will be delayed until tomorrow. By then, it will be too late.

"I'll take you," Shang Mingyan insisted on seeing Jiang Tian off.

"No, Chu Mo has run away now and could harm you at any time. It's a little better if I'm with you; Chu Mo will definitely be wary. But if I'm not there, who will save you if danger strikes? While I'm away from Yunhai, you should stay at the Municipal Bureau."

Jiang Tian stopped Shang Mingyan. Good heavens, nobody knows how crazy Chu Mo can be. He'd been stopped twice this time; who knows what will happen next?

Oh no.

Who knows what earth-shattering thing Chu Mo will do next?

"This..." Shang Mingyan knew this too.

But...

Shang Mingyan looked at Jiang Tian, ​​his eyes practically shining.

Mainly because things had already progressed somewhat between them, and Jiang Tian had to be so mysterious today, making Shang Mingyan's heart itch with anticipation.

It's like someone's gossiping about the most exciting and interesting part, but then suddenly stops halfway through—the urge to kill is irresistible.

Of course, this is just a metaphor. Shang Mingyan isn't interested in gossip, but he's incredibly interested in these cases and solving them. For him, today's events and the methods used to solve the case completely defy logic.

It's utterly absurd.

"No, I have something to ask you, unless you tell me how you solved the case today. Teach me that method." This completely shattered Shang Mingyan's worldview.

Good heavens, is it really as simple as Jiang Tian says? A whole team of experts, the top criminal investigation team in the country—can't they figure out such a simple trick? This is utter nonsense.

First of all, such a vicious criminal should be ruled out as a student from the very beginning. Why is Jiang Tian so sure? What makes him so certain?

What makes you so sure?

Furthermore, it's one thing to be certain it was a student, but the crucial point is that the incident happened several days ago. What makes you so sure that someone sitting a kilometer away, in the dead of night, actually returned to the scene?

While, strictly speaking, it was possible to observe a little from that angle.

However, given the direction and range, it was simply impossible. Even if Shang Mingyan walked right past them, he wouldn't have noticed their disguise.

Unless a major flaw was revealed.

Psychology isn't as invincible as it seems.

“You really want to know,” Jiang Tian knew perfectly well.

“Yes, I really want to know, tell me quickly.” Shang Mingyan was impatient.

Jiang Tian pondered for a moment.

He stroked his chin, seemingly lost in thought.

Shang Mingyan beside him was incredibly impatient!

“Tell me quickly, tell me quickly, I really want to know!” Shang Mingyan grabbed Jiang Tian's arm and shook it.

Hiss!!

This is her being coquettish.

Oh my god!

The sky is falling.

Just then.

Inside the anti-drug squad on the third floor, Zeng Fan, who had just finished his overtime shift after handing over a case, was stunned, dumbfounded. Looking at the scene before him, he felt like he'd gone mad.

Yes, it was Zeng Fan.

Zeng Fan had previously worked at headquarters, but a series of major cases occurred in Yunhai, so he was transferred there. However, due to a significant oversight while handling these cases, he was suspended for over a month for self-reflection.

Later, Zeng Fan's mentor used connections to bring him back. However, returning to headquarters was impossible; the system dictates that everyone has their own position. Being suspended doesn't mean the world will stop turning without him, so his original position was filled by someone else.

And then...

Zeng Fan's records were transferred to Yunhai to atone for his actions.



Chapter 691 The sky collapsed and the earth caved in, faith crumbled, how could you...


The higher-ups, considering Zeng Fan's feelings, agreed to the request, but unfortunately, there weren't enough positions available.

Therefore, he could only be temporarily placed on hold within the Narcotics Control Committee, a second-line position handling ideological and cultural management related to narcotics control.

Of course, entering this position was equivalent to entering a government agency; making it difficult to achieve anything significant or earn merit.

For those who were complacent, it was paradise, but for those with ambition, it was a cage, and they were like birds in a cage.

They could coast along, even wielding considerable power, but achieving merit was impossible; they would never have the chance to make a name for themselves on the front lines, apprehending criminals.

Initially, Zeng Fan was very resentful, even when suppressed by Jiang Tian.

But as time went on, watching Jiang Tian rise step by step until he could no longer see Jiang Tian's back,

he understood that Jiang Tian was now on the path to advancement.

He was utterly incomparable.

Even if someone starts lying low now, without achieving anything, doing nothing, they can still make progress around the age of thirty. And who knows what the future holds? At the very least, that's progress.

He was nothing!

Completely worthless.

Now, with a blemish on his resume, he was immediately ruled out for promotion. He was completely finished. Unless his political career saw a major turnaround, he was beyond saving.

Zeng Fan had come to terms with it.

He focused on his work here, and it must be said, this second-line work in Yunhai was incredibly rewarding for Zeng Fan, especially the opportunity to closely study the details and circumstances of Jiang Tian's case-handling methods.

The more he studied, the more astute Zeng Fan, no fool, realized something terrifying.

Jiang Tian often dismantled entire criminal gangs within two days of discovering a case, sometimes even overnight. There were no traditional plans, no surveillance, no stings—he simply went straight for the mastermind.

In other words, everyone else works their way in from the outside, layer by layer.

Jiang Tian started his operation from the core. Normally, you arrest people from the outside in, but Jiang Tian did the opposite, arresting from the inside out.

This is simply outrageous.

Directly arresting the mastermind behind a crime often leads to crucial evidence.

They didn't give the mastermind any time or opportunity to react.

Then, using the core mastermind as a breakthrough point, they used the upper levels to expose the lower levels.

The more he watched, the more dumbfounded he became. The more he watched, the more he realized that Jiang Tian was a god, practically a deity—a god in his eyes.

In terms of arresting drug trafficking groups, Zeng Fan admitted he was inferior, even to his master. The key was Jiang Tian's arrest methods; even if you were shown them, you couldn't learn them.

You can't learn them. Can you, like Jiang Tian, ​​do nothing and yet already establish the mastermind's criminal activities, gather irrefutable evidence, and use it as a breakthrough?

Obviously, that's impossible.

Zeng Fan admired him greatly, so he calmed down and prepared to study Comrade Jiang Tian's excellent case examples.

But the result was completely unexpected!

What did he see?

What did Zeng Fan see?

Shang Mingyan.

That's right, it was Shang Mingyan.

Of course, there's an age gap between Zeng Fan and Shang Mingyan. More importantly, Shang Mingyan's reputation is such that while she might not know many people, very few people wouldn't recognize her.

In this field, in criminal investigation, Shang Mingyan, without exaggeration, has reached the pinnacle in the country—she's the ceiling for everyone.

While Zeng Fan works in drug enforcement, within the civil service, Shang Mingyan is a god. In terms of fame, within the police system and even nationwide, Shang Mingyan far surpasses Minister Tan.

Moreover, they both work at headquarters and meet frequently; how could Zeng Fan not be familiar with Shang Mingyan?

In his mind, Shang Mingyan is a god.

In fact, it was because he saw Shang Mingyan on television after solving a case that he resolved to become a police officer serving the people.

After meeting him, after learning about Shang Mingyan...

Zeng Fan's fear of Shang Mingyan was genuine.

This wasn't because Shang Mingyan had done anything to him, or because he had put on a cold, intimidating act.

It was something far more terrifying: Shang Mingyan's aloof demeanor, coupled with his penetrating eyes and stunningly beautiful face, combined to create a devastating combination.

He could see through any despicable behavior at a glance. Crucially, you could even sense that he had seen through you. In that moment, you could even recognize how ugly you were. To Zeng Fan, Shang Mingyan was like an untouchable god, high above him.

And now...

His god, his heaven, had collapsed. His faith had crumbled.

What was this most sacred, untouchable god, the god whom even a glance from him made him want to slap himself, doing now?

The high and mighty fairy, who could see through people's hearts, was now actually holding a man's arm and shaking it.

And that's not all, what are you doing?

Stop!

You're Shang Mingyan!

Remember your place.

You are a fairy.

You are the heavens.

You are faith.

You are authority.

How could you do such a thing?

*Crack!!!*

Zeng Fan felt his faith had completely collapsed.

And seeing the man's face... yes, yes, Jiang Tian.

That's right.

Zeng Fan's eyes widened.

He watched in disbelief as Shang Mingyan pressed his face against Jiang Tian's.

That look...

That...that...

*Buzz!!!*

The sky collapsed.

Zeng Fan was stunned. He slapped himself hard, a resounding slap. It hurt, but it proved he wasn't dreaming.

But why would Shang Mingyan do such a thing?

It's terrifying.

This world is terrifying.

Zeng Fan's face turned pale.

But at this moment, neither Jiang Tian nor Shang Mingyan saw Zeng Fan hiding in the shadows. Of course, even if they had, they wouldn't have cared.

"Tell me, tell me quickly! I... I want to know so badly!"

Shang Mingyan was going crazy. This damned thirst for knowledge!

Jiang Tian deserves to die!

Jiang Tian, ​​having been kissed, realized that a certain true aspect of Shang Mingyan was gradually being revealed.

“Okay, I’ll demonstrate it for you again. How much you learn depends on you,” Jiang Tian suddenly said.



Chapter 692 Huge Changes, Shang Mingyan: You've Changed


"Demonstration? What do you mean?"

This question completely baffled Shang Mingyan.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian grabbed Shang Mingyan's hand and led him directly to the car. This was Jiang Tian's car for the airport, his Phaeton.

A standard Phaeton.

Understated yet exuding luxury. Even Shang Mingyan, who had achieved so much, had never owned such a nice car. He was quite impressed by the ride.

Of course, the most important thing was the undeniable comfort. The interior was absolutely luxurious; a million-dollar car truly lived up to its name.

However, Shang Mingyan wasn't concerned about any of that right now. He was mainly being pulled along by Jiang Tian, ​​and suddenly, he felt a little hot.

"Your face is very red. Are you too hot?" Jiang Tian asked casually.

"Yeah, it's a bit too hot."

How infuriating! While trying to appease him, Shang Mingyan secretly gritted her teeth, wishing she could bite Jiang Tian to death. Mainly, she clearly knew—no, she was certain—that Jiang Tian already knew why she was blushing, yet he still said those things.

"No wonder, people always say, the better the car, the hotter the passenger seat," Jiang Tian remarked.

This joke still exists today, especially now. There are far too many people using cars to pick up girls. Compared to the vast number of luxury cars that have gone bankrupt after taking out loans in later generations, the prestige of using a car to pick up girls is still very high in this era.

In later generations, there are all sorts of scammers, car rental companies, and bankruptcies, etc. There's a saying that those who drive luxury cars envy those who drive ordinary cars, those who drive ordinary cars envy those who ride bicycles, and those who ride bicycles envy those who drive luxury cars.

Shang Mingyan clearly knew this joke and couldn't help but reach out and lightly tap Jiang Tian on the passenger seat.

It wasn't hard, just a light tap.

But Jiang Tian grabbed her wrist.

Shang Mingyan's face flushed even more.

Especially after feeling Jiang Tian's hand tighten slightly, Shang Mingyan's face turned even redder, and she even guessed what was going on. This brought a feeling she had never experienced before to her heart.

This was the first time in her life that Shang Mingyan had felt this way.

Especially in this atmosphere, with a boy in this space.

Shang Mingyan's heart raced; her heart, colder than a fish that had been killed for ten years, pounded wildly like a deer.

Until they were close enough.

The Phaeton drove along the wide road.

Jiang Tian focused on driving.

But at this moment, Shang Mingyan's eyes were bright and sparkling as she looked at Jiang Tian.

She kept glancing outside, then back at Jiang Tian—goodness, she looked like an eighteen or nineteen-year-old girl.

This terrifying contrast gave Jiang Tian a sense of accomplishment.

To be honest, even Jiang Tian was filled with mixed feelings.

"Why are you staring at me?" Jiang Tian asked, continuing to drive seriously.

Although he hadn't slept, Jiang Tian was surprisingly energetic. Shang Mingyan had effectively revitalized Jiang Tian's weary spirit.

Moreover, Jiang Tian possessed terrifying superhuman attributes; with his mind no longer fatigued, he felt more energetic and full of vitality than if he had slept for over ten hours.

"Didn't you say it was dangerous? What if Chu Mo makes a move when we get back?" Shang Mingyan asked curiously.

He didn't understand why Jiang Tian had suddenly changed his tune halfway through.

"And what were you just saying you'd demonstrate for me?"

Shang Mingyan asked again.

Shang Mingyan was generally uninterested in most things except solving cases, but she now strangely found that when Jiang Tian was involved, every move and word he made could deeply affect her.

A single sentence could shatter her heart, which had been frozen for over thirty years.

Her emotions fluctuated wildly.

"Your two questions are actually one thing," Jiang Tian said, a slight smile playing on his lips.

"What do you mean?" Shang Mingyan asked in surprise.

"Hehe."

Jiang Tian smiled.

Then he looked out the window.

To be honest, Jiang Tian greatly admired Chu Mo.

Why?

He was bold, meticulous, and adept at playing the field.

Now, the entire Yunhai was under search; in fact, Jiang Tian had already been checked at two intersections along his journey.

Of course, it wasn't easy to get used to at first, but now the people living in Yunhai have adapted. Being suddenly asked to show their ID while walking is commonplace, and street checks are second nature.

However, it's clear.

This method might work against ordinary criminals, but it's far from enough against someone like Chu Mo.

Jiang Tian wasn't unaware of this.

He just hadn't expected Chu Mo to be so audacious.

He did the opposite.

A normal person running away at this point would immediately flee. At the very least, if there's nowhere to run, they'd stay in the deep mountains and forests. If there's somewhere to run, they'd flee to Yunhai overnight.

But Chu Mo didn't do that. He could have done that, but Chu Mo chose a different path: following Jiang Tian.

Oh no.

To be precise, not following.

It was surveillance.

In fact, Jiang Tian had already discovered Chu Mo at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

However, the distance was a bit far, and Jiang Tian wasn't in a hurry to act. He knew that if he left with Shang Mingyan, Chu Mo would definitely follow.

Sure enough.

Along the way, Chu Mo didn't even seem to have passed the two checkpoints. He was always following from a few hundred meters away, even just a couple of intersections away, and his tracking methods were incredibly sophisticated.

He wasn't always following; for example, now he had completely missed Jiang Tian, ​​appearing at two different intersections.

And when he reappeared, he had already changed cars and even moved ahead of Jiang Tian.

If it weren't for the scanning radar, even normal counter-surveillance would have been difficult to detect. Even Shang Mingyan hadn't noticed anything unusual about the vehicle ahead.

The main problem was that the two vehicles were even separated by two or three intersections. In fact, the other vehicle had even turned right at the previous intersection and moved directly in front of Jiang Tian.

From this direction, he could only see a glimpse of taillights, sometimes not even that, and they kept changing cars.

What were they trying to do?

Jiang Tian had neither the inclination nor the desire to know.

Whom did Chu Mo want revenge on? Did he want to kill him to make a name for himself, or was he simply after Shang Mingyan, etc.?

Jiang Tian was no longer interested in any of this, nor did he have the patience for it. Mainly, if he continued digging, Chu Mo would become worthless.

Capturing him now was just right.

Therefore, Jiang Tian gradually increased the speed of his car.

"Red light! Red light!" Shang Mingyan patted Jiang Tian's arm, mainly because the car was going too fast.

It was going so fast that Shang Mingyan was starting to panic and get scared. Even though it was three or four in the morning, there were still some cars around.

Jiang Tian had sped up to over 100 km/h, and even at a red light.

Of course, red lights weren't a big deal, mainly because the car was already registered with the traffic management department and there wouldn't be any penalties.

But this behavior was very dangerous, extremely dangerous!

How could he not be scared?



Chapter 693 Shocked and utterly devastated, this is impossible.


"Watch my performance." Jiang Tian floored the accelerator, accelerating even further. They were only a kilometer apart.

In less than half a minute, Jiang Tian had overtaken them.

Then, before Jiang Tian could finish speaking, he slammed on the brakes with lightning speed, the Phaeton's terrifying engine roaring, its exhaust note echoing through the night.

"Car, car behind us, get in quicks!" Shang Mingyan cried out, his face pale, not shouting, but his voice trembling with panic.

Jiang Tian remained silent.

This only fueled Shang Mingyan's anxiety. No, we've only just begun, you don't need to be so excited!

Shang Mingyan thought that things had progressed too quickly with Jiang Tian, ​​and that Jiang Tian was just venting his excitement by driving.

But...

Shang Mingyan was a bit too fast!

At this speed, if they crashed, Shang Mingyan felt his chances of survival were slim.

But the next moment...

The Phaeton's wheels screeched against the pavement, unleashing a terrifying cloud of burning smoke. The car, originally traveling east to west, had suddenly turned completely around, and in the next instant,

Jiang Tian's car was face-to-face with a white taxi, almost colliding.

The driver of the other car, seemingly caught off guard, stopped abruptly. Jiang Tian had already unbuckled his seatbelt and quickly got out.

In that instant the other car stopped, Jiang Tian's pistol was already pointed at the driver's side, and the Phaeton's extremely bright headlights pierced through the other car's window.

However, at that moment,

it was still dark, so a normal person could only see the interior of the other car as dark.

Shang Mingyan, still shaken, was about to get out of the car to look for Jiang Tian when he turned his head and saw Jiang Tian holding a pistol.

Huh?

Shang Mingyan thought he was seeing things.

But then he jerked his head back to look again.

Huh.

It was true.

He really was holding a gun.

Not just aiming, but the next moment...

Bang! Bang!!!

Sizzle! Sizzle! Sizzle!!!

Jiang Tian fired suddenly, two shots, and the tires exploded.

Both tires exploded.

The vehicle intercepted by Jiang Tian instantly came to a standstill.

Even though Shang Mingyan was utterly shocked by this scene, she didn't panic too much. After all, she had seen all sorts of big scenes, so her first instinct was to quickly duck, unbuckle her seatbelt, bend over, and open the car door to take cover.

She knew that Jiang Tian wouldn't do something like this for no reason.

But the next moment...

Jiang Tian spoke.

"Chu Mo, get out. I gave you a chance, but you didn't appreciate it. I know there's a bomb in your car, but there's no one here. You can't threaten them. I just gave you a chance, and you just gave it to them. Why bother?" Jiang Tian said calmly.

Boom!!!

"Chu Mo?"

Is Chu Mo in that stopped car?

Buzz!!!

Shang Mingyan, who had opened the car door to avoid the car behind it, was completely stunned.

Of course, Jiang Tian glanced at it out of the corner of his eye but didn't pay any attention.

Although a normal person can't see what's happening inside the driver's seat, Jiang Tian could see it very clearly.

Every movement inside, every detail, even the astigmatism, was crystal clear to him.

In the pitch-black night, Jiang Tian's eyes were sharp as well.

Moreover, Chu Mo wasn't entirely without disguise. If he hadn't, he would have been caught at the intersection, not yet.

After all, Chu Mo's photo had already spread far and wide.

But it must be said, Chu Mo's disguise was strange. It seemed like only a little makeup, a slight alteration, yet he appeared to be a completely different person from his previous image.

Even if Jiang Tian saw him on the street, without the aid of his scanning radar, he might not recognize him, let alone someone lying in wait on the road.

So, Jiang Tian smiled.

They already knew how powerful he was.

Yet they still dared to appear near him—yes, only near him.

But it was enough to make Chu Mo regret it for the rest of his life.

Shocked, Shang Mingyan was utterly shocked, but at the same time, he was completely dumbfounded by Jiang Tian's words.

Good heavens.

Absolutely good heavens.

Chu Mo?

Chu Mo, why are you so sure?

The key is, even if you're certain, why are you so sure it's Chu Mo from such a distance?

Furthermore, why are you so certain that the person in the car is Chu Mo? The crucial point is that the two of them were together the entire time. Shang Mingyan can guarantee that no one else appeared in their line of sight.

So, the question arises.

How was Chu Mo exposed?

Furthermore, this car was one Chu Mo had just changed into, and it was even appearing for the first time, just two blocks away from them. Why did Jiang Tian seem to have already accelerated before this car appeared? So, how was Jiang Tian so certain?

This...

It's utterly baffling.

It's impossible to understand, and even more so...

The most important thing is...

This is utterly outrageous, absolutely outrageous!

So, what Jiang Tian meant when he said at the Municipal Police Bureau, "I'll demonstrate it again and then I'll show you," was this? His silence was because he wanted to personally demonstrate it again, to arrest Chu Mo once more?

Wait a minute.

Thinking this, Shang Mingyan suddenly paused. She seemed to have realized something terrifying.

That is...

Damn it.

Jiang Tian seemed to have discovered everything at the Municipal Police Bureau.

Then the problem is, at the Municipal Police Bureau, the two were talking downstairs, and there was definitely no one else around. Chu Mo entering the Municipal Police Bureau and then being discovered by Jiang Tian is even more impossible.

How did Jiang Tian suddenly discover it?

A god?

Unfortunately, Shang Mingyan, who was hiding, didn't have a chance to ask, even though she was practically bursting with curiosity.

Because it seemed there was still no certainty. What if the person in the car wasn't Chu Mo?

But Shang Mingyan had no idea at this moment how complex Chu Mo's expression was in the driver's seat of the vehicle that had been intercepted.

And how...

utterly bewildered.

He'd been discovered.

No.

This is what being discovered means?

And then...

He'd been intercepted?

He'd been exposed?

No, no, no.

Other things were hard to say, but Chu Mo possessed terrifyingly unparalleled confidence in this kind of tactic.

Chu Mo was certain that his disguise and makeup skills were absolutely infallible; he couldn't possibly be discovered, and no one could possibly notice.

Besides,

he had just been checked, but Chu Mo was confident that nothing unusual had been detected.

Even acquaintances wouldn't recognize him, let alone someone who recognized him from a photograph.

So the question is...

How was he discovered?

And why wasn't he stopped and questioned when he changed cars?

This....



Chapter 694 Audacious Assassination: Hunting Down Jiang Tian?


Chu Mo was shocked.

Completely incredulous.

He was insane.

Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian.

Chu Mo's face was hidden in shadow, but it kept repeating one name: Jiang Tian.

He stared intently at Jiang Tian, ​​at Jiang Tian's calm face, at Jiang Tian's condescending gaze.

There's always a special connection between powerful individuals.

Chu Mo disdained rumors; he never believed hearsay, only what he saw with his own eyes.

And after seeing Jiang Tian in person.

Or rather, after carefully studying the things Jiang Tian had done, Chu Mo finally concluded that it was impossible. Putting himself in Jiang Tian's shoes, Chu Mo could say with absolute certainty that even he himself could not have done such a thing, or rather, could not possibly do it.

For a normal person, someone like Chu Mo, who could kill seven or eight people, already possessed considerable skill. But to instantly kill hundreds, even millions, of armed men in a short time was simply impossible; in Chu Mo's view, it was mostly hearsay.

However, after meeting Jiang Tian, ​​Chu Mo understood that those claims weren't rumors or exaggerations; they were real. Jiang Tian genuinely possessed the ability to do so, posing a terrifying threat that ordinary people couldn't perceive.

It was as if Jiang Tian, ​​upon seeing Chu Mo and observing his every move, immediately assessed Chu Mo's formidable fighting prowess.

Chu Mo, on the other hand, couldn't see through Jiang Tian at all.

But it was precisely this incomprehensibility that was most terrifying.

At that moment, Chu Mo knew he was no match.

But the saying "It's easy to dodge a spear in the open, but hard to guard against an arrow in the dark" was true. If he couldn't fight openly, he would fight from the shadows. Even Jiang Tian would never expect that Chu Mo, instead of fleeing, would use this opportunity to turn against them.

This is essentially psychological warfare.

In the past, Chu Mo liked to do things this way, but high risk also means high reward. At this moment, no one would expect him not to be hiding, but instead to be preparing an attack.

A normal person couldn't possibly guess this at this point.

But, unfortunately, he encountered someone who was anything but normal.

At least in Jiang Tian's view, Chu Mo's modus operandi was completely different from that of a normal person.

Because this current phase, with martial law in effect, is actually the most dangerous time; no one would choose to commit a crime at this time.

But Chu Mo happened to be at this time, at the most dangerous time, but also, at this most dangerous time, people's vigilance is often at its lowest, and the success rate of an operation is highest.

Anyone should be hiding at this time.

But this person was walking carelessly on the street, and everyone even thought that Chu Mo had already fled overseas through the wilderness.

The authorities even further strengthened border defenses, hoping to capture Chu Mo, though the chances were slim, it was still an attempt.

The result was completely unexpected.

No one expected it.

Even Jiang Tian was surprised when he saw it.

Who is being targeted?

Jiang Tian or Shang Mingyan.

But Jiang Tian believes it's not targeting Shang Mingyan.

Because Chu Mo never anticipated Shang Mingyan's trip, or rather, given the immense risk he's taking, his target couldn't possibly be Shang Mingyan. Otherwise, he'd had many opportunities to kill Shang Mingyan directly in the past.

Of course, it's also possible that Chu Mo was overconfident before meeting Jiang Tian, ​​and these possibilities cannot be ruled out.

If the target is Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian needs to reassess. Moreover, judging from his behavior, coming out at such a dangerous time to hunt Shang Mingyan is clearly unnecessary. Therefore, the target can only be himself.

This is an attempt to hunt him down.

Interesting.

Thinking this through in just a brief moment.

"Get out of the car. You should know me. I'm watching your every move. Don't even think about taking out a gun. I'm watching you. Now, put your hands up. You should understand that you might have a chance to resist others, but with a gun pointed at you, no one has a chance," Jiang Tian said calmly.

It has to be said.

Although this person was an enemy, as an enemy, this person was indeed someone Jiang Tian would remember vividly.

At least among all the criminals Jiang Tian had encountered, regardless of their wealth, the number of major crimes they had committed, or how audacious they were, none of that mattered. The most important thing was that none of them had ever been taken seriously by Jiang Tian.

Except for Chu Mo.

He was the one who truly left a lasting impression on Jiang Tian. At least in terms of criminal methods and tactics, Jiang Tian was willing to call Chu Mo the most troublesome and strongest criminal he had ever encountered.

Anyone else, it's estimated, wouldn't be able to outmaneuver Chu Mo.

Even more so.

Jiang Tian was certain that without him, Chu Mo could truly wreak havoc on the world. Even with Shang Mingyan in the shadows and Chu Mo in the open, it was possible that Chu Mo could kill Shang Mingyan at any moment.

A dangerous element.

Such a dangerous person couldn't be allowed to live.

But Jiang Tian's patience had its limits.

"Get out of the car. I'll give you two seconds. To be honest, you're of no value to me anymore. So, there's no point in you staying in the car. You only have two paths before you. You should understand: live to face judgment, or die here right now," Jiang Tian said.

A pity.

In reality, he was a talented person. If he could have him as a subordinate, he would definitely be the most formidable.

Jiang Tian lacked just such a subordinate.

But unfortunately...

It was obvious that given the enormity of Chu Mo's crimes, that was no longer possible.

Right?

At this moment, Shang Mingyan's gaze was fixed on the car. She couldn't see inside because the glass was too reflective in the pitch-black night.

Therefore, she found it even harder to believe that Jiang Tian could see the other person.

Was it really Chu Mo?

Or was it that since we're both people, why couldn't I see him, but he could see me clearly? Were we looking at different things?

As Shang Mingyan was pondering these questions,

she saw the next moment.

*Click!*

The car door opened.

Then, a man who initially seemed unfamiliar, dressed very differently—wearing a suit and gold-rimmed glasses, and appearing to be in his thirties or forties—stepped in.

Anyone.

Even Si Qinghan, at this moment, if they had simply walked past each other on the street, she probably wouldn't have recognized him.

But if she looked closely, with the help of the lighting...



Chapter 695 Are you even human? A dimensional reduction attack.


Shang Mingyan looked closely, again and again.

She still felt a little unfamiliar.

But those body language, she knew it all too well, even the micro-expressions.

Those subconscious movements were hard to change.

In other words, this was Chu Mo, exactly the same Chu Mo as before.

Only the appearance and temperament had changed drastically.

"Hiss!!!"

Shang Mingyan's mind exploded, her face filled with disbelief.

It really was Chu Mo.

"I didn't expect to meet you so soon, and I certainly didn't expect how you discovered my disguise. I believe no one in this world could have discovered it!"

Although it was the dead of night, even the early hours of the morning, it was still a first-tier city, and there were still some vehicles passing by. Besides, this was already on the outer ring road, and at this hour, there were still quite a few vehicles carrying containers.

Even at this moment.

A large truck carrying a huge container was driving on the main road.

He was still video chatting with his wife on his phone. She was asleep, and whenever the driver felt tired, he would glance at her, which would rouse him from sleep.

After all, it was for his family.

His wife was also worried about him driving at night, so she kept the video chat on even while sleeping.

It was a heartwarming scene of a very loving family.

But then, the next moment.

The silence suddenly erupted.

"Holy shit!"

The driver cursed, then rubbed his eyes hard.

As the distance drew closer, the driver finally saw clearly.

Damn it.

A gunfight.

A gunfight on the main road.

This…

Is this a movie shoot?

The thought crossed his mind.

People with similar thoughts, occasionally passing by a truck carrying goods, would curse and express disbelief, some even frantically recording videos.

But unfortunately.

Even if they recorded videos, getting them published would be extremely difficult.

Yunhai is currently the subject of much online discussion, but some revelations have already begun to be censored.

The authorities are condoning this only to create momentum for Jiang Tian.

However, condoning doesn't mean indulgence. There needs to be controlled indulgence, a limited degree of hype to create a dramatic turnaround, not something that disrupts social stability. That would be putting the cart before the horse. Therefore, the hype must be conducted in an orderly manner while maintaining social stability.

It was precisely because of the external environment that Chu Mo believed his disguise wouldn't be compromised, which is why he confidently began tracking and preparing to act.

Jiang Tian wasn't wrong about Chu Mo's target; it wasn't Shang Mingyan from the beginning.

Because eliminating Shang Mingyan no longer brings Chu Mo much satisfaction. Comparatively, assassinating or murdering Jiang Tian would bring Chu Mo unparalleled pleasure.

Under normal circumstances, it would be very difficult or impossible, but that doesn't mean there's no opportunity.

To ensure success, he even devised two plans: one that succeeded, and another to smuggle a bomb into the plane.

Then they would both perish.

Even in death, the thrill of dying alongside someone like Jiang Tian would be immense for Chu Mo.

For this reason,

Chu Mo even abandoned the idea of ​​blowing up the dormitory building.

Everyone thought Chu Mo was acting for his own life, but they were all wrong. Chu Mo wasn't afraid of death; what he truly wanted was to enjoy life before he died, or rather, to pursue a process and an art.

Simply put, he had developed serious psychological problems.

The thinking of someone like him was beyond the comprehension of normal people.

His logic, combined with his high intelligence, created a dangerous situation where no one could predict his actions.

But no one expected it!

Chu Mo never imagined he would be discovered before he even acted.

He was clearly preparing to find a place to set up a sniper rifle.

He was even preparing to take the bomb into the plane.

Of course.

In reality, what seemed like a pipe dream—smuggling a bomb into a plane—wasn't difficult for Chu Mo.

The backwardness of security and inspection methods in this era was a major factor.

At least for Chu Mo, it wasn't difficult at all.

However, imagination is often beautiful, but reality is harsh. Chu Mo never expected that things would turn out this way. He arrived safely, his plans were perfect, but...

Damn it, he was caught by Jiang Tian almost immediately.

"How did you find me? I shouldn't have exposed anything, right?" Chu Mo believed he had done everything perfectly, that it was impossible for him to be discovered. He had used this method many times, even abroad, and it had always been very effective.

This wasn't to say that his reconnaissance methods were ineffective; it was just that he had encountered Jiang Tian.

"Your tracking methods and techniques are indeed very strong. No one else could possibly detect you. But since you knew I was the most dangerous person, you still dared to come looking for me. So, do you think I'm the same as everyone else?"

"Impossible! I was perfectly disguised." Chu Mo couldn't believe it.

“Others couldn’t have noticed or detected you, but I actually noticed you the moment you appeared from the Municipal Bureau,” Jiang Tian said.

He glanced at Shang Mingyan, whose hands were trembling as he dialed the number.

Good heavens.

Shang Mingyan was incredibly excited!

And even more so, incredibly shocked!

Damn it.

Now Shang Mingyan understood that the so-called demonstration was actually a demonstration of capturing Chu Mo.

No.

We mean, Chu Mo is on the wanted list in Beijing. The higher-ups are searching for him like crazy, sparing no expense to capture him.

A person like that is terrifying and dangerous; even more terrifying problems could arise at any moment.

And then...

What happened?

They were just having a meeting—no, Shang Mingyan was certain—that the leaders were probably working themselves to the bone, overtime, coordinating the entire Yunhai manhunt.

In short, sparing no expense.

And then Jiang Tian stepped out.

The man was caught.

So...

Shang Mingyan was incredibly excited!

This was what solving a case truly meant!

At this moment, Shang Mingyan suddenly felt that all his previous case-solving skills were utterly insignificant compared to Jiang Tian's. It was like child's play.

So Shang Mingyan was excitedly making the call, completely unaware of how terrifying and devastating Jiang Tian's words were to Chu Mo at that moment.

No, in other words...

"I found out as soon as I started following you?" Chu Mo was stunned.

"No, to be more precise, I actually knew you the moment you saw me," Jiang Tian said.

Pfft!!!

I sprayed it.

Chu Mo nearly spat out a mouthful of blood.

Is this even human? Seriously, is this even human?

It's not my fault for being weak; it's just that Jiang Tian is too monstrous!

"You're not human!"

Chu Mo exploded. He never dreamed that one day he would be attacked in a completely different dimension.



Chapter 696 Will this punch, imbued with fifty years of internal energy, shatter Jiang Tian?


"Bragging?"

"But now, I've been inexplicably stopped. The key issue is, I wasn't following Jiang Tian; I had just changed cars and was even several intersections ahead of him. How could you have discovered me like that?"

"Your choice to live or die is in your hands. I have some urgent business to attend to. If you choose to live, don't make any unnecessary moves. If you do, I'm sorry, you'll die instantly. I don't want to waste time crippling you, because you have no value to me."

If Chu Mo possessed any intelligence, information, or connections to uncover, Jiang Tian might be interested.

But Chu Mo has no value whatsoever, nothing that Jiang Tian can use anymore.

Therefore, whether he lives or dies has no impact on Jiang Tian's reputation or achievements. What he needs now is to eliminate this threat, Chu Mo. That's his thought, and that's Jiang Tian's thought as well.

"You."

Chu Mo had indeed considered for a fleeting moment whether his skills allowed him to escape, but he hadn't had the chance in the car.

Would he still have a chance after getting out?

Obviously not.

A bitter smile appeared on Chu Mo's face.

But when Jiang Tian took three steps closer, bringing them to within two or three meters, Chu Mo was astonished to find that Jiang Tian had immediately holstered his pistol.

Then he pulled out handcuffs from behind his back.

No, seriously, does he look down on me that much?

"You've even put your gun away? Aren't you really afraid I'll turn around and fight back, or escape alive?" Chu Mo's lips were dry.

This composure, this confidence emanating from Jiang Tian, ​​filled Chu Mo with astonishment and disbelief. Shouldn't anyone be on high alert when facing a criminal like himself?

Why was Jiang Tian acting like this?

"Actually, for me, once I get close, three or five meters makes no difference. Within that range, no one can resist or withstand me. Of course, if you don't believe me, you can try, but I suggest you don't try it lightly, because the price is not something you can bear."

Jiang Tian said slowly.

There was one more thing he didn't say: the fact that Jiang Tian only put away his pistol after getting within three meters, and that Chu Mo was already quite proud of himself, was enough.

Otherwise, Jiang Tian would have simply dealt with him like a human being.

Or rather, for those without value, or even back at the city police station, Jiang Tian would have just shot them.

At two hundred meters, the effective range of a pistol is only fifty or sixty meters, but for Jiang Tian, ​​wherever the bullet can reach is within his effective range; there's no difference.

As long as he has the gun, even if the bullet deviates a hundred meters or so, it has no impact on Jiang Tian hitting the target.

This is Jiang Tian's marksmanship and confidence.

And at this moment, naturally, it was also agonizing for Chu Mo.

At this moment, Chu Mo looked at Jiang Tian, ​​who seemed almost completely unprepared. But was it possible?

Chu Mo knew it was impossible.

There were only two possibilities for Jiang Tian's actions.

It was either a bluff or sheer confidence. A bluff?

Chu Mo pondered. Considering Jiang Tian's legendary past, Jiang Tian seemed to have no need for bluffing.

But should he just surrender?

Cold sweat broke out on Chu Mo's forehead.

As Jiang Tian drew closer, within a step, Chu Mo knew his chance had arrived.

This time, if he didn't act, Chu Mo didn't know if this was his only chance in this life.

Rumors were just rumors, after all. Chu Mo wanted to see it for himself.

Besides, within a meter, this was already Chu Mo's domain.

Rumors said Jiang Tian's marksmanship was divine.

In comparison, his martial arts skills were also strong, but there was no concrete evidence.

What if?

Chu Mo clung to a sliver of hope.

Besides, he himself wasn't weak; Chu Mo had always been very confident.

So Chu Mo made his move.

His move was swift.

It was incredibly fast.

Fast, accurate, and ruthless.

Chu Mo's fist flew directly towards Jiang Tian's throat, a killing blow from the start.

Forget all the fancy moves. In all his years abroad, Chu Mo had used and learned nothing of those techniques. If there were any, it was all for the purpose of killing; everything he did was aimed at eliminating his target.

"Oh, even at this point, you still chose to make a move?"

Jiang Tian, ​​holding the handcuffs, spoke calmly.

The voice was unusually calm.

This placed terrifying psychological pressure on Chu Mo, who had just made the move.

"What are you bluffing about?"

Chu Mo gritted his teeth, silently roaring inwardly. A barking dog doesn't bite, especially when killing; never waste words.

This was a principle Chu Mo had always adhered to.

But today...

This punch seemed to have caught Chu Mo off guard. He had held back two or three-tenths of his strength, intending to react instantly to Jiang Tian's counterattack and then unleash his subsequent killing blows with lightning speed.

But...

The expected resistance didn't materialize; the punch landed far too easily.

"Just that simple?"

Chu Mo's face was filled with surprise.

A cold smile also appeared on his lips.

It was all boasting. This time, Chu Mo was certain it was all boasting.

Damn it.

"I never expected that an ordinary piece of trash could actually fool me."

Chu Mo grinned, his face contorted with rage.

He was furious.

Yes, Jiang Tian was weak, and Chu Mo felt insulted. Back in school, Jiang Tian clearly had the potential for great things, but because of his unremarkable appearance, he gave off a sense of simplicity and ordinariness, coupled with his feigned aura, creating a significant sense of pressure and danger for Chu Mo, as if he were incredibly dangerous.

But it turned out that it was all a facade, an atmosphere created by Jiang Tian.

In reality, he seemed utterly pathetic!

Chu Mo's lips curled into a snarl. Why was he so confident at this moment? Because no one knew the power of his punch better than Chu Mo.

In this world, no one could withstand that punch with a thunderous roar. Even if they did, Chu Mo could change his attack in a fraction of a second, transforming his fist into a claw, squeezing the opponent's throat and crushing it instantly.

With this one move alone, Chu Mo had already killed at least a three-digit number of people abroad.

Thinking of this, a hint of nostalgia flickered in Chu Mo's eyes.

The flames of war and infiltration abroad had tempered Chu Mo in iron and fire; those days were the most exciting and unrestrained of his life.

Once he'd caused enough trouble back home, it was time to go abroad again.



Chapter 697 There are only eternal interests, no eternal friends.


After all, it was necessary to give both sides some time to cool down; they couldn't go too far in one go, otherwise, things wouldn't be so fun later.

Chu Mo was already thinking about what to do after killing Jiang Tian.

Because the speed was too fast, because this move had come so close.

Jiang Tian hadn't reacted yet, which meant

there was no such thing as a backup plan. Because at this point, Chu Mo was confident that no one could survive; this would be a fatal blow. Even if he reacted now, it would be too late.

But the result was unexpected.

Just as Chu Mo was thinking about killing Jiang Tian, ​​creating a sensation in Yunhai, and whether that sensation would be enough, he would definitely need to add fuel to the fire, perhaps even do something big, before going out to enjoy himself for a while, or perhaps going to a war-torn area to vent his anger.

While he was still fantasizing in his mind,

just when he thought he had the kill in hand,

Clang!!

A terrifying sound rang out.

Immediately afterwards...

*Crack!*

A snapping sound rang out, or rather, the sound of bones breaking.

Chu Mo felt a surge of excitement, almost bursting into a shout.

It's solved.

It's solved so easily.

Chu Mo's eyes lit up with joy. He had already assumed Jiang Tian's neck was broken, completely severed, and utterly beyond saving.

But the next moment.

Hiss!*

A piercing pain.

A terrifying pain.

It was an excruciating agony emanating from Chu Mo's arm.

No, it wasn't Jiang Tian's neck that was broken; it was just his own arm.

Chu Mo looked up at Jiang Tian in horror, only to find his arm twisted in a horrific position, a comminuted fracture.

The crucial point was that Jiang Tian seemed to have done nothing—no, his hand had been used to shield his neck.

In truth, given Jiang Tian's strength, even if that punch landed, Jiang Tian wouldn't be hurt. But Jiang Tian wouldn't try; even superhumans are still human.

What if he were caught off guard and ended up losing?

Even if the chance was only one in ten thousand, Jiang Tian wouldn't gamble with his life.

At the last moment, with terrifying speed, he simply placed one hand on his neck.

As the punch came, he merely pushed it outwards slightly.

But the force of that push, combined with the force reflected from the fist itself...

You must understand, the human body has its limits. Like many people who are incredibly strong, they often have a weakness: they seem prone to fractures. This isn't because their bodies are weak, but simply because their physical strength can't keep up with their power.

It's like your fist might have the force of 800 pounds, but your wrist's ability to withstand it—a careless mistake—could easily break a bone.

Stunned.

So dumbfounded!

That simple?

Chu Mo gasped, before he could even react, Jiang Tian had already grabbed his wrist, which was fractured in a comminuted fracture. With a sudden, forceful twist, Chu Mo was thrown to the side. Jiang Tian, ​​however, kicked Chu Mo in the back of the knee, sending him sprawling to his knees.

*Crack!!!*

Immediately following, handcuffs were placed on Chu Mo.

“Why bother? You should be grateful you put me in a good mood, which is crucial to my success, so I spared your life. Otherwise, you would have been dead before you even threw a punch.”

The words were crude, but the logic was sound. Jiang Tian's words sounded like boasting, but it was true.

The main reason was Chu Mo's importance and influence.

Chu Mo was a huge and terrifying threat.

But as the saying goes, to maximize the benefits for criminals, you must keep them alive.

If they were dead, their usefulness would be greatly reduced.

Even...

They could be used as a case study later, to interrogate them about any other acts that endangered public safety.

And in the end, they could still be used as a deterrent.

In short.

“If I hadn’t made my move just now, I think I would have regretted it for the rest of my life, because in my entire life, I have never seen anyone as terrifying as you.” After the initial pain, Chu Mo’s face instead showed an expression of excitement.

"A lunatic," Jiang Tian judged at a glance. Chu Mo was a pure, even undisguised, madman.

This man has gone insane.

No.

Or rather, he's been a lunatic from beginning to end, mentally unstable.

And everything here is over. In fact, Shang Mingyan's call had only just gone through. That's why it's said.

Mainly because everything that just happened was too fast.

Shang Mingyan dialed for help the moment he got out of the car. At that moment, the call had barely connected when the outcome was already decided.

Shang Mingyan didn't know what others would gain from capturing Chu Mo, but he knew perfectly well what reward Jiang Tian would receive. Because Jiang Tian was going to get a top-class merit!

So, Chu Mo in Jiang Tian's hands was essentially a top-class merit in Jiang Tian's hands.

Just as things were settling down there, when Shang Mingyan made the call, the Municipal Public Security Bureau was actually holding an emergency meeting.

Especially since almost all the leaders were present.

At least, all the key leaders from various departments, and even the Municipal Public Security Bureau itself, were present, including the various branch captains and brigade commanders.

Besides, as a leader in the political and legal system, Jiang Peng had the responsibility and obligation to oversee this terrorist case, as he was indeed a directly responsible person within the local public security, procuratorate, and judicial system in Yunhai.

In addition to Jiang Peng, there was also Zhou Zhao, the Party Secretary.

Zhou Zhao's expression was relatively calm.

However, if one were to observe closely, it would be noticeable that Zhou Zhao's expression was not particularly pleasant at this moment.

There was no other reason than that Zhou Zhao hadn't been having an easy time lately.

In fact, for Zhou Zhao, coming to Yunhai was naturally to achieve something.

And what does achievement require? The first step is to gain control of his own voice.

The entire voting meeting involved seven people from Yunhai, namely the seven standing committee members. If they could secure four votes, that would be sufficient.

This would allow them to control the entire Yunhai leadership.

However, Zhou Zhao's plan was beautiful, or rather, every top official has this idea upon taking office. But in reality, reality and ideals are often completely reversed.

For example, the mayor of Yunhai was a powerful local figure.

He had cultivated his influence there for many years.

Zhou Zhao had absolutely no power or influence, only a title.

The better news was that, due to Jiang Tian's involvement in the case, this local tyrant was imprisoned in a private cell.

He finally wanted to be happy.

But then...

something happened.

Someone from above came and won over Jiang Peng.

His influence was about to disappear again. Jiang Peng's winover also meant... becoming an enemy.



Chapter 698 Jiang Peng's High-Stakes Gamble: A Gamble on a meteoric rise to power


In terms of results, Zhou Zhao's world has improved, and his future looks brighter.

The future is even brighter.

Zhou Zhao is already envisioning a future where he controls the Thunder in the Cloud Sea, everything is in his hands, and he can begin to establish a one-man rule.

Of course, a one-man rule sounds unpleasant, and even though there have been repeated crackdowns from above, these crackdowns are still crackdowns. The poison of power is something that some people might be able to withstand at first, but over time, this poison will erode everyone.

Ambitious people want to control all the discourse.

Only in this way can they smoothly implement their ideas. However, similarly, over time, this gradually becomes a one-man rule, and because of this, being transferred to another place every two or three years is commonplace.

Once you reach a certain position, it's about how much you contribute to the local economic development and the local area. If you contribute more and do better than others in the same position, then you are the next candidate for promotion.

Zhou Zhao, now in his prime, naturally harbored this same mentality, eager to seize power and influence.

He hoped to smoothly implement his ideas in the future.

With the local strongman gone, Zhou Zhao thought his opportunity had arrived, but to his utter surprise, someone unexpectedly appeared out of nowhere. Crucially, this person was rumored to have a powerful and terrifying background.

Upon arriving, this person became the mayor of Yunhai.

Of course, this in itself wasn't unusual.

In terms of existing power dynamics, someone parachuted in would naturally be less influential than Zhou Zhao.

But Zhou Zhao, who thought he had everything under control, was completely taken aback.

Good heavens, before he even officially took office, this person convened a meeting.

The issue was raised during the meeting. Three people voted in favor, one abstained, and the three votes were Zhou Zhao's veto.

It exploded.

Zhou Zhao instantly exploded.

Unless someone nearing retirement and already enjoying their retirement, who could withstand this?

And one of the three who agreed was Jiang Peng, that scoundrel.

Zhou Zhao truly hadn't expected this!

Back then, when Jiang Peng was being promoted, what was wrong with me observing from the sidelines, watching the power struggle? What was wrong with secretly pulling a few strings behind the scenes? Did he have to retaliate like this?

Now look at him.

He's only been here a short while and already controls half the people, and that old bastard Jiang Peng is the first one to rush to his office to report.

There's nothing wrong with that.

In short.

Zhou Zhao is now furious.

The Yunhai bombing incident presented Zhou Zhao with an opportunity. After all, Jiang Peng was a leader in the political and legal system; surely it wouldn't be too much to give him a hard time.

Thinking this, Zhou Zhao's gaze fell upon a square-faced man in his forties sitting next to Jiang Peng.

That's right, this man was the newly appointed mayor of Yunhai.

He too had seemingly fallen from the sky; rumors circulated that he had connections at the highest levels, sent down from above for experience. Of course, if he performed well, he would be permanently stationed in Yunhai.

In short, the "gilded" level of connections is no longer viable, especially in Yunhai's current situation. It may seem appealing, but those with real influence wouldn't dare come here.

After all, Yunhai is now a double-edged sword; getting involved only brings trouble.

If done well, the benefits are significant, but equally complex, making substantial achievements here is extremely difficult.

And Jiang Tian's contributions are minimally attributed to them, almost negligible. At their level, the most important thing is economic development.

Zhou Zhao's brows furrowed, his eyes flashing with anger.

"Yunhai is in such a state now, with such a huge problem. The bombing at Yunhai University—although it was prevented from exploding—once it happened, it's bound to happen again, and countless times. Why did this happen? I think the Municipal Bureau has been busy lately and might have overlooked something, which is understandable. But as a leader in the political and legal system, Comrade Jiang Peng, you are undeniably responsible for this!"

Zhou Zhao directly attacked Jiang Peng.

Jiang Peng's expression changed slightly.

He seemed surprised to be targeted, but his gaze shifted to his side.

That's right, this was the mayor who had come down from above.

Jiang Peng was undeniably a clever man. Recently, Jiang Tian had been very busy, and Jiang Peng hadn't communicated with him. However, Jiang Peng had recently made full use of his ability to muddy the waters.

In terms of handling affairs, problem-solving, economic matters, and personal abilities, Jiang Peng might be mediocre, even considered below average among the leaders.

But Jiang Peng was unstoppable.

His skill at muddying the waters was exceptional!

In the situation before Yunhai, even bureau chiefs couldn't hold out. Whether they were newly appointed leaders or local officials, they either colluded with the corrupt officials or met with unexpected deaths. But Jiang Peng managed to survive, thanks to his masterful ability to muddy the waters and feign madness.

He adhered to the principle that the more you do, the more mistakes you make; the less you do, the fewer mistakes you make. In short, the more you do, the more mistakes you make; the less you do, the fewer mistakes you make; and if you do nothing, you make no mistakes.

Upon reaching his position in the political and legal system, he only had some say in the municipal bureau, his original promotion unit. He had absolutely no control over the procuratorate and the courts.

It wasn't that he lacked ability; it was mainly that he lacked experience and influence in negotiations. In fact, they were observing his potential and whether he was worthy of their attention.

Sometimes mediocrity is the greatest political wisdom.

But if you call Jiang Peng mediocre, he was incredibly clever at crucial moments.

Jiang Tian didn't even know Jiang Peng had such audacity.

He also knew it deeply.

At this position, ability was no longer the primary consideration; the most important thing was choice.

Choose correctly, and you'll rise rapidly; choose incorrectly, and well, sorry.

Aside from having abilities like Jiang Tian's, to the point where you can disregard everything, you also have top-tier big shots protecting you all the way.

While everyone else was still debating the new mayor and whether they should even come, Jiang Peng was already the first to rush over to express his determination and report on his work.

Under his influence, two other standing committee members even followed him.

Jiang Peng was the culprit behind Zhou Zhao's predicament; how could Zhou Zhao not hate him?

Whether Jiang Peng's choice was right or wrong is hard to say.

But the most important point is that this person has completely accepted Jiang Peng as a confidant.

They're practically inseparable.

Jiang Peng, now being targeted, glanced at the person beside him, seeing their unwavering gaze, and breathed a sigh of relief.

This choice was a high-stakes gamble for Jiang Peng.

If he won, he would rise rapidly through the ranks.

If he lost, he would remain in this position until retirement.



Chapter 699 A Struggle Without Gunfire, A Shocking Strike


Jiang Peng was well aware of his own abilities. His son, Jiang Tian, ​​clearly had connections; the powerful figures valued Jiang Tian, ​​and his current position was solely due to Jiang Tian's influence. Further promotion was impossible.

However, he could take a roundabout route, relying on himself instead of Jiang Tian!

The stage was already set.

Jiang Peng watched his son rapidly surpass him.

What if his son's position was higher than his own?

Jiang Peng also shrewdly knew that supporting Zhou Zhao was best limited to this position. It wouldn't offer him anything, but it would support this new leader.

If Zhou Zhao succeeded in his promotion, perhaps remembering his past favors—no, given their current relationship, once Jiang Peng became a trusted confidant, Zhou Zhao would definitely not forget that he had been there for him.

In the future, he might even aspire to a higher position. Even if he failed, it wouldn't matter much; with Jiang Tian around, it was impossible for him to be removed from his post.

After careful consideration, Jiang Peng made his choice this time.

Now, Jiang Peng didn't know if he was right or wrong, but he had become a focal point of the power struggle, Zhou Zhao's direct target. Within the city police bureau, he was being attacked directly. At this moment, he knew he couldn't shirk responsibility.

"I bear responsibility for this," Jiang Peng said immediately.

As the top official in charge of law enforcement, he couldn't escape this responsibility.

Other matters were negotiable, but the bombing at Yunnan University had a huge and egregious impact. Fortunately, it didn't escalate into a full-blown incident; otherwise, it would have truly shocked the entire nation, no, perhaps even the world.

"You're responsible? Can you afford to be responsible? Over two thousand teachers and students at Yunnan University! As the top official in charge of law enforcement, you are the primary person responsible. Luckily, it was discovered at the last minute; otherwise, even if you were executed, could you bring back two thousand teachers and students?"

Zhou Zhao launched his attack.

He directly attacked Jiang Peng.

Of course, Zhou Zhao also knew that Jiang Peng had only been in office for a short time, and such things were even more difficult to predict in advance, hence the problem.

Therefore, Zhou Zhao's purpose was actually quite simple: to suppress Jiang Peng, using Jiang Peng's demonstration against the new mayor as a warning to others who were still observing or lacked resolve.

“This is such a big case. As the leader of the political and legal system, as the top official, your responsibility is to lead the staff to effectively carry out the work and effectively prevent such cases. We were lucky this time it was discovered, but what if we weren't? Would all two thousand teachers and students be sacrificed?”

“I will definitely guard against such things at all costs and ensure that such problems never happen again,” Jiang Peng said, forcing a smile.

Zhou Zhao changed the subject, but his attacks on Jiang Peng didn't stop. He sneered, "Supervise? What good is supervision? Can it prevent it? Don't you know how dangerous this murderer is? He's utterly vicious and ruthless. Most importantly, he wasn't caught in the end; he escaped. Such cases could happen again at any time. How will you take responsibility? How will you prevent it?"

Zhou Zhao continued his attack.

"This kind of problem isn't just Comrade Jiang Peng's fault. Besides, Comrade Jiang Peng has only just taken office and hasn't even grasped many issues yet. How can he solve or prevent them? Comrade Zhou Zhao, you're asking too much. As a leader, you should be thinking about solving problems, not how to shirk responsibility."

At this moment, the mayor, who was standing next to Jiang Peng, naturally couldn't sit idly by. He knew even better that Zhou Zhao was using this opportunity to make an example of Jiang Peng, demonstrating against the other standing committee members.

If he didn't speak up, he wouldn't be able to stay in Yunhai anymore.

And this statement was quite cleverly worded, practically accusing Zhou Zhao of shirking responsibility.

Even worse, it was a direct name-calling: "You've been the secretary for so long and haven't been able to prevent or solve these problems. Comrade Jiang Peng has only just been promoted, and you're already shirking responsibility? Your behavior is too ugly."

Zhou Zhao chuckled and said, "Comrade Qin Limin, you're wrong about that. Am I wrong? The murderer escaped. Who dares to take responsibility for similar problems? I'm not shirking responsibility. If we don't prevent these things from happening in the first place, do we expect similar problems to occur again? Will we have the same luck as this time? The murderer fled in a vicious manner. I'm currently investigating the murderer's situation."

Qin Limin frowned.

Zhou Zhao was indeed right.

This was a big problem.

It could even be a major earthquake—no, a major bomb—exploding and potentially killing Qin Limin, who had just arrived in Yunhai.

As for their argument,

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong observed, neither interjecting nor speaking.

Mainly, they were also considering solutions.

The words were rough, but the principle was sound.

Sometimes, solutions can indeed be found in arguments.

At this point, seeing that the argument between the two sides had subsided, Minister Tan gestured to calm things down, saying, "We've roughly gathered the opinions of your Yunhai leaders. What you've said is all correct. To summarize, the most troublesome aspect is the killer's dangerousness. With ordinary pursuit, let alone catching him, even if we were lucky enough to find him, thirty to fifty people would be easy prey for him. That's the most difficult part. But no matter how difficult, the problem must be solved; otherwise, what are we for? So, now the main thing is for everyone to brainstorm and think about the key points for capturing the killer."

"That's right. The killer has probably left the country, but he could return at any time. We've already witnessed the killer's methods, for example, Director Han Xian," Gao Xiaolong said, looking at Han Xian.

Although it was the early hours of the morning,

all the leaders, high and low, were gathered together.

Is this a small matter?

Good heavens.

Now, let's think about how to write a report to submit to the higher-ups.

It can't be contained.

No one can contain this bombshell.

"The killer's investigative and counter-investigative skills are very strong; he has definitely undergone the most professional training. His methods are extremely sophisticated. Two of our comrades were killed with a single close-range shot, and the others were killed with a single bullet. His methods are very similar to Comrade Jiang Tian's. This person's methods are approaching Comrade Jiang Tian's fighting style," Han Xian sighed.

Although they were enemies, Han Xian had to admit Jiang Tian's prowess; at least in terms of individual combat ability, no one could match him.

Just then, Minister Tan's phone rang.

"Who is it?"



Chapter 700: The contrast is too great, the damage is too great.


For both Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong, this kind of thing was actually nothing new; in fact, within the system, the probability of such incidents occurring was far too high.

Struggles exist everywhere, and healthy competition can even promote local economic development. Besides, while Zhou Zhao's words were aggressive,

strictly speaking, as the top leader of the political and legal system, Jiang Peng couldn't escape responsibility. He needed to make a statement on this matter, regardless of his abilities; in his position, he had to demonstrate his capabilities.

Moreover, these two had seen it all; this kind of superficial struggle was really insignificant.

The only excessive thing was Zhou Zhao using this as an excuse, but as members of the police system, they couldn't interfere in local issues.

Fortunately, after the argument, Minister Tan's phone rang.

This interrupted the verbal sparring between the two sides, while Zhou Zhao sneered and glanced at Jiang Peng and the newly appointed Qin Limin.

As long as Chu Mo wasn't caught, Zhou Zhao could continue to use this to his advantage and maintain his dominance.

It felt great.

This feeling of controlling the entire situation was indeed quite satisfying.

"Heh, see? This is the consequence."

Zhou Zhao looked around, as if telling everyone that betraying him or opposing him would have no good end. What, you say you have powerful connections? So what? If you're a dragon, you'd better coil up; if you're a tiger, you'd better coil up. Coming to Yunhai and wanting to control the narrative? You'd better stay here and quietly gain experience.

As for connections, it's the same for everyone. In this position, Zhou Zhao wasn't without his own connections, nor were he without superiors. These were all carefully selected after many successful attempts to gain influence.

"What? What did you say?"

Just then, Minister Tan, who was frowning in thought, suddenly stood up. In this already complex and multifaceted environment, Minister Tan's action instantly drew everyone's attention in the quiet conference room.

Not only did he stand up, but his face was filled with shock and disbelief, and his breathing became rapid.

"Say it again, I didn't hear you clearly," Minister Tan said, his breath coming in short gasps.

Meanwhile,

The one who called Minister Tan was Shang Mingyan. She stood by the roadside, phone in handcuffs. Beside her, Jiang Tian had completely restrained Chu Mo. Of course, to prevent Chu Mo from having anything on him that could unlock the handcuffs, Jiang Tian didn't look away; instead, he kept all his attention fixed on Chu Mo.

Others might not know, but Jiang Tian knew all too well the threat Chu Mo posed.

If he were outside, Jiang Tian would have a hard time finding him in a crowd, but he had to admit that Chu Mo's talent and thought process were simply astonishing.

Or perhaps, it was a case of skill breeds daring; Chu Mo was too confident in himself, even to the point that Jiang Tian felt he could kill him in secret.

Killing one merchant is nothing; if we're going to kill someone, let's kill Jiang Tian too.

That kind of commotion was exactly what Chu Mo wanted to see, but unfortunately, in the end, he was facing Jiang Tian.

Or rather, anyone else facing Chu Mo's sneak attack at this moment might have been caught off guard and suffered a crushing defeat.

Even Jiang Tian hadn't expected Chu Mo to dare to jump out at this point. Of course, that aside, the most important thing was that Jiang Tian even found various dangerous items in Chu Mo's car.

How dangerous?

Hand grenades.

High explosives.

And a sniper rifle and a pistol.

All hidden under the spare tire in the trunk.

This man was far too dangerous. One wrong move, and he could be deadly. Jiang Tian had considered taking the opportunity to kill Chu Mo, but in the end, he decided against it and held back.

Otherwise, Chu Mo would be a corpse right now.

The main reason is that many serious criminals, if apprehended and tried alive, can potentially achieve 100% of the credit. However, if they die, the credit drops to around 50-60%. Although the boss promised Jiang Tian over the phone that regardless of whether Chu Mo was alive or dead, solving Chu Mo would result in a promotion, a raise, and even an exceptional continuation of the special merit award.

However, the death of Chu Mo wouldn't look good, and this needs to be considered. Also, while Jiang Tian isn't interested in where Chu Mo got his explosives and weapons, or in the case itself or the potential credit,

the investigation should reveal a significant case and a huge lead. For example, how were the weapons transported in? What were the routes? Where were they transported? Who were the members of the smuggling ring? Who did they buy them from?

These findings might even lead to a massive arms trafficking operation.

Jiang Tian lit a cigarette, slowly exhaled a puff, and glanced sideways at Shang Mingyan. Shang Mingyan's expression was somewhat excited. Jiang Tian couldn't help but feel a strange sense of accomplishment at seeing Shang Mingyan's emotions fluctuate like this.

As for showing off, Jiang Tian had already passed that stage. He didn't particularly enjoy it, especially since he'd gotten used to it so many times. Showing off in front of those people no longer interested him.

But Shang Mingyan...

This was a legend in criminal investigation. Previously, Jiang Tian could only see him in internal news, and he was someone Jiang Tian studied and learned from—someone incredibly high and mighty.

And now...

He was experiencing shock, then fear, and then admiration because of him.

It could be said that Shang Mingyan had displayed a lifetime's worth of emotional fluctuations in just one day with Jiang Tian—or rather, more emotional fluctuations than he'd experienced in his entire life.

Especially now, Shang Mingyan glanced at Jiang Tian, ​​a slight blush creeping onto his smug face. He had to admit, this moment was incredibly satisfying.

Listening to Minister Tan's shocked, disbelieving, and utterly bewildered tone on the phone, Shang Mingyan felt an overwhelming sense of pride.

It was exhilarating.

Although Jiang Tian was the one who did all this, Shang Mingyan couldn't help but feel a surge of pride about their current relationship.

"Well, Comrade Tan, I'm telling you solemnly, you didn't hear wrong, you didn't hear wrong at all. Chu Mo has been arrested. Thanks to Comrade Jiang Tian's wit and courage, Chu Mo was no match for him and has been captured alive. You can come and take him away without worry. Oh, we're currently... we're currently..."

Shang Mingyan glanced around and then reported the location.

Meanwhile, at the Municipal Public Security Bureau...

Silence, a deathly silence.
